Text.en: Oh! We just have very particular roles, you see. <continue>He is a Servitor. He tends to the congregation. Quite an important task. <continue>I am a missionary. My role is to spread word to those willing to hear about the New Tomorrow.
Text.zh: 哦！我们只是有着非常严格的职责划分，你懂的。<continue>他是仆从。负责照管信众。这是相当关键的工作。<continue>我是传教士。我的使命是向那些愿意聆听新明日福音的人传播真理。

Text.en: So you keep close watch over the story...
Text.zh: 所以你们对信息传播管控很严……

Text.en: So you're the <i>{better} manipulator...
Text.zh: 所以你算是<i>{更高明}的操控者……

Text.en: Ah. So you're in charge of how the Order is perceived...
Text.zh: 所以你负责教会的形象工程……

Text.en: So you control the narrative...
Text.zh: 所以你们掌握着话语权……

Text.en: I see. So he's not allowed to talk much...
Text.zh: 我懂了。你们不让他多嘴……

Text.en: So <i>{his} tongue is bound, while <i>{yours} is free...
Text.zh: 所以<i>{他的}嘴被封住，而<i>{你的}却很自由……

Text.en: So his role is to shut up? Charming.
Text.zh: 所以他的工作就是装哑巴？真有意思。

Text.en: Aye... so you're the tongue, and he the teeth.
Text.zh: 嗯…这么说你是舌头，他是牙齿。

Text.en: Not... exactly.
Text.zh: 倒也不是这样。

Text.en: Don't worry, friend. It all becomes clearer the closer you get. <continue>With a large operation such as ours, defined roles are an important part of achieving success. <continue>So many moving parts, the world over. It is freeing knowing your place in it all! <continue>I am very pleased you are showing such interest in our efforts!
Text.zh: 别担心，朋友。越深入了解，真相就越清楚。<continue>像我们这样规模庞大的组织，明确的分工是取得成功的关键要素。<continue>全世界有这么多错综复杂的环节。明白自己在其中的角色是多么解脱！<continue>我很欣慰你对我们的事业表现出如此浓厚的兴趣！

Text.en: What does 'success' look like to the Order?
Text.zh: 在教会看来，怎样才算成功？

Text.en: How do you <i>{measure} success?
Text.zh: 你们如何<i>{衡量}成功？

Text.en: So how will you know when you have succeeded?
Text.zh: 所以你们如何判断是否已经大功告成？

Text.en: When will your mission be over?
Text.zh: 你们的使命何时才算结束？

Text.en: How do you know if you've accomplished what you need to?
Text.zh: 你们怎么知道是否达成了目标？

Text.en: What is success to you?
Text.zh: 对你们而言，成功意味着什么？

Text.en: What's it look like to you, all said and done?
Text.zh: 在你们眼中，最终的胜利会是什么模样？

Text.en: So, this Order... what is it ye're after, exactly?
Text.zh: 所以你们这个暮光会…到底有什么追求？

Text.en: No more pain. No more suffering. No more horrors upon the world. <continue>A fresh start. A time we can be proud of. <continue>Wraeclast is beleaguered by the most detestable of creations. Unnatural creations. <continue>Creations that manipulate our existence. <continue>Their time must come to an end!
Text.zh: 再无痛苦。再无苦难。世上再无恐怖之物。<continue>一个崭新的开端。一个我们可以引以为傲的时代。<continue>瓦尔克拉斯被最令人憎恶的造物所折磨。违逆自然的造物。<continue>操控我们生存的造物。<continue>它们的末日必须降临！

Text.en: Do you mean... gods?
Text.zh: 你是指……诸神吗？

Text.en: Oh of course, you're talking about Divinity.
Text.zh: 哦当然，你说的是神性力量。

Text.en: Hmm. I am not sure I follow what you mean.
Text.zh: 嗯。我不太理解你的意思。

Text.en: Sounds like mankind to me...
Text.zh: 听起来像是在说人类……

Text.en: Who do you speak of? Beasts? <i>{Rathin}?
Text.zh: 你指的是什么？邪兽？还是<i>{腐化}？

Text.en: The Gods.
Text.zh: 那些神祇。

Text.en: Who the heck are you talking about?
Text.zh: 你到底在说谁？

Text.en: Until that last part, I thought you meant kinfolk. But I'm guessin' you mean gods.
Text.zh: 听前半句还以为你说的是亲眷族，搞半天是指诸神啊。

Text.en: The gods must never come back. And Divinity must forever return to its dormancy within whatever void it sprung from. <continue>Our Saviour is almost ready... He will remake the world. <continue>A world free of Divinity. And thus, a world free of Corruption.
Text.zh: 诸神绝不能重返人间。神性必须永远回到它诞生的虚无深渊中长眠。<continue>我们的救世主已经快准备就绪了……他将重新塑造这个世界。<continue>一个摆脱神性的世界。因此，也是一个远离腐蚀的世界。

Text.en: And how will the Saviour achieve this?
Text.zh: 救世主要怎么实现这个目标？

Text.en: Evangelists hard at work. I must say, I am not compelled.
Text.zh: 传教士们在卖力宣传。坦白说，我并不买账。

Text.en: The gods are responsible for much pain... but also, much beauty.
Text.zh: 诸神确实带来了许多痛苦……但同样也带来了许多美好。

Text.en: The First Ones have never brought me harm. Why should I fear them?
Text.zh: 初生之子从未伤害过我。我为什么要畏惧它们？

Text.en: The Spirit is all I know. It's outside the folly of man... which gods are not.
Text.zh: 万灵是我的一切。它超脱了人类的愚蠢……而诸神并非如此。

Text.en: The gods are flawed. Divinity is flawed. As they are derived from man. <continue>But the Dreamer... he is without these deficiencies.
Text.zh: 诸神是有缺陷的。神性是有缺陷的。因为它们源于人类。<continue>但幻梦者……他没有这些瑕疵。

Text.en: The gods have caused these lands a lot of trouble. I can see that.
Text.zh: 诸神确实给这片大地制造了不少麻烦。这点我能理解。

Text.en: The gods are flawed, aye... but mortals no less so.
Text.zh: 诸神确有缺陷，但凡人亦非完美。

Text.en: Utopia will not remember these gods. They will have no name there. They will never be, nor will they ever have been. <continue>Praise be in Utopia's name! Praise be upon the Saviour! <continue>Now... are you ready to take the pledge?
Text.zh: 乌托邦不会记住这些神祇。它们在那里将无名无姓。它们既不存在，也从未存在过。<continue>赞美乌托邦之名！赞美救世主！<continue>现在……你准备好宣誓了吗？

Text.en: You are interested, yes? I knew it! Visit Arastas when you are sure you are ready.
Text.zh: 你有兴趣，对不对？我就知道！当你确定准备好时，请造访阿拉斯塔。

Text.en: Raving beasts, pillaging nature for all it has... <continue>Craiceann of the deep, Fenumus of the night, Farrul of the plains, Saqawal of the sky... <continue>And of course, the Great Wolf and the Gull. <continue>Primitive fiends! How can one base their existence on such monsters? <continue>They serve no purpose in the new tomorrow.
Text.zh: 疯狂的野兽，为了私欲而掠夺自然……<continue>深渊的克林斯昂，暗夜的费努姆斯，荒野的法瑞尔，苍穹的萨奇沃……<continue>当然，还有巨狼和鸥喙。<continue>他们都是原始的恶魔！我们怎么能把自己的性命托付给这些怪物？<continue>它们在新的明日中毫无立足之地。

Text.en: The Greatwolf is a powerful symbol to our clans. One that inspires fear, but also with it... <continue>Loyalty.
Text.zh: 巨狼对我们部族来说是强大的图腾。它激发敬畏，而敬畏带来了……<continue>忠诚。

Text.en: It is said the Gull is what protects us from infinite darkness. From ceasing to exist when our time comes. <continue>Would you banish us to the void as well?
Text.zh: 传说鸥喙庇护我们免受无尽黑暗的吞噬。当我们的大限来临时就不会消散在虚无中。<continue>你也要把我们驱逐到虚无里吗？

Text.en: Loyalty to what cause? Roaming for blood with the pack? <continue>To lycanthropy? To eat their own young? <continue>And what of poor Rigwald? What became of him once he sought the Great Wolf?
Text.zh: 对什么的忠诚？与狼群一起四处游荡杀戮？<continue>对兽性的忠诚？对后代张开血盆大口？<continue>可怜的瑞佛呢？当他寻得巨狼时，他的结局又如何？

Text.en: You will come to see that the promises the First Ones made are meaningless. <continue>The gods are self-serving opportunists. <continue>In your case, they are but scavenger animals who deserve no notoriety or worship.
Text.zh: 你终将明白初生之子的承诺都是空话连篇。<continue>诸神都是自私自利的投机分子。<continue>以你们的情况来说，它们不过是不值得声名和崇拜的食腐走狗。

Text.en: The First Ones are symbols. They give meaning in darkness, where the Ezomytes saw none to be found. <continue>This does not mean they must be perfect.
Text.zh: 初生之子是精神象征。它们在艾兹麦人找不到任何意义的黑暗中赋予意义。<continue>这并不代表它们必须尽善尽美。

Text.en: This is your position. Not mine.
Text.zh: 这是你的观点。不是我的。

Text.en: One can forgive imperfection, but not monstrosities. <continue>You care about your adopted homeland. Its occupants. But do not be fooled by their beliefs. <continue>Think on it for a time. You owe it to yourself to do so.
Text.zh: 人们可以宽恕不完美，但无法容忍怪物行径。<continue>你关心那收养了你的“故土”。关心那里的居民。但别被他们的信仰所蒙蔽。<continue>好好想想这个问题。你欠自己一个深思熟虑的答案。

Text.en: Think about it, dear Ezomyte. Ask yourself, what have the First Ones done for <i>{you?} <continue>And what have <i>{you} done for yourself?
Text.zh: 想想吧，亲爱的艾兹麦人。问问你自己，初生之子为<i>{你}做过什么？<continue>而<i>{你}又为自己做过什么？

Text.en: Who do you worship, covenite? What Coven God tethers to you?
Text.zh: 你崇拜什么，巫会中人？哪位巫会之神与你相连？

Text.en: I worship my own potential. I've no need for the divine either.
Text.zh: 我崇拜自己的潜能。我同样不需要神明。

Text.en: Well then, it seems we may be a good fit for each other!
Text.zh: 那么，看起来我们可能志趣相投！

Text.en: I've also no need for a giant operation of smiling, overly pleasant idiots either.
Text.zh: 我也不需要一群笑容满面、过分热情的蠢货。

Text.en: Kindness costs nothing. But the reward is immense.
Text.zh: 善意不需要任何成本。但回报却是巨大的。

Text.en: If that kindness is fake... it costs you your dignity. <continue>But perhaps you lost that long ago. So... keep smiling.
Text.zh: 如果那份善意是伪装的……它会让你丢掉尊严。<continue>不过也许你早就没有尊严了。所以……继续保持微笑吧。

Text.en: Hmm... there is one you are tethered to. Perhaps you aren't even aware.
Text.zh: 嗯……有某个存在与你建立了联系。也许你甚至察觉不到。

Text.en: What?
Text.zh: 什么？

Text.en: It is too late for you. Your activities of necromancy have made it such.
Text.zh: 对你来说为时已晚。你的死灵术活动造成了这种后果。

Text.en: What do you know of this? What do you mean!?
Text.zh: 你知道些什么？你这话是什么意思！？

Text.en: Do not fret. As long as you live... it won't matter.
Text.zh: 别担心。只要你还活着……这就无关紧要。

Text.en: Sisters of the Sun and Moon. Solerai and Lundara. <i>{Sekhemas} of ancient times.
Text.zh: 日月双姝。索瑞和兰拉。远古时代的丝克玛。

Text.en: Yes. They saved the Vastiri and united our people. We honour them still.
Text.zh: 没错。她们拯救了瓦斯提里，团结了我们的族群。我们至今仍在缅怀她们。

Text.en: Yet they waged war upon each other with little care for your desert. Little care at all for the world. <continue>In their pure hatred... they would have torn the very sky apart. <continue>Where would your desert be if they never ended the Winter of the World, due to their fury at each other?
Text.zh: 然而她们互相开战，对你们的沙漠漠不关心。对整个世界都漠不关心。<continue>在她们纯粹的仇恨中……她们会把苍穹都撕成碎片。<continue>如果她们因为彼此的愤怒而从未终结世界之冬，你们的沙漠还会存在吗？

Text.en: That... is a hypothetical question. It is of no relevance. You know, that is not what happened.
Text.zh: 这……这只是假设，毫无现实意义。你明知事实并非如此。

Text.en: Yet you know very well that it could have. And they... they would not have cared. <continue>So long as they could keep fighting. Keep hating. <continue>Are these really idols to revere?
Text.zh: 然而你心知肚明那完全可能发生。而她们……她们根本不会在乎。<continue>只要她们能继续厮杀。继续仇恨。<continue>这些真的是值得景仰的偶像吗？

Text.en: They fought for their values. All you do is <i>{talk} about yours. Enough of this.
Text.zh: 他们为各自的信念而战，而你们不过<i>{夸夸其谈}。我听够了。

Text.en: Sin... and Innocence. Brothers of disparate dispositions. <continue>Templar writings declare them born Azmeri, in their mortal lives. But what became of them?
Text.zh: 罪……与纯净。性情截然不同的兄弟。<continue>圣堂教团的典籍宣称他们在凡人时期是阿兹莫里人。但他们后来变成了什么？

Text.en: Well... I've an idea what became of at least one of 'em.
Text.zh: 嗯……我至少知道他们当中一人的下落。

Text.en: They plunged the world into danger as they saw fit. Deceived us all, as necessary. <continue>But what if the story didn't end there? What if it didn't even begin where they had said?
Text.zh: 他们随心所欲地把世界拖入险境。在需要时欺骗我们所有人。<continue>但如果故事并未在那里落幕呢？如果它甚至没有从他们声称的地方开始呢？

Text.en: They <i>{were} Azmeri. But they were nothing really like us mountain-born.
Text.zh: 他们<i>{曾是}阿兹莫里人，但他们与我们这些山民截然不同。

Text.en: Precisely! So who were they <i>{really?} Putting aside Templar propaganda. <continue>Who... <i>{were they?}
Text.zh: 正是如此！那么他们的<i>{真实}身份是什么？撇开圣堂教团的大肆宣传。<continue>他们……<i>{究竟是谁}？

Text.en: I dunno. And to put it plainly, I don't care. <continue>The Spirit guides me. Not those ancient wayward brothers.
Text.zh: 我不知道。而且坦率地说，我也不在乎。<continue>指引我的是万灵，不是那些远古的迷途兄弟。

Text.en: Exactly. And the Spirit is no god. It is a force within you!
Text.zh: 正是如此。而万灵并非神祇。它是你内在的力量！

Text.en: Don't speak to me of things you don't understand.
Text.zh: 别跟我谈论你一窍不通的事情。

Text.en: Consider my words, it is all I ask.
Text.zh: 考虑一下我的话，这是我唯一的要求。

Text.en: Sin... and Innocence. Brothers of disparate dispositions. <continue>Templar writings declare them born Azmeri, in their mortal lives. But what became of them?
Text.zh: 罪……与纯净。性情截然不同的兄弟。<continue>圣堂教团的典籍宣称他们在凡人时期是阿兹莫里人。但他们后来变成了什么？

Text.en: Well... I've an idea what became of at least one of 'em.
Text.zh: 嗯……我至少知道他们当中一人的下落。

Text.en: They plunged the world into danger as they saw fit. Deceived us all, as necessary. <continue>But what if the story didn't end there? What if it didn't even begin where they had said?
Text.zh: 他们随心所欲地把世界拖入险境。在需要时欺骗我们所有人。<continue>但如果故事并未在那里落幕呢？如果它甚至没有从他们声称的地方开始呢？

Text.en: They <i>{were} Azmeri. But they were nothing really like us mountain-born.
Text.zh: 他们<i>{曾是}阿兹莫里人，但他们与我们这些山民截然不同。

Text.en: Precisely! So who were they <i>{really?} Putting aside Templar propaganda. <continue>Who... <i>{were they?}
Text.zh: 正是如此！那么他们的<i>{真实}身份是什么？撇开圣堂教团的大肆宣传。<continue>他们……<i>{究竟是谁}？

Text.en: Something else. More ancient than we even realised. I don't know what to make of it. <continue>What I do know is that the Spirit is my calling, not man-made gods, Azmeri or otherwise.
Text.zh: 是别的某种存在，比我们所知的更为古老。我自己也没想明白。<continue>但召唤我的是万灵，不是人造的神祇，无论阿兹莫里的还是别的什么。

Text.en: Yes! The Spirit is within you. A compass. A feeling.
Text.zh: 没错！万灵就在你心中。一个罗盘。一种感应。

Text.en: Enough. You know not of what you speak, Lorandis.
Text.zh: 够了。洛兰迪斯，你根本不知道自己在胡说什么。

Text.en: Just think on my words, that is all I ask.
Text.zh: 只要思考我的话就够了，这是我全部的要求。

Text.en: The Benevolent Dreamer. One who might choose to doom us all, given the chance.
Text.zh: 仁慈的幻梦者。一个如果有机会很可能会毁灭我们所有人的存在。

Text.en: The Dreamer is beyond man's woes. He is committed to attaining transcendence. <continue>And I will share that path with him.
Text.zh: 幻梦者超越了凡人的烦扰。他致力于达成超越境界。<continue>而我将追随他，和他一道走下去。

Text.en: So... he seeks Divinity.
Text.zh: 所以……他追求神性。

Text.en: Absolutely not.
Text.zh: 绝非如此。

Text.en: What <i>{is} transcendence?
Text.zh: 究竟何为<i>{超凡入圣}？

Text.en: Unbridled potential. Becoming attuned to your very existence. And overcoming it.
Text.zh: 无拘无束的潜能。与你的存在本质产生共鸣。并且超越它。

Text.en: So then, becoming... Divine? <continue>I'm sorry to say, your <i>{Dreamer} is being purposefully vague. <continue>Then you have gone and filled in the gaps, to appease your logical mind.
Text.zh: 也就是，成为……神圣的存在？<continue>很抱歉，你的<i>{幻梦者}故意语焉不详。<continue>然后你自己填补了空白，好安抚你的理性思维。

Text.en: Nonsense. My path lies wide open before me! I see and feel his guidance at all times! <continue>It is why I am still alive, and my brothers... are not.
Text.zh: 胡说八道。我的道路在我面前清晰展开！我时时刻刻都能看到并感受到他的指引！<continue>这就是为什么我还活着，而我的兄弟……已经不在人世了。

Text.en: Emotional detachment. A commonplace symptom of those that blindly follow.
Text.zh: 情感疏离。盲目追随者的典型症状。

Text.en: You cannot understand. It is a pointless conversation.
Text.zh: 你无法理解。这是毫无意义的对话。

Text.en: I simply beg the question. Answer it in your own time.
Text.zh: 我只是抛出疑问。用你自己的时间来回答它吧。

Text.en: Coin. Drink. Bounty. Women...
Text.zh: 金币。烈酒。财宝。女人……

Text.en: Sounds like a good night out.
Text.zh: 听起来像是个美妙的夜晚。

Text.en: These fleeting moments of gratification you so worship... <continue>What happiness have they brought you?
Text.zh: 你如此痴迷的这些稍纵即逝的满足感……<continue>它们给你带来了什么真正的幸福？

Text.en: I could go into detail about that, if you've got the time...
Text.zh: 如果你有时间的话，我可以给你详细描述一番……

Text.en: Pleasure of the flesh, is only skin deep. <continue>Tell me, what makes <i>{you} who you are?
Text.zh: 肉欲的愉悦，只是表面功夫。<continue>告诉我，是什么造就了<i>{你}这个人？

Text.en: You wanna poke around inside my head? Be my guest. <continue>You don't know the first bloody thing about me.
Text.zh: 你想在我脑袋里翻箱倒柜？随你的便。<continue>你对我毫无了解。

Text.en: You are from Trarthus. Judging from your hot head, you're of House Azadi.
Text.zh: 你来自特拉特斯。从你这暴脾气来看，你是阿扎迪家族的人。

Text.en: Well... you don't know... the second thing about me.
Text.zh: 呃……除了那个……你对我根本毫无了解……

Text.en: As mentioned, you thrive on the illicit underbelly offerings the world presents to you. <continue>But that brings you no fulfillment. <continue>You will realise one day, you have spent a lifetime pursuing nothing. With nothing to show for it.
Text.zh: 如我所说，你沉迷于世界塞给你的那些见不得光的勾当。<continue>但那并不能给你带来真正的满足。<continue>总有一天你会醒悟，你花了一辈子追求虚无缥缈的东西，到头来却是一无所获。

Text.en: We're done here, mate.
Text.zh: 我们聊完了，伙计。

Text.en: Think about it. We can offer you hope. I promise you that.
Text.zh: 想想吧。我们能给你带来希望。我向你保证这一点。

Text.en: The Druids of Skothe... once proud, now nothing but whispers. <continue>Slaughtered, or bent into service of something darker. <continue>And here you stand... a relic of their folly. Proof that their path was doomed from the start.
Text.zh: 斯科提的德鲁伊…曾经荣耀加身，如今只余风中残烬。<continue>惨遭屠戮，或被迫屈从于更黑暗的存在。<continue>而你立于此处…正是他们愚行的活遗迹，证明其道路自始便注定覆灭。

Text.en: Doomed? Nay. We did that to ourselves.
Text.zh: 注定覆灭？不。那不过是我们自作自受。

Text.en: Your past pains you still - that much is plain. But you need not bear it forever.  <continue>A brighter future awaits - if only you've the courage to seize it.
Text.zh: 你的过去仍在灼痛你——这再明显不过。但你不必永远承受这份重担。<continue>更明亮的未来正在前方——只需你鼓起勇气去把握。

Text.en: Dinna speak to me of courage, preacher man.
Text.zh: 少跟我谈什么勇气，传道的。

Text.en: Think on my words. That is all I ask.
Text.zh: 想想我的话吧。我只求如此。

Text.en: The Druids of Skothe... once proud, now nothing but whispers. <continue>Slaughtered, or bent into service of something darker. <continue>And here you stand... a relic of their folly. Proof that their path was doomed from the start.
Text.zh: 斯科提的德鲁伊…曾经荣耀加身，如今只余风中残烬。<continue>惨遭屠戮，或被迫屈从于更黑暗的存在。<continue>而你立于此处…正是他们愚行的活遗迹，证明其道路自始便注定覆灭。

Text.en: Doom, folly... call it what ye like. I dinnae remember, nor care to. What's gone is gone.
Text.zh: 覆灭，愚行…随你怎么称呼。我既不记得，也无意深究。都过去了。

Text.en: Yet still it weighs on you - I can see it in your eyes. In the way you carry yourself.
Text.zh: 可它依然压在你身上——我从你眼中看得出来，从你步履间感受得到。

Text.en: Ye know naught of me, nor my burdens.
Text.zh: 你对我一无所知，更不配评判我身上的担子。

Text.en: Then cast them off. Leave your failures behind. Pledge yourself to tomorrow.
Text.zh: 那就卸下重担，把过往的失败留在身后，向明天立下你的誓言。

Text.en: We do not despise the Kalguurans. We merely consider them lucky fools... <continue>In their faraway land, they have no concept of the problems we face here! <continue>So they do not take our struggle seriously.
Text.zh: 我们并不鄙视卡古兰人。我们只是认为他们是走运的笨蛋……<continue>在他们遥远的土地上，他们对我们在这里面临的问题毫无概念！<continue>所以他们不把我们的苦战当回事。

Text.en: What of the Kalguurans <i>{here}? They seem honourable.
Text.zh: <i>{这里}的卡古兰人怎么样？他们看起来值得尊敬。

Text.en: No gods, no Corruption. What do they do for fun?
Text.zh: 没有神明，没有腐化。他们怎么寻找乐趣？

Text.en: What rubbish. They seem very honourable to me. They are wondrous people.
Text.zh: 一派胡言。在我看来，他们十分高尚，是非凡的族群。

Text.en: We've always been on our own. Why should they help us?
Text.zh: 我们一直都是孤军奋战。他们凭什么要帮我们？

Text.en: We should be working together. Can we win them over?
Text.zh: 我们应该通力合作。我们能争取到他们吗？

Text.en: What of it? Do we care about the struggles on their distant continent?
Text.zh: 他们遥远大陆上的苦难，我们有什么好关心的？

Text.en: Everywhere's got problems, believe me.
Text.zh: 哪里都有麻烦事，相信我。

Text.en: Easy to sneer at what ye dinnae understand.
Text.zh: 对你理解不了的东西说风凉话，倒是轻松。

Text.en: Kalguurans are not like us. They stand apart. Their souls are clean. No Divinity, no Corruption. <continue>Wraeclast has gods capable of horrors beyond comprehension. <continue>Yet their sole fear looms largest - their aging tyrant King. Absurd.
Text.zh: 卡古兰人与我们迥然不同。他们独成一派。他们的灵魂是纯净的。没有神性，没有腐化。<continue>瓦尔克拉斯的神祇能够制造出难以想象的恐怖。<continue>然而他们最大的恐惧却是——他们那位年迈的暴君国王。真是荒唐。

Text.en: None of them talk about their King. Do you know something?
Text.zh: 他们当中没有人谈论他们的国王。你知道点什么吗？

Text.en: Absurd <i>{is} the word. What is their King trying to do, anyway?
Text.zh: 荒唐这个词用得恰当。他们的国王到底想干什么？

Text.en: Some gods can lead their people to glory. Kings only cause suffering.
Text.zh: 有些神祇能引领子民走向荣耀。国王只会带来苦难。

Text.en: Have they told you about their King? What's his aim?
Text.zh: 他们跟你说过他们国王的事吗？他的目的是什么？

Text.en: Why do they fear their King?
Text.zh: 他们为什么害怕自己的国王？

Text.en: We fear the danger we know more than the danger that lurks on the horizon.
Text.zh: 我们更畏惧已知的威胁，而不是潜伏在远方的危险。

Text.en: Their King? What's he up to, anyhow?
Text.zh: 他们的国王？他到底在搞什么名堂？

Text.en: They're awful tight-lipped about their King. What do <i>{you} know of him?
Text.zh: 他们对自己国王的事可真够嘴严的。关于这位陛下，<i>{你}知道些什么？

Text.en: The Kalguuran King seeks Vaal artefacts. I know not why, but the reason must be sordid. <continue>The Vaal destroyed themselves. We will not make the same mistake.
Text.zh: 卡古兰国王在搜寻瓦尔遗物。我不知道原因，但动机肯定不纯。<continue>瓦尔人自取灭亡，我们绝不会重蹈覆辙。

Text.en: Behold, Marius!
Text.zh: 看哪，马里乌斯！

Text.en: I am so pleased you made it, friend. Arastas and the Order welcomes you! What's ours is yours... break bread with us... share–
Text.zh: 我很高兴你赶来了，朋友。阿拉斯塔和教会欢迎你！我们的一切都是你的……与我们共享食物……分享——

Text.en: I'm here for information.
Text.zh: 我来这里是为了收集情报。

Text.en: Hush now. I don't think so.
Text.zh: 闭嘴，我没兴趣。

Text.en: Please. Hold your tongue a moment. I need to know something...
Text.zh: 请暂时闭上你的嘴。我需要了解一些事情……

Text.en: I'm not here for bread.
Text.zh: 我来这不是为了填饱肚子。

Text.en: Please... you're givin' me a headache. I need information.
Text.zh: 拜托……你让我头疼得要命。我需要的是情报。

Text.en: Enough. I am seeking information.
Text.zh: 够了。我在寻找信息。

Text.en: Quit it. I'm looking for some information.
Text.zh: 停下。我在找一些消息。

Text.en: Oh, keep yer bread. I'm here for answers.
Text.zh: 留着你的救济粮吧。我是来问话的。

Text.en: I see. How can we assist you?
Text.zh: 我明白了。我们如何为你提供帮助？

Text.en: I'm looking for an ancient forge. Have you seen anything like that here?
Text.zh: 我在寻找一座远古熔炉。你在这里见过类似的东西吗？

Text.en: I'd like to take a little tour of this island. See the sights.
Text.zh: 我想在这座岛上四处逛逛。欣赏一下风景。

Text.en: Have you seen any signs of an ancient forge on this island?
Text.zh: 你在这座岛上见过远古熔炉的迹象吗？

Text.en: I'm looking for ancient ruins. There should be some here. Have you seen them?
Text.zh: 我在寻找远古遗迹。这里应该有一些。你见过吗？

Text.en: Have you seen any worn old ruins on this island?
Text.zh: 你在这座岛上见过任何年代久远的破旧遗迹吗？

Text.en: I must find an ancient forge. I have reason to believe it's on this island.
Text.zh: 我必须找到一座远古熔炉。我有理由相信它就在这座岛上。

Text.en: I'm lookin' for some real old ruins. Got any 'round here?
Text.zh: 我在找一些真正古老的废墟。这附近有没有？

Text.en: I'm after a forge - near old as the land itself. Know of such a thing?
Text.zh: 我在找一座熔炉——跟脚下这片大地一样古老。听说过这等事物吗？

Text.en: Hmm... I think I <i>{may} be able to help. But it will require a notable member of the Order. <continue>Please, follow me.
Text.zh: 嗯……我想我<i>{或许}能够帮到你。但这需要我们教会的一位重要成员出面。<continue>请跟我来。

Text.en: Welcome to Arastas, friend. Praise those who seek Utopia! <continue>I am Servitor Marius. I'm pleased to tell you, the Twilight Order opens its arms to all. <continue>However, only those who pledge to our cause are welcome within this hall...
Text.zh: 欢迎来到阿拉斯塔，朋友。赞美那些追寻乌托邦的人！<continue>我是仆从马里乌斯。我很荣幸地告诉你，暮光会向所有人敞开怀抱。<continue>不过，只有那些向我们的事业宣誓的人才被允许进入这座大厅……

Text.en: Are you seeking answers, strong one?
Text.zh: 你在寻求答案吗，壮士？

Text.en: Are you seeking redemption, covenite?
Text.zh: 你在寻求救赎吗，巫会中人？

Text.en: Are you seeking hope, desert-born?
Text.zh: 你在寻求希望吗，沙漠之子？

Text.en: Are you seeking belonging, recluse?
Text.zh: 你在寻求归属感吗，隐世者？

Text.en: Are you seeking forgiveness, Azmeri?
Text.zh: 你在寻求宽恕吗，阿兹莫里人？

Text.en: Are you seeking enlightenment, disciple?
Text.zh: 你在寻求开悟吗，信徒？

Text.en: Are you seeking fulfilment, degenerate?
Text.zh: 你在寻求满足吗，堕落者？

Text.en: Are you seeking peace, wild one?
Text.zh: 你在寻求安宁吗，荒野之子？

Text.en: Aren't we all?
Text.zh: 我们不都在寻求吗？

Text.en: Redemption? I've no need for redemption, dear.
Text.zh: 救赎？我不需要救赎，亲爱的。

Text.en: Hope could be what helps us save the world.
Text.zh: 希望或许是拯救世界的关键所在。

Text.en: Well, that was brutal. I don't think I am, no.
Text.zh: 嗯，那话说得挺狠。我想我不是在寻求这个。

Text.en: I seek the Spirit. Only in that, can I have my forgiveness.
Text.zh: 我寻求万灵。只有在那里，我才能得到宽恕。

Text.en: No. The Dreamer has <i>{already} enlightened me.
Text.zh: 不。幻梦者<i>{已经}开悟了我。

Text.en: Hang on...
Text.zh: 等等……

Text.en: I seek many things, preacher-man... but unless ye've a cure for amnesia, I suggest ye move on.
Text.zh: 我要找的东西多了，传道的……除非你有治失忆症的药，不然我劝你继续赶路吧。

Text.en: Indeed. And here you shall find them! <continue>Join us. Pledge yourself to a new tomorrow!
Text.zh: 确实如此。你将在这里找到这些！<continue>加入我们。向崭新的明日宣誓！

Text.en: Sometimes we are blind to our own reflection. <continue>But there is hope for you. Pledge yourself to a higher destiny!
Text.zh: 有时我们对自己的倒影视而不见。<continue>但你还有希望。向更崇高的命运宣誓！

Text.en: You are very wise. Yes, indeed. <continue>You could be a part of that future. Pledge yourself to a shining horizon!
Text.zh: 你很睿智。是的，确实如此。<continue>你可以成为那个未来的一份子。向光辉的地平线宣誓！

Text.en: I see it in your eyes. You <i>{need} a sense of purpose. <continue>I am pleased for you. Here, you will find it. Pledge yourself to a new beginning!
Text.zh: 我在你眼中看出来了。你<i>{需要}一种使命感。<continue>我为你感到欣慰。在这里，你会找到它。向全新的开端宣誓！

Text.en: You believe in your <i>{Spirit.} But I believe in <i>{you.} <continue>Untether yourself from pain. Pledge yourself to a worthy cause!
Text.zh: 你信仰你的<i>{万灵}。但我信仰<i>{你。}<continue>从痛苦中解脱出来。向一个值得的事业宣誓！

Text.en: We must not rely on <i>{dreams}, but reality. <continue>The Order can provide all you will ever need. Pledge yourself to find your true potential!
Text.zh: 我们不能依靠<i>{幻想}。而要依靠现实。<continue>教会能够提供你永远需要的一切。宣誓去发现你真正的潜力！

Text.en: Because we are willing to–
Text.zh: 因为我们愿意——

Text.en: Ah, but that is where you are mistaken! <continue>The Order does not ask you to remember - only to begin anew.  <continue>Forget what you've lost. Pledge yourself to the Order, and claim what you yet could be.
Text.zh: 哈！这你可就大错特错了！<continue>暮光会并非要你铭记过往——而是邀你重获新生。<continue>忘却你所失去的。向暮光会宣誓效忠，去争取你尚未成就的将来。

Text.en: No, no... hang on. Degenerate?
Text.zh: 不，不……等等。堕落者？

Text.en: We are willing to absolve your past self of his crimes! <continue>In his place will stand a new man. Pledge yourself to a fresh start!
Text.zh: 我们愿意赦免你过去的一切罪孽！<continue>一个全新的人将取代过去的他。向崭新的开始宣誓！

Text.en: Yes! The pledge is something we take very seriously. <continue>It is a commitment. A commitment to yourself, and to Utopia. <continue>All we ask is that you join us wholeheartedly, without fear. <continue>Doubt is natural, and we will answer all your questions. <continue>Leaving only pure determination toward the betterment of the world!
Text.zh: 没错！宣誓是我们极其郑重对待的事情。<continue>这是一种承诺。对你自己和对乌托邦的承诺。<continue>我们所要求的就是你全心全意地加入我们，不要畏惧。<continue>疑虑是人之常情，我们会解答你所有的疑问。<continue>只留下对改善世界的纯粹决心！

Text.en: I will listen... but I cannot promise more than that.
Text.zh: 我会听的……但别的我保证不了。

Text.en: I'm not trying to better the world. I'm trying to <i>{conquer} it.
Text.zh: 我可没想过要造福世界吗，我要的是<i>{征服}它。

Text.en: I <i>{am} looking for a place in this world... but I'm not sure this is it. Still, share your tale.
Text.zh: 我<i>{确实}在寻找安身之所……我不确定这是不是。不过，你的故事但说无妨。

Text.en: My home is littered with the rusted consequences of such hubris.
Text.zh: 我的家园到处都是这种狂妄自大的锈蚀后果。

Text.en: I'm already pledged to the Spirit, but we should work together.
Text.zh: 我已经向万灵宣誓了，但我们应该携手合作。

Text.en: My allegiance lies <i>{fully} elsewhere.
Text.zh: 我的忠诚<i>{全然}另属他处。

Text.en: It's a cult, mate. It's definitely a cult. But... cults can be fun.
Text.zh: 这是个邪教，伙计。绝对是个邪教。不过……邪教也可能很有趣。

Text.en: Lot o' fancy words. Not sure they mean much.
Text.zh: 漂亮话倒是不少。只怕没多少分量。

Text.en: You need not decide immediately, of course. <continue>Dine with us. Come to know us. <continue>We always say: you arrive a stranger on Arastas, but remain as a friend. <continue>The pledge can come later. For now, how about tithing?
Text.zh: 当然，你不必立即做决定。<continue>与我们共进晚餐。了解我们。<continue>我们常说：你作为陌生人来到阿拉斯塔，但会作为朋友留下。<continue>宣誓可以稍后再说。那么现在，不如请你慷慨解囊吧？

Text.en: Tithing is a noble action indeed. Your support would be much appreciated.
Text.zh: 奉纳确实是高尚的行为。我们会非常感激你的支持。

Text.en: What would I be supporting with this tithe?
Text.zh: 我奉纳的税金会支持什么？

Text.en: Oh look. Now he wants coin.
Text.zh: 哦瞧。现在他开始要钱了。

Text.en: You want me to pay you? For what?
Text.zh: 你要我付钱给你？为了什么？

Text.en: You just have to have <i>{something}, don't you?
Text.zh: 就是非得要点<i>{什么}，对吧？

Text.en: I might have a spare coin or two... wait, what even is it you're doing?
Text.zh: 我可能有一两个闲钱……等等，你们到底在搞什么？

Text.en: I hesitate to invest in your ideas. I am not even sure what they are.
Text.zh: 我对投资你们的理念有所顾虑。我甚至不确定那些理念是什么。

Text.en: Are you kiddin' me?
Text.zh: 你在跟我开玩笑吗？

Text.en: Ah, there it is. The coin.
Text.zh: 这就来了，开始要钱了。

Text.en: All in service of Utopia.
Text.zh: 一切都是为了乌托邦。

Text.en: We will gladly educate you about our cause.
Text.zh: 我们很乐意让你了解我们的事业。

Text.en: Lorandis, our missionary, will happily fill you in. I'm sure he'll visit Kingsmarch soon enough.
Text.zh: 我们的传教士洛兰迪斯会很高兴为你详细介绍。我确信他很快就会造访君锋镇。

Text.en: You should seek the counsel of our missionary, Lorandis, once more.
Text.zh: 你应该再次寻求我们传教士洛兰迪斯的指导。

Text.en: He can speak to the details of our plight better than I.
Text.zh: 他比我更能详细说明我们困境的细节。

Text.en: I can take your pledge, or a tithing. <continue>No more, no less.
Text.zh: 我可以接受你的宣誓，或税金。<continue>不多，不少。

Text.en: I'd just like to hear <i>{you} speak more about this place.
Text.zh: 我只是想听<i>{你}多聊聊这个地方。

Text.en: Come now... cat got your tongue?
Text.zh: 来吧……猫把你舌头叼走了？

Text.en: You can tell me nothing substantial?
Text.zh: 你不能告诉我任何有价值的东西？

Text.en: You don't like questions, do you?
Text.zh: 你不喜欢被问问题，对吧？

Text.en: What's wrong? You can't tell me nothing?
Text.zh: 怎么了？你什么都不能告诉我？

Text.en: You fear something. I can sense it.
Text.zh: 你在恐惧什么。我能感觉得到。

Text.en: What's going on? What you worried about?
Text.zh: 怎么回事？你在担心什么？

Text.en: So that's it? Nothin' more ye can say?
Text.zh: 就这些？没别的可说了？

Text.en: A servitor knows his place. Utopia awaits!
Text.zh: 仆从明白自己的职责所在。乌托邦在等待着！

Text.en: The Karui are strong, but their youth are thirsting for change. <continue>The Way meant survival in the old world. It will not be needed in Utopia.
Text.zh: 卡鲁人很强大，但他们的年轻人渴望改变。<continue>传统之道在旧世界意味着生存。在乌托邦中将不再需要它。

Text.en: [DNT]
Text.zh: 长老们对此不会高兴的。

Text.en: Well, they never are.
Text.zh: 嗯，他们从来就没高兴过。

Text.en: [DNT]
Text.zh: 哈！你说得一针见血。

Text.en: You need not waste my time, covenite. I know you don't truly intend to listen.
Text.zh: 你不用在我身上浪费时间，巫会中人。我知道你根本没打算真心倾听。

Text.en: What gave it away?
Text.zh: 何以见得？

Text.en: Whether you appreciate it or not, we're building Utopia for you, too. <continue>We will lift your pain, and absolve that which drives you to dark magics.
Text.zh: 无论你是否领情，我们也在为你建造乌托邦。<continue>我们将减轻你的痛苦，并赦免驱使你使用黑暗魔法的根源。

Text.en: I like my pain, thank you. It's what drives me ever forward, a relentless whip at my heels.
Text.zh: 多谢，但我喜欢我的痛苦。它驱使我永不停歇地前进，像无情的鞭子抽打着我的后背。

Text.en: I see. You have no home, no friends, not even a pet - not a living one, anyway. <continue>You fear you would be nothing, if not for your ambition.
Text.zh: 我明白了。你没有家，没有朋友，甚至没有宠物——至少没有活着的。<continue>你担心如果没有野心，你就什么都不是了。

Text.en: Shut your mouth, old man, before I rip it out.
Text.zh: 住口，老头，否则我就撕烂你的嘴。

Text.en: The Maraketh are a vibrant success story. <continue>Harsh traditions kept them alive for eons in a brutal world. <continue>You won't need to be so militant in our Utopia.
Text.zh: 马拉克斯人是个充满活力的成功典范。<continue>严酷的传统让他们在残酷的世界中生存了无数个世纪。<continue>在我们的乌托邦中，你不需要如此好斗。

Text.en: What if I <i>{prefer} being 'militant?'
Text.zh: 要是我<i>{偏偏喜欢}“好斗”怎么办？

Text.en: Oh... well... you don't have to stay in Utopia. I suppose you could always sail to Kalguur. <continue>Perhaps their way of life would suit you better.
Text.zh: 哦……嗯……你不必待在乌托邦里。我想你总是可以航行到卡古兰去。<continue>也许他们的生活方式更适合你。

Text.en: That's it? You're not going to try to convince me?
Text.zh: 就这样？你不打算试图说服我？

Text.en: Young lady, I'm not a preacher. More of a... realist idealist. <continue>If Utopia doesn't work for you, you're not a prisoner.
Text.zh: 年轻女士，我不是传教士。更像是个……现实主义的理想家。<continue>如果乌托邦不适合你，你又不是囚犯。

Text.en: I suppose that's fair.
Text.zh: 我觉得这挺公平的。

Text.en: There are not many Ezomytes joining us. Most won't even listen. <continue>What is it that compels them to resist our message?
Text.zh: 没有多少艾兹麦人投靠我们。大多数人甚至不愿意听。<continue>是什么让他们抗拒我们的信息？

Text.en: We don't like being told what to do.
Text.zh: 我们不喜欢被别人指手画脚。

Text.en: Hahah! You'll never meet anyone more irrepressible.
Text.zh: 哈哈！你绝找不到比他们更桀骜不驯之人了。

Text.en: Ah, the spirit of rebellion burns strong in Ezomyte blood. <continue>We could use that in the new world... just not pointed in our direction.
Text.zh: 啊，反叛的精神在艾兹麦人的血管中熊熊燃烧。<continue>我们在新世界中可以利用这一点……只是别指向我们的方向。

Text.en: It strikes me, you know... the Azmeri really have no need of our teachings.
Text.zh: 这让我很震惊，你知道……阿兹莫里人真的不需要我们的教导。

Text.en: What are you sayin' to me right now?
Text.zh: 你这是在说什么话？

Text.en: You already live in a sort of Utopia. In harmony. What else could we possibly teach you?
Text.zh: 你们已经生活在某种乌托邦中。和谐融洽。我们还能教给你们什么呢？

Text.en: Oh. Unexpected. True enough, though. I wish I could go home.
Text.zh: 哦。出人意料。不过确实如此。我希望我能回家。

Text.en: You can't go home? Perhaps you could find a place here, with us.
Text.zh: 你回不了家？也许你可以留在这里，在我们之中找到一席之地。

Text.en: We'll see.
Text.zh: 我们走着瞧吧。

Text.en: Trarthans are the lowest scum this world has to offer. <continue>The throw-aways of Wraeclast. Cast in the image of crassness and treachery.
Text.zh: 特拉特斯人是这个世界上最为低劣的渣滓。<continue>瓦尔克拉斯的弃儿。以粗鄙和背叛的形象塑造而成。

Text.en: Watch it. I'm Trarthan.
Text.zh: 说话小心点。我就是特拉特斯人。

Text.en: Or so I've heard, from the Eternal Empire's histories. <continue>Not my opinions. Just from the scholars of the time.
Text.zh: 这都是我从永恒帝国的史料中看来的。<continue>不是我个人的观点。只是来自当时学者们的记述。

Text.en: Ehh I'm just gettin' your goat! You're spot on. <continue>We're a bunch of arseholes, and proud of it!
Text.zh: 呃，我只是在逗你玩！你说得对极了。<continue>我们就是一群混球，而且以此为荣！

Text.en: You bear an odd mix of tattoos. <continue>A few Karui, most Ezomyte, one or two Oriathan slave marks. <continue>What is your tale, I wonder?
Text.zh: 你身上混杂的文身很独特。<continue>一些是卡鲁的，大部分是艾兹麦的，还有一两个奥瑞亚的奴隶印记。<continue>你究竟经历过什么？

Text.en: I wish I knew. I was found on the shores of Ogham by my father.
Text.zh: 我要是知道就好了。我父亲在欧甘的海岸边捡到了我。

Text.en: Ahh... there were many shipwrecks and tragedies when Oriath fell. <continue>It's possible your ancestry is lost to time. <continue>Supposedly, the Karui can speak to their ancestors. Perhaps you should ask them.
Text.zh: 啊……当奥瑞亚陷落时，发生了许多海难和悲剧。<continue>你的血统可能已经湮没在时间的长河中了。<continue>据说，卡鲁人可以与他们的祖先对话。也许你应该去问问他们。

Text.en: I might just do that... if they'll listen.
Text.zh: 我可能真的会去……如果他们肯听的话。

Text.en: Rumours abound of what befell the Druids of Skothe. <continue>Some say slaughtered, others... that they linger in the shadows, now beholden to some darker purpose.  <continue>Tell me, wild one - which tale is true?
Text.zh: 关于斯科提的德鲁伊们的遭遇众说纷纭。<continue>有人说他们惨遭屠戮，也有人说...他们仍在暗影中徘徊，如今侍奉着某种更黑暗的存在。<continue>告诉我，荒野之子——哪个传说才是真的？

Text.en: Ye'd have to ask someone who remembers - if any yet live.
Text.zh: 这你得去问记得的人——如果他们还活着的话。

Text.en: And you? No memories at all?
Text.zh: 你呢？一点都不记得了？

Text.en: Nothin' I care to speak of.  <continue>The Druids are gone. That's all there is to say.
Text.zh: 没什么好说的。<continue>德鲁伊已经消失了。这就是全部。

Text.en: Pain leaves scars, wild one - but scars are not the end of you. <continue>Devote yourself to the Order, and you may yet find healing.
Text.zh: 痛苦会留下伤疤，荒野之子——但伤疤不会永远跟随你。<continue>若你愿献身暮光会，或许终能寻得治愈。

Text.en: Rumours abound of what befell the Druids of Skothe. <continue>Some say slaughtered, others... that they linger in the shadows, now beholden to some darker purpose. <continue>Tell me, wild one - which tale is true?
Text.zh: 关于斯科提的德鲁伊们的遭遇众说纷纭。<continue>有人说他们惨遭屠戮，也有人说...他们仍在暗影中徘徊，如今侍奉着某种更黑暗的存在。<continue>告诉我，荒野之子——哪个传说才是真的？

Text.en: Ye'd have to ask someone who remembers - if any yet live.
Text.zh: 这你得去问记得的人——如果他们还活着的话。

Text.en: And you? No memories at all?
Text.zh: 你呢？一点都不记得了？

Text.en:
Only fragments... fog and fear and screamin' in the dark.   
<continue>Bah. The Druids are gone. That's all there is to say.
Text.zh:
只有些记忆碎片……浓雾、恐惧、还有黑暗中的惨叫。
<continue>呸。德鲁伊已经没了，没什么好说的了。

Text.en: Pain leaves scars, wild one - but scars are not the end of you. <continue>Devote yourself to the Order, and you may yet find healing.
Text.zh: 痛苦会留下伤疤，荒野之子——但伤疤不会永远跟随你。<continue>若你愿献身暮光会，或许终能寻得治愈。

Text.en: What possessed you to break our ancestral binding? You have released <i>{Tekaru,} the Deceiver! <continue>Now, he's free to wreak havoc on Wraeclast once more. I should take your head where you stand!
Text.zh: 你怎么敢打破我们先祖的封印？你放出了<i>{特卡鲁}，那个狡徒！<continue>现在他又能在瓦尔克拉斯上作恶了。我真该当场砍了你的脑袋！

Text.en: You will do no such thing! How were we to know? I didn't even know about that prison, or what was held there!
Text.zh: 你不能那么做！我们怎么可能知道？我根本不知道有那个监狱，更不知道里面关着什么！

Text.en: It was the concern of Chieftains and the <i>{hatungo,} not those who reject the Way!
Text.zh: 这是酋长和<i>{哈图纳格斯}该管的事，不是你们这些背叛卡鲁之道的人！

Text.en: The typical arrogance of authority. You must be the local Chieftain.
Text.zh: 权威者典型的傲慢。你就是当地酋长吧。

Text.en: It was? Says who, then? You?
Text.zh: 是吗？谁说的？你吗？

Text.en: So, you're the Chieftain?
Text.zh: 所以，你就是酋长？

Text.en: Oh my... a Chieftain in the flesh. What remarkable size.
Text.zh: 哦，天哪……酋长亲自出马。真是威风凛凛。

Text.en: Honoured Chieftain, I take it?
Text.zh: 你就是受人敬仰的酋长，对吧？

Text.en: So... you must be the local Chieftain.
Text.zh: 所以……你就是当地的酋长。

Text.en: Oh, mate, mate. I had no idea I was talking to a Chieftain!
Text.zh: 哦，伙计，且慢。我不知道自己在跟酋长说话！

Text.en: Chieftain, eh? That'd be you, then?
Text.zh: 酋长？这么说就是你咯？

Text.en: I am not your <i>{mate}. But...
Text.zh: 我不是什么<i>{伙计}。不过……

Text.en: Yes. I am Tavakai, Chieftain of the Tukohama Tribe. These waters belong to the Karui. <continue>You, along with the... <i>{Kalguurans}... are our guests. And your welcome is already wearing thin.
Text.zh: 没错。我是塔瓦凯，图克哈玛部落的酋长。这片水域属于卡鲁人。<continue>你，还有那些……<i>{卡古兰人}……都是我们的客人。而你们已经快不受欢迎了。

Text.en: What's done is done. I made the choice. That's on me.
Text.zh: 事情已经发生了。是我做的决定。责任在我。

Text.en: I am sorry, Chieftain. I had no idea.
Text.zh: 对不起，酋长。我真不知道。

Text.en: Fate led me there, it was not a choice of my own.
Text.zh: 是命运把我带到那里的，不是我自己的选择。

Text.en: I knew something was wrong from the start. I just wanted to see what would happen.
Text.zh: 我从一开始就觉得不对劲。我只是想看看会发生什么。

Text.en: This must be made right. I will find and slay this <i>{Tekaru.}
Text.zh: 这事必须解决。我会找到并解决这个<i>{特卡鲁}。

Text.en: The Deceiver fooled us. There was no way to know.
Text.zh: 是那狡徒蒙蔽了我们。压根儿没法预料。

Text.en: I just go where I'm pointed. Look, not to throw little Tujen under the boat, but...
Text.zh: 我只是听指挥行事。听着，我不是想把小图金往坑里推，但是……

Text.en: Had I known it was the Raven Trickster's prison, I wouldnae have done what I did.
Text.zh: 早知这是黑鸦欺诈者的监牢，我断不会铸下此等大错。

Text.en: You should have been wary. And...
Text.zh: 你本该保持警惕，况且……

Text.en: What stupidity falls from your mouth?
Text.zh: 你在说什么胡话？

Text.en: It is too late for that! Besides...
Text.zh: 现在为时已晚！况且…

Text.en: What? Have you hit your head?
Text.zh: 什么？你脑袋被撞了吗？

Text.en: You're damn right it's on you.
Text.zh: 你说得对，这当然该你负责。

Text.en: It is too late to be sorry.
Text.zh: 现在道歉已经太迟了。

Text.en: No excuse. He's a drunk. Not to be trusted! Besides...
Text.zh: 别找借口。他就是个酒鬼，根本不值得相信！况且…

Text.en: Yet you did it all the same. Now we all bear the cost.
Text.zh: 但你终究做了。如今这苦果，要我们所有人承担。

Text.en: If you weren't trespassing on our shores, it would never have happened! <continue>Consider this your first and last warning, <i>{pakarui.}
Text.zh: 要不是你们闯入我们的领海，这种事根本不会发生！<continue>这是对你的第一次也是最后一次警告，<i>{帕卡鲁}。

Text.en: If you weren't trespassing on our shores, it would never have happened! <continue>Consider this your first and last warning.
Text.zh: 要不是你们闯入我们的领海，这种事根本不会发生！<continue>这是对你的第一次也是最后一次警告。

Text.en: Yes. You are all <i>{guests} here on these waters. Waters that have belonged to us since the Beginning of Time. <continue>Our <i>{Way} is our identity. It is beyond life and death. The Way gave us the strength to endure all the horrors of Wraeclast. <continue>Corruption. Disease. Slavers... Ugh. Never again.
Text.zh: 确实如此。你们在这片海域都只是<i>{访客}。这片从初诞之时起就归属我们的海域。<continue>我们的<i>{卡鲁之道}便是我们的身份象征。它凌驾于生死之上。卡鲁之道赋予我们力量，让我们度过了瓦尔克拉斯的种种浩劫。<continue>腐化。瘟疫。人贩子……够了。永远不会再有了。

Text.en: I grew up surrounded by that lesson. Survival of the fittest.
Text.zh: 我从小就在这样的教诲中长大。弱肉强食。

Text.en: My tribe survived by living in harmony... but now I'm on me own, with only me own strength.
Text.zh: 我的部落靠着和睦相处才得以存续……但如今我孤身一人，只能依靠自己的力量。

Text.en: We are promised a better world. But for now, it appears violence is the only answer.
Text.zh: 众神承诺我们大同世界，然以当世观之，暴力确乎是唯一的解决之道。

Text.en: Beyond life and death? Haha! Hmm... I suppose belief is a kind of strength.
Text.zh: 凌驾生死？哈哈！嗯……我想信仰也算是一种力量吧。

Text.en: The Maraketh have endured many of the same woes. All that truly matters is strength. What else ensures survival?
Text.zh: 马拉克斯人也经历过许多相同的磨难。归根结底，唯有力量才关键。除此之外，还有什么能确保生存？

Text.en: I understand. As a child, my back was branded by foul hands. <continue>Some of my earliest memories are of pain. I vowed never to be weak again.
Text.zh: 我能体会。幼时，我的脊背被邪恶之手烙下印记。<continue>我最初的记忆就是痛苦。我立誓再不做弱者。

Text.en: I can respect that. No one should kneel to the weak.
Text.zh: 我认同这点。任何人都不该向弱者屈膝。

Text.en: Folk are naught without a code to live by. I respect it.
Text.zh: 人活着若没有准则，就毫无价值。我敬重这种态度。

Text.en: Then my words are not wasted on you. Now... it is your choice, and you choose to be strong!
Text.zh: 那我对你说的话也不算白费。现在……选择权在你，而你选择了变强！

Text.en: It can take many forms, yes...
Text.zh: 力量有着诸多种形态，确实……

Text.en: Nothing does! The Maraketh know this!
Text.zh: 什么都不屈从弱者！马拉克斯人应该最明白这一点！

Text.en: We are promised nothing. We must make the world better for ourselves... with our own hands.
Text.zh: 从来没有天赐的承诺。我们必须亲手打造更美好的世界……

Text.en: It is not enough to just survive! Why merely settle for that?
Text.zh: 仅仅生存还远远不够！怎能满足于区区生存？

Text.en: Return to them! You belong to your tribe, as they do to you. <continue>The strength of the individual is nothing against the tide of many...
Text.zh: 回到族人中去！你属于部族，部族也属于你。<continue>个人的力量在部族的浪潮面前微不足道……

Text.en: Yes... I'd sooner join the Mother of Death's embrace!
Text.zh: 要让我屈膝，我宁可投入死亡之母的怀抱！

Text.en: Respect is a beginning. But the code must be lived, every day - or it is nothing.
Text.zh: 敬重只是开端。准则需日日践行——否则终成空谈。

Text.en: Strength is the key! And yet here we are... <continue>Slowly but surely, outsiders forsake us. The Vaal, the Eternal Empire, and now... the Kalguurans. <continue>I thought their leader, Dannig, was true of heart... until he allowed the harvest of our sacred whale! And it was all downhill from there... <continue>Twenty years ago, we allowed only four Kalguurans to stay here... and now look at this place. Ugh.
Text.zh: 没错！力量就是一切！但我们却落得这般田地……<continue>外来者一个接一个地抛弃了我们，缓慢而又无情。瓦尔人，永恒帝国，还有现在……卡古兰人。<continue>我曾认为丹宁怀有真心，直到他默许猎杀我们的圣鲸，从那以后，情况就彻底失控了……<continue>二十年前，我们只允许四个卡古尔人留在此地……现在瞧瞧这片土地变成了什么样！真是恶心。

Text.en: It began with our remote tribes. They would disappear from their islands. Gone without a trace. <continue>For generations, the Eternal Empire... and the bastards of Oriath... raided the weakest of our people and took them as slaves. <continue>But where are those slavers now? Dead! And gone!
Text.zh: 这始于我们边远的部落。他们会从岛屿上神秘消失。不留任何痕迹地消失。<continue>世世代代，永恒帝国……还有奥瑞亚的那些恶棍……袭击我们最弱小的族人，将他们掳为奴隶。<continue>但那些奴隶贩子如今安在？全死了！全消失了！

Text.en: Even in Ogham, the Ezomytes fought to throw off the Empire's boot.
Text.zh: 就连在欧甘，艾兹麦人也起来反抗帝国的压迫。

Text.en: It's a shame the Eternals were descended from the mountainborn. They should have never left their mountain. It were the wrong path.
Text.zh: 遗憾的是永恒帝国源自山民血脉。他们本不该离开山岭。那是条歧路。

Text.en: History erases us all, eventually.
Text.zh: 历史终将埋葬我们所有人。

Text.en: Dead's not always gone, but I see your point.
Text.zh: 死去不等于消逝，但我理解你的观点。

Text.en: As they deserve. I despise this part of humanity. Past and present.
Text.zh: 他们罪有应得。我厌恶人性的这一面，无论过去还是现在。

Text.en: Good riddance.
Text.zh: 好死不如赖活着。

Text.en: And yet Trarthus lives on, long after. To hell with them Eternal bastards!
Text.zh: 可特拉特斯却延续至今，远超他们的存在。那些永恒帝国的混账去死吧！

Text.en: A fate well-earned.
Text.zh: 咎由自取。

Text.en: Long... may they burn.
Text.zh: 我诅咒他们……万劫不复。

Text.en: Your dark tongue does not humour me.
Text.zh: 你的巫术黑话可不怎么好笑。

Text.en: Yes! A thousand times over!
Text.zh: 没错！千倍奉还！

Text.en: So, we should care about nothing? Be subjugated? Live in fear? <continue>The beauty of freedom is the essence of life.
Text.zh: 所以我们就该冷漠无情？甘受压迫？活在恐惧之中？<continue>自由才是生命的精髓。

Text.en: And look where the Empire is now...
Text.zh: 看看帝国如今的下场……

Text.en: The Azmeri are not to blame for their descendants' mistakes... <continue>They must only answer for themselves.
Text.zh: 山民不该为后裔的罪行担责…<continue>他们只需为自己负责。

Text.en: Yes! The only good Eternal bastard... is a dead one!
Text.zh: 说得好！永恒帝国的孽种，通通都该死！

Text.en: So it was. And may their spirits find no peace.
Text.zh: 确实如此。愿他们的灵魂永世不得安宁。

Text.en: Now, we must rise, stronger than ever! We will not stray again from the old Way, the true Way. The Way that carried us through history. <continue>We must be strong for the world to see, so that <i>{we} are the masters of our own destiny!
Text.zh: 如今，我们必须重新崛起，变得前所未有的强大！我们绝不会再偏离古老的卡鲁之道，那条真正的道路。那条引领我们穿越历史的道路。<continue>我们必须向世人彰显我们的强大，这样<i>{我们}才能成为自己命运的主宰！

Text.en: Long ago, Tukohama put axes in our hands and taught us to fight. <i>{That} is the most important lesson of our Way. We were nothing before we were given strength.
Text.zh: 远古时代，图克哈玛将战斧置于我们手中，传授我们战斗之道。<i>{这}是我们卡鲁之道中最核心的教义。在获得力量之前，我们一无所有。

Text.en: It makes sense to me.
Text.zh: 这在我看来很有道理。

Text.en: This 'Tukohama'... I like the sound of him.
Text.zh: 这个“图克哈玛”……我很欣赏他的理念。

Text.en: You believe you couldn't fight before Tukohama taught you? Curious.
Text.zh: 你们真的相信在图克哈玛教导之前无法战斗？令人好奇。

Text.en: Tukohama is dead, isn't he?
Text.zh: 图克哈玛不是已经逝世了吗？

Text.en: Strength is vital to overcoming our own weaknesses. It is true.
Text.zh: 力量是克服自身弱点的关键，千真万确。

Text.en: Tukohama seems to have taught you well.
Text.zh: 图克哈玛似乎是位出色的导师。

Text.en: Yeah, mate. Strong is good. Agreed.
Text.zh: 对的，伙计。强者为尊。我赞同。

Text.en: Strength's well and good... provided it's used to protect, not to conquer.
Text.zh: 力量固然可贵……但须用于守护，而非征伐。

Text.en: Show some respect, will you?
Text.zh: 请展现一些敬意，好吗？

Text.en: Could someone think before they had a brain? It is obvious.
Text.zh: 一个人在拥有大脑前能够思考吗？这显而易见。

Text.en: Protection means nothing if you lack the will to do what is necessary.
Text.zh: 若没有行必行之事的决心，所谓守护不过空谈。

Text.en: Had we been united in our strength, perhaps history would have told a different tale. <continue>Now, the tribes must stand together and put the Karui first. <continue>We will always have neighbours, but they must acknowledge our strength. And one way, or another... they shall.
Text.zh: 若我们能团结所有力量，或许历史会书写不同的篇章。<continue>现在，各部落必须携手并进，将卡鲁部落置于首位。<continue>我们永远都会有邻居，但他们必须认可我们的实力。无论用什么方式……他们都会认可的。

Text.en: Tukohama tasked his tribe—<i>{my} tribe—with keeping that Deceiver in his prison. <continue>Our sacred duty kept us in this region, even though we longed for the sea... but it is good that we stayed.
Text.zh: 图克哈玛委托他的部落——<i>{我的}部落——看守那个狡徒的囚笼。<continue>我们的神圣使命将我们束缚在这片区域，尽管我们向往大海……但是，留下来是明智的选择。

Text.en: It was foolish to not have further protections.
Text.zh: 没有更多防护措施真是愚蠢。

Text.en: I feel guilty for my trespass and for doing what I did. But I didn't know.
Text.zh: 我为自己的入侵和所作所为深感愧疚。但我当时并不知情。

Text.en: Ah, so it was your job to keep him there.
Text.zh: 啊，原来看守他是你们的职责。

Text.en: Chieftain I must say... it seemed a little too easy to set him free...
Text.zh: 酋长大人，恕我直言……你们释放他似乎太过儿戏了……

Text.en: Such a powerful being, left out in the open? I cannot comprehend how this has happened.
Text.zh: 如此强大的存在，就这样逍遥在外？我不明白怎么会发生这种事。

Text.en: Why was he not guarded further?
Text.zh: 为何没有更严密的看守？

Text.en: I've seen better security in a Korathin pleasure den.
Text.zh: 我在柯拉辛娱乐场见过更严密的防护。

Text.en: I've long known of the Raven Trickster's deceit. I was a damn fool to free him.
Text.zh: 我早知黑鸦欺诈者奸诈成性。竟亲手释放了他，真是蠢笨至极。

Text.en: Best you mind your mouth.
Text.zh: 你说话放尊重点。

Text.en: That is no excuse.
Text.zh: 这不是借口。

Text.en: Yes. It is what has kept our people here for so many generations.
Text.zh: 正是。这是我们部族世代留守于此的原因。

Text.en: All thanks to you and these damn Kalguurans...
Text.zh: 这全都是拜你和那些该死的卡古兰人所赐……

Text.en: Capturing him was a miracle in itself... it was then impossible to move him.
Text.zh: 能抓住他已经是奇迹了……根本没办法转移他。

Text.en: That binding... had stood the test of time.
Text.zh: 束缚法阵……原本经受住了时间考验。

Text.en: I don't want to know what that means...
Text.zh: 我不想听你的花花故事……

Text.en: Yes. You were.
Text.zh: 对，你确实是。

Text.en: We tried posting warriors to keep watch over the prison. He'd drive them mad. They'd throw themselves into the sea. <continue>Instead, we allowed his fog to wreak its havoc and in turn... keep him hidden. <continue>Besides... I had no idea Kalguuran runes could undo our ancestral binding. Yet another reason for us to not get along.
Text.zh: 我们曾派遣战士轮流看守牢笼。但他会驱使战士发狂，使其不由自主、投海自尽。<continue>于是，我们任由他的迷雾肆虐，反而……让他保持隐匿。<continue>再说……我根本不知道卡古兰符文能够破解我们先祖的封印。这又是我们关系紧张的一个原因。

Text.en: As I've said... what's done is done.
Text.zh: 正如我所言……事已至此。

Text.en: It's remarkable you kept him there this whole time.
Text.zh: 能将他囚禁如此之久已经很了不起了。

Text.en: In time, he will not be of any concern.
Text.zh: 终有一日，他将不再构成任何威胁。

Text.en: Well, can't do much about it now, can we?
Text.zh: 好吧，现在我们也无力回天了，不是吗？

Text.en: Please forgive me. It is a regrettable situation. I did not mean harm.
Text.zh: 请原谅我，这确实是令人遗憾的局面。我绝非有意造成伤害。

Text.en: It was an unfortunate series of mistakes.
Text.zh: 这是一连串不幸的失误。

Text.en: Well, least you lot are free of worrying if he's still there, I guess.
Text.zh: 至少你们不必再担心他是否还被关押着了，我想。

Text.en: Mistake's been made. Naught we can do now but move forward.
Text.zh: 大错既已铸成。如今唯能向前。

Text.en: Mmmph.
Text.zh: 哼。

Text.en: For untold generations, he slept soundly in that prison. <continue>But the Deceiver woke from his slumber twenty years ago, and has been causing more and more problems ever since. <continue>The fog... crashing ships... foul dreams... cursed voices... <continue>Even one of our Elders fell for his lies, and went missing. <continue>But it will be much worse now that the Deceiver is free, thanks to you.
Text.zh: 无数世代以来，他在那座牢笼中酣然沉睡。<continue>可这个狡徒在二十年前醒了过来，从那以后就麻烦不断。<continue>海上浓雾……船只失事……噩梦缠身……诅咒低语……<continue>连我们的一位长老都被他的谎言蒙骗，就此失踪。<continue>现在狡徒重获自由，全是拜你所赐，后果将更加严重。

Text.en: For untold generations, he slept soundly in that prison. <continue>But the Deceiver woke from his slumber twenty years ago, and has been causing more and more problems ever since. <continue>The fog... crashing ships... foul dreams... cursed voices... <continue>Even Matiki, the Elder you found, was deceived and led astray by Tekaru's lies. <continue>It will be much worse now that the Deceiver is free, thanks to you.
Text.zh: 无数世代以来，他在那座牢笼中酣然沉睡。<continue>可这个狡徒在二十年前醒了过来，从那以后就麻烦不断。<continue>海上浓雾……船只失事……噩梦缠身……诅咒低语……<continue>连你救回的长老玛提吉都被特卡鲁的谎言蒙骗，误入歧途。<continue>现在狡徒重获自由，后果将更加严重。这全是拜你所赐！

Text.en: It is... disappointing... to see our young abandoning the Way and becoming... what? <continue>Workhands? For the Kalguurans? It is ridiculous! She could be so much more!
Text.zh: 看着我们的年轻人背弃卡鲁之道，沦为……什么？<continue>苦力？还是给卡古兰人？简直荒唐！她本来能有更大作为的！

Text.en: <i>{I} find her rather impressive. You should give her a chance.
Text.zh: <i>{我}觉得她挺厉害的。你该给她个机会。

Text.en: Makoru is capable and skilled. I don't see the problem, meself.
Text.zh: 马克鲁既能干又有本事。我看不出有什么问题。

Text.en: Ah, but if only we had such clear insight for ourselves, the way we do for others.
Text.zh: 要是反思自己时也像旁观者看得那么清楚就好了。

Text.en: I think she's doing just fine. Makoru's the least intolerable person I've met here.
Text.zh: 我觉得她表现不错。马克鲁是我在这里遇到的最不讨厌的人了。

Text.en: But Chieftain, with respect, who are you to judge her path?
Text.zh: 可是酋长，恕我直言，你凭什么评判她的选择？

Text.en: She will make a name for herself, in her own way.
Text.zh: 她会走出自己的路，闯出名堂的。

Text.en: Hey, don't knock hired-hands. Trade's the bones of it all. Gets us soft beds, privies, and such. All the things that make life worth living.
Text.zh: 嘿，别瞧不起雇工。贸易是一切的根本。给我们带来软床、厕所这些好东西。所有让生活值得过下去的东西。

Text.en: She forges her own path. Is that not enough?
Text.zh: 她要走出自己的路，不行吗？

Text.en: I am her father! So I am more than qualified!
Text.zh: 我是她父亲！我比谁都更有资格！

Text.en: She is more than capable! Which is why she belongs with us!
Text.zh: 她不只是能干而已！所以她应该跟我们在一起！

Text.en: If that is all that makes your life worth living, then I pity you.
Text.zh: 如果那些东西是你人生的寄托，那我真为你感到悲哀。

Text.en: Enough? Who are you to say what is 'enough' for a daughter of the Karui?
Text.zh: 够了？你算什么人，也配评判卡鲁儿女该过什么样的人生？

Text.en: Ugh. The First Karui Captain of some outsider's fleet, is there really honour in that? I doubt it. <continue>Kanu rejected us to be a lowly '<i>{first mate}', as they call it. I know his father. He is deeply ashamed. <continue>The youth these days are more foolish than ever.
Text.zh: 哼。某个外族舰队的首位卡鲁船长，这真有什么荣誉可言？我看不出来。<continue>卡努抛弃了我们，去当一个低贱的“<i>{大副}”，他们这么叫的。我认识他父亲。他深感耻辱。<continue>现在的年轻人比以往任何时候都要愚蠢。

Text.en: Kanu was always headstrong. Loud and disrespectful... all the makings of a great warrior! <continue>He threw it all away to work for the Kalguurans. <continue>We have lost many to foreign causes. That's why Ngakanu is now off limits to outsiders.
Text.zh: 卡努一直很倔强。吵闹又无礼……这些都是成为伟大战士的品质！<continue>结果他把这一切都抛弃了，去为卡古兰人打工。<continue>我们已经失去太多人去为外族卖命。所以纳玛鲁伊现在禁止外人进入。

Text.en: You can't control the young. It's wrong to try.
Text.zh: 你管不了年轻人。想管就是错的。

Text.en: The Azmeri welcome new ideas. Home cannot be a prison.
Text.zh: 阿兹莫里人欢迎新想法。家乡不能成为牢笼。

Text.en: False faith can often lead the youth astray.
Text.zh: 错误的信仰常常把年轻人引入歧途。

Text.en: Ideas can be more dangerous than any weapon.
Text.zh: 思想有时比任何武器都危险。

Text.en: I, too, chose to walk my own path. It really is not as easy as you think.
Text.zh: 我也选择了走自己的路。这比你想象的要难。

Text.en: Ah, I understand now.
Text.zh: 啊，我现在懂了。

Text.en: Gotta lock the young ones down, I say. They'll end up slaves... or worse.
Text.zh: 我觉得得把这些小家伙关起来。不然他们会沦为奴隶……或者更惨。

Text.en: The boy's no coward. There's strength in him still.
Text.zh: 这孩子不是懦夫。他骨子里仍透着韧性。

Text.en: Yes. I expected you would.
Text.zh: 对，你早就该懂了。

Text.en: Perhaps... but try defending yourself with an idea.
Text.zh: 或许吧……或许你该试试用思想御敌。

Text.en: Few choices are easy. But some... are right.
Text.zh: 选择从来就不好做。但有些事……就算再难，也要去做。

Text.en: Mm. They do not know, what they do not know.
Text.zh: 哼。他们不懂装懂，根本没有自知之明。

Text.en: It is not about control! It's about honour. Respect. Tradition.
Text.zh: 这不是控制的问题！这是荣誉的问题。尊重。传统。

Text.en: Perhaps that suits you, up on your mountain. But the sea moves quickly.
Text.zh: 在你们的群山上或许行得通，但大海上总是风云变幻。

Text.en: Yes. That is my fear. A history... we shall never relive.
Text.zh: 正是……这是我最忧心的。那段历史……我们绝不能重蹈覆辙。

Text.en: Strength he lends to the Kalguurans, not his own people.
Text.zh: 他把力量借给了卡古兰人，而非自己的族人。

Text.en: We cannot afford to lose more of our young to these <i>{dangerous} ideas...
Text.zh: 我们再也不能把年轻人拱手让给这些<i>{危险}思想了……

Text.en: He showed promise when he arrived on our shores. I saw the warrior within. <continue>But I will never forgive him for allowing the hunt of our sacred whale.
Text.zh: 他刚到我们海岸时很有潜力。我看出了他内心的战士本质。但我永远不会原谅他允许猎杀我们的圣鲸。

Text.en: Dannig has deeper respect for you than you give him credit. He tried to intervene but was too late.
Text.zh: 丹宁对你的尊重比你以为的要深。他曾经试图阻止，但已经来不及了。

Text.en: Aye, Dannig regrets that. But it weren't his fault. It were his bloody King who gave the order.
Text.zh: 没错，丹宁很后悔。但那不是他的错，是他那该死的国王下的命令。

Text.en: The Kalguuran was not to blame. He tried to stop it. You are too quick to judge.
Text.zh: 不能怪那个卡古兰人。他本来是想阻止的。你太急着下结论了。

Text.en: Oh, come now. The blacksmith was too late to stop that. If you'd really like, perhaps I can bring it back to life.
Text.zh: 拜托。那个铁匠来不及阻止。如果你真想的话，也许我能让它起死回生。

Text.en: I must tell you, Sir Dannig was not to blame. He wishes he could have stopped it.
Text.zh: 我必须说明，这不能怪丹宁。他也希望能够阻止那件事。

Text.en: Dannig has spoken to me of this. He tried to stop it... but he was too late. He regrets that it ever took place.
Text.zh: 丹宁跟我谈过这事。他想阻止……但来不及了。他对发生的事很后悔。

Text.en: It's not my place to care or say... but it wasn't his fault, mate. He tried to stop that from even happening.
Text.zh: 这事本不该我来管或评判……但确实不怪他，伙计。他曾经试图阻止那样的事情发生。

Text.en: He allowed no such thing. Dannig tried to stop it... but he was too late.
Text.zh: 他绝不容许这种事发生。丹宁曾试图阻止……可惜为时已晚。

Text.en: I... did not know that. Hmm. <continue>Either way, the Kalguuran monster that is Kingsmarch is too large for any one man to <i>{fully} control.
Text.zh: 我……我不知道这事。嗯。<continue>不管怎样，君锋镇这头卡古兰巨兽过于庞大，任何人都无法<i>{完全}驾驭。

Text.en: I am Tavakai, Chieftain of the Tukohama Tribe. These waters, and all the islands within, belong to the Karui. <continue>You and these... <i>{Kalguurans}... are our guests. I expect you to behave like it.
Text.zh: 我是塔瓦凯，图克哈玛部落的酋长。这片海域，以及其中的所有岛屿，都属于卡鲁。<continue>你和这些……<i>{卡古兰人}……算是我们的客人。我希望你们守规矩。

Text.en: I have no issue with your people, but I'll do what I like.
Text.zh: 我想做什么就做什么，我对你们的族人没意见。

Text.en: I shall treat your seas with the same respect as I do the land.
Text.zh: 我会一视同仁地尊重海洋和陆地。

Text.en: I bow to your request, Chieftain.
Text.zh: 我接受你的要求，酋长。

Text.en: Yes, tell one of the Coven to behave, see what that gets you...
Text.zh: 是啊，试着让巫会的人乖乖听话，看看你能得到什么……

Text.en: Of course, honoured Chieftain. The Karui are still considered allies where I come from.
Text.zh: 当然，尊敬的酋长。在我的家乡，卡鲁依然被视为可敬的盟友。

Text.en: I know my place here. But respect must be mutual.
Text.zh: 我会有分寸。但尊重是相互的。

Text.en: What? Really? Fair enough, boss.
Text.zh: 什么？真的？行吧，老大。

Text.en: Ye worry for naught, Chieftain. This old Druid means ye no harm.
Text.zh: 您多虑了，酋长。这位老德鲁伊对您没有恶意。

Text.en: I care not for your words, only your actions. I'll be watching.
Text.zh: 我不在乎你的废话，只看你的行动。我会盯着你。

Text.en: Well then... what is it that brings you here? <continue>You've clearly made yourself at home on our waters.
Text.zh: 那么……是什么风把你吹到这里的？<continue>你显然已经在我们的海域扎下根了。

Text.en: I don't know that it's any of your business, but I suppose we're on the same side. <continue>I'm looking for pieces of an ancient Weapon that can supposedly defeat Corruption.
Text.zh: 我不觉得这关你什么事，不过我想咱们算是一边的。<continue>我在找一件据说能够击败腐化的古代武器碎片。

Text.en: I seek an answer to the <i>{rathin}. To that end, I'm lookin' for pieces of an ancient Weapon.
Text.zh: 我在寻求<i>{腐化}的解答。为此，我在寻找一件古代武器的碎片。

Text.en: The Dreamer wills that I seek out pieces of an ancient Weapon against Corruption.
Text.zh: 幻梦者的意志指引我寻找对抗腐化的古代武器碎片。

Text.en: I'm just here on a lark, really. Or looking for parts of an ancient Weapon that can destroy Corruption. One of those two.
Text.zh: 我就是来逛逛的。或者说在找能够摧毁腐化的古代武器部件。二选一吧。

Text.en: I am on a mission to find and rebuild an ancient Weapon. A Weapon that may help us destroy Corruption.
Text.zh: 我的任务是寻找并重塑一件武器，一件或许能帮助我们摧毁腐化的古代武器。

Text.en: I seek pieces of an ancient Weapon against Corruption.
Text.zh: 我在寻找对抗腐化的古代武器碎片。

Text.en: I'm on a quest, boss. Looking for pieces of an ancient Weapon that I can use to take down Corruption.
Text.zh: 我在执行任务，老大。找一些能用来对付腐化的古代武器碎片。

Text.en: I seek an end to Corruption - and yer waters may hide the key. An ancient Weapon, to be precise.
Text.zh: 我为终结腐化而来——而您的水域中或许藏着关键。确切地说，是一把古代兵器。

Text.en: Hmm. Our islands have become more and more <i>{sickened} of late. <continue>Those who stray, fall ill with the blood fever, and are cast out to die alone. <continue>My <i>{hatungo} worries that the Beast has returned to the world once more.
Text.zh: 嗯。我们的岛屿最近变得越来越<i>{病态}。<continue>那些迷失的人会染上血热病，被驱逐出去，孤独死去。<continue>我的<i>{哈图纳格斯}在担心邪兽再次降临世间。

Text.en: Your <i>{hatungo} is right. I need that Weapon to drain and remove Corruption.
Text.zh: 你的<i>{哈图纳格斯}说得对。我需要那武器来吸取和清除腐化。

Text.en: Your <i>{hatungo} sure is wise. That Weapon can drain and remove <i>{rathin}. It's our only chance to destroy the Beast.
Text.zh: 你的<i>{哈图纳格斯}真是睿智。那武器能够吸取和清除<i>{腐化}。这是我们击败邪兽的唯一希望。

Text.en: It has returned, indeed. That is why humanity needs the Weapon - to drain and remove Corruption.
Text.zh: 它确实回来了。这正是人类需要那武器的原因——吸取和清除腐化。

Text.en: Oh, it <i>{has.} That's what the Weapon is for... to drain and remove Corruption.
Text.zh: 哦，它<i>{确实}回来了。这就是武器的用途……吸取和清除腐化。

Text.en: The Beast has indeed returned... and the Maraketh are on the move once again. I need the Weapon to drain and remove Corruption.
Text.zh: 邪兽确实归来了……马拉克斯人已经再次开始行动。我需要武器来吸取和清除腐化。

Text.en: It has. That's why we need the Weapon... to drain and remove Corruption.
Text.zh: 没错。这就是我们需要武器的原因……吸取和清除腐化。

Text.en: That's spot on. It's why I need the Weapon. To drain and remove Corruption.
Text.zh: 说得对。这就是我需要武器的原因。吸取和清除腐化。

Text.en: Aye - it <i>{has} returned. And only <i>{that} Weapon can drain and remove Corruption.
Text.zh: 正是——它<i>{已经}归来。唯有<i>{那柄}兵器能彻底净化腐化。

Text.en: This Weapon... it sounds like it could cure the blood fever. <continue>We believe it is caused by Corruption contaminating our very blood, so removing it may heal that sickness. <continue>Hmmm... a powerful Weapon indeed... <continue>I suppose I can allow you to continue your search, for now. <continue>We will not interfere with your passage on our waters... but you best remain respectful.
Text.zh: 这武器……听起来能治愈血热病。<continue>我们相信它是腐化污染了我们的血液所致，清除腐化或许能治好这病。<continue>嗯……确实是件强大的武器……<continue>我想我可以允许你继续搜寻，暂时如此。<continue>我们不会阻挠你在海域的通行……但你最好保持敬意。

Text.en: Well, it seems I owe you a debt of thanks. Old Matiki here says you were quite the hero.
Text.zh: 看来我得谢谢你了。老玛提吉说你表现得像个英雄。

Text.en: Welcome to Ngakanu. I am Tavakai, Chieftain of the Tukohama Tribe. These shores belong to the Karui.
Text.zh: 欢迎来到纳卡努。我是塔瓦凯，图克哈玛部落的酋长。这些海岸属于卡鲁。

Text.en: And I am Kaimana, Medicine Woman of the Tukohama Tribe!
Text.zh: 我是凯玛纳，图克哈玛部落的药师！

Text.en: And I am Matik... oh, yes we've met. Never mind.
Text.zh: 我是玛提吉……哦，对，咱们见过。当我没说。

Text.en: You are our guest here. And it seems we owe you a debt of thanks... Old Matiki here says you were quite the hero.
Text.zh: 你是我们的客人。看来我们得谢谢你……老玛提吉说你表现得像个英雄。

Text.en: It was a fight I won't soon forget! Tukohama himself would delight at what I saw you do back there!
Text.zh: 那场战斗我永远不会忘记！图克哈玛本人都会为我看到你的表现而高兴！

Text.en: The waters knew Matiki was alive, yes! I told you, Tavakai. <continue>Keep looking, I said! The old coot still breathes!
Text.zh: 海水知道玛提吉还活着，没错！我早就告诉过你，塔瓦凯。<continue>继续找，我说！这老家伙还在喘气呢！

Text.en: Hey now! I may be old... but I am wise.
Text.zh: 嘿！我可能老了……但我有智慧。

Text.en: Best not fool around offshore anymore, Matiki.
Text.zh: 玛提吉，你最好别再在海边瞎折腾了。

Text.en: The mountainborn look after their Elders too. It was my honour to return you home.
Text.zh: 山民也会照顾他们的长者。能把你平安送回家是我的荣幸。

Text.en: Perhaps you best stay here from now on, Elder.
Text.zh: 也许你从现在开始最好就待在这里，长者。

Text.en: Not wise enough it seems...
Text.zh: 看来还不够明智……

Text.en: Oh how the Tale-women back home would love you two...
Text.zh: 家乡的说书人会喜欢你们俩的……

Text.en: I'm pleased to have returned your wisdom to where it belongs.
Text.zh: 我很高兴能把你的智慧送回它该在的地方。

Text.en: Just look after yourself, old mate.
Text.zh: 照顾好自己，老伙计。

Text.en: Wisdom's no use to ye in a kinfolk cage. Best be more careful, next time.
Text.zh: 若被亲眷族囚禁，纵有智慧亦无用武之地。下次，最好谨慎些。

Text.en: For your good deed, I will allow you to come and go from this sacred hall as you please. <continue>But no further inland. The heart of the Tukohama tribe is for our people only. <continue>You have my thanks.
Text.zh: 为了你的善举，我允许你随意进出这座神圣大厅。<continue>但不能再往内陆去。图克哈玛部落的腹地只属于我们族人。<continue>多谢了。

Text.en: Hmm... how do you know of this place?
Text.zh: 嗯……你怎么知道这地方的？

Text.en: Oh! I must have mumbled a thing or two in my sleep.
Text.zh: 哦！我一定是在睡梦中嘀咕了几句。

Text.en: For a moment I thought you may have taken them there...
Text.zh: 有那么一瞬间，我还以为你可能带他们去过那里……

Text.en: That would be a first, wouldn't it?
Text.zh: 那可就是头一回了，不是吗？

Text.en: Is it an issue that he already–
Text.zh: 他已经……这有什么问题吗？

Text.en: I'd never seen anything like it. I'd love to go ba–
Text.zh: 我从没见过那样的景象。我很想再去……

Text.en: The sinkhole there there was quite rem–
Text.zh: 那里的天坑确实值得一提……

Text.en: What's wrong, old man? Don't want to tell him that we sailed-
Text.zh: 怎么了，老头？不想告诉他我开船去过……

Text.en: Huh? We did travel to this Eye of Hinek-
Text.zh: 嗯？我们确实去过那个什么之眼……

Text.en: But we did go th–
Text.zh: 但我们确实去过……

Text.en: Oh no. Definitely haven't been there. Certainly not. No place for outsiders.
Text.zh: 哦不。绝对没去过那里。当然没有。外人不该去的地方。

Text.en: That sinkhole... Never seen the like of it bef–
Text.zh: 那个天坑……我这辈子都没见过那种——

Text.en: Definitely would be a first. But that's mad. Unless of course, the Ancestors approved. Then, there'd be no problem at all.
Text.zh: 绝对是头一回。不过，那太疯狂了。当然，除非得到了先祖的认可。那样的话，完全没问题。

Text.en: I suppose so. Still, I don't think it's a good idea...
Text.zh: 我想也是。不过，我觉得这不是好主意……

Text.en: Exactly.
Text.zh: 确实如此。

Text.en: This has been home to the Tukohama Tribe for countless generations. <continue>Though we stayed for our solemn duty, we thrived because of our love of these shores.
Text.zh: 这里是图克哈玛部落世世代代的家园。<continue>虽然我们因神圣职责而留守，但我们因热爱这片海岸而繁荣。

Text.en: The tides wash ashore, bringing life and abundance. Yes! This is our home and our tribe is an ever growing family. <continue>Together, we thrive, until Tukohama welcomes us again into his embrace.
Text.zh: 潮汐冲刷着岸边，带来生机和富饶。对！这是我们的家园，我们的部落是个不断壮大的大家庭。<continue>我们共同繁荣，直到图克哈玛再次将我们拥入怀抱。

Text.en: I've never known a real <i>{family}. All I've known is isolation. I've learned to trust only myself.
Text.zh: 我从未体验过真正的<i>{家庭}。我只知道孤独。我学会了只相信自己。

Text.en: Oh, my child! That hurts my heart to hear. But I understand you. Yes! <continue>You know the pain of silence is nothing compared to the hurt of a misplaced word from one that we adore.
Text.zh: 哦，我的孩子！听到这话真让我心疼。但我理解你。是的！<continue>你知道沉默的痛苦远比不上心爱之人的一句错话带来的伤害。

Text.en: That... is true.
Text.zh: 那……确实如此。

Text.en: But closing off your heart and soul... from the world around you... that does more damage than any word can. For it is death in life. <continue>The beasts and the trees can only carry your soul so far. We need each other, child. We need connection.
Text.zh: 但封闭你的心灵和灵魂……与周围世界隔绝……这比任何言语都更具破坏性。因为这是活着的死亡。<continue>野兽和树木只能承载你的灵魂到一定程度。我们需要彼此，孩子。我们需要连接。

Text.en: I can't... I won't.
Text.zh: 我不能……我不会。

Text.en: You can. And one day, when you are ready... You will. I can see it in your eyes. <continue>When the time comes, don't be frightened. Yes! You <i>{will} heal. I promise you.
Text.zh: 你能做到。总有一天，当你准备好时……你会的。我从你眼中看得出来。<continue>当那时候到来，别害怕。对！你<i>{会}痊愈的。我向你保证。

Text.en: The mountainborn are much less forgiving of each other than the Karui appear to be.
Text.zh: 山民对待彼此，远没有卡鲁人看上去那样 宽容。

Text.en: Don't let our kindness blind you. There are limits. Boundaries we will maintain. <continue>The weak will not survive. And this is the brutal nature of life. We do not stand in its way.
Text.zh: 别让我们的善良蒙蔽了你。凡事都有底线。我们会坚守的界限。<continue>弱者无法生存。这就是生活的残酷本质。我们不会阻挡它的道路。

Text.en: Tukohama showed us strength. Strength <i>{is} a kindness. Showing weakness does nothing for your community. <continue>Weakness takes many forms. Strength is truth. Strength is honour. In most cases, it is simple to tell one from the other.
Text.zh: 图克哈玛向我们展示了力量。力量<i>{就是}一种仁慈。表现弱点对你的族群毫无益处。<continue>弱点有很多形式。力量就是真理。力量就是荣誉。大多数时候，很容易分辨两者。

Text.en: Perhaps we aren't so different after all.
Text.zh: 也许我们没有那么不同。

Text.en: My sense of family and belonging is solely tied to the Dreamer. His path is all I see.
Text.zh: 我的家庭归属感完全依托于幻梦者。他的道路就是我所见的一切。

Text.en: I do not know who you speak of. This... Dreamer. What do <i>{you} know of him?
Text.zh: 我不知道你说的是谁。这个……幻梦者。<i>{你}对他了解什么？

Text.en: I know that he has well laid plans that will free me. Wholly. And that my potential will continue to soar under his guidance.
Text.zh: 我知道他有周密的计划，可以彻底解放我。我的潜力会在他的指导下继续飞跃。

Text.en: Well.. my father once told me, Matiki, if you are to speak, make sure your words mean something. <continue>Empty words carry no message. No meaning. Consider this lesson, my friend.
Text.zh: 嗯……我父亲曾对我说，玛提吉，如果你要开口，确保你的话有意义。<continue>空话没有信息。没有意义。好好想想这个教训，我的朋友。

Text.en: I'm not entirely sure how death works around here...
Text.zh: 我不太确定这里的死亡规则……

Text.en: Death and honour are bound to each other. This is important, yes! <continue>If the ancestors see you, if they recognise you, then you can never truly die. Oh yes! It is part of the Way.
Text.zh: 死亡和荣誉紧密相连。这很重要，对！<continue>如果先祖看见你，如果他们认可你，那你就永远不会真正死去。哦对！这是卡鲁之道的一部分。

Text.en: I've no idea what you're talking about.
Text.zh: 我完全不明白你在说什么。

Text.en: Oh come now! You must surely have seen that there is more to life and death than what it seems?
Text.zh: 拜托！你肯定已经看出生死之间还有更多奥秘吧？

Text.en: Trust me, dear, I'm <i>{very} aware of that.
Text.zh: 相信我，亲爱的，我<i>{非常}清楚这一点。

Text.en: We share a love of our home. The Maraketh cherish the Vastiri, even though at times... it can feel like it holds no love for us.
Text.zh: 我们都热爱自己的家园。马拉克斯人珍视瓦斯提里，尽管有时……感觉它并不爱我们。

Text.en: Sand and water are not so different. The tide is not always on our side either. <continue>But we know our place in the world. We know that we owe respect to its power. <continue>I am sure the Maraketh know this of the Vastiri, mmm?
Text.zh: 沙子和水没那么不同。潮汐也不总是站在我们这边。<continue>但我们知道自己在世界中的位置。我们知道要对它的力量心存敬畏。<continue>我相信马拉克斯人对瓦斯提里也是如此，嗯？

Text.en: It is true. We fear the sands. And admire them. You are both wise, indeed.
Text.zh: 确实如此。我们既敬畏沙漠，也钦佩它。你们俩确实很有智慧。

Text.en: It is a beautiful place. I envy this sense of timeless belonging.
Text.zh: 这是个美丽的地方。我羡慕这种永恒的归属感。

Text.en: Listen to me now. You may come here from distant shores, but you are still connected to us. <continue>The tribes all have their own songs, their own tastes, their own Way... but we are all Karui. <continue>We are bound beyond what we see. Beyond who we think we are. And I am proud to know you.
Text.zh: 听我说。你可能来自遥远的海岸，但你仍与我们相连。<continue>各部落都有自己的歌谣，自己的喜好，自己的卡鲁之道……但我们都是卡鲁。<continue>我们的联系超越了表象。超越了我们自以为的身份。我为认识你而自豪。

Text.en: I... am touched. Thank you.
Text.zh: 我……很感动。谢谢。

Text.en: All sounds quite lovely. Where I'm from it isn't so pretty. Death means coin and is commonplace. <continue>Maybe I've gotten a bit... desensitised to it all.
Text.zh: 听起来都很美好。我来的地方可没这么美。死亡就意味着金钱，而且司空见惯。<continue>也许我对这一切变得有些……麻木了。

Text.en: You have known love in your life. Yes! You can't hide that. Try as you may.
Text.zh: 你生命中感受过爱。对！你藏不住的。无论你怎么试图掩饰。

Text.en: We can't help where we are born. Who we are born to. <continue>But... with some hard work... we can shape who we choose to become. <continue>So, who do you choose to be?
Text.zh: 我们无法选择出生地。无法选择父母。<continue>但是……通过努力……我们可以塑造自己选择成为的人。<continue>那么，你选择成为什么样的人？

Text.en: Good question.
Text.zh: 好问题。

Text.en: Yes! And you have your whole life to answer it.
Text.zh: 对！你有一辈子的时间来回答。

Text.en: Ye've a gift here. A true home. Guard it well... for not all of us kept ours. <continue>Bah. Listen to me, wallowin' like an old fool. Forget it.
Text.zh: 你拥有这片福地，一个真正的家。守好了……不是所有人都能保住自己的家园。<continue> 呸。瞧我这老糊涂，竟在这儿伤春悲秋的。当我没说。

Text.en: Such sorrow. Yours is a heart that has known true pain, yes...
Text.zh: 真是令人心痛。没错……你这颗心确实饱经沧桑。

Text.en: But that means it has known love, too - and can do again.
Text.zh: 但这意味着它也曾尝过爱的滋味——并且能再次尝到。

Text.en: Ye think so?
Text.zh: 你真这么认为？

Text.en: I know so. A benefit of being as old as I am. Hah!
Text.zh: 这点把握我还是有的。活到我这个岁数，总该有点好处。哈！

Text.en: We gather from time to time. Not as often as we should. But it usually always becomes some sort of... altercation. <continue>Some of us just have very different ideas.
Text.zh: 我们偶尔会聚在一起。没有应该的那么频繁。但通常总会变成某种……争执。<continue>我们中有些人想法截然不同。

Text.en: It is our differences that make us stronger.
Text.zh: 正是我们的差异让我们变得强大。

Text.en: Perhaps. But... there must be someone who carries the truth.
Text.zh: 也许吧。但必须有人掌握真理。

Text.en: Why are you so confident in <i>{your} interpretation?
Text.zh: 你为什么对<i>{自己的}理解这么自信？

Text.en: But Tavakai... how do you know you're right?
Text.zh: 但是塔瓦凯……你怎么知道自己是对的？

Text.en: What makes you so sure in your belief?
Text.zh: 是什么让你对自己的信念如此确信？

Text.en: And what makes you so sure, in all your arrogance, that yours is the right <i>{Way?}
Text.zh: 你真是傲慢，凭什么确信你的<i>{卡鲁之道}才是正确的？

Text.en: How do you know that your truth is the truth for all Karui?
Text.zh: 你怎么知道你的真理就是所有卡鲁人的真理？

Text.en: How do you know that <i>{your} version of The Way is true?
Text.zh: 你怎么知道只有<i>{你}关于卡鲁之道的说法是正确的？

Text.en: What makes you bet it all on what you believe?
Text.zh: 是什么让你把一切都押在自己的信念上？

Text.en: And if ye're wrong, Chieftain? What then?
Text.zh: 若您错了呢，酋长？那又当如何？

Text.en: Hmm... it is not your business, but I will share this with you. <continue>I appealed to Tukohama. To all the Ancestors. I asked them for guidance. <continue>Tukohama heard me. His presence rumbled the very earth. And my axe... it glowed against the night, filled with his strength. <continue>It was a reminder of what he taught us so long ago.
Text.zh: 嗯……这不关你的事，但我可以告诉你。<continue>我向图克哈玛祈求，向所有先祖祈求，我请求他们的指引。<continue>图克哈玛回应了我，他的威能震撼了我脚下的大地。我的斧头……在夜色中发光，充满了他的力量。<continue>它让我能够时刻铭记他的教诲。

Text.en: A good question.<continue>I appealed to Tukohama. To all the Ancestors. I asked them for guidance. <continue>Tukohama heard me. His presence rumbled the very earth. And my axe... it glowed against the night, filled with his strength. <continue>It was a reminder of what he taught us so long ago.
Text.zh: 好问题。<continue>我向图克哈玛祈求，向所有先祖祈求，我请求他们的指引。<continue>图克哈玛回应了我，他的威能震撼了我脚下的大地。我的斧头……在夜色中发光，充满了他的力量。<continue>它让我能够时刻铭记他的教诲。

Text.en: We have not known the Ancestors to reveal their Will to us in such a way for a very long time. <continue>Tavakai is our true <i>{Ngakuramakoi.} Where he leads, we will follow without question. <continue>It is only a matter of time before the other tribes recognise his strength.
Text.zh: 我们很久没见过先祖以这种方式向我们显示意志了。<continue>塔瓦凯是我们真正的<i>{纳库雷默柯}。他指向哪里，我们就毫不质疑地跟到哪里。<continue>其他部落认识到他的力量只是时间问题。

Text.en: The word represents a fighter of fierce conviction. Someone who truly embodies 'The Way'. <continue>A fighter who, when called upon, would give it all to honour 'The Way', or die trying.
Text.zh: 这个词代表一位信念坚定的战士。真正体现“卡鲁之道”的人。<continue>一个在被召唤时，会倾尽一切来捍卫“卡鲁之道”，或为此而死的战士。

Text.en: As you know, the word represents a fighter of fierce conviction. Someone who truly embodies 'The Way'. <continue>A fighter who, when called upon, would give it all to honour 'The Way', or die trying.
Text.zh: 如你所知，这个词代表一位信念坚定的战士。真正体现“卡鲁之道”的人。<continue>一个在被召唤时，会倾尽一切来捍卫“卡鲁之道”，或为此而死的战士。

Text.en: What informs you that someone <i>{is} such a person?
Text.zh: 什么能证明某人<i>{就是}这样的人？

Text.en: How do you find these warriors?
Text.zh: 你们如何找到这些战士？

Text.en: How is this even achieved or recognised?
Text.zh: 这究竟要如何达成，如何才能得到认可？

Text.en: So what qualifications are needed to fulfil that role?
Text.zh: 那么担任这个角色需要什么条件？

Text.en: How does one become such a person?
Text.zh: 一个人如何成为这样的人？

Text.en: How can you know someone truly is a <i>{Ngakuramakoi}?
Text.zh: 你如何才能确认某人是真正的<i>{纳库雷默柯}？

Text.en: What gives it away? How do you find them?
Text.zh: 什么能暴露这一点？你们如何找到他们？

Text.en: Plenty claim conviction. But how can ye truly separate the grain from the chaff?
Text.zh: 声称怀有信念的人很多。但你究竟要如何分辨谁是真心，谁是假意呢？

Text.en: Our ancestors were all <i>{Ngakuramakoi} in their own way, while they were alive. It is what made them special. <continue>You can feel it radiate from the person. An aura. A truth.
Text.zh: 我们的先祖生前都以各自的方式成为了<i>{纳库雷默柯}。这就是他们的特别之处。<continue>你能感受到它从这个人身上散发出来。一种气场。一种真理。

Text.en: Yes! And we believe the Ancestors choose their fighter. They bless them with passion for the tribe. <continue>And we are blessed to have Tavakai, even if he may be a little headstrong at times...
Text.zh: 对！我们相信先祖会选择他们的战士。他们用对部落的热情祝福他们。<continue>我们很幸运拥有塔瓦凯，即使他有时可能有点固执……

Text.en: Kaimana...
Text.zh: 凯玛纳……

Text.en: <i>{Ati-ati!} Said with love, my Chieftain.
Text.zh: <i>{阿蒂-阿蒂！} 是出于爱意说的，我的酋长。

Text.en: Of course we know of the Beast. Every culture on Wraeclast knows of the Beast. It kept the gods asleep. Tekaru in his slumber. <continue>But the Corruption... the blood fever... this price is too heavy to pay.
Text.zh: 我们当然知道邪兽。瓦尔克拉斯的每个文明都知道邪兽。它让众神沉睡。让特卡鲁陷入沉眠。<continue>但腐化……血热病……这代价太沉重了。

Text.en: Divinity... and Corruption... yes! Both unnatural curses upon the world. Think about how simple things would be without them. But, a mystic knows, what's simple isn't always true.
Text.zh: 神性……和腐化……对！都是加诸世界的不自然诅咒。想想没有它们世界会多么简单。但是，神秘学者知道，看似简单的东西并不总是真实的。

Text.en: There is always something to cause suffering in the world. Good intentions or not. <continue>Until the day bloodlust overtakes us, we shall try our best to maintain balance.
Text.zh: 世界上总有东西会带来痛苦。无论动机如何。<continue>直到嗜血吞噬我们的那一天，我们会尽力维持平衡。

Text.en: Speak more of this bloodlust.
Text.zh: 多说说这种嗜血的事情。

Text.en: What do you mean by that?
Text.zh: 你这话什么意思？

Text.en: What of this bloodlust you mention?
Text.zh: 你提到的嗜血是什么？

Text.en: You got my attention with that last bit... care to elaborate?
Text.zh: 你刚才那句话吸引了我的注意……能详细说说吗？

Text.en: Bloodlust... this sounds a worrisome tale.
Text.zh: 嗜血……这听起来令人担忧。

Text.en: What bloodlust do you speak of?
Text.zh: 你说的嗜血是什么？

Text.en: Hang on... what was that bit about bloodlust overtaking us?
Text.zh: 等等……刚才嗜血吞噬我们那句是什么意思？

Text.en: Bloodlust, ye say?
Text.zh: 你说嗜血？

Text.en: The tribes all have their own 'end of time' stories. Ours follows the one true <i>{Ngakuramakoi.} <continue>Through no fault of their own, this warrior will inspire the entire world to war. A war to end all. <continue>And, the <i>{Ngakuramakoi} will fall in that war.
Text.zh: 各部落都有自己的“末日”传说。我们的传说围绕着那位真正的<i>{纳库雷默柯}。<continue>并非出于他们的过错，这位战士会激发整个世界走向战争。一场终结一切的战争。<continue>而且，<i>{纳库雷默柯}会在那场战争中阵亡。

Text.en: Yes! And his corpse... his corpse shall be where the seed of a new day is sown. A time when all can be strong.
Text.zh: 对！而他的尸体……他的尸体将成为新时代种子播撒的地方。一个人人都能强大的时代。

Text.en: Sounds quite ridiculous. But... do you believe <i>{you} are this person?
Text.zh: 听起来挺荒谬的。但是……你相信<i>{你自己}就是这个人吗？

Text.en: Spirit save me. What a tale. So you believe you are the <i>{Ngakuramakoi}?
Text.zh: 万灵庇佑，好一个精彩传说。所以你相信自己就是这个<i>{纳库雷默柯}？

Text.en: And are you the one to bring this doom?
Text.zh: 所以，你会是那个带来末日的人吗？

Text.en: Well, mighty Chieftain... are you the world ender?
Text.zh: 那么，伟大的酋长……你就是这个世界的终结者？

Text.en: So... am I speaking to this <i>{Ngakuramakoi?}
Text.zh: 所以……我在和这位<i>{纳库雷默柯}对话吗？

Text.en: Wait. You think this person is Tavakai?
Text.zh: 等等。你认为这个人就是塔瓦凯？

Text.en: Shite. Better get my affairs in order, eh? So, are you that fella?
Text.zh: 糟糕。最好把后事安排好，嗯？那么，你就是那位吗？

Text.en: And ye think yerself this Nga... Ngakura... this <i>{thing}?
Text.zh: 而你认为自己就是那个纳…纳库雷…那<i>{玩意}吗？

Text.en: Who is to say? It is not my place to know or to guess. I do not even really care. <continue>If I am, so be it. If not, I will still do what is right for my people. Whatever that may be.
Text.zh: 谁说得准呢？这不是我该知道或猜测的。我甚至不太在乎。<continue>如果我是，那就这样。如果不是，我仍会为族人做正确的事。无论那是什么。

Text.en: Do you worry about the people on Arastas?
Text.zh: 你担心阿拉斯塔岛上的人吗？

Text.en: What do you make of them lot on Arastas?
Text.zh: 你如何看待阿拉斯塔岛上的那帮人？

Text.en: Are you concerned about this Twilight Order?
Text.zh: 这个暮光会让你感到不安吗？

Text.en: What of those fanatics on Arastas?
Text.zh: 你如何看待阿拉斯塔岛上的那些狂热分子？

Text.en: Do you worry about the people of Arastas?
Text.zh: 你担心阿拉斯塔岛上的邪教分子吗？

Text.en: What do you make of this Twilight Order?
Text.zh: 你如何看待这个暮光会？

Text.en: What you reckon about them Twilight Order folks?
Text.zh: 你觉得那些暮光会的家伙怎么样？

Text.en: This Twilight lot on Arastas... What do ye make of 'em?
Text.zh: 盘踞在阿拉斯塔岛上的暮光会…你怎么看他们？

Text.en: Bah. The very name Arastas sets my teeth on edge. That is <i>{not} our name for those ancient shores. <continue>The Twilight Order has been there in some form for a very long time. <continue>There are sacred burial sites deep within the island... but they are not Karui. <continue>Other than that, we know very little about them.
Text.zh: 呸。阿拉斯塔这个名字就让我牙根发痒。那<i>{不是}我们对那片古老海岸的称呼。<continue>暮光会已经以某种形式在那里存在很久了。<continue>岛屿深处有神圣的墓地……但不是卡鲁的。<continue>除此之外，我们对他们所知甚少。

Text.en: Yes! But... we know enough to be concerned. <continue>Unlike the Kalguurans, the Order actively recruits any that will listen. Listen long enough, and you may pledge just to shut them up! <continue>Once you're recruited, you say goodbye to your old life. Goodbye to your family. Goodbye to yourself. It is worrisome, yes!
Text.zh: 对！但是……我们知道的足以令人担忧。<continue>与卡古兰人不同，教团积极招募任何愿意听的人。听得足够之久，你可能会让他们闭嘴而加入教团！<continue>一旦被招募，你就要告别旧生活。告别家人。告别自己。这很令人担忧，对！

Text.en: We have lost many of our youth to their <i>{cause}. They promise a new future. A new world. <continue>But... we do not know <i>{how} they plan to achieve it. There is more to uncover about their motives.
Text.zh: 我们当中，许多年轻人失去了投身的<i>{事业}。他们承诺新的未来。新的世界。<continue>但是……我们不知道他们<i>{如何}打算实现。关于他们的动机还有更多需要查明的。

Text.en: Put simply, they may soon know the meaning of the true Way. <continue>I will not console another mother, sister or child who has lost their loved one to those wretched fools, never to hear from them again.
Text.zh: 简单说，他们可能很快就会领教真正卡鲁之道的含义。<continue>我再不也会安慰那些母亲、姐妹或孩子，那些可恶的蠢货招揽他们心爱之人，使其从此杳无音信。

Text.en: If diplomacy doesn't work, I understand it.
Text.zh: 如果外交不管用，那我理解你的做法。

Text.en: Sounds like they've got it coming, if talking won't work.
Text.zh: 听起来，如果谈判不行，那他们就是自找的。

Text.en: Why not try to speak with them?
Text.zh: 为什么不试着和他们对话？

Text.en: No open conversation with them as of yet, I take it?
Text.zh: 我猜，你们目前为止还没和他们公开对话过？

Text.en: Have you not spoken to their elders?
Text.zh: 你和他们的长者谈过吗？

Text.en: Have you tried speaking with them?
Text.zh: 你试过和他们对话吗？

Text.en: You tried having a chat with 'em?
Text.zh: 你试过和他们聊聊吗？

Text.en: And did ye try talkin' sense to 'em?
Text.zh: 那你试过跟他们讲道理没？
