Text.en: The head of the sea serpent is hard to find in stormy seas. Their hierarchy is... confusing. Titles upon titles. <continue>It reeks of a militant structure, no matter how they try to make it look.
Text.zh: 刮暴风雨的时候，很难找到海蛇头。他们的等级制度……令人困惑。头衔套着头衔。<continue>无论他们如何试图伪装，都散发着军事体制的臭味。

Text.en: The swords give a hint or two!
Text.zh: 那些剑就暴露了一两分！

Text.en: We've tried talking. I'm done with it.
Text.zh: 我们试过谈判。我受够了。

Text.en: Not just Tavakai. I also tried. Yes.
Text.zh: 不只是塔瓦凯。我也试过。没错。

Text.en: And me too. All I got out of it was about half a day of listening to nonsense that went in one ear... and out the other. <continue>I think <i>{they} might not even know what they are going on about. <continue>They <i>{do} make a good mead, though...
Text.zh: 我也试过。结果就是花了大半天听一堆左耳进右耳出的废话。<continue>我觉得<i>{他们}甚至可能不知道自己在胡说什么。<continue>不过他们<i>{确实}会酿造不错的蜂蜜酒……

Text.en: Not <i>{all} bad, then.
Text.zh: 看来，也不<i>{全是}坏事。

Text.en: Forgive me... daughter. The fever took hold, and I... I lost my way.
Text.zh: 原谅我……女儿。热病控制了我，我……我迷失了方向。

Text.en: You must not forget your mistake, Tavakai. For the coming war, we need you and your people to fight alongside us. <continue>I will use the Weapon to heal those infected here. In return, the Karui must aid us in fighting Oriana and her forces.
Text.zh: 你不能忘记自己的错误，塔瓦凯。为了即将到来的战争，我们需要你和你的族人与我们并肩作战。<continue>我会用武器治愈这里的感染者。作为回报，卡鲁必须帮助我们对抗奥莉安娜和她的军队。

Text.en: You have my word.
Text.zh: 我向你承诺。

Text.en: I don't trust you. I think we're done here.
Text.zh: 我不信任你，我们到此为止吧。

Text.en: This isn't over. It's only the beginning.
Text.zh: 这事还没完，刚刚开始而已。

Text.en: What do you think we should do now?
Text.zh: 你觉得我们现在该当如何？

Text.en: We should go.
Text.zh: 我们该走了。

Text.en: You will redeem yourself. But first, we must decide what is next.
Text.zh: 你会有机会救赎自己。但首先，我们要决定下一步行动。

Text.en: You will have your chance to prove the value of your word. What's our next move?
Text.zh: 你会有机会兑现承诺。我们下一步该怎么做？

Text.en: Just do your part. Let's go.
Text.zh: 做好你该做的。走吧。

Text.en: Talk's cheap. Let yer actions prove it.
Text.zh: 空谈无用，行动为证。

Text.en: Return to Kingsmarch. We must prepare to bring the fight to Oriana. <continue>With the Weapon in hand... we must face the Beast.
Text.zh: 回到君锋镇。我们必须准备向奥莉安娜发起攻击。<continue>握着武器……我们必须面对邪兽。

Text.en: I am... ashamed. Somehow, I must live with my actions. <continue>The madness I felt... the fervor... <continue>We must not let that madness take hold of the world. <continue>The Karui will aid you. We shall sail the tides of war.
Text.zh: 我……深感羞愧。无论如何，我必须承受自己的行为所带来的后果。<continue>我当时所感受到的疯狂……那种狂热……<continue>我们绝不能任由那种疯狂掌控这个世界。<continue>卡鲁人将会援助你。我们将乘风破浪，驶向战争的狂潮。

Text.en: I hope those are more than just words.
Text.zh: 我希望那不只是空话。

Text.en: We shoulda worked together from the start.
Text.zh: 我们从一开始就应该合作。

Text.en: A new perspective is admirable. I hope it lasts.
Text.zh: 新的觉悟值得称赞。希望能持续下去。

Text.en: Some lessons are hard to stomach.
Text.zh: 有些教训很难咽下。

Text.en: We must put our differences aside. We must move forward together.
Text.zh: 我们可以搁置分歧，我们必须携手前进。

Text.en: I'm glad to hear it. We must fight as one if we are to succeed.
Text.zh: 很高兴你这么说。要想成功，我们就必须团结作战。

Text.en: If we win, then none of this'll matter.
Text.zh: 如果我们赢了，这些都不重要了。

Text.en: Fine words. Now let's see the steel behind 'em.
Text.zh: 漂亮话谁都会。现在，做点实事看看吧。

Text.en: Yes... I will pay my debt of shame until my last breath. <continue>My people stand by me. They see my heart is true. But I shall wait to learn what the ancestors make of me.
Text.zh: 没错…我会带着羞耻偿还债务直到咽气。<continue>我的族人原谅了我。他们看到我的心是真诚的。但我会等着看先祖如何评判我。

Text.en: Forget the past. You'll fight better without it.
Text.zh: 忘记过去吧。没有负担你会战斗得更好。

Text.en: That's beyond me, but I think you'll be alright.
Text.zh: 我可能无法理解这种事，但我觉得你会没事的。

Text.en: Forgiveness is a powerful force in this tainted world.
Text.zh: 宽恕是这个污浊世界中的强大力量。

Text.en: Perhaps they won't condemn you. They don't appear hateful.
Text.zh: 他们或许不会谴责你。他们似乎并没有怀恨在心。

Text.en: We are often defined by our mistakes... but you are worthy of your people's forgiveness. Your ancestors will see this too. I know it.
Text.zh: 世人总是执着于自己的过错……但你配得上族人的宽恕。你的先祖也将见证这一切。我对此深信不疑。

Text.en: I'm certain they will judge you worthy.
Text.zh: 我确信他们会认为你配得上宽恕。

Text.en: I'm sure they'll give you a pass.
Text.zh: 我确信他们会网开一面的。

Text.en: I've sins o' me own, ones I'll never wash away  - but that shouldnae keep me, or you, from tryin'.
Text.zh: 我自个儿也背着些洗不净的罪孽——但这不该阻碍你我，试着往前走。

Text.en: I hope you are right. But now, I must redeem my name by the strength of Tukohama. <continue>As promised... you have the might of the Karui alongside you.
Text.zh: 希望你说得对。但现在，我要以图克哈玛的力量挽回名声。让全世界见证！<continue>如我所承诺的……你有卡鲁的力量与你同在。

Text.en: Watch your step, eh! Slip overboard and you're on your own. <continue>I'm Kanu. First Mate of the Aurora May.
Text.zh: 小心脚下，嗯！掉到海里你就只能自求多福了。<continue>我是卡努。极光五月号的大副。

Text.en: If you're lookin' for a ship, best speak to the boss, Makoru. And mind your manners while you're at it!
Text.zh: 如果你要找船，最好和老大马克鲁谈。说话时注意礼貌！

Text.en: If you're after a ship and crew, you'll need a charter. <continue>We don't just work for anyone who shuffles aboard off the dock, you know! <continue>Speak to Rog about the charter. Yeah, he can sort you out.
Text.zh: 如果你想要船和船员，那就先弄到租船许可证。<continue>不是随便从码头上晃悠过来的人，我们都会为他干活，知道吧！<continue>找罗格谈租船许可证。对，他能给你搞定。

Text.en: Well, quite the introduction. Good to meet you, Kanu.
Text.zh: 嗯，这自我介绍挺有意思。很高兴认识你，卡努。

Text.en: Young Kanu. Confidence... or arrogance? Which is it? We may get along, depending on which you choose.
Text.zh: 年轻的卡努。是自信……还是狂妄？我们能不能处得来，就看你怎么选了。

Text.en: Good day, Kanu. You have such confidence. I hope you are a capable seafarer.
Text.zh: 你好，卡努。你这么有自信，希望你是个有本事的水手。

Text.en: You're a boisterous one. But your smile seems... kind.
Text.zh: 你挺活泼的。但你的笑容看起来……很善良。

Text.en: Well, Kanu... quite the mouth you've got.
Text.zh: 嗯，卡努……你这嘴皮子还真厉害。

Text.en: Please. No need for all the theatrics.
Text.zh: 拜托。不用搞这些花招。

Text.en: Alright, mate. Not looking for trouble, just a crew.
Text.zh: 好吧，伙计。我不是来找茬的，就是要个船员。

Text.en: Yer full o' bark, lad. We'll see if you've the bite to match.
Text.zh: 你小子嘴炮震天响。倒要看看你有没有你说的那种本事。

Text.en: Phwoar. Your voice is thick as a rock! Powerful. <continue>I won't mess around with you after a few drinks, eh?
Text.zh: 哇。你的嗓音厚得像石头！有力量。<continue>我要是喝高了，可不敢跟你耍酒疯。

Text.en: Let's wait and see. I have a way of growing on people, or so I'm told.
Text.zh: 拭目以待吧。我有办法给人留下印象，至少别人是这么说的。

Text.en: My nana always said, a smile speaks a thousand words. Pleased to meet you, my lady.
Text.zh: 我奶奶常说，一个微笑胜过千言万语。很高兴见到你，女士。

Text.en: It's gotten me in my fair share of trouble. And out of it, too.
Text.zh: 这给我惹过不少麻烦。也帮我脱过险。

Text.en: Hey, come on. Life is for livin'. Live loud - and die quietly, that's what I say.
Text.zh: 嘿，别这样。人生就是要活得精彩。活得响亮——死得安静，这就是我的座右铭。

Text.en: Well, this crew does a good job of getting into trouble. So... guess you found both! <continue>I'm just having some fun. Welcome aboard.
Text.zh: 嗯，这支船员确实擅长惹麻烦。所以……看来你两样都找到了！<continue>我只是开开玩笑。欢迎上船。

Text.en: Yeah, that's right. I'm the best!
Text.zh: 没错，对的。我是最棒的！

Text.en: Hah! Plenty of both. You'll see.
Text.zh: 哈！要多少有多少。你就瞧好吧。

Text.en: Oh, I can imagine. One after me own heart.
Text.zh: 哦，我能想象。是个志同道合之人。

Text.en: Hmm. Not a bad mantra, I will admit.
Text.zh: 嗯，不得不说是不错的人生格言。

Text.en: The best?
Text.zh: 最棒的？

Text.en: Uhh... the second best!
Text.zh: 呃……第二棒的！

Text.en: Hah! Well done.
Text.zh: 哈！不错。

Text.en: So what is this... <i>{thing...} you've found? <continue>Seems only fair to ask, since we're doing all the hard work sailing you around.
Text.zh: 那么这个……<i>{东西}……你找到的是什么？<continue>既然我们在做所有繁重的航海工作载着你四处跑，问问似乎挺公平的。

Text.en: It is one piece of an ancient Weapon. <continue>It may allow us to slay the Beast and put an end to Corruption.
Text.zh: 这是一件古代武器的一块碎片。<continue>它可能让我们杀死邪兽并终结腐化。

Text.en: Don't you worry your pretty little head about that.
Text.zh: 别让你那漂亮的小脑袋为这个操心。

Text.en: They tell me it's a piece of an ancient Weapon. But... we shall see.
Text.zh: 他们告诉我这是古代武器的一部分。但是……还是拭目以待吧。

Text.en: It's a piece of an ancient weapon. But I'll keep the rest to myself, thank you very much.
Text.zh: 这是古代武器的一块碎片。但其余的我要保密，谢谢。

Text.en: It's part of some Weapon, though I don't see how it all works yet.
Text.zh: 这是某个武器的一部分。不过我还没搞清楚它怎么运作。

Text.en: This is a piece of a Weapon. A grand creation of unknown power.
Text.zh: 这是一件武器的碎片。一个拥有未知力量的伟大造物。

Text.en: Just another ancient thing I had to 'acquire.' All I do these days, really.
Text.zh: 就是我必须“获取”的另一个古代玩意儿。我这些天净干这个了。

Text.en: A piece of a Weapon. Ancient. Dangerous.
Text.zh: 一块武器碎片。远古之物，凶险异常。

Text.en: Slay the who and end what?
Text.zh: 杀死谁并终结什么？

Text.en: She called me pretty! D'you guys hear that?
Text.zh: 她叫我漂亮！你们听到了吗？

Text.en: Alright, alright! Each to their own!
Text.zh: 好吧，好吧！萝卜青菜各有所爱！

Text.en: It could save the world.
Text.zh: 它可能拯救世界。

Text.en: <i>{Not...} what I <i>{meant!}
Text.zh: 我<i>{不是}这个 <i>{意思}！

Text.en: Oh. How interesting... I guess. So we're riskin' our necks for some old collector's item, eh?
Text.zh: 哦。真有意思啊……所以我们冒着生命危险，就是为了这些破古董？

Text.en: It's more than that. It could be our only way to fight back.
Text.zh: 不只是这样。它可能是我们反击的唯一办法。

Text.en: It is more than just some collector's item. It could be what helps us save the world.
Text.zh: 这不只是什么随随便便的收藏品。它可能是我们拯救世界的关键。

Text.en: Oh no... It's much more than that. Could be what helps us save the world.
Text.zh: 哦不……不只是这样。它可能是我们拯救世界的关键。

Text.en: We do as we must. It could be our salvation.
Text.zh: 我们必须做该做的事。它可能是我们的救赎。

Text.en: Pretty much. But... could be what helps us save the world. Or somethin'.
Text.zh: 差不多吧。但是……可能是帮助我们拯救世界的东西。反正就是这样。

Text.en: It's <i>{hope,} lad. And on Wraeclast, hope's a rare thing.
Text.zh: 这是<i>{希望}，小子。在瓦拉克拉斯，希望可是稀罕物。

Text.en: Phwoar. That's deep! Glad I'm on your side.
Text.zh: 嚯！这话真有分量！幸好我跟你是一头的。

Text.en: Could be? So... it's uncertain? Ah well, either way, we're doing our part. Let's see what happens!
Text.zh: 可能是？所以……不确定？算了，无论如何，我们在尽职责。看看会发生什么吧！

Text.en: So... found more of that fancy Weapon, eh? Can I see it?
Text.zh: 所以……又找到更多那花哨武器了，嗯？我能看看吗？

Text.en: We cannot touch it. The Hooded One keeps the pieces safe.
Text.zh: 我们不能碰它。兜帽密师保管着那些碎片。

Text.en: No. You cannot.
Text.zh: 不行。你不能。

Text.en: The Hooded One has it concealed... I dare not ask where. It cannot be safely touched by mortal hands.
Text.zh: 兜帽密师把它藏起来了……我不敢问在哪里。凡人之手无法安全触碰它。

Text.en: I don't think so.
Text.zh: 我看不行。

Text.en: Would show ya if I could. But they're full of <i>{rathin}.
Text.zh: 我想给你看，但不行。它们充满了<i>{腐化}。

Text.en: That would not be a good idea.
Text.zh: 那不是好主意。

Text.en: Yeah, why not? I'll bring it around at some point. I just don't have it on me.
Text.zh: 行啊，为什么不呢？我改天拿给你看。我只是现在没带在身上。

Text.en: Unless you want to lose yer mind to Corruption... it's best you don't.
Text.zh: 除非你想让腐化侵蚀神智——最好别这样。

Text.en: Oh... okay. Well, the more I hear, the more curious I get. Can't really blame me, I'm a simple fella. <continue>Something like this... entire generations come and go without having eyes on a thing so significant!
Text.zh: 哦……好吧。嗯，我了解得越多，就越好奇。你真不能怪我，我就是个想法简单的人。<continue>像这样的东西……一代代人来来去去都没机会见到如此重要的东西！

Text.en: So <i>{now} you take it seriously?
Text.zh: 所以<i>{现在}你当回事了？

Text.en: So now you are interested in its power. Curious.
Text.zh: 所以现在你对它的力量感兴趣了。有意思。

Text.en: It is something to be taken seriously. Whether it works, remains to be seen.
Text.zh: 这确实该当回事。至于是否有效，还得看情况。

Text.en: Why take it so seriously all of a sudden?
Text.zh: 为什么突然这么重视它？

Text.en: So... you're taking note of it now. Fair enough.
Text.zh: 所以……你现在注意到它了。挺好的。

Text.en: I see that you take it more seriously now.
Text.zh: 我看到你现在更重视它了。

Text.en: Oh look, someone's taking it all a bit more serious!
Text.zh: 哦瞧，有人开始把这事当回事了！

Text.en: It's just as well they do. This Weapon is no mere trinket.
Text.zh: 他们这样谨慎是对的。这件兵器可绝非寻常玩物。

Text.en: No kidding.
Text.zh: 可不是说笑。

Text.en: The crew talks. I listen. Some of them heard that Doryabi guy arguing with the spooky one in the cape. It did sound all very serious.
Text.zh: 船员们在聊天。我在听。他们有些人听到那个多里亚比家伙和穿斗篷的怪人在争论。听起来确实很严肃。

Text.en: That'd be Doryani and the Hooded One.
Text.zh: 那应该是多里亚尼和……兜帽密师。

Text.en: Doryani. And the Hooded One. Fool.
Text.zh: 多里亚尼。还有兜帽密师。笨蛋。

Text.en: You mean Doryani and the Hooded One.
Text.zh: 你是指多里亚尼和兜帽密师。

Text.en: I think you mean Doryani and the Hooded One.
Text.zh: 我想你是指多里亚尼和兜帽密师。

Text.en: Oh, Doryani and... the Hooded One?
Text.zh: 哦，多里亚尼和……兜帽密师？

Text.en: Their titles are Doryani and... the Hooded One.
Text.zh: 他们的称号是多里亚尼和……兜帽密师。

Text.en: You mean Doryani and... oh well, whatever. Close enough.
Text.zh: 你是指多里亚尼和……哦算了，差不多。

Text.en: Doryani and the Hooded One. Names worth rememberin', lad.
Text.zh: 多里亚尼和兜帽密师。最好记住他俩的名字，小子。

Text.en: Yeah. Those fellas. Ah well. Good luck findin' the other piece. We got your back!
Text.zh: 对的。就是那两位。算了。祝你在寻找另一块碎片的旅途上好运。我们挺你！

Text.en: You found all the pieces, then? So... what now? Do they just sort of... snap together?
Text.zh: 那么你找到所有碎片了？所以……现在怎么办？把它们……拼起来吗？

Text.en: If only it were that simple...
Text.zh: 要是有那么简单就好了……

Text.en: Yes, the ancient Weapon spread in pieces across the sea for thousands of years just <i>{snaps together.}
Text.zh: 对啊，流落在海上几千年的古代武器碎片就这样简简单单，<i>{拼起来}。

Text.en: We're not sure yet... the Hooded One and Doryani are deliberating. Or arguing. Depends on their mood, it seems.
Text.zh: 我们还不确定……兜帽密师和多里亚尼正在商量。或者争论。取决于他们的心情。

Text.en: Somehow, I don't think we'll be that lucky.
Text.zh: 不知怎么的，我觉得我们不会那么走运。

Text.en: Got no idea. Your guess is as good as mine. But it probably won't be <i>{that} easy.
Text.zh: 完全不知道。你猜得和我一样准。但可能不会<i>{那么}容易。

Text.en: It's just another step in the puzzle of history. But this... is no easy puzzle.
Text.zh: 这只是历史谜题的又一步。但这……可不是简单的谜题。

Text.en: I'll suggest they try that. Chances are it's a bit more complicated. Always is.
Text.zh: 我会建议他们试试。这事八成没那么简单。每次都是这样。

Text.en: I've no idea, lad. But somethin' tells me it won't be so simple.
Text.zh: 我哪知道呢，小子。但直觉告诉我，事情不会这么简单。

Text.en: Sheesh! What's your problem?
Text.zh: 天哪！你有什么毛病？

Text.en: You ask too many questions.
Text.zh: 你问题太多了。

Text.en: Hmm. Yeah, guess you're right. Nothing's that easy, is it? <continue>Let me ask around, eh? Maybe somebody's heard a rumour, or a myth. You never know.
Text.zh: 嗯。没错，你说得对。没什么事会这么简单，对吧？<continue>让我四处打听打听，嗯？也许有人听过什么传言，或者传说。说不定会有收获。

Text.en: My family wasn't always from these parts, you know. We came out here a few generations ago. <continue>I was always out exploring the waters and fishing, even as a young boy. That's just in my blood. <continue>My family's tribe... the Tasalio tribe... they value being on the water above all, but the young ones were never allowed to go very far. <continue>I used to drive my parents nuts. I looked out across the water... and oh boy, I wanted to keep going. <continue>But I never went too far. I couldn't leave my family behind. Where <i>{they} are... <i>{that's} my home.
Text.zh: 要知道，我的家族并不是一直生活在这些地方的。我们是几代人之前迁移到这里的。<continue>从我还是个小男孩开始，我就总是跑出去探索海域和钓鱼。这就是我与生俱来的天性。<continue>我家族的部落……塔萨里奥部族……他们最崇尚海上生活，但从不允许年轻人走得太远。<continue>我过去总是把父母搞得心烦意乱。我凝视着海面……哦，天哪，我想要继续向前航行。<continue>但我从不真的走太远。我不能抛下家人。他们在<i>{哪里}……<i>{那里}就是我的家园。

Text.en: Home is wherever you can find those you care about.
Text.zh: 家园就是你能找到牵挂之人的地方。

Text.en: It's just an illusion, this 'home' people keep talking about.
Text.zh: 这不过是错觉罢了。人们总是在谈论的这个“家园”。

Text.en: Sometimes we do not value home until it is gone. I thought I didn't need my home, but, in truth, it is difficult to be without it.
Text.zh: 有时我们失去了家园才会懂得珍惜。我以为自己不需要家，但事实上失去它是很痛苦的。

Text.en: Home is wherever you find yourself.
Text.zh: 家园就是你落脚安身的地方。

Text.en: Some of us don't have a choice.
Text.zh: 我们当中有些人别无选择。

Text.en: I understand. Home is where we ground ourselves.
Text.zh: 我理解。家园是我们扎根的地方。

Text.en: I ain't ever had a home. Just places to sleep where I probably won't get stabbed.
Text.zh: 我从来没有真正的家。只有一些大概不会被人捅刀子的过夜地方。

Text.en: And if you've no family? What then?
Text.zh: 若是无家可归呢？那该怎么办？

Text.en: That's something I'd rather not think about.
Text.zh: 这事...我不愿多想。

Text.en: Sheesh. That's grim.
Text.zh: 嘶，这太可怕了。

Text.en: I said <i>{won't} get stabbed.
Text.zh: 我说的是<i>{不会}被人捅刀子。

Text.en: Aye. Don't blame ye...
Text.zh: 唉...能理解...

Text.en: Well hey... here I am. And I'm thankful the boss showed me that I can make a new life for myself, one that I choose.
Text.zh: 嗯，嘿……我就在这里。我感激老大让我明白，我可以为自己开创新的人生，一个由我自己选择的人生。

Text.en: The boss? You mean Makoru?
Text.zh: 老大？你是指马克鲁？

Text.en: The boss? Address her by her name, why don't you? Insolent boy.
Text.zh: 老大？你怎么不直呼你老大的名字？没礼貌的臭小子。

Text.en: By the boss you mean... Makoru?
Text.zh: 老大？你是指……马克鲁？

Text.en: I assume by the boss you mean Makoru?
Text.zh: 我想，你指的老大是马克鲁？

Text.en: Makoru is the boss, right? Stupid question really.
Text.zh: 马克鲁就是老大，对吧？这问题确实挺愚蠢的。

Text.en: Makoru is the boss. I get it.
Text.zh: 马克鲁就是老大。我明白了。

Text.en: Makoru seems like a fitting boss.
Text.zh: 马克鲁看起来确实是个称职的老大。

Text.en: Makoru's the one you call 'boss'?
Text.zh: 你们管马克鲁叫“老大”？

Text.en: That's right! Makoru's the boss! But more than that... she's a role model. A true leader. <continue>She showed the Kalguurans what the Karui have to offer. And honestly, I'm just lucky to be following in her footsteps.
Text.zh: 没错！马克鲁就是老大！但不仅如此……她还是个榜样。真正的领袖。<continue>她向卡古兰人展示了卡鲁人的价值所在。说实话，我只是很幸运能够追随她的足迹。

Text.en: Alright, alright. You know flattery doesn't work on me, Kanu.
Text.zh: 好了，好了。你知道阿谀奉承对我没用，卡努。

Text.en: It is simply the truth!
Text.zh: 这只是在说实话！

Text.en: Keeping an entire crew sober and on their toes is no small feat.
Text.zh: 保持整支船员清醒和专注可不是什么小事情。

Text.en: I admire Makoru, yes. You, not so much.
Text.zh: 我确实钦佩马克鲁。至于你嘛，就不太行了。

Text.en: You have done well, Makoru. There is no denying that.
Text.zh: 你干得很出色，马克鲁。这点无可争议。

Text.en: I respect you, Makoru. You lead with precision and knowledge. Not bluster.
Text.zh: 我敬重你，马克鲁。你凭借精准和学识来领导。而不是靠张牙舞爪。

Text.en: Aye. You're an impressive leader, Makoru.
Text.zh: 确实如此。你是个令人钦佩的领袖，马克鲁。

Text.en: A true leader delivers more than just orders. You lead by a fine example, Makoru.
Text.zh: 真正的领导绝不只是大吼大叫地发号施令。你是以身作则的好榜样，马克鲁。

Text.en: I can tell she knows what she's doing. Can see the crew's respect for her.
Text.zh: 我能看出她清楚自己在做什么。也能看出船员对她的敬重。

Text.en: Aye. A fine boss she is.
Text.zh: 好。她是个称职的老大。

Text.en: The Karui have no ships like the Aurora May. Our vessels are meant to travel between islands or to fish. <continue>But the Aurora May... phwoar... <continue>She feels like she could take us to the end of the world.
Text.zh: 卡鲁人没有像极光五月号这样的船只。我们的船只是用来往返岛屿之间或者捕鱼的。<continue>但是极光五月号……哇……<continue>她让人感觉能够载我们抵达世界的尽头。

Text.en: She might have to.
Text.zh: 她可能真的得这么做。

Text.en: Careful, you'll jinx it.
Text.zh: 当心点，你会招来霉运的。

Text.en: The ships remind me of the carts back home. Always in motion. A masterful construction.
Text.zh: 这些船让我想起故乡的马车。始终在运动之中。真是精湛的工艺。

Text.en: I'll take your word on that. Give me steady earth any day.
Text.zh: 我相信你说的。给我脚踏实地就够了。

Text.en: It's a wonder. The Azmeri got nothin' like it.
Text.zh: 真是个奇迹。阿兹莫里没什么东西能与之相比。

Text.en: You speak more truly than you know.
Text.zh: 你说得比自己意识到的更加真实。

Text.en: Better than the one I took out of Trarthus. Ripe old flotsam, that was.
Text.zh: 比我从特拉特斯开出来的那艘强太多了。那就是一堆烂木头。

Text.en: She might well do.
Text.zh: 她或许真能胜任。

Text.en: I'm just happy to live in times like these. <continue>I think about it sometimes, you know. What a day to be alive, to breathe in the air, feel the sun. Could be a lot worse.
Text.zh: 我很庆幸能生活在这样的时代。<continue>我有时候也会想，活着多么美好啊，呼吸着空气，感受着阳光。情况本来可能会更糟糕。

Text.en: When the Kalguurans showed up, it brought a lot of tension to the tribes. Also brought doubt among the outer islands. <continue>My family left the Tasalio tribe when I was only a boy. They then joined the Tukohama tribe. <continue>My parents found hope in their message of strength.
Text.zh: 当卡古兰人出现的时候，给各个部落带来了许多紧张局势。也给外围岛屿带来了疑虑。<continue>在我还是个小男孩的时候，我的家人离开了塔萨里奥部落。然后加入了图克哈玛部落。<continue>我的父母在他们的力量信条中找到了希望。

Text.en: Did <i>{you}?
Text.zh: <i>{你}也是这样吗？

Text.en: You sound skeptical.
Text.zh: 你听起来很怀疑。

Text.en: As they should. Did you feel the same?
Text.zh: 他们理应如此。你也有同样的感受吗？

Text.en: I suppose when you have children, you can't afford to go it alone.
Text.zh: 我想当你有了孩子，就不能孤军奋战了。

Text.en: And I 'spose... you didn't?
Text.zh: 我猜……你并没有？

Text.en: I can understand that. But did you find hope in that message?
Text.zh: 我能够理解。 不过，你在信条中看到希望了吗？

Text.en: Gotta give it to 'im, he's consistent.
Text.zh: 不得不承认，他确实很坚持。

Text.en: Strength alone's a poor compass.
Text.zh: 匹夫之勇，终难成事。

Text.en: Well, look... I don't reject the Way. It just feels different to me. <continue>Late at night, out on the sea... I can still feel what our old tribe called 'the Flow.' It's real. It's powerful.
Text.zh: 嗯，听着……我并不抗拒卡鲁之道。只是对我而言感觉有所不同。<continue>在深夜，在海上……我仍然能够感受到我们老部落所称的“流动”。它是真实存在的。它很强大。

Text.en: The Flow? Is that similar to the Way?
Text.zh: “流动”？和卡鲁之道很相似吗？

Text.en: I've always felt a flow of energy underneath the world. Is it... like that?
Text.zh: 我总是感觉世界之下流动着能量。这……是一回事儿吗？

Text.en: The sea has a power?
Text.zh: 海洋拥有力量？

Text.en: And what makes your 'Flow' different from the Way?
Text.zh: 你的“流动”和卡鲁之道有什么区别？

Text.en: What is it, this 'Flow'?
Text.zh: 你的“流动”究竟是什么？

Text.en: Tell me more of this 'Flow.'
Text.zh: 多跟我说说这个“流动”。

Text.en: Whatchu mean?
Text.zh: 你这话什么意思？

Text.en: What <i>{is} it, exactly?
Text.zh: 它到底<i>{是}什么？

Text.en: Oh... it's hard to put into words... <continue>All I know is, Tavakai's version of 'The Way' is definitely not something I can flow with.  <continue>Tavakai doesn't trust outsiders. He wants to drive the Karui further away into isolation. <continue>And that doesn't feel right to me. Feels backwards. <continue>We should at least hear new ideas out. See what they can offer us.
Text.zh: 哦……这话很难说出口……<continue>我只知道，塔瓦凯所信奉的“卡鲁之道”绝不是我所认同的。<continue>塔瓦凯不信任外人。他想把卡鲁族推向更加孤立的境地。<continue>这感觉不对劲，就像是在倒退一样。<continue>我们至少应该倾听新的声音。看看它们能给我们带来什么。

Text.en: These waters have long been Karui. Do the Elders speak of anything like a forge?
Text.zh: 这片水域自古以来就属于卡鲁族。长老可曾提及过类似熔炉的东西？

Text.en: Please tell me the Karui know something that might get this moving. We need an ancient forge.
Text.zh: 请告诉我卡鲁族是否知晓一些能推进此事的线索。我们急需一座古老的熔炉。

Text.en: I am seeking an island with ancient technology. Likely buried beneath the earth. Do the Karui tales mention anything like this?
Text.zh: 我在寻找一座拥有远古技术的岛屿。很可能深埋于地底。卡鲁族的传说中可有提及此类之地？

Text.en: Makoru, have the Karui passed down any stories about an island with an ancient forge on it?
Text.zh: 马克鲁，卡鲁族可有流传关于某座岛屿上古老熔炉的故事？

Text.en: We must find an ancient forge. Have you lot heard anything about summat like that?
Text.zh: 我们必须找到一座古老熔炉。你们听说过类似的东西吗？

Text.en: May I entreat the wisdom of the Karui? We are looking for an ancient forge.
Text.zh: 能否请教卡鲁族的智者？我们正在寻找一座古老的熔炉。

Text.en: Miss Makoru, please tell me you got some ideas about where to find an old forge, or an island with a bunch of ancient shite on it.
Text.zh: 马克鲁女士，请告诉我你是否知道哪里能找到古老的熔炉，或是一座布满远古遗迹的岛屿。

Text.en: I seek a forge, old as the land itself. Do ye know of anythin' like that?
Text.zh: 我在寻找一座熔炉，跟大地一样古老。听说过这样的东西吗？

Text.en: I've never heard any mention of such a place.
Text.zh: 我从未听闻过这样的地方。

Text.en: I have.
Text.zh: 我倒是听说过。

Text.en: Really? Where?
Text.zh: 真的？在哪里？

Text.en: My old tribe tells a tale of a foreign medicine woman. It's a very old story. <continue>Took place after a great fire scoured the world. My ancestors had to dive into the sea, just to survive. <continue>When they came to the surface, they found a burned woman, floating in the tide.
Text.zh: 我们古老的部落流传着一个关于异族医者的传说。那是个极其古老的故事。<continue>发生在大火焚尽世界之后。我的先祖们为了求生，不得不潜入海底。<continue>当他们浮出海面时，发现了一个烧伤的女子，随波逐流。

Text.en: What's this have to do with a forge? Get to the point.
Text.zh: 这跟熔炉有何关系？直说要点。

Text.en: She begged them to take her somewhere and promised to forge them a great reward. <continue>But... the toll was too heavy. She was too injured. And she died trying to use the forge. <continue>She was buried there.
Text.zh: 她恳求先祖带她前往某地，并许诺为他们锻造珍贵的回报。<continue>但是……代价过于沉重。她伤势太重了。她在尝试使用熔炉时死去了。<continue>她就葬在那里。

Text.en: Where?
Text.zh: 哪里？

Text.en: Arastas. Well... wasn't called that back then though.
Text.zh: 阿拉斯塔。不过……那时还不叫这个名字。

Text.en: Arastas? Why didn't you mention this sooner?
Text.zh: 阿拉斯塔？你为什么不早说？

Text.en: Mention what? An old myth about a foreign woman who died trying to use a forge some unknown thousands of years ago? <continue>I only knew you were looking for pieces of some old artefact. I didn't hear nothing about looking for a forge!
Text.zh: 提及什么？一个几千年前的古老传说？有个异族女人想用熔炉，结果把自己作死了？<continue>我只知道你在搜寻某些古老神器的碎片。我可没听说要找什么熔炉！

Text.en: That sounds promising. Let's set sail.
Text.zh: 听起来很有希望。我们启程吧。

Text.en: That's quite a specific myth. If it's true, Arastas must be the place.
Text.zh: 这传说有鼻子有眼的。如果是真的，那一定就在阿拉斯塔。

Text.en: Well, it is at least something to investigate. We must go to Arastas.
Text.zh: 好吧，至少有线索可循。我们必须前往阿拉斯塔。

Text.en: It seems we have no choice but to travel to Arastas.
Text.zh: 看来我们别无选择，只能去阿拉斯塔了。

Text.en: We're lucky you were here, Kanu. Let's try Arastas.
Text.zh: 幸好有你在这里，卡努。咱们试试阿拉斯塔。

Text.en: The final piece of the puzzle becomes clear. Arastas is our destination.
Text.zh: 谜团的最后一块拼图终于明朗了。阿拉斯塔就是我们的目标。

Text.en: We need to work on our communication, mate. Sorry, <i>{first mate.} Let's visit Arastas.
Text.zh: 我们的沟通需要改善，伙计。抱歉，<i>{大副。}走，去阿拉斯塔。

Text.en: Then the tide carries us to Arastas.
Text.zh: 那便让潮水将我们送往阿拉斯塔。

Text.en: <i>{Tala moana}! Oh... you're one of us but from somewhere else. Hmm.
Text.zh: <i>{愿你安好}！哦……你是我们的族人，却来自他乡。嗯。

Text.en: Yes. Born Karui. Traded as property. Then left to the tide to wash ashore. And finally... raised an Ezomyte.
Text.zh: 没错。生为卡鲁族人。被当作货物交易。然后任由潮水冲上岸头。最终……被埃佐迈特人收养。

Text.en: You have lived many lives. Hard though, it is true. But now... your skin is thicker than most.
Text.zh: 你历经了多重人生。尽管艰辛，但这是事实。如今……你的心志比常人更为坚韧。

Text.en: Some scars never heal.
Text.zh: 有些创伤永远不会愈合。

Text.en: Well... on this day, by your actions, you have honoured an Elder of the Tukohama tribe. <continue>My name is Matiki. And I owe you a debt of thanks.
Text.zh: 嗯……今日，凭借你的行为，你为图克哈玛部落的一位长老增光添彩。<continue>我叫玛提吉。我欠你一个人情。

Text.en: You need not thank me, Matiki. I am just glad I got here in time to help you.
Text.zh: 无需感谢我，玛提吉。我只是庆幸能及时赶到相助。

Text.en: Humble too! Hmm... Well, I shall thank you. I would honour you in the best way I can. <continue>I offer you the blessing of our ancestors. They know the true merit of a warrior!
Text.zh: 还如此谦逊！嗯……我必须感谢你。我要以最崇高的方式向你致敬。<continue>我献上先祖的祝福。他们懂得识别真正的勇士！

Text.en: <i>{Tala moana}, my dear! You have travelled far, haven't you!
Text.zh: <i>{愿你安好}，我的孩子！你远道而来，是吗！

Text.en: Well, you're awfully cheery for someone who was almost eaten alive.
Text.zh: 对一个差点葬身怪物之口的人来说，你倒是挺乐观。

Text.en: Ah, yes... almost! It seems old Matiki has more to do in life before he is summoned to the Halls of the Dead.
Text.zh: 啊，是啊……差点！看来老玛提吉在被召唤到亡者之境前还有事要做。

Text.en: Halls of the Dead? Okay, you have my attention.
Text.zh: 亡者之境？好吧，你引起了我的兴趣。

Text.en: You see, I'm an Elder of the Tukohama tribe. And I have something in mind for you... <continue>I would honour you the best way I can, dear lady. With the blessing of our ancestors! <continue>They can tell if someone is worthy of their honour, and you have strength in you.
Text.zh: 你看，我是图克哈玛部落的长老。我对你有个想法……<continue>我要以最崇高的方式向你致敬，亲爱的女士。用先祖的祝福向你致敬！<continue>他们能看出谁配得上他们的荣耀，而你身怀无上力量。

Text.en: <i>{Tala moana}, tale-woman! It's been many a year since old Matiki has seen one of your kind in the flesh!
Text.zh: <i>{愿你安好}，说书人！老玛提吉许多年没亲眼见过你这样的人了！

Text.en: Not quite a tale-woman but... it is a fair assumption. Greetings to you, Matiki.
Text.zh: 不算是说书人，不过……这推测倒也合理。向你致敬，玛提吉。

Text.en: There is always more to the story. I knew that even before I was an Elder of the Tukohama tribe! <continue>Now... I must honour you the best way that I can. With the blessing of our ancestors! <continue>If you are worthy, they will see you for who you are... and more. Quite clever really.
Text.zh: 故事总有后续。早在我成为图克哈玛部落长老前就明白这个道理！<continue>现在……我必须以最崇高的方式向你致敬。用先祖的祝福向你致敬！<continue>若你配得上，他们会看透你的本质……甚至更多。确实很巧妙。

Text.en: <i>{Tala moana}, young man! An impressive display you put on there.
Text.zh: <i>{愿你安好}，年轻人！你刚才的表现令人瞩目。

Text.en: Well... that display saved your life, it seems.
Text.zh: 嗯……看起来那表现救了你一命。

Text.en: Indeed it did! My name is Matiki, an Elder of the Tukohama tribe. And I must thank you for saving my life.
Text.zh: 的确如此！我是玛提吉，图克哈玛部落的长老。我必须感谢你救命之恩。

Text.en: I accept your thanks.
Text.zh: 我接受你的谢意。

Text.en: Oh, no, no! I can't leave that debt unpaid. I would honour you the best way that I can... <continue>With the blessing of our ancestors! Prove yourself to them. Show them your might.
Text.zh: 哦，不不不！我不能让这份恩情不得到回报。我要以最崇高的方式向你致敬……<continue>用先祖的祝福！向他们证明自己。展示你的实力。

Text.en: I'm unfamiliar with Karui traditions. Enlighten me.
Text.zh: 我不熟悉卡鲁的传统。请指教。

Text.en: <i>{Tala moana}, stranger! My eyes could barely keep up with you. Remarkable!
Text.zh: <i>{愿你安好}，陌生人！我的眼睛几乎跟不上你的动作。了不起！

Text.en: You are safe, for now. Best you get going.
Text.zh: 你现在安全了。最好赶紧离开。

Text.en: Oh, no. I must thank you first. As an Elder of the Tukohama tribe, I have something in mind...
Text.zh: 哦，不行。我必须先感谢你。作为图克哈玛部落的长老，我有个安排……

Text.en: Please. It isn't necessary... I can just be on my way and then you never have to–
Text.zh: 拜托，真没必要……我这就走，你不用特意再……

Text.en: You'll have the opportunity to gain the blessing of our ancestors!
Text.zh: 你将有机会获得先祖的祝福！

Text.en: But I really don't–
Text.zh: 但我真的不用——

Text.en: Yes! A perfect reward, indeed.
Text.zh: 没错！这确实是完美的报答。

Text.en: Guess there's no way around this. Go on, then.
Text.zh: 看来免不了了。那就继续吧。

Text.en: Do my eyes tell lies? Do I see an Azmeri before me?
Text.zh: 我眼花了吗？我面前站的是阿兹莫里人？

Text.en: Your eyes tell the truth.
Text.zh: 你没看错。

Text.en: Wow. What a sight for Matiki's old eyes. As an Elder of the Tukohama tribe, age tends to reveal fewer surprises as the years pass.
Text.zh: 哇。这可让玛提吉的老眼开了眼界。作为图克哈玛部落的长老，年纪越大，惊喜就越少。

Text.en: Elder? I'm truly honoured to meet you. Even more so to have saved you.
Text.zh: 长老？能见到你真是我的荣幸。能救你更是如此。

Text.en: Ah, the grace of the Azmeri. Not something often spoken of. But your people and mine, have an abundance of similarities. <continue>Now... I must thank you for what you've done this day.
Text.zh: 啊，阿兹莫里人的风度。不常被人提及。但你的族人和我们有许多相似之处。<continue>现在……我必须为你今日的善举致谢。

Text.en: It's not needed, you know...
Text.zh: 你知道，这没必要……

Text.en: You shall have the opportunity to gain the blessing of our ancestors! <continue>Prove your worth to them. Show them what you showed me here.
Text.zh: 你将有机会获得先祖的祝福！<continue>向他们证明你的价值。展示你刚才向我展示的本领。

Text.en: <i>{Tala moana}, outsider! I can't quite make out your origins... hmm...
Text.zh: <i>{愿你安好}，外乡人！我看不出你的来历……嗯……

Text.en: Just out here doing what I can. Doesn't really matter where I'm from.
Text.zh: 我只是在这里尽力而为。来自何方并不重要。

Text.en: True enough. Old Matiki can't help his curiosity. <continue>I've been an Elder of the Tukohama tribe for many years and it's not often I get stumped like this.
Text.zh: 说得对。老玛提吉就是控制不住好奇心。<continue>我担任图克哈玛部落长老多年，很少遇到如此让我摸不着头脑的情况。

Text.en: An Elder, eh? Tavakai would likely be pleased for you to be returned in one piece...
Text.zh: 长老，是吗？塔瓦凯看到你平安归来会很高兴的……

Text.en: Oh, yes. I'm sure the Chieftain had concerns. But first...
Text.zh: 哦，是的。我确信酋长很担心。但首先……

Text.en: I must thank you for your good deed here. You've honoured me. I must honour you back. <continue>I will give you the opportunity to gain the blessing of our ancestors! <continue>Show them your prowess. They will see you for who you are.
Text.zh: 我必须为你在此的善行致谢。你给了我荣耀。我也必须回报你荣耀。<continue>我将给你获得先祖祝福的机会！<continue>向他们展示你的英勇。他们会看清你的本质。

Text.en: Uhh... hopefully they don't see too much.
Text.zh: 呃……希望他们别看得太深。

Text.en: <i>{Tala moana!} Do my eyes deceive me, or is that a Druid of Skothe I see?
Text.zh: <i>{塔拉，莫阿娜！} 我没看错吧？眼前这位可是斯科提的德鲁伊？

Text.en: Aye. Of... Caer Tarth.
Text.zh: 正是。来自…塞尔塔斯。

Text.en: Thought your kind were all but wiped out.
Text.zh: 还以为你们这一脉早已绝迹。

Text.en: We were.
Text.zh: 确实如此。

Text.en: Then lucky me - one of the last Druids of Skothe on Wraeclast, here to rescue me. <continue>I am an Elder of the Tukohama Tribe. It grieves me, what became of your people.
Text.zh: 那我可真是走运——瓦拉克拉斯上斯科提的德鲁伊最后的血脉，竟来出手相救。<continue>我是图克哈玛部落的长老。听闻斯科提群岛上你们族人的遭遇.……实在令人扼腕。

Text.en: You heard about that?
Text.zh: 你听说过那件事？

Text.en: Of course I did! We Karui may keep to ourselves, but that does not mean we close our eyes to the world beyond our shores. <continue>Now allow me to repay you. Old Matiki offers you the chance to earn the blessing of our ancestors!
Text.zh: 我岂能不知！我们卡鲁人虽偏安一隅，却从未对海外之事不闻不问。<continue>现在请让我回报这份恩情——老玛提吉愿为你争取先祖赐福的机会！

Text.en: Let us return to your ship. I will show you the way to our most sacred place... the Eye of Hinekora. <continue>From there... I will speak for you to enter our Halls of the Dead. You will be tested. But also, rewarded.
Text.zh: 我们回到你的船上去。我会给你指路前往我们最神圣的地方……辛格拉之眼。<continue>从那里……我会为你引荐，进入我们的亡者之境。你会接受考验。但也会获得奖赏。

Text.en: Ah, Makoru! It is good to see you.
Text.zh: 啊，马克鲁！见到你真好。

Text.en: Matiki! You're alive! I'd heard you were missing.
Text.zh: 玛提吉！你还活着！我听说你失踪了。

Text.en: Yes. They told me I was too old to keep taking out my canoe... maybe they were right.
Text.zh: 是的。他们说我太老了，不该再驾独木舟出海……也许他们说得对。

Text.en: From canoe to captured... what happened out there?
Text.zh: 从泛舟到被囚……你究竟遭遇了什么？

Text.en: You went out on a canoe... and ended up <i>{there?}
Text.zh: 你划着小舟出海……结果跑到<i>{那种地方}去了？

Text.en: Tell us, Matiki... how did you find yourself caged?
Text.zh: 告诉我们，玛提吉……你是怎么被关起来的？

Text.en: From canoeing to trapped in a cage... Explain yourself.
Text.zh: 从划独木舟到被困笼中……你具体讲讲吧。

Text.en: Sounds like you had a rough day at sea.
Text.zh: 听起来你在海上度过了糟糕的一天。

Text.en: Matiki... how'd you end up caged like that?
Text.zh: 玛提吉……你怎么会被关在笼子里？

Text.en: Went out for a nice row, then found yourself a prisoner, eh?
Text.zh: 本想惬意地划个船，结果却成了阶下囚，对吧？

Text.en: Rowed your canoe right into a cage, did ye?
Text.zh: 划着独木舟自投罗网，对吧？

Text.en: It is quite the tale... <continue>... and I was weak. I admit. <continue>When the fog swept over the water... I should've known what I saw wasn't real! <continue>But there she was. My Rakana. Sweet and strong as the day we met. <continue>She plowed on through the fog. And I chased her like she was alive! Like we were young again. <continue>Then... crash! Next thing I know... I'm wet as anything and preparing to be bird food!
Text.zh: 这说来话长了……<continue>……我那时很脆弱，我承认这一点。<continue>当迷雾笼罩海面时……我本该知道眼前所见并非真实！<continue>但她就在那里，我的拉卡娜。美丽而坚毅，如同我们初遇时一般。<continue>她穿透迷雾而来。而我如痴如醉地追随着她，仿佛她依然在世！仿佛我们重回青春年华。<continue>然后……轰然一击！等我回过神来……全身湿透，简直像是专程来给海鸟加餐的！

Text.en: It is quite the tale... <continue>... and I was weak. I admit. <continue>When the fog swept over the water... I should've known what I saw wasn't real! <continue>But there she was. My Rakana. Sweet and strong as the day we met. <continue>She plowed on through the fog. And I chased her like she was alive! Like we were young again. <continue>Then... crash! Next thing I know... I'm wet as anything and cooped up in that bloody cage.
Text.zh: 这说来话长了……<continue>……我那时很脆弱，我承认这一点。<continue>当迷雾笼罩海面时……我本该知道眼前所见并非真实！<continue>但她就在那里，我的拉卡娜。美丽而坚毅，如同我们初遇时一般。<continue>她穿透迷雾而来。而我如痴如醉地追随着她，仿佛她依然在世！仿佛我们重回青春年华。<continue>然后……轰然一击！等我回过神来……全身湿透，被困在那可恶的牢笼之中。

Text.en: It is quite the tale... <continue>... and I was weak. I admit. <continue>When the fog swept over the water... I should've known what I saw wasn't real! <continue>But there she was. My Rakana. Sweet and strong as the day we met. <continue>She plowed on through the fog. And I chased her like she was alive! Like we were young again. <continue>Then... crash! Next thing I know... I'm inside that damn prison counting my blessings!
Text.zh: 这说来话长了……<continue>……我那时很脆弱，我承认这一点。<continue>当迷雾笼罩海面时……我本该知道眼前所见并非真实！<continue>但她就在那里，我的拉卡娜。美丽而坚毅，如同我们初遇时一般。<continue>她穿透迷雾而来。而我如痴如醉地追随着她，仿佛她依然在世！仿佛我们重回青春年华。<continue>然后……轰然一击！等我回过神来……我已被关在那该死的牢笼里，庆幸自己捡回条命！

Text.en: It is quite the tale... <continue>... and I was weak. I admit. <continue>When the fog swept over the water... I should've known what I saw wasn't real! <continue>But there she was. My Rakana. Sweet and strong as the day we met. <continue>She plowed on through the fog. And I chased her like she was alive! Like we were young again. <continue>Then... crash! Next thing I know... I'm wet as anything and held prisoner in that bloody cave. <continue>And let me tell you... it was hard to keep my eyes to myself, heh.
Text.zh: 这说来话长了……<continue>……我那时很脆弱，我承认这一点。<continue>当迷雾笼罩海面时……我本该知道眼前所见并非真实！<continue>但她就在那里，我的拉卡娜。美丽而坚毅，如同我们初遇时一般。<continue>她穿透迷雾而来。而我如痴如醉地追随着她，仿佛她依然在世！仿佛我们重回青春年华。<continue>然后……轰然一击！等我回过神来……全身湿透，被困在那可恶的牢笼之中。<continue>说实话…在那种地方管住自己的眼睛可真不容易，呵呵。

Text.en: Good thing our patron here got there when they did.
Text.zh: 幸而我们的恩人及时赶到。

Text.en: Yes! And about that... <continue>There is the small matter of what I've promised our hero here...
Text.zh: 是的！说到这个……<continue>关于我向我们的英雄许下的承诺，还有个小小的麻烦……

Text.en: Why do I get the feeling this involves me?
Text.zh: 为何我有种预感这会牵扯到我？

Text.en: Show me your map, Makoru. There's a place I want to take our friend, when they have the time.
Text.zh: 把你的海图给我看看，马克鲁。有个地方，等我们的朋友有空时，我想带他们去。

Text.en: Yes... there it is. The Eye of Hinekora. Gateway... to the Halls of the Dead!
Text.zh: 是的……就在那里。辛格拉之眼。通往……亡者之境的门扉！

Text.en: You want to take them to the Karui's most sacred place? Were you hit on the head?
Text.zh: 你要带他们去卡鲁最为神圣的圣地？你脑子被撞坏了吗？

Text.en: Trust me. My hero here... is special. They deserve a chance to prove themselves to our ancestors.
Text.zh: 相信我。我们的英雄……与众不同。他们理应有机会向我们的先祖证明自己。

Text.en: Tavakai will lose his mind if he finds out about this...
Text.zh: 要是塔瓦凯知道了这事，他会气疯的……

Text.en: Ah. Well, perhaps in this case it's best for him to only hear what he needs to hear.
Text.zh: 唉。或许在这种情况下，最好只让他知道该知道的事情。

Text.en: Alright, but after this I'm taking you back to Ngakanu.
Text.zh: 好吧，不过事后我得带你回纳卡努。

Text.en: Yes, yes... that's fine.
Text.zh: 好的，好的……没问题。

Text.en: We call this place... the Eye of Hinekora. Far below, Hinekora herself resides. <continue>She, The Mother of Death, sees the choices we have made, and from that... has foresight of the path ahead. <continue>Now. This here... this is Navali. She is the Speaker. The word of the will of Hinekora. <continue>Oh! And that on her shoulder... that's Yama. He's a good boy.
Text.zh: 我们称这地方为……辛格拉之眼。在遥远的深处，辛格拉本尊栖息于此。<continue>她，死亡之母，洞察我们所作的抉择，并由此……预见前路的走向。<continue>现在，这位……是娜瓦莉。她是神谕者，辛格拉意志的传声者。<continue>哦！还有她肩头那位……那是雅玛，是个乖孩子。

Text.en: I am honoured to meet you, Navali. And Yama.
Text.zh: 见到你们我深感荣幸，娜瓦莉，还有雅玛。

Text.en: Greetings, Speaker. I cannot wait to meet your goddess. And hello, monkey.
Text.zh: 你好，神谕者。我迫不及待想要拜见你的女神。你好，小猴子。

Text.en: Good day, Navali. Greetings, Yama.
Text.zh: 日安，娜瓦莉。向你致意，雅玛。

Text.en: You are a curious sort, Navali. I am intrigued to know more about your master. And... Yes, hello Yama.
Text.zh: 你很有趣，娜瓦莉。我很想了解更多关于你主人的事。还有……对，你好，小猴子。

Text.en: Hello, Navali and Yama. If I get the chance, I look forward to asking Hinekora some rather... personal questions.
Text.zh: 你好，娜瓦莉和雅玛。如果有机会，我很想向辛格拉请教一些相当……私人的问题。

Text.en: Spirit watch over you, Navali. And monkey.
Text.zh: 愿万灵庇佑你，娜瓦莉，还有……你这小猴子。

Text.en: Well, pleased to meet you Navali. Also, nice monkey.
Text.zh: 很高兴认识你，娜瓦莉。还有你这可爱的猴子。

Text.en: Well met, lass. And the wee lad on yer shoulder too.
Text.zh: 你好啊姑娘，还有你肩膀上那个小不点。

Text.en: The Elder's tale of your victory is well received. He vouches for you. But that is not quite enough... <continue>The Mother of Death must decide if you may enter. <continue>Step forth. Look into the bowl. Hinekora will see you. <continue>All of you.
Text.zh: 长老述说你英勇事迹的故事广受赞誉。他为你作保。然而，这还远远不够……<continue>死亡之母将决定你是否可以进入。<continue>上前来，凝视圣碗。辛格拉将审视你。<continue>审视你的全部。

Text.en: Hinekora... has spoken. You are worthy! Prove to our ancestors that which I already know to be true.
Text.zh: 辛格拉……已然开口。你配得上这份荣耀！去向我们的先祖证明我早已明了的真理吧。

Text.en: Wait... am I dead?
Text.zh: 等等……我死了？

Text.en: What... What just happened? Am I dead?
Text.zh: 什么……刚才发生了什么？我死了？

Text.en: Why am I on the ground!?
Text.zh: 我怎么倒在地上了！？

Text.en: I... am dead, aren't I?
Text.zh: 我……死了，对吧？

Text.en: What just happened? Why is my body <i>{there}?
Text.zh: 刚才发生了什么？为什么我的肉体<i>{在那边}？

Text.en: What in the Spirit is goin' on here?!
Text.zh: 到底是怎么回事！？

Text.en: Well blimey. I'm bloody well dead, aren't I?
Text.zh: 天哪，我真的死透了，是吧？

Text.en: If that's <i>{me} lyin' <i>{there}... then I'm... dead?
Text.zh: 如果躺在<i>{那里}的是<i>{我}……那我现在……已经死了？

Text.en: Oh... in a manner of speaking... yes. You are dead. But do not worry. The Mother of Death has you in her embrace.
Text.zh: 哦……从某种意义上说……没错，你死了。但别担心，死亡之母正拥抱着你。<continue>当你的灵魂踏上征程向先祖证明自己时，你的肉身在此会安然无恙。

Text.en: Well... put simply... you are dead. But do not worry. The Mother of Death has you in her embrace.
Text.zh: 嗯……简单来说……你死了。但别担心，死亡之母正拥抱着你。

Text.en: Your body will be safe here, while your spirit moves forward to prove yourself to our ancestors.
Text.zh: 当你的灵魂踏上征程向先祖证明自己时，你的肉身在此会安然无恙。

Text.en: You might have told me that this was going to happen!
Text.zh: 你本该提前告诉我会有这种事……

Text.en: So this is what Death feels like from the other side...
Text.zh: 所以，死亡是这种感觉……

Text.en: Matiki... you might have told me I was about to die.
Text.zh: 玛提吉……你难道不该提醒我即将面临死亡吗？

Text.en: Death is not nearly so fearsome an experience as I expected.
Text.zh: 死之体验，远不如我预期那般恐怖。

Text.en: Dying was not exactly what I was expecting to happen.
Text.zh: 死亡可不在我的预期之内。

Text.en: I guess I've no choice but to trust in Hinekora.
Text.zh: 这回我真是别无选择，只能相信辛格拉了。

Text.en: I should be mad at ya... But dying ain't so bad, is it? I feel like I lost a bit of weight.
Text.zh: 我该对你发火才对……不过死亡也没那么糟糕，对吧？感觉身体都轻盈了不少。

Text.en: Just the ancestors, is it? Or might I see... others?
Text.zh: 仅有先祖之灵？或许我还能得见……其他亡魂？

Text.en: I didn't want to worry you about it!
Text.zh: 我不想让你太担心！

Text.en: Not too bad, eh?
Text.zh: 还不赖吧？

Text.en: Hmm... maybe I should have, yes. Oh well!
Text.zh: 嗯……或许我确实该提前告知。算了！

Text.en: Surprise! Heheh.
Text.zh: 惊喜吧！嘿嘿。

Text.en: She will look after your soul. Do not worry.
Text.zh: 她会守护你的灵魂，不必担心。

Text.en: Quite soothing really, isn't it?
Text.zh: 其实相当安逸，不是吗？

Text.en: Hah! Yes, lighter on your feet, perhaps?
Text.zh: 哈！很好，脚步都轻盈了，不是吗？

Text.en: Only the ancestors, I'm afraid. The rest remain beyond our sight.
Text.zh: 恐怕唯有先祖之灵。其余亡魂，我们仍然无法窥见。

Text.en: You may enter. We will be watching.
Text.zh: 你可以进入了。我们会守望着你。

Text.en: At the end of our lives, Hinekora catches the <i>{Ngakuramakoi} in her embrace.
Text.zh: 在生命的终点，辛格拉会将<i>{纳库雷默柯}拥入怀中。

Text.en: Death is not the end. The Halls of the Dead are merely the first rest on a long journey.
Text.zh: 死亡并非终结。亡者之境只是漫长旅途中的第一处歇脚地。

Text.en: A journey? To where?
Text.zh: 旅途？通往何方的旅途？

Text.en: Tell me what you know of Death, sister.
Text.zh: 告诉我你对死亡的理解，姐妹。

Text.en: The first rest? What lies after?
Text.zh: 第一处歇脚地？那之后呢？

Text.en: What natural journey is your 'first rest' interfering with?
Text.zh: 你所说的“第一处歇脚地”打断了什么自然进程？

Text.en: You don't rejoin the Spirit? What's next, then?
Text.zh: 你们不重归万灵怀抱吗？接下来是什么？

Text.en: What do you believe happens after?
Text.zh: 你认为死后会发生什么？

Text.en: How long a journey? Further than Trarthus? Kalguur?
Text.zh: 多漫长的旅途？比特拉珊还远？比卡古兰还远？

Text.en: If ye speak true... then <i>{where} has their journey taken them?
Text.zh: 若你所言非虚……那他们的旅途终究通往<i>{何方}？

Text.en: That is not for man or god to know.
Text.zh: 那不是凡人或神明所能知晓的。

Text.en: Ah, in other words, we have no idea, hahaha! <continue>But the Halls give us some comfort from total oblivion, at least.
Text.zh: 啊，换句话说，我们一无所知，哈哈哈！<continue>不过亡者之境至少让我们免于彻底的虚无。

Text.en: The Halls of the Dead are open to the worthy, and their children. <continue>We come here to consult with our ancestors in times of great need.
Text.zh: 亡者之境向配得上的人及其子嗣敞开大门。<continue>我们在极度需要时来此向先祖求教。

Text.en: Your ancestors take heart from seeing their descendants.
Text.zh: 你的先祖从见证后代中获得慰藉。

Text.en: It's good to hear that, but it's not all so kindhearted. <continue>When the sky permits, we come to watch the Trials of the Ancestors. <continue>The living may only see it properly under the light of a full moon.
Text.zh: 听到这话很欣慰，但并非一切都如此温馨。<continue>当苍穹允许时，我们会来观看命运试炼。<continue>生者只有在满月之光下才能真正目睹。

Text.en: The light of the moon? Why is that needed?
Text.zh: 月光？为什么需要月光？

Text.en: The light of a full moon? This deep?
Text.zh: 满月之光？在这么深的地方？

Text.en: Why is that?
Text.zh: 为什么？

Text.en: The moon, eh? How's that work?
Text.zh: 月亮，嗯？怎么做到的？

Text.en: How does the light reach them down here?
Text.zh: 光线是怎么照到这里的？

Text.en: How's that then?
Text.zh: 这是怎么做到的？

Text.en: Moonlight reachin' all the way down here? That's a wonder I'd like to see.
Text.zh: 月光竟能能照到这里？这般奇景，真想亲眼得见。

Text.en: Well, you see... great crystal veins guide the light down on certain nights of the year. <continue>Warrior souls can be seen by the grace of Lani Hua.
Text.zh: 你瞧……巨大的水晶矿脉在一年中特定的夜晚将月光引导至此。<continue>战士的魂灵得以在拉尼·华的恩典下显现。

Text.en: It is a gift from a goddess who cannot be with us in person.
Text.zh: 这是一位无法亲临我们身边的女神赐予的礼物。

Text.en: You'll never see anything like it as long as you live! <continue>All of our greatest warriors from history on the same field, no holding back. <continue>After all, why pull punches? They're already dead!
Text.zh: 只要你还活着，就永远见不到这样的奇观！<continue>我们历史上最伟大的战士齐聚同一战场，毫无保留。<continue>毕竟，何必手下留情？他们早已作古！

Text.en: The Trial is not a sport. It is a sacred tradition.
Text.zh: 试炼并非娱乐，而是神圣的传统。

Text.en: Can't it be both? <continue>Different tribes shoulder-to-shoulder, cheering on their heroes. <continue>We bring meat and ale. We make a real night of it.
Text.zh: 难道两者不能兼而有之吗？<continue>不同部落并肩而立，为各自的英雄喝彩。<continue>我们带来美酒佳肴，尽情狂欢一夜。

Text.en: I suppose there is joy in community and competition. <continue>Just don't speak this way in front of Hinekora.
Text.zh: 我想在团结和竞争中确实能找到快乐。<continue>只是别在辛格拉面前这样说话。

Text.en: I wouldn't dream of it.
Text.zh: 我绝不敢那样做。

Text.en: Surely she hears us here...
Text.zh: 她一定能听到我们在这里说话……

Text.en: Can she hear us? Here?
Text.zh: 她能听到我们在这里说话吗？

Text.en: Can your goddess hear us now?
Text.zh: 你的女神能听到我们吗？

Text.en: Does she hear us here?
Text.zh: 她在这里能听到我们吗？

Text.en: Can she hear us?
Text.zh: 她能听到我们吗？

Text.en: Can Hinekora hear us now?
Text.zh: 辛格拉现在能听到我们吗？

Text.en: What, I thought she could hear us in here.
Text.zh: 什么，我还以为她能在这里听到我们呢。

Text.en: If this is her hall, then surely she hears every word?
Text.zh: 若这里真是她的圣殿，那我们的每句话想必她都听见了？

Text.en: I do.
Text.zh: 我听得到。

Text.en: Praise the Mother of Death!
Text.zh: 赞颂死亡之母！

Text.en: Indeed.
Text.zh: 确实如此。

Text.en: When we speak of our ancestors and their wisdom, it's not just poetic. <continue>All Karui since the time of Tukohama linger here still, in the Halls of the Dead. <continue>Their advice and counsel is our greatest source of strength.
Text.zh: 当我们谈及先祖及其智慧时，这绝非空泛的诗意表达。<continue>自图克哈玛时代以降的所有卡鲁人至今仍驻留于此，在亡者之境之中。<continue>他们的劝告和指引是我们力量的最大源泉。

Text.en: Elder... please tell me, do I have ancestors here?
Text.zh: 长老……请告诉我，我的先祖也在这里吗？

Text.en: Of course. Do you know their names? Their song?
Text.zh: 当然。你知道他们的姓名吗？他们的歌谣？

Text.en: I... do not.
Text.zh: 我……不知道。

Text.en: I'm sorry, child, but there are countless souls here. <continue>Without a name and a tribe, we cannot find them. Would they recognise your face?
Text.zh: 很遗憾，孩子，这里有无数的魂灵。<continue>没有姓名和部落，我们无法寻得他们。他们会认得你的面容吗？

Text.en: No. I was lost as a boy.
Text.zh: 不会。我自幼便失散了。

Text.en: That is most unfortunate. My heart goes out to you. <continue>When this time of trouble is over... I'll ask around on your behalf.
Text.zh: 那真是太不幸了。我的心为你感到痛惜。<continue>等这段动荡时期结束后……我会代你四处打听。

Text.en: I underestimated the Karui. Necromancy is woven into the fabric of your culture.
Text.zh: 我小看了卡鲁人。通灵法术已深深融入你们的文化根基。

Text.en: We have something like this, the <i>{barya.} Not on this scale, of course. We mainly use them as punishment for criminals...
Text.zh: 我们也有类似的东西，叫作<i>{巨灵。}当然体型没这么大。<continue>我们主要用来惩罚罪犯……

Text.en: It seems wrong to keep souls out of the natural order.
Text.zh: 让魂灵脱离自然循环似乎是不对的。

Text.en: When we Azmeri die, we rejoin the Spirit. Our voices become one.
Text.zh: 当我们阿兹莫里人死去时，会重新融入万灵。我们的声音归于一体。

Text.en: Ah, that is how your Way can be enforced so strongly. The knowledge is not lost.
Text.zh: 啊，这就是为什么你们的道途能够延绵千秋。知识不会失传。

Text.en: Think I'd rather take a dirt nap. Imagine never getting a break from being <i>{you.}
Text.zh: 我宁可长眠地下。想象一下永远无法摆脱<i>{自己}。

Text.en: Ye speak as though none are ever truly gone. Yet... I cannae help but wonder if some losses cut too deep, even for these Halls.
Text.zh: 听你之言，仿佛逝者从未真正离去。可……我不禁怀疑，有些失去带来的伤痕太深，恐怕连这圣殿也无法容纳。

Text.en: Necro what now? Well... I don't know about that.
Text.zh: 死灵什么？呃……我可搞不懂这些。

Text.en: This place is no punishment... It is a blessing!
Text.zh: 待在这儿不是受罪……而是恩赐！

Text.en: For us, this is natural.
Text.zh: 对我们来说，这很自然。

Text.en: One voice for all your people? That'd never work for the Karui...
Text.zh: 全体族人共享一个声音？这对卡鲁族根本行不通……

Text.en: Yes. No teaching is lost if the teacher lives on!
Text.zh: 没错。导师永存，他的教诲就绝不会失传！

Text.en: It is a curious thought. But... you can always strive to keep learning something new!
Text.zh: 你的想法倒挺新鲜。可是……你可以坚持不懈地学习新事物啊！

Text.en: Grief is heavy. But even the deepest cut leaves a scar, not an absence.
Text.zh: 悲痛固然沉重。但即便最深的痛苦，最终留下的也是伤疤，而非空无。

Text.en: In anycase, my feelings on all this <i>{are} complicated, but the Way was established long before my time. <continue>Sometimes it really just is... what it is.
Text.zh: 无论如何，我对这一切的感受<i>{很}复杂，但卡鲁之道早在我的时代之前就已确立。<continue>有时候事情就是……如此而已。

Text.en: The gods were once mortal. They hunted, fished, and ate like we do. <continue>Many of Tukohama's deeds were achieved <i>{before} his ascension.
Text.zh: 众神曾经也是凡人。他们和我们一样狩猎、捕鱼、进食。<continue>图克哈玛的诸多功业都是在他升华<i>{之前}完成的。

Text.en: What were his deeds?
Text.zh: 他的功业有哪些？

Text.en: I suppose one must achieve great things to become divine.
Text.zh: 我想一个人必须成就非凡之事才能成神。

Text.en: Yes. Indeed.
Text.zh: 对，没错。

Text.en: Tell me. I'll carry his tale.
Text.zh: 请告诉我，我会传承他的传奇。

Text.en: As just a man? What did he do?
Text.zh: 作为一介凡人？他做了什么？

Text.en: And what did he achieve, as a mortal?
Text.zh: 他作为凡人成就了什么？

Text.en: What exactly was he capable of doing as a mere mortal?
Text.zh: 作为一个普通的凡人，他究竟能做到什么？

Text.en: Ah yeah. What'd that fella get up to then?
Text.zh: 啊对，那家伙到底干了些什么？

Text.en: And what were his deeds?
Text.zh: 他究竟有何事迹？

Text.en: During the Great Wasting, he united the tribes. <continue>He put axes in our hands, and taught us to survive. <continue>But he was not just a... bringer of violence. No. <continue>He was a bringer of hope. Hope among all the tribes. Some forget that.
Text.zh: 在大荒废的岁月里，他联合了分散的部落。<continue>他将战斧置于我们掌中，传授我们生存之道。<continue>然而他绝非仅仅是……杀戮的使者。绝非如此。<continue>他是希望的使者，点燃各部族心中的希望之火。有些人已将此遗忘。

Text.en: Some <i>{choose} to forget.
Text.zh: 有些人<i>{刻意选择}遗忘。

Text.en: Exactly.
Text.zh: 说得没错。

Text.en: Each tribe has its own story of the Great Wasting. <continue>Some call it a Great Fire, or the Barren Centuries. Others, an Endless Winter. <continue>The truth lies buried in all these tales. There was an ancient time of horror and famine. <continue>But in those dark years, our gods first stepped forward. Tukohama, Hinekora, Tawhoa, and the rest.
Text.zh: 每个部落都流传着各自的大荒废传说。<continue>有的称其为大焚毁，或是荒芜纪元。还有的叫它永夜寒冬。<continue>真相深埋于这些传说之中。那是一个充满恐怖与饥荒的远古时代。<continue>正是在那些至暗时刻，我们的神明初次现身。图克哈马、辛格拉、塔赫亚，还有其他诸神。

Text.en: The Karui had no gods before that?
Text.zh: 卡鲁人在那之前没有神明吗？

Text.en: <i>{All} your gods appeared then? There weren't any before that?
Text.zh: 你们<i>{所有的}神明都是那时降临的？此前一个都没有？

Text.en: We call it the Winter of the World... The Maraketh had no gods before that. Did you?
Text.zh: 我们称之为世界之冬……马拉克斯人在那之前也没有神明。你们呢？

Text.en: You had no gods before the Great Wasting?
Text.zh: 你们在大荒废之前就没有神明？

Text.en: We know the Winter. Did you not have gods before then?
Text.zh: 我们了解那个寒冬。你们之前也没有神明吗？

Text.en: Curious. All your gods arose in the same era? None before that?
Text.zh: 真奇怪。你们的神明全都在同一个时代现身？此前竟无一神？

Text.en: You telling me you didn't have gods before these bad times?
Text.zh: 你的意思是在那些灾难降临前，你们就没有神明？

Text.en: And afore that? Ye had no gods at all?
Text.zh: 在那之前呢？难道你们当时完全不敬神祇吗？

Text.en: No. For us, history begins there. <continue>The Great Wasting erased all that came before. <continue>Maybe... there wasn't even a <i>{before,} for us. <continue>When did the first Karui emerge from that volcano? <continue>Something to ask Hinekora, Navali.
Text.zh: 没有。对我们而言，历史正是从那时开始。<continue>大荒废湮灭了此前的一切记忆。<continue>或许……对我们来说根本就没有<i>{此前}。<continue>最初的卡鲁人何时从那座火山中诞生？<continue>这种问题该去问辛格拉，娜瓦莉。

Text.en: The Mother of Death can recall only her own memories. <continue>History begins at the rise of the gods, because that is when Hinekora was born.
Text.zh: 死亡之母只能追忆她自身的记忆。<continue>历史始于众神的兴起，因为那正是辛格拉诞生之时。

Text.en: Hmm. Curious. <continue>So the End of Time, as she recalls it, is really... her death.
Text.zh: 嗯，真是奇特。<continue>那么，按她的回忆，时间的终结，实际上就是……她的陨落。

Text.en: Yes.
Text.zh: 正是如此。

Text.en: So... you did it! I knew you could. I knew... you would.
Text.zh: 那么……你成功了！我就知道你能行。我早知道……你一定可以。

Text.en: This has been exhilarating. It was an honour to meet Hinekora.
Text.zh: 这经历真是激动人心。能见证辛格拉实在是我的殊荣。

Text.en: The old woman's got true power. I wouldn't mind picking her brain someday.
Text.zh: 这老太婆拥有真正的神力。哪天我倒想跟她好好聊聊。

Text.en: Hinekora... is the true tale-woman of this world and beyond. It is... humbling.
Text.zh: 辛格拉……她是这个世界乃至更广阔天地的真正传说述者。这让人……深感敬畏。

Text.en: I see now. I'm part of something bigger. Hinekora has shown me that.
Text.zh: 我现在领悟了。我原来是宏大事物的一部分。辛格拉让我明白了这点。

Text.en: It's wondrous. As if, somehow, maybe... the Spirit knew I would end up here. Seeing her.
Text.zh: 太神奇了。仿佛，不知为何，或许……万灵早就知道我会来到这里。见证她的存在。

Text.en: I was destined for this. To be here, witnessing all I have seen. I am now certain of it.
Text.zh: 我命定来到此地，有此经历，见证所见。我坚信不疑。

Text.en: Not much impresses me anymore, but Miss Hinekora... now that's someone worth believing in.
Text.zh: 已经很少有事物能震撼我了，但辛格拉女士……她确实值得信仰。

Text.en: Saw right through me, she did. Spirit, scars and all.
Text.zh: 她可真是把我看透了——从灵魂到伤疤，一切无所遁形。

Text.en: Ah, yes. The Mother of Death is truly a wonder. I hope you have enjoyed your time here... <continue>And perhaps learned a little along the way. <continue>I look forward to seeing what you achieve next.
Text.zh: 啊，没错。死亡之母确实是个奇迹。希望你在这里过得愉快……<continue>或许也在这过程中有所收获。<continue>我很期待看到你接下来的成就。

Text.en: So... you did it! I knew you could. I knew... you would. I am proud of you!
Text.zh: 那么……你成功了！我就知道你能行。我早知道……你会的。我为你感到骄傲！

Text.en: Did what?
Text.zh: 成功什么？

Text.en: Oh... no matter. Not really anything significant. Hah.
Text.zh: 哦……无关紧要。真的没什么大不了的，哈。

Text.en: How does one become an Elder?
Text.zh: 怎样才能成为长老？

Text.en: So, are you just well aged, or is there a ritual required to become an Elder?
Text.zh: 那么，你只是因为年长，还是需要经过某种仪式才能成为长老？

Text.en: Is your title of Elder a special duty? How does one become such a thing?
Text.zh: 长老这个称号是特殊的职责吗？怎样才能获得这样的地位？

Text.en: Tell me - how did you become an Elder?
Text.zh: 请告诉我，你是怎么成为长老的？

Text.en: We've Elders, too. How'd you become one?
Text.zh: 我们那里也有长老。你是如何成为长老的？

Text.en: Why do they call you Elder? Is it a title? How did you earn it?
Text.zh: 他们为什么称呼你长老？这是个头衔吗？你是怎么获得的？

Text.en: Is Elder a rank? Did you work your way up or somethin'?
Text.zh: 长老是个等级吗？你是一步步晋升上来的吗？

Text.en: So what makes an Elder? Just the number o' wrinkles, or what ye've learned from them?
Text.zh: 所以怎样才能成为长老？光看脸上褶子够多，还是得看你从岁月里学到了多少真东西？

Text.en: Becoming an Elder... hmm. Good question.
Text.zh: 成为长老……嗯，这问题问得好。

Text.en: It simply requires being old... and not dead yet.
Text.zh: 只需要活得够久……还没死掉。

Text.en: Oh come now, Kaimana. A bit of wisdom as well.
Text.zh: 得了吧，凯玛纳。还得有点智慧才行。

Text.en: Yes! I have plenty of <i>{all} that.
Text.zh: 没错！这些我<i>{全都}有。

Text.en: I am just making fun, yes? Matiki became an Elder during the worst plague of blood fever these shores have ever seen... Hmmm. <continue>He gave himself. All of himself. Without a care, yes? <continue>I still don't know how he survived. <continue>But many would not have, had he not been so brave... and well, so... wise.
Text.zh: 我只是开个玩笑罢了，对吧？玛提吉成为长老时，正值这片海岸遭遇史上最严重的血热病……嗯。<continue>他献出了自己，奉献了一切。毫不顾惜，对吧？<continue>我至今不明白他是怎么活下来的。<continue>但若非他如此勇敢……如此……睿智，许多人都活不到今天。

Text.en: You honour me, Kaimana.
Text.zh: 你过奖了，凯玛纳。

Text.en: Yes. Deserved.
Text.zh: 确实，实至名归。

Text.en: It was wonderful to see those Makoru and Kanu again... it has been some time.
Text.zh: 能再次见到他们俩真是太好了……已经很久没见了。

Text.en: What keeps them away?
Text.zh: 是什么让他们远离此地？

Text.en: I assumed they weren't welcome here.
Text.zh: 我还以为他们不被允许来这里。

Text.en: Are they forbidden to enter this place?
Text.zh: 他们被禁止踏入这个地方吗？

Text.en: They're allowed to come here?
Text.zh: 他们有权来这里吗？

Text.en: What keeps them away?
Text.zh: 是什么让他们远离？

Text.en: Too long, perhaps? I wonder what keeps them from visiting.
Text.zh: 时间太久了吗？我好奇是什么阻止了他们前来。

Text.en: Do they have permission to visit?
Text.zh: 他们有权限进入吗？

Text.en: Are they not welcome here?
Text.zh: 他们在此不受待见吗？

Text.en: We don't stop them.
Text.zh: 我们并不阻拦他们。

Text.en: That's right. We don't. It is their choice not to come.
Text.zh: 说得对，我们不阻拦。是他们自己选择不来。

Text.en: But that is not the whole story, mmm? <continue>They left the tribe. They bear that burden. <continue>They know where your heart is, Tavakai.
Text.zh: 但这并非事情的全貌，对吧？<continue>他们离开了部落，背负着那份重担。<continue>他们明白你的心意何在，塔瓦凯。

Text.en: Yes. Though they <i>{know} they can come, it does not mean they <i>{feel} they can.
Text.zh: 是的。虽然他们<i>{知晓}自己可以前来，但这不代表他们<i>{感觉}自己可以。

Text.en: It is still their choice. And they have made it.
Text.zh: 这依然是他们的选择。他们已经做出了决定。

Text.en: Yes. They have. For now.
Text.zh: 是的，他们确实如此。至少现在是这样。

Text.en: I am Kaimana, yes! You are welcome here, but... no further! Beyond remains a sacred place to our people. <continue>By our Way, to pass through, a newcomer must sing our tribe's song of greeting, yes! <continue>And... I am quite sure that you do not know it.
Text.zh: 我是凯玛纳，没错！你在这里是受欢迎的，不过……不能再往前了！前方依然是我族的圣地。<continue>按照我们的传统，要想通过，外来者必须吟唱我们部落的迎宾歌谣，对吧！<continue>而且……我很确定你并不会唱。

Text.en: I know a song or two... but not that one.
Text.zh: 我会唱一两首歌……但不是那一首。

Text.en: A song? I could sing. I... I just don't want to.
Text.zh: 一首歌？我倒是能唱，只是……我不太想唱。

Text.en: Yes, tale-woman. It is true. I do not know your song.
Text.zh: 是的，说书人。我确实不懂你们的歌谣。

Text.en: Indeed. I am not much of a singer.
Text.zh: 确实如此。我不太擅长唱歌。

Text.en: Fine. I'll just be on my way then.
Text.zh: 好吧，那我就继续赶路了。

Text.en: I know many songs. But sadly, not yours.
Text.zh: 我懂很多歌曲，可惜不包括你们的。

Text.en: I can hold a tune or two, after a few drinks at least...
Text.zh: 我倒是能哼上几句，特别是喝了几杯之后……

Text.en: Ye'll get no argument from me. My voice would curdle the sea itself.
Text.zh: 我岂会反驳。就我这破锣嗓子，一开口怕是小孩都会吓哭。

Text.en: Ahahaha! I'm sure.
Text.zh: 哈哈哈哈！我相信如此。

Text.en: Wait... what do you have there? <continue>Is that... a fin? From Whakapanu Island?
Text.zh: 等等……你手里拿的是什么？<continue>那是……鱼鳍？来自瓦卡帕努岛的？

Text.en: You recognise it? Could it be useful?
Text.zh: 你认得出来？它可能有什么用处吗？

Text.en: Um... yes?
Text.zh: 呃……是的？

Text.en: It is. Does it hold a hidden use?
Text.zh: 没错。它有什么特殊用途吗？

Text.en: Yes, that's where I found it.
Text.zh: 对，我就是在那里找到的。

Text.en: It is. What of it?
Text.zh: 是的，怎么了？

Text.en: Yes, it is. Do you need it?
Text.zh: 对对，就是这个。你需要它吗？

Text.en: Uh, I think so. Is it important?
Text.zh: 呃，应该是吧。它很重要吗？

Text.en: Aye, the very same.
Text.zh: 对，就是它。

Text.en: Blazing <i>{Valako!} I don't believe it. I was seeking <i>{something} from that beast for my next elixir. <continue>I am experimenting day in... day out... yes? Trying to find a cure...
Text.zh: 燃烧的<i>{瓦拉克！}真不敢相信。我一直在寻找那头巨兽身上的<i>{某种东西}来调制我的新药剂。<continue>我日夜不休地在做实验……对吧？努力寻找治疗之法……

Text.en: A cure?
Text.zh: 治疗之法？

Text.en: A cure? Of course... for what?
Text.zh: 治疗之法？当然了……治疗什么病？

Text.en: A cure? For what sickness?
Text.zh: 治疗之法？治什么病？

Text.en: A cure? For what ailment?
Text.zh: 治疗之法？治疗什么病症？

Text.en: What kind of cure?
Text.zh: 什么样的治疗之法？

Text.en: A cure? For what?
Text.zh: 治疗之法？治疗什么？

Text.en: Someone got a sickness?
Text.zh: 有人生病了？

Text.en: A cure, ye say? For what ill?
Text.zh: 你是说，治疗之法？能治什么病？

Text.en: Why... the blood fever, of course! It has been the death of many of our people of late... <continue>I am under Tavakai's order... to cure it! Quite the task, hmmm? <continue>Please, let me have it. I have something for you in return!
Text.zh: 当然……是血热病！这病最近夺走了我们很多族人的性命……<continue>我奉塔瓦凯之命……要治愈此病！这可是个艰巨的任务，嗯？<continue>拜托，把它给我吧。我会给你些东西作为回报！

Text.en: Many blessings. Thank you. Now... I must see what I can do with it.
Text.zh: 万分感激。谢谢你。现在……我得看看能用它做些什么。

Text.en: Though you have done me a great deed... perhaps don't stay <i>{too} long. <continue>Tavakai can get a bit upset with unexpected guests... hmmm?
Text.zh: 虽然你帮了我大忙……不过最好别逗留<i>{太}久。<continue>塔瓦凯对不速之客会有些不悦……你懂的？

Text.en: Though... perhaps you could be of some use, yes?
Text.zh: 不过……或许你还能为我做点别的，对吧？

Text.en: Hmmm... I wonder if you could do something for me, yes?
Text.zh: 嗯……不知道你是否愿意为我做件事，如何？

Text.en: I am working on a new elixir... another experiment, another concoction, hah! <continue>But I'm looking for an ingredient. Though it requires a little... bravery, to fetch. <continue>Travel to Whakapanu Island. Seek a great shark. Then... see what you can... acquire... and bring it back here! <continue>Then I'll see what I can do with it!
Text.zh: 我正在调制新的药剂……又一次实验，又一种配方，哈！<continue>但我需要一种原料。不过获取它需要一些……胆量。<continue>前往瓦卡帕努岛。寻找一头巨鲨。然后……看看你能……取得……什么，把它带回这里！<continue>然后我看看能用它做什么！

Text.en: What do you need it for?
Text.zh: 你要它做什么用？

Text.en: And why in the world would I do that?
Text.zh: 我为什么要这么做？

Text.en: I have never heard of such a creature. What purpose does this serve?
Text.zh: 我从未听说过这种生物。你要它有什么用？

Text.en: What use is a shark?
Text.zh: 鲨鱼能有什么用？

Text.en: I don't mind a hunt, but what's the catch?
Text.zh: 我不介意狩猎，不过有什么条件吗？

Text.en: Why must I seek a shark?
Text.zh: 我为什么非得去找鲨鱼？

Text.en: That's a new one. Never fought a shark before. What's, uh, the mission?
Text.zh: 这倒是新鲜事。我以前从没跟鲨鱼交过手。呃，具体任务是什么？

Text.en: What d'ye want with a shark, then?
Text.zh: 那你打鲨鱼的主意是想干啥？

Text.en: The blood fever... Tavakai insists we bring it to an end, yes. And that is my critical task. <continue>It is said... the great shark of Whakapanu Island contains unique blood. I must at least try!
Text.zh: 血热病……塔瓦凯坚持要我们根除它，对吧。这是我的核心任务。<continue>据说……瓦卡帕努岛的巨鲨体内流淌着独特的血液。我至少得试试看！

Text.en: I will do what I can.
Text.zh: 我会竭尽所能。

Text.en: Fine. If I see a shark, I'll kill it.
Text.zh: 好吧。如果遇见鲨鱼，我会杀了它。

Text.en: If it means so much to your people, I will track down this... shark... whatever it is.
Text.zh: 既然这对你的族人如此重要，我会去追踪这头……鲨鱼……不管它到底是什么。

Text.en: It'll be a shame to cull such a creature, but perhaps it must be done.
Text.zh: 杀死这样的生灵确实可惜，但或许在所难免。

Text.en: The hunt is on.
Text.zh: 狩猎开始了。

Text.en: I will do what must be done.
Text.zh: 我会完成必须完成的任务。

Text.en: Yeah, alright.
Text.zh: 是啊，行吧。

Text.en: Aye. In that case, I'll bring ye the beast.
Text.zh: 好。既然这样，那我来搞定这野兽。

Text.en: Hatuko... ma koro, hmm? When you're ready, please, be on your way, yes?
Text.zh: 哈图科……马科罗，嗯？等你准备妥当，就请启程吧，对吧？

Text.en: It is probably best that you're gone before Tavakai gets back...
Text.zh: 你最好在塔瓦凯返回之前离开……

Text.en: Ah! You have a fin from the great shark! Yes! Well done! I will get to work at once. Let us see what comes of it. <continue>The ancestors smile upon you this day, yes! Ooo haha!
Text.zh: 啊！你弄到了巨鲨的鳍！太好了！干得漂亮！我会立刻着手研究。让我们看看能有什么成果。<continue>先祖们今日对你展露笑颜，没错！哦哈哈！

Text.en: Tukohama heard him... heard him beg for guidance, yes! <continue>And then... Tukohama <i>{saw} him. And showed him 'The Way'. <continue>We've not known such a thing since the time of Akoya! So... who am I to question our leader?
Text.zh: 图克哈马聆听了他的声音……听到了他恳求指引的祈祷，对吧！<continue>然后……图克哈马<i>{注视}着他，向他揭示了“卡鲁之道”。<continue>自阿寇亚时代以来，我们再未见过这样的奇迹！所以……我有何资格质疑我们的首领？

Text.en: Leadership should be determined by the character of a man's decisions.
Text.zh: 领导能力应当取决于一个人抉择时的品格。

Text.en: I heard Tukohama was dead. This sounds wrong to me.
Text.zh: 我听说图克哈马已经陨落了。这听起来不太对劲。

Text.en: The most capable should be in charge. It is their duty, and their right.
Text.zh: 最有才能的人应当掌权。这既是他们的义务，也是他们的权利。

Text.en: I've never gotten on with authority. I only do what I feel is right.
Text.zh: 我从不与权威为伍。我只做自己认为正确的事。

Text.en: So long as he's making things right. That's what it takes.
Text.zh: 只要他能把事情做对，这就足够了。

Text.en: Questions lead to doubt. Doubt can be lethal. He must do what he thinks is right.
Text.zh: 疑问滋生怀疑，怀疑可能致命。他必须按自己认为正确的方式行事。

Text.en: I just do what I'm told, 'til the next guy's in charge. I don't do... morality.
Text.zh: 我只是听命行事，直到下一个人接管。我不搞……道德判断。

Text.en: Wouldnae be the first man to claim a god's favour. Won't be the last.
Text.zh: 自称受神眷顾之人向来不缺，往后也绝不了种。

Text.en: Right and wrong and all that's in between, yes? Hard to know. Hard to find. Hard to... learn.
Text.zh: 对与错，以及介于两者之间的一切，对吧？难以认知，难以寻觅，难以……领悟。

Text.en: We learn all the time. With every tide and every moon, yes? <continue>I would invite you to join us. To eat with us. To learn your story. <continue>To see beyond your eyes, yes? Talk and... share. But it is not up to me.
Text.zh: 我们时刻都在学习。伴随着每次潮起潮落，每轮月圆月缺，对吧？<continue>我想邀请你加入我们。与我们共进晚餐。倾听你的故事。<continue>透过你的双眼看世界，对吧？交谈与……分享。但这决定权不在我。

Text.en: I was hoping to meet some of my people. May I enter?
Text.zh: 我希望能见到我的一些族人。我可以进去吗？

Text.en: I think I'd pass on that, regardless. I'm not a... people person.
Text.zh: 不管怎样我都会拒绝。我不是个……善于交际的人。

Text.en: I would never ask you to betray your traditions.
Text.zh: 我绝不会要求你违背自己的传统。

Text.en: Fine. I didn't want to go in there anyway.
Text.zh: 算了，反正我也不想进去。

Text.en: You can't let me in?
Text.zh: 你就不能让我进去吗？

Text.en: I understand. Now is not the time.
Text.zh: 我理解。现在确实不是时候。

Text.en: Oh come on, maybe just a quick little visit?
Text.zh: 拜托，或许就简单参观一下？

Text.en: I've stories o' my own, if ye cared to hear them.
Text.zh: 我这儿也有故事，想听就吱声。

Text.en: I am... a simple medicine woman. I know my place, yes!
Text.zh: 我只是……一个普通的药师女人。我知道自己的本分，对吧！

Text.en: What <i>{is} your place?
Text.zh: 你的本分<i>{是}什么？

Text.en: And what <i>{is} your place?
Text.zh: 那你的本分<i>{是}什么呢？

Text.en: Are you a tale-woman?
Text.zh: 你是个说书人吗？

Text.en: What is your role here?
Text.zh: 你在这里是什么角色？

Text.en: What's your place in your tribe?
Text.zh: 那你在部落里是什么本分？

Text.en: What is it that <i>{you} do here?
Text.zh: <i>{你}在这里做些什么？

Text.en: If I might ask, what is your place here?
Text.zh: 请问，你在这里担任什么职务？

Text.en: So tell me, then - what <i>{is} yer place here?
Text.zh: 那就告诉我——你在这里<i>{是}有什么本分？

Text.en: Well... I'm a mystic. A mother. A doctor. A protector. A follower... yes? <continue>It is my duty to remedy the blood fever that increasingly plagues our people. <continue>I have not found a cure... not yet, at least. <continue>I continue to search for the right herbs, minerals and... organic matter.
Text.zh: 呃……我是个秘术师。是个母亲。是个医生。是个守护者。也是个信徒……懂吗？<continue>我的使命是治好日益困扰我们族人的血热病。<continue>我还没找到根治的方法……至少目前还没有。<continue>我还在继续寻找合适的草药、矿石和……生物材料。

Text.en: Can herbs and minerals really cure blood fever?
Text.zh: 草药和矿石真能治好血热病？

Text.en: Organic matter? You should be delving into the dark arts instead.
Text.zh: 草药矿石？你应该去研究黑魔法才对。

Text.en: Do herbs and minerals truly hold such power?
Text.zh: 草药和矿石真有这种威力？

Text.en: I've seen many gifts from nature, but a cure for blood fever?
Text.zh: 我见过大自然的很多奇迹，但治血热病？

Text.en: Do these islands have summat like that?
Text.zh: 这些岛上真有这种东西？

Text.en: Would that... work?
Text.zh: 这……真的管用？

Text.en: Well, good luck with that.
Text.zh: 好吧，祝你好运。

Text.en: Can <i>{nature} cure somethin' so... <i>{un}natural?
Text.zh: <i>{自然之力}能否治愈如此…<i>{非}自然的存在？

Text.en: Ahhh! Don't underestimate the remedies the world provides us! <continue>Everything, alive or not, holds a secret. Some underlying truth, yes. <continue>We must keep trying new things. The truth will reveal itself in the patterns we see!
Text.zh: 啊哈！别小瞧大自然给我们的馈赠！<continue>世间万物，生死皆有，都藏着秘密。都有着根本的道理，对吧。<continue>我们得不断尝试新方法。规律显现时，真相自然就明了！

Text.en: Yes... it plagues us more and more of late. Starts with a fever. <continue>Progresses to boils rising from under the skin... hmm? Then... madness! Rage.
Text.zh: 对……最近病情越来越严重。先是发烧。<continue>然后皮肤下冒出脓疱……嗯？接着就是……发疯！暴怒。

Text.en: It is heart breaking. Watching so many loved ones ripped apart. <continue>Sent away to die alone.
Text.zh: 太让人心碎了。眼睁睁看着这么多亲人被撕碎。<continue>被赶出去独自等死。

Text.en: Why are they sent away?
Text.zh: 为什么要把他们赶走？

Text.en: That seems cruel, even to me.
Text.zh: 即使对我来说，这也太残忍了。

Text.en: Such things must be done for the greater good.
Text.zh: 为了大局着想，只能这么做。

Text.en: Many animals crawl off to die alone. I never thought people might.
Text.zh: 很多动物都会自己找地方死去。没想到人也会这样。

Text.en: Why would ya do that?!
Text.zh: 你们干嘛要这样？！

Text.en: You send them away? Why?
Text.zh: 你们把他们赶走？为什么？

Text.en: Some Trarthans do that, too. Dying with dignity and all that.
Text.zh: 有些特拉特斯人也这样。说什么体面地死去。

Text.en: Cruel fate... but that's Wraeclast for ye. It takes what it will, and leaves naught but sorrow behind.
Text.zh: 残酷的命运……但这正是瓦拉克拉斯。它肆意掠夺，留下的唯有悲戚。

Text.en: We have no choice. Let one stay, their family falls. Let the family stay... well... no more tribe, yes...
Text.zh: 我们没得选择。留下一个，全家遭殃。留下全家……嗯……整个部落就完了，明白吗……

Text.en: The blood fever must end. No matter what it takes.
Text.zh: 血热病必须根除。不惜一切代价。

Text.en: Why do you speak of Kitava here? It was <i>{our} Tukohama that gave him his scars!
Text.zh: 你干嘛在这提奇塔弗？给他留疤的可是<i>{我们的}图克哈玛！

Text.en: Those cultists that massacred Oriath were no <i>{Ngakuramakoi.} They were... undesirables.
Text.zh: 那些屠杀奥瑞亚的狂热分子根本不是<i>{纳库雷默柯} 。他们是……被抛弃的败类。

Text.en: You hold no connection to them?
Text.zh: 你跟他们没有任何关系？

Text.en: How convenient for you.
Text.zh: 你说得倒轻松。

Text.en: Ah, much like the Faridun.
Text.zh: 啊，跟法里顿人一个样。

Text.en: I don't understand the distinction.
Text.zh: 我搞不懂这有什么区别。

Text.en: They were cast out? Or driven away?
Text.zh: 他们是被流放的？还是被驱逐的？

Text.en: Are we not all beholden to each other?
Text.zh: 我们彼此之间不都有联系吗？

Text.en: So that's why you never tried to free them.
Text.zh: 怪不得你们从来不想救他们。

Text.en: And castin' 'em out... did that not make 'em what they became?
Text.zh: 而将他们驱逐……这不正是他们落得如此下场的根源吗？

Text.en: The cult of Kitava grew like a tumour, when we allowed our culture to weaken. <continue>Kitava would have consumed the world. Had I been of age, I would have slain him myself. <continue>Let them all rot, forgotten.
Text.zh: 当我们的文化开始衰落时，奇塔弗邪教就像毒瘤一样扩散。<continue>奇塔弗差点毁灭了整个世界。如果我当时够大，我会亲手宰了他。<continue>让他们统统腐烂，被世人遗忘。

Text.en: Ah. Ngamakanui is all around us. The waters. The Way. The Karui. Ngakanu is our home. But Ngamakanui is our heart, yes?
Text.zh: 啊。纳玛鲁伊包围着我们。河流。道路。卡鲁族。纳卡努是我们的居所。但纳玛鲁伊才是我们的灵魂，懂吗？

Text.en: So Ngamakanui is not... a place?
Text.zh: 所以纳玛鲁伊不是……一个具体的地方？

Text.en: I have no idea what any of that means. Where is Ngamakanui?
Text.zh: 这些我完全听不懂。纳玛鲁伊到底在哪？

Text.en: Makes me think of the Vastiri.
Text.zh: 这让我想起瓦斯提里人。

Text.en: I think I understand.
Text.zh: 我想我懂了。

Text.en: Sounds like you've a touch of the Spirit, yourself.
Text.zh: 听起来你自己也挺信仰万灵的。

Text.en: I feel something like this as well, in my own way.
Text.zh: 我也能用自己的方式感受到类似的东西。

Text.en: Uhh... what?
Text.zh: 呃……什么？

Text.en: Not a place, but a people.
Text.zh: 重要的不是地方，而是族人。

Text.en: We are nomadic... usually. We've tarried too long in this place. <continue>Ngamakanui is wherever the <i>{Ngakuramakoi} currently call home.
Text.zh: 我们平时都是游牧的。在这地方待得太久了。<continue>纳玛鲁伊就是<i>{纳库雷默柯} 现在安家的地方。

Text.en: We guard and protect the land, and it provides for us in return. <continue>We defend it with blood and axe. We cull that which should not be; that which Corrupts. Then, we travel on. <continue>Eventually, another tribe will arrive, finding it unspoiled. <continue>We do this so that when we move on, we too will find bounty waiting for us.
Text.zh: 我们守卫保护这片土地，它就会回馈我们所需。<continue>我们用鲜血和战斧捍卫它。清除那些不该存在的东西；那些腐败的事物。然后继续前行。<continue>最终会有别的部落到来，发现这里依然纯净。<continue>我们这么做，是为了在我们离开时，也能找到为我们准备好的富饶之地。

Text.en: Yes! That is a beautiful aspect of the Way. Seen like this, the tribes are one! Mmm.
Text.zh: 没错！这就是“卡鲁之道”的美妙之处。这样看来，所有部落都是一体的！嗯。

Text.en: Hinekora <i>{spoke to you?} What did she say?
Text.zh: 辛格拉<i>{跟你说话了}？她说了什么？

Text.en: She did <i>{what?}
Text.zh: 她做了<i>{什么？}

Text.en: I'm not sure what she meant... I'll have to think on it.
Text.zh: 我不太确定她的意思……我还要好好琢磨琢磨。

Text.en: It was nothing... it meant nothing.
Text.zh: 没什么……都是些无关紧要的话。

Text.en: It is hard to say.
Text.zh: 很难说清楚。

Text.en: Nothing I didn't already know.
Text.zh: 我早就知道了。

Text.en: Perhaps it's a way for me to fix what's broken.
Text.zh: 或许，她是在指引我去弥补往日缺憾。

Text.en: Something I won't soon forget.
Text.zh: 我将毕生铭记的话。

Text.en: It was strange. Very strange.
Text.zh: 很诡异。非常诡异。

Text.en: She spoke o' pain long past... but not forgotten.
Text.zh: 她说了陈年旧痛……虽已远去，但从未被遗忘。

Text.en: Ah, the Mother of Death tells us not what we want to know, but what we need to hear, yes! <continue>The meaning of her prophecies will become clear... when the time is right.
Text.zh: 啊，死亡之母从不告诉我们想听的，只说我们需要听的，对吧！<continue>她预言的真正含义终会明朗……只要等待时机已到。

Text.en: The Hinekora tribe seems to have endless time to sit around playing with cryptic messages. <continue>We do not. We live in the real world, where we must fight or die. <continue>In any case... I don't want to know how you came to speak with the Mother of Death.
Text.zh: 辛格拉部落有的是时间坐在那里解读神秘预言。<continue>我们可没有。我们活在现实中，不战斗就得死。<continue>总之……我不想知道你是怎么跟死亡之母谈话的。

Text.en: Kaimana! How did you survive?
Text.zh: 凯玛纳！你怎么活下来的？

Text.en: You're still sane? Don't tell me your <i>{herbs and minerals} actually worked.
Text.zh: 你还很清醒？别告诉我你的<i>{草药矿石}真的有用。

Text.en: You're not sick! What's happening?
Text.zh: 你没生病！怎么回事？

Text.en: You're still healthy! How?
Text.zh: 你还健健康康的！怎么做到的？

Text.en: You didn't catch the Fever?
Text.zh: 你没得热病？

Text.en: Kaimana... you're sane. Are you alright?
Text.zh: 凯玛纳……你还神智清醒。你没事吧？

Text.en: You don't seem sick. What's goin' on?
Text.zh: 你看起来没病。到底怎么了？

Text.en: Kaimana... How is it ye're not as mad as the rest of 'em?
Text.zh: 凯玛纳……你怎么就没像其他人那样发疯？

Text.en: Yes! But for how long, I can't be certain. <continue>Now... please. Stop Tavakai. He is lost!
Text.zh: 对！但能撑多久我也不敢肯定。<continue>现在……拜托了。阻止塔瓦凯。他已经疯了！

Text.en: I will do what I must. Find somewhere safe. Hide.
Text.zh: 我会做该做的事。找个安全地方躲起来。

Text.en: Oh, I plan to take care of <i>{him.} Try not to die in the meantime.
Text.zh: 哦，我会料理<i>{他}。你尽量别死了。

Text.en: I will put an end to this. Hide until I return!
Text.zh: 我会解决这件事。躲好了等我回来！

Text.en: I will stop him. You stay safe. Hide.
Text.zh: 我会阻止他。你躲安全点。

Text.en: I'm on the hunt. Find somewhere safe.
Text.zh: 我要去猎杀他了。找地方躲好。

Text.en: Yes. I will stop him. Run and hide until this is over.
Text.zh: 对，我会阻止他。赶紧跑去躲起来，直到这件事结束。

Text.en: That's the plan, miss. You just keep yourself alive.
Text.zh: 就是这个计划，女士。你只管保命就行。

Text.en: I'll see it done. Now go - get to safety.
Text.zh: 交给我。快走，去安全的地方。

Text.en: I will, yes. Go. Save our people.
Text.zh: 我会的。去吧。拯救我们的族人。

Text.en: Paka...ruiiiiii!
Text.zh: 帕卡……卢伊伊伊！

Text.en: Fleshhhh!
Text.zh: 血肉啊！

Text.en: No! Kaimana! I will put this right!
Text.zh: 不！凯玛纳！我要纠正这一切！

Text.en: Smart move, putting yourself in a cage.
Text.zh: 把自己关笼子里，聪明的选择。

Text.en: Even their medicine woman is sick... this is dire.
Text.zh: 连他们的医生都病了……情况太糟了。

Text.en: Not you, too.
Text.zh: 连你也不例外。

Text.en: She's gone... how sad.
Text.zh: 她也死了……真可悲。

Text.en: I'm sorry, Kaimana.
Text.zh: 抱歉，凯玛纳。

Text.en: Ah, shite.
Text.zh: 啊，见鬼。

Text.en: Ach... Not as you as well!
Text.zh: 老天…连你都这样了！

Text.en: Oh, you made us all proud, yes! You will forever hold a special place in the hearts of the Tukohama tribe.
Text.zh: 哦，你让我们所有人都骄傲，没错！你会永远在图克哈玛部落的心中占据特殊地位。

Text.en: Yes - you will be long remembered. The outsider... who saved us all. We won't forget.
Text.zh: 对——你会被永远铭记。那个外来者……拯救了我们所有人。我们不会忘记。

Text.en: Yes - you will be long remembered. The lost son who saved us all. We won't forget.
Text.zh: 对——你会被永远铭记。那个迷途的孩子拯救了我们所有人。我们不会忘记。

Text.en: I will remember you both in kind.
Text.zh: 我也会记住你们两个。

Text.en: I <i>{suppose...} you two have irritated me less than most.
Text.zh: 我<i>{觉得…}你们俩还算顺眼，不怎么招我烦。

Text.en: I will remember your tales.
Text.zh: 我会记住你们的传奇。

Text.en: I won't, either.
Text.zh: 我也不会忘记。

Text.en: You have me thanks, and my fondness.
Text.zh: 谢谢你们，我很喜欢你们俩。

Text.en: We will each take lessons from this. I will remember you, as well.
Text.zh: 我们都会从中学到东西。我也会记住你们。

Text.en: Been wild, seeing people livin' happily without money, slaves, or drugs. I won't soon forget it.
Text.zh: 真是太神奇了，亲眼看到一群人活得那么幸福，不靠金钱、不靠奴隶、也不靠毒品。我这辈子都不会忘记的。

Text.en: Nor will I. This day'll stay with me, sure as the tide.
Text.zh: 我也一样。今日种种，将如潮汐往复，铭刻在心。

Text.en: Well, of course. We're pretty special, yes?
Text.zh: 那当然。我们可是很特别的，不是吗？

Text.en: Unforgettable! Ha!
Text.zh: 难以忘怀！哈！

Text.en: Doryani sees the world through a different lens. We must remember that he is of a bygone era...
Text.zh: 多里亚尼会用不同的眼光看待世界。我们得记住他来自遥远的过去……

Text.en: ... I can hear you, you know.
Text.zh: ……我能听到你，你知道的。

Text.en: Indeed.
Text.zh: 可不是。

Text.en: And of what era are you, exactly?
Text.zh: 那你又是哪个时代的？

Text.en: Older still. I have seen many civilisations rise and fall.
Text.zh: 更古老的时代。我见证过许多文明的兴起和衰落。

Text.en: We all have our differences.
Text.zh: 人各有别，何必争执？

Text.en: Yes... fight!
Text.zh: 好……快打一架吧！

Text.en: Must I listen to squabble?
Text.zh: 我非得听你们斗嘴不可吗？

Text.en: You are both equally questionable.
Text.zh: 你们俩半斤八两，谁也别说谁。

Text.en: Hey now... Break it up.
Text.zh: 行了……别吵了。

Text.en: Please. We must remain calm.
Text.zh: 冷静，我们必须保持冷静。

Text.en: Heh. I'd pay money to watch you two brawl it out.
Text.zh: 嘿。我愿意花钱看你们俩打一架。

Text.en: Aye, you're both as old as stone. Let's not quarrel over it.
Text.zh: 行了，你们都是老古董了。就别为这个争了。

Text.en: It is a strange feeling, meeting someone so gifted in sciences I unintentionally brought to the world. <continue>All of Vaal technology and advancements, derived from my ultimate mistake...
Text.zh: 遇到在我无意创造的科学领域如此天赋异禀的人，感觉很奇妙。<continue>所有瓦尔的技术和成就，都源自我犯下的那个终极错误……

Text.en: You call the Beast a mistake? On this, I do not agree. <continue>The gods were nothing but monstrous tyrants. <continue>The power of Corruption allowed humanity to seize back control of their destiny!
Text.zh: 你说邪兽是个错误？这点我不认同。<continue>神明不过是些怪物暴君罢了。<continue>腐化之力让人类重新掌控了自己的命运！

Text.en: And what did you do with that control? <continue>In your experiments, I saw death. Dismemberment. A living nightmare.
Text.zh: 你们用这份掌控做了什么？<continue>在你的实验中，我看到的是死亡。肢解。活生生的噩梦。

Text.en: A means to an end. They were all willing sacrifices.
Text.zh: 这是达成目标的必要手段。他们都是自愿牺牲的。

Text.en: That is, somehow, even more frightening. The zealotry. The fervor. <continue>I've seen it all too often... a kind of madness, seemingly fundamental to mankind.
Text.zh: 这反而更可怕。那种盲信。那种狂热。<continue>我见过太多次了……某种疯狂，似乎是人类与生俱来的。

Text.en: Perhaps, but it was their choice to make. <continue>They gave themselves in the hopes of saving our people. <continue>My only regret is that I failed.
Text.zh: 也许吧，但那是他们自己的选择。<continue>他们献出生命是希望能拯救我们的族人。<continue>我唯一后悔的就是失败了。

Text.en: Oh to bear but <i>{one} regret...
Text.zh: 哦，只后悔<i>{一}件事……

Text.en: One day, all regrets will be cast aside. We will put things right.
Text.zh: 总有一天，所有后悔都会被抛到脑后。我们会把一切纠正过来。

Text.en: And this is what I get for trying to make small talk.
Text.zh: 我就是随便聊聊，结果就这样。

Text.en: Regrets are what shape us. Learnings observed about ourselves. No one is perfect.
Text.zh: 后悔能塑造我们。让我们认识自己。没人是完美的。

Text.en: Regrets are useless. We have to move forward. Best you both try to as well.
Text.zh: 后悔没用。我们得往前看。你们俩最好也这样。

Text.en: I've certainly made some mistakes I wish I could take back. But we must accept them and move on.
Text.zh: 我确实做过一些恨不得能撤回的事。但我们必须学会接受，然后继续前进。

Text.en: The burdened mind drags itself down. The past is behind us.
Text.zh: 背负重担的心灵只会拖累自己。过去的就过去了。

Text.en: Oof, sorry I asked.
Text.zh: 呃，抱歉我多嘴了。

Text.en: Regret's a root ye cannae cut. <continue>Best learn to grow around it.
Text.zh: 悔恨是无法斩断的树根。 <continue>最好学会绕开它。

Text.en: The more I consider it, the more I believe I've seen a depiction of the Weapon. <continue>Especially now that I can study the piece in Doryani's possession...
Text.zh: 我越想越觉得我见过那件武器的描绘。<continue>特别是现在我能研究多里亚尼手里的这块碎片……

Text.en: A depiction? Where? The scraps I know, I learned from incredibly ancient ruins.
Text.zh: 描绘？在哪？我知道的那点信息是从极其古老的遗迹里学来的。
