Text.en: Yes... when I consulted the ancient being Kalandra, she told me where to find the Seed of Corruption... <continue>She was cryptic, and said that it already belonged to me. <continue>That it always had. <continue>I can still only guess what she meant... <continue>In the ruins where the Seed was sealed, I saw carvings on the wall. <continue>It was a message, left for anyone who might follow in the eons to come. <continue>A series of giant murals. The first was broken, but the second depicted the creation of the Seed. <continue>The third mural portrayed the Seed's destruction. At the time, I thought it was a warning. <continue>Now, I see the Weapon was depicted quite literally, being driven into the Seed. <continue>It might not have been a warning. It might have been... <i>{instructions.}
Text.zh: 对……当我向古老存在卡兰德请教时，她告诉我腐化之种的所在……<continue>她话中有话，说那东西原本就归我所有。<continue>从始至终都是。<continue>我至今也只能揣摩她的真意……<continue>在封印种子的废墟中，我看见了墙壁上的石刻。<continue>那是给后来探寻者留下的讯息。<continue>一系列宏伟的壁画。第一幅已经残破，但第二幅描绘了种子的创造。<continue>第三幅壁画展示了种子的毁灭。当时我觉得那是警示。<continue>现在我明白了，武器被清晰明确地刻画出来，刺入种子当中。<continue>那可能不是警示。那可能是……<i>{指令。}

Text.en: This confirms my hypothesis. The Weapon is the key to destroying the Beast. <continue>We must find and reassemble it, no matter the cost.
Text.zh: 这印证了我的假设。武器就是摧毁邪兽的钥匙。<continue>我们必须寻获并重新拼装它，无论付出什么代价。

Text.en: I will do whatever it takes.
Text.zh: 我将竭尽所能。

Text.en: Leave it to me.
Text.zh: 让我来吧。

Text.en: I am honour-bound to do this, but I would still volunteer.
Text.zh: 我有责任这样做，不过我也心甘情愿。

Text.en: If it's the only way to save the lands I love, I will give my all.
Text.zh: 如果这是拯救我深爱土地的唯一方式，我会拼尽全力。

Text.en: I will give my very life, if I must.
Text.zh: 必要时，我愿意献出性命。

Text.en: We will succeed. Together.
Text.zh: 我们会成功的。携手并进。

Text.en: Least we've got a plan.
Text.zh: 起码我们有方向了。

Text.en: Aye. The Beast falls – or we do.
Text.zh: 没错。不是邪兽死——就是我们亡。

Text.en: It is curious that the Weapon was broken into pieces and dispersed here.
Text.zh: 令人费解的是，武器竟然被拆散成碎片分布各处。

Text.en: From what I've seen, I don't believe the Weapon was broken apart. It was never fully completed at all. <continue>I have only ever seen it portrayed in pieces. The third mural, the one you saw, <i>{must} have been an instruction. <continue>It is my belief that the creators of the Weapon were... interrupted.
Text.zh: 根据我的观察，我认为武器并非被拆解。它根本就未曾完工。<continue>我见到的始终都是碎片状态。你看到的第三幅壁画<i>{必定}就是指令。<continue>我相信武器的铸造者们被……中断了。

Text.en: Interrupted? How?
Text.zh: 中断？什么意思？

Text.en: Ooh that sounds ominous! What do you think happened?
Text.zh: 哦，这听起来很不祥！你觉得发生了什么事？

Text.en: Let me guess, did something destroy them?
Text.zh: 让我猜猜，是不是有什么东西消灭了他们？

Text.en: What befell them?
Text.zh: 他们遭遇了什么？

Text.en: Sounds a nice way of putting it...
Text.zh: 说得挺含蓄的……

Text.en: Do you know their fate?
Text.zh: 你知道他们的结局吗？

Text.en: Interrupted? By what then?
Text.zh: 中断？被什么中断？

Text.en: By who? Or what?
Text.zh: 被谁？或者被什么东西？

Text.en: It's impossible to know for certain. They were wiped out thousands of years before my time. <continue>Do... <i>{you} have any notion?
Text.zh: 无从得知。他们在我所处年代的数千年前就已灭绝。<continue><i>{你}……有什么看法？

Text.en: I was too young to remember much. I was among the Azmeri when it happened. <continue>But they did tell stories... I recall tales of... a great fire, a great wasting...
Text.zh: 我当时年纪太小，记忆模糊。事发时我在阿兹莫里人中间。<continue>但他们确实流传着故事……我记得关于……大焚毁和大荒废的传说……

Text.en: <i>{You} were alive for <i>{that?} The historical record... it's always been conjecture...!
Text.zh: <i>{你}经历过<i>{那个时代？}史料记载……向来都只是推断……！

Text.en: I remember fear. I remember sorrow. <continue>But the Azmeri had chosen their homes carefully. <continue>They were separatists. They rejected technology. <continue>And in the end, they were right...
Text.zh: 我记得恐惧。我记得哀伤。<continue>但阿兹莫里人审慎地择定了安身之地。<continue>他们是分离主义者。摒弃科技。<continue>最终证明他们的选择是正确的……

Text.en: I am glad the Tukohama Tribe still thrives. <continue>In the early days, Tukohama was a good man.
Text.zh: 我欣慰图克哈玛部落依然昌盛。<continue>早年的图克哈玛是个正直的人。

Text.en: You... knew him?!
Text.zh: 你……认识他？！

Text.en: Yes... and I also helped kill him, too. Much to my regret.
Text.zh: 是的……我也参与了杀死他的行动。这令我深感愧疚。

Text.en: It's true, then? The tales of the Godslayer, in decades past...
Text.zh: 这是事实？数十年前关于弑神者的传说……

Text.en: So you really did rampage with the Godslayer?
Text.zh: 所以你真的和弑神者一同征战过？

Text.en: The tales are true! You journeyed with the Godslayer?
Text.zh: 传说竟是真的！你真的与弑神者同行？

Text.en: So, the Godslayer really existed.
Text.zh: 这么说，弑神者确有其人。

Text.en: The stories of the Godslayer are true?
Text.zh: 弑神者的故事是真实的？

Text.en: Ah, so the Godslayer was indeed real.
Text.zh: 啊，所以弑神者确实存在。

Text.en: Oh, so the Godslayer wasn't just a tavern tale after all.
Text.zh: 哦，原来弑神者不只是酒馆传说。

Text.en: I <i>{did} hear tell of a 'Godslayer'... but I thought it meaningless drivel.
Text.zh: 我<i>{确实}听过“弑神者”的传闻……但我以为那只是吹牛。

Text.en: Not drivel. You heard truth.
Text.zh: 绝非妄言。你所听闻的，即是真相。

Text.en: Yes... at the time, there was no other way... <continue>There is one consolation... his spark was not lost...
Text.zh: 是的……彼时别无选择……<continue>唯一的慰藉是……他的神火并未熄灭……

Text.en: I thought I could deal with the gods single-handedly, without consequence. <continue>It should always have been a communal choice. I applaud them for their efforts.
Text.zh: 我以为我能独力应对众神，不会招致恶果。<continue>这本该是集体的抉择。我钦佩他们的壮举。

Text.en: It seems they despise the gods. I dare say, it is understandable. <continue>Mankind has no need of divine tyrants.
Text.zh: 看来他们对诸神恨之入骨。说实话，这完全可以理解。<continue>人类不需要高高在上的暴君。

Text.en: I wonder if the Order <i>{could} be of some aid to us. They have the numbers.
Text.zh: 我想知道教会<i>{能否}助我们一臂之力，毕竟他们人多势众。

Text.en: I find those overly-happy types truly unsettling... leave them to it, I say.
Text.zh: 我觉得那些过度乐观的家伙真让人毛骨悚然……要我说，随他们去吧。

Text.en: The gods are not inherently evil. We have seen them do great things for the Vastiri and our people.
Text.zh: 诸神本质上并非邪恶。我们亲眼见过他们为瓦斯提里人和我们的族人立下功德。

Text.en: I don't trust this 'Twilight Order.' Keep your beliefs to yourself, I say.
Text.zh: 我不相信这个“暮光会”。我建议收起你的信仰。

Text.en: Gods derived from mankind are flawed. The Spirit... is beyond all of that.
Text.zh: 来源于人类的诸神都有弱点。圣灵……却超脱于此。

Text.en: We cannot fathom the weight of such a thing... to be divine.
Text.zh: 我们无法体会那种重担……成为神祇。

Text.en: Either way, them lot on Arastas... they feel a bit off.
Text.zh: 不管怎样，阿拉斯塔上那帮家伙……给人感觉怪怪的。

Text.en: Gods rise and fall... but the Draíocht abides in all livin' things. The Order won't change that.
Text.zh: 众神起起落落……但德莱契蕴藏在万物之中。暮光会改变不了这一点。

Text.en: Oriana... Ogham's downfall... was part of the Twilight Order all along! Greatwolf ravage her!
Text.zh: 奥莉安娜……欧甘的掘墓人……原来一直都是暮光会的人！愿巨狼撕碎她！

Text.en: So, the Twilight Order and Oriana are one and the same. Who knew?
Text.zh: 原来如此，暮光会和奥莉安娜就是一伙的。

Text.en: It makes sense. Oriana is part of this Twilight Order.
Text.zh: 这说得通。奥莉安娜本就是暮光会的成员。

Text.en: Oriana's working for the Twilight Order. First Ogham, then the Vastiri... now the world.
Text.zh: 奥莉安娜在为暮光会卖命。先害了欧甘，再害瓦斯提里……现在要害整个世界。

Text.en: Oriana and the Twilight Order... she's got a lot to answer for.
Text.zh: 奥莉安娜和暮光会……她欠我们太多解释了。

Text.en: Oriana is behind the Twilight Order. Pulling its strings.
Text.zh: 奥莉安娜就是暮光会背后的主使。在暗中操控一切。

Text.en: Count Geonor's missus is the leader of the Twilight Order! What a bloody twist!
Text.zh: 吉恩诺伯爵的老婆竟然是暮光会的头目！真是个惊天逆转！

Text.en: Oriana and the Twilight Order - one and the same, all along.
Text.zh: 奥莉安娜和暮光会——就是一伙的，一直都是。

Text.en: We should have known. Their goals were in alignment. It's obvious, in retrospect.
Text.zh: 我们早就该察觉的。他们的目标如此一致。现在想来简直明摆着。

Text.en: We had centuries of science at our disposal... and still we could not control the Beast. <continue>These religious fools are going to cause another Cataclysm.
Text.zh: 我们掌握着几百年的科学积淀……却依然无法驾驭邪兽。<continue>这帮宗教疯子会引发第二次灾变。

Text.en: We must find them. We must make them see reason!
Text.zh: 我们得找到他们。得让他们看清现实！

Text.en: I was the most reasonable man of my era, and until my hand was forced, I didn't listen. <continue>I suspect... they won't, either.
Text.zh: 我曾是那个时代最理性的人，可直到被逼到绝境，我也听不进劝告。<continue>我怀疑……他们也一样听不进去。

Text.en: This piece of the Weapon... it has writing on it...
Text.zh: 这片武器碎片……上面刻着文字……

Text.en: The first example of Precursor writing I've ever seen!
Text.zh: 这是现存的先驱文字，我头一回见到！

Text.en: I... recognise some of these symbols...
Text.zh: 我……认出了其中一些符号……

Text.en: What? How?
Text.zh: 什么？怎么会？

Text.en: When I was very young, but a boy, and still mortal... hmm. Such a faded memory... <continue>I was... in her lap... and symbols like these, decorated a vivid book... <continue>I can almost hear her saying the words... what they mean...
Text.zh: 那时我还很小，只是个孩子，还是个凡人……嗯。记忆如此模糊……<continue>我……坐在她膝上……这样的符号装饰着一本精美的书籍……<continue>我几乎还能听到她念出那些词语……它们的意思……

Text.en: Who was this person?
Text.zh: 这个人是谁？

Text.en: Are you saying what I think you're saying?
Text.zh: 你的意思是我想的那样吗？

Text.en: Please. You must recall. What do they mean?
Text.zh: 拜托。你一定要想起来。它们是什么意思？

Text.en: What's happening? What do you remember?
Text.zh: 怎么了？你想起什么了？

Text.en: What's goin' on?
Text.zh: 发生什么了？

Text.en: Clear your mind. Try to recall. What do they mean?
Text.zh: 静下心来。努力回想。它们什么意思？

Text.en: What are you saying, old fella?
Text.zh: 你在说什么，老头？

Text.en: My own memory's a blur. If yours is still in there – dinnae let it slip.
Text.zh: 我的记忆也一片模糊。如果你还有记忆——千万要回想起来。

Text.en: I'm not sure... I have to think on it... I have to try to remember...
Text.zh: 我不太确定……我得仔细想想……得努力回忆……

Text.en: Then <i>{we} must find the other pieces. We need more information.
Text.zh: 那<i>{我们}就得找到其余的碎片。我们需要更多信息。

Text.en: This piece has writing, too! I have some sense of the meaning...
Text.zh: 这片碎片上也有文字！我大概能感受到它的含义……

Text.en: Give me everything you recall, no matter how insignificant. We must try to translate them.
Text.zh: 把你想起的一切都告诉我，再小的事都别放过。我们得试着翻译出来。

Text.en: What does it mean to you? Do you remember?
Text.zh: 对你而言这意味着什么？你记得吗？

Text.en: Something very strange is going on here... explain!
Text.zh: 这里正在发生非常诡异的事情……快说怎么回事！

Text.en: There must be a tale, locked in the back of your mind. Focus on it.
Text.zh: 你的记忆深处肯定藏着一个故事。集中注意力去想它。

Text.en: Do you know why, yet? Why they seem familiar to you?
Text.zh: 你现在知道原因了吗？为什么它们让你觉得眼熟？

Text.en: Some mysteries are best left untouched, you know.
Text.zh: 有些秘密，还是不去触碰为好。

Text.en: We are stumbling upon some sort of hidden truth here.
Text.zh: 我们正在无意中触及某种隐秘的真相。

Text.en: Oh boy. This is gettin' good.
Text.zh: 我的天。这越来越精彩了。

Text.en: Strange what lingers - and what's lost. I'd give much to know <i>{my} past.
Text.zh: 记得无关的细节，忘掉重要的事，记忆真是奇怪。我愿意付出任何代价，来换回<i>{我的}过去。

Text.en: As a child, I was sent to live among the Azmeri with my brother and sister... <continue>But before that, I lived... <i>{somewhere else...} <continue>Somewhere with great works of stone and metal and glass... <continue>And a kind, smiling face... a woman's face... <continue>I haven't thought about this in thousands of years... it's a mortal memory, faded to static...
Text.zh: 小时候，我被送去和兄弟姐妹一起在阿兹莫里人中生活……<continue>但在那之前，我住在……<i>{其他地方……}<continue>那里有宏伟的石制、金属和玻璃建筑……<continue>还有一张慈爱微笑的脸庞……一个女人的脸……<continue>我几千年来都没想过这些……这是凡人的记忆，早已模糊成了白噪音……

Text.en: Don't get distracted. Keep the thought in your mind. <continue>You must find the last piece. Now go. We will work on this.
Text.zh: 别走神。把这个念头留在心里。<continue>你必须找到最后那片碎片。现在就去。我们会继续研究这个。

Text.en: The final piece... these symbols...
Text.zh: 最后一片碎片……这些符号……

Text.en: The translation work we've done... is it enough?
Text.zh: 我们的翻译工作……足够了吗？

Text.en: Something about a forge... <continue>This part says 'You must finish it,' or something like it. 'You must forge it.' <continue>The word forge is drawn like a name. It's not an action. It's a place.
Text.zh: 关于熔炉的什么内容……<continue>这部分写的是“你必须完成它”，或者类似意思。“你必须铸造它。”<continue>铸造这个词画得像个专有名词。它不是动词。而是地名。

Text.en: So, we must find an ancient Precursor forge.
Text.zh: 所以，我们必须找到一座古老的先驱熔炉。

Text.en: But where? It could be anywhere.
Text.zh: 可在哪呢？它可能在任何地方。

Text.en: And how are we supposed to do that then?
Text.zh: 那我们该怎么办？

Text.en: A forge? How do we find it?
Text.zh: 熔炉？我们怎么找？

Text.en: Do we have a trail? Where do we start looking?
Text.zh: 有线索了吗？该从哪里开始找？

Text.en: I've hunted many things... never a forge. Where do we look?
Text.zh: 我猎杀过各种东西……就是没猎过熔炉。该从哪找起？

Text.en: A forge? Where are we to start looking?
Text.zh: 熔炉？我们从哪开始找？

Text.en: What! How do we do that?
Text.zh: 什么！我们要怎么做？

Text.en: A forge? Hmm... where would such a place be?
Text.zh: 一座熔炉？唔..……这样的地方会在哪儿呢？

Text.en: This is the first time in my life I'm forced to admit - I have no idea. <continue>Ask the crew perhaps. The Karui. They live here, after all.
Text.zh: 这辈子头一回我被迫承认——我完全没头绪。<continue>也许问问船员们。问问卡鲁人。他们毕竟是本地人。

Text.en: There is no doubt now in my mind. It <i>{is} the Weapon I saw in that third mural. <continue>The forging revealed an inscription. <continue>The symbols declare it 'The instrument of the Third Edict.' <continue>It was designed for the sole purpose of cleansing Corruption... <continue>... and destroying the Beast.
Text.zh: 现在我心里再无疑虑。这<i>{就是}我在第三幅壁画里看到的武器。<continue>铸造过程显现了一段铭文。<continue>符号表明它是“第三敕令的器具”。<continue>它被设计出来就只有一个目的——净化腐化……<continue>……并摧毁邪兽。

Text.en: The Third Edict? What is the meaning... of these Edicts? <continue>Perhaps it follows that the Seed was the instrument of a <i>{Second} Edict... <continue>But why? What part of this tale are we missing? <continue>Why would any civilisation intentionally create the Seed of Corruption?
Text.zh: 第三敕令？这些敕令……有何含义？<continue>或许腐化之种是<i>{第二}敕令的器具……<continue>但为何如此？我们遗漏了这故事的哪个部分？<continue>为什么任何文明都会故意创造腐化之种？

Text.en: A question lost to time. All we can do now is destroy it.
Text.zh: 这个谜题已随时光湮灭。我们现在唯一能做的就是摧毁它。

Text.en: The pursuit of power, obviously. Now, we have to clean up their mess.
Text.zh: 显然是为了追逐权力。现在，我们得收拾他们留下的烂摊子。

Text.en: They must have had a good reason... but we have no choice. We must destroy the Beast.
Text.zh: 他们肯定有充足的理由……但我们别无选择。必须摧毁邪兽。

Text.en: The hubris of man never changes. Just look at history.
Text.zh: 人类的狂妄从未改变。翻翻历史就明白了。

Text.en: You can fuss over that. We have the Weapon. The hunt is on.
Text.zh: 你尽管为此发愁。我们拿到武器了。该开始狩猎了。

Text.en: The truth may come to us in time. Until then, we have what we need.
Text.zh: 真相或许终将大白。在那之前，我们有了所需的一切。

Text.en: That's above my pay grade. Let's go destroy the Beast and be done with it.
Text.zh: 这事超出我的能力范围。咱们去灭了邪兽，然后收工。

Text.en: I'll not concern myself with the motives of dead men. What matters is we wield it true.
Text.zh: 我不会去想那些古人发什么神经。重要的是我们把它用好。

Text.en: Now, we need allies. <continue>I believe we have earned good will throughout Wraeclast. <continue>It is time to call upon those debts. <continue>One last journey, recruiting any who will join us, before we assault Oriath. <continue>The Karui are already with us. We should call upon the Ezomytes and the Maraketh.
Text.zh: 现在，我们需要盟友。<continue>我相信我们在瓦尔克拉斯已经积累了不少人情。<continue>是时候要他们兑现这些人情债了。<continue>最后一次征程，招募一切愿意加入我们的力量，然后攻打奥瑞亚。<continue>卡鲁人已经站在我们这边。我们还应该争取艾兹麦人和马拉克斯人。

Text.en: As it so happens, I may... also have some forces in reserve.
Text.zh: 恰好，我或许……也有一些后备力量。

Text.en: Forces in reserve? You mean <i>{living} Vaal?
Text.zh: 后备力量？你是说<i>{活着的}瓦尔人？

Text.en: Where would you be hiding something like that?
Text.zh: 你会把这种东西藏在哪？

Text.en: You have <i>{living} warriors? Where?
Text.zh: 你还有<i>{活着的}战士？在哪儿？

Text.en: What? Living Vaal? How?
Text.zh: 什么？活的瓦尔人？怎么可能？

Text.en: Really? Where might they be?
Text.zh: 真的吗？他们会在哪？

Text.en: What does <i>{that} mean?
Text.zh: <i>{此言}何意？

Text.en: You're a tricky one, aren't ya, mate?
Text.zh: 你还挺狡猾的，不是吗伙计？

Text.en: Oh, kept that close to the chest, didn't ye?
Text.zh: 哦，你这嘴可真严啊。

Text.en: Suffice to say, I've always got a contingency plan.
Text.zh: 这么说吧，我总是留有后手。

Text.en: On we go, then, to build an alliance against the end.
Text.zh: 那我们继续，去组建对抗末日的联盟。

Text.en: To Ogham, then. Hopefully, they've had time to rebuild.
Text.zh: 那就去欧甘。希望他们有时间重建家园。

Text.en: At this time of year, I know where to find the Ardura <i>{akhara.} Let us travel to the Vastiri.
Text.zh: 在一年中的这个时节，我知道哪能找到阿杜拉人的<i>{阿卡拉}。我们前往瓦斯提里吧。

Text.en: Let us seek out Doryani's reserves... whatever they may be.
Text.zh: 让我们去寻找多里亚尼的后备力量……不管那是什么。

Text.en: It has been thousands of years since I've seen an alliance such as this. <continue>Wraeclast has finally united the way I always hoped it would. <continue>Come, let us plan our assault on Oriath.
Text.zh: 我已经几千年没见过这样的联盟了。<continue>瓦尔克拉斯终于以我一直期望的方式团结起来。<continue>来吧，让我们筹划对奥瑞亚的进攻。

Text.en: I've been working on a strategy of sorts.
Text.zh: 我一直在制定某种战略。

Text.en: Let's hope it's a good one this time...
Text.zh: 希望这次是个靠谱的战略……

Text.en: That's good. For once, I don't think blasting my way in will work.
Text.zh: 很好。这一回，我觉得一路轰炸过去行不通了。

Text.en: Indeed, the time of heroes is upon us once more.
Text.zh: 没错，英雄时代再次来临。

Text.en: I'm ready. Let's end this.
Text.zh: 我准备好了。让我们终结这一切。

Text.en: The final rush of the hunt.
Text.zh: 狩猎的最终冲刺。

Text.en: The end is nigh, one way or another.
Text.zh: 终局将至，无论结果如何。

Text.en: I'm glad you're with us, boss.
Text.zh: 我很庆幸你在我们身边，老大。

Text.en: Then let's be done with it. Time the Beast met its end.
Text.zh: 那就准备动手吧。是时候送邪兽上路了。

Text.en: The time has come. <continue>We can only hope our alliance will be enough. <continue>That fateful hour... a return to Oriath. We can set sail immediately.
Text.zh: 时候到了。<continue>我们只能祈祷联盟的力量足够强大。<continue>那决定性的时刻……重返奥瑞亚。我们随时可以启航。

Text.en: How far we've come... and now, to war.
Text.zh: 我们走了这么远……现在，奔赴战场。

Text.en: Oriath. How I've always wanted to see that fell city from the inside.
Text.zh: 奥瑞亚。我一直想从内部见识见识那座罪恶之城。

Text.en: Let us show the world that we will not allow it to be destroyed!
Text.zh: 让我们证明，我们绝不允许世界惨遭毁灭！

Text.en: I feel calm. And dread. All at once.
Text.zh: 我感到平静。还有恐惧。两种情绪并存。

Text.en: Spirit guide me.
Text.zh: 愿万灵指引我。

Text.en: The Dreamer leads and I follow.
Text.zh: 幻梦者引路，我紧随其后。

Text.en: Lock n' load.
Text.zh: 荷枪实弹，准备上阵。

Text.en: To war, then - and whatever end awaits.
Text.zh: 那就开战吧——无论前路如何。

Text.en: The Weapon is radiating intense Corruption... <continue>It will sicken and madden all those who near it... <continue>Quickly... he has returned to Ngakanu... <continue>You must stop him!
Text.zh: 武器正在释放强烈的腐化能量……<continue>它会让所有靠近的人生病发疯……<continue>快点……他已经回到纳卡努了……<continue>你必须阻止他！

Text.en: Have no fear. I am both here <i>{and} there. Just as you are now.
Text.zh: 别害怕。我既在这里<i>{也}在那里。正如你现在的状态一样。

Text.en: With respect, Honoured One, how is this possible?
Text.zh: 恕我直言，尊者，这怎么可能做到？

Text.en: This isn't necromancy... what <i>{is} this?
Text.zh: 这不是通灵法术……到底<i>{是}什么？

Text.en: It cannot be. This is some sort of strange illusion.
Text.zh: 不可能。这是某种诡异的幻象。

Text.en: It sounds like a riddle, yet it must be fact. How?
Text.zh: 听上去像谜语，但肯定是事实。怎么做到的？

Text.en: This is magic beyond the fey. Beyond the First Ones. How?
Text.zh: 这是超越精灵的魔法。超越初生之子的。如何做到的？

Text.en: By the Spirit! How've you done this?
Text.zh: 以万灵之名！你是怎么办到的？

Text.en: Am I hallucinating? I quit the opium dens years ago.
Text.zh: 我在出现幻觉吗？我几年前就戒掉鸦片了。

Text.en: How can that be?
Text.zh: 怎么会这样？

Text.en: The Water of Sight does not slay the living, just nearly so. <continue>The Water of Sight does not raise the dead... just nearly so. <continue>On the verge, we have one eye in each world. <continue>And so, I serve Hinekora. I am her Speaker, both in life... and in death.
Text.zh: 灵视圣水不会杀死生者，只会让人濒死。<continue>灵视圣水不会让死者复生……只会让人接近死亡。<continue>在生死边缘，我们在两个世界都各有一只眼睛。<continue>因此，我侍奉辛格拉。我是她的神谕者，在生时……也在死后。

Text.en: This place... is an afterlife? For all Karui?
Text.zh: 这个地方……是所有卡鲁人的来世吗？

Text.en: I almost don't want to question further... but I can't help myself.
Text.zh: 我几乎不想继续追问……但我控制不住自己。

Text.en: I still cannot comprehend this place. It makes no sense.
Text.zh: 我仍然无法理解这个地方。这完全说不通。

Text.en: Intriguing. The Karui have their own unique afterlife.
Text.zh: 有意思。卡鲁人有他们专属的来世。

Text.en: The only dead I've seen come back to life... well, that's no fate to wish upon anyone.
Text.zh: 我见过的唯一复活的死者……嗯，那不是任何人都愿意承受的命运。

Text.en: I feel sick. Please, let me wake up from whatever this is.
Text.zh: 我觉得恶心。拜托让我从这个鬼地方醒过来。

Text.en: So... you're just lying there, having a real deep snooze then?
Text.zh: 所以……你就是躺在那里，睡得很香？

Text.en: So death's no rest at all. Just... echoes that never fade.
Text.zh: 原来死亡并非安息。不过是……永不消散的余响。

Text.en: The Mother of Death is all that stands between us and oblivion. <continue>Before she embraced us, those that died simply ceased to be. <continue>Now, the souls of the Karui people continue on, lending their wisdom and strength to those that follow...
Text.zh: 死亡之母是我们和湮灭之间唯一的阻拦。<continue>在她接纳我们之前，逝者都会直接灰飞烟灭。<continue>如今，卡鲁人的灵魂都可以存续，继续向信徒传授智慧和力量。……

Text.en: Our Mother of Death is gifted with the ability to see into the past. <continue>Her prophecies guide us on the eternal search for the way forward.
Text.zh: 我们的死亡之母拥有洞察过去的天赋。<continue>她的预言指引我们永无止境地寻找前进之路。

Text.en: How does seeing into the past help guide us?
Text.zh: 洞察过去如何帮助指引我们？

Text.en: This sounds suspiciously like a fanciful claim in the forbidden tomes...
Text.zh: 这听起来像禁忌典籍里那些可疑的奇幻说法……

Text.en: Our tale-women do something similar. The tales they tell guide us.
Text.zh: 我们的说书人也干类似的事。我们的传说为我们指路。

Text.en: She sees into the past? How does that create prophecy?
Text.zh: 她能看到过去？这如何产生预言？

Text.en: Where do her prophecies come from, if she only sees the past?
Text.zh: 如果她只能看到过去，她的预言从哪来？

Text.en: What am I missin'? She sees the past, but how does that make it prophecy?
Text.zh: 我搞不明白？她能看到过去，可这怎么能产生预言呢？

Text.en: So what, she learns from your mistakes? Makes some new plans?
Text.zh: 什么意思，她从你们的错误中吸取教训？制定新的计划？

Text.en: She sees the past... but divines the future?
Text.zh: 她能洞悉过往……但能预知未来吗？

Text.en: Before Wraeclast, before the Lake, before the primordial molten world, before the stars themselves... <continue>Time circled upon itself into infinity. <continue>Hinekora sees not what you <i>{will} do, only what you <i>{did,} and what you shall do again. <continue>Probably...
Text.zh: 在瓦尔克拉斯，在卡兰德之湖，在原始的熔火世界，在群星诞生之前……<continue>时间环绕着自身并化为永恒。<continue>辛格拉没有看到你<i>{将会}做什么，只看到你<i>{做过}什么。<continue>也许……

Text.en: What did you mean by 'Time circled upon itself?'
Text.zh: 你说的“时间自我循环”是什么意思？

Text.en: I've read a claim like this once before, but I'm still skeptical. Tell me about this supposed 'Cycle of Time.'
Text.zh: 我以前读过类似的观点，但我依然保持怀疑。跟我讲讲这所谓的“时间循环”。

Text.en: Our tales have no mention of this 'circling of Time.' What is that?
Text.zh: 我们的传说里没有这种“时间循环”。那是什么？

Text.en: I've... dreamt about this... How does Time circle upon itself...?
Text.zh: 我曾……梦到过……时间是如何自我循环的？

Text.en: What's this about Time circling upon itself?
Text.zh: 这个时间自我循环是怎么回事？

Text.en: My Elders once told me a story about a place where Time is a circle. What does that mean?
Text.zh: 我的长者曾经跟我讲过一个故事，说有个地方时间是个圆环。这意味着什么？

Text.en: Time circled upon itself, eh? Do I dare ask?
Text.zh: 时间自我循环，是吗？我劳烦打听一下可以吗？

Text.en: So ye're sayin' all this has happened before... and will again?
Text.zh: 你是说……这一切以前也曾上演，且必将重演？

Text.en: One day, Time will end. Then, Time will begin again. <continue>Hinekora remembers the Future-Past. Not very clearly, but enough to guide our way forward. <continue>You do not remember our previous meetings... but I do, by the grace of Hinekora's sight.
Text.zh: 终有一日，时间会走到尽头。然后，时间会重新开始。<continue>辛格拉记得未来的过去。虽然不太清楚，但足够为我们指引方向。<continue>你不记得我们以前的见面……但凭借辛格拉的视觉恩赐，我记得。

Text.en: I shouldn't have asked.
Text.zh: 我真不该多嘴的。

Text.en: Some ends are peaceful, even quiet. <continue>Some are bloody, loud, and reckless. <continue>Some are lonely... and those are the worst. <continue>But the End of Time is not to be feared. It is a release. A mercy. <continue>All the pains of a long and weary existence will fall away from us. <continue>Then... we shall see the world with innocent eyes once more.
Text.zh: 有些结局是平静的，甚至寂静无声。<continue>有些则充满血腥、喧嚣和疯狂。<continue>有些是孤独的……那些最让人心碎。<continue>但时间的终结无需恐惧。它是解脱。是仁慈。<continue>漫长疲惫人生中的所有苦痛都会从我们身上剥落。<continue>然后……我们将再次用纯真的眼睛凝视世界。

Text.en: I never had the chance to feel young and free... <continue>Are we doomed to do this all again?
Text.zh: 我从来没有机会感受年轻和自由……<continue>我们命中注定要重新经历这一切吗？

Text.en: Hah, innocent eyes? Sure. <continue>So, you truly believe in the Cycle of Time?
Text.zh: 哈，纯真的眼睛？好吧。<continue>所以，你真心相信时间循环这回事？

Text.en: Ah, I see. You fear the end, but long for the new beginning.
Text.zh: 啊，我懂了。你畏惧终结，却向往崭新的开始。

Text.en: You speak of renewal. Of new life.
Text.zh: 你说的是重生。崭新的生命。

Text.en: That sounds... lovely. <continue>Like the cycle of life, the end is not to be feared.
Text.zh: 那听起来……很美妙。<continue>就像生命的轮回，终结并不可怕。

Text.en: I've never felt so small. It's humbling.
Text.zh: 我从未感到如此微不足道。这很让人谦卑。

Text.en: Forgive me, miss, but it sounds like you <i>{remember} all these dooms.
Text.zh: 恕我直言，女士，但听起来你<i>{记得}所有这些毁灭。

Text.en:
Ye call it mercy... but what mercy's there in livin' the same hurts, over and over, with no chance to mend 'em?  
<continue>Sounds like no matter what I do... I'm bound to end up here again.
Text.zh:
您称之为慈悲……可若注定要反复承受同样的伤痛，却永远无法愈合，这算什么慈悲？
<continue>看来无论我如何挣扎……终将重归此地，周而复始。

Text.en: It is not a question of belief. <continue>Hinekora has seen it - and through her eyes, so have I.
Text.zh: 无关信与不信。<continue>辛格拉已预见此番因果——借她之眼，我亦看穿因果。

Text.en: They are one and the same. <continue>The end gives birth to the beginning. The beginning, to the end.
Text.zh: 始与终本是一体。<continue>终结孕育开端，开端暗藏终结。

Text.en: [NOAUDIO]  And in between... I will end up back here.
Text.zh: 而在两者之间……我终将重归此地。

Text.en: All life is renewal. All death, return. <continue>The circle closes, only to open again.
Text.zh: 生即是新，死即是归。<continue>完成循环之日，亦是重启之时。

Text.en: And at the end of it all, we'll be here... having the same conversation.
Text.zh: 待万物终末，你我将在此地……重演此番对话。

Text.en: You are not small, child. Only part of something vast.
Text.zh: 你并非渺小，孩子。仅是浩瀚中的片段。

Text.en: And this <i>{vastness} just... never ends?
Text.zh: 而这<i>{浩瀚}……永无尽头？

Text.en: It ends... and begins. And ends, and begins. On and on into eternity.
Text.zh: 它终结……又开始。终结，又开始。直至永恒。

Text.en: I do, and so much more besides.
Text.zh: 我确实记得，而且远不止于此。

Text.en: What about this? Do you remember this chat we're havin' right now?
Text.zh: 那现在呢？你可回忆起此刻这番对话？

Text.en: Yes. You and I shall have this conversation again... <continue>...as we have before... many times.
Text.zh: 是的。你我将再次进行这番对话……<continue>……正如我们以前……无数次那样。

Text.en: Of course. And how long is one of these Cycles, supposedly?
Text.zh: 当然。据说这些循环中的一次有多长？

Text.en: The life of an Elder, but twice, a hundred times over, then thrice and once.
Text.zh: 一个长者的寿命，乘二，再乘百，然后乘三加一。

Text.en: That's... um... quite specific...?
Text.zh: 那……呃……相当精确啊……？

Text.en: That is the longest we have ever endured. But really, It depends on the nature of the end...
Text.zh: 那是我们忍受过的最长时间。但实际上，这要看终结的性质……

Text.en: When are we now, compared to all that?
Text.zh: 与这一切相比，我们现在身处何时？

Text.en: There are very few prophecies that have yet to come to pass.
Text.zh: 很少有预言尚未成真。

Text.en: So... this is what fear feels like... I don't think I like it.
Text.zh: 所以……这就是恐惧的滋味……我觉得我不喜欢这种感觉。

Text.en: Time passes slowly for me, but it does pass. My service is not endless. <continue>The renowned <i>{hatungo} Narumoa served before me, and others served before her.
Text.zh: 对我而言时间过得很慢，但确实在流逝。我的使命不是永无止境的。<continue>著名的<i>{哈图纳格斯}纳鲁摩奥在我之前履职，还有其他人在她之前履职。

Text.en: What will happen to you when your service ends?
Text.zh: 当你的使命结束时会怎样？

Text.en: And when your time is up - what then?
Text.zh: 等你的时间到了—然后呢？

Text.en: How could you step aside, knowing what you know?
Text.zh: 明知道自己知道的一切，你怎么能退位？

Text.en: I see. One day, you will be free.
Text.zh: 我明白了。总有一天，你会重获自由。

Text.en: One day... they'll just replace you?
Text.zh: 总有一天……他们就会取代你？

Text.en: It'd be a hard role to give up, I bet.
Text.zh: 我觉得那个位置很难割舍吧。

Text.en: Guess we're all just footmen in someone's war, eh? Burdened by our memories.
Text.zh: 我猜我们都只是别人战争中的棋子，对吧？被我们的记忆所拖累。

Text.en: And when yer service ends? What then?
Text.zh: 待你卸下这身差事……之后呢？

Text.en: I can only remember my Future-Past while my soul is bound to Hinekora. <continue>Life and death draw ever closer together...  <continue>...and oblivion lies in wait.
Text.zh: 只有当我的灵魂与辛格拉相连时，我才能记起我的未来的过去。<continue>生死之间的距离越来越近……<continue>……而遗忘在旁等候。

Text.en: Hinekora sees you. The past is laid bare before her. She knows you. <continue>But now... she listens. <continue>You may speak.
Text.zh: 辛格拉看见了你。过去在她面前毫无遮掩。她知晓你的一切。<continue>但现在……她在聆听。<continue>你可以说话了。

Text.en: Hinekora... I am honoured. I would ask you, how do I find my true family? How do I learn of my tribe?
Text.zh: 辛格拉……我万分荣幸。我想请问您，我如何找到我真正的血亲？我如何了解我的部族？

Text.en: The greatest smith crafts armour for the heart. The joy of today is the only happiness most ever find. A good man stands in for the absent.
Text.zh: 最伟大的匠人，锻造的是护心之甲。今日之欢愉，便是众生仅有的幸福。有人倒下，有德行的人便挺身而出。

Text.en: I believe... I understand. Thank you.
Text.zh: 我相信……我明白了。感谢您。

Text.en: From one Mother of Death to another, what words of wisdom do you have for me?
Text.zh: 我们均为死亡之母，你有什么智慧话语赐给我？

Text.en: They scarred and violated the hated criminal, but she punishes herself far more than they ever could. The wounds are obvious for all to see, despite the concealing bandages.
Text.zh: 他们给那个可恨的罪犯留下了伤疤与屈辱，但她对自己的惩罚却远比他们的手段更严酷。尽管绷带试图遮掩，但那伤痕却明显得无人不晓。

Text.en: I... don't know what she's talking about. That's just... rambling nonsense.
Text.zh: 我……不知道她在说什么。那就是……一堆胡话。

Text.en: Tell me, Mother of Death, will I find my place in this world?
Text.zh: 告诉我，死亡之母，我能否在这世上找到自己的位置？

Text.en: A weary traveler stumbles down the mountain, smiling, only to see a vast range ahead. <continue>Surrounded by watching faces, a child plays in the sand.
Text.zh: 疲惫的旅人踉跄下山，面带笑意，但见前方山脉连绵不绝。<continue>孩童在注视的目光下，嬉戏于砂砾之中。

Text.en: Oh! Is he... happy?
Text.zh: 哦！他……快乐吗？

Text.en: Yes.
Text.zh: 能。

Text.en: That's... good. That is... good to hear.
Text.zh: 这……很好。听你这么说……真的很好。

Text.en: If I may ask... I seek the truth of my beliefs. I suspect, I fear...
Text.zh: 请容我一问……我追问我所信奉之道的真相。我隐隐怀疑，我还担心……

Text.en: A foreign prince brings a sliver of hope to a land in peril. <continue>A rat gnaws its way out of the crocodile's stomach.
Text.zh: 异国王子为危境中的土地带来一丝希望。<continue>老鼠啃穿鳄鱼的肚腹，得以逃生。

Text.en: Yes.. it certainly does...
Text.zh: 对……确实如此……

Text.en: Did my mother's soul pass this way? If not, where might I find her? Does she still exist... somewhere?
Text.zh: 我妈妈的灵魂可曾路过这里？如果没有，我该去哪里找她？她是不是还存在在……某个地方？

Text.en: One alone will stand on dunes of silver and breathe deep the void. <continue>The leaping cat catches the mouse, but loses its way. <continue>It will never return home. Nor will it make amends.
Text.zh: 孤身立于银色沙丘，深深呼吸虚无。<continue>腾跃的猫擒住硕鼠，却迷失了归途。<continue>永无归家之日，永无弥补之时。

Text.en: I fear I can never forgive her... but I have to try.
Text.zh: 我不知道我能不能原谅她……但我必须试试。

Text.en: I have but one question, honoured Mother of Death. How do I hear the Spirit's voice again?
Text.zh: 我只有一个问题，尊敬的死亡之母。我如何才能再次听到万灵的声音？

Text.en: Whispered words offer guidance to the pure, but silence is a - <continue>...yes... now?... <continue>The sea goes still.
Text.zh: 耳畔低语只为纯净者指引，而沉默乃则是<continue>……是的……现在？……<continue>大海归于沉寂。

Text.en: What? That's it? What's happening?
Text.zh: 什么？就这样？发生了什么？

Text.en: So what do ya think, Miss Hinekora? Are we gonna survive this?
Text.zh: 那么辛格拉女士，你觉得我们能从这次危机中活下来吗？

Text.en: Night promises peace, but the sun refuses to set. <continue>There is no one waiting on the other side of the river.
Text.zh: 长夜许诺安宁，烈日却拒不西沉。<continue>河流对岸，无人等候。

Text.en: You... you what? You can't just... you can't say things like that, mate...
Text.zh: 你……你什么？你不能……你不能说这种话，伙计……

Text.en: If ye've wisdom to share, Mother of Death... let's hear it. What awaits?
Text.zh: 死亡之母啊，若您真有智慧相授……但请明示。前路将会如何？

Text.en: A wound unhealed, though the years smother it in dust. <continue>One day, the dust will blow away. The wound will bleed again.
Text.zh: 一道永不愈合的伤口，岁月徒然将其掩于尘埃。<continue>待到风起尘散之日，伤口终将再度泣血。

Text.en: Och... Clear as mud, that is.
Text.zh: 唉……说得如此云山雾罩。

Text.en: The Mother of Death... has spoken. <continue>And... gives a gift!
Text.zh: 死亡之母……已经开口了。<continue>而且……赐予了礼物！

Text.en: The Mother of Death... has spoken.
Text.zh: 死亡之母……已经开口了。

Text.en: You will one day be welcomed here.  <continue>Hinekora invites you to fight in our Trial of the Ancestors, to test yourself to your absolute limits. <continue>But now is not that time. <continue>When the time comes, you will know...
Text.zh: 总有一天你会在这里受到欢迎。<continue>辛格拉邀请你参加我们的命运试炼，将自己考验到极致。<continue>但现在还不是时候。<continue>时机到来时，你自然会明白……

Text.en: It is a great honour to participate in our Trial of the Ancestors. <continue>It is very rare that an outsider is allowed to participate.
Text.zh: 参与我们的命运试炼是无上的荣耀。<continue>外来者获准参加极其稀少。

Text.en: I am honoured to be part of this heritage.
Text.zh: 我很荣幸能成为这份传承的一部分。

Text.en: Of course. I'm rather exceptional.
Text.zh: 当然。我相当杰出。

Text.en: I will respect and honour your ways.
Text.zh: 我会敬重并遵循你们的传统。

Text.en: I am honoured, and I will attend.
Text.zh: 我深感荣幸，我会参加。

Text.en: Alright. Maybe I'll come by.
Text.zh: 好的。也许我会去。

Text.en: You have my thanks, and I will do my best.
Text.zh: 谢谢你们，我会竭尽全力。

Text.en: No need for all that, I'm game.
Text.zh: 不必如此客套，我乐意参加。

Text.en: What makes me so special, then?
Text.zh: 那我究竟何德何能，得此殊荣？

Text.en: You will know when the time is right. <continue>For now, we are not undertaking the Trials. Too many are... refusing.
Text.zh: 时机成熟时你自然会明白。<continue>现在，我们暂停试炼。目前有太多人在……挣扎抵抗。

Text.en: One among us has elevated their angry voice above the others. <continue>Utula, of the Kitava Tribe, has long found the afterlife... constricting. <continue>He has convinced several others to become unruly. <continue>They are refusing to fight, and causing great disturbances.
Text.zh: 我们中有一个人的愤怒之声盖过了其他所有人。<continue>奇塔弗部落的尤图拉，长久以来都认为来世……令人窒息。<continue>他说服了其他几个人，使其变得叛逆不驯。<continue>他们拒绝战斗，引发巨大的骚乱。

Text.en: Why are they being so disrespectful?
Text.zh: 他们为何如此失礼？

Text.en: Is being dead not to his liking?
Text.zh: 死了都不消停吗？

Text.en: That sounds like a great dishonour.
Text.zh: 这听起来是莫大的耻辱。

Text.en: The dead can do such things?
Text.zh: 死者也能干这种事？

Text.en: Why're they doin' that?
Text.zh: 他们为什么要这样？

Text.en: I don't understand. Why would he do that?
Text.zh: 我不理解。他为什么要这么做？

Text.en: What's up his arse?
Text.zh: 他哪根筋搭错了？

Text.en: Bah. Even the dead cannae let well enough alone.
Text.zh: 啧，连亡者都非要搅弄风云。

Text.en: Utula has convinced them they could overthrow this place, or even escape. <continue>We have done the best we can for the souls here, but it is not enough for some. <continue>This often happens with souls new to this timeless existence. <continue>It will pass... as it always does.
Text.zh: 尤图拉说服他们相信能够推翻这个地方，甚至从中逃脱。<continue>我们已经为这里的灵魂尽了最大努力，但对某些人而言仍然不足。<continue>这种情况常常发生在刚踏入这种永恒存在的新灵魂身上。<continue>这会过去的……一如往常。

Text.en: The Mother waited for you! The years come and go. Still, she waits. <continue>Last time, an outsider here, very disappointing! Every failure brings less hope.
Text.zh: 死亡之母一直在等候你！年岁更迭。她仍在等待。<continue>上一次，有个外来者来到这里，极其令人失望！每次失败都让希望愈发渺茫。

Text.en: Less hope? Why?
Text.zh: 希望渺茫？为什么？

Text.en: Of course they were disappointing. They weren't <i>{me.}
Text.zh: 他们当然令人失望。他们可不是<i>{我}。

Text.en: When did this happen?
Text.zh: 那是什么时候？

Text.en: Why? What happened?
Text.zh: 什么，出了什么事？

Text.en: What are you sayin'?
Text.zh: 你在说什么？

Text.en: Did another come here before me?
Text.zh: 在我之前还有其他人来过这里？

Text.en: So, the usual?
Text.zh: 所以，又是老一套？

Text.en: Another outsider? Who was it?
Text.zh: 又来个外来者？这次是谁？

Text.en: You think you know. You imagine more. <continue>Cannot imagine... <i>{longer than you think!}
Text.zh: 你自以为明白。你想象得过多。<continue>无法想象……<i>{比你以为的更久！}

Text.en: I'm not sure I understand.
Text.zh: 我不确定我明白。

Text.en: I'm losing patience with this, monkey.
Text.zh: 我对这个话题失去耐心了，猴子。

Text.en: Don't be so sure. The Maraketh have a very long memory.
Text.zh: 别太自负。马拉克斯人记忆力好得很。

Text.en: Are you just saying random things?
Text.zh: 你只是在说些莫名其妙的话吗？

Text.en: Er, what?
Text.zh: 呃，什么？

Text.en: I don't understand your wisdom, but I'll consider it.
Text.zh: 我不理解你的智慧，但我会深思。

Text.en: I dunno, I can imagine pretty long.
Text.zh: 我不知道，我能想象得相当久。

Text.en: Time is a river, aye. Wider than any man - or <i>{monkey} - can see.
Text.zh: 时间如长河，没错。其广阔远超凡人——抑或<i>{猴子}——所能窥见。

Text.en: Ahahahaha!
Text.zh: 啊哈哈哈哈！

Text.en: Yama has known many Speakers. Walk among the dead, walk among the living, but belong to neither! <continue>Mmm... Navali is kind. Kindest of all!
Text.zh: 雅玛见过许多神谕者。在死者中游走，在生者中游走，但两边都不归属！<continue>嗯……娜瓦莉很仁慈。是最仁慈的！

Text.en: The kindest? Why?
Text.zh: 最仁慈？为什么？

Text.en: What makes <i>{her} so special then?
Text.zh: 是什么让<i>{她}如此特别？

Text.en: This is how it should be. Tale-women should always be kind.
Text.zh: 本应如此。说书人本就应该仁慈。

Text.en: That's... nice.
Text.zh: 那……很不错。

Text.en: What does she do?
Text.zh: 她是做什么的？

Text.en: The kindest? In what way?
Text.zh: 最仁慈？表现在哪些方面？

Text.en: What, like she gives ya treats or somethin'?
Text.zh: 什么，比如她给你小点心之类的？

Text.en: Is she now? How so?
Text.zh: 真的吗？何以见得？

Text.en: Give Yama nuts! The others never did... <continue>They were dead, risen. One heartbeat Navali still has, every shortest day.
Text.zh: 给雅玛坚果！其他人从来不给……<continue>他们都死了，复活了。娜瓦莉仍有一次心跳，在每个最短暂的日子里。

Text.en: No destiny, no fate... and no hope yet. <continue>The Mother sees only failure!
Text.zh: 没有宿命，没有命运……也暂无希望。<continue>死亡之母只看见失败！

Text.en: Only failure? When?
Text.zh: 只有失败？什么时候？

Text.en: I make my own fate, thank you.
Text.zh: 我创造自己的命运，多谢。

Text.en: Why does she only see failure? It must be sad for her.
Text.zh: 为什么她眼里只有失败？这对她来说一定很可悲。

Text.en: Then what do we do?
Text.zh: 那我们该如何是好？

Text.en: That don't bode well.
Text.zh: 这可不是好兆头。

Text.en: On the path ahead, or the path behind?
Text.zh: 在前路上，还是后路上？

Text.en: Odds be damned.
Text.zh: 管它什么概率。

Text.en: Failure, ye say? Then what chance have <i>{we?}
Text.zh: 失败？那<i>{咱们}还能有什么胜算？

Text.en: We try this, we try that, and we fail. You fail. She remembers all.
Text.zh: 我们试这个，试那个，然后失败。你失败。她统统记得。

Text.en: She remembers? Remembers what?
Text.zh: 她记得？记得什么？

Text.en: I'm not going to fail. Trust me on that.
Text.zh: 我不会失败。相信我的话。

Text.en: We must defy history. We must succeed.
Text.zh: 我们必须对抗历史。我们必须成功。

Text.en: She... remembers?
Text.zh: 她……记得？

Text.en: I fail? What can I do differently?
Text.zh: 我失败了？我能做什么不同的事？

Text.en: Is there any hope left?
Text.zh: 还有希望剩下吗？

Text.en: Job's the job, though. I won't give up.
Text.zh: 该干的活还得干，我不会放弃。

Text.en: She remembers... <i>{everything}?
Text.zh: 她记得……<i>{一切}？

Text.en: Hope is a tasty worm, crawling on an unknown leaf... <continue>Cut down trees, one by one... the worm might still hide, haha! <continue>But... the forest grows thin.
Text.zh: 希望是条美味的虫，在未知的叶片上爬行……<continue>一棵棵砍倒树木……虫子或许依然藏匿，哈哈！<continue>但是……森林日渐稀疏。

Text.en: And the fall of night grows near.
Text.zh: 然后夜幕也悄悄逼近了。

Text.en: Oh dear the monkey is getting philosophical. Get me out of here.
Text.zh: 天啊，这猴子又开始讲人生哲理了。快带我出去透透气。

Text.en: And one day, the forest could vanish entirely.
Text.zh: 总有一天，森林会消失殆尽。

Text.en: I will persevere.
Text.zh: 我会一直坚持下去。

Text.en: I understand.
Text.zh: 我明白了。

Text.en: We are running out of chances...
Text.zh: 机会已所剩无几。

Text.en: Alright mate. Whatever you say.
Text.zh: 好吧伙计。你爱怎么说就怎么说。

Text.en: Ach! Speak plain, damn ye!
Text.zh: 呸！说人话，该死的！

Text.en: Hah! Yama speak plain as the wind. You cannot hold, but it cuts just the same.
Text.zh: 哈！雅玛说话像风一样直白。你抓不住它，可还是会被割得生疼。

Text.en: Ah! You beat Yama. Yama is impressed... but not surprised! <continue>We saw you. Yes. saw! Haha! The Mother of Death sees all.
Text.zh: 啊！你战胜了雅玛。雅玛觉得你很厉害……但并不意外！<continue>我们早就看到你了。是的，早就看到了！哈哈！死亡之母洞察一切。

Text.en: I must concur with... the Hooded One. Someone intentionally carved information meant to be discovered in the future. <continue>To do such a thing, they must have believed that their civilisation might be endangered. <continue>However, the most important clues I found were done differently. Hand carvings. <continue>We found them in this region. The stones depicted pieces of the Weapon being thrown into the sea.
Text.zh: 我必须同意……兜帽密师的观点。有人刻意雕刻了意在未来被发现的信息。<continue>要做这样的事，他们必须相信自己的文明可能遭遇危险。<continue>然而，我发现的最重要线索采用了不同的方式完成。手工雕刻。<continue>我们在这一区域发现了它们。描绘着武器碎片的石头，被抛入了大海。

Text.en: Did someone want to get rid of it?
Text.zh: 有人想要处置掉它吗？

Text.en: Someone didn't want it around?
Text.zh: 所以有人不希望它存在？

Text.en: Why would someone try to dispose of it?
Text.zh: 为什么会有人试图销毁它？

Text.en: Why'd they try to get rid of it?
Text.zh: 他们为什么要试图丢弃它？

Text.en: Someone tried to make it missin'?
Text.zh: 有人想让它消失？

Text.en: Interesting. Someone endeavoured to make it disappear?
Text.zh: 有趣。有人试图让它彻底消失？

Text.en: Someone wanted it gone then, eh?
Text.zh: 这么说，有人希望它消失，对吧？

Text.en: So they sought to bury it... but why?
Text.zh: 那他们是想要埋葬它……但这是为什么呢？

Text.en: Their reasoning was unclear. <continue>But from what I saw, it was thrown not by soldiers, but by a woman. A scientist... <continue>... and I have no idea who she was.
Text.zh: 他们的动机不明。<continue>但据我观察，抛掷它的不是士兵，而是一个女人。一个学者……<continue>……我不知道她的身份。

Text.en: What do you think the Edicts were?
Text.zh: 你认为那些敕令是什么？

Text.en: What of these... Edicts then?
Text.zh: 那么这些……敕令呢？

Text.en: What were these 'Edicts'?
Text.zh: 这些“敕令”是什么？

Text.en: Do you know what the Edicts were?
Text.zh: 你知道这些敕令是什么吗？

Text.en: What were these 'Edicts'?
Text.zh: 这些“敕令”究竟是什么？

Text.en: Do we have any information on the Edicts?
Text.zh: 我们对敕令有任何了解吗？

Text.en: Know anything about them 'Edicts'?
Text.zh: 知道那些“敕令”的情况吗？

Text.en: These 'Edicts'... what are they?
Text.zh: 那些“敕令”……究竟是什么？

Text.en: We have very little information, but if the Seed of Corruption was the instrument of the Second Edict... <continue>It may have been created in response to an unknown <i>{First} Edict.
Text.zh: 我们掌握的信息寥寥无几，但如果腐化之种是第二敕令的器具……<continue>它可能是为了应对某个未知的<i>{第一}敕令而被创造出来的。

Text.en: The first mural I saw was broken... but there was definitely something there. <continue>They must have been facing some disaster... or enemy... or change in the world. <continue>Did they create the Seed of Corruption in response?
Text.zh: 我看到的第一幅壁画已经破损……但肯定曾有内容。<continue>他们必然面临着某种灾难……或敌人……或世界的剧变。<continue>他们是为了应对而创造了腐化之种吗？

Text.en: Hmm. Whenever the Vaal built something dangerous, we also ran a parallel project... <continue>...as a contingency. <continue>The Weapon. The instrument of the Third Edict. I believe it is a failsafe. <continue>If the Seed of Corruption grew out of control, then perhaps it was designed to... shut down the experiment.
Text.zh: 嗯。每当瓦尔人建造危险物品时，我们也会同步开展相应的项目……<continue>以此作为应急预案。<continue>武器。第三敕令的器具。我相信它是安全装置。<continue>如果腐化之种失去控制，那么也许它的设计初衷就是……终止这项实验。

Text.en: Then that is exactly what we will do.
Text.zh: 那我们就这样决定了。

Text.en: After this is over... you want the Weapon, don't you? To go back and prevent your Cataclysm?
Text.zh: 这件事了结之后……你想要那把武器，对不对？你想回去阻止你们那里的灾变？

Text.en: Come now, Doryani. You clearly want to take back the Weapon to save your people. I'm not stupid.
Text.zh: 得了吧，多里亚尼。你显然想带着武器回去拯救你的子民。我又不是傻子。

Text.en: Do you intend to go back and stop your Cataclysm after this?
Text.zh: 你是不是打算回去阻止你们那里的灾变？

Text.en: One day, will you go back and save your people with the Weapon?
Text.zh: 总有一天，你会带着武器回去拯救你的子民吗？

Text.en: What will you do, when all this is over? Will you return to your people and try to save them?
Text.zh: 一切结束后你准备做什么？回到你的子民那里试图拯救他们？

Text.en: What of your people? Will you try to save them with the Weapon, when this is over?
Text.zh: 你的子民呢？事了之后你会试图用武器拯救他们吗？

Text.en: Well, how about the Vaal? We got this Third Edict thing - can we save them, too?
Text.zh: 那瓦尔人怎么办？我们有这个第三敕令，能救救他们吗？

Text.en: Speak plain - when this is over, will ye wield the Weapon to save yer people?
Text.zh: 直说吧——等这一切结束，你会动用那武器去拯救你的人民吗？

Text.en: I cannot. Unfortunately, that is not how Time works. <continue>Everything one <i>{knows} will happen, <i>{did} happen. <continue>This is the one case where ignorance can be a scientist's greatest asset. <continue>I must learn as little about history as possible while I am in this era...
Text.zh: 我办不到。遗憾的是，时间法则不是这样运转的。<continue>一个人<i>{已知}的所有将发生之事，都是<i>{既定}事实。<continue>这是唯一一种无知反而能成为科学家最大优势的情况。<continue>身处这个时代，我必须尽可能少地了解历史进程……

Text.en: What might this Precursor forge look like?
Text.zh: 这个先驱熔炉可能是什么模样？

Text.en: This forge of the Precursors... what are we looking for?
Text.zh: 先驱熔炉……我们要找的是什么？

Text.en: What do we know of the forge?
Text.zh: 关于这个熔炉我们知道些什么？

Text.en: Do we know anything about the forge?
Text.zh: 我们对熔炉有什么了解吗？

Text.en: Tell me about the forge.
Text.zh: 告诉我熔炉的情况。

Text.en: What are we looking for? What does the forge look like?
Text.zh: 我们要追寻何物？这熔炉什么模样？

Text.en: We're lookin' for a forge? What kind?
Text.zh: 我们要找熔炉？什么类型的？

Text.en: This Precursor forge... what are we huntin' for, exactly?
Text.zh: 这个先驱熔炉……我们找的到底是什么东西？

Text.en: It is older than any other civilisation on Wraeclast. Only stone will remain. <continue>Anything else would have long since decayed.
Text.zh: 它比瓦尔克拉斯上任何文明都要久远。只有石制结构能保存至今。<continue>其他材质早已化为尘土。

Text.en: But how will we know when we see it? There's stone everywhere.
Text.zh: 可我们怎么认出来？到处都是石头建筑。

Text.en: Any other telltale signs of Precursor handiwork?
Text.zh: 先驱的手艺还有别的显著特征吗？

Text.en: I do not know how to tell what I am looking for.
Text.zh: 我不知道该如何识别目标。

Text.en: Anything else about their architecture I should keep in mind?
Text.zh: 关于他们的建筑风格，还有什么我需要注意的？

Text.en: I've seen plenty of stone in my time. How will I know I'm lookin' at their work?
Text.zh: 我见过不少石制建筑。怎么分辨哪些是他们的作品？

Text.en: That is not a lot to go by. How will I know when I see something Precursor designed?
Text.zh: 线索太少了。我如何知道眼前的是先驱设计的？

Text.en: Well, how do I tell something has been made by them Precursors?
Text.zh: 我怎么判断某样东西是先驱制造的？

Text.en: Stone's everywhere, lad. Might as well tell me to search the sea for water.
Text.zh: 小伙子，这儿到处都是石头。你这话就跟让我去海里找水差不多。

Text.en: They were masters of their world. They built structures whose mere remnants inspire awe... even in me. <continue>You will know it when you see it. <continue>The Vaal would have supplanted their legacy... had we but time.
Text.zh: 他们曾是那个世界的霸主。他们建造的结构，哪怕只剩残骸也足以震撼人心……连我也深受震撼。<continue>你见到时自然就明白了。<continue>假以时日，瓦尔本可以超越他们的传承……

Text.en: We face a dark enemy, below the dirt. <continue>They seem to be trying to use Vaal technology... to bring about darkness above ground.
Text.zh: 我们面对的是来自地底的黑暗势力。<continue>他们似乎想利用瓦尔技术……将黑暗蔓延到地面世界。

Text.en: The Abyssals are trying to use Vaal spires. I guess we shouldn't let that happen...
Text.zh: 深渊生物正试图利用瓦尔尖塔，我们总不能坐视不管吧。

Text.en: There were Vaal spires in the darkness. The ancient enemy is plotting something.
Text.zh: 黑暗中出现了瓦尔尖塔。远古的敌人一定别有所图。

Text.en: I saw Vaal structures, deep down. Used by the Abyssals.
Text.zh: 我在地底深处见到了瓦尔建筑。正被深渊生物所利用。

Text.en: Under the earth, there were... spires. Like the ones I saw in Utzaal.
Text.zh: 地下有……尖塔。和我在乌扎尔见过的一模一样。

Text.en: I believe I saw Vaal architecture in use by the Abyssals.
Text.zh: 我看到了深渊生物在使用瓦尔建筑。

Text.en: These creatures down there - they're toyin' with some of your Vaal technology. <continue>Seems they're trying to plunge the world into darkness or somethin'.
Text.zh: 下面那些生物在摆弄你们的瓦尔科技。<continue>看样子想把整个世界拖入永恒黑暗。

Text.en:
I encountered dark beings, way down below.  
<continue>They were using a spire... seekin' to plunge the world into Darkness. Looked like Vaal work.
Text.zh:
我在地底深处遭遇了一些黑暗生物。
<continue>它们正在利用一座尖塔……试图将世界拖入黑暗。看上去像是瓦尔造物。

Text.en: Hmm. It does sound like our technology. Architect Topotante was conducting experiments with the weather. <continue>I am uncertain how these devices may have been modified. <continue>You should destroy all traces of them. Otherwise, it could mean our doom.
Text.zh: 听起来确实像我们的技术。建筑师托钵塔特曾进行过气象实验。<continue>我无法确定这些装置是怎么被篡改的。<continue>你必须彻底摧毁它们的所有痕迹。否则后果不堪设想。

Text.en: Before us stands Mount Kriar, the most isolated peak in the Azmeri Ranges. At its summit, you will find the Cuachic Vault. <continue>There is no predicting the state of those within. If their discipline and loyalty endure after all these generations, they will know us by a certain phrase. <continue>Tell them, 'the time of the sparrow has come.'
Text.zh: 眼前矗立着克里亚山，阿兹莫里山脉最孤立的峰顶。山巅之上，你将找到库阿奇克宝库。<continue>无法预知其中住民的现状。若他们历经世代仍保持纪律与忠诚，便会认得特定的暗语。<continue>对他们说“雀鸟时代的钟声已经响起”。

Text.en: 'The time of the sparrow has come.' I will remember this.
Text.zh: “麻雀的时刻已至。”我记下了。

Text.en: They'll hear that and suddenly surrender? You best be right about that.
Text.zh: 他们听到这话就会乖乖投降？你可别搞错了。

Text.en: I will give them this message...
Text.zh: 我会将这个口信传达给他们……

Text.en: The time of the sparrow has come. Alright, got it.
Text.zh: 麻雀的时刻已至。明白了，记住了。

Text.en: Maybe you should come with me...
Text.zh: 或许你该跟我一块去……

Text.en: I shall seek out the Cuachic and deliver your message.
Text.zh: 我会找到这些宝库守护者并传达你的话。

Text.en: What happens if I forget?
Text.zh: 万一我忘了怎么办？

Text.en: The time o' the sparrow? Easy enough to remember.
Text.zh: 雀鸟时代？这很好记。

Text.en: <i>{Don't} forget... but...
Text.zh: <i>{千万}别忘……不过……

Text.en: If they will not listen, I will come to assist you. <continue>Remember, subdue them, but do not kill them. They will fight for us, one way or another.
Text.zh: 若他们拒绝配合，我会前来相助。<continue>记住，要制服他们，但不可取其性命。无论如何，他们都将为我们而战。

Text.en: In my youth, all I wanted was power. I was the smartest. The sharpest. I rose to prominence on sheer force of will. <continue>Once I became the right hand of Queen Atziri, I realised that the system of merit I believed in was an illusion. <continue>Knowledge and science were merely ruthless tools for the nobles in their mad quest for eternal dominion. I had no <i>{power.} I was a slave. <continue>From then on, I spent every spare moment secretly pursuing ways to save my people. <continue>I placated Atziri and the nobles by day, but fought a war against death itself by night. <continue>The flesh. The soul. Machinery. Time itself. No possibility was left unexplored. <continue>If all else failed... that is what the Cuachic Vault was for. <continue>Should the entire world be ravaged by the Cataclysm, some Vaal would still remain, deep in the most remote mountain. <continue>The final and most desperate contingency...
Text.zh: 年少时我只渴望权力。我是最聪慧的。最敏锐的。我凭借纯粹的意志力平步青云。<continue>当我成为阿兹里女王的心腹，才意识到我深信的精英体制不过是个幻象。<continue>知识和科学只是贵族在疯狂追求永恒统治时的冷血工具。我毫无<i>{实权}。我只是一个奴隶。<continue>从那时起，我将每一分空闲都秘密用于寻找拯救族人的道途。<continue>白天我迎合阿兹里和贵族，夜晚我则向死神本身宣战。<continue>血肉之躯。不朽灵魂。机械造物。时间本身。没有任何可能从我手中遗漏。<continue>若一切皆告失败……库阿奇克宝库就是最后的依靠。<continue>纵使整个世界遭灾变蹂躏，仍有瓦尔族人能在最偏远的山峰深处存活。<continue>这是最终也是最绝望的应急预案……

Text.en: I am wary, now, after hearing about the... <i>{people...} living in sunken Utzaal in your era. <continue>Isolation and containment drove them to horrific ends. <continue>There were systems put in place in Cuachic to avoid such problems, but the Vault was never intended to last this long. <continue>If the people in the vault are still healthy and sane after all this time, it will be proof that the Vaal are indeed worthy of my extreme efforts. <continue>If they are <i>{not...} I already suspect what might have gone wrong...
Text.zh: 听你说起那个时代沉没的乌扎尔城里的<i>{居民……}，我现在很不安。<continue>与世隔绝的生活将他们推向了恐怖的深渊。<continue>库阿奇克建立了防范机制来避免类似问题，但宝库从一开始就没打算运行这么久。<continue>如果宝库中的人们历经如此漫长岁月仍能保持身心健全，那就说明瓦尔部族确实配得上我的一切牺牲。<continue><i>{若非如此……}我已经猜到可能发生了什么……

Text.en: The Cataclysm came sooner than I'd hoped. The full complement of originally intended inhabitants was not achievable. <continue>I had to fill out the ranks with some candidates that I had previously ruled out due to their... religious proclivities. <continue>Blood rituals and sacrificial rites were not uncommon in the Empire, but I disdained such wastefulness. <continue>More than that, religion tends to spread in confined spaces. I can only hope sanity has prevailed within the Vault.
Text.zh: 灾变爆发得比预期要早。原定的居住者配置无法完成。<continue>我只好启用一些此前因为……宗教狂热而被排除的人选来填补空缺。<continue>血腥祭祀和献祭仪式在帝国司空见惯，但我厌恶这种无谓的消耗。<continue>更要命的是，宗教信仰容易在密闭环境中蔓延。我只能祈祷宝库内还有人保持清醒。

Text.en: Fools! Did you not hear the code phrase?
Text.zh: 蠢货！你们没听见暗语吗？

Text.en: ... is it really you?
Text.zh: ……你真的是……？

Text.en: By Atziri! Doryani himself!? I am not worthy!
Text.zh: 阿兹里在上！多里亚尼大人亲临？！小人何德何能！

Text.en: You seem sane enough. The spirit of the Vaal endures. <continue>Assemble yourselves. The time has come. You will march forth unto war.
Text.zh: 你们看上去还算清醒。瓦尔的血脉得以延续。<continue>整队集合。时机已到。你们要踏上战场了。

Text.en: As you wish, Master Thaumaturge.
Text.zh: 遵命，奇术师大人。

Text.en: Let us meet back in Kingsmarch. The Cuachic shall join our cause.
Text.zh: 君锋镇再会。库阿奇克将加入我们的大业。

Text.en: The Vaal shall stand with Wraeclast against the Beast. <continue>If we had this alliance in my era, things would have turned out differently. <continue>Let us make things right. This must be the last cycle of history.
Text.zh: 瓦尔将与瓦尔克拉斯联手对抗邪兽。<continue>若是我的时代有这样的同盟，结局定会截然不同。<continue>让我们拨乱反正。这必将是历史的终章。

Text.en: Welcome aboard. I'm Makoru, Captain of the Aurora May. <continue>Yes, we are indeed looking for work, but we only operate under the Meridian Free Traders of Kingsmarch. <continue>You can't just turn up with gold and demand passage.
Text.zh: 欢迎登船。我是马克鲁，极光五月号的船长。<continue>没错，我们确实在接活儿，但我们只听命于君锋镇的 子午线自由贸易公会。<continue>你不能随便带着金币过来，就要求搭船。

Text.en: My apologies. What do I need to do?
Text.zh: 抱歉，那我需要做什么？

Text.en: Are you sure? What if I had quite a lot of gold?
Text.zh: 你确定吗？如果我有很多很多金币呢？

Text.en: I meant no offence. What is the proper custom?
Text.zh: 我无意冒犯，那请问按规矩应该怎么来？

Text.en: Oh... I have no idea how this kind of thing works.
Text.zh: 哦……我完全不懂这方面的规矩。

Text.en: Ugh. What should I do then?
Text.zh: 呃，那我该怎么办？

Text.en: Of course. What must I do?
Text.zh: 当然，需要什么手续？

Text.en: I'm sure <i>{someone} wants this gold.
Text.zh: 总<i>{有人}会想得到这些金币吧。

Text.en: I dinnae know yer customs. What needs doin'?
Text.zh: 我不懂你们的规矩。需要我做什么？

Text.en: Not to worry...
Text.zh: 不用担心……

Text.en: I don't take bribes! We have a process you must follow.
Text.zh: 我不接受贿赂！你必须按规矩来。

Text.en: That's fine. Let me explain.
Text.zh: 没关系，我来告诉你。

Text.en: It's simple really.
Text.zh: 简单的很。

Text.en: Just a bit of paperwork.
Text.zh: 只需要一些手续。

Text.en: It won't take a moment...
Text.zh: 不会耽搁太久……

Text.en: Oh we want it alright... but first...
Text.zh: 我们当然想要了……不过首先……

Text.en: Oh, not to worry...
Text.zh: 噢，不用担心……

Text.en: You'll need to arrange an account and charter. Rog's your man for that. Once it's all sorted, we can talk.
Text.zh: 你需要开个账户，再办个许可证。去找罗格帮你办吧。等这些都办妥了，我们再谈。

Text.en: Welcome aboard. I'm Makoru. Captain of the Aurora May.
Text.zh: 欢迎登船。我是马克鲁，极光五月号的船长。

Text.en: Welcome back!
Text.zh: 欢迎回来！

Text.en: I've acquired a charter, and I'm told you're the best Captain for the job.
Text.zh: 我拿到了许可证。听说你是这一片最厉害的船长。

Text.en: So, this charter here means you have to do what I say, right?
Text.zh: 所以，有了这个许可证，你就得对我唯命是从，是这样吧？

Text.en: Good day. I have received a charter and am looking for a crew.
Text.zh: 你好，我拿到了许可证，正在招募船员。

Text.en: I've got a... paper thing... A charter.
Text.zh: 我拿到了……一张纸……租船，许可证。

Text.en: Got a charter here.
Text.zh: 我这儿有份租船许可证。

Text.en: I have a charter. And I seek a ship and a crew.
Text.zh: 我有租船许可证，我在寻找船只和船员。

Text.en: I've bought a charter for this ship so, guess you work for me now.
Text.zh: 我已拿到这艘船的租用许可证，所以你现在算是为我工作了吧。

Text.en: The sea's never agreed with me - but I've a charter, all the same.
Text.zh: 我天生不适应航海——但我还是拿到了许可证。

Text.en: Well... I think I might be the <i>{only} Captain at the moment. But that is nice to hear!
Text.zh: 嗯……我想我可能是目前<i>{唯一}的船长。但听你这么说真好！

Text.en: Not exactly... but we're happy to oblige what we can.
Text.zh: 也不尽然……但我们会尽力满足您的需求。

Text.en: Look no further. The Aurora May and her crew are at your service.
Text.zh: 不用招了，极光五月号和全体船员听候您的差遣。

Text.en: Then the hard work is done. We are at your service.
Text.zh: 那就好办了，我们随时为您效劳。

Text.en: Great! Then the Aurora May and her crew are at your service.
Text.zh: 太好了！极光五月号与全体船员为您效劳。

Text.en: Seek no further. We'll take you wherever you need to go.
Text.zh: 不用找了，无论您要去哪儿，我们都能带您顺利到达。

Text.en: It seems that way, doesn't it?
Text.zh: 确实，还真是！

Text.en: The sea can be a fickle beast. But lucky for you we know how to tame her.
Text.zh: 大海这畜生确实反复无常。不过算你走运，我们最懂怎么驯服她。

Text.en: We can take you wherever you need. Places other captains would never dream of going! <continue>Your business is your business. Now, we can set sail whenever you like.
Text.zh:
无论您要去哪儿，我们都能带您顺利到达，即便是别的船长想都不敢想的地方！
<continue>您的事是您的事，我们绝不插手。现在，只要您发话，我们随时可以启航。

Text.en: I'm a freshly-minted captain, but not new to these waters. <continue>I joined the Trade Guild as a deckhand after I ran away from home. I then worked my way up ever since. <continue>I finally took the Captain's Challenge not too long ago. and now, I'm the first Karui captain on a Kalguuran ship!
Text.zh: 我虽然是新任船长，但我很熟悉这片海域。<continue>当年离家出走后，我从水手做起，加入了贸易公会，一路摸爬滚打才有了今天。<continue>前不久，我终于通过了船长考核。如今，我可是卡古兰舰船上的第一位卡鲁族船长！

Text.en: What a story. Good for you.
Text.zh: 真是了不起的经历，真为你高兴。

Text.en: Well, well... What a go-getter.
Text.zh: 啧啧……真是个有志气的人。

Text.en: I admire you for your achievements, Makoru.
Text.zh: 马克鲁，我很钦佩你取得的成就。

Text.en: Well done. It can't have been easy.
Text.zh: 干得漂亮，这肯定不容易。

Text.en: Aye, you've done well. Good on ya.
Text.zh: 嗯，你做得很好，我为你高兴。

Text.en: So long as you are capable, it makes little difference to me.
Text.zh: 只要你能胜任，对我来说没什么区别。

Text.en: Bit of an entrepreneur, eh? Go get it, I say!
Text.zh: 挺有事业心的嘛！那就加油干吧！

Text.en: Not many who could make that climb. Ye've done well.
Text.zh: 没几个人能完成如此壮举。你可真厉害。

Text.en: Thank you, but my story has only just begun.
Text.zh: 谢谢，但我的故事才刚刚开始。

Text.en: Well! Aren't you a delight...
Text.zh: 好哇！您可真讨人喜欢……

Text.en: [DNT]
Text.zh: 您可真是位马屁大师！

Text.en: Now I've just got to get out there and make a name for myself. I've put a lot on the line to get to where I am. <continue>Who knows? Maybe we'll achieve great things together.
Text.zh: 现在，我只盼着快点扬帆起航，打响自己的名号。能走到今天，我可是赌上了一切。<continue>谁知道呢？或许我们能一起闯出些名堂。

Text.en: They don't talk very much about their homeland, and well... I'm definitely not allowed to sail there.
Text.zh: 他们对故乡之事避而不谈，而且……更是明令禁止我航向那片海域。

Text.en: Are only Kalguurans permitted there?
Text.zh: 那里只允许卡古兰人进入吗？

Text.en: Is it because you're not one of them?
Text.zh: 就因为你不是卡古兰人？

Text.en: You are not permitted because of your lineage?
Text.zh: 你是因为血统才被拒之门外吗？

Text.en: That seems oddly cruel. For what reason?
Text.zh: 这也太残忍了，为什么呢？

Text.en: Do you need to prove yourself first, perhaps?
Text.zh: 或许你需要先证明自己？

Text.en: Do they only allow Kalguurans?
Text.zh: 他们只允许卡古兰人进入？

Text.en: What? Why not? They scared of ya?
Text.zh: 什么？为什么不行？他们怕了你不成？

Text.en: Why not? The Kalguurans don't strike me as hostile folk.
Text.zh: 为什么不行？我看卡尔古人挺和善的啊。

Text.en: Nah, it's nothing like that. It's just part of their culture. <continue>First, someone has to immortalise my family's triumphs into an epic poem. <continue>Then, it must be told at their royal court, before their King. <continue>This sort of custom is not too different from Karui oral traditions, but it's a tricky situation for me. <continue>I am no longer part of my tribe, so I have no family to immortalise in song. <continue>Tujen suggested that <i>{we} could be family, but he was drunk at the time...
Text.zh: 不，完全不是那回事儿。这是他们文化的一部分。 <continue>首先，得有人将我家族的丰功功绩谱写成一部史诗。 <continue>然后，还要在他们的宫廷里，当着国王的面吟诵传唱。 <continue>这种习俗与卡鲁族的口头传统大差不差，但对我来说却很棘手。 <continue>我已经脱离了我的部族，没什么家族事迹可供传颂。 <continue>图金倒是说过<i>{我跟他}可以算一家人，不过他当时醉得厉害……

Text.en: I don't know what to make of the Kalguuran King. I've only heard whispers. Rumours.
Text.zh: 我也搞不懂这位卡古兰国王，只听过些风言风语，都是些传闻。

Text.en: Gossip usually isn't very reliable, but go on.
Text.zh: 流言通常不可尽信，但说来听听无妨。

Text.en: Oh I do love some juicy gossip now and then!
Text.zh: 哎呀，我可喜欢时不时听点劲爆八卦呢！

Text.en: I know very little of this King. Tell me what you've heard.
Text.zh: 我对这位国王知之甚少，说说你听说过什么。

Text.en: A monarch with secrets. Typical.
Text.zh: 藏着秘密的国王啊，太老套了。

Text.en: Not the good kind, I take it?
Text.zh: 怕不是什么好事吧？

Text.en: Don't believe everything you hear.
Text.zh: 别听风就是雨。

Text.en: Go on then, spill the beans.
Text.zh: 那就继续说吧，别卖关子了。

Text.en: Whispers spread like weeds. What've ye heard?
Text.zh: 流言传得比野火还快。外头都传什么了？

Text.en: Well, they say he's a bit obsessed with Vaal artefacts. <continue>But something tells me this is quite a concern for the Kalguurans. <continue>I've noticed certain types of Vaal devices seem to up and disappear before being loaded onto ships headed for Kalguur.
Text.zh: 据说这位国王有点痴迷于瓦尔遗物。<continue>但直觉告诉我，这让卡古兰人颇为不安。<continue>我发现某些类型的瓦尔装置，在装上前往卡古兰的船之前，总是会不翼而飞……

Text.en: Perhaps this King seeks something he ought not to have.
Text.zh: 或许，这位国王在追寻本不该染指之物。

Text.en: Hah! His own people are undermining his goals. Brilliant.
Text.zh: 哈！他自己的人民都在暗中阻挠他的计划，妙极了。

Text.en: Treasonous actions. You should be careful with this information, Makoru.
Text.zh: 这可是叛国之举啊。马克鲁，你掌握的消息很危险。

Text.en: Let the coward come and find what he wants himself then.
Text.zh: 那就让这懦夫亲自来找他想要的东西好了。

Text.en: The Kalguurans ought to be careful. A leaders wrath can be brutal.
Text.zh: 这些卡古兰人最好小心行事，君王一怒，伏尸百万。

Text.en: Perhaps they protect him. Or... themselves. Intriguing.
Text.zh: 或许他们意在保护他？亦或是……自保。有意思。

Text.en: Hah! Cheeky buggers. I'm startin' to really like these Kalguurans!
Text.zh: 哈！这群胆大包天的家伙。我倒开始欣赏这些卡古兰人了！

Text.en: They starve their King o' poison - and wisely so.
Text.zh: 他们截断了国王的毒害——此举实属明智。

Text.en: If you mention this, they will deny it, just like they did when I asked... <continue>And just like I will deny ever telling you this.
Text.zh: 若你提起这事，他们肯定会矢口否认，就像当初回应我那样……<continue>我也打死不会承认向你们透漏过风声。

Text.en: My people's traditions have remained the same for thousands of years! <continue>Until <i>{my} generation, that is... <continue>When I was just a young girl, Tukohama, the Karui god of war, was slain by a mortal. <continue>That turned my world upside down. Our own ancestor... killed by a mortal? <continue>I am looking for my own Way now. One that doesn't rely on gods or old traditions. <continue>I thought I might find my path in trade and commerce. So here I am, sailing for the Kalguurans. <continue>Other Karui like me have spread out across Wraeclast in search of meaning. The Elders call us <i>{kaivana.} <continue>They think we have rejected the Way, that we dishonour our families.
Text.zh: 我们民族的传统千百年来始终如一！<continue>直到<i>{我}这一代才……<continue>当我还是小女孩时，我们的战神图克哈玛被凡人所弑。<continue>这颠覆了我的世界。我们的先祖……竟死于凡人之手？<continue>如今我在追寻自己的道路，一条不依赖神明与古老传统的道路。<continue>我本以为或许能在商业中找到答案，所以我为卡古兰人扬帆航行。<continue>像我这样流落在瓦尔克拉斯的卡鲁人，都在寻找生命的意义。长者们称我们为<i>{凯瓦纳}。<continue>他们认为我们背弃了卡鲁之道，令家族蒙羞。

Text.en: Do you take that to heart?
Text.zh: 你很在意这些评价吗？

Text.en: One can guess what that means.
Text.zh: 其中的蔑视不言自明。

Text.en: I, too, was insulted for choosing my own destiny.
Text.zh: 我也曾因选择自己的命运而遭受辱骂。

Text.en: It's your life, not theirs. Live it how you choose.
Text.zh: 这是你的人生，不是他们的。按自己的意愿活着就好。

Text.en: Oh, Makoru... I know that pain well.
Text.zh: 唉，马克鲁……这种痛苦我再熟悉不过。

Text.en: Is there nothing you can do to heal this wound?
Text.zh: 难道这伤口无法愈合了吗？

Text.en: What's a <i>{kaivana?}
Text.zh: <i>{凯瓦纳}究竟是什么？

Text.en: Bah. What good are traditions if they do naught but bind us?
Text.zh: 呸！若传统只会束缚手脚——恪守它还有何用？

Text.en: Not really. At least, not anymore.
Text.zh: 其实没什么。至少现在我不在乎了。

Text.en: Yes... one can.
Text.zh: 是啊……明眼人都懂。

Text.en: Well, look at us now. We're doing okay.
Text.zh: 嗯，看看现在的我们，不也过得挺好么？

Text.en: Oh I do! Can't you tell? Heh.
Text.zh: 我当然是啦！难道你看不出来？

Text.en: It's not a pain I feel these days.
Text.zh: 我如今已经感觉不到痛了。

Text.en: Time heals all wounds. As does following your own heart!
Text.zh: 时间可以治愈所有伤口，追随自己的内心同样也可以！

Text.en: It means <i>{nothing} good.
Text.zh: 这称号可<i>{没}一点好意。

Text.en: You'll get no argument from me.
Text.zh: 我完全同意。

Text.en: Now, when I was a young girl, I visited the Halls of the Dead... <continue>... my ancestors told me that the Way was meant to empower us, not to control us! <continue>So who am I dishonouring by seeking <i>{my} truth? <continue>It's a debate that I am tired of having. But either way, it has separated me from my family, and I must live with that.
Text.zh: 当我还是小女孩时，我曾拜访过亡灵之境……<continue>……先祖们亲口告诉我：卡鲁之道的存在只为赋予我们力量，而非束缚我们！<continue>我追寻<i>{自己}的道路，到底丢了谁的脸？<continue>我已经厌倦了这种争辩。但无论如何，这终究让我和族人天各一方，而我必须承受这个结果。

Text.en: I became a captain against the wishes of the Trade Guild Council. <continue>To lead a Kalguuran ship, you must pass a test. <continue>I was given a makeshift crew and tasked with sailing a course in a certain amount of time. <continue>At the most dangerous point of the course, between two shallow reefs on a moonless night, our cargo of refined Verisium caught fire. <continue>It was set to explode at any moment.
Text.zh: 我成为船长其实违背了公会理事会的意愿。 <continue>想要指挥卡古兰舰船，必须通过测试。 <continue>他们给了我一支临时拼凑的船队，要求我们在规定时间内完成特定航线。 <continue>那是个无月之夜，在最危险的航段，两处浅礁之间，我们运输的精炼维金突然起火。 <continue>货物随时可能爆炸。

Text.en: What an ordeal. How did you handle it?
Text.zh: 真是惊心动魄，你当时是如何应对的？

Text.en: Gripping! Go on.
Text.zh: 真是扣人心弦！你继续说。

Text.en: What an adventure! Please, continue.
Text.zh: 真是场冒险！请接着说下去。

Text.en: Well clearly you didn't explode. What did you do?
Text.zh: 显然你没被炸飞，那后来你怎么应对的？

Text.en: Oh, my... This puts me off the water even more.
Text.zh: 天啊……这让我更不敢出海了。

Text.en: I suppose your leadership was tested in that moment.
Text.zh: 我想，你的领导能力在关键时刻经受了考验。

Text.en: Blimey. What a predicament.
Text.zh: 老天，这可真是九死一生。

Text.en: Tough spot for any captain. What did ye do?
Text.zh: 这对任何船长来说都很棘手。您当时如何应对的？

Text.en: I was faced with a choice: abandon ship, or order some of the crew into the lethal smoke to put out the fire... <continue>... At the cost of their own lives, no less. <continue>They say it's a rather horrible death, gasping for breath with burning lungs that no longer work. <continue>So I chose to put out the fire myself. <continue>That's just how I was raised.
Text.zh: 我面临选择：要么弃船，要么命令船员冲进致命浓烟之中灭火…… <continue>这无异于让他们去送死。 <continue>据说那是极为痛苦的死法，肺部灼烧衰竭，只能在剧痛中无助地喘息。 <continue>所以我选择自己去灭火。 <continue>我从小接受的教育让我必须这么做。

Text.en: Yet here you stand.
Text.zh: 而你竟然安然无恙。

Text.en: Hmm... are you dead?
Text.zh: 嗯……莫非您已经死了？

Text.en: But you lived?
Text.zh: 而你活了下来？

Text.en: You lived to tell the tale though, it seems.
Text.zh: 看来你终究是活着讲出了这个故事。

Text.en: Such bravery! How'd you survive?
Text.zh: 何等英勇！你是如何幸存下来的？

Text.en: And yet, you live.
Text.zh: 而你依然健在。

Text.en: Alright. I gotta know. How'd you make it out?
Text.zh: 好吧，你必须告诉我，你究竟是怎么脱险的？

Text.en: Faced the fire yerself... and lived to tell it.
Text.zh: 独自灭火……还能亲口讲述这场事故。

Text.en: Well luckily for me, it was all fake. <continue>It was the main part of the test. <continue>The truth of it is, the candidate passes or fails depending on the votes of the crew aboard. <continue>I earned their loyalty by my choice, and many of them still follow me to this day, here on this ship. <continue>The Council does not aid me in any way, but they also leave me to it. <continue>It's the best situation for now, until I show some true profit!
Text.zh: 算我走运，那全是假的。 <continue>那才是测试的主要部分。 <continue>真相是，候选人的成败完全取决于船员的投票。 <continue>我的选择赢得了他们的忠诚，他们中的不少人至今仍追随着我。 <continue>理事会虽然不再给我任何支持，但他们也从不干涉我。 <continue>在我做出成绩之前，眼下已经算是最好的局面了！

Text.en: Are you telling me there may be <i>{another} Cataclysm? <continue>You should have told me sooner! <continue>If this 'Weapon' can truly save us all, we'll do whatever it takes to aid you.
Text.zh: 你是说，可能会<i>{再}发生一场大灾变？<continue>你早该告诉我！<continue>如果这件"武器"真的能拯救所有人，我们会不惜一切代价帮助你。

Text.en: You're doing a fine job as it is.
Text.zh: 你已经做得够好了。

Text.en: As you should. But don't worry, I've got it handled.
Text.zh: 确实如此。不过别担心，我能搞定。

Text.en: My people, too, have sworn a pledge to fight Corruption. Welcome to the war.
Text.zh: 我的族人也立誓对抗腐化。欢迎加入这场战争。

Text.en: You may come to regret that promise, but let's hope not.
Text.zh: 你大概会后悔这么说的，但愿不会吧。

Text.en: You're doing just fine, Makoru. But I accept your vow.
Text.zh: 你做得很好，马克鲁。但我接受你的誓言。

Text.en: It may take everything we have... and more.
Text.zh: 这可能需要我们付出所有……甚至更多。

Text.en: It's a longshot, but it's all we've got really. Don't sweat it.
Text.zh: 虽然希望渺茫，但这确实是我们最后的希望，别太担心。

Text.en: Ye already are, lass.
Text.zh: 你已经帮了，姑娘。

Text.en: Not yet, I'm not.
Text.zh: 还差得远呢。

Text.en: I won't fail. Maybe this is my chance to prove myself.
Text.zh: 我绝不会失败。或许这正是我证明自己的机会。

Text.en: In a strange way... all of this may be what ends up healing my relationship with my father. <continue>Tavakai has seen what comes of his stubborn Way... <continue>And I've seen that there is some wisdom to be found in our tribe's strength and loyalty.
Text.zh: 说来也怪……或许这一切反而能让我与父亲重归于好。<continue>塔瓦凯已亲眼见识了固守卡鲁之道的后果……<continue>而我也逐渐领悟，部族的力量与忠诚蕴藏着智慧。

Text.en: You two finding common ground is at least one good thing to come of all this.
Text.zh: 你们俩能找到共同语言，确实是不幸中的万幸。

Text.en: Well that's a relief. I thought you two might be the death of each other.
Text.zh: 真是让人松了口气，我还以为你们俩会斗个你死我活呢。

Text.en: I am pleased. Let your differences bring you together, not drive you apart!
Text.zh: 令人欣慰。但愿你们之间的不同会成为纽带，而非隔阂！

Text.en: I hope for your sake, he will not let you down again.
Text.zh: 为了你着想，但愿你父亲不会再让你失望。

Text.en: Don't be a stranger to your tribe. That's all I'll say on it.
Text.zh: 别再疏远你的族人。这是我唯一的忠告。

Text.en: Good. You are best suited as allies.
Text.zh: 很好。联合才是你们俩最好的归宿。

Text.en: All's well that ends well, I guess.
Text.zh: 这也算是大团圆结局了吧。

Text.en: Blood's thicker than water, as they say.
Text.zh: 常言道：血浓于水。

Text.en: You're not wrong there.
Text.zh: 你说的没错。

Text.en: I guess blood truly is thicker than water.
Text.zh: 毕竟血浓于水嘛。

Text.en: There'll be a lot of conversations to come, but I am hopeful.
Text.zh: 我跟他还有很多话要说，但我很乐观。

Text.en: Well, if there is one thing I know about my father, he learns from his mistakes.
Text.zh: 我父亲唯一的优点就是他知错能改。

Text.en: I know... I won't be.
Text.zh: 我明白……我不会再那样了。

Text.en: We are more than that. We're family.
Text.zh: 不止如此。我们还是一家人。

Text.en: We'll just have to see where it goes from here.
Text.zh: 顺其自然吧。

Text.en: And I'd say I'm well-versed in both.
Text.zh: 那我只能说，我对“血”和“水”都很了解。

Text.en: So, it seems Tavakai has pledged to aid you and... well... <continue>The crew and I already discussed it. We also volunteer! <continue>I didn't think I'd be fighting side-by-side with my father any time soon... <continue>Truly, these are the end times! Ha!
Text.zh: 看来塔瓦凯已经立誓帮助你，而且……<continue>船员们都和我谈过了，他们也都愿意！<continue>真没想到我竟然会与父亲并肩作战……<continue>看来世界末日真的要来啦！哈哈！

Text.en: So, a united Wraeclast sails at our back. Who would've thought? <continue>We'll split off and deliver you to Oriath harbour, as planned. <continue>Dark clouds gather on the horizon... guess that's our cue.
Text.zh: 看来，整个瓦尔克拉斯都与我们同舟共济。谁能料到呢？<continue>我们会按计划分头行动，护送你抵达奥瑞亚港。<continue>天边已经乌云密布了……是时候行动了。

Text.en: That's less an island and more an active volcano. <continue>There's a swarm of deadly little things that make their home in the warrens further inland. <continue>We call them: the kinfolk.
Text.zh: 与其说是岛屿，不如说是活火山。<continue>还有致命的小家伙成群结队地栖息在内陆深处的洞穴网络里。<continue>我们称之为：亲眷族。

Text.en: They don't sound too bad.
Text.zh: 听起来似乎不算太糟糕。

Text.en: Oh, they sound cute!
Text.zh: 哦，听着还挺可爱的！

Text.en: Kinfolk? So they are some sort of tribe?
Text.zh: 亲眷族？所以是某种部落族群？

Text.en: People who live off the land? Maybe I'll fit in.
Text.zh: 在这片土地上生存的人？或许我能融入他们。

Text.en: Maybe I can make peace with them?
Text.zh: 也许我能与他们和平共处？

Text.en: Perhaps I can look around, unnoticed.
Text.zh: 我应该四处看看，别被发现。

Text.en: Nothin' a drink or two can't smooth over between us.
Text.zh: 只要酒过三巡，没有化解不了的隔阂。

Text.en: I know their kind. Damn fools tried to cage a bear - caught themselves an angry Druid instead. <continue>They didnae have long to regret it.
Text.zh: 我太懂这种人了。蠢货妄想关住巨熊——结果抓了个暴怒的德鲁伊。<continue>他们连后悔的时间都没捱过。

Text.en: They're feisty little buggers. They'll swarm you and eat the flesh off your bones while you're still screaming.
Text.zh: 这些暴躁的小家伙会一拥而上，一边欣赏你的惨叫，一边把你吃干抹净。

Text.en: I stand corrected.
Text.zh: 我收回刚才的话。

Text.en: Oh yes, I knew it! They <i>{are} cute!
Text.zh: 果然！他们<i>{确实}很可爱呢！

Text.en: Never mind then.
Text.zh: 当我没说。

Text.en: Guess not.
Text.zh: 看来不行。

Text.en: Ah. Right.
Text.zh: 呃，好吧。

Text.en: Hmm. Stealth may not work then.
Text.zh: 嗯，潜行恐怕行不通了。

Text.en: Okay. Might need more than a couple of drinks then.
Text.zh: 看来光靠几杯酒是搞不定了。

Text.en: Aye. Let them try.
Text.zh: 行啊。尽管试试。

Text.en: It's really their tyrant I'm worried about. <continue>Reports say he's getting bigger and bigger every time he's spotted. <continue>But sure... we can take you there I guess...
Text.zh: 其实我真正担心的是统领他们的暴君。<continue>听人说，每次有人发现他时，他都变得愈发巨大。<continue>不过……我们应该能把你送过去……

Text.en: You killed the kinfolk tyrant?! <continue>They won't soon forget that! <continue>Maybe we can start mining that sulphite ourselves at some point...
Text.zh: 你竟然杀了亲眷族的暴君？！ <continue>他们绝不会善罢甘休！ <continue>或许我们也该找机会开采那里的亚硫酸矿……

Text.en: That island has been shrouded in mist for generations... <continue>... even since before the Vaal empire! <continue>The tale told by our <i>{hatungo} goes: if the mists part and the island shows itself, you will be compelled to land on its shores. <continue>You'll claim to hear the voices or see the visions of loved ones, or of your gods, or whatever else is dear to you. <continue>If we go there, there's a real chance this ship will never return.
Text.zh: 数个世代以来，那座岛屿一直被迷雾笼罩……<continue>甚至可以追溯到瓦尔帝国之前！<continue>我们的<i>{哈图纳格斯}传颂着这样的传说：当迷雾散开，岛屿显现之时，航海者会不由自主地靠岸。<continue>你会坚称听见了挚爱之人的呼唤，或是目睹了神明的幻影，乃至任何内心珍视之物的感召。<continue>如果前往那里，这艘船很可能有去无回。

Text.en: You're the best Captain for the job. We'll make it back.
Text.zh: 你是这项任务的最佳人选，我们一定能平安归来。

Text.en: Don't be so silly. We'll be fine.
Text.zh: 别犯傻啦，我们会没事的。

Text.en: We are not just any crew. We will be alright.
Text.zh: 我们可不是普通船员，一定能化险为夷。

Text.en: Don't fear the myth you hear. Fear the reality you see.
Text.zh: 不必畏惧听来的传说，该畏惧的是亲眼所见的现实。

Text.en: I'm not afraid. We'll slay anything in our way.
Text.zh: 我无所畏惧，我们会斩杀一切挡路的人。

Text.en: This <i>{hatungo} may be mistaken. Let us see how we fare.
Text.zh: 这位<i>{哈图纳格斯}或许所言有误，不妨让我们亲自验证。

Text.en: Ah yeah but ya know... you're the best there is.
Text.zh: 啊，对，但你也知道……你是这一片最厉害的嘛。

Text.en: Visions of loved ones?  <continue>Ah, it's no matter. Whatever's waitin', we'll meet it head on.
Text.zh: 挚爱之人的幻影？<continue>罢了。无论前方有何在等待，我们都将迎难而上。

Text.en: Well... what could possibly go wrong?
Text.zh: 好吧……还能出什么岔子呢？

Text.en: Well... the fog is gone. But... you let out some fearsome god. <continue>Win some, lose some... I guess. <continue>I wonder what might be left over from the crashed ships there... hmm...
Text.zh: 好吧……迷雾是散了……可你们放出了个可怕的神明。<continue>有得必有失……倒也合理。<continue>不知道那堆沉船里能捞出什么好东西……嗯……

Text.en: Whakapanu is one of the most beautiful islands in Ngamakanui. A tropical paradise. <continue>Some time ago, I heard that a Karui tribe decided to land and settle there. <continue>Maybe it would be worth asking if they've seen any sign of what you're looking for.
Text.zh: 瓦卡帕努岛是纳玛鲁伊最美的岛屿之一，一座热带天堂。<continue>前些时候，我听说卡鲁部落决定登陆并在那里定居。<continue>不妨去问问他们是否见过你要找的东西。

Text.en: I look forward to meeting more Karui tribes.
Text.zh: 我期待结识更多卡鲁族人。

Text.en: Sure. Let's go say hello sometime.
Text.zh: 好啊，改天一起去打个招呼吧。

Text.en: I look forward to meeting them.
Text.zh: 我很期待与他们见面。

Text.en: This island is of particular interest to me.
Text.zh: 这座岛屿让我特别感兴趣。

Text.en: Hmm. Maybe I could have a hunt or two while we're there...
Text.zh: 嗯……或许我们还能顺道打打猎。

Text.en: I will seek out these settlers and see what they have to say.
Text.zh: 我会寻访这些定居者，聆听他们的智慧。

Text.en: Alright. Let's go make some mates.
Text.zh: 行，咱们去交个朋友吧。

Text.en: Paradise, ye say? Nice change from our usual destination.
Text.zh: 你说天堂？总算能换个新鲜去处了。

Text.en: I also look forward to meeting some Karui who <i>{don't} know me...
Text.zh: 我也盼着能遇见些<i>{未曾}谋面的卡鲁族同胞呢……

Text.en: Sad to hear about the Karui settlers... but at least you've dealt with the <i>{creature} in Whakapanu's caves. <continue>Now there's some hope for future settlers. Or maybe even we could set up something there!
Text.zh: 卡鲁定居者的噩耗令人悲痛……庆幸的是，你肃清了瓦卡帕努洞穴洞穴中的<i>{怪物}。<continue>这样一来，未来的拓荒者总算有了希望，或许我们也能在那里建立据点！

Text.en: I'm sure Ange would be interested!
Text.zh: 我敢说安吉肯定会感兴趣！

Text.en: Seems like you and Ange are already onto it!
Text.zh: 看来你和安吉早就行动起来了嘛！

Text.en: That prison island was built by the Eternal Empire. <continue>It's been three hundred years since the Cataclysm, but the inmates still wail from within the walls there. <continue>Can you believe it? Nobody ever let them out!
Text.zh: 那座监狱岛是永恒帝国建造的。<continue>大灾变已经过去了三百年，但囚犯的哀嚎仿佛还在石墙内内回荡。<continue>你敢信吗？根本没人把他们放出来！

Text.en: The Eternals <i>{are} long dead, I suppose...
Text.zh: 永恒之子<i>{想必}早就灭绝了吧。

Text.en: Forgotten souls, left to die. This one might be my favourite...
Text.zh: 被遗忘的灵魂，自生自灭。这地方很合我胃口……

Text.en: Perhaps they are the shame of their people.
Text.zh: 或许他们是各自族群的耻辱。

Text.en: Maybe that was the intention.
Text.zh: 也许这就是这座监狱的用途。

Text.en: Sounds bloody dreadful.
Text.zh: 听来真是太可怕了。

Text.en: The Eternal Empire... not so Eternal it seems.
Text.zh: 永恒帝国……并非永恒。

Text.en: Prisoners aren't usually the first to be saved... or even the last.
Text.zh: 囚犯总是被遗忘在角落……甚至最后也没人搭救。

Text.en: A punishment without end. Cruel work.
Text.zh: 永世折磨。真是残忍。

Text.en: It's a horrible place. You can still hear them pounding at the bars of their cells... <continue>Considering how awful it is, I guess you'll want us to sail straight there, right?
Text.zh: 那地方可怕至极，至今仍能听见囚犯锤打牢笼的声音……<continue>考虑到那儿这么恐怖，你肯定想要直接开船过去，对不？

Text.en: <i>{What} was locked up in there? Drowning... all those years? <continue>Hard to imagine that place was even spookier than I thought! <continue>The Eternal Empire really were a bunch of depraved monsters.
Text.zh: 他们究竟囚禁了<i>{什么}？经年累月……一直在溺水？<continue>难以置信那地方比我想象的还要阴森恐怖！<continue>永恒帝国真是一群堕落的恶魔。

Text.en: I am still astounded that Matiki told you about our Halls of the Dead. <continue>I was brought there only once, as a child... <continue>... and I spoke with my grandfather, and his grandfather before him. <continue>They gave me advice I still follow to this day - to stay true to myself.
Text.zh: 玛提吉竟然向你透露了死亡之境，我现在还觉得惊讶……<continue>我只在小时候去过那里一次……<continue>与我的祖父，与先祖之灵对话。<continue>他们给予了我铭记至今的箴言：忠于自我。

Text.en: Sound advice.
Text.zh: 金玉良言。

Text.en: How original.
Text.zh: 真有新意呢。

Text.en: A timeless sentiment.
Text.zh: 亘古不变的真理。

Text.en: Words I live by.
Text.zh: 这也是我的人生信条。

Text.en: There's more than just ourselves... but I do understand.
Text.zh: 世间并非只有自我……但我能理解。

Text.en: Being true to oneself, can also mean being true to one's calling.
Text.zh: 忠于自我，也意味着忠于使命。

Text.en: Strong independent lady.
Text.zh: 坚强而独立的女人。

Text.en: Wise words. Once ye lose yerself, there's no comin' back.
Text.zh: 很有道理。人若彻底迷失自我——便再无回头路可走。

Text.en: Unfortunately, staying true to myself means I can never return... <continue>But if you have an invitation, I can at least take <i>{you} there.
Text.zh: 只可惜，忠于自我意味着我再也回不去了……<continue>不过，如果你收到邀请，至少我能带<i>{你}去那儿。

Text.en: You spoke to the Mother of Death, herself? <continue>You're really getting a reputation around here! <continue>One day, I'd love to speak to Hinekora. I have questions of my own.
Text.zh: 你和死亡之母本尊说过话？<continue>你在这片海域要出大名了！<continue>但愿有一天，我也能面见辛格拉，我有不少问题想请教她。

Text.en: For generations, the Isle of Shrikes was used for a coming of age trial for several tribes. <continue>Young Karui try to sneak onto the rocks, climb the cliffs, and take the eggs of the shrikes that nest there. <continue>The eggs are a valuable delicacy that many Karui would pay through the nose for. <continue>In recent years, though, the trial became far more deadly. <continue>The things that nest there... they are vile things.
Text.zh: 数个世代以来，百舌岛一直是诸多部落举行成人礼的试炼之地。<continue>年轻的卡鲁人会尝试潜入礁石，攀越峭壁，窃取伯劳鸟巢穴中的鸟蛋。<continue>这种鸟蛋是珍贵佳肴，许多卡鲁人不惜为之付出高昂代价。<continue>然而近年来，这场试炼变得愈发致命。<continue>那些在岛上筑巢的生物……尽是些邪秽之物。

Text.en: I wish I'd had the chance to run the trial in my youth.
Text.zh: 真希望我年轻时也有机会参加这种试炼。

Text.en: Squawkers all around, I take it?
Text.zh: 想必到处都是叽叽喳喳的怪鸟吧？

Text.en: We have all manner of vultures through the Vastiri. I'm sure I can handle it.
Text.zh: 瓦斯提里什么品种的秃鹫都有，我能应付得来。

Text.en: I'm not scared. I've faced many a creature.
Text.zh: 我才不怕，我什么妖魔鬼怪没见识过。

Text.en: Sounds like a hunt is afoot.
Text.zh: 看来狩猎时刻到了。

Text.en: I am disciplined. I can handle the task.
Text.zh: 我训练有素，足以应付。

Text.en: Birds. Loud. Annoying. Ugh.
Text.zh: 鸟群。聒噪。烦人。呕。

Text.en: Of all nature's creations, birds might be the <i>{most} annoyin'. <continue>No wonder the Karui consider it a trial.
Text.zh: 在自然孕育的所有生灵中，鸟类怕是<i>{最}恼人的存在。<continue>难怪卡鲁人将驯鸟视为一种试炼。
