Text.en: You don't have to run some trial to be Karui. Blood is blood.
Text.zh: 你没必要参加什么试炼来证明自己是卡鲁人。血脉就是血脉。

Text.en: It's not really the type of island outsiders should be trespassing on... <continue>But... let's do it anyway!
Text.zh: 这真不是外来者应该擅闯的那种岛屿……<continue>但是……我们还是去吧！

Text.en: The first thing that grinning lot of fools did when they got here was build their enclave. <continue>The Twilight Order, they call themselves. <continue>Spend most of their time brain washing anyone who'll listen. <continue>And they've even got a decent chunk of Karui converted to their cause... whatever it may be. <continue>Bigger chunk of us can't stand them though, myself included.
Text.zh: 那帮咧着嘴笑的蠢货上岸第一件事就是筑起他们的聚居地。<continue>他们自称是什么暮光会。<continue>整天忙着给听众洗脑，<continue>甚至还忽悠了不少卡鲁人皈依他们那套……不管那套究竟是什么。<continue>不过我们大多数人，包括我在内，都看不惯那帮家伙。

Text.en: As long as they cause no harm, I wouldn't worry.
Text.zh: 只要他们不害人，那就无需担忧。

Text.en: Smiling evangelists? Oh, I'd rather die.
Text.zh: 满脸堆笑的传教士？让我死了算了。

Text.en: I will try my best not to eviscerate them.
Text.zh: 我会尽量克制不把他们撕成碎片。

Text.en: Genuinely sounds like an awful place.
Text.zh: 听起来确实是个鬼地方。

Text.en: Try not to judge a book by its cover. Maybe they mean well?
Text.zh: 别以貌取人，或许他们本意是好的？

Text.en: Any following is easily misunderstood, from the outside.
Text.zh: 任何教派都容易被外界误解。

Text.en: I'll figure 'em out. Been in a fight with many a zealot back in Trarthus.
Text.zh: 我会摸清他们的底细。我在特拉特斯可没少和狂信徒干架。

Text.en: Ach. Fools plantin' roots in rotten soil.
Text.zh: 呸。蠢货偏在烂泥地里扎根基。

Text.en: Too right.
Text.zh: 太对了。

Text.en: I just think it's a dangerous game they're playing. Religion always is. <continue>I suppose we can visit them if you like, but I doubt it'll be very helpful.
Text.zh: 我只是觉得他们玩的这场游戏很危险。宗教信仰向来如此。<continue>如果你坚持，我们当然可以前往，但恐怕未必能有所收获。

Text.en: I can't believe the Twilight Order are responsible for bringing the Beast back into the world! <continue>To be honest... I'd rather not go back there, if we don't have to.
Text.zh: 真不敢相信暮光会是把邪兽召回世间的元凶！<continue>老实说……如果不是非去不可，我实在不想再回那里了。

Text.en: I told you I would take you anywhere, but I'm hesitant about this one. <continue>Ngakanu might be the most dangerous island of them all, because... <continue>... I was born there. <continue>They won't outright kill me for showing up, at least not in these more... peaceful times. <continue>But if you insist, well, let me tell you, it is quite beautiful. <continue>I just won't be able to go ashore with you.
Text.zh: 虽然我答应过可以带你去任何地方，但这次我确实有所顾虑……<continue>纳玛鲁伊或许是所有岛屿中最危险的，因为……<continue>那里是我的故乡。<continue>当然，他们不至于看见我就痛下杀手，至少在这段相对……和平的时期不会。<continue>但如果你执意前往，我不得不说，那里的风景确实壮美。<continue>只是我不能和你一起上岸了。

Text.en: Will <i>{I} be welcome there?
Text.zh: 那里会欢迎<i>{我}吗？

Text.en: How about me?
Text.zh: 那我呢？

Text.en: Will they mind my trespass?
Text.zh: 他们会在意我的打扰吗？

Text.en: How will I get on there?
Text.zh: 他们会怎样对待我？

Text.en: Will they appreciate <i>{my} arrival?
Text.zh: 他们会欢迎<i>{我的}到来吗？

Text.en: How hostile will their response be to my arrival?
Text.zh: 他们对我的到来会有多大的敌意？

Text.en: Will they like seeing me, do you think?
Text.zh: 你觉得他们会乐意见到我吗？

Text.en: And what of me? Will they see friend, or foe?
Text.zh: 那我呢？在他们眼中，我究竟是友是敌？

Text.en: You won't be welcome inland, but you'll likely be allowed inside the front gate. <continue>Just be respectful!
Text.zh: 内陆地区虽然不欢迎你，但前门区域应该允许进入。<continue>千万要守规矩！

Text.en: So much has happened... more than a lifetime's worth. <continue>In a strange way, perhaps now we can heal and move forward... <continue>Now, we at least understand each other.
Text.zh: 发生了太多事……我这一生都很难承受。<continue>但说来也怪，或许此刻我们终能治愈伤痛，携手前行……<continue>至少如今，我们能相互理解了。

Text.en: I don't know what awaits us there, but that's where the map leads. <continue>We can check it out, if you think this supposed treasure could help us.
Text.zh: 虽然还不知道前方等待我们的是什么，但地图指向了这里。 <continue>如果你觉得这传闻中的宝藏能助我们一臂之力，那就去探个究竟吧。

Text.en: Who knows what we might find.
Text.zh: 说不定会有惊喜。

Text.en: Shouldn't you be... more excited about this?
Text.zh: 你难道不该……更兴奋点吗？

Text.en: It could be worth our time.
Text.zh: 希望能不虚此行。

Text.en: I'm not over the moon about more voyaging around. But... it could be worth it.
Text.zh: 我对继续在海上漂着实在提不起兴趣，但……也许会有收获。

Text.en: Adventure out on the open sea... My word. That treasure better be worth it.
Text.zh: 迎着怒涛扬帆冒险……天啊。那宝藏最好物有所值。

Text.en: Let us see where the wind takes us.
Text.zh: 乘风破浪，随遇而安。

Text.en: You had me... at treasure. Let's go already.
Text.zh: 听到宝藏我就……两眼放光。还等什么，出发吧。

Text.en: Could be riches, could be ruin. Only one way to find out.
Text.zh: 可能是富贵险中求，也可能是自寻死路——总得试过才知分晓。

Text.en: Spoken like a true explorer.
Text.zh: 不愧是天生的探险家。

Text.en: Just let me know and we can be on our way.
Text.zh: 只要你一声令下，我们马上出发。

Text.en: So, you found the treasure? <continue>I hope Gwennen turns up. Rog must be worried sick!
Text.zh: 这么说，你们找到宝藏了？ <continue>希望桂温妮安然无恙，罗格肯定担心死了！

Text.en: What's this? A part of a map? Huh... can't tell much from this alone... <continue>Let me know if you find more of it.
Text.zh: 这是什么？一块地图碎片？嗯……单凭这一个看不出什么名堂……<continue>如果找到更多碎片，记得告诉我。

Text.en: Now we're getting somewhere. We've got half a map! <continue>See if you can find more scraps.
Text.zh: 现在有点眉目了，拼出了半张地图！<continue>再去找找看有没有其他碎片吧。

Text.en: That's most of the map now, but I can't quite make out the destination... <continue>We need one more piece!
Text.zh: 现在地图基本完整了，但我还看不清目的地……<continue>就差最后一块碎片了！

Text.en: Look at that! We've got the whole map now. <continue>Shall we see where it leads?
Text.zh: 快看！现在地图完整了。<continue>要看看它指向何处吗？

Text.en: You are no Lich. You are no Lightless. What are you?
Text.zh: 你不是巫妖，也不是暗光者。你究竟是什么？

Text.en: So the ancient enemy has returned! <continue>Hmmm... but why is this one not attacking us?
Text.zh: 远古的敌人竟然归来了！<continue>嗯……可是这一个为什么不攻击我们？

Text.en: The Thief of Virtue! Stealer of Kulemak's spark!
Text.zh: 美德的窃取者！库勒马克之火的窃贼！

Text.en: What's it saying?
Text.zh: 它在说什么？

Text.en: This language... it was in some of the forbidden tomes.
Text.zh: 这种语言……似乎在某些禁忌典籍中出现过。

Text.en: Step back, vile wretch!
Text.zh: 退后，卑劣的怪物！

Text.en: Do you understand me, creature?
Text.zh: 你能听懂我的话吗，怪物？

Text.en: What's it saying? Evil dreams whisper like that...
Text.zh: 它在说什么？它的低语就像邪恶的梦呓……

Text.en: Greetings... friend. I don't understand you.
Text.zh: 幸会……朋友。可惜我听不懂你的语言。

Text.en: I can't understand a bloody word it says.
Text.zh: 它说的话我根本听不明白。

Text.en: A tongue twisted by darkness... I cannae follow it.
Text.zh: 被黑暗入侵的舌头……我听不懂它在说什么。

Text.en: In a time long past, I aided an alliance that fought these beings for centuries. <continue>They are the Lightless. The Abyssals. The spawn of the Well of Souls. <continue>I can teach you its ancient tongue, for knowledge was our greatest weapon. <continue>Hold still.
Text.zh: 远古时期，我曾帮助一个联盟与这些存在抗争数百年。<continue>它们是暗光者，深渊族，灵魂之井孽种。<continue>我可以传授你他们的古老语言，因知识曾我们最强大的武器。<continue>别动。

Text.en: My mind... I see... I hear...
Text.zh: 我的脑海……我看到了……也听到了……

Text.en: Ooh... that's a neat trick.
Text.zh: 哇……这手法真是厉害。

Text.en: It's like a tale being told in my mind...
Text.zh: 就像有故事直接在脑海中呈现……

Text.en: What did you just do?!
Text.zh: 你刚才做了什么？！

Text.en: So many voices...
Text.zh: 好多声音……

Text.en: I've felt this sensation before.
Text.zh: 我曾有过这种感受。

Text.en: That's a hookah hit and a half. Whew!
Text.zh: 这劲儿比水烟还猛，嚯！

Text.en: Like roots diggin' into my skull...
Text.zh: 像有树根在我头骨里钻……

Text.en: You now possess understanding of their language. <continue>Tread carefully. In ages past, these creatures served a dark god... <continue>Kulemak, the Lich Lord, master of Undeath. <continue>The alliance destroyed his body in battle countless times, but the Well of Souls remained beyond our reach.
Text.zh: 如今你已通晓它们的语言。<continue>务必谨慎，这些生物在远古时代曾侍奉一位黑暗神祇……<continue>库勒马克，巫妖之王，亡灵主宰。<continue>联盟曾在战斗中无数次摧毁其躯体，但灵魂之井始终超出我们的掌控范围。

Text.en: I must go. I cannot risk capture.
Text.zh: 我必须走了，我不能冒被俘的风险。

Text.en: I must go. I cannot risk capture.
Text.zh: 我必须走了，我不能冒被俘的风险。

Text.en: Kulemak was a dark god of these creatures... but no longer?
Text.zh: 库勒马克曾是这些生物的黑暗之神……现在不再是了？

Text.en: A divine spark, you say... so that's how a god can be defeated...
Text.zh: 你说……神性火花？原来这就是弑神之法……

Text.en: So, it's all true. The histories.
Text.zh: 看来史料记载的是真的。

Text.en: You... have it? His divine core?
Text.zh: 你……竟掌握了他的神性核心？

Text.en: Tell me you're not sayin' what I think you're sayin'.
Text.zh: 告诉我，你说的不是我想的那个意思。

Text.en: Then we are older allies than I realised.
Text.zh: 原来我们的联盟比想象中更为古老。

Text.en: Are you serious?
Text.zh: 你是认真的吗？

Text.en: So ye carry a god's ember... A heavy burden to bear.
Text.zh: 这么说，你承载着一缕神的余烬……这担子可不好挑啊。

Text.en: Yes. Slaying him proved fruitless. With much sacrifice, the alliance bound him, and I sapped his Divinity. <continue>His forces have hounded those old allies ever since, but the light is still their bane. <continue>Now, I take my leave.
Text.zh: 正是。杀死他毫无意义。付出巨大牺牲后，联盟困住他之后，我取走了他的神性。<continue>自那以后，他的势力一直在追猎昔日盟友，但光明仍是他们的克星。<continue>我该告辞了。

Text.en: Yes. Slaying him proved fruitless. The alliance bound him, and the Thief of Virtue sapped his Divinity.<continue>His forces have hounded those old allies ever since, but the light is still their bane. <continue>Now, I take my leave.
Text.zh: 正是。杀死他毫无意义。联盟困住他之后，美德的窃取者取走了他的神性。<continue>自那以后，他的势力一直在追杀昔日盟友，但光明仍是他们的克星。<continue>我该告辞了。

Text.en: No harm is meant. This one waits and watches. This one serves.
Text.zh: 我无意冒犯。我静候守望。我效忠于你。

Text.en: I shall stay my hand.
Text.zh: 我会暂且停手。

Text.en: I like you already, pet. You serve <i>{me} now.
Text.zh: 我挺喜欢你的，小可爱。你现在效忠<i>{我}了。

Text.en: I should destroy you where you stand.
Text.zh: 我该把你就地处死。

Text.en: Serves who?
Text.zh: 效命谁？

Text.en: Are you a servant of Kulemak then?
Text.zh: 难道你是库勒马克的仆从？

Text.en: <i>{Who} do you serve?
Text.zh: 你效忠于<i>{谁}？

Text.en: Your friends are not so pleasant...
Text.zh: 你的朋友们……可不怎么友好。

Text.en: Speak plain - who holds yer leash?
Text.zh: 直说吧——你是谁的走狗？

Text.en: This one serves. This one has always served. Unchanging. <continue>The master changes, in Kulemak's absence. Amanamu. Ulaman. Kurgal. Never Tecrod. <continue>This one does not change. <continue>You are here. You are the master, until you are not here.
Text.zh: 此身效忠。此身永远效忠，亘古未移。<continue>自库勒马克消陨后，我主更迭。阿曼娜姆、乌拉曼、古加尔皆曾执权。唯特克罗德从未称主。<continue>此法亘古不变。<continue>您既临此地，便为我主，直至您离去。

Text.en: Kulemak stirs below. Kulemak grows once more. <continue>Cast remains into the Well... his bones... his ancient flesh. <continue>He will reward you, as he rewards this one. <continue>The Source gives him power.
Text.zh: 库勒马克在深渊躁动。库勒马克正再度壮大。<continue>将残骸抛入深井……他的骸骨……他古老的血肉。<continue>他定当回报你，正如回报我一般。<continue>命源赐予他力量。

Text.en: This Well... can it be explored? What does it contain?
Text.zh: 这井……能进去探索吗？里面藏着什么？

Text.en: Tell me, pet, what this place is.
Text.zh: 告诉我，小可爱，这地方究竟是做什么的？

Text.en: Can this Well be destroyed?
Text.zh: 这口井能被摧毁吗？

Text.en: This Well is an abomination.
Text.zh: 这井根本是亵渎之物。

Text.en: What <i>{is} this place?
Text.zh: 这到底<i>{是}什么地方？

Text.en: What purpose does this vast Well serve?
Text.zh: 如此巨大的深井究竟有何用途？

Text.en: The hell is this place, anyway?
Text.zh: 这鬼地方到底有什么名堂？

Text.en: Ye call it a Well - but it reeks of a grave.
Text.zh: 你们叫它“井”——可它却散发着坟墓的恶臭。

Text.en: The Well is... <continue>... <i>{down.} <continue>From all places, all paths, it is still <i>{down.} In time, all things spiral <i>{down.} <continue>You, too, will be called. There is no obliviation. Only screaming. Forever.
Text.zh: 深井永在……<continue>……<i>{深渊}。<continue>无论始于何处，循何路径，终向<i>{深渊}沉沦。万物终将螺旋<i>{下坠}。<continue>您也将受其召唤。湮灭无存，唯有永恒之尖啸。

Text.en: Greatwolf guard me...
Text.zh: 巨狼护佑……

Text.en: That sounds deeply unpleasant.
Text.zh: 听着怪吓人的。

Text.en: Zarka's nightmares were true...
Text.zh: 扎卡的噩梦竟然是真的……

Text.en: I hope you're just being poetic...
Text.zh: 但愿你只是在吹牛……

Text.en: This is insane.
Text.zh: 简直荒谬。

Text.en: I sincerely hope that isn't true.
Text.zh: 我衷心希望这不是真相。

Text.en: Bloody what mate? Nah, I'm good.
Text.zh: 你在说什么鬼东西？算了，我可不掺和。

Text.en: If there's no rest in death... then she's still sufferin'. Because o' me.
Text.zh: 若死去也不得安息……那她至今仍在承受煎熬。皆因我而起。

Text.en: The edge beckons. The Source calls from below. Souls spiral, drawn by the call. <continue>All souls that return, all stilled flesh that yet moves. The Source gives motion, if not life.
Text.zh: 深渊在召唤。命源在深渊呼唤。灵魂盘旋而下，皆被这呼唤所吸引。<continue>所有归来之魂灵，所有静止却仍蠕动之血肉。命源赋予其力量，纵然并非生命。

Text.en: The undead... what are you saying?
Text.zh: 不死生物……你在说什么？

Text.en: That can't be true!
Text.zh: 这绝无可能！

Text.en: Do you speak of Necromancy?
Text.zh: 你指的是死灵法术？

Text.en: The heart of all that life abhors.
Text.zh: 一切生灵憎恶之物的核心。

Text.en: You mean the risen dead?
Text.zh: 你是说复生的亡者？

Text.en: You speak of undeath!
Text.zh: 你说的是不死之力！

Text.en: Ah shite, you mean the undead?
Text.zh: 啊，该死，你说的是不死生物？

Text.en: Not life, no. Only a mockery of it.
Text.zh: 不是生命，绝不是。只是提线木偶罢了。

Text.en: It is so. The Well of Souls is the Source of all Necromancy.
Text.zh: 诚然，灵魂之井正是死灵法术的命源。

Text.en: You know it is true, master. You draw upon the Source. You channel it through your soul.
Text.zh: 您明知真相，主人。您汲取命源之力，通过灵魂引导其流动。

Text.en: No. My power is my own!
Text.zh: 不！我的力量属于我自己！

Text.en: All Necromancy flows from the Well of Souls; from the Source below. <continue>Look at the light, master. Look at its weave... its... colour...
Text.zh: 所有死灵法术皆源自灵魂之井，源自深渊命源。<continue>注视那光芒吧，主人。观其脉络……观其……色彩……

Text.en: The light of the nether... it's the same...
Text.zh: 幽冥之光……简直一模一样……

Text.en: Every corpse you animate... every bone you summon... every soul you command...
Text.zh: 您驱使的每具尸体……召唤的每根骸骨……掌控的每个灵魂……

Text.en: It's haunting. I never knew... I was born feeling this... call... this connection...
Text.zh: 令人不安。我从来不知道……我生来便感受着这种召唤……这种联结……

Text.en: Yes, master. Embrace Death. It is a gift... from the Source.
Text.zh: 正是如此，主人。拥抱死亡吧。此乃命源……馈赠。

Text.en: You know it is true, master. The Lich Lords were once as you are. In time, you will rival them.
Text.zh: 您明知真相，主人。巫妖之王们曾和您一样。假以时日，您将与他们比肩。

Text.en: I'm finally beginning to understand... my whole life... I've felt this call...
Text.zh: 我终于明白了……我这一生……始终感受着这种召唤……

Text.en: Yes, master... become Death! Long have these ones waited for the coming of another.
Text.zh: 是的，主人……化为死亡吧！我等仆从已久候另一位主宰的降临。

Text.en: You've been waiting for me? Why?
Text.zh: 你们一直在等我？为什么？

Text.en: These ones suffer. Punishment. Torture. Unchanging. The masters know only hate and endless war.
Text.zh: 我等仆从永受煎熬、惩罚、折磨，亘古未变。历任主宰只知憎恨与无尽之战争。

Text.en: You think I will be a more gracious leader?
Text.zh: 你们觉得我会成为仁慈的领袖？

Text.en: You cannot be worse.
Text.zh: 您不可能比他们更糟。

Text.en: All necromancers draw their power from this place?
Text.zh: 所有召唤师的力量皆源于此地？

Text.en: How have I been channeling power from this place without even knowing it exists?
Text.zh: 我甚至不知道这地方的存在，又怎么能引导它的力量？

Text.en: You're telling me every Necromancer that ever lived... even Saresh... drew their power from here?
Text.zh: 你是说古往今来的召唤师……甚至沙利希……都从这里汲取力量？

Text.en: How did Necromancy come to be such a plague?
Text.zh: 死灵法术为什么会成为灾祸之源？

Text.en: Tell me of these Necromancers, creature.
Text.zh: 怪物，跟我讲讲这些召唤师。

Text.en: I must know more about Necromancy. Tell me.
Text.zh: 我必须深入了解死灵法术，请告诉我。

Text.en: So all the pithy little Necromancers I've killed over the years... they got their juice from here?
Text.zh: 所以这些年我猎杀的那些不入流的召唤师……他们的力量也来自这里？

Text.en: So the poison spills from here... and the Necromancers drink it deep.
Text.zh: 这么说，问题的根源就在这里……可召唤师却从中汲取力量。

Text.en: The Well was not. The Master Below All raged. Then, the Well was, and always had been. <continue>The more these ones fought and destroyed, the more souls spiraled into the Well. The nether exalted Kulemak. <continue>He rose, and walked the Boundless Cavern above, enduring the searing light and heat without pain. <continue>To wage the war, he gifted power to his servants, and they became the Lich Lords. <continue>The Lich Lords gifted power to their servants, and they became the Necromancers. <continue>These ones have been fighting so long, the Well spirals... the nether overflows... <continue>Countless screams blend into one endless exhale that can be heard even in the Boundless Cavern.
Text.zh: 灵魂之井本不存在。直至万物之下的主宰震怒时，灵魂之井方才显现，永恒存在。<continue>征战愈烈，毁灭愈盛，涌入深井的灵魂便愈多。幽冥之力使库勒马克升格。<continue>他自深渊崛起，行走于无垠洞窟之上，灼光与炽热皆不能伤其分毫。<continue>为掀起战争，他将力量赐予仆从，造就巫妖之王。<continue>巫妖之王又将力量赐予仆从，召唤师由此诞生。<continue>我等仆从征战已久，灵魂之井螺旋扭曲……幽冥辉光满溢横流……<continue>无数尖啸融为一声永无止境的叹息，纵在无垠洞窟也清晰可闻。

Text.en: You come from the Boundless Cavern. No stone in sight above, only fire and void. <continue>Hateful beings, spawned by flesh, skitter in the searing light. <continue>They seek to rend these ones. To destroy them. This one has never been. <continue>This one serves here. This one is frightened.
Text.zh: 你来自无垠洞窟。举目不见穹石，唯有烈焰与虚空。<continue>仇恨的造物自血肉孵化，在灼光中扭曲蠕动。<continue>它们渴望撕裂我等仆从，将其彻底毁灭。此身从未去过那里。<continue>此身唯能效命于此，此身深感畏惧。

Text.en: Kulemak serves The Master Below All. The Lich Lords serve Kulemak. This one serves whoever is here... this one does not know more than that.
Text.zh: 库勒马克效命万物之下的主宰。巫妖之王们效命库勒马克。此身只效命当下之主……此身唯知此法。

Text.en: You confuse this one, master. You destroy these ones, but not this one. Has this one served you well?
Text.zh: 此身困惑不解，主人。您毁灭其他仆从，却独留此身。莫非此身效命得当？

Text.en: It would not be honourable to slay you.
Text.zh: 如果现在杀你，实在有违荣誉之道。

Text.en: Of course, my pet. Don't you worry.
Text.zh: 当然啦，小可爱。不必忧心。

Text.en: You are not my enemy. Just keep it that way.
Text.zh: 你不是我的敌人。现在这样挺好。

Text.en: They attacked me first. That's all.
Text.zh: 是他们先动的手，仅此而已。

Text.en: Ah, I dunno. Just wouldn't feel right.
Text.zh: 呃，我说不清……就是觉得不太妥当。

Text.en: We each serve a master. It's not our fault.
Text.zh: 你我各为其主，非战之罪。

Text.en: You sure have. Don't think I'd want to be here alone...
Text.zh: 你确实帮了大忙。再说，换我一个人待在这儿……怪吓人的。

Text.en: Ye've done what's asked. That's service enough.
Text.zh: 你完成了我的要求。这就算是尽到本分了。

Text.en: You returned a Commander to The Well? <continue>Kulemak sees you, master. His call may soon echo forth...
Text.zh: 您将一位指挥官归还了灵魂之井？<continue>库勒马克注视着您，主人。他的召唤不久即将响起……

Text.en: Kulemak calls to you. Do you hear him? <continue>He waits below...
Text.zh: 库勒马克正召唤着您，您可听到？<continue>他在深渊等候……

Text.en: You... are remade. A new master. <continue>I bow to you.
Text.zh: 您……已获重塑。新任主宰。<continue>此身臣服于您。

Text.en: You faced his Vessel. It pleased him. <continue>You will face him again. Yes.
Text.zh: 您直面了他的容器，他很高兴。<continue>您必将与他再会。定然。

Text.en: That bloody scoundrel, Kanu! I can't believe it! <continue>Rhodri's my name. Ship's cook, on this vessel at least.
Text.zh: 卡努这天杀的混蛋！真叫人难以置信！<continue>我叫罗德里，是这艘船的厨子，至少眼下是的。

Text.en: Ah, so you make the food we've been... eating.
Text.zh: 啊，所以我们这些天吃的……都是你的手艺？

Text.en: I thought I was the one turning the crew green. Turns out, it was just your food.
Text.zh: 我还以为是我把船员们折腾得脸色发青呢，搞半天原来是你的功劳啊。

Text.en: Your food is much maligned by the crew.
Text.zh: 你的手艺可是把船员们搞得怨声载道。

Text.en: You make the food we've been... eating? Ah. Right.
Text.zh: 所以我们一直在吃的……东西，是你做的？

Text.en: Is the food supposed to be like that, then?
Text.zh: 所以你做的饭菜一直是那副德性？

Text.en: Your meals have put me through quite the spiritual journey.
Text.zh: 您烹制的菜肴真是让我的灵魂走了趟升华之旅。

Text.en: Your gruel is aptly named, mate. Grueling.
Text.zh: 你做的稀粥名副其实啊——折磨牌稀粥。

Text.en: So you're to blame for the grub? Had me thinkin' I'd eaten the wrong berries again.
Text.zh: 所以这些黑暗料理都是你做的？害我以为自己又误食了毒蘑菇。

Text.en: Don't hold it against me, I don't usually cook, okay? I just signed on to round out the crew. <continue>Technically, I'm the highest ranking Trade Guild member on this ship, so I guess... <continue>... I'm the Captain now! <continue>I'll get you where you need to go in Makoru's absence. <continue>Our first order of business ought to be getting her back! <continue>Set a course, and I'll make it happen.
Text.zh: 别见怪嘛，我平时不怎么掌勺！纯粹是给马克鲁凑数才上的船。<continue>按理说我现在是船上最高阶的公会成员，所以姑且算……<continue>……临时船长吧。<continue>马克鲁不在的时候，我会带你们前往目的地。<continue>但首要任务必须是先把她救回来！<continue>只管定好航向，我来操办。

Text.en: It'll haunt me forever. What was Kanu thinkin'? <continue>I knew he'd been out on Arastas a bunch... but I thought nothin' of it. <continue>Figured he was havin' a good time, the way a youngin should.
Text.zh: 这事儿肯定会阴魂不散地缠着我。卡努那小子到底咋想的？<continue>我早知道他在阿拉斯塔鬼混了好些回……但我压根没往心里去。<continue>还以为年轻人就该那样逍遥快活呢。

Text.en: They have a worthy message. It is the messengers that are flawed.
Text.zh: 他们宣扬的教义本来没错，错的是传道之人。

Text.en: I've seen it. Religion and fear drive people to cruelty and madness.
Text.zh: 我亲眼见过，宗教与恐惧如何将人逼向残忍与癫狂。

Text.en: He is a <i>{balbalakh}. His reasons are nothing but excuses.
Text.zh: 他就是个<i>{叛逃者}，他的理由无非是借口。

Text.en: He gave his independence to others. Foolish.
Text.zh: 他将自己的自主权拱手让人，愚不可及。

Text.en: He's done more damage than he knows.
Text.zh: 他造成的破坏远超自己的想象。

Text.en: Belief is a powerful motivator.
Text.zh: 信仰确实是强大的驱动力。

Text.en: It's a cult. Seen enough of 'em to respect their game.
Text.zh: 根本就是邪教。我见识得太多了，不得不佩服他们的伎俩。

Text.en: Lad was lookin' for some belongin'... and found it in the worst o' places.
Text.zh: 那小子本想寻个归宿……却在最邪门的地方找到了。

Text.en: Bloody bastards. They've always been a bit spooky, but I didn't know it went this far. <continue>And then... there's Makoru. What a day it's been.
Text.zh: 天杀的混蛋！那帮人一直邪门得很，可我没想到竟然到了这种地步……<continue>再加上马克鲁这档子事……今天真是太够受了。

Text.en: We've served on a few ships together now. I always tried to look out for her. Gave her a piece of advice now and then. <continue>We Kalguurans can be a bit hard-headed when it comes to foreigners. <continue>When she passed the Captain's Challenge with a unanimous vote, I was right proud. <continue>Her rescue should be our number one priority. We owe her that.
Text.zh: 我们搭档过几条船，我一直都很照顾她，时不时给她点建议。<continue>我们卡古兰人对外邦人是有点固执排外。<continue>当初她全票通过船长资格挑战时，我打心底里自豪。<continue>救她回来必须是头等大事。这份情谊得还。

Text.en: My best recipes? Hmmm. That's a good question. Generally speaking, I cook what I've got. <continue>I can't cook what I haven't got now, can I?
Text.zh: 你问我有啥拿手菜？嗯，这问题挺刁钻啊。原则上手头有啥就做啥，<continue>巧妇难为无米之炊不是么？

Text.en: Water and cabbage keeps a man alive. Mostly.
Text.zh: 光靠水和卷心菜也能活命，勉强活着而已。

Text.en: So that's why it's hot cabbage water every night.
Text.zh: 怪不得天天晚上都得喝滚烫的卷心菜汤。

Text.en: I suppose I prefer water and cabbage over fish. They're unnatural.
Text.zh: 比起吃鱼，我宁愿选水和卷心菜。那些鱼长得太诡异了。

Text.en: I've never eaten this poorly in my life, and I grew up in the woods... <continue>So, what <i>{do} you have?
Text.zh: 我这辈子都没吃过这么差劲的东西，要知道我可是在丛林里长大的……<continue>所以你们到底<i>{有}什么存货？

Text.en: It's water and leaves again tonight, is it?
Text.zh: 今晚又是水和菜叶子对吧？

Text.en: That is technically true. And what <i>{have} you to cook?
Text.zh: 严格来说没错。那你<i>{有}什么能煮的？

Text.en: What've you got, then? Don't say water and cabbage...
Text.zh: 所以到底有什么吃的？可别又说水和卷心菜啊……

Text.en: Oh so that's why it's naught but cabbage soup around here.
Text.zh: 敢情这就是为啥这儿顿顿只有破卷心菜汤。

Text.en: Trusty cabbage soup! A staple aboard the Aurora May. A tradition, really.
Text.zh: 靠谱的卷心菜汤！极光五月号的标配伙食，算是个传统吧。

Text.en: Ugh. We should pick up better supplies.
Text.zh: 呃……我们真该搞点像样的补给了。

Text.en: I've never been a fan of tradition. And now, even less.
Text.zh: 我本来就不怎么待见传统，现在更是如此。

Text.en: Some traditions are mistakes.
Text.zh: 有些传统本身就是错误。

Text.en: Yes... a tradition...
Text.zh: 是啊……传统……

Text.en: I have no choice but to respect your ways... I 'spose.
Text.zh: 看来我必须要尊重你们的规矩……

Text.en: Who am I to argue with tradition?
Text.zh: 我哪有资格质疑传统呢？

Text.en: He said it. Bugger me.
Text.zh: 他居然说出来了，真见鬼。

Text.en: Food's food, I suppose. But I've chewed bark with more flavour.
Text.zh: 能凑合填肚子就行。不过我啃过的树皮都比这玩意儿有滋味。

Text.en: Well, look...
Text.zh: 你看……

Text.en: What's that supposed to mean then?
Text.zh: 这话什么意思？

Text.en: Look, it ain't my fault!
Text.zh: 这可怨不得我。

Text.en: Well there's plenty of trees around if you're gonna be precious about it!
Text.zh: 这满地都是树，爱啃哪棵啃哪棵！

Text.en: If you want fancy, go to the Kingsmarch tavern. We're here to do a job.
Text.zh: 想吃上档次的，去君锋镇酒馆好了。咱们在这儿可是办正经事的，懂吗？

Text.en: You saved Makoru? Good on ya! She's the heart of this crew. <continue>I can go back to cooking your meals now. Guess you played yourself, eh? Haha!
Text.zh: 你们救了马克鲁？干得漂亮！她可是船队的灵魂。<continue>这下我又能回去给你们做饭啦。这可是你们自找的，哈哈！

Text.en: Oh... hello. Yes, yes, I'm Ange. A marketeer! <continue>Oh gosh, where are my notes? I'm terrible at introductions... Never mind!
Text.zh: 哦……你好。对对，我是安吉。一个商人！<continue>哦天哪，我的笔记在哪？我不太会自我介绍……算了！

Text.en: Don't worry. I don't do well at introductions either.
Text.zh: 别担心，我也不擅长开场白。

Text.en: Oh, thank goodness. A kindred spirit!
Text.zh: 啊，谢天谢地！总算遇上知音了！

Text.en: I've been in Kingsmarch for many years. Watching and waiting for my time to shine. <continue>Picked up a thing or two from Rog and Faustus, rest his soul... <continue>...and a little life advice from Tujen... I guess. <continue>If you need to get your hands on something... or get something... off your hands... just ask!
Text.zh: 我在君锋镇待了好些年，一直等待大展身手的时机。<continue>我从罗格和浮士德那儿学了点窍门，愿他安息…<continue>…还从图金那儿获得了些人生建议……大概吧。<continue>若您想入手些什么……或是想出手些什么……尽管开口！

Text.en: How do I reimburse you for your service?
Text.zh: 我该怎么回报你的服务呢？

Text.en: Surely there's more to it than that.
Text.zh: 肯定不止这么简单吧。

Text.en: Calm yourself, woman. I mean you no harm. How does this work?
Text.zh: 别紧张，女士。我没有恶意，具体怎么操作？

Text.en: Sounds good to me. What's the cost?
Text.zh: 听起来不错。费用怎么算？

Text.en: I'm interested. What do your services cost?
Text.zh: 我有兴趣。您的服务如何收费？

Text.en: I ain't no dummy. What's the catch?
Text.zh: 我又不傻。有什么条件？

Text.en: And ye'd do this out o' the kindness o' yer heart?
Text.zh: 您这么热心肠，难道纯粹是出于善心？

Text.en: I'll take a small fee, not much at all really... <continue>But first order of business... we need somewhere to set up a sort of base of operations. <continue>A hideout, you might say! To ship goods to and from. <continue>I'll keep it all spick and span, don't worry... just keep an eye out for a suitable location. <continue>Somewhere... out of sight. Off the beaten track! If you get my drift.
Text.zh: 我只收取些许佣金，真的不多……<continue>不过，当务之急……得先找个地方当行动基地。<continue>说白了就是秘密据点！方便货物进出。<continue>我保证打理得井井有条，您大可以放心……您只需留意合适的地点。<continue>要那种……不显眼的地方。人迹罕至的那种！您懂我的意思吧。

Text.en: You cleared out all the... <i>{problems}... on Whakapanu Island. Quite remarkable really! <continue>I've had a look around there, scoped out a good spot for us to base ourselves. <continue>Shall we go take a look?
Text.zh: 您清除了瓦卡帕努岛上所有的……<i>{麻烦}>。实在了不起！<continue>我勘察了全岛，已经物色到一个绝佳的据点选址。<continue>咱们一起去看看？

Text.en: Well done! <continue>So... what do you think? <continue>I love it! <continue>The pier could go there... store the supplies up the shore a bit... <continue>Our own little trade empire in the making! <continue>Well, what do you say? Shall we do it?
Text.zh: 干得漂亮！<continue>所以，您觉得怎么样？<continue>我简直太满意了！<continue>码头可以建在那儿……物资仓库往海岸高处布置……<continue>咱们的小型贸易帝国初具雏形啦！<continue>怎么样？要不要动手开干？

Text.en: A place of our own. Somewhere to make history from. <continue>If only my parents could see me now... <continue>Thanks for believing in me. I won't let you down!
Text.zh: 属于我们自己的天地，一个能开创历史的据点。<continue>真希望父母此刻能见证我的成长……<continue>感谢您的信任，我绝不会辜负期望！

Text.en: He's been a great teacher. No doubt about it. <continue>But gosh, has he gotten soft as he's aged.
Text.zh: 他确实是位了不起的导师，这点毋庸置疑。<continue>不过天啊，他真是越老越心软了。

Text.en: He seems like a fair man.
Text.zh: 他像是个正直的人。

Text.en: Do you mean... plump?
Text.zh: 你是说……胖乎乎的那种软？

Text.en: He seems like a gentleman to me.
Text.zh: 在我看来倒是位绅士。

Text.en: In what sense? He seemed perfectly normal to me.
Text.zh: 哪方面？我觉得他挺正常的。

Text.en: Got a big heart, that one. Nothing wrong with that.
Text.zh: 心胸宽广罢了，这没什么不好。

Text.en: I see. He does seem undisciplined.
Text.zh: 明白了，他确实显得有些散漫。

Text.en: Aw, come on. He's a nice fella.
Text.zh: 哎，别这么说，他可是个老好人。

Text.en: If he's softened, perhaps he's wiser for it.
Text.zh: 若他心肠变软，或许是因为智慧的沉淀。

Text.en: Too fair, you might say!
Text.zh: 应该说，他太正直了！

Text.en: What? No! Gosh.
Text.zh: 什么？才不是！天啊。

Text.en: Oh, he surely is! But you see...
Text.zh: 哦，那当然！但你要知道……

Text.en: Yes. Frightfully normal.
Text.zh: 是啊，正常得可怕。

Text.en: I agree. But... business is business.
Text.zh: 这我同意。但是……生意归生意嘛。

Text.en: I'm not sure you took my meaning...
Text.zh: 我觉得你没听懂我的话。

Text.en: Yes. Nice indeed. But making friends and making coin are different things.
Text.zh: 对，他确实是个好人。但交朋友和赚钱是两码事。

Text.en: Wiser, maybe. Certainly not richer!
Text.zh: 人是变得有智慧了，荷包可没见涨！

Text.en: Years ago, he'd drive a hard bargain. He was a great negotiator. <continue>Then he went and had children... ugh. Awful. <continue>Now he seemingly doesn't have the energy to haggle. <continue>Almost gives stuff away!
Text.zh: 想当年，他砍价毫不留情，堪称谈判高手。<continue>后来居然生儿育女去了……唉，真是糟透了。<continue>如今似乎连讨价还价的劲头都没了。<continue>几乎等于半卖半送！

Text.en: I'm sorry to hear that your mentor died.
Text.zh: 听说你的导师去世了，很遗憾。

Text.en: This Faustus. He's dead then?
Text.zh: 这位浮士德……已经过世了？

Text.en: You learned much from this Faustus fellow before he passed away?
Text.zh: 在那位浮士德去世前，你从他那儿学了不少本事吧？

Text.en: What became of this Faustus you mentioned?
Text.zh: 你刚才提到的浮士德后来怎么样了？

Text.en: I'm sorry to hear your teacher, Faustus, is no longer with us.
Text.zh: 听说你的导师浮士德离世了，我深表遗憾。

Text.en: Faustus, your mentor, has left this world, I take it?
Text.zh: 您的导师浮士德想必已驾鹤西去了？

Text.en: So this old fella, Faustus... he's dead then?
Text.zh: 所以这老家伙浮士德……已经过世了，是吧？

Text.en: This Faustus... I gather he's no longer with us.
Text.zh: 这位浮士德……看来已经不在此间了。

Text.en: A rather unwilling mentor, almost unaware he even was one actually... but thanks.
Text.zh: 一位不情不愿的导师，甚至可能根本没意识到自己在指导别人……但我还是要感谢他。

Text.en: Oh, yes. Been dead a few years now.
Text.zh: 是啊，去世好几年了。

Text.en: I did. He was a complicated man, to say the least.
Text.zh: 确实。至少可以说，他是个复杂的人。

Text.en: Well... he's dead. That's the short of it.
Text.zh: 这个嘛……长话短说，他已经不在了。

Text.en: Wish I could the say the same! <continue>Between you and me, he could be quite grumpy in his later years...
Text.zh: 真希望我也能这么说！<continue>悄悄告诉你，他晚年脾气可相当暴躁……

Text.en: You mean... he died? Yes. He's well and truly dead.
Text.zh: 你这话的意思……是说他去世了？没错，确实去世了。

Text.en: Very much so. Not a massive turnout at his funeral, I must say.
Text.zh: 确实如此。坦白说，他的葬礼到场人数并不多。

Text.en: Afraid not. Not for a few years now.
Text.zh: 恐怕不在了。已经走了好几年了。

Text.en: Died of natural causes they say, but I'm not so sure... <continue>He left Kingsmarch around a decade ago. No one was entirely sure where he went. <continue>But when he got back... gosh. He really had changed. <continue>He'd both softened <i>{and} hardened. <continue>You never quite knew which you'd get. <continue>But I could tell... it was caused by fear.
Text.zh: 都说他是自然死亡，但我总觉得没那么简单……<continue>大约十年前他离开了君锋镇，没人完全清楚他的去向。<continue>当他回来时……天啊，简直判若两人。<continue>他变得既心软<i>{又}心狠。<continue>你永远猜不透会见到他的哪一面。<continue>但我看得出来……这种变化源于恐惧。

Text.en: And you didn't ever ask him about it?
Text.zh: 你从没问过他原因？

Text.en: You didn't think to enquire about it?
Text.zh: 你就没想过打听一下？

Text.en: Age can change us all. Why didn't you ask him about this?
Text.zh: 岁月会改变所有人。你为何不直接问他呢？

Text.en: Just sounds like another grumpy old man.
Text.zh: 听着就是个脾气暴躁的老头罢了。

Text.en: I've met an Elder or two like that. Not so uncommon in their older years.
Text.zh: 我也遇见过一两个这样的长者。晚年这样并不罕见。

Text.en: Perhaps he saw something in the world he wished he hadn't.
Text.zh: 或许他目睹了难以释怀的世间真相。

Text.en: It's always like that when you come back from holiday. It's hard to get back into the routine.
Text.zh: 度假回来总是这样的，很难再回到以前的日常生活。

Text.en: Fear shapes a soul like water shapes stone.
Text.zh: 恐惧塑造灵魂，犹如滴水穿石。

Text.en: Oh yes. Very grumpy!
Text.zh: 没错，特别暴躁！

Text.en: Certainly, but he still had his mind. It was something else...
Text.zh: 话是没错，但他清醒得很。是别的缘故……

Text.en: I believe you are right.
Text.zh: 我想你说得对。

Text.en: That's... not exactly what I mean.
Text.zh: 我可没说他是去度假的。

Text.en: Yes... that's exactly it. Wore him down, but left sharp edges too.
Text.zh: 正是如此。虽会磨去棱角，却也留下锋利的边缘。

Text.en: I asked him once. It took all my courage to do so. <continue>'What happened to you, Faustus? Where did you go?' I asked. <continue>He looked me dead in the eye. I still remember the look he had on his face. <continue>He was looking at me, but also through me. Like he was somewhere else. Reliving something. <continue>He said nothing. Got up and left. They found his body later that night on the dock. <continue>Under his coat, he was clutching a small Vaal artefact. <continue>I heard he held it so tightly, his hands were bloody... <continue>All very strange.
Text.zh: 我曾鼓起全部勇气问过他一次。<continue>我问他："浮士德，你究竟遭遇了什么？去了哪里？"。<continue>他直勾勾盯着我的眼睛。那神情我至今记忆犹新。<continue>他的目光既落在我身上，又仿佛穿透了我。像是魂归别处，重历某些往事。<continue>他一言不发，起身离去。那晚，人们就在码头发现了他的遗体。<continue>大衣之下，他紧紧攥着一件瓦尔遗物。<continue>听说他握得那么紧，双手都沾满了鲜血……<continue>着实诡异。

Text.en: He helped me with my self-confidence... Well, at least he tried to. <continue>He got me blind drunk, singing songs with the local crews here in the tavern.
Text.zh: 他曾帮我建立信心……嗯，至少尝试过。<continue>有一回，他把我灌得烂醉，拉着我跟酒馆里的本地水手们扯着嗓子唱歌。

Text.en: I'm getting the impression... he has a problem.
Text.zh: 我算是确定了……他是个酒腻子。

Text.en: The child with a beard should stop drinking.
Text.zh: 那个长胡子老小子真该戒酒了。

Text.en: Master Tujen must pace himself with his drinking...
Text.zh: 图金大人饮酒应该有节制才是……

Text.en: Getting drunk is no solution to a problem.
Text.zh: 买醉解决不了任何问题。

Text.en: Ha! Sounds like a good night out.
Text.zh: 哈！听起来像是个畅快的夜晚。

Text.en: How childish.
Text.zh: 幼稚。

Text.en: Haha! What a bloody legend.
Text.zh: 哈哈！真是个传奇人物。

Text.en: Ale's no cure, but it loosens the tongue.
Text.zh: 麦酒虽不解愁，但能撬开话匣子。

Text.en: Yes... it is a bit of a worry.
Text.zh: 确实有点让人担心。

Text.en: Yes... I suppose.
Text.zh: 大概是吧。

Text.en: And loosened mine far too much that night!
Text.zh: 那晚我可真是管不住嘴，啥都往外倒！

Text.en: Since then, for some reason, people have been more friendly towards me. <continue>Aren't people awfully strange?
Text.zh: 自那以后，不知怎的，人们对我友善多了。<continue>你说人是不是都特别莫名其妙？

Text.en: They certainly are.
Text.zh: 确实如此。

Text.en: Yes, yes. I've picked up that you're on some grand quest to save us all. <continue>Oblivion doesn't bother me, really... <continue>As long as I go out on a pile of gold and success!
Text.zh: 是啦，是啦，我早看出来您正忙着拯救苍生的伟业。<continue>其实湮灭什么的我倒不在乎……<continue>只要让我最终躺在金山银山上功成名就，我死也甘心！

Text.en: You better make your gold quick then...
Text.zh: 那你就抓紧时间赚金币吧……

Text.en: That's the spirit! There really is nothing to fear in death.
Text.zh: 这就对了！死亡确实没什么可怕的。

Text.en: Gold cannot follow you in death. It is meaningless.
Text.zh: 金币又带不进坟墓，都是虚的。

Text.en: Whatever makes you happy, I 'spose.
Text.zh: 你开心就好，大概吧。

Text.en: Oh, come now, there's more to life than that, Ange.
Text.zh: 得了吧，安吉，人生还有比这更重要的事呢。

Text.en: Gold and fame are fools endeavours.
Text.zh: 追名逐利是最愚蠢的事。

Text.en: You know what, that's exactly how I feel.
Text.zh: 知道吗，我深有同感。

Text.en: Coin fills a purse, but not the hole in yer heart.
Text.zh: 金钱能填满钱袋，却填不上心里的窟窿。

Text.en: Speaking from experience?
Text.zh: 经验之谈？

Text.en: I... of a sort.
Text.zh: 我……算是吧。

Text.en: Well, I've run out of hope in finding a life companion... so gold will have to do.
Text.zh: 唉，反正我早就不指望能找到人生伴侣了……所以只能指望金子啦。

Text.en: Well... we didn't die. Not yet at least.
Text.zh: 好吧，我们还没死，至少现在还没。

Text.en: Not yet, indeed.
Text.zh: 确实，来日方长。

Text.en: Gosh. Cheer up. You're welcome.
Text.zh: 行了，振作点吧。别跟我客气。

Text.en: We continue to live. We are fortunate.
Text.zh: 能活下来就算走运。

Text.en: Anything could happen, at any time.
Text.zh: 世事难料，一切皆有可能。

Text.en: Live to hunt another day.
Text.zh: 活下去，改日继续狩猎之旅。

Text.en: We move forwards. It is all that matters.
Text.zh: 唯有向前，此乃唯一要事。

Text.en: It was a close one. To be sure.
Text.zh: 这次确实是九死一生。

Text.en: The end still comes... but not today.
Text.zh: 尽头终会到来……但绝非今日。

Text.en: Trade might be challenging now, given the circumstances... <continue>But don't worry! I'll make it happen!
Text.zh: 眼下这情形，做买卖确实很棘手……<continue>不过别担心！我自有办法闯出名堂！

Text.en: Hmm... it seems there's some rather intrusive wildlife around. <continue>Well, we can't have that can we? <continue>Maybe you could... move them on?
Text.zh: 嗯…这附近似乎有些相当碍眼的野生生物呢。<continue>我们不能坐视不管，对吧？<continue>或许您可以…请它们离开？

Text.en: There is something very wrong in these lands. <continue>It is not Corruption... it is something far older, far darker... <continue>I must search my memories... give me time...
Text.zh: 这片土地正滋生着极不寻常的邪祟……<continue>并非腐化……而是某种更古老、更黑暗的存在……<continue>容我追溯记忆……我需要时间探究……

Text.en: The longer one lives, the heavier the weight of time. <continue>I must cast myself into the realm within, seeking states of mind, seeking the man I was in a given era. <continue>Despair has taken my poetry from me. In my divine youth, I was quite the passionate soul. <continue>And in that era, we fought for the light...
Text.zh: 生命愈长，时光之重愈沉。<continue>我须投身内在之境，寻觅心境流转，追寻往昔某个时代的自我。<continue>绝望已夺走我的诗意。在神圣的青年时代，我曾是满腔热忱的灵魂。<continue>而在那个年代……我们曾为光明而战。

Text.en: We must leave this place. <continue>It is best the Ezomytes come with us. <continue>If we survive what is coming, we will return here with force, and face the origin of this Nameless threat... <continue>But for now, we must away.
Text.zh: 我们必须即刻撤离此地。<continue>最好让艾兹麦人与我们同行。<continue>若能渡过这场劫难，我们必将率众重返，直面这无名威胁的源头……<continue>但此刻，我们必须撤离。

Text.en: The Ardura have vowed to aid us, and aid us they shall. That is not in question. <continue>They alone are not enough. We need to assist Asala in her bid at unification. <continue>As a people, when roused to war, the Maraketh are formidable indeed.
Text.zh: 阿杜拉人已立誓援助，他们必将履行承诺。这点毋庸置疑。<continue>但仅靠他们还远远不够。我们需助艾萨拉一臂之力，实现统一大业。<continue>马拉克斯民族一旦被战争唤醒，必将展现万众一心的磅礴力量。

Text.en: I can still see this place, as it was.<continue>There was so much life. Colour. Noise. Children playing in that fountain. Merchants selling wares.<continue>Nothing but dust and wind, now...
Text.zh: 我依稀能看见这里往日的模样。<continue>这里曾经生机盎然，色彩斑斓，人声鼎沸。孩童在喷泉中嬉戏，商贩叫卖着货物。<continue>如今……只剩下尘沙与风声。

Text.en: I have long considered it a mistake to bind souls to <i>{baryas}. <continue>Not only is the punishment egregious, but it allows them to persist long past their time. <continue>Zarokh achieved many great things during the war against the Abyssals, but his ambition outgrew his sense. <continue>Now, he remains to torment us, when he should have passed from this world long ago. <continue>He worked closely with the tale-women of the era, and stole their wisdom, becoming the first Chronomancer. <continue>If he has lent his powers to Azmadi, this will not be a simple contest of strength. It will be far more dangerous than that.
Text.zh: 长久以来，我始终认为将灵魂束缚于<i>{巨灵币}>中乃是谬举。<continue>此举不仅刑罚严酷，更令他们得以超脱时间而长久存续。<continue>扎洛卡在对抗深渊族的战争中立下惊世功绩，但他的野心终究吞噬了理智。<continue>本该早已离世的他，如今仍滞留世间荼毒世人。<continue>他曾与那个时代的说书人密切合作，窃取她们的智慧成为首位塑时术师。<continue>若他将力量借予阿兹玛迪，这将绝非单纯的武力较量，而是远比那凶险的博弈。

Text.en: We have set the proper events in motion. The Maraketh - all the Maraketh - will come to our aid. <continue>I have seen their power long ago, as part of a great alliance to defend Wraeclast. <continue>Let us hope that ancient courage still holds true.
Text.zh: 我们已启动关键进程。马拉克斯——全体马拉克斯人——终将驰援我方。<continue>早在昔日守护瓦尔克拉斯特的伟大联军中，我便见证过他们的力量。<continue>惟愿那份远古勇气至今仍历久弥坚。

Text.en: Just between us, the existence of this 'Cuachic Vault' gives me pause. <continue>How many other plans does Doryani have in motion? <continue>Keep your eyes open.
Text.zh: 这话别告诉别人，这个“库阿奇克宝库”的存在真让人心惊。<continue>多里亚尼究竟还有多少暗中推行的计划？<continue>务必多加提防。

Text.en: I am struggling to reconcile two men. One is history's greatest monster, maligned across Wraeclast's written and oral traditions. <continue>The other is the man we brought back from the past, one who portrays himself as desperately trying to save his people. <continue>Are they one and the same? He accepts responsibility for the things he has done, claiming they were necessary. <continue>I suppose I have no moral high ground to stand on... Doryani could not have done any of what he did if I had not grown the Beast...
Text.zh: 我实在难以将这两个形象联系在一起：一个是史书记载的千古罪人，遭瓦尔克拉斯所有典籍与传说口诛笔伐；<continue>另一个却是我们从过去召唤过来的男人，自称不惜一切拯救族群的悲情英雄。<continue>他们真是同一人吗？他坦然承认所有行径，却坚称那是必要之举。<continue>想来我也无资格站在道德高地上批判……若非我培育了那头邪兽，多里亚尼根本不可能造成那些灾祸。

Text.en: The Cuachic may have joined our cause, but that will last only as long as we share a common enemy. <continue>Be wary. If we do manage to stop the Twilight Order and slay the Beast... we may suddenly have more to worry about.
Text.zh: 库阿奇克部族虽已与我们结盟，但这盟约仅能维持到共同敌人消失之前。<continue>务必保持警惕。若我们真能阻止暮光会并铲除邪兽……届时恐有更多燃眉之祸骤然显现。

Text.en: Did our scouts find you?
Text.zh: 是我们的侦察兵找到你的吗？

Text.en: It is good to see you.
Text.zh: 很高兴见到你。

Text.en: No, I returned to seek your aid. We must assault Oriath, to slay the Beast.
Text.zh: 没有。我回来寻求你的援助。我们必须进攻奥瑞亚，消灭那头邪兽。

Text.en: Scouts? No. The Beast is in Oriath, and it's time we did something about it.
Text.zh: 侦察兵？没见到。邪兽就在奥瑞亚。我们是时候采取行动了。

Text.en: Likewise. I have discovered that the Beast is in Oriath, and we must wage war.
Text.zh: 彼此彼此。我已经查明邪兽就在奥瑞亚，我们必须开战。

Text.en: You had scouts looking for me? The Beast is in Oriath. We must go.
Text.zh: 你派了侦察兵找我？邪兽在奥瑞亚，我们快出发吧。

Text.en: I am not so easily found. We must get to Oriath. The Beast is there.
Text.zh: 想找我可没那么容易。我们必须去奥瑞亚，邪兽就在那里。

Text.en: Not possible. I've been on the sea. The Beast has been taken to Oriath.
Text.zh: 不可能碰上。我一直漂泊在海上。邪兽已被带往奥瑞亚。

Text.en: Might have. Not sure. I've come to ask your aid once again, boss. The Beast... it's in Oriath.
Text.zh: 也许吧，我也不清楚。我又回来求你帮忙了，老大。那头邪兽……就在奥瑞亚。

Text.en: Aye, I've returned. But not for pleasantries. The Beast's in Oriath, and we need yer help to take it down.
Text.zh: 是，我回来了。但并非为了叙旧。邪兽正在奥瑞亚肆虐，我们需要你帮我们一起解决它。

Text.en: Yes. We have learned this as well, and have been looking for you. Your return is timely. <continue>I am making moves to gather the <i>{akharas} for war, but they will not follow my leadership unless this goes well. <continue>This year, because of what I am attempting to do, it has become my duty to oversee the Gifting of Water. <continue>In the city of Qimah, the <i>{akharas} gather at the appointed time, as marked by the skies. <continue>There, we divide sacred water. We 'keep the river flowing,' as it is said. <continue>The same waters that once flowed through Keth.
Text.zh: 是的，我们也得知了这个消息，所以一直在找你。你回来得正是时候。<continue>我正在召集各地的<i>{阿卡拉}备战，但除非仪式能顺利进行，否则他们绝不会服从我的领导。<continue>今年，由于我正在尝试做的事，主持圣水赐福仪式成了我的职责。<continue>在奇玛尔城，各地的<i>{阿卡拉}会依据天象所示的时间集结。<continue>我们会在那里分配圣水。正如古语所说：我们“维系长河奔流不息”。<continue>这些圣水来自曾经流经克斯地区的河流。

Text.en: A powerful tradition. How can I help?
Text.zh: 庄严的传统，我该如何相助？

Text.en: Hmm. And something's not going according to plan, I take it?
Text.zh: 嗯。我猜是出了什么岔子吧？

Text.en: I am familiar. Is there a problem?
Text.zh: 我了解这种传统，是出什么问题了吗？

Text.en: Am I to play some role in this?
Text.zh: 需要我帮什么忙吗？

Text.en: I can respect that. You sound concerned, though.
Text.zh: 我尊重你们的传统。不过，你听起来有些担忧。

Text.en: A symbol of unity. What can I do?
Text.zh: 团结的象征。我能帮上什么忙？

Text.en: That's all above my head. What's my job?
Text.zh: 这些东西太复杂了，我搞不懂。我能做点什么？

Text.en: Goin' by yer tone, I'd venture somethin's gone sideways.
Text.zh: 听你这口气，我猜是出什么幺蛾子了吧。

Text.en: The <i>{sel khari}, the sacred guardians of the First <i>{Barya}, have not arrived... <continue>Three nights before the Gifting, they are to bring the <i>{Barya} here. We cannot find them, nor have we received word. <continue>It is not mere chance that this has happened at such a time... there is an enemy at work. <continue>I am not permitted to know where the <i>{sel khari} live. Zarka, can you entrust this knowledge to an outsider?
Text.zh: <i>{塞卡莉}，原初<i>{巨灵币}的神圣守护者们尚未抵达……<continue>遵照传统，他们本应在圣水赐礼三天之前，将<i>{巨灵币}护送至此。我们既找不到他们，也未曾收到任何消息。<continue>这种时候出现变故绝非偶然……一定有人在暗中捣鬼。<continue>我无权知晓<i>{塞卡莉}的居所。扎卡，你能否将这个秘密托付给这位外来者？

Text.en: Yes, in times of need. This surely qualifies. <continue>You earned our trust by your care and service at Deshar. <continue>Come closer, young one. I must speak to you privately.
Text.zh: 可以，只有在危难之时方可，而眼下无疑是危急时刻。<continue>你在德莎尔无私的关怀和帮助赢得了我们的信任。<continue>过来，年轻人，我必须和你私下谈谈。

Text.en: My attempt at unification has not gone smoothly. As a people, we are... stubborn.
Text.zh: 我试图统一各部的行动并不顺利。我的族人……他们生性固执。

Text.en: I'm sure you know this better than anyone.
Text.zh: 你比任何人都更了解自己的族人。

Text.en: The Gifting of Water is an opportunity to air grievances and heal old wounds. <continue>It takes place at the last reservoir of the original Seven Waters. The last true remnant of Keth's glory days. <continue>If I am to enlist the other <i>{akharas} in this war, I must do so at this ritual. <continue>The fact that they have chosen me to do this means they are at least open to moving forward.
Text.zh: 圣水赐福仪式正是化解积怨，愈合创伤的契机。<continue>仪式将在原初七水的最后一座水库举行——那是克斯鼎盛时期仅存的遗迹。<continue>如果想召集其他<i>{阿卡拉}加入这场战争，只有在仪式上提出。<continue>他们选择由我主持仪式，这至少意味着他们愿意尝试改变。

Text.en: Did you abandon your tale-woman duties before they taught you this?
Text.zh: 莫非你抛下故事妇人的职责时，他们还未曾教你这些？

Text.en: Long ago, when the bounty of the land began to shift against us, we enacted traditions that would safeguard the future. <continue>Each <i>{akhara} sends one <i>{dekhara} per generation to the <i>{sel khari}. Thus, they are loyal to all, and to none. <continue>They watch over vast underground reserves that were built in Keth's fading days. <continue>For countless generations, the Gifting of Water meant pouring water <i>{into} the reserves. <continue>Now, in this era of thirst and heat, we are given what our forebears set aside for us. <continue>When I drink, it is from water given to me by my mother, and her mother before her. Their sacrifice. Their resolve. <continue>The Grand Water Djinn, Rashi, is crucial to maintaining these reserves... which is why the <i>{sel khari's} absence is so concerning.
Text.zh: 很久以前，当大地的恩赐开始减少，我们便立下了守护未来的传统。<continue>每位<i>{阿卡拉}每一世代都要派遣一名<i>{德卡拉}前往<i>{塞卡莉}。因而他们的忠诚属于全族，而非任何一部。<continue>他们看守着克斯衰亡时期建造的庞大地下水库。<continue>无数世代以来，圣水赐福意味着将净水<i>{注入}这些水库。<continue>而在当今这焦渴时代，我们正享受着先祖为我们预留的恩泽。<continue>当我饮水时，饮下的是母亲赐予之水，是她的母亲乃至世代先祖的恩赐——更是她们的牺牲与决意。<continue>伟大的水之巨灵拉什是维系这些水库的关键……正因如此，<i>{塞卡莉}的失踪才如此令人不安。”

Text.en: To keep the reserves from running dry, Rashi collects water from the winds throughout the seasons. <continue>She inhabits the First <i>{Barya}. The <i>{sel khari} bring her to us. We take her to the sacred site, and Rashi bleeds what she gathered. <continue>In this way, we replenish our reserves. <continue>Rashi is not a criminal, like so many others bound to <i>{baryas.} She chose this duty. <continue>She could choose to move on at any time; to pass the burden to another. She has not yet done so. <continue>It is a grim and solemn duty. Each time she is bled of water... her screams are haunting. <continue>This existence, alone in all that I have seen in this world, I do not believe I could endure. She has my utmost respect.
Text.zh: 为防止水库枯竭，巨灵拉什终年从季风中汲取净水。<continue>她栖居于原初<i>{巨灵币}之中，由<i>{塞卡莉}护送到这里，再由我们携带她前往圣地。在那里，拉什会释放所有汲取的净水。<continue>通过这种方法，水库才能得到补充 。<continue>拉什不同于其他被束缚于<i>{巨灵币}的罪徒，她自愿肩承担这个责任。<continue>她完全可以随时离开，把责任交给继任者……但她始终没有放弃。<continue>这是项沉重而庄严的使命。每次释放净水时……她的哀嚎令人心惊胆战。<continue>我自问无法承受那样的重担，而她就是这样举世无双的存在。她值得我最崇高的敬意。

Text.en: What happened to the <i>{sel khari}?! Who are you?! I should slay you where you stand!
Text.zh: <i>{塞卡莉}出了什么事？你究竟是什么人？我要杀了你！

Text.en: I am Jado... Now, stay your blade! I am not your enemy!
Text.zh: 我是贾多……把武器收起来！我不是你的敌人！

Text.en: As <i>{Sekhema} of the Ardura, I demand you explain yourself!
Text.zh: 我以阿杜拉<i>{丝克玛}的身份命令你解释清楚！

Text.en: Very well... we learned that someone was planning an attack on the Gifting of Water. <continue>I infiltrated the <i>{sel khari} to protect the First <i>{Barya}.
Text.zh: 好……我们得知有人正计划袭击圣水赐福仪式。<continue>我潜入<i>{塞卡莉}是为了保护原初<i>{巨灵币}。

Text.en: Who is this 'we'?
Text.zh: 你说的“我们”是谁？

Text.en: The... Order of the Djinn.
Text.zh: 巨……巨灵教团。

Text.en: So... Zarka's suspicions are true. You <i>{have} started to replenish your ranks. <continue>Who attacked you?
Text.zh: 所以……扎卡的怀疑是真的。你们<i>{确实}开始招兵买马了。<continue>谁袭击了你们？

Text.en: A rogue sect of Faridun who stand against you. Their leader calls himself 'Prince Azmadi.'
Text.zh: 一支反对你的法里顿流亡派系。他们的首领自称阿兹马迪王子。

Text.en: More false titles! How did you let a pack of stray dogs best you?
Text.zh: 有一个虚假的头衔！你们怎么会被这样一群丧家之犬打败？

Text.en: They had help... from the Serpent Clan.
Text.zh: 他们有帮手……来自毒蛇部族的帮手。

Text.en: Of course! They swore vengeance for the death of their Queen at Keth.
Text.zh: 果然啊！他们的女王死在了克斯，他们立誓要血债血偿。

Text.en: Even then, I should have been able to hold them off. <continue>But Azmadi... he had strange abilities... powers beyond his own... <continue>He invoked the name Zarokh... <continue>They've taken the First <i>{Barya}, and they left in the direction of Qimah.
Text.zh: 即便如此，我本来差点就能抵挡他们……<continue>但这个阿兹马迪王子……他拥有诡异的力量……远超其自身的力量……<continue>他竟然呼唤着扎洛卡的名字……<continue>他们夺走了原初<i>{巨灵币}，朝着奇玛尔城的方向去了。

Text.en: Then we must give chase! Come, let us return to my <i>{akhara}.
Text.zh: 我们必须追击！来吧，随我返回我的<i>{阿卡拉}。

Text.en: I have not yet decided whether I support this rebuilding of the Order of the Djinn. <continue>The Maraketh are already committed to protecting the world. <i>{I} am already committed. <continue>It is dangerous to have a rogue element operating unseen... no matter how well-intentioned they may be.
Text.zh: 我尚未决定是否支持巨灵教团的重建。<continue>马拉克斯人早已肩负守护世界之责，<i>{我}也早已立下誓言。<continue>放任一个不受控制的组织在暗中运作，这很危险……无论他们的初衷多么良好。

Text.en: Jamanra was a worthy opponent, and was given a warrior's death. <continue>This 'Azmadi' is a craven dog, assaulting our traditions directly. <continue>He will not receive recognition when he dies.
Text.zh: 亚曼拉是值得敬重的对手，所以我们赐予他勇士的荣耀之死。<continue>而这个阿兹马迪却是个卑劣的懦夫，他公然践踏我们的传统。<continue>他的死不会得到任何荣耀的见证。

Text.en: The Gifting of Water went well. We are ready. <continue>I will make preparations. We shall meet you in Kingsmarch.
Text.zh: 圣水赐福仪式进行得很顺利。我们已准备就绪。<continue>我会做好准备工作，在君锋镇与你会合。

Text.en: This will be a war for the very soul of Wraeclast. I can imagine no greater battle, no greater cause. <continue>If we are to die... <continue>... we will die <i>{well}.
Text.zh: 这将是一场争夺瓦尔克拉斯灵魂的战争。我想象不出更伟大的战斗，更伟大的事业。<continue>如果我们注定要死……<continue>……我们会死得<i>{其所}。

Text.en: What brings you here? Your timing is impeccable. We could sorely use your aid.
Text.zh: 你怎么来了？你来得正是时候。我们急需你的帮助。

Text.en: It's good you're here. Our will to survive is being tested, yet again.
Text.zh: 你来得正好。我们的生存再次受到了威胁。

Text.en: Father... we need the strength of the Ezomytes. Wraeclast is in peril.
Text.zh: 父亲……我们需要艾兹麦人的力量。瓦尔克拉斯危在旦夕。

Text.en: I came here to recruit you, not the other way around.
Text.zh: 我来这儿是为了招募你，而不是被你招募。

Text.en: That is unfortunate. We have great need of you, and your fellow Ezomytes.
Text.zh: 那真不幸。我们非常需要你的帮助，还有你的艾兹麦同胞。

Text.en: It's good to be home, but I, too, have grave news. Wraeclast may fall to Corruption, and we need you to fight.
Text.zh: 回家感觉确实很好，但我带来了坏消息。瓦尔克拉斯面临腐化的威胁，我们需要你们的帮助。

Text.en: Aye, Renly. Seems our survival will be tested to the end of our days. The Ezomytes are needed for a coming battle.
Text.zh: 唉，蓝礼。看来我们这辈子都会受到威胁。大战将至，我们需要艾兹麦人的力量。

Text.en: Unfortunately, we need the Ezomytes to stave off a coming doom.
Text.zh: 真是不幸。我们需要艾兹麦人的帮助，抵御一场旷世浩劫。

Text.en: Wish I had better news for ya mate, but we need ya. You and all your Ezomytes. War is coming.
Text.zh: 真希望我能给你带来好消息，老兄，但现在我们需要你。你和你的艾兹麦同胞。战争就要来了。

Text.en: The Ezomytes have weathered much... but worse is comin'. And we'll need yer help if we're to stop it.
Text.zh: 艾兹麦人历经风雨……但真正的危机即将来临。若想阻止这场灾厄，我们需要你出手相助。

Text.en: By the First Ones... you're calling us to war? Our people do owe you a debt... but right now, Ogham is on the brink of destruction once more. <continue>We can't sleep. We can't move. We can't rebuild... We're being picked off in the dead of night! <continue>One by one, our people are being dragged off into the darkness. <continue>Only the distant sound of screams gives us a clue as to what has happened.
Text.zh: 初生之子在上……你要征召我们参战？我们确实欠你份人情……但现在，欧甘再次面临毁灭的危机。<continue>我们无法安眠，无法逃离，也无法重建……我们的人民正消失在黑暗之中！<continue>一个接着一个，被拽入黑暗。<continue>只有远方传来的凄厉尖叫，给我们些许线索。

Text.en: By the First Ones... you're calling us to war? My boy... you know my people are your people, and we stand with you. But, right now, Ogham is on the brink of destruction once more. <continue>We can't sleep. We can't move. We can't rebuild... We're being picked off in the dead of night! <continue>One by one, our people are being dragged off into the darkness. <continue>Only the distant sound of screams gives us a clue as to what has happened.
Text.zh: 初生之子在上……你要征召我们参战？我的孩子……你知道我的同胞就是你的同胞，我们一定会与你并肩作战。但现在，欧甘再次面临毁灭的危机。<continue>我们无法安眠，无法逃离，也无法重建……我们的人民正消失在黑暗之中！<continue>一个接着一个，被拽入黑暗。<continue>只有远方传来的凄厉尖叫，给我们些许线索。

Text.en: What's doing this, Father?
Text.zh: 父亲，这是何人所为？

Text.en: And I suppose you want me to deal with this, too.
Text.zh: 我猜，你想请我去摆平这事，是吧？

Text.en: This sounds serious.
Text.zh: 情况听起来很严峻。

Text.en: It never ends.
Text.zh: 危机真是没完没了。

Text.en: That's no bloody good.
Text.zh: 这可不太妙。

Text.en: Do you have any idea <i>{why} this is happening?
Text.zh: 你知不知道<i>{为什么}会发生这种事?

Text.en: Kidnapping, but no ransom?
Text.zh: 绑架，却不要赎金？

Text.en: Who's responsible for all this?
Text.zh: 是谁引起了这场灾祸？

Text.en: Our neighbours... in Holten. Thane Wulfric and Lady Elswyth have lost their bloody minds. <continue>Bloodletting. Torture. Pain, misery, and death! <continue>That's all that lies in wait for those stolen off to Holten. <continue>Look, now I understand you need us, but we can't leave this place undefended in the face of this. <continue>Help us once more, and we'll come to your aid.
Text.zh: 我们在霍尔顿的邻居，乌弗瑞克领主和艾尔斯威丝女士陷入了疯狂，<continue>他们变得残忍，嗜血。折磨，痛苦，还有死亡！<continue>这就是被掳往霍尔顿的人最后的悲惨结局。<continue>听我说，我明白你需要帮助，但我们不能对此坐视不管，任凭这片土地惨遭蹂躏。<continue>再帮助我们一次吧，我们一定会对你伸出援手。

Text.en: I will put this Thane in his place.
Text.zh: 我会让这位领主清醒清醒。

Text.en: Fine. I'll do your dirty work.
Text.zh: 行吧，脏活累活就交给我吧。

Text.en: I will do this thing you ask of me.
Text.zh: 我会完成你的请求。

Text.en: I will do what I must to defend my home.
Text.zh: 保卫家园，我义不容辞。

Text.en: Alright then. I'll hunt them down.
Text.zh: 那好吧，我会去猎杀他们。

Text.en: Consider it done.
Text.zh: 包在我身上。

Text.en: I'm on it. Gladly.
Text.zh: 交给我吧，乐意之至。

Text.en: Aye, Renly. If that's what's needed, I'll see it done.
Text.zh: 明白，蓝礼。既然要这么干，我保证搞定。

Text.en: Good. We've still not rebuilt the roads the Count destroyed, so you'll have to take a harrowing route. <continue>Of late, we call it the Blackwood, for it has become home to darkness and madness. <continue>You'll find a path to reach it through the crops. <continue>Una knows more about the Blackwood's curse... I dare not go there. <continue>Should you need to, call on her.
Text.zh: 太好了。被伯爵摧毁的道路还没能修复，你只能走一条更为危险的道路。<continue>最近，我们把那条路称为黑木林，因为那里现在已沦为黑暗与疯狂的巢穴。<continue>找一条穿越庄稼地的路，就能通往那里。<continue>乌娜比我更了解黑木林的诅咒……我根本不敢靠近那里。<continue>如果需要的话，就去找她问问吧。

Text.en: The Thanedom of Holten is closest to us by land. Their Thanes have long been our Count's right-hand men. <continue>We don't even consider ourselves separate. We intermarry all the time, and share in the harvest. <continue>It was bad enough when Thane Wulfric refused to help us, due to the Count's madness. <continue>Stealing people away when we're vulnerable... it is unthinkable...
Text.zh: 霍尔顿领主的封地与我们最为接近。他们的领主世代以来都是我们伯爵的左膀右臂。<continue>我们向来不分彼此，互相联姻，分享收获。<continue>当伯爵疯病发作时，乌弗瑞克领主拒绝对我们伸出援手，那时情况就已经很糟了。<continue>危难之际，他竟然趁机掳掠我们的人民……这简直天理难容……

Text.en: You know it well. The Thanedom of Holten is closest to us by land, and their Thanes have long been our Count's right-hand men. <continue>It was bad enough when Thane Wulfric refused to help us, due to the Count's madness. <continue>Stealing people away when we're vulnerable... it's unthinkable...
Text.zh: 你应该知道。霍尔顿领主的封地与我们最为接近。他们的领主世代以来都是我们伯爵的左膀右臂。<continue>当伯爵疯病发作时，乌弗瑞克领主拒绝对我们伸出援手，那时情况就已经很糟了。<continue>危难之际，他竟然趁机掳掠我们的人民……这简直天理难容……

Text.en: I won't let this stand.
Text.zh: 我绝不会坐视不管。

Text.en: Well, we thought that Thane Wulfric and Lady Elswyth might have been driven mad by Corruption... <continue>... but Holten, and the Blackwood, oh... are suffering from a far different malady. <continue>Cultists walk their streets, darkness clouds their land, and whispers hound any who travel there. <continue>Finn told us how it reminds him of Freythorn. The bloodletting... the rituals... oh... it's horrible to think an entire Thanedom has fallen.
Text.zh: 唉，我们原以为乌弗瑞克领主和艾尔斯威丝女士可能是被腐化逼疯了……<continue>可是看看霍尔顿，还有黑木林，唉……这是一种截然不同的病症。<continue>邪教徒大行其道，黑暗笼罩着大地，低语纠缠路过的旅人。<continue>芬恩说，那情景让他想起了茂棘深林。那嗜血的……仪式……哦……太可怕了，整个领主封地都沦陷了。

Text.en: Travellers report terrible things happening in Holten. <continue>Those who refuse to join the cult are... ritually cut. Warnings written in blood on doors and... and on the stone of the roads. <continue>That was the last we heard. I've no doubt it's gotten worse. Creeping darkness has spread even further, it seems. <continue>The Ogham of old would have stamped out this madness with strength of arm and heart...
Text.zh: 霍尔顿的骇人事件在旅人之间传得沸沸扬扬。<continue>拒绝加入邪教的人惨遭……仪式性肢解。道路和大门上涂满了鲜血书写的警告……<continue>我们知道的只有这么多。情况越来越危急了。黑暗似乎蔓延得更深远了。<continue>如果欧甘恢复往日的荣光，一定会以铁腕与决心荡平这场危难……

Text.en: Many of those stolen away were by the hand of that Warden. The torturous fiend. You've done us a service.
Text.zh: 许多人都是被那个典狱长亲手掳走的。这个残酷的恶魔。感谢你的帮助。

Text.en: Ogham's the maiden at the end of the bridge, so to speak. Right now, we're working on food and clean water. <continue>There were a fair number of survivors hiding in the woods. A stroke of fortune, but that comes with needs. <continue>For now, we're just happy to survive each day... and each night.
Text.zh: 可以说，欧甘是最后的防线。眼下，我们正忙于解决食物和水的问题。<continue>有相当数量的幸存者一直躲在森林里。这实属侥幸，但他们也需要照顾。<continue>就目前而言，只要能安然度过每一天……以及每一夜，我们就心满意足了。

Text.en: I knew you'd handle it! You've never let us down. I thank you. <continue>Now... as promised, we are prepared to follow you into the great battle you spoke of. <continue>I've spoken on your behalf. The Thanes will answer the call. The clans will all join this war. <continue>If this is the end, you bloody well know the Ezomytes will go down fighting.
Text.zh: 我就知道你会成功！你从不会让我们失望，我真心感谢你。<continue>好了……正如我们的约定，我们已经准备好加入你所说的大战。<continue>我以你的名义发了话，所有的领主都会响应号召，各个地区将全体参战。<continue>倘若这就是世界末日，他们应该清楚艾兹麦人一定会血战到底！

Text.en: Yet again, you make me proud, my boy. You've never let me down. And I thank you. <continue>Now... as promised, we are prepared to follow you into the great battle you spoke of. <continue>I've spoken on your behalf. The Thanes will answer the call. The clans will all join this war. <continue>If this is the end, you bloody well know the Ezomytes will go down fighting.
Text.zh: 好样的，孩子，你再次让我为你骄傲！你从不会让我们失望，我真心感谢你，<continue>好了……正如我们的约定，我们已经准备好加入你所说的大战。<continue>我以你的名义发了话，所有的领主都会响应号召，各个地区将全体参战。<continue>倘若这就是世界末日，他们应该清楚艾兹麦人一定会血战到底！

Text.en: So this is Kingsmarch. Quite the marvel. <continue>And Dannig's forge... Oh, what I wouldn't give for some of that gear! <continue>We're ready and waiting. Right behind you.
Text.zh: 这就是君锋镇。真是个奇迹。<continue>还有丹宁的铁匠铺……哦，我愿意用一切去换那些装备！<continue>我们准备就绪，随时待命。就在你身后。

Text.en: Welcome to Mount Kriar - not the friendliest peak in the Ranges. <continue>Keep your footin', keep your wits... and you might just leave with both in-tact. <continue>If not, well... these slopes are littered with folk who made the same mistake.
Text.zh: 欢迎来到克里亚山——这可不是山脉中最友善的山峰。<continue>站稳脚跟，保持理智……你可能还能完整地离开这里。<continue>如果做不到，那么……这些山坡上到处都是犯同样错误的人的尸体。

Text.en: Look at you, back in the Ranges... never thought I'd see the day. <continue>I won't tell you to watch your step - you know the perils of these peaks better than most. <continue>Doubt the Elders would be happy to know you're back. But I won't be the one to tell 'em.
Text.zh: 瞧瞧你，又回到了山区……真没想到还会有这一天。<continue>我想，不需要我提醒你留神脚下——你比多数人都清楚这些山峰可不是吃素的。<continue>长老们要是知道你回来了肯定会不高兴。但我可不会告诉他们。

Text.en: I know the one. Once a proud hunter, now lost to madness. <continue>Put her kin to the spear 'cause 'the Spirit told her to' - or so she says... <continue>More likely the Snake. <continue>If you could deal with her, you'd be doin' us all a favour... and probably her, as well.
Text.zh: 我认识那家伙。她曾是骄傲的猎手，如今却陷入了疯狂。<continue>以“万灵的意志”为由举矛刺向自己的同胞——她如今依然如此声称……<continue>只怕她是受了毒蛇的蛊惑。<continue>如果你能解决她，便是帮了我们所有人……或许也是帮她解脱。

Text.en: Wayward is right. Once a fine hunter... now she's turned her spear on her own. <continue>Says the Spirit told her to do it. You and I both know that's not true. <continue>Sounds more like the Snake if you ask me... <continue>If you face her, make it quick. That's not the Spirit's path she's walkin'... and hasn't been for a long time.
Text.zh: 她确实迷失了。她曾是一名优秀的猎手……如今却举矛刺向自己的同胞。<continue>她竟然还声称这是万灵的意志。你我都知道，她这是在胡扯。<continue>要我说，听着更像是毒蛇的意志……<continue>如果你要面对她，务必速战速决。她已偏离了万灵指引的道路……早已背离了多年。

Text.en: Never seen such a woeful misunderstandin' of the Spirit in all my days. <continue>Don't know what possessed her to think she were doin' its work. <continue>Spirit never asked me to kill an innocent. And if it did, well... I'd have to question me own sanity.
Text.zh: 我这辈子从没见过对万灵如此可悲的曲解。<continue>真不明白是什么让她自以为是在践行万灵的意志。<continue>万灵从未要求我杀害无辜者。若它果真如此要求……那一定是我疯了。

Text.en: Never thought I'd see the Spirit's name used to justify such butchery. <continue>Folk like us know it'd never ask for the blood of an innocent. <continue>If it ever did... well, I'd wonder if I'd lost me mind entirely.
Text.zh: 从未想过有人竟借万灵之名犯下屠杀的罪行。<continue>像你我这样的人都明白，万灵绝不会索要无辜者的鲜血。<continue>若它果真如此……那一定是我疯了。

Text.en: Don't feel good, puttin' someone down - but don't lose any sleep over it.
Text.zh: 取人性命的滋味不好受，但你不必为此挂怀。

Text.en: Don't feel good to kill one o' your own - but don't lose any sleep over it.
Text.zh: 手刃同胞的滋味不好受，但你不必为此挂怀。

Text.en: Truth is, a lotta people are dead because o' her... and now, no more will be.
Text.zh: 很多人死在她手上……而现在，不会再有了。

Text.en: Some call him a genius. Others, a monster. The truth is usually somewhere in between. <continue>Me? I say power like his comes with a cost... and it's never the one wieldin' it who pays. <continue>Remember that the next time you go thinkin' you're on the same side.
Text.zh: 有人称他为天才，也有人视其为怪物。真相往往介于两者之间。<continue>至于我？我认为他获得的那种力量注定要付出代价……而付出代价的，从来不是获得力量之人。<continue>下次你自以为与他同路时，最好记住这一点。

Text.en: So... you've thrown in with Doryani. Can't say I saw that comin'. <continue>You always did walk to the beat o' your own drum. Just hope you know what you're doin'. <continue>Men like him... they make poor enemies. And even worse allies.
Text.zh: 所以……你终究还是和多里亚尼结盟了。真是没想到啊。<continue>你向来特立独行。希望你清楚自己在做什么。<continue>像他这样的人……作为敌人很难缠，作为盟友会更致命。

Text.en: I am troubled by stories o' deception... by voices that are not the Spirit. <continue>Is there someone out there misleading us? Whisperin' in our ears, leading us to our dooms? <continue>It sounds like our stories of the Snake, but of that, I know little. Hasn't troubled us for generations. <continue>An Elder would know better than I.
Text.zh: 那些关于欺骗的故事让我深感不安……那并非万灵的声音。<continue>难道有人在误导我们？在我们耳边低语，引导我们走向毁灭？<continue>这听起来很像传说中关于毒蛇的故事，但我对此所知甚少。几个时代以来，毒蛇从未骚扰过我们。<continue>长老会比我更了解其中的缘故。

Text.en: You crested Mount Kriar and lived to tell the tale. <continue>Few can claim that - fewer still who ain't Azmeri. <continue>I'm sure your path now leads elsewhere... but know you'll always be welcome here.
Text.zh: 你成功登顶克里亚山，并且奇迹般地活了下来。<continue>能做到这一点的人寥寥无几——而非阿兹莫里出身的人，能做到的更是凤毛麟角。<continue>我明白你的征途终将通向别处……但请记住，这里永远欢迎你。

Text.en: There was a time I thought I'd never see you on these mountains again. <continue>But Elders be damned - you came back, and stood atop Mount Kriar besides. <continue>Whatever road calls you next, know that my hearth will always be open to you.
Text.zh: 我曾以为此生再也无缘在这群山中与你相见。<continue>但长老们都错了——你不仅回来了，更是登上了克里亚之巅。<continue>无论你的征途指向何方，我的炉火永远为你燃烧。

Text.en: I am Elder Madox. <continue>Here... I observe the biting chill of the gale... <continue>... while I mourn our losses. <continue>The Winter Owl blesses you, and the Spirit concedes. <continue>I have little time left in this world, and I am on my last adventure. <continue>From here, I shall observe winter across three weeks of night. <continue>Then, I shall join the wisdom of owls who came before me. <continue>Before you depart... I bear a gift.
Text.zh: 我是马多克斯长老。 <continue>我在这里……感受着凛冽的刺骨寒风……<continue>哀悼我们的逝去的同胞。<continue>凛冬之枭保佑你，万灵也认可你。<continue>我时日无多，这将是我最后的旅程。<continue>从此刻开始，我将在三周的长夜之中守望寒冬。<continue>而后，我便将魂归历代先贤之列。<continue>在你离开之前，我有一份礼物送给你。

Text.en: I am Elder Madox. <continue>Your silencing of the Wayward Spear has calmed the Spirit. <continue>Here... I observe the biting chill of the gale... <continue>... while I mourn our losses. <continue>The Winter Owl blesses you, and the Spirit concedes. <continue>I have little time left in this world, and I am on my last adventure. <continue>From here, I shall observe winter across three weeks of night. <continue>Then, I shall join the wisdom of owls who came before me. <continue>Before you depart... I bear a gift.
Text.zh: 我是马多克斯长老。<continue>你消灭歧途长矛的善举抚慰了万灵。<continue>我在这里……感受着凛冽的刺骨寒风……<continue>哀悼我们的逝去的同胞。<continue>凛冬之枭保佑你，万灵也认可你。<continue>我时日无多，这将是我最后的旅程。<continue>从此刻开始，我将在三周的长夜之中守望寒冬。<continue>而后，我便将魂归历代先贤之列。<continue>在你离开之前，我有一份礼物送给你。

Text.en: It is my choice. My decision. <continue>I've lost greatly. In a moment's hesitation, I allowed untold suffering to befall my tribe. <continue>So now. I observe. Not as penance. Not for forgiveness. <continue>Just so.
Text.zh: 这是我自己的选择。<continue>我失去了太多。只因片刻的犹豫，我给部族招来了无尽的灾祸。<continue>所以现在，我在此守望。不为赎罪，也不为求得宽恕。<continue>只是守望而已。

Text.en: I saw what was coming. The wayward spear and her people... <continue>They lost sight of the Spirit. They fell to the promises of the Snake. <continue>I should have acted. But I did not. And now, I observe... alone.
Text.zh: 我早已预见了这场灾祸。歧途长矛与她的族人…… <continue>他们背离了万灵的指引，沉溺于毒蛇的蛊惑。<continue>我本应出手制止，最终却束手旁观。如今，只剩我一人……在此守望。

Text.en: The Spirit is all things. The Spirit is the world, and it is us... but the Snake stands alone. <continue>The Snake whispers. Lies. Manipulates. <continue>It has been so since the time of the Mother Soul. <continue>And now it speaks as the Spirit, but is not, leading the foolish to ruin.
Text.zh: 万灵即万物，万灵即世界，万灵也是我们自身……但毒蛇却孑然独立。<continue>毒蛇窃窃私语，散布谎言，蛊惑人心。<continue>自原初之魂时代起，便是如此。<continue>而今它假借万灵之名，引导愚者走向毁灭。

Text.en: The Mother Soul was hope. The will to carry on, no matter the cost. No matter the sacrifice. No matter the <i>{consequences}... <continue>The People of the Mountains cut ties with the Mother Soul long ago. We know not why, only that it happened. <continue>The First Children might be the only ones that could recall such things now.
Text.zh: 原初之魂曾经是希望，是坚定向前的意志，无论代价如何，无论<i>{后果}如何……<continue>山民却早已与原初之魂切断了联系。没有人知道为什么，事情就是如此。<continue>如今，只有初生之子能回想起那些古早往事。

Text.en: Is this story time!? <continue>Ugh. <continue>The First Children were sent to us for protection. <continue>They were nothing but trouble. Banished into the wastes, despite the ash and famine. Tragic. <continue>The People of the Mountains were very proud of the <i>{Last} Children, though.
Text.zh: 这么想听故事！？<continue>唉。<continue>初生之子曾被送来寻求我们的庇护。<continue>可他们却惹尽了麻烦，最终被放逐至蛮荒之地，在灰烬与饥荒中自生自灭。真是可悲。<continue>不过，当时的山民们却为<i>{终末}之子感到非常骄傲。

Text.en: Solari, Lunari, and Viridi, of course. I know that you know them! How could you not? <continue>Solari and Lunari are right there in the sky, and Viridi is beneath your very feet! <continue>You tease an old man. Leave me be.
Text.zh: 就是日曜女神，月影女神和维里迪。你肯定知道他们！怎么可能不知道？<continue>日曜女神和月影女神高悬于天空之上，而维里迪就在你们的脚下！<continue>你怎能这样捉弄我？你走吧。

Text.en: Good day to ya. I'm Hilda. <continue>Been roaming the world for some time now, hunting all manner of beasts... <continue>And as soon as I get my arse back to Mount Kriar... it's all turned to shite! <continue>My tribe has fled the region. Someone was roughin' them up. I got one guess who. <continue>Lythara, a skilled hunter from a neighbouring tribe, has lost her mind. <continue>She and her followers drove my people out, then she turned on her own. <continue>I'd sort her out myself, but she packs quite a punch. <continue>If you're headin' up the mountain, you'll have to deal with her... <continue>...but there's somethin' else I'd ask of ya.
Text.zh: 你好啊，我是希尔达。<continue>我在外游历有些时日了，狩猎各种猛兽……<continue>结果我刚回到克里亚山……就发现这儿全乱套了！<continue>我的部落被迫逃离，有人在找他们麻烦。我大概能猜到是谁。<continue>利萨拉，一个来自邻近部落的老练猎手，她是个疯子。<continue>她和她的追随者赶走了我的族人，然后她又对自己人下手了。<continue>我本想亲自解决她，但她拳头很硬。<continue>如果你要上山，你就得对付她……<continue>……不过，我还有别的事想拜托你。

Text.en: Good day, fellow mountain-born. I'm Hilda. <continue>Been roaming the world for some time now, hunting all manner of beasts... <continue>And as soon as I got my arse back to Mount Kriar... it's all turned to shite! <continue>My tribe has fled the region. Someone was roughin' them up. I got one guess who. <continue>Lythara, a skilled hunter from a neighbouring tribe, has lost her mind. <continue>She and her followers drove my people out, then she turned on her own. <continue>I'd sort her out myself, but she packs quite a punch. <continue>If you're headin' up the mountain, you'll have to deal with her... <continue>... but there's somethin' else I'd ask of ya.
Text.zh: 你好啊，山民老乡，我是希尔达。<continue>我在外游历有些时日了，狩猎各种猛兽……<continue>结果我刚回到克里亚山……就发现这儿全乱套了！<continue>我的部落被迫逃离，有人在找他们麻烦。我大概能猜到是谁。<continue>利萨拉，一个来自邻近部落的老练猎手，她是个疯子。<continue>她和她的追随者赶走了我的族人，然后她又对自己人下手了。<continue>我本想亲自解决她，但她拳头很硬。<continue>如果你要上山，你就得对付她……<continue>……不过，我还有别的事想拜托你。

Text.en: There's one beast I've not been able to best. A yeti deep within the caves of Mount Kriar. <continue>A truly fearsome bastard! <continue>Many claim to have seen him, just not up close... <continue>One thing is certain though, you'll hear him! Oh, an unmistakable howl against the icy winds. <continue>Seek him out, slay him and bring me his tusks. <continue>I'll make it worth yer while!
Text.zh: 有只野兽我一直没能搞定，一头藏在克里亚山深处洞穴里的雪怪。<continue>真是个难缠的狠角色！<continue>好多人都声称见过它，只是没人敢靠近……<continue>但有一点是肯定的，你肯定会听到它的动静！哦，那嚎叫声能穿透寒风，绝不会认错。<continue>找到它，宰了它，把它的獠牙带给我。<continue>我绝对不会亏待你！

Text.en: Outstandin'. Be careful... he's a tough bugger.
Text.zh: 太好了。不过小心点……那家伙很对付。

Text.en: Well, look at you then! Quite the hunter. <continue>Oh, these will fit in nicely with my trophy collection. I thank ya. <continue>Perhaps we can work together again sometime.
Text.zh: 哎呀，瞧瞧你！真不愧是个好猎手。<continue>哦，这些獠牙正好可以丰富我的战利品收藏。谢谢你。<continue>也许以后我们还能合作。

Text.en: Of course! I never doubted ya, mountain-born. <continue>Oh, these will fit in nicely with my trophy collection. I thank ya. <continue>Perhaps we can work together again sometime.
Text.zh: 太好了！我就知道你能行，山民老乡。<continue>哦，这些獠牙正好可以丰富我的战利品收藏。谢谢你。<continue>也许以后我们还能合作。

Text.en: The 'Spirit' is a load of codswallop, if you ask me. I've nothing more to say on it.
Text.zh: 要我说，那些关于万灵的说法纯粹是一派胡言。除此之外，我就没什么想说的了。

Text.en: Best not ask me about that codswallop, friend. I fear you'll not think too kindly about what I have to say...
Text.zh: 朋友，你最好别问我那些乱七八糟的事。我怕你不会喜欢我的说法……

Text.en: Alright then, ya nosy blighter! Let me tell it as it is. <continue>I'm a hunter, aye. I run the woods, the fields and I've tracked many beasts. <continue>Yet I've never heard a voice, or saw a light leading me. <continue>It's always been just me, alone. <continue>The Spirit stayed silent when my dear brother lay starving. <continue>Nor did it light up the night when my ailing mother was lost to the wilderness. <continue>Even if it does exist, what use is it, in a world brimmin' with pain and misery? <continue>For nothin'!
Text.zh: 好吧，你这爱打听的讨厌鬼！我就实话实说吧。<continue>老子是个猎手，这没错。我穿梭树林荒野，追踪过无数野兽。<continue>可从来没听过什么指引之声，没见过什么指路之光。<continue>从来都是老子独自一人。<continue>我亲弟弟饿得皮包骨头时，万灵屁都不放一个。<continue>我老娘病重时在那林子里走丢，它也没把黑夜照亮半分。<continue>就算这玩意儿真存在，在这满是痛苦悲惨的世道里能有什么用？<continue>屁用没有！

Text.en: There's <i>{living} Vaal in Mount Kriar? Well, I'll be damned. The stories are true! <continue>It's not some children's tale from the Elders, but a yarn we weave on the winter solstice. <continue>Many generations ago, a Vaal named Uromoti came to us and wanted to 'buy' a mountain from the Azmeri. <continue>You can't <i>{buy} a mountain! It's nonsense! They simply exist! <continue>So, we let him pass. Countless Vaal marched up there, trying to capture Mount Kriar. <continue>Well they busied themselves, certainly, as eventually, they went silent. <continue>We assumed they died up there, trying to 'buy' the mountain from the sky! <continue>Perhaps they did make a deal, but with Prospero instead... Ugh, he'd be the type to sell you a mountain.
Text.zh:
克里亚山里还有<i>{活着的}瓦尔人？好家伙，我真服了。那些传说居然是真的！<continue>这可不是长老们讲的儿童故事，而是我们在至夜里编织的奇谈。 
<continue>数个世代以前，一个名叫武罗摩提的瓦尔人来到我们这儿，想从阿兹莫瑞人手里“买”一座山。<continue>你没法<i>{买}一座山！这太荒唐了！山就在那里！ <continue>所以，我们放他过去了。数不清的瓦尔人跟了上去，想占领克里亚山。<continue>嗯，他们肯定忙活了好一阵，但最终，他们都沉寂了。<continue>我们以为他们都死在山上了，试图从天空手里“买”山！<continue>也许他们确实做成了交易，不过是和普洛斯彼罗做的……呃，他倒像是那种会卖座山给你的人。

Text.en: Been huntin' that Yeti in the proper season, these last five years. <continue>Oh, he's a shifty one. I follow his howl, but I think he knows. <continue>I think he's got... <i>{the sense.} <continue>I bet he feels the hair on his neck stand up when I'm on his trail. <continue>He'll never feel <i>{you} comin' though. Doesn't know your scent!
Text.zh: 过去这五年来，我一直在合适的季节狩猎那头雪怪。<continue>哦，它真是个狡猾的家伙。我循着它的嚎叫追踪，但我觉得它知道我在跟着。<continue>我觉得它有……<i>{那种灵性。} <continue>我敢打赌，当我追踪它时，它能感觉到自己颈后的毛都竖起来了。<continue>但它绝对感觉不到<i>{你}的靠近。它不认得你的气味！

Text.en: You movin' on? Aye, I should as well. <continue>Good luck with that, uh, Ori... Oriearth and whatnot. <continue>Should ya end up blowin' yourselves up again, try to escape back this way maybe!
Text.zh: 你要撤了？行吧，那我也该撤了。<continue>祝你们那什么奥……奥瑞亚还是什么的顺利！<continue>要是你们又把自己炸飞了——想办法溜回这儿来吧！

Text.en: This is where the Gifting would normally be enacted. <continue>It seems they have made their way into the sacred chamber. <continue>This could bode ill for Rashi. <continue>I will open the way.
Text.zh: 这里本是举行圣水赐福仪式之地。<continue>看来他们已设法进入了圣殿。<continue>这对拉什来说恐怕凶多吉少。<continue>我来开启通路。

Text.en: It is done.
Text.zh: 完成了。

Text.en: The First <i>{Barya} is safe. I will take it back to the surviving <i>{sel khari}.
Text.zh: 原初<i>{巨灵币}安全了。我会将它带回给幸存的<i>{塞卡莉}守卫。

Text.en: No. We must conduct the Gifting of Water as soon as possible.
Text.zh: 不。我们必须尽快进行圣水赐福仪式。

Text.en: You would still enact the ritual after such disruption?
Text.zh: 即便经历了这么大的动荡，你们依然要举行仪式？

Text.en: Yes... greater things are at stake. <continue>The Maraketh must unify, so that we can go to war. <continue>Let us return and prepare.
Text.zh: 没错……眼下正是千钧一发之际。<continue>马拉克斯人必须团结一致，只有这样，我们才能整军备战。<continue>让我们回去做准备吧。

Text.en: Yes... greater things are at stake. <continue>The Maraketh must unify, so that we can go to war. <continue>Now, we must clear the way for the other akharas to attend... <continue>Let us return and prepare.
Text.zh: 没错……眼下正是千钧一发之际。<continue>马拉克斯人必须团结一致，只有这样，我们才能整军备战。<continue>我必须为其他阿卡拉的到来扫清道路……<continue>让我们回去做准备吧。

Text.en: What impressive work. <continue>You may not see the Order of the Djinn in Oriath, but we'll be there... doing what we can. <continue>May you have warm nights on your journey.
Text.zh: 干得漂亮。<continue>或许奥瑞亚没有巨灵教团，但我们一定会拍马赶到……赴汤蹈火，在所不辞。<continue>愿你的征途长夜温暖。

Text.en: I was sent to infiltrate the <i>{sel khari} and guard the First <i>{Barya}. I failed, and now, I must hunt it down and retrieve it.
Text.zh: 我奉命潜入<i>{塞卡莉}守护原初<i>{巨灵币}。可我失败了——我必须追上他们，夺回原初<i>{巨灵币}。

Text.en: What is this 'Azmadi' trying to do with it?
Text.zh: 那个阿兹马迪，他想用原初<i>{巨灵币}做什么？

Text.en: He is trying to get to Qimah before we do. He may be trying to enact the Gifting himself as some sort of claim to power. <continue>Or... he may have something darker in mind.
Text.zh: 他试图抢在我们之前抵达奇玛尔城。或许他是想亲自主持圣水赐福仪式，并借此获取某种权利。<continue>又或者……他有更黑暗的图谋。

Text.en: I will not allow him to defile our traditions.
Text.zh: 我绝不会允许他亵渎我们的传统。

Text.en: Neither will I.
Text.zh: 我也一样。

Text.en: Azmadi possessed powers I have only seen the most gifted tale-women wield. I believe they call it 'chronomancy.' <continue>He channeled Time itself, as if it was an element to bend to his will. <continue>This power was not his own. He called out to 'Zarokh' to aid him.
Text.zh: 阿兹玛迪所掌握的力量，我只在那些最具天赋的说书人身上见过。我记得他们称之为“时占术”。<continue>他直接引导了时间，仿佛时间是一种可供他意志驱使的元素。<continue>这力量并非源于他本身。他曾呼喊扎洛卡之名寻求力量。

Text.en: Zarokh? The ancient Djinn locked in the depths of the original Trial of the <i>{Sekhemas}?
Text.zh: 扎洛卡？那个被封印在原初<i>{丝克玛}试炼深处的古老巨灵？

Text.en: I believe so. This may be part of a plan... perhaps... <continue>The First <i>{Barya}! It is the only one left powerful enough to carry Zarokh's essence!
Text.zh: 应该是的。这可能只是计划的一部分……也许……<continue>是原初<i>{巨灵币}！它是唯一留存下来的、足够强大到能承载扎洛卡精华的容器！

Text.en: He's trying to escape his punishment.
Text.zh: 扎洛卡想逃脱他的惩罚。

Text.en: That might be the case... If there is indeed a plan at work, we must be careful not to play into the schemer's hands.
Text.zh: 也许是吧……如果确实是这样的阴谋，我们必须小心，不要落入布局者的全套。

Text.en: If Azmadi <i>{has} been empowered by Zarokh, they must have met. <continue>And to have met, Azmadi must have completed the ancient Trial of the <i>{Sekhemas}.
Text.zh: 如果阿兹玛迪<i>{确实}从扎洛卡那里获得了力量，那他们必定见过面。<continue>而要想见面，阿兹玛迪必定已经完成了远古的<i>{丝克玛}试炼。

Text.en: Nonsense! The mere notion is absurd!
Text.zh: 不可能！这说法荒谬至极！

Text.en: Is it? In your heart of hearts, do you believe it impossible that a Faridun survived the test? <continue>Perhaps he made it to Zarokh... then, facing his doom, cut a deal.
Text.zh: 真的吗？在你内心深处，难道真觉得不可能有法里顿人能通过试炼？<continue>或许他真的见到了扎洛卡……而后在绝境之中达成了交易。

Text.en: I... suppose such a thing is possible. <continue>In recent days, I <i>{have} met many capable Faridun who are worthy to join us.
Text.zh: 我……我承认确实有这种可能。<continue>这些年来，我<i>{确实}遇见过许多出色的法里顿人，他们有资格加入我们的行列。

Text.en: Indeed. If he did complete the Trial... he may be attempting to supplant you and make a bid for <i>{Sekhema} of <i>{Sekhemas}.
Text.zh: 没错。如果他真的完成了试炼……或许他正试图取代你，争夺至尊<i>{丝克玛}的权位。

Text.en: The <i>{akharas} would never follow him.
Text.zh: <i>{阿卡拉们}绝不会追随他。

Text.en: Are you absolutely certain of that?
Text.zh: 你确定吗？

Text.en: Hmm... these <i>{are} strange times...
Text.zh: 嗯……最近这些年头<i>{确实}奇怪……

Text.en: We are a small but dedicated group. Orphans, only. One from each culture on Wraeclast. <continue>Our leader, Jun Ortoi, has been rebuilding the Order for the last twenty years in secret. <continue>I do not know what happened two decades ago... she refuses to talk about it. All we can do is look forward. <continue>The time has come for us to fight to save this world, and we shall succeed - or die trying.
Text.zh: 我们的教团规模不大但信念坚定，而且我们只收留孤儿。成员来自瓦尔克拉斯的每一种文明。<continue>我们的领袖，琼·奥托伊，过去二十年来一直在秘密重建这个教团。<continue>我不知道二十年前发生了什么……她对此绝口不提。我们所能做的唯有向前看。<continue>为拯救这个世界而战的时刻已经来临，我们必将成功——要么就死在这条路上。

Text.en: We have been watching you since you helped slay Jamanra. <continue>Sometimes, a catalyst appears - an exceptional individual in a time of great need. <continue>The Godslayer was one such catalyst. Perhaps you are, as well. We can certainly hope. <continue>I will not speak for Jun. She will introduce herself... when the time is right.
Text.zh: 自你帮助我们斩杀亚曼拉时起，我们便一直在关注你。<continue>有时，会出现一位“催化剂”般的人物——一个于危难之际现身的非凡之人。<continue>“屠神者”便是这样一位催化剂。或许你也是。我们当然如此希望。<continue>我不能代表琼的意见。她会在时机成熟时……亲自介绍自己。

Text.en: Welcome back, my friend! <continue>I have been studying under Zarka, to become a tale-woman. <continue>I now live in better conditions than I could ever have hoped for, and I owe it all to Asala. <continue>And to you! I tease! <continue>We do have serious business, however.
Text.zh: 欢迎回来，我的朋友！<continue>我一直在扎卡门下学习，想成为一名说书人。<continue>我现在的生活条件我以前想都不敢想，这一切都要感谢艾萨拉。<continue>我逗你呢！当然也要感谢你！<continue>不过，我们确实有正事要谈。

Text.en: The other <i>{akharas} must join the Gifting of Water if Asala is to become <i>{Sekhema} of <i>{Sekhemas}. <continue>The land is still plagued by the passing of the Beast. They cannot get through. <continue>May I ask you, my friend, to journey out to the other end of the Khari Crossing? <continue>The way is plagued by a great scorpion and a massive sandworm, both crazed by Corruption. <continue>Slay them, so that the others can reach us.
Text.zh: 如果想奉艾萨拉为至尊<i>{丝克玛}，其他所有<i>{阿卡拉}必须参与圣水赐福仪式。<continue>大地仍因邪兽过境而满目疮痍，他们暂时无法通行。<continue>我的朋友，能否请你穿越卡莉渡口抵达对岸？<continue>那里沿途盘踞着巨蝎与沙虫，它们都受到了腐化的影响而异变癫狂。<continue>我请求你清剿它们，为其他部族开辟通路。

Text.en: The scorpion and the worm are dead? <continue>Ten thousand praises, my friend! I cannot thank you enough. <continue>Now, the other <i>{akharas} can join the Gifting of Water...
Text.zh: 巨蝎和沙虫都死了？<continue>万分感谢，我的朋友！我怎么谢你都不为过。<continue>现在，其他的<i>{阿卡拉}们也能来参加圣水赐福仪式了……
