Text.en: The Ardura sleep wherever we must, like rice scattered in baskets, but I have my own pillow now. <continue>Have you ever had a pillow? It's incredible! <continue>Truly, these are the times of glory! Praise Asala!
Text.zh: 我们阿杜拉人随遇而安，如同散落于各个篮中的米粒，但我现在有了自己的枕头。<continue>你有过枕头吗？这感觉真是太棒了！<continue>这真可谓是荣耀的时代！赞美艾萨拉！

Text.en: She has given me a fair chance to catch up. <continue>Others want to be the next tale-woman, but it will go to the one who proves herself. <continue>I am more motivated. It will be me! <continue>It must be me. I owe her that.
Text.zh: 她给了我们公平竞争的机会。<continue>所有人都想成为下一代说书人，但唯有证明了自己的人才有机会。<continue>我比任何人都更有斗志。机会是我的！<continue>必须是我……这是我欠她的。

Text.en: One day, we will number enough to make Asala divine. <continue>Praise be to that day! She will bring rains to the Vastiri, and glory to our people!
Text.zh: 终有一日，我的人民将繁衍富足，足以尊奉艾萨拉为神。<continue>赞美那一天的到来！她将为瓦斯提里带来甘霖，为我们的人民带来荣耀！

Text.en: I know nothing of this 'Oriath,' but if you need us, we will answer. <continue>If I am to die in this war, I will do so gladly, as part of a family.
Text.zh: 虽然我不知道奥瑞亚远在何处，但只要你需要，我们一定赴汤蹈火，在所不辞。<continue>如果终将战死沙场，我也将作为家人与你并肩而战，我死而无憾。

Text.en: Welcome back. It is good to see you.
Text.zh: 欢迎回来。见到你真好。

Text.en: And you're alive!
Text.zh: 而且你还好好的！

Text.en: You're home!
Text.zh: 欢迎你回家！

Text.en: Bring it in here! Give your old pal Finn a hug! No? Alright. Fair.
Text.zh: 快过来！给老朋友芬恩一个拥抱！不要？好吧。

Text.en: Much has happened since you've been gone. <continue>Please, speak to Renly. He's our reluctant leader now.
Text.zh: 你离开之后，发生了很多事。<continue>去和蓝礼谈谈吧，他现在这个首领当的是不情不愿。

Text.en: We call it the Blackwood now because it is overrun by darkness. <continue>It was once a beautiful forest known to us as the Beltimber, but now, we dare not go there. <continue>The sun never reaches the forest floor, and cultists have made it their home.
Text.zh: 我们如今称之为黑木林，因为黑暗已将其吞噬。<continue>那里曾是一片美丽的森林，我们称之为芯木林。但如今，我们已不敢靠近那里。<continue>阳光再也照不到森林的地表，邪教徒把那里当做了老巢。

Text.en: It's the same sort of darkness we saw in Freythorn. <continue>The whispering, creeping, lurking shite, that killed all my friends.
Text.zh: 这与我们在茂棘深林见过的黑暗力量如出一辙。<continue>就是那种低语着、蔓延着、潜伏的鬼东西——夺走了我所有朋友的性命。

Text.en: It's alright. You've got a new family now.
Text.zh: 没关系，你现在有新的家人了。

Text.en: Yeah... I do.
Text.zh: 是啊……现在有了。

Text.en: The darkness creeping across our lands is some remnant of Corruption... <continue>The being that whispers from beyond... it's an opportunist.
Text.zh: 蔓延在我们土地上的黑暗，是某种“腐化”的残余……<continue>那个在界外低语的存在……是个投机者。

Text.en: Kickin' us while we're down. <continue>We saw this coming, too. Saw them take hold of Freythorn.
Text.zh: 这完全是落井下石。<continue>我们早预见到这场灾难，眼睁睁看着那些鬼东西蚕食茂棘深林。

Text.en: What scares me the most? The cultists... they're not insane. <continue>They <i>{choose} to follow the King in the Mists. They're not mad. They're fanatics.
Text.zh: 最让我恐惧的是什么？是那些邪教徒……他们并没有疯。<continue>他们是<i>{自愿选择}追随迷雾之王的。他们不是疯子，他们是狂热分子。

Text.en: I think the Thane and Lady of Holten made a deal. <continue>Reckon they felt vulnerable after all that happened here in Ogham. <continue>Many think they were forced into all this, but I think... yeah, I think they were <i>{willing}.
Text.zh: 我认为霍尔顿的领主与领主夫人暗中做了笔交易。<continue>估计是欧甘的连番变故让他们觉得自身难保。<continue>大多数人觉得他们是迫不得已……但我认为截然相反……没错，他们根本就是<i>{心甘情愿}。

Text.en: I fear you may be right.
Text.zh: 恐怕你说得对。

Text.en: There is a henge at the centre of the Stones of Serle. We could try to summon the fey spirits there.
Text.zh: 瑟尔之石中央有一座祭坛。我们或许能召唤奇异的万灵。

Text.en: Them old stones, eh? There's a strange power there. An old power. <continue>Makes your hair stand on end, some days.
Text.zh: 那些古老的石阵？那儿确实有股诡异的力量。非常古老的力量。<continue>有时候真让人毛骨悚然。

Text.en: It was one of the first sites the Ezomytes built when newcomers taught us runes so very long ago. <continue>That's why I hold out hope that we can still be heard. <continue>As the song goes, <i>{'the darker the night, the brighter the runelight...'} <continue>If I can reach the fey spirits, they will help you push through the darkness of the Blackwood. They can show you the way.
Text.zh: 这里是艾兹麦人最初建立的据点之一，早在远古时期外来者教会我们符文工艺时就已存在。<continue>这就是为什么我仍抱有希望，相信我们的声音仍然能被聆听。<continue>正如歌谣中所唱：<i>{“夜色愈深，符文之光愈亮……”}<continue>如果我能与奇异的万灵形成联结，它们一定能帮助你穿越黑木林。他们会照亮你前进的方向。

Text.en: This henge was one of the first sites the Ezomytes built when newcomers taught us runes so very long ago. <continue>I knew it would still hold power! As the song goes, <i>{'the darker the night, the brighter the runelight...'} <continue>The fey spirits heard us. They'll show you the way!
Text.zh: 这座巨石阵是艾兹麦人最早建造的圣地之一，早在远古时期外来者教会我们符文工艺时就已存在。<continue>我早就说过它还蕴藏着力量！正如歌谣中所唱：<i>{“夜色愈深，符文之光愈亮……”}<continue>奇异的万灵回应了我们。它们会为你照亮前进的方向。

Text.en: The henge at the centre of the Stones of Serle was one of the first sites the Ezomytes built when the newcomers taught us runes so very long ago.
Text.zh: 瑟尔之石中央的石阵，是艾兹麦人最早建造的圣地之一，早在远古时期外来者教会我们符文工艺时就已存在。

Text.en: Yeah, those stones are creepy. There's an odd power there. An old power. Makes your hair stand on end, some days.
Text.zh: 是啊，那些石头确实有些瘆人。那儿确实有股诡异的力量。非常古老的力量。有时候真让人毛骨悚然。

Text.en: I knew it would still hold power! As the song goes, <i>{'the darker the night, the brighter the runelight...'}
Text.zh: 我早就说过它还蕴藏着力量！正如歌谣中所唱：<i>{“夜色愈深，符文之光愈亮……”}

Text.en: Yeah, it's true, too. Some nights, way back when, I'd huddle by a runed stone all night... <continue>...just so the wolves wouldn't get me.
Text.zh: 千真万确。很久以前的某些夜晚，我会整夜蜷缩在刻着符文的巨石旁边……<continue>……这样就能躲开狼群的利齿。

Text.en: Not to worry, it's okay. You've got a home now.
Text.zh: 别担心，没关系的。你现在有个家了。

Text.en: Don't think I'm not happy with it. I just dream big!
Text.zh: 别误会，我不是不满意！我只是梦想变大了而已！

Text.en: <i>{Two} kitchens, <i>{four} bedrooms... yes, I know.
Text.zh: <i>{两个}厨房，<i>{四间}卧室……是的，我知道。

Text.en: We keep our history in the form of songs. <continue>I don't exactly know how runes were first brought to us. <continue>All we know is that 'newcomers' arrived and gave us knowledge in exchange for food and shelter. <continue>One song says: <i>{'There came a great storm, crashing across the sky...'} <continue><i>{'There came a great need, or the sodden would die.'} <continue>A children's tale speaks of fey folk who became human. <continue>A tribe that appeared out of a 'ring' of runes in the forest.
Text.zh: 我们以歌谣的方式传承历史。<continue>我并不确切知道符文最初是如何传到我们这里的。<continue>我们只知道，曾有外来者到来，用知识换取了食物和栖身之所。<continue>有一首歌谣这样唱道：<i>{“风暴骤起，席卷天际……”}<continue><i>{“绝大的饥渴降临，否则浸透者必将死亡。”}<continue>还有一手儿歌讲述了化作人形的精灵。<continue>一个从森林中的符文“石环”里出现的部落。

Text.en: Hmm... I guess I never really thought about all the runed stones lying all over Phaaryl before. <continue>They just <i>{were}. Always have been there.
Text.zh: 嗯……我似乎从来没有认真思考过法瑞欧之前遍布符文石阵的意义。<continue>它们就那样<i>{浑然天成}地存在着，亘古如此。

Text.en: Someone must know. The newcomers never moved on. <continue>Their names are part of our clan histories now.
Text.zh: 肯定有人知道。那些外来者从未离开。<continue>他们的名字如今已写入我们部族的史册。

Text.en: Wait, so... they... you know... with our ancestors?
Text.zh: 等等，意思是……他们……和我们的祖先……

Text.en: Yes.
Text.zh: 没错。

Text.en: Well that means I might never have been clanless! <continue>Maybe I'm from some long lost clan of heroic types from faraway!
Text.zh: 那意味着我可能从来就不是没有部族之人！<continue>或许我来自某个早已湮灭的英雄部族，来自非常遥远的地方！

Text.en: Yes, darling. You might very well be a hero's descendant.
Text.zh: 没错，亲爱的。你很可能是英雄的后裔。

Text.en: I friggin' knew it!
Text.zh: 我就知道！

Text.en: Somehow, the forces in the Blackwood know about the henge. <continue>The darkness has thickened around the site. It will not let us do this without a fight.
Text.zh: 不知为什么，黑木林中的势力知道了石阵的事。<continue>黑暗已在那周围汇聚起来。他们绝不会让我们轻易成功。

Text.en: It seems they gather here for a reason... seems they were trapped within that cursed one... <continue>With their aid, perhaps you'll be able to pass through the Blackwood...
Text.zh: 因为某种缘故，奇异的万灵汇聚在这里……就像是曾经被囚禁在那个受诅咒的怪物体内……<continue>有了他们的帮助，你就能穿越黑木林。

Text.en: You've saved us again. I can't thank you enough. <continue>Now... our people follow you to save the world, I suppose.
Text.zh: 你又一次拯救了我们。真实不知道该怎么感谢你。<continue>现在……我的人民将追随你去拯救这个世界。

Text.en: As long as we're together, we can face anything.
Text.zh: 只要并肩作战，我们无所畏惧。

Text.en: I'll play songs to soothe our brave soldiers' nerves.
Text.zh: 我会用歌声抚慰勇士们的心灵。

Text.en: Ahh... my talented songbird.
Text.zh: 啊……我才华横溢的夜莺。

Text.en: Oh, It's our old local hero!
Text.zh: 哟！这不是咱们本地那位老英雄嘛！

Text.en: Gotta admit, he's done a good job.
Text.zh: 不得不说他干得真漂亮！

Text.en: In this time of struggle, Renly's the good man we need.
Text.zh: 在这困难时期，蓝礼确实是我们需要的好领袖。

Text.en: He doesn't want to be in charge, and that's exactly why he should be.
Text.zh: 他越不想掌权，就越该掌权。

Text.en: I'm surprised to hear you support him so openly.
Text.zh: 没想到你会公开支持他。

Text.en: I can still bend his ear now and then. It works out.
Text.zh: 偶尔还能给他吹吹耳旁风，挺管用。

Text.en: Is that so?
Text.zh: 当真？

Text.en: Yep. Can't have people think I've gone soft... <continue>Got a reputation to uphold.
Text.zh: 当然。总不能让人觉得我耳根子软了……<continue>还得维持我的狠角色名声呢。

Text.en: Oh, you've got a reputation alright. No need to worry about that.
Text.zh: 哈，你的名声可是如雷贯耳，压根不用操心。

Text.en: Not everything's beans and cheese, even under Renly. <continue>I pushed to have the clanless... well, what's left of 'em... fill the ranks. <continue>To... join the clans. <continue>But that's not up to him. He can't change how people are.
Text.zh: 并不是所有事都尽如人意，即便是蓝礼也摆平不了有些事。<continue>我曾极力主张让那些无氏族者……嗯，让那些剩下的人……来填补我们队伍的空缺。<continue>让他们……加入各个氏族。<continue>但这并不是蓝礼能决定。他无法改变人们根深蒂固的观念。

Text.en: These trying times have brought out the best in us... but also the worst.
Text.zh: 困难时期逼出了最好的我们……同样也暴露了最差劲的我们。

Text.en: I'm gonna rebuild Freythorn, I am! <continue>Even though <i>{I'm} not clanless anymore, I need to do it. <continue>I'll build that palace in the trees, the one I told you about. With <i>{two} stocked kitchens. <continue>Just uh... well, it won't be for me anymore... It'll be for them.
Text.zh: 我要重建茂棘深林，我一定会的！<continue>尽管<i>{我已}不再是无氏族者，但我必须这么做。<continue>我会建起树上的宫殿，就是我跟你提过的那座。还要有<i>{两个}储备充足的厨房。<continue>只是呃……嗯，这次不仅仅是为我自己建的……而为了他们而建。

Text.en: Thank you for returning me to my place of service.<continue>Please, allow me to repay you. Choose.
Text.zh: 感谢你把我送回我的履职之地。<continue>聊表心意，请随意挑选。

Text.en: My crime is my own. I suffer and serve so that I may redeem myself.<continue>Please, do not do me the kindness of conversation.<continue>I do not deserve it.
Text.zh: 我实乃自作孽，不可活。我受苦服役，只为赎清自己的罪孽。<continue>恳请你不必屈尊与我交谈。<continue>我不配。

Text.en: A ring, you shall have.
Text.zh: 戒指一枚，属于你。

Text.en: An amulet, for the worthy.
Text.zh: 项链一串，赠予相配之人。

Text.en: A jewel, for one who shines.
Text.zh: 宝石一颗，授予璀璨之人。

Text.en: Are you serious? I'd finally convinced the Serpent Clan to free me!<continue>I will give you something to leave me alone. Choose.
Text.zh: 你是认真的吗？我好不容易才说服毒蛇部族放了我！<continue>我会给你些东西，求你别烦我。快选吧。

Text.en: What do you care? You're not going to free me.<continue>Fine.<continue>I fled. Kulemak personally assaulted our caravan, and I ran for my life.<continue>Everyone else died... and rose again, to serve the Abyssals.<continue>We should all have run. It was foolish to fight. I am no coward, and I'll never submit.
Text.zh: 你在乎什么？你又不会放了我。<continue>好吧。<continue>我逃跑了。库勒马克亲自袭击了我们的商队，我为了活命逃走了。<continue>其他人都死了……然后又复活了，去为深渊族效命。<continue>我们当初都该逃跑的。选择战斗太愚蠢了。我不是懦夫，我也永远不会屈服。

Text.en: May it rot your finger.
Text.zh: 愿它再也脱不下来。

Text.en: May it chafe your neck.
Text.zh: 愿它割伤你的动脉。

Text.en: Where do you wear such a jewel exactly? Oh... disgusting!
Text.zh: 你准备把这宝石用在哪儿？哦……太恶心了！

Text.en: The location of the <i>{sel khari} is closely guarded. You must never speak of it to anyone... or there will be consequences! <continue>Ascend the tower of Ab Binayi. At its height you will find a guiding seal. <continue>Activate it, and you will be shown the way.
Text.zh: <i>{塞卡莉}的所在地是我们严密守护的秘密。你绝不能向任何人透露……否则你会受到严厉的惩罚！<continue>登上阿布·比纳伊之塔。你会在塔顶找到一个指引印记。<continue>激活它，前路自会显现。

Text.en: The <i>{sel khari} were attacked? This is exactly why we keep their location secret! <continue>These criminals deserve the greatest punishment we can give... <continue>Hmm... Balbala's prison <i>{is} free...
Text.zh: <i>{塞卡莉}竟然遭到了袭击？这正是我们严守秘密的原因！<continue>这些罪犯必须受到最严厉的惩处……<continue>嗯……贝尔巴拉的监狱<i>{正好}空着……

Text.en: There are certain steps that need to be taken to recognise a <i>{Sekhema} of <i>{Sekhemas.} <continue>The Gifting of Water is a sign of solidarity between our <i>{akharas}. <continue>If it does not go well, the others may see it as a sign that Asala is too weak to lead... <continue>Ahh... we cannot let that happen.
Text.zh: 认可<i>{至尊丝克玛}需要完成特定的仪式。<continue>圣水赐福仪式是我们各<i>{阿卡拉}之间团结一致的象征。<continue>如果仪式进行得不顺利，其他人可能会认为艾萨拉缺乏力量、无力领导……<continue>唉……我们绝不能让这种情况发生。

Text.en: For most, the <i>{sel khari's} secrecy is kept on pain of death... but for you, there will be no violence. <continue>No. If you reveal this secret... <continue>I will tell embarrassing stories about you, and pass them down to those that come after me! <continue>You <i>{will} be remembered, but not as a hero... as a bumbling fool. A subject of mockery. <continue>It is a form of retaliation worse than death, second only to being forgotten. <continue>Please do not force me to fulfil this duty.
Text.zh: 对于多数人，<i>{塞卡莉}的秘密必须以死亡来守护……但对你，我不会动用暴力。<continue>我不会。如果你泄露这个秘密……<continue>我会把你的糗事编成故事，代代相传！<continue>你<i>{必将}被铭记，但不是以英雄之名……而是个闹笑话的蠢材，沦为青史留名的笑柄。<continue>这是比死亡更可怕的惩罚，仅次于被彻底遗忘。<continue>请你别逼我履行这份职责。

Text.en: Ah, so the Order of the Djinn <i>{has} been rebuilding these last twenty years. I suspected this was the case. <continue>The other tale-women talk of movements in the shadows. Tombs found open. Artefacts stolen. <continue>When these powerful relics never appeared in the hands of the Faridun, I knew it had to be someone more trustworthy... <continue>There are tales we tell, and tales we do <i>{not} tell. <continue>The Order has long fought to preserve this world... <continue>You would do well to earn their trust, young one.
Text.zh: 啊，看来<i>{巨灵教团}这二十年来一直在暗地里重建。我早料到会如此。<continue>其他说书人都在谈论暗处的异动：陵墓无故被开启，法器不翼而飞。<continue>这些强大圣物并没有落入法里顿人之手，我就猜到背后定有更可信之人……<continue>有些传说我们倾情讲述，有些传说我们<i>{绝口不提}。<continue>长久以来，巨灵教会都在守护这个世界……<continue>年轻人，如果你能赢得他们的信任，对你很有好处。

Text.en: Risu's training is coming along well. She has a mind for tales. <continue>When she is ready to replace me, I am going to go on a journey. <continue>I'd like to visit all the places I've been describing my whole life. <continue>Ezomyr, Ngamakanui... I hear Trarthus has rather invigorating vapours... <continue>Don't act so shocked. I've done my duty. It's time to live a little! <continue>Especially if the world is about to end...
Text.zh: 里苏的修行进展很顺利，她很有成为说书人的天赋。<continue>等到她能接替我的时候，我就要踏上旅程。<continue>我想去亲眼看看我念叨了一辈子的那些地方。<continue>艾兹麦，纳玛鲁伊……我听说特尔特斯清晨的薄雾令人神清气爽……<continue>别这么惊讶，我尽了我的职责！是时候享受享受人生了！<continue>更别说世界末日可能就要来了……

Text.en: Ah, the Pools of Khatal! I was young and spry last time I dared their rejuvenating depths. <continue>Long ago, there was a tale-man - yes, that's right, a male tale-woman! It's true! <continue>He had a unique gift for shaping water. <continue>His <i>{akhara} was beset by creatures of darkness in the early years of the Winter of the World, before the alliance was forged. <continue>It was the cold season, and the river had frozen. They built a fortress out of ice, but water was scarce. <continue>Ironic, yes? Being surrounded by water, even defended by it, for months... but dying of thirst. <continue>Khatal sensed water. He knew not where. He pulled with all his mystic might. He lifted his hands to the sky, beseeching the winds. <continue>The winds did not answer. This was long before Garukhan tamed them. <continue>Khatal would not give up. He tore at <i>{something}, water <i>{somewhere}... and finally, it emerged. <continue>From beneath! It ripped from the ground in great geysers, bringing both water <i>{and} warmth. <continue>Not only that, the water collapsed the enemy's tunnels, crushing an entire legion of creatures. <continue>His <i>{akhara} was saved... but Khatal turned to dust. He gave his very soul in the effort. <continue>Ahh... we honour his sacrifice still, by stopping at his mud pools on the way to the Gifting of Water.
Text.zh: 啊，卡哈塔之池！上次我浸入那焕发生机的池水时，还是个身手矫健的小伙子呢。很久以前，有位说书人——而且是位男性说书人！千真万确！他拥有驾驭水流的独特天赋。早在世界之冬初期、联盟尚未结成之时，他的{阿卡拉}在黑暗生物的围攻下岌岌可危。当时正值严冬，河流封冻。他们用冰筑起堡垒，却面临缺水的困境。多讽刺啊，不是吗？被水包围甚至被水守护数月……却即将渴死。要紧关头，卡哈塔感知到某处水源。他倾尽全部力量，举起双手向苍天祈求风声的回应。风没有回应。那时，格鲁坎尚未驯服狂风。卡哈塔不愿放弃，他撕扯着某物，牵引某处之水……最终，他成功了。奔流之水自地底喷涌而出！化作巨大的间歇泉，带来温水与暖意。更是一举冲垮了敌人的隧道，碾碎了整支黑暗军团。他的{阿卡拉}得救了……但卡哈塔化为了尘埃，为此献祭了灵魂。啊……我们至今仍在传颂他的牺牲，每次前往圣水赐福的途中，我们都会在他的泥水池畔驻足缅怀。

Text.en: I knew you would not let us down. <continue>In turn, <i>{we} will not let <i>{you} down. <continue>The Maraketh have been roused to war, and it is war we shall bring! <continue>If this is the end of times, then we will forge one last great tale to tell before Nekraata! <continue>The sum total of life's meaning - and it will be glorious. <continue>To Oriath, young one.
Text.zh: 我早就知道你不会令我们失望。<continue>相应地，<i>{我们}也绝不会辜负<i>{你}。<continue>马拉克斯人已吹响战争号角，我们必将带来真正的战争！<continue>若此刻真是末世降临，那我们就在涅克拉塔面前铸就的最后一段传奇！<continue>这将是生命意义的终极诠释，一场辉煌的壮举。<continue>向奥瑞亚进发吧，年轻人。

Text.en: Leave us be, outlander.
Text.zh: 别来烦我们，外来者。

Text.en: We answer only to Doryani.
Text.zh: 我们只听命于多里亚尼。

Text.en: Hail! That's what you outsiders say, right? <continue>I thought I heard the clink of gold in your pocket... <continue>I'm Ruhaki. I'm an apprentice carver here on Ngakanu. <continue>And I'd be keen to trade for some of that gold of yours...
Text.zh: 幸会！你们外来者都是这么打招呼的，对吧？<continue>我似乎听见你口袋里金币叮当作响……<continue>我叫鲁哈基，是纳卡努的雕刻学徒。<continue>我很乐意用些好东西换你那些金币……

Text.en: Hail! That's what you outsiders say, right? <continue>I thought I heard the clink of gold in your pocket... <continue>I'm Ruhaki. I'm an apprentice carver here on Ngakanu. <continue>I'd be keen to trade for some of that gold of yours...<continue>Seeing as you freed my favourite Elder, I'll even give you a discount.
Text.zh: 幸会！你们外来者都是这么打招呼的，对吧？<continue>我似乎听见你口袋里金币叮当作响……<continue>我叫鲁哈基，是纳卡努的雕刻学徒。<continue>我很乐意用些好东西换你那些金币……<continue>你救了我最敬重的长老，我甚至可以给你打点折！

Text.en: I knew I liked you. Thank you for saving Matiki. He's my favourite Elder! <continue>He never punished me... not that I ever did anything wrong... <continue>You know what, I think I could even budge on some of my prices... <continue>Just for you though!
Text.zh: 我早就知道我没看错你！谢谢你救了玛提吉，他可是我最敬重的长老！<continue>他从没处罚过我……虽然我也确实没犯啥错……<continue>要不这样，我可以破例给你打点折……<continue>只给你一个人哦！

Text.en: Well... just between you and I... <continue>I'm tired of Tavakai's teachings. I don't just need strength. I need a future! <continue>There isn't a single girl my age who'll marry a man with no prospects...
Text.zh: 这个嘛……咱俩私下说……<continue>我早受够塔瓦凯那套训诫了。光有力量还不够，还要有前途！<continue>这年纪的姑娘谁愿嫁没指望的男人？

Text.en: Wait... you're looking for a strong bloke? <continue>Hmm... I know a guy! <continue>...it's me. Keep it in mind, eh?
Text.zh: 等下……你在找靠谱的汉子？<continue>嗯……我有个不错的人选！<continue>……那就是我，不考虑考虑吗？

Text.en: Do you know of any? <continue>I know a quality fella who'll treat them like a queen. <continue>... It's me. Keep it in mind, eh?
Text.zh: 你知道哪有好对象？<continue>我认识个靠谱家伙，他会把姑娘当女王似的宠……<continue>……嗯，还是我。真的不考虑考虑吗？

Text.en: Amazing work, traveller! I am Torbik. And I... am in your debt. <continue>Perhaps I could interest you in a trinket or two... <continue>With a very special discount, in your honour, of course.
Text.zh: 干得漂亮啊，旅人！我是托尔比克，我欠你份人情。<continue>或许这玩意儿你能用得上……<continue>我会破例给你打点折，私人专享优惠！

Text.en: Amazing work— <continue>Oh. <continue>I didn't realise I was in the presence of someone such as yourself. <continue>I have only humble trinkets, but whatever I have, I offer to you. <continue>With a very special discount, in your honour, of course.
Text.zh: 干得漂亮……<continue>呃。<continue>真抱歉没认出您这样的大人物。<continue>虽只有些不入流的小玩意儿，但只要是我有的，都可以卖给您您。<continue>当然，我会破例给你打点折！

Text.en: I've taken this route for many years. I hold some hope that the bazaar will once again become a trade hub, like it was in ancient times. <continue>But... yes, the path is not without its dangers! <continue>My treasured rhex took off into the sands... <continue>I hope he'll come back.
Text.zh: 我行走这条商路多年，总巴望着这集市能再次成为贸易枢纽。<continue>但……没错，这条商路绝非太平无事！<continue>我心爱的鸟儿窜进了沙海……<continue>我真希望它能平安归来。

Text.en: He's a headstrong one, like me. Answers to 'Dabu.' Or, doesn't answer, as it were.
Text.zh: 这伙计跟我一样倔，叫它"达布"会应，或者不应，完全不理人，就这死相。

Text.en: Struck off on my own several years ago. It's tough, surviving the Vastiri without a caravan, but I like the freedom. <continue>Don't get me wrong, I miss home, but the expectations there were quite rigid. <continue>I'm hoping to get a cart full of worthwhile goods and haul it on down to the Karui archipelago. <continue>I hear there's water there, <i>{everywhere}, as far as the eye can see... <continue>Ridiculous, right? <continue>I've gotta see it for myself.
Text.zh: 几年前我就开始单打独斗了。没有商队庇护在瓦斯提里求生确实很艰难，但我贪恋这份自由。<continue>别误会，我也想家，但那儿条条框框太多了。<continue>我盼着能囤满一车好货，然后全都拖去卡鲁群岛。<continue>听说那儿到处是水，<i>{一眼望不到头}的那种……<continue>离谱吧？<continue>真得亲眼见识下不可！

Text.en: Hello, outlander. I've spent my life studying you in our archives... <continue>I can't believe I get to meet one of you in the flesh. <continue>This will be an interesting time.
Text.zh: 幸会，外来者。我一生都在档案库中皓首穷经地研究你们……<continue>难以置信竟能得见真容。<continue>接下来这段时光必将妙不可言。

Text.en: The texts talk of these altars. In the last days before the fall, they began appearing on the fringes of our Empire. <continue>It was an opportunist, an entity called the 'King in the Mists,' which sought tribute through those altars. <continue>None knew what he wanted, other than the faith of those foolish enough to follow him.
Text.zh: 典籍记载着这些祭坛。帝国倾覆前夕，它们开始出现在疆域边缘。<continue>有个名为"迷雾之王"的投机者，企图通过祭坛索取贡礼。<continue>除了那些愚昧追随者献上的信仰，无人知晓他到底想要什么。

Text.en: I know little of this entity's origin, only that he is described as emerging from nothing. <continue>That's not a metaphor. He comes from a place that is nothing. A void bursting with things that don't exist. <continue>Cuachic's ranking system holds me as the most intelligent among us, but even I can't fathom what that actually means.
Text.zh: 对此存在的起源无从考证，只知典籍描述其为凭空显现。<continue>这并非隐喻，他确实源自真正的"无物之境"。那是充盈着悖论性存在的虚空。<continue>按库阿奇克的排名体系，我虽被视为智力巅峰……但连我也难以参透这究竟意味着什么。

Text.en: The King in the Mists would have conquered us long ago, if he was capable of such. <continue>He is an opportunist, lurking about in times of strife. <continue>Go, attend to him, and bring me his heart. I wish to see his Nameless flesh.
Text.zh: 若那迷雾之王真有这等本事，早该将我们征服了。<continue>他不过是个趁乱而动的投机之徒，专在动荡之时蛰伏窥伺。<continue>去，处理掉他，带回他的心脏。我倒要瞧瞧他那无名血肉究竟是何物。

Text.en: You failed to defeat the King in the Mists? But you are the Demon of Atzoatl! <continue>Ahh, you jest! You're toying with him! Yes, draw out his suffering!
Text.zh: 你竟会败给迷雾之王？你可是艾兹麦的恶魔啊！<continue>哈，你定是在说笑！分明是在戏耍那家伙！没错，延长他的痛苦才更痛快！

Text.en: Where is the heart of that so-called 'King'? You did not bring it? <continue>Ah, perhaps he did not have one. A Nameless, heartless beast. <continue>He is not destroyed, for we are still aware of his existence. He will return... eventually.
Text.zh: 那个所谓的“迷雾之王”的心在何处？你没带来？<continue>呵，或许他本就没有心，是个无名无心的孽物。<continue>他并未彻底消亡，因为我们仍能感知其存在。他终将……卷土重来。

Text.en: Those that give faith to the unknown are fools. <continue>The King in the Mists will use that power to draw ever closer to our existence. <continue>He and his Nameless kind seek to exist, but I say that Wraeclast is already occupied. <continue>This is the land of the Vaal empire that was, and the Vaal empire that is to come. <continue>Other cultures may also live on the fringes... human cultures. That is acceptable.
Text.zh: 那些将信仰托付给无名之辈的，皆是痴人说梦！<continue>迷雾之王正利用这份力量，不断蚕食侵蚀我们的现实存在。<continue>他和那些无名同类妄图存续于世，但我要说——瓦尔克拉斯早有主人。<continue>这片土地属于曾经的瓦尔帝国，更属于即将再临的瓦尔帝国！<continue>其他文明……若甘愿偏安一隅，譬如人类文明，倒也容得下他们的生存。

Text.en: The first generation sealed in the Cuachic Vault passed down stories about you. <continue>I never thought you were real... but here you are, with Master Doryani, no less. <continue>Truly, this is a time of wonders! <continue>We thought you were a curse sent to plague us, but you may be our salvation instead.
Text.zh: 被封印在库阿奇克宝库的第一代人，曾流传过关于您的传说。<continue>我从未想过您竟真实存在……而今，您竟与多里亚尼大师并肩而立。<continue>当真是奇迹涌现的时代！<continue>我们曾视您为降下灾厄的诅咒，谁承想您或许正是我们的救赎之光。

Text.en: In the vault, we had our duties, and little else. I spent my free time reading tomes in the archives. <continue>They contained highly detailed information about Wraeclast's plant life, animal life, and surface cultures. <continue>I thought I knew everything... but knowing is nothing, next to seeing.
Text.zh: 在宝库之中，我们除了履行职责，几乎别无他事。闲暇时我总是在档案库翻阅典籍。<continue>那些典籍有着极为详尽的资料，记载着瓦尔克拉斯的植物生态、动物种群以及地表文明。<continue>我曾以为自己知晓一切……然而百闻不如一见，纸上得来终觉浅。

Text.en: As I understand it, the Vaal were a shining beacon of power that kept the world safe. <continue>They were surrounded by savage cultures that burned, pillaged, and ravaged Wraeclast. <continue>Without us, modern life must have been quite terrifying... <continue>By wiping that all away, the Cataclysm may have been a blessing in disguise.
Text.zh: 据我所知，瓦尔一族曾是守护世界的璀璨灯塔。<continue>周边尽是些焚烧掠夺、肆虐瓦尔克拉斯的野蛮文明。<continue>若无我们守护，现世生活想必会恐怖至极……<continue>而那场灾变将一切夷为平地，或许反倒是因祸得福。

Text.en: A dark force seeks to hound my dreams. <continue>I welcome it. The principles of Cuachic have been on our walls since the first generation. <continue>To endure is to suffer. To suffer is to triumph. The spirit of the Vaal will not cower from nightmares.
Text.zh: 有一股黑暗力量企图侵扰我的梦境。<continue>我正求之不得。自初个时代起，库阿奇克的训诫便铭刻于我们的石壁之上，一脉相承。<continue>忍耐即苦修，苦修即凯旋。瓦尔之魂岂会畏缩于噩梦之中，破晓终将降临。

Text.en: We did not have access to the Trial in the Cuachic Vault. <continue>We entreated the favour of Chaos several times over the centuries, but they were not interested. <continue>There were very few possibilities in the Vault. <continue>All of us were dedicated and disciplined to our cause, so there was no infighting. No... dramatic turns. <continue>Boring to watch, I imagine.
Text.zh: 在库阿奇克宝库中，我们无缘经历试炼的考验。<continue>数个世纪依赖，我们曾数次祈求混沌之神的垂青，可他始终不发一言。<continue>宝库之中可能性寥寥。<continue>众人皆恪守誓言，为共同使命恪尽职守，从无内讧纷争，更无……波澜起伏。<continue>想来参观者必觉乏善可陈。

Text.en: I am intrigued by Alva's existence. We were sent to the vault to act as a failsafe for the Vaal, should they be wiped out. <continue>Her bloodline proves that at least some of us survived on the outside... <continue>Perhaps, despite all of Master Doryani's efforts, the best plan was simply to have faith... <continue>... and let the spirit of our people win through.
Text.zh: 阿尔瓦的存在令我深思。我们被送入宝库，本是为瓦尔文明保留最后的火种，以防他们遭遇灭顶之灾。<continue>她的血脉印证了至少仍有族人在外界存续……<continue>或许，纵使多里亚尼大师穷尽心血，最好的计划仍是秉持信念……<continue>……静待我们民族的精神星火燎原。

Text.en: The Maraketh were not so bad, according to our tomes. They were militaristic and headstrong. <continue>Criminals were tattooed and cast out for a time. The intention was good, but the implementation was wasteful. <continue>They should have been using all that blood - and those organs - for more useful ends. <continue>In Cuachic, we used everything we possibly could. Nothing discarded.
Text.zh: 据典籍所载，马拉克斯人倒也不算恶劣。他们尚武而顽固。<continue>他们会将罪犯刺青后暂时流放。本意虽好，实施方式却完全是浪费。<continue>他们早该将那些血液与器官物尽其用方为上策。<continue>在库阿奇克，我们竭尽所能利用万物，绝不浪费分毫。

Text.en: A few tomes described the Karui as rabid diseased animals that ate each other and fought for dominance. <continue>I don't know if I believe that, though. One account was very different. It was a diary. <continue>A noblewoman fell in love with a Karui man from the Rongokurai tribe. <continue>She described them as a culture of poetic warriors with intensely strong communal bonds. <continue>A biased source, clearly, but a strong note of dissension among the texts.
Text.zh: 据典籍所述，卡鲁人如同染疾疯犬般互相撕咬，争夺霸权。<continue>我对此表示怀疑，某本日记中记载了截然不同的景象：<continue>一位贵族女子与龙戈库莱部落的卡鲁男子相恋，<continue>她笔下的卡鲁人是充满诗意的战士民族，有着血脉共鸣般的强韧羁绊。<continue>虽显偏颇，却是典籍中不容忽视的异见之声。

Text.en: This may be subjective, but you don't look like an animal to me.
Text.zh: 这话可能主观了些，但在我看来，你可半点也不像动物。

Text.en: I've never read anything about these 'Ezomytes,' as you call them. <continue>The system of runes you describe is also unfamiliar to me. <continue>To be quite honest, the concept of 'runes' sounds like a jest... <continue>... and I am not your fool to toy with.
Text.zh: 我从未读过关于这些你所谓的"艾兹麦人"的记载。<continue>你所描述的符文体系对我而言同样闻所未闻。<continue>坦白说，"符文"这概念听来简直是无稽之谈……<continue>……我可不是任你摆弄的蠢人。

Text.en: Trarthus is now home to a culture of exiles from the Eternal Empire? <continue>I've never heard of this 'Eternal Empire.' <continue>They sound like upstarts who took advantage of our absence. <continue>In our era, Trarthus was inhabited by scattered tribes that originally came from the south. <continue>They found the slightly warmer climate to their liking... that, and the geological vapours. <continue>They were described as quite intoxicating.
Text.zh: 如今特拉特斯成了永恒帝国流放者的聚居地？<continue>我从未听过什么"永恒帝国"。 <continue>听来像是趁我们不在时崛起的暴发户。 <continue>在我们那个时代，特拉特斯住着从南方迁徙而来的零星部落。 <continue>他们觉得此地气候平和温暖，颇合心意……还有那些地质蒸汽。 <continue>据记载，那些蒸汽颇为令人沉醉呢。

Text.en: The Azmeri hold the oldest wisdom. <continue>They were already in their mountains when the first Vaal was born from Xibaqua's remains. <continue>They will likely still be up there, even now, after this Cataclysm. <continue>Troubled, but alive.
Text.zh: 阿兹莫里人掌握着最古老的智慧。<continue>当第一位瓦尔人刚从夏巴夸亚遗骸中诞生时，他们早已盘踞群山之巅。<continue>即便是历经灾变后的今日，他们很可能仍屹立于高山之上。<continue>虽遭困厄，犹存于世。

Text.en: Oriath? Hmm... <continue>Ah, a derivative of the original name for the island the Golden Cult called home. <continue>They split off from the Azmeri during a difficult time in history. <continue>There was a time of famine, fire, and despair... and the gods rose from the ashes. <continue>Or so they say. The only god I've ever seen, supposedly, is lying over there doing nothing to aid us.
Text.zh: 奥瑞亚？嗯……<continue>啊，这名字衍生于黄金教派所居岛屿的古称。<continue>他们在历史上某个艰难时期从阿兹莫里分裂而出。<continue>那时饥荒肆虐，烈火焚世，绝望蔓延……而神祇自灰烬中崛起。<continue>至少传说是如此。至于我唯一见过的所谓神祇，此刻正瘫倒在那边，对我们无动于衷。

Text.en: The histories are confusing when it comes to the culture I believe you're asking about. <continue>Uzaza, Aul, Putembo... these names are treated more like myths than historical facts. <continue>Over the years, I've spent a few of our excess newborns trying to scry for dreams about them. <continue>So far, I've seen nothing useful.
Text.zh: 据史料记载，您所询问的文明实在令人困惑。<continue>乌扎萨、奥尔、普藤博……这些名字更像是神话传说而非史实。<continue>这些年来，我甚至耗费过几个过剩的新生儿，试图通过窥梦之术探寻他们的踪迹。<continue>可惜至今仍未获得任何有价值的线索。

Text.en: The tomes in the archives only go back to the time of worldwide fire and famine. <continue>Even then, it's only conjecture and speculation. It was already ancient history to the Vaal. <continue>You'd have to ask the Azmeri. They are the only culture that was in-tact before and after the era of despair.
Text.zh: 档案中的典籍只能追溯到全球火灾和饥荒的时代。<continue>即便如此，也只是猜测和推测。对瓦尔人来说，这已经是古代史了。<continue>你得去问阿兹美里人。他们是绝望时代前后唯一完整延续下来的文明。

Text.en: Not much to say about that lot. Other than... <continue>They're a bunch of nutters. <continue>And to think what they've been up to all this time...
Text.zh: 要我说啊，那帮人啊……<continue>纯粹是一群神经病。<continue>想想他们这些年来搞的鬼名堂……

Text.en: I've heard tell of this place. To see it with me own eyes... <continue>Blimey. I'd count myself a lucky man. <continue>More than happy to take ya there!
Text.zh: 那地方的传闻我都听腻了！今天总算能亲眼见识以下……<continue>好家伙！真是上辈子修来的福气！<continue>给你带路那必须乐意之至啊！

Text.en: You met the Karui Mother of Death? <continue>I don't think I wanna hear any more about that. <continue>Consider me right spooked.
Text.zh: 你见过卡鲁族的死亡之母？<continue>我可不敢再听下去了……<continue>我现在鸡皮疙瘩都掉了一地！

Text.en: I stay well away from that lot. The kinfolk. They're bloody well rabid for whatever's in that mountain. <continue>... guess you wanna take a look, right?
Text.zh: 那帮家伙我可躲得远远的！那些个亲眷族啊，对山里那点邪门东西疯魔似的着迷……<continue>……我猜你肯定想去开开眼，对吧？

Text.en: So it wasn't all blunder, eh? There was a giant in the mountain! And you took it down! <continue>Well, I'll be damned...
Text.zh: 所以不是胡说的啊？山里真藏着个巨人！还让你给收拾了！<continue>好家伙，真是吓人！

Text.en: That fog is the cause of a lot of problems. <continue>Let me tell you, every time I get near it... I start seeing things. <continue>I'd really rather not go there!
Text.zh: 那团迷雾太麻烦了！<continue>跟你说实在的，每回靠近那迷雾……我就开始0见鬼了！<continue>打死都不想去那鬼地方！

Text.en: Well... the fog is gone. But... you let out some fearsome thing. <continue>Win some, lose some...  I guess. <continue>I wonder what might be left over from all the crashed ships there... hmm...
Text.zh: 雾是散了……可你放出了不得了的玩意儿啊。<continue>有得有失是吧……<continue>不过那堆沉船里说不定能摸出点好东西……嗯……

Text.en: Tavakai fled there with Makoru. <continue>I still can't believe all that's happened. <continue>I knew he was a difficult fella, but this... <continue>He's lost his Way.
Text.zh: 塔瓦凯带着马科鲁逃往那里了。<continue>我至今还觉得难以置信……<continue>我早就知道他不好说话，没想到竟然到了这种地步。<continue>他偏离了自己的卡鲁之道。

Text.en: Ahh... what've we got here then? <continue>I got no idea about this location. <continue>Guess we could take a look? <continue>But you're splittin' whatever you find with me, alright?
Text.zh: 啊……这儿藏着什么宝贝呢？<continue>这地方我可一点儿也不熟悉……<continue>要不进去看看？<continue>不过见者有份！淘着好东西可得分我一份儿，不许赖账！

Text.en: Nothin' much to see there no more. Got the treasure. Got out. <continue>Can go back if you like... I guess.
Text.zh: 早掏空啦！带上宝贝，赶快撤吧。<continue>你要是想回去，那就随便你了。

Text.en: Oh come on now... we needn't go there. <continue>Can smell death as soon as you near the shore! <continue>And the moanin' and yellin'... look. It's no good. Let's go somewhere else!
Text.zh: 真是求求你了……能别去那鬼地方吗！<continue>刚靠近岸边就闻到冲天死气！<continue>又是哀嚎又是尖叫的……听我句劝，这地方没好！咱们换个地方吧！

Text.en: So you ran through that damned place and killed something... unkillable? <continue>You're gonna give me bloody nightmares at this rate!
Text.zh: 所以你真闯过了那鬼地方，还宰了个……杀不死的玩意儿？<continue>照这么下去，我非得做三天噩梦不可！

Text.en: As much as I respect Karui culture, that place... it's not for me. <continue>The shrikes that nest there are right bloody fearsome. <continue>I can take a seagull or two, but them... they'll take your bloody head off!
Text.zh: 我尊重卡鲁文化，但那鬼地方……我真受不了！<continue>在那儿栖息的伯劳鸟凶得很！<continue>海鸥我还能对付，那玩意儿……可是真要啄人脑袋的！

Text.en: Hard one to wrap your lips around innit? <continue>At first it feels like yer cursin'! But... it has quite a beautiful meaning in the Karui tongue.
Text.zh: 太拗口了对吧？<continue>刚学这个词的时候，听起来就像骂脏话！不过呢……在卡鲁语里其实藏着段诗意的典故。

Text.en: So, you've got a piece of land for yourself and Ange, eh? <continue>Good on ya. I've got half a mind to set up shop myself one of these days. <continue>Rhodri's Soup Shop! Haha! Just kiddin'. You know I can't bloody cook.
Text.zh: 你和安吉总算有自己的地盘啦？<continue>真不赖！改天我也琢磨整个铺面。<continue>"罗德里浓汤馆"！哈哈！逗你的。你知道我煮个开水都能糊锅！

Text.en: There was a siren in the caves of Whakapanu Island? Well I'll be... you sure pack a wallop, don't ya? <continue>It's a lovely place. I look forward to stopping by sometime.
Text.zh: 瓦卡帕努岛洞穴里还藏着海妖？天啊……你可真有两把刷子！<continue>那地方确实很漂亮，改天我也想去溜达溜达。

Text.en: The trail of Corruption leads east, so east we shall go. It seems fate conspires to return me to the Plains of Vastiri...
Text.zh: 腐化的痕迹在向东蔓延，我等便向东行进。命运之手正将我推往瓦斯提里平原。

Text.en: Yes, we can take you to the edge of the desert. Then, we will continue to track the Beast. You <i>{must} find a way to contain it. When we find out where it is being taken, we will seek you out. <continue>Until then, may the winds ever be calm on your path.
Text.zh: 是的，我们会护送你到沙漠边缘。而后，我们将继续追踪那头邪兽。<continue>你<i>{务必}找到困住它的方法。等我们查出他被运往哪里，我们会去找你。<continue>那时再见吧，愿风沙止息，前路顺遂。

Text.en: Hello again! I... I mean, that is to say, my husband's told me all about you... you're the local that caused such a ruckus! <continue>I'm Gwennen, his luckier half. And his more exhausted half. <continue>Let's put you to work then, shall we?
Text.zh: 你好！我……我想说的是，我丈夫把你的事都告诉了我……听说你就是那位闹得满城风雨的家伙！<continue>我是桂温妮，他走了好运的妻子。同时也是累得够呛的妻子。<continue>那我们就开工吧，怎么样？

Text.en: When you've been on a quest as long as I have, you tend to wind down a bit. <continue>Sometimes, I get the whole gang back together for a run or two. <continue>But mostly, it's just me... over and over...
Text.zh: 像我这样常年在外的人，难免会想放松一下。<continue>偶尔也会召集老友们再闯荡一两回。<continue>但大多时候，我都是孤身一人……一次又一次。

Text.en: Yet another Cataclysm on Wraeclast. <continue>What happened this time? You didn't reach Oriath soon enough? <continue>Hmm... when exactly did it happen? The ship had just gotten to the harbour... alright... <continue>Tell me everything. I'll memorise every detail. For... posterity. And I'll have Alva write it down.
Text.zh: 瓦尔克拉斯有一次面临灾变的威胁。<continue>这次又是为什么？你没能及时抵达奥瑞亚？<continue>嗯……什么时候的事？你的船才刚进港……好吧……<continue>跟我说说吧，我会记下每个细节，为后世……留下证据。我会让阿尔瓦做好记录。

Text.en: Her pluckiness really gets on a persons nerves over the years... <continue>Or so I assume. That it would. Eventually.
Text.zh: 再过些年，她那莽撞劲儿早晚会让人受不了……<continue>我是这么猜的，肯定会，早晚的。

Text.en: He and the children are why... I do what I do. <continue>And I'll never give up, even if it all seems hopeless.
Text.zh: 我这么做的原因……是他和孩子们。<continue>就算一切看似无望，我也不会放弃。

Text.en: The relics of our ancestors are where they're supposed to be, at least... <continue>Which is to say, scattered all over? <continue>Ugh, this job is pure chaos.
Text.zh: 我们的先祖遗物都还待在该待的地方……<continue>说白了就是散落在各地？<continue>唉，这差事简直一团糟。

Text.en: Chaos? Why would you ask me about <i>{them?}
Text.zh: 混沌？你为什么要问我关于<i>{混沌}的事？

Text.en: I'm glad to see another living soul...
Text.zh: 看到还有人活着真是太好了……

Text.en: Yes, I'm alive... at least for now. What has happened here?
Text.zh: 是的，我还活着……至少目前是。这里怎么了？

Text.en: Oh, dear... you've one foot in the grave. What happened to you?
Text.zh: 哦，天哪……你看着都快入土了……这是怎么回事？

Text.en: Your leg... what took place here?
Text.zh: 你的腿……这儿发生了什么？

Text.en: You're wounded. What happened?
Text.zh: 你受伤了。怎么回事？

Text.en: You look unwell, friend. What happened?
Text.zh: 你看着不太好，朋友。出什么事了？

Text.en: I live. But you are bleeding out. What happened here?
Text.zh: 我还活着。但你在流血。这里怎么了？

Text.en: Yeah. I'm alright. But you on the other hand... What got ya, mate?
Text.zh: 嗯。我没事。可是你有事……你怎么受伤了，哥们？

Text.en: Och... I've got problems of my own to tend.
Text.zh: 唉……我自个儿还有烂摊子要收拾。

Text.en: W-what? You'd just leave me to die?
Text.zh: 什……什么？你就想丢下我等死？

Text.en: Nay, lad. Now speak - what befell ye?
Text.zh: 傻小子，现在老实交代——你遭了什么灾？

Text.en: Oh, First Ones bless you.
Text.zh: 噢……愿初生之子庇佑你。

Text.en: The Miller went off to warn Clearfell 'bout a strange <i>{sickness}, plaguing our men. <continue>I was fixin' to go after him, until that <i>{bloody} thing got to me first. <continue>Now, all those who drowned in the floodin'... suddenly they ain't so dead no more.
Text.zh: 米勒去了克里费尔，想警告他们一种诡异的 <i>{瘟疫}在蔓延。<continue>我正打算去找他，结果那个 <i>{该死}的东西先盯上了我。<continue>现在，所有在洪水中淹死的人……突然间，他们都复活了。

Text.en: [Text Pass] Good luck out there. If you get that sea gate open, I'll be there, fighting the good fight.
Text.zh: 祝你们好运。只要你们打开海闸，我就会赶到，跟你们并肩作战。

Text.en: [Text Pass] We'll make our ancestors proud.
Text.zh: 我们会让先祖为之骄傲。

Text.en: [Text Pass] That we will. Maybe our songs will share a few stanzas.
Text.zh: 一定会的。也许歌谣里会有我们共同的篇章。

Text.en: [Text Pass] Don't use a sword. It'll be far deadlier to feed them your cabbage stew instead.
Text.zh: 别用剑。把你的卷心菜炖汤喂给他们，比啥都致命。

Text.en: [Text Pass] Ha! I'll miss your sense of humour.
Text.zh: 哈！我会怀念你的幽默感的。

Text.en: [Text Pass] I wasn't joking. I want to win this thing.
Text.zh: 我没开玩笑。我想赢下这一仗。

Text.en: [Text Pass] I... suppose I'll get cooking, then.
Text.zh: 我……看来我得去下厨了。

Text.en: [Text Pass] It is my honour to fight side-by-side with Kalguurans.
Text.zh: 能与卡古兰人并肩作战，是我的荣幸。

Text.en: [Text Pass] And mine, to fight alongside the Maraketh.
Text.zh: 与我而言，能与马拉克斯人并肩，亦是荣幸。

Text.en: [Text Pass] Watch Makoru's back, won't you?
Text.zh: 保护好马克鲁，好吗？

Text.en: [Text Pass] You know I will.
Text.zh: 你知道我会的。

Text.en: [Text Pass] I'll fight twice as hard knowing this crew'll be there.
Text.zh: 知道有你们这帮伙计在，我会加倍奋勇作战。

Text.en: [Text Pass] Indeed. You'll always be one of us.
Text.zh: 当然。你永远是我们的一员。

Text.en: [Text Pass] Our paths will meet again. I'm certain.
Text.zh: 我们定会重逢。我深信不疑。

Text.en: [Text Pass] Someone's got to sail you out of here when's all said and done, aye?
Text.zh: 等这一切结束了，总得有人开船送你们离开，对吧？

Text.en: [Text Pass] Once we're in there, grab whatever you can. Idols. Jewels. If it ain't nailed down, load it up.
Text.zh: 一旦我们进去，能拿什么就拿什么。神像、珠宝。只要没钉在地上的，全都搬走。

Text.en: [Text Pass] Heh. You can count on it. I plan to retire after this.
Text.zh: 嘿。包在我身上。我打算干完这票就退休。

Text.en: [Text Pass] Don't jinx it, mate!
Text.zh: 别说这种不吉利的话，哥们！

Text.en: [Text Pass] Hopefully, this will all be over soon.
Text.zh: 希望这一切能尽快结束。

Text.en: [Text Pass] A man can dream.
Text.zh: 人总得有梦想嘛。

Text.en: This is where it all began... Lira Vaal. Atziri's royal court. <continue>The death of my people...
Text.zh: 此地便是一切的起点……利拉瓦尔，阿兹里的王宫。<continue>我族覆灭的根源……

Text.en: Yes. Well... we've been, ah, <i>{exploring} the central temple... very respectfully, of course! It's still in pretty good shape...
Text.zh: 没错。我们一直在，呃，<i>{探索}中央神庙……自然是怀着万分敬畏之心！它保存得相当完好……

Text.en: I believed all this would be destroyed... <continue>Intriguing. <continue>Hmm... I wonder... <continue>Configure the temple. When you next open it, I am going to attempt something.
Text.zh: 我曾深信这一切都将被摧毁……<continue>真有趣。<continue>嗯……我在想……<continue>去配置神庙吧。待你再次开启它时，我将尝试做些什么。

Text.en: Yes... through that gateway lies the royal temple, on the day of the Cataclysm.
Text.zh: 正是……穿过那道传送门，便是灾变当日的皇家神庙。

Text.en: You intend to slay your own Queen, don't you?
Text.zh: 你打算弑杀自己的女王，对吗？

Text.en: Would you not do whatever it takes to save your own?
Text.zh: 你难道不会不惜一切代价拯救自己的族人吗？

Text.en: Yes. And I did. What do you suggest?
Text.zh: 会。而且我做过。你有什么想说的？

Text.en: So there'll be two of me running around the Vaal empire? One here, one in Utzaal?
Text.zh: 所以会有两个我同时活跃在瓦尔帝国？一个在此处，一个在乌札尔？

Text.en: Yes... make sure not to meet yourself.
Text.zh: 没错……切记避开另一个自己。

Text.en: Probably for the best. I doubt we'd get along. What's the plan, then?
Text.zh: 这样最好。想必我们处不来。具体计划是？

Text.en: It'll be like entering a tale... but can the ending be changed?
Text.zh: 这如同踏入传说故事……但结局能否被改写？

Text.en: I do not know. But we have to try.
Text.zh: 我不得而知。但我们必须尝试。

Text.en: Do you believe we can change what happened?
Text.zh: 你相信我们能改变既定的过去吗？

Text.en: I do not know. Truly... but you, tale-woman, you have a special relationship with the element Time, do you not?
Text.zh: 我不得而知。但说书人你啊……与时间元素有着特殊的羁绊，不是吗？

Text.en: Yes... there may be a chance. I will do my best.
Text.zh: 是的……或许存在一线希望。我自当尽力而为。

Text.en: And I suppose you'll be wantin' me to dish out a bit o' violence?
Text.zh: 我猜你又需要我施展些“物理说服”的手段了？

Text.en: I'd be disappointed if you didn't.
Text.zh: 你要是不出手，我反倒要失望了。

Text.en: Heh. What's the plan?
Text.zh: 呵。具体计划是？

Text.en: Is this wise? I know what you're thinking.
Text.zh: 这么做真的明智吗？我知道你打算做什么。

Text.en: Wise, perhaps not. Necessary... yes.
Text.zh: 无关明智……而是势在必行。

Text.en: This seems like a bad idea.
Text.zh: 这似乎是个昏招。

Text.en: Yes, it is. It's a very bad idea.
Text.zh: 确实。是个糟糕透顶的主意。

Text.en: Just so long as we're clear. Now, what do you have in mind?
Text.zh: 只要我们都清楚这一点就好。现在你有什么想法？

Text.en: Tryin' to get me killed, are ya?
Text.zh: 存心想让我送命是吧？

Text.en: Not killed – I mean to change what happened that day. If such a thing is even possible.
Text.zh: 非是送命——我要扭转那日的结局。倘若时空法则可以更改的话。

Text.en: Fine. Tell me what needs doin'.
Text.zh: 行。告诉我该做什么。

Text.en: If we can stop Atziri... could other things be changed?
Text.zh: 若能阻止阿兹里……是否其他悲剧也能被改写？

Text.en: If we manage to defy the rules of existence once... twice seems just as likely.
Text.zh: 如果我们这次能成功挑战存在法则……那再来一次也未必不可。

Text.en: Then tell me what needs doin'.
Text.zh: 那就告诉我该怎么做。

Text.en: Enter the gateway. Find Xipocado. He is the only one who can give us access to the Queen's chambers. <continue>If he is not willing, then bring me his personal blood medallion. It may suffice. <continue>Everything I know as a man of science tells me the past cannot be changed... but I am still asking you to try. <continue>Not for me, but for the millions of lives at stake.
Text.zh: 穿过传送门，找到夏波卡多。唯有他能让我们进入女王的寝宫。<continue>若他不愿配合……便将他的鲜血纹章带给我。或许能派上用场。<continue>身为科学工作者，我所有的认知都表明过去不可更改……但我仍要恳请诸位尝试。<continue>并非为我，而是为那数百万悬于一线的生命。

Text.en: We'll do our best. In the meantime, we can get our hands on Atziri's considerable assets!
Text.zh: 我们自当尽力。趁这机会，正好把阿兹里那些惊人的宝藏弄到手！

Text.en: <i>{Excuse me?}
Text.zh: <i>{你说什么？}

Text.en: Her legendary treasure vaults!
Text.zh: 她那些传奇藏宝库！

Text.en: Oh... of course. Do as you wish in that regard. It hardly matters now.
Text.zh: 哦……当然了。这方面诸位随意。眼下这些已不重要了。

Text.en: That's what I like to hear. Let's go!
Text.zh: 就等这句话呢。出发！

Text.en: Hello, can you hear me?
Text.zh: 能听见我说话吗？

Text.en: Yes, yes. Go on.
Text.zh: 能，请讲。

Text.en: Yes, I can hear you.
Text.zh: 可以听到。

Text.en: I hear you.
Text.zh: 听得见。

Text.en: Nope. <continue>Haha. Just messing with ya.
Text.zh: 听不见。<continue>哈哈，逗你玩的。

Text.en: I can hear ye, somehow.
Text.zh: 不知怎地，竟能听见你的声音。

Text.en: Somehow, I do.
Text.zh: 不知怎的，我能听见。

Text.en: I do. Where are you?
Text.zh: 能听见。你在何处？

Text.en: Aye. I hear ya.
Text.zh: 嗯。我听见了。

Text.en: My voice is being relayed. Good. <continue>What became of Xipocado?
Text.zh: 我的声音传过去了。很好。<continue>夏波卡多现在如何？

Text.en: His soul belongs to me now.
Text.zh: 他的灵魂已归我所有。

Text.en: He has been slain.
Text.zh: 他已被诛杀。

Text.en: I was forced to slay him.
Text.zh: 我不得已出手了结了他。

Text.en: He's done for.
Text.zh: 他已经没气了。

Text.en: Buried by his own folly.
Text.zh: 他自取灭亡。

Text.en: He was... uncooperative.
Text.zh: 他始终……拒不配合。

Text.en: He remained loyal to the end.
Text.zh: 他坚持效忠直至最后一刻。

Text.en: Apologies. He's dead.
Text.zh: 很遗憾，他已身亡。

Text.en: A waste of a great mind, but... he made his choice. <continue>That bastard... he anticipated this, and sealed the Royal Access Chamber designs.  <continue>Without it, we cannot reach Atziri... <continue>Do as you wish with the temple and the console for now. I will work to undo his machination... <continue>Though it may take some time.
Text.zh: 可惜了一位英才……但他做出了自己的选择。<continue>那混蛋……他预判了这一步，把皇家谒见厅的设计图封印了。<continue>没有图纸，我们无法接近阿兹里……<continue>神庙和控制台暂时随你使用。我会想办法破解他的诡计……<continue>不过可能需要点时间。

Text.en: I've done it! We now have full access. <continue>Use Xipocado's console to open a path to the Royal Access Chamber. <continue>From there, we will be able to gain entry to Atziri's private chambers on our next attempt.
Text.zh: 成功了！如今前路已畅通无阻。<continue>使用夏波卡多的控制台开启通往皇家谒见厅的通道。<continue>从那里开始，我们下次尝试时便能进入阿兹里的私人寝宫。

Text.en: Good... I can see the configuration at this end. It is done. <continue>Return to the present. For now, there's no more to accomplish there.
Text.zh: 很好……我这边收到系统反馈，路径已经开始。<continue>先返回现在的时间点。此地暂时没有其他任务了。

Text.en: What are you doing? You didn't open a path to the Royal Access Chamber! <continue>You're wasting precious time!
Text.zh: 你在做什么？你根本没有打开通往皇家通廊的通道！<continue>你是在浪费宝贵的时间！

Text.en: You slew Xipocado... but you didn't use his medallion to open a path to the Royal Access Chamber?
Text.zh: 你们解决了夏波卡多……却没用他的鲜血纹章开启通往皇家通廊的通道？

Text.en: Oh, dear.
Text.zh: 噢，糟糕。

Text.en: We'll have to do this all again... and this time, don't forget the <i>{entire point of attacking the temple!}
Text.zh: 我们得重来一遍了……这次可别忘了<i>{攻打神庙的核心目标！}

Text.en: She is within our grasp! <continue>Next time you activate the temple, make your way to the Royal Access Chamber. <continue>We will meet you there.
Text.zh: 她已近在咫尺！<continue>下次激活神庙时，务必前往皇家通廊。<continue>我们将在那儿与你们会合。

Text.en: I must teach someone what I know, in case I die in the attempt. <continue>You managed to activate the Gateway at the Temple of Kopec... you are an acceptable understudy.
Text.zh: 若我在此次行动中死去，总要有人传承我的知识。<continue>你们既然能激活科佩克之殿的传送门……倒也算合格的继任者。

Text.en: That was almost a compliment! You must be serious about this.
Text.zh: 你这算是夸奖了吧！看来你是认真的。

Text.en: There is nothing in this world I take more seriously.
Text.zh: 世间再无他事会让我如此郑重以待。

Text.en: Alright then. I'll do it. <i>{We} will meet you at the Royal Access Chamber.
Text.zh: 好吧。我答应你。<i>{我们}会在皇家通廊与你碰面。

Text.en: Xipocado's medallion is the key to this, but he built this locking mechanism himself, so I am unsure how to open it.
Text.zh: 夏波卡多的鲜血纹章是关键所在，但锁具由他亲手打造，开启方法恕我无从得知。

Text.en: Hmm, this tome has drawings that look just like this room.
Text.zh: 嗯，这古籍里的图画看起来就是这个房间。

Text.en: Tome? What tome?
Text.zh: 古籍？什么古籍？

Text.en: This one! I... found it in our explorations.
Text.zh: 这本！我……在探索中偶然寻得。

Text.en: That's the Eztli Pilli! How do you have that?
Text.zh: 这是《埃兹特利皮利》！你从哪儿找到的？

Text.en: It's forbidden knowledge... everything in there is too volatile or dangerous to use!
Text.zh: 这可是禁忌之书……其中记载皆是不稳定的凶险法术！

Text.en: It's a bit late to worry about that, isn't it?
Text.zh: 现在才担心这个，是不是有点太迟了？

Text.en: When fortune smiles, shake her hand, I always say.
Text.zh: 我常说：机缘既至，自当牢牢把握。

Text.en: Fine. Use it we shall. What does it say?
Text.zh: 罢了。那便依书行事。书上怎么写的？

Text.en: It seems two High Priests must give a sacrifice of living blood.
Text.zh: 嗯……需要两位高阶祭司献上活血为祭。

Text.en: The medallion should then unlock the royal chambers.
Text.zh: 鲜血纹章随后将开启皇家谒见厅。

Text.en: Step forward with me. I am possessed by a peculiar notion.
Text.zh: 请与我一起上前。此刻有种古怪的直觉驱使着我。

Text.en: It worked! But why?
Text.zh: 成了！但这是何原理？

Text.en: If I survive this, we'll speak on it later. Now, we must face my Queen.
Text.zh: 若我此番能幸存，日后再与你探讨。现在……该直面我的女王了。

Text.en: Understood. Good luck, you two!
Text.zh: 明白。祝二位好运！

Text.en: These are my mechanisms... my creations. Xipocado must have had spies amongst my workshops. <continue>Perhaps that is why my Triumph failed to defeat you... <continue>They must have sabotaged it! Yes, in retrospect, that is the only thing that makes sense...
Text.zh: 这些都是我的机关……我的心血之作。夏波卡多肯定在我工坊里安插了内鬼。<continue>说不定这就是我的“杰作”没能干掉你的原因……<continue>他们绝对动了手脚！没错，回头想想，只有这个解释说得通……

Text.en: Of course. The only thing.
Text.zh: 当然。只有这个解释。

Text.en: Ha! You <i>{must} be joking.
Text.zh: 哈！你<i>{绝对}是在说笑。

Text.en: When he has nothing else, a man must still have his pride.
Text.zh: 当人一无所有时，总得留住尊严。

Text.en: I've... been there. You keep your pride. It's the only thing that no one can take from you.
Text.zh: 我……也有过这种经历。尽管守住你的尊严吧，这是谁都无法夺走的东西。

Text.en: Then you should rebuild it, and we should have a fair rematch.
Text.zh: 那你该重造那机关，我们改日再来公平对决。

Text.en: A rematch? Ah... perhaps... once all this business is over with.
Text.zh: 改日再来？呵……或许……等这一切了结之后。

Text.en: I would never have let any machine stop me.
Text.zh: 我从来不会被任何机械造物阻挡。

Text.en: After seeing your resolve... I believe you.
Text.zh: 我看到你的决心了……我相信你。

Text.en: Too bad it's over and done with now.
Text.zh: 可惜现在都已尘埃落定。

Text.en: Yes... a shame.
Text.zh: 是啊……真是遗憾。

Text.en: Yeah, I barely had ya. If it hadn't been sabotaged, I'da been cooked for sure.
Text.zh: 嗯，我压根没打赢你。若它没被动过手脚，我早被烤熟了。

Text.en: Do not mock me.
Text.zh: 休要嘲弄我。

Text.en: What, I'm tryin' to be polite!
Text.zh: 怎的？我这是在跟你客套！

Text.en: And I appreciate it.
Text.zh: 心领了。

Text.en: Hmm. Yes. Surely.
Text.zh: 哼。对。好吧。

Text.en: Say no more.
Text.zh: 不必多言。

Text.en: Could you really have won? Time and history had already been decided.
Text.zh: 你真的会赢吗？时间与历史早已注定。

Text.en: Hmm... true... it was a doomed battle from the start.
Text.zh: 嗯……确实……从一开始就是场必败之战。

Text.en: Look around you. This is the height of progress. <continue>Our civilisation was built on the channeling and control of Corruption, and nowhere was it mastered so perfectly as this chamber.
Text.zh: 看看你的周围。这才是文明的巅峰。<continue>我们的文明建立在驾驭与控制腐化的基础上，这一技艺正是在这间密室臻至化境。

Text.en: I've only ever seen Corruption <i>{ruin} good weapons.
Text.zh: 我只见过腐化<i>{毁掉}好端端的武器。

Text.en: Perhaps it's more art than science.
Text.zh: 或许这更像是艺术而非科学。

Text.en: What did that entail, exactly?
Text.zh: 具体是指什么？

Text.en: We delved into the very secrets of existence here, and even began altering reality itself, in a limited fashion...
Text.zh: 我们在此钻研存在本身的奥秘，甚至能以有限的方式篡改现实……

Text.en: That sounds... oddly familiar.
Text.zh: 听起来……莫名耳熟。

Text.en: Hmmm... like the Precursors themselves, perhaps we were already on a doomed path...
Text.zh: 嗯……就像先驱那样，或许我们早已走上了一条不归路……

Text.en: The Maraketh are honour-bound to seek out and destroy Corruption such as this.
Text.zh: 马拉克斯人以荣耀立誓，必将追猎并清除这种腐化。

Text.en: Perhaps you should think carefully before you reject the power Corruption offers.
Text.zh: 在拒绝腐化的力量之前，你或许应该先想清楚。

Text.en: I said 'the Maraketh.' I left before I took the oath.
Text.zh: 我说的是“马拉克斯人”。我立誓前就离开了。

Text.en: In such a place, I can understand how such power seduced your people.
Text.zh: 在此地，我能理解这种力量如何蛊惑了你的族人。

Text.en: It is as beautiful as it is dangerous.
Text.zh: 它既危险又迷人。

Text.en: Lotta <i>{rathin}, all in one place. Gives me shivers. <continue>Spirit once cut me off just for usin' a gem.
Text.zh: 这么多<i>{腐化}挤在一块儿，看得我发怵。<continue>上次用了颗宝石就被万灵切断了连接。

Text.en: Best not touch anything in here, then.
Text.zh: 那最好别碰这里的任何东西。

Text.en: Is not very impressive. Quite dim in here.
Text.zh: 不怎么样，屋里暗得很。

Text.en: You don't see the intense red light?
Text.zh: 你看不见这刺眼的红光？

Text.en: Looks grey to me.
Text.zh: 我瞅着全是灰的。

Text.en: I never know if you're being serious or not.
Text.zh: 我永远分不清你是不是在说笑。

Text.en: Dead serious, mate. Can't see shite.
Text.zh: 我很认真，兄弟，屁都看不见。

Text.en: So red... and probably green... wait, how do you know which gems are which?
Text.zh: 红得发亮……应该还有点绿……等等你怎么分得清宝石种类？

Text.en: Gems have colours?!
Text.zh: 宝石还有颜色？！

Text.en: Your people truly were impressive.
Text.zh: 你的族人确实了不起。

Text.en: There's no telling what we could have achieved, had we more time.
Text.zh: 若时间充裕，我们的成就难以估量。

Text.en: The height of progress... the deepest perversion of the natural order.
Text.zh: 这是文明的巅峰……也是对自然秩序最彻底的亵渎。

Text.en: And what did serving the natural order get you and your kind?
Text.zh: 敢问侍奉自然秩序又让你和你的族人得到了什么？

Text.en: You best watch yourself.
Text.zh: 你最好当心点。

Text.en: You know what this was, don't you? <continue>Another prototype Solar Nexus. My attempt to steer our civilisation away from reliance on Corruption.
Text.zh: 你清楚这是什么吧？<continue>又一个烈日核心原型机。我曾想借此让文明摆脱对腐化的依赖。

Text.en: You sought to turn from Corruption?
Text.zh: 你曾试图摆脱腐化？

Text.en: Steer<i>{away} from Corruption? <i>{You?}
Text.zh: 让文明<i>{远离}腐化？<i>{你？}

Text.en: Of course.
Text.zh: 当然。

Text.en: That is... surprising.
Text.zh: 这……真是出乎意料。

Text.en: That's the first truly wise thing I've heard you say.
Text.zh: 这是我听你说过最明智的话。

Text.en: I envisioned a future run on this power. Clean, safe, eternal. No mutations. No madness.
Text.zh: 我曾构想以此为能源驱动的未来——洁净、安全、永恒。没有异变，没有疯狂。

Text.en: Did your people simply put up with being constantly poisoned?
Text.zh: 你的族人难道甘愿长期忍受毒害？

Text.en: The danger of Corruption... was it not obvious?
Text.zh: 腐化的危害…难道不够明显吗？

Text.en: Do you think us stupid?
Text.zh: 你觉得我们很愚蠢？

Text.en: For centuries, our greatest minds worked to channel Corruption more efficiently, more safely. <continue>They did make great strides, but it could never be truly mastered. <continue>In the end, we were all fools. Them, for their adherence to old ways, and me, for believing I could... change anything.
Text.zh: 数百年来，最杰出的学者不断研究如何更高效安全地引导腐化。<continue>他们确实取得巨大进展，但始终无法真正驾驭它。<continue>最终，我们全是傻瓜——他们固守旧路，而我竟妄想……能改变什么。

Text.en: Change will come. It always does.
Text.zh: 改变终将到来。历来如此。

Text.en: Nothing ever changes, mate. You just play the hand you're dealt.
Text.zh: 什么都改变不了，兄弟。发到什么牌就打什么。

Text.en: This was no place of industry. It was meant to torture those that drew the ire of the Queen.
Text.zh: 这里根本不是什么加工厂，而是折磨触怒女王之人的地方。

Text.en: How could your people allow such horrors?
Text.zh: 你的族人怎能容许此等暴行？

Text.en: The strongest gems belonged to the royal family. Who could stop her?
Text.zh: 最强大的宝石都握在皇室手中。谁能阻拦她？

Text.en: Torture should be a tool, not a pleasure.
Text.zh: 折磨应是手段，不该成为乐趣。

Text.en: A moral I wish my Queen shared.
Text.zh: 但愿我的女王也能明白这个道理。

Text.en: How much do those souls trapped in Cores suffer?
Text.zh: 被困在灵核中的意识要承受多大的痛苦？

Text.en: There is no way to measure the level of awareness possessed by those trapped in Soul Cores... <continue>But I am reasonably certain it is not zero.
Text.zh: 我们无法量化灵核中残留的感知程度……<continue>但基本能确定绝非毫无知觉。

Text.en: And there is no way out? No penance, no term of service?
Text.zh: 难道没有解脱之路？没有赎罪或服役期限？

Text.en: None.
Text.zh: 没有。

Text.en: Barbaric.
Text.zh: 野蛮至极。

Text.en: Isn't this what you two sought? Wouldn't you last forever - in one of those Cores?
Text.zh: 这不正是你俩追求的么？被封进灵核不就获得永生了？

Text.en: It is a kind of immortality, yes, but not the kind sought by the sane.
Text.zh: 确实算某种永生，但绝非神志清醒者会追求的方式。

Text.en: Spirit's breath... This is horrifyin'. Tell me there's an end to it', at least.
Text.zh: 万灵在上……这太瘆人了。这种折磨至少会有尽头吧？

Text.en: Eventually, the crystal will degrade.
Text.zh: 最终，水晶会逐渐崩坏。

Text.en: Eventually? Ugh.
Text.zh: 最终？呕……

Text.en: Seems a bit excessive.
Text.zh: 未免太过分了。

Text.en: The cruelty <i>{is} the point.
Text.zh: 残忍<i>{就是}目的。

Text.en: How long can the mind last, being trapped without end in a Soul Core?
Text.zh: 一个意识被永远困在灵核中，究竟能保持清醒多久？

Text.en: There is no way to know, but I would hope not to find out.
Text.zh: 无从得知，但我不愿亲身验证。

Text.en: No one could stop her?
Text.zh: 没人能阻止她么？

Text.en: They didn't want to. They adored her.
Text.zh: 他们根本不想阻止。他们崇拜她。

Text.en: Aye... I've seen how madness like that takes root.
Text.zh: 是啊……我见过那种疯狂如何扎根蔓延。

Text.en: I cannot believe it... damn it all! <continue>We must not give up... perhaps if we acted differently? We must try again! <continue>I bought us time by destroying the mirror... but not much. We must leave, while we still can!
Text.zh: 我简直无法相信……该死！<continue>我们绝不能放弃……或许换种方式能行？我们必须再试一次！<continue>摧毁镜子争取了时间……但不多。趁还能走，我们必须立刻离开！

Text.en: That was... I thought I knew what to expect, but I could never have guessed how brutal the Red Communion would truly be. <continue>Steal what you must, but do not tarry long. This world, my world, is about to end...
Text.zh: 刚才……我原以为早有心理准备，却没想到红色圣礼的仪式竟如此残暴。<continue>该拿什么就拿，但切勿久留。这个世界，我的世界，即将终结……

Text.en: If you reset the systems that power Lira Vaal... <continue>If you reactivate it, the same way you did before... <continue>We can try again. We <i>{must} try again!
Text.zh: 若能重启利拉瓦尔的能源系统……<continue>像之前那样重新激活它……<continue>我们就能再试一次。我们<i>{必须}再试一次！

Text.en: You should leave quickly. Your gems... when the first wave of Corruption radiates, you'll suffer a fate worse than death.
Text.zh: 你们该尽快离开。你们的宝石……当第一波腐化辐射爆发时，你们会遭遇比死亡更凄惨的命运。

Text.en: For a time, the Queen's interests aligned with my own. <continue>She sought immortality, and so did I... for different ends.
Text.zh: 曾几何时，女王的追求与我的目标不谋而合。<continue>她寻求永生，我也是……虽目的不同。

Text.en: You were complicit.
Text.zh: 你曾是帮凶。

Text.en: Yes. I make no excuses. I did what I had to do.
Text.zh: 是。我不找借口。当时必须如此。

Text.en: A dangerous pursuit.
Text.zh: 此路凶险。

Text.en: The most dangerous. My search risked everything.
Text.zh: 凶险至极。我为这番探索赌上了一切。

Text.en: We were not the first to do so, either. The Eztli Pilli was already many centuries old when it passed to me. <continue>It contains all the knowledge possibly related to immortality we possess, both from our own civilisation, and... others.
Text.zh: 我们也非先行者。《埃兹特利皮利》传到我手中时早已流传数百年。<continue>其中收录了关于永生的全部知识，既有本文明所得，亦含……他族秘辛。

Text.en: From what I learned, the Vaal grew fat off the riches - and ruins - of others.
Text.zh: 据我所知，瓦尔人是靠着他族财富与遗迹发家的。

Text.en: You built on lost knowledge that led others to ruin.
Text.zh: 你们建立在让其他文明覆灭的灭世绝学之上。

Text.en: So your learning stemmed from what others grew before you.
Text.zh: 所以你们的知识，源自前人的探索。

Text.en: Yes. The Vaal developed the ability to implant souls into crystals based on ancient necromantic artifacts found deep underground. <continue>Our sophisticated golems stemmed from study of the Primevals, who once lived in the same regions we do. <continue>Blood thaumaturgy, however... that was us. Our unique contribution to science.
Text.zh: 没错。瓦尔人根据深埋地底的古代亡灵法器，研发出将灵魂封存于水晶的技术。<continue>我们的高阶傀儡源自对原始族的研究——它们曾栖息于我们如今所在的土地。<continue>至于鲜血秘法……这才是我们独有的创举，对科学界的独特贡献。

Text.en: Everything we deemed too volatile, dangerous, or useless, we sealed away in the Eztli Pilli, guarded by eighty-seven traps. <continue>I should have expected that... <i>{impossible woman...} would walk right up and pluck our deepest secrets without a care in the world.
Text.zh: 所有被判定为过于危险、不稳定或无用的知识，都被封存在《埃兹特利皮利》中，由八十七道机关保卫。<continue>我早该料到……那个<i>{天选之女}会如此满不在乎地取走我们掩埋最深的秘密。

Text.en: That's our Miss Valai.
Text.zh: 不愧是我们的维莱小姐。

Text.en: She does tend to do that.
Text.zh: 她向来如此。

Text.en: How do you put up with her?
Text.zh: 你怎么受得了她？

Text.en: Her optimism is repulsive but... she's not the worst.
Text.zh: 她那股乐观劲确实恼人……不过嘛，忍忍也就过去了。

Text.en: From you, that's high praise. Perhaps I'll rethink my annoyance.
Text.zh: 从你嘴里说出来，这算是盛赞了。看来我该重新想想她那些烦人之处了。

Text.en: She's not going to change just because you wish it.
Text.zh: 她不会因你的期望而改变。

Text.en: I would never demean a lady of the Vaal by asking her to diminish herself. It's just...
Text.zh: 我绝不会要求一位瓦尔淑女收敛锋芒，那是对她的侮辱。只是……

Text.en: The <i>{pluckiness.}
Text.zh: 这<i>{莽劲儿。}

Text.en: Yes. The 'pluckiness.'
Text.zh: 对。就这「莽劲儿」。

Text.en: I argued with Xipocado for years. Pleaded with him, at times. <continue>He knew she was dangerous, and that her communion with the Beast might be the death of us.
Text.zh: 我与夏波卡多争论数年，有时甚至苦苦哀求。<continue>他明知她无比危险，与邪兽的联结可能招来灭顶之灾。

Text.en: Let me guess... he didn't listen.
Text.zh: 让我猜猜……他根本没听进去。

Text.en: He didn't listen, did he?
Text.zh: 他充耳不闻，对吧？

Text.en: He did not.
Text.zh: 确实没有。

Text.en: He refused to listen?
Text.zh: 他拒绝听从？

Text.en: You should have fought to make him understand.
Text.zh: 你该竭力让他醒悟。

Text.en: My attempts only made him proclaim his loyalty all the more.
Text.zh: 我的劝诫反倒让他愈发宣誓效忠。

Text.en: That is Atziri's power over others... she sticks the knife of her beauty into their gut, and twists it for years. <continue>But she never gives them the sweet release of acceptance... or rejection. <continue>They increasingly bend to her every whim, trying to please her, desperate for acknowledgement or validation. <continue>It excuses nothing. In the end, he made his choice.
Text.zh: 这就是阿兹里的掌控力……她将美貌化作利刃刺入他人肺腑，经年累月地扭转搅动。<continue>却始终不给予他们渴望的接纳……或决绝的拒绝。<continue>他们逐渐屈从于每个心血来潮的指令，拼命取悦，渴求一丝认可。<continue>但这不能开脱任何事。终究是他自己的选择。

Text.en: And he shall own the consequences.
Text.zh: 他理当自食其果。

Text.en: Beauty is a weapon like any other. It, too, can be used for tyranny.
Text.zh: 美丽与刀剑无异，亦可成为暴政的工具。

Text.en: Can't say I blame the guy... I've seen her statues...
Text.zh: 倒也不全怪他……我见过她的雕像……

Text.en: I've felt the sway of a charismatic leader. It's a hard call to resist.
Text.zh: 我曾亲身体会过魅力领袖的蛊惑力。那种感召确实难以抗拒。

Text.en: In the game of lies and intrigue, secrecy was critical to all agendas. <continue>We developed a method of secure access that guarantees only specific individuals can open certain troves or doors. <continue>Xipocado's medallion contains a trace of his blood. It controls the design and function of the temple we built for Atziri. <continue>Without it, we can accomplish nothing.
Text.zh: 在这场充满谎言与阴谋的博弈中，保密是所有行动的关键。<continue>我们研发出一种秘法，可以确保只有特定人员才能开启宝库或通道。<continue>夏波卡多的鲜血纹章蕴藏着他的生命印记，掌控着为阿兹里建造的神庙的结构与机能。<continue>若无此物，我们将寸步难行。

Text.en: Then I shall obtain this medallion.
Text.zh: 那我便去取得这枚鲜血纹章。

Text.en: Well I'll just go fetch it, then. This shouldn't take but a minute.
Text.zh: 我这就去取，耽误不了多少工夫。

Text.en: Understood. I'll get the medallion.
Text.zh: 明白。我去取鲜血纹章。

Text.en: So you're sayin' we need that medallion.
Text.zh: 就是说咱们非得弄到那鲜血纹章不可。

Text.en: ... yes.
Text.zh: ……没错。

Text.en: I'm on it!
Text.zh: 交给我！

Text.en: Will the medallion still work if he's dead?
Text.zh: 人死了鲜血纹章还能用吗？

Text.en: Yes... if it comes to that.
Text.zh: 可以……如果真到那一步。

Text.en: I'll see what I can do for ye.
Text.zh: 我尽量帮你弄来。

Text.en: I'll do what I must.
Text.zh: 我会尽我的责任。

Text.en: I see. Very well, I'll do what I can.
Text.zh: 知道了。我会尽力而为。

Text.en: Atziri is beautiful, as most women are, in their own ways. Her aura of dominance stems from a very different source... her callousness. <continue>Your desires do not matter to her. You could serve her faithfully for decades, and she would still cast you out the moment you displease her. <continue>Such behavior builds a society of desperate servile worms. Lies become truth, and cowardice is rewarded. <continue>Over time, that kind of environment twists you, warps you. Breaks your spirit.
Text.zh: 阿兹里确实美丽——正如世间女子各有其美。但她那份凌人之气，却有着截然不同的根源……那就是她的冷酷。<continue>你的渴求于她毫无意义。即便忠心侍奉数十载，只要稍不顺意，她便会将你弃如敝履。<continue>这般行径滋养出无数心灰意冷的奴性蛆虫。谎言成为真理，怯懦反受嘉奖。<continue>久而久之，这样的环境会侵蚀你、扭曲你，直至击碎你的魂魄。

Text.en: That's a war a man cannot win on his own.
Text.zh: 那种仗，单靠一个人是打不赢的。

Text.en: Aptly put.
Text.zh: 说得在理。

Text.en: That's pathetic.
Text.zh: 真可悲。

Text.en: Sad thing, seein' men fall so low.
Text.zh: 看着人沦落至此真是可悲。

Text.en: Yes... it is.
Text.zh: 是啊……确实如此。

Text.en: That is why Sekhemas are bound by duty, not whim. You let your Queen run amok.
Text.zh: 所以丝克玛以责任立誓，而非顺从私欲。你们却纵容女王恣意妄为。

Text.en: Yes. We all did.
Text.zh: 是……我们都有罪。

Text.en: The court of a tyrant.
Text.zh: 暴君的朝堂。

Text.en: I know that feeling well. It creeps upon you like a shadow.
Text.zh: 我太了解那种感觉了。它就像阴影一样悄然侵蚀你。

Text.en: Do men really behave like that? That just sounds... crazy.
Text.zh: 人真的会变成那样吗？听起来……太疯狂了。

Text.en: It must be a form of insanity, yes.
Text.zh: 这根本就是疯了，没错。

Text.en: She could break my spirit any day.
Text.zh: 她随时都能击垮我的意志。

Text.en: Please. Do not jest.
Text.zh: 求你了，别开这种玩笑。

Text.en: You see me laughin', mate?
Text.zh: 你看我笑了吗，老兄？

Text.en: Lira Vaal was the ruling capital of the Vaal Empire for many centuries, but it had fallen into disuse. <continue>Xipocado restored it. Rebuilt it. Turned it into a modern wonder. <continue>I helped him build many of the mechanisms... I had to, if I was to keep my head.
Text.zh: 利拉瓦尔曾作为瓦尔帝国的统治中心延续了数个世纪，但早已废弃不用。<continue>是夏波卡多让它重获新生。他重建了这座城市，将其打造成了现代化的奇迹。<continue>其中不少机关装置都是我参与建造的……我不得不这么做，要想活命就得干。

Text.en: Save your excuses.
Text.zh: 少找借口了。

Text.en: Not excuses. Strategy.
Text.zh: 不是借口。是策略。

Text.en: Call it what you will.
Text.zh: 随你怎么说。

Text.en: Can't you disable it all, then? Let us walk right through?
Text.zh: 那你能把机关都关掉吗？让我们畅通无阻？

Text.en: If you know the terrain within, surely you can show me some hidden paths?
Text.zh: 既然你熟悉里面的地形，总该知道些秘道吧？

Text.en: Ha, knowin' you, surely there's a secret door?
Text.zh: 呵，以你的作风，肯定准备了暗门吧？

Text.en: I tried to build such routes, but Xipocado was far too paranoid...
Text.zh: 我确实尝试过设计这类通道，但夏波卡多实在太过多疑..……

Text.en: An empire built on terror is no empire at all.
Text.zh: 建立在恐怖之上的帝国根本不算帝国。

Text.en: Perhaps, but its people are still worth saving.
Text.zh: 或许吧，但这里的百姓依然值得拯救。

Text.en: From what I've seen, I'm not certain I agree.
Text.zh: 据我观察，倒不见得。

Text.en: We are not to blame for the wrongs of our masters.
Text.zh: 主君犯下的罪过不该由我们承担。

Text.en: Sometimes, we do terrible things to survive.
Text.zh: 有时候，为了活下去不得不做些可怕的事。

Text.en: That's what I try to tell myself.
Text.zh: 我也是这么劝说自己的。

Text.en: You could have just run, mate. It's a skill I've practiced well.
Text.zh: 你本可以一走了之啊，哥们。这可是我的拿手好戏。

Text.en: I had too much to preserve. Too much to protect. You wouldn't understand.
Text.zh: 我有太多需要守护的东西。太多必须保全的事物。你不会懂的。

Text.en: Bit rude...
Text.zh: 这话有点伤人……

Text.en: Atziri has long been obsessed with the Beast, ever since I discovered its hiding place in the mountain... <continue>She would divert sacrifices to the dig site. Feed subjects to it, just to watch the process. <continue>She seemed to believe she was winning its favour, like a master earning the loyalty of a pet.
Text.zh: 阿兹里对那头邪兽的痴迷由来已久，自从我在深山里发现它的藏身之处后……<continue>她就把献祭品源源不断运往挖掘现场。把活人喂给它，只为了观察整个过程。<continue>她似乎相信自己正在赢得它的好感，就像主人驯服宠物那样。

Text.en: ...and was she right?
Text.zh: ……她驯服它了吗？

Text.en: It is alive, but I don't believe it feels the way even basic animals do...
Text.zh: 它确实还活着，但我不认为它具备哪怕最基本的动物情感……

Text.en: Can the Beast truly be tamed in such a way?
Text.zh: 用这种方式真的能驯服那头邪兽吗？

Text.en: Can the Beast obey a master?
Text.zh: 邪兽会听命于主人吗？

Text.en: I don't believe it can ever truly be controlled.
Text.zh: 我认为它永远不可能被真正掌控。

Text.en: Not all creatures feel things the way we do.
Text.zh: 不是所有生物都像我们一样拥有感情。

Text.en: Indeed. I found no evidence that the Beast is capable of such a bond.
Text.zh: 确实。我找不到任何证据表明那头邪兽能建立这种羁绊。

Text.en: I studied it myself, under the guise of studying it for her. I delayed wherever I could, buying time. <continue>But in the end, I was forced to devise the Red Communion. She wouldn't let me return to Utzaal unless I completed its design.
Text.zh: 我以协助研究为名亲自观察过它。能拖延就拖延，尽可能争取时间。<continue>但最终，我还是被迫设计了红色圣礼。除非完成这个方案，否则她根本不让我回乌扎尔。

Text.en: So this is all your fault?
Text.zh: 所以这全是你的错？

Text.en: Aw, see. That's where you burnt the stew. It's on you.
Text.zh: 呵，看吧。关键就是你在这儿搞砸了。这有你的责任。

Text.en: Partially. History will likely remember me as a monster, but it could not be avoided.
Text.zh: 有一部分是。历史大概会把我记载成怪物，但当时别无选择。

Text.en: Did you try to sabotage what you gave her?
Text.zh: 你试过在交给她的方案里动手脚吗？

Text.en: I tried... but she had her loyal Architects verify my work.
Text.zh: 试过……但她让那些忠心的建筑师核验了我的成果。

Text.en: She didn't trust you?
Text.zh: 她不信任你？

Text.en: Atziri trusts no one.
Text.zh: 阿兹里从不相信任何人。

Text.en: You were an animal caught in a trap, gnawing off its own leg to survive...
Text.zh: 你就像被困在陷阱里的野兽，只能咬断自己的腿求生……

Text.en: Direct study of the Beast propelled our technology forward by centuries. <continue>Our nobles used gems for generations, but never truly understood what they were.
Text.zh: 直接研究那头邪兽，让我们的科技水平向前发展了数百年。<continue>我们的贵族世代使用宝石，却从未真正理解其本质。

Text.en: What are they, truly?
Text.zh: 它们究竟是什么？

Text.en: Sounds like <i>{you} do, though. Tell me.
Text.zh: 听上去<i>{你}很清楚。告诉我。

Text.en: They come from the Beast's outer flesh, its carapace. They are Corruption, in crystallised form.
Text.zh: 它们源自那邪兽的外皮，它的甲壳。是腐化的结晶形态。

Text.en: This new level of understanding swept our greatest minds along with the tide of innovation.
Text.zh: 这种认知的新境界，裹挟着我们最杰出的智者，一同沉溺于创新的浪潮中。

Text.en: You learned the truth, and did not reconsider?
Text.zh: 你们知晓了真相，却未曾反思？

Text.en: There was no thought given to where it would lead.
Text.zh: 当时根本没人考虑过这会将我们引向何方。

Text.en: Perhaps that is why the Spirit has abandoned me. <continue>When I used a gem, I took up the power of Corruption as my own.
Text.zh: 或许这就是万灵抛弃我的原因。<continue>当我使用宝石时，我便将腐化的力量纳为己用。

Text.en: That explains why the Maji must never touch them...
Text.zh: 这就解释了为什么众神不肯触碰它们……

Text.en: You tellin' me gems are chunks of Corruption?
Text.zh: 你是说，宝石就是一块块的腐化？

Text.en: Gems are made of Corruption? Why didn't you say anything?
Text.zh: 宝石是由腐化构成的？你怎么不早说？

Text.en: Gems are... Corruption? How? They don't drive us mad, or... change us.
Text.zh: 宝石是……腐化？怎么会？它们又不会让我们发疯，或者……改变我们。

Text.en: <i>{You} safely place them in geomantic sockets, a technique I believe the Titans created for humanity during the Third Pact. <continue>We Vaal generally implant our gems in our flesh. They are far stronger when used that way, but come with... unwanted consequences. <continue>In that respect, the people of your era are far wiser.
Text.zh: <i>{你}安全地将它们放置在几何插槽中，我相信这是泰坦在第三盟约时期为人类创造的技术。<continue>我们瓦尔人通常将宝石植入血肉。那样使用起来力量要强大得多，但会带来……相应的代价。<continue>在这一点上，你们时代的人要明智得多。

Text.en: Each gem we mined seemed to take after a particular portion of a myth or belief. <continue>Abilities our people believed our gods to possess. Arakaali's poison. Kopec's fire. Never anything new. Never the unknown.
Text.zh: 我们开采的每一颗宝石，似乎都对应着某个神话或信仰的片段。<continue>我们的族人深信那些能力是神祇所拥有的。阿拉卡力的中毒。科佩克的火焰。从来都不是新事物。从来都没有未知。

Text.en: So they come from the gods?
Text.zh: 所以它们源自神祇？

Text.en: No... they come from us.
Text.zh: 不……它们源自我们自身。

Text.en: Curious. I think I'm beginning to understand the true nature of this world.
Text.zh: 有意思。我想我开始理解这个世界的本质了。

Text.en: You have a unique mind. I have no doubt you will expand humanity's knowledge.
Text.zh: 你的思想很独特。我相信你会拓展人类的知识疆界。

Text.en: Not if I keep it all for myself.
Text.zh: 如果我不分享出去，那就不会了。

Text.en: Ah, so what people believe becomes fragmented... crystallised...
Text.zh: 人们的信仰会碎片化……然后结晶……

Text.en: So the gems of each era are different, because prevailing beliefs have changed...
Text.zh: 所以每个时代的宝石都不同，因为主流信仰已经改变了……

Text.en: Precisely.
Text.zh: 正是如此。

Text.en: I see the danger. What mortal could turn down the power of the gods?
Text.zh: 我明白这其中的危险了。哪个凡人能拒绝神祇的力量呢？

Text.en: The strength of the gods, lyin' around in forests and valleys... free for anyone to pick up and use to kill each other.
Text.zh: 神祇的力量，就散落在森林山谷间……任人拾取，用来互相残杀。

Text.en: Our royalty became addicted to the power of these gems long ago. In that sense, we have always been doomed.
Text.zh: 我们的皇族早在很久以前就对这些宝石的力量上瘾了。从这个意义上说，我们早就注定要毁灭了。

Text.en: Perhaps gems could be seen as... rewards. The fruits of our faith.
Text.zh: 或许宝石可以被视为……一种奖赏。我们信仰的果实。

Text.en: The Beast is merely a mechanism. The gods have nothing to do with it.
Text.zh: 那邪兽仅仅是一种机制。与神祇毫无关系。

Text.en: Sin grew the Beast. He is a god... and it is our beliefs that shaped and formed these gems.
Text.zh: 是罪孕育了那邪兽。他是一位神祇……而正是我们的信仰塑造并形成了这些宝石。

Text.en: You... have me there. Perhaps my response was too callous.
Text.zh: 你……这话倒让我无言以对了。或许我之前的回应太过刻薄。

Text.en: You sure? Wait, which god uses a crossbow?
Text.zh: 你确定？哪位神祇会用弩？

Text.en: Kuetzakala, of course. Goddess of the siege.
Text.zh: 当然是库埃扎卡拉，攻城女神。

Text.en: Huh. Wow. Learn somethin' new every day 'round you lot.
Text.zh: 哇。跟你们这帮人在一起真是天天都能学到新东西。

Text.en: My Patron warned that Fate would torment me. And yet, even knowing the futility of my efforts, I am compelled to try anyway. <continue>We are so close. We have access to Atziri in the moments before the Cataclysm! <continue>I will try again and again, no matter how long it takes.
Text.zh: 我的守护者曾警告说，命运会折磨我。然而，即便明知努力终将徒劳，我依然要去尝试。<continue>我们只差一步了——我们马上就能接触到灾变前夕的阿兹里！<continue>无论要耗费多少时光，我都会一次又一次地尝试。

Text.en: It is the fight that matters, not victory.
Text.zh: 重要的是抗争本身，而非胜利。

Text.en: Can we really change what has already transpired? Is it <i>{possible?}
Text.zh: 我们真能改变既定的过往吗？这真的<i>{可能}吗？

Text.en: No matter how hard we fight, Time will not bend to your will.
Text.zh: 无论我们如何奋战，时间都不会屈从于你的意志。

Text.en: The past is not ours to change.
Text.zh: 过去不是我们能改变的。

Text.en: Maybe we can't change it. But I'll keep trying, as long as we're allies.
Text.zh: 或许我们无法改变它。但只要我们还是盟友，我就会不断尝试。

Text.en: I once lamented that I could save the Vaal, had I but time. Now I have all the time I could ever want. <continue>I will not stop until we undo the Red Communion.
Text.zh: 我曾悲叹：若当时能多些时日，或可拯救瓦尔帝国。如今我拥有了无穷无尽的时间。<continue>除非我们彻底毁掉红色圣礼，否则我绝不停止。

Text.en: You mean <i>{we} will try again and again.
Text.zh: 你是说<i>{我们}要一遍遍地尝试？

Text.en: That way lies madness, but I cannae fault you.
Text.zh: 这条路通往疯狂……但我没法指责你。

Text.en: That's it. Keep up the good fight. What Patron is that, by the way?
Text.zh: 没错。就这样坚持奋战吧。话说回来，你那位守护者究竟是？

Text.en: We will talk about that when the time is right. They took an interest in me after my Trial... greatly amused them.
Text.zh: 时机成熟时我自会告知。自从我通过试炼后，他们便对我产生了兴趣……似乎觉得我相当有趣。

Text.en: Now there's a story I'm dyin' to hear.
Text.zh: 这故事我可太想听了。

Text.en: At her behest, I devised everything necessary for the Red Communion. I built the cradle of gems. The systems for Xipocado's temple. <continue>The moment itself, however, was far more brutal than I imagined...
Text.zh: 在她的授意下，我设计了红色圣礼所需的一切。我建造了宝石基座，为夏波卡多的神庙构建了系统。<continue>然而，仪式本身的残酷程度远超我的想象……

Text.en: The Beast does not seem predictable.
Text.zh: 那邪兽似乎难以捉摸。

Text.en: It always is.
Text.zh: 向来如此。

Text.en: I've only seen such horrors once before...
Text.zh: 我此前只见过一次类似的恐怖景象……

Text.en: Our presence may have destabilised the process. I can't imagine it helped to have the Demon of Atzoatl present... we may have made the Cataclysm worse.
Text.zh: 我们的存在可能破坏了仪式的稳定性。阿佐亚特恶魔在场恐怕只会雪上加霜……我们或许让灾变更惨烈。

Text.en: It is never wrong to fight for something better.
Text.zh: 为更好的结局而战永远没有错。

Text.en: I fail to see how it could <i>{be} worse.
Text.zh: 我实在无法想象还能怎么<i>{变得}更惨烈。

Text.en: Or perhaps it would have been worse without us. There's no way to tell.
Text.zh: 也许没有我们介入情况会更惨烈。这根本无法判断。

Text.en: I suppose that's true...
Text.zh: 我想确实如此……

Text.en: Then we'll try again, until we make a difference.
Text.zh: 那我们就继续尝试，直到改变结局。

Text.en: That's kinda our thing, inninit? Making things worse?
Text.zh: 让情况更惨烈，不正是我们的拿手好戏吗？

Text.en: So far...
Text.zh: 目前是这样……

Text.en: That is what we called... it's hard to explain. <continue>During its construction, our Temple of Atzoatl was plagued by an impossible, elusive, rampaging murderer who appeared at random to assassinate our Architects. <continue>Then, when it was completed, they showed up one last time, to slaughter everyone inside and loot everything it contained. <continue>The Demon's appearance changed often, but their companion's did not. I now know her to be... Alva Valai. <continue>We did determine that the pair were coming from the future, but we could never have guessed how far. <continue>It's still unclear when those events are meant to happen... for her. <continue>Also... <i>{why?} Why will she go back and aid a rampage through Atzoatl? <continue>Actually, maybe that one's obvious.
Text.zh: 我们当时称之为……这很难解释。<continue>在建造阿佐亚特神庙期间，我们被一个不可能的、难以捉摸的、狂暴的杀手所困扰，他随机出现刺杀我们的建筑师。<continue>然后，当神庙建成时，他们最后一次出现，屠杀了里面的所有人，并洗劫了其中的一切。<continue>那位恶魔的样貌时常改变，但他同伴的样貌不会改变。我现在知道了她的身份……阿尔瓦·维莱。<continue>我们当时确定这两人来自未来，但我们绝不可能猜到他们来自如此遥远的未来。<continue>至今仍不清楚，那些事件对她而言……将发生在何时。<continue>还有……<i>{为什么？} 她为何要回到过去，协助那场对阿佐亚特的洗劫？<continue>实际上，也许这个问题的答案很明显。

Text.en: The treasure.
Text.zh: 为了宝藏。

Text.en: The treasure.
Text.zh: 为了宝藏。

Text.en: The treasure.
Text.zh: 为了宝藏。

Text.en: The treasure.
Text.zh: 为了宝藏。

Text.en: The treasure.
Text.zh: 为了宝藏。

Text.en: The treasure.
Text.zh: 为了宝藏。

Text.en: The treasure.
Text.zh: 为了宝藏。

Text.en: The treasure.
Text.zh: 为了宝藏。

Text.en: The treasure.
Text.zh: 为了宝藏。

Text.en: The treasure.
Text.zh: 为了宝藏。

Text.en: The treasure.
Text.zh: 为了宝藏。

Text.en: The treasure.
Text.zh: 为了宝藏。

Text.en: Yes. The treasure.
Text.zh: 对，为了宝藏。

Text.en: Aoife...? C-can it be?
Text.zh: 艾欧菲……？真……真的是你吗？

Text.en: Yes. I'm here, love.
Text.zh: 是我，亲爱的，我在这儿。

Text.en: But how...?
Text.zh: 可这怎么可能……？

Text.en: It matters not. <continue>Tell me... do you feel it? Your memories returning.
Text.zh: 这不重要。<continue>告诉我……你感觉到了吗？你的记忆正在恢复。

Text.en: I feel it, aye. But they're... <i>{fractured.} There's still so much I dinnae understand.
Text.zh: 我感觉到了，没错。但它们……<i>{支离破碎}。 还有太多事我想不明白。

Text.en: Remember how we were drawn to him... believing him one of the Maji of old.
Text.zh: 还记得我们当初如何被他吸引……都以为他是上古众神之一。

Text.en: Aye... we were fools...
Text.zh: 是啊……我们太傻了……

Text.en: Not fools... just deceived. Even I could not see the King's true face until it was too late.<continue>And when I did, he... took me from you.
Text.zh: 不是傻……只是被蒙蔽了。就连我也没能看清国王的真面目，直到为时已晚。<continue>而当我看清时，他……将我从你身边夺走了。

Text.en: I should have saved ye... I should have done more—
Text.zh: 我本该救你的……我本该做得更多——

Text.en: Hush... that is not your blame to carry. <continue>That grief made you wander the wilds, lost in your pain.
Text.zh: 嘘……这不该由你来背负。<continue>正是这份悲痛让你在荒野中徘徊，沉沦在无尽的痛苦之中。

Text.en: I... remember... years of solitude... roaming...
Text.zh: 我……想起来了……那些年独自流浪……漫无目的地……

Text.en: And yet here you are. That is what matters.
Text.zh: 可如今你在这里。这才是最重要的。

Text.en: Nay... none of it matters. Not without you.
Text.zh: 不……没有你，这一切都毫无意义。

Text.en: What's going on here?
Text.zh: 这是怎么回事？

Text.en: After I activate six of these, an old Vaal temple opens.
Text.zh: 等我激活六个这种灯塔，一座古老的瓦尔神庙就会开启。

Text.en: I <i>{suppose} I could use your treasure hunting skills... these lead to an old temple.
Text.zh: 我<i>{想}或许能用上你的寻宝技巧……这些灯塔通向一座古老神庙。

Text.en: These structures are connected to an ancient Vaal temple.
Text.zh: 这些建筑连接着一座古老的瓦尔神庙。

Text.en: There is a lost temple... these open the way...
Text.zh: 有座失落的神庙……这些灯塔将开启通路……

Text.en: A temple, of sorts. Dinnae know what to make of it.
Text.zh: 算是某种神庙吧。我也搞不清具体来历。

Text.en: When enough of these light up, we can get into an ancient Vaal temple.
Text.zh: 只要点亮足够数量的灯塔，我们就能进入古老的瓦尔神庙。

Text.en: There's a Vaal temple. Could use your expertise.
Text.zh: 那儿有座瓦尔神庙，或许需要你的专业知识。

Text.en: There's a temple, Miss Valai. Right up your alley.
Text.zh: 那儿有个神庙，维莱小姐。正对你的胃口。

Text.en: A temple! I like the sound of that. <continue>Well go on then. Don't dally!
Text.zh: 神庙！听起来真不错。<continue>赶紧打开呀！别磨蹭！

Text.en: This is Lira Vaal, once the capital of the empire!<continue>And <i>{that} is the Royal Temple, home to Queen Atziri in her final days...
Text.zh: 这里就是利拉瓦尔，昔日帝国的都城！<continue>而<i>{那}就是皇家神庙，阿兹里女王在那里度过了最后的时光……

Text.en: I haven't seen any throne rooms in those ruins.
Text.zh: 我在那些废墟里可没看见什么王座厅。
