Text.en: You really are... strange.
Text.zh: 你这个人……可真奇怪。

Text.en: I hope the Maraketh survived...
Text.zh: 希望马拉克斯人还活着……

Text.en: In the chaos... I don't know what became of our allies. Though...
Text.zh: 如今一片混乱……我也不知道我们的盟友怎么样了。不过……

Text.en: Suddenly... the world feels a much lonelier place.
Text.zh: 突然间……这个世界变得格外孤单。

Text.en: We must have hope. Without that, what else is there?
Text.zh: 我们必须心怀希望，不然还能指望什么？

Text.en: We need more than hope. We need to take action.
Text.zh: 光有希望还不够，我们得行动起来。

Text.en: Diabolical, the bloody lot of it. How many have died?
Text.zh: 真是造孽，这一切简直就是灾难。到底死了多少人？

Text.en: I can't be sure. But yes. It is... substantial. But...
Text.zh: 我说不准。但可以肯定数量……很多。只是……

Text.en: All they sought to achieve... and <i>{this} is the result.
Text.zh: 他们汲汲营营所求的一切……结果却变成了<i>{这样}。

Text.en: The Twilight Order are no longer alive to contemplate their mistakes. We must focus on those who <i>{are} alive.
Text.zh: 暮光会已经没机会反省他们的错误了。我们得把注意力放在<i>{还}活着的人身上。

Text.en: The world mourns. I feel it in the dirt. In the trees. I just hope... it can forgive us all.
Text.zh: 世界在哀悼。我在泥土里，在树木中，都能感觉到。我只希望……它能原谅我们所有人。

Text.en: That... sounds like nonsense. But I think I understand your meaning.
Text.zh: 这……听起来有点玄乎。但我想我明白你的意思……

Text.en: [DNT]
Text.zh: 那些还活着的人……我们必须把他们放在第一位。

Text.en: [DNT]
Text.zh: 幼年？我看那玩意儿可大得吓人……

Text.en: [DNT]
Text.zh: 想想之前……那邪兽可是长得跟山一样大。不管怎样……

Text.en: [DNT]
Text.zh: 我们受到了审判。但我们仍然站立在此。这本身……就是我们品格的证明。

Text.en: [DNT]
Text.zh: 活下来并不意味着我们是正义的。我们不过是运气好罢了。

Text.en: [DNT]
Text.zh: 要不是那些该死的蠢货已经死了……我现在非要把暮光会杀得汇成一片血海。

Text.en: [DNT]
Text.zh: 他们的血……早就流干了，这我可以向你保证。不过……

Text.en: As it stands, there are survivors out there, and they will be facing extreme hardship. <continue>We must do whatever it takes to ensure humanity's survival.
Text.zh: 眼下，外面还有幸存者，他们正面临极其艰难的处境……<continue>我们必须竭尽所能，确保人类能够活下去。

Text.en: Our first order of business is survival. The cataclysmic surge of Corruption has mutated beast and man alike, and they are roving wild. <continue>That's where you come in. Your... set of skills... is crucial now.
Text.zh: 眼下最要紧的，就是活下来。这次腐化的爆发规模太大，不管是野兽还是人类都发生了变异，它们正四处肆虐。<continue>这就需要你出手了。你的……那套本事……现在至关重要。

Text.en: That always seems to be the case...
Text.zh: 这话我怎么听着这么耳熟呢……

Text.en: Ah, yes. I'm in hot demand.
Text.zh: 啊，没错。我现在可是抢手货。

Text.en: Of course. I will do what I must.
Text.zh: 当然。我会尽我的责任。

Text.en: Saving the world is eating up all my spare time...
Text.zh: 天天忙着拯救世界，我连点空余时间都没有了……

Text.en: I'll rise to the occasion. Don't you worry.
Text.zh: 放心，这事我能搞定。

Text.en: Well, I'm the man for the job. What can I say?
Text.zh: 这活儿非我莫属，还用说吗？

Text.en: The path ahead... is uncertain but necessary.
Text.zh: 前路……虽不明朗，却非走不可。

Text.en: Yes, of course. We're in the thick of it, after all.
Text.zh: 是的，当然。毕竟，我们现在正身处风暴中心。

Text.en: The modern era is relentlessly violent without the peacekeeping efforts of the Vaal.
Text.zh: 没有瓦尔人维护和平，这世道可真是暴力横行……

Text.en: Vaal society appeared anything but peaceful...
Text.zh: 瓦尔社会看上去跟和平可不沾边……

Text.en: We suffered for our causes. But it was not without reason.
Text.zh: 我们都为自己的事业付出过代价，但这都是有原因的。

Text.en: So... the widespread sacrifice of your own people wasn't violent at all?
Text.zh: 所以……大规模献祭自己的人民，完全算不上暴行？

Text.en: That... was different. It was not without purpose.
Text.zh: 那……不一样。我们那么做是有目的的。

Text.en: I saw the bodies piled high in Utzaal. The people you fed to your sacrificial altars in the name of progress. <continue>If you think the Vaal were peaceful, perhaps you are not as intelligent as you believe you are.
Text.zh: 我在乌扎尔见过那些堆积如山的尸体。那些你以进步之名献上祭坛的人。<continue>如果你觉得瓦尔人爱好和平，那你可能远没有自己想象的那么聪明。

Text.en: You cast stones... but what of your own people? <continue>The Maraketh discard their lessers at a horrific rate. A tragic waste.<continue>That is to say nothing of the armies of slaves that stand at the fore of your caravans.  <continue>Next time you think of casting aspersions against my people, I suggest you look inward first.
Text.zh: 你在指责我们……那你自己的人民呢？<continue>马拉克斯人会以惊人的速度扫除弱者。真是白白糟蹋生命。<continue>更别提商队最前面那些成群的奴隶了。<continue>下次想指责我的人民之前，先自己照照镜子吧。

Text.en: The Vaal, peacekeeping? You need to brush up on your history.
Text.zh: 瓦尔人，维护和平？你该好好补补历史课了。

Text.en: Answer me this: does history remember us as conquerors, or as innovators?
Text.zh: 那我问你，历史是将我们铭记为征服者，还是创新者？

Text.en: It remembers you as monsters, and rightly so.
Text.zh: 历史将你们铭记为怪物，这也是应该的。

Text.en: Whatever our faults, we were not warmongers. <continue>Perhaps it is you who needs to 'brush up' on their history.
Text.zh: 无论我们犯过什么错，我们都不是战争贩子。<continue>或许需要“补历史课”的，是你才对。

Text.en: As opposed to how peaceful it was back in your time.
Text.zh: 相比之下，你们那个时代可真太平啊。

Text.en: Precisely.
Text.zh: 确实。

Text.en: That were sarcasm. I I saw Utzaal with me own eyes. The way blood stained the stones.
Text.zh: 我那是讽刺。我亲眼见过乌扎尔。石头上的血迹历历在目。

Text.en: We sacrificed our own people, yes. But it was a sacrifice that many were happy to make in the name of progress.
Text.zh: 我们确实献祭了自己的人民，没错。但那是很多人为了进步而甘愿做出的牺牲。

Text.en: Call it progress all ya like. I saw where your 'progress' got your people.
Text.zh: 你要说那是进步就说吧。我可亲眼瞧见，你那“进步”把你的人民害成了什么样。

Text.en: Somethin' about Vaal diplomacy doesn't strike me as very wholesome soundin'...
Text.zh: 总觉得瓦尔那套外交手段，听着就不太正常……

Text.en: Tell me... how many wars did we wage? <continue>Our empire grew from our devotion to progress. Not to domination.
Text.zh: 告诉我……我们发动过多少战争？<continue>我们的帝国靠追求进步而逐渐壮大，而不是靠征服。

Text.en: Of all the words to describe the Vaal, 'peacekeepers' is not one I would use.
Text.zh: 可以形容瓦尔人的词多了去了，“维护和平”这个词我可绝不会用到他们头上。

Text.en: Then you have fallen prey to your modern biases. History is written by the victor, and there are few left to advocate for the Vaal now.
Text.zh: 那你就是被你们现代人的偏见蒙蔽了。历史是由胜利者书写的，如今没几个人愿意为瓦尔说话了。

Text.en: Perhaps there are simply few who would want to.
Text.zh: 也许只是根本没人想替你们说话。

Text.en: Be that as it may... the Vaal's interest was in science. In progress! Not in waging war.
Text.zh: 不管怎样……瓦尔人热衷的是科学。是进步！不是发动战争。

Text.en: I saw your chambers. I saw the death you wrought with your own hands. Don't be lecturing me now on such matters.
Text.zh: 我看过你的密室。我见证过你亲手造就的死亡。<continue>现在别来教训我这些。

Text.en: I understand how that might appear to you.
Text.zh: 我知道你会怎么想。

Text.en: The towers we caused to emerge are clearly more ancient than anything we have seen. <continue>I can safely assume that they are Precursor in origin. <continue>You should secure as many as you can, so that I may study them for whatever secrets they may possess...
Text.zh: 我们激活的那些塔楼，显然比我们见过的任何东西都要古老。<continue>我基本可以肯定，它们起源于先驱者。<continue>你应该尽可能多地占领它们，这样我就能研究其中藏匿的秘密……

Text.en: What kind of secrets?
Text.zh: 什么样的秘密？

Text.en: Knowing you, you've already got a pet theory. Let's hear it.
Text.zh: 依你的性子，肯定已经有了一套自己的猜测。说来听听。

Text.en: How are you able to divine so much from so little? I look at these Precursor structures and see nothing but mystery.
Text.zh: 你怎么能从这么点东西里看出那么多门道？我看着这些先驱者建筑，除了谜团什么也看不出来。

Text.en: As ever, there is a fair amount of guesswork involved – at least in the early stages. <continue>I cannot always be certain of my conclusions, but I have years of experience in separating truth from falsehood. <continue>Each society is different, beholden to their own beliefs and biases. Understanding these biases is key to understanding how they think. <continue>Once you understand this, it becomes easier to discern their intentions – and by extension, their technologies.
Text.zh: 跟往常一样，其中掺了不少猜测，至少在初期阶段是这样。<continue>我不能保证所有结论都完全正确，但多年的经验让我能辨别真伪。<continue>每个社会都不一样，都受制于各自的信仰和偏见。弄明白这些偏见，是理解他们思维方式的关键。<continue>一旦搞懂了这一点，再去辨别他们的意图就容易了，进而也能弄懂他们的技术。

Text.en: You make it sound so easy. <continue>What, then, do you expect to learn of these towers?
Text.zh: 你说得倒轻松。<continue>那你想从这些高塔里了解到什么？

Text.en: You want to study them? Go there yourself. Would save me doing all the legwork for once.
Text.zh: 你想研究它们？那你自己去啊。也省得我替你跑腿了。

Text.en: There is too much I must do here. It would be senseless of me to go frolicking around old Precursor ruins... not when we have you ready and able to perform such a task.
Text.zh: 我在这里有太多事要做。跑到那些古老的先驱者遗迹里闲逛，实在是浪费时间……更何况有你这么趁手的人选，随时都能去完成任务。

Text.en: Lucky for some, I suppose.
Text.zh: 只能说，有些人命就是好啊。

Text.en: Would you prefer to remain here? Perhaps you can engage your keen scientific brain and unravel the mystery of these towers.
Text.zh: 你更愿意留在这里？也许你可以开动你敏锐的科学头脑，解开这些塔楼的秘密。

Text.en: Alright... point taken.
Text.zh: 行吧……我懂你意思了。

Text.en: What've you learned so far?
Text.zh: 到目前为止，你都了解到了什么？

Text.en: I'm guessin' you've figured out a few o' their secrets already.
Text.zh: 我猜你已经摸清它们不少秘密了吧。

Text.en: Have you any theories as to their purpose?
Text.zh: 你知道它们到底有什么用了吗？

Text.en: I suppose you've some idea as to their function already.
Text.zh: 关于它们的用途，你应该已经有头绪了吧。

Text.en: From their placements alone, I suspect they were intended to exert refined energies over vast distances. <continue>At this point, I can only speculate as to why.
Text.zh: 仅从它们的分布来看，我推测它们曾被用于远距离传输某种精炼过的能量。至于原因，目前只能猜测。

Text.en: We have found a vast underground web of Precursor leylines... <continue>They were indistinguishable from natural bedrock before, but now that they have reactivated, it is clear to me our entire continent bears a lattice beneath.
Text.zh: 我们发现了先驱者建造的庞大地下地脉网络……<continue>它们之前与天然岩层别无二致，但现在既然已经重新激活，我敢肯定，整个大陆之下都存在着这样的网络。

Text.en: The entire continent? Are you sure?
Text.zh: 整个大陆？你确定？

Text.en: But why? Why take the time to build this lattice?
Text.zh: 可是为什么？为什么要花这么大功夫建这个网络？

Text.en: Connections so vast it beggars belief. Why would they build such a thing?
Text.zh: 如此庞大的连接规模，令人难以置信。他们为什么要建这种东西？

Text.en: The <i>{entire} continent? But why?
Text.zh: <i>{整个}大陆？可是为什么呢？

Text.en: Can't have been easy for them to do. So why do it?
Text.zh: 这事对他们来说肯定不轻松。所以为什么要建呢？

Text.en: Must've taken them a while. Why go to all that effort?
Text.zh: 怕是花了不少时间吧。费这么大劲图什么呢？

Text.en: That would have taken a great deal of effort. What did they hope to accomplish?
Text.zh: 这得耗费多少人力物力啊。他们到底有什么目的？

Text.en: Wonder why they would make such a thing.
Text.zh: 真不明白他们为什么要造这东西。

Text.en: I believe this lattice connects the towers. There is a vast intent at work here, but one so ancient, we may never know what they were trying to do...
Text.zh: 我相信这个网络连接着那些塔楼。这其中隐藏着宏大的意图，但年代太久远了，我们或许永远无法知晓他们的目的……

Text.en: The leylines all lead to the fortress. If we are going to find anything that may help our situation, we will find it there. <continue>I do not believe it was a city, nor a simple bastion. It is the heart of some colossal 'grand project'. <continue>That much is clear.
Text.zh: 所有地脉都通往那座堡垒。要想找到对我们有用的东西，就得去那儿找。<continue>我不认为它是一座城市，也不仅仅是座堡垒。它是某个庞大宏伟计划的核心。<continue>这一点已经很清楚了。

Text.en: What kind of grand project?
Text.zh: 什么样的宏伟计划？

Text.en: A grand project? To do what, exactly?
Text.zh: 宏伟计划？具体是要干什么？

Text.en: A grand project? To what end?
Text.zh: 宏伟计划？有什么目的？

Text.en: What do you mean, 'grand project'?
Text.zh: “宏伟计划”是什么意思？

Text.en: Grand project? What's that supposed to mean?
Text.zh: 宏伟计划？这又是什么说法？

Text.en: What kinda project are we talkin' about here?
Text.zh: 咱们说的到底是什么计划？

Text.en: What was this project meant to achieve?
Text.zh: 这计划原本是想达成什么目的？

Text.en: What d'ye think they were tryin' to accomplish with this project o' theirs?
Text.zh: 你觉得他们搞这计划，到底是想做什么？

Text.en: The leylines that stretch across Wraeclast... that fortress is at the heart of it. <continue>We will not know for sure until we get inside.
Text.zh: 地脉遍布整个瓦尔克拉斯……那座堡垒就是它们的核心。<continue>不进去看看，我们永远无法确认真相。

Text.en: Those murals were meant for the future. Not just depictions, but warnings. Instructions.
Text.zh: 那些壁画是为后世而作的。不只是描绘，更是警告，是指引。

Text.en: What were they trying to warn us about?
Text.zh: 他们想警告我们什么？

Text.en: What some consider a warning, I consider an invitation.
Text.zh: 有些人觉得是警告，我倒觉得更像是邀请。

Text.en: In that respect, we are alike.
Text.zh: 在这一点上，我们倒是挺像的。

Text.en: So which is this? Should we heed this warning, or not?
Text.zh: 那到底是哪种？我们该不该理会这个警告？

Text.en: What were they warning us of?
Text.zh: 他们是在警告我们什么？

Text.en: Don't you think we're placing an awful lot of trust in these Precursors? <continue>How do we know these instructions are even worth following?
Text.zh: 你不觉得我们有点太相信这些先驱者了吗？<continue>我们怎么知道应该遵循这些指示？

Text.en: We don't. But it is the best option available to us.
Text.zh: 我们不知道。但这是我们目前最好的选择。

Text.en: The threat must've been great, for them to feel the need to warn future generations.
Text.zh: 肯定是因为特别危险，他们才会觉得有必要警告后人。

Text.en: Yes. We can be thankful that they considered such a warning their responsibility.
Text.zh: 没错。我们得庆幸，他们觉得有责任发出这样的警告。

Text.en: Can think of a few better ways to relay instructions than a bloody mural.
Text.zh: 居然用该死的壁画来传递指示，我都能想出好几种更好的办法。

Text.en: It is not without its shortcomings. But a mural transcends language barriers. It is an elegant, if imperfect, means of communication.
Text.zh: 它确实有不足之处。但壁画能跨越语言障碍。这种沟通方式虽不完美，却很雅致。

Text.en: Sure. So... what were these murals tryin' to tell us?
Text.zh: 好吧。那这些壁画到底想告诉我们什么？

Text.en: Why a mural? Why not something with less room for interpretation?
Text.zh: 为什么要用壁画？就不能用些不容易产生歧义的方式？

Text.en: This room for interpretation is both a blessing and a curse. <continue>Yes, it is possible that someone might misinterpret what is depicted in the mural. <continue>But it is also true that a mural transcends language. It is not reliant on a shared understanding of culture or creed.
Text.zh: 这种解读空间，既是优点，也是缺点。<continue>没错，可能有人会误解壁画描绘的内容。<continue>但壁画确实可以超越语言，也不依赖于共同的文化或信仰背景。

Text.en: A fair point. What, then, does this warning tell us?
Text.zh: 有道理。所以这个警告到底告诉了我们什么？

Text.en: It's just as well we found them. We'd have little to go on without it.
Text.zh: 幸好我们找到了它们。没有这些，我们简直无从下手。

Text.en: The Precursors must have been at war with the Abyssals. There was a direct depiction of the fortress under siege. <continue>They tried to do something... elect some sort of champion. Did they fail? We cannot be certain. <continue>It seems the Burning Monolith is a final contingency... which bodes ill for us.
Text.zh: 先驱者当年必定跟深渊魔物交过手。有一幅壁画就明确描绘了那座堡垒遭受围攻的场景。<continue>他们打算做些什么……选定某种勇士。他们失败了吗？我们不得而知。<continue>看来，燃石巨塔是最后的应急手段……这对我们来说可不是什么好兆头。

Text.en: After some research, I surmise the Lich Lord we freed was one known as Tecrod. <continue>Essentially, he was missing from history, known only as 'the Lost' or 'the Forsaken.'
Text.zh: 经过一番研究，我推测我们释放的那位巫妖之王叫作特克罗。<continue>他在历史上几乎毫无踪迹，只留下“失落者”或“被遗弃者”的称号。

Text.en: He must have been in there a long time indeed.
Text.zh: 他肯定被关在里面很久了。

Text.en: Longer than most mortals could fathom.
Text.zh: 久到超出大多数凡人的想象。

Text.en: Did Kulemak not want his servant back? You'd think he would have come looking for him.
Text.zh: 库勒马克不想找回他的仆人吗？我还以为他会来找他。

Text.en: The Abyssals themselves are locked in an endless cycle of death and rebirth. They disappear for a time, only to return, remade. <continue>If one of these disappearances was ever to coincide with Tecrod's imprisonment...
Text.zh: 深渊魔物本身就被困在无尽的死亡与重生的轮回中。他们会消失一段时间，然后再度归来，重塑形体。<continue>如果其中一次消失刚好跟特克罗被囚禁的时间重合…………

Text.en: Then there'd be no one left to free him.
Text.zh: 那就没人能来放他自由了。

Text.en: Precisely. At least until the next cycle is complete.
Text.zh: 正是如此。至少得等下一个轮回结束。

Text.en: It is my dearest regret that he does not remain 'lost'.
Text.zh: 他没能继续“失落”下去，真是我莫大的遗憾。

Text.en: Understandably so.
Text.zh: 可以理解。

Text.en: Was he in a kind of stasis? If he was trapped long enough to go missing from history, how is it that he didn't go mad?
Text.zh: 他是不是处于某种停滞状态？既然被囚禁了那么久，久到被历史遗忘，那他怎么没有发疯？

Text.en: What seems a long time to you or me becomes insignificant when viewed through the lens of the Abyssals. <continue>Consider that to a being who is functionally immortal, an eon of imprisonment is little more than a brief impediment.
Text.zh: 在你我眼中漫长得难以忍受的时光，对于深渊魔物来说却微不足道。<continue>想想看，对于近乎永生的存在来说，万古的囚禁也不过是耽搁了片刻。

Text.en: How's such a powerful bein' become lost to history?
Text.zh: 这么强大的存在，怎么会被历史遗忘？

Text.en: He was imprisoned by a people whose collective power rivalled, if not dwarfed, his own.
Text.zh: 囚禁他的种族，其整体的力量足以与他匹敌，甚至可能远胜于他。

Text.en: Sounds like we did him a favour, then.
Text.zh: 这么说来，我们倒是帮了他一个忙。

Text.en: We most certainly did... unintentional as it was.
Text.zh: 确实如此……尽管纯属无心之举。

Text.en: How does one go missing from history?
Text.zh: 一个人怎么能从历史中消失？

Text.en: By being imprisoned within a sarcophagus for generations.
Text.zh: 被关在一具石棺里，历经无数世代。

Text.en: But with nary a trace left behind?
Text.zh: 连一丝痕迹都不会留下？

Text.en: He was certainly forsaken by his people, to be left trapped for that long.
Text.zh: 他肯定是被自己的族人抛弃了，才会被困这么久。

Text.en: Perhaps they simply trusted that no prison could hold him forever. That on a long enough timeline, someone or some<i>{thing} would precipitate his release.
Text.zh: 也许他们只是觉得，没有牢笼能永远困住他。只要时间足够漫长，总会有人或某种<i>{东西}让他重见天日。

Text.en: Got that right, didn't they?
Text.zh: 还真让他们猜对了，是吧？

Text.en: Indeed they did.  <continue>The Abyssals are not like us mortals. They have the luxury of time, and thus operate under no urgency.
Text.zh: 确实猜对了。<continue>深渊魔物与我们这些凡人不同。他们最不缺的就是时间，因此行事从不紧迫。

Text.en: It seems the Precursors somehow trapped not only his body, but his soul... and we let him out.
Text.zh: 看来先驱者当年困住的不只是他的身体，还有他的灵魂……而我们把他放了出来。

Text.en: I am no expert on the Abyssals, as the Vaal never faced them, but I am astounded they once rivalled the Precursors.
Text.zh: 我对深渊魔物知之甚少，毕竟瓦尔人从未与它们交过锋，但听说它们曾经能与先驱者匹敌，我深感震惊。

Text.en: How is that possible?
Text.zh: 这怎么可能？

Text.en: Why? The Precursors were powerful, yes... but the Abyssals are <i>{infinite.}
Text.zh: 为什么？先驱者们确实很强大……可深渊魔物是<i>{无穷无尽的}。

Text.en: Yes... that much is true.
Text.zh: 是的……这倒不假。

Text.en: It once took an alliance that spanned the Vastiri and beyond to hold the Abyssals at bay. <continue>That they could rival the Precursors does not surprise me in the slightest.
Text.zh: 当年，要遏制深渊魔物，得靠横跨瓦斯提里乃至更远地域的庞大联盟。<continue>说他们能与先驱者抗衡，我丝毫不觉得意外。

Text.en: We've seen what these Precursors were capable of. It's hard to imagine <i>{anyone} could stand toe to toe with them.
Text.zh: 我们见识过这些先驱者的能耐。很难想象竟然<i>{有人}能跟他们分庭抗礼。

Text.en: So how'd they do it?
Text.zh: 那他们是怎么做到的？

Text.en: You and me both, mate. How'd they manage it?
Text.zh: 我也想知道，老兄。他们怎么办到的？

Text.en: It does not surprise me. <continue>Were it not for the Third Pact, the Abyssals would have long since run rampant over Wraeclast.
Text.zh: 我并不觉得意外。<continue>若非第三盟约，深渊魔物早就把整个瓦尔克拉斯踏平了。

Text.en: So how did they?
Text.zh: 那他们到底是怎么做到的？

Text.en: Putting the pieces together... led by this 'Kulemak,' who could continually rebuild their bodies, they must have been an endless legion. <continue>But then... the Burning Monolith activated, perhaps. Doing something so devastating, the Abyssals retreated from history for thousands of years...
Text.zh: 综合种种线索……在“库勒马克”的带领下，它们可以不断重塑自己的身体，简直就是一支杀不尽的大军。<continue>但随后……或许是燃石巨塔被激活了，造成了毁灭性的打击，以至于深渊魔物从历史上销声匿迹了数千年……

Text.en: If the builders of the fortress were intelligent - and they most certainly were - they would have created a contingency in the event the Abyssals returned.
Text.zh: 如果堡垒的建造者足够聪明——他们显然很聪明——那他们必定留了后备方案，以防深渊魔物卷土重来。

Text.en: What kind of contingency?
Text.zh: 什么样的后备方案？

Text.en: What kind of contingency?
Text.zh: 什么样的后备方案？

Text.en: What kind of contingency?
Text.zh: 什么样的后备方案？

Text.en: What kind of contingency?
Text.zh: 什么样的后备方案？

Text.en: What kind of contingency?
Text.zh: 什么样的后备方案？

Text.en: What kind of contingency?
Text.zh: 什么样的后备方案？

Text.en: What kind of contingency?
Text.zh: 什么样的后备方案？

Text.en: What kind of contingency?
Text.zh: 什么样的后备方案？

Text.en: The Burning Monolith is collecting energy. If we do not reach it in time, it will most likely activate. <continue>That would be... very bad.
Text.zh: 燃石巨塔正在积蓄能量。如果我们没能及时赶到，它很可能会被激活。<continue>那后果将……不堪设想。

Text.en: <i>{Assuming} the experience you described, this... memory battle... was a real thing, I suppose it may be some sort of... test.
Text.zh: <i>{假设}你描述的经历，那场……记忆中的战斗……是真实发生的，那么我想它可能是某种……考验。

Text.en: A test? What for?
Text.zh: 考验？考验什么？

Text.en: Oh, you believe me now, do you?
Text.zh: 哦，现在你开始相信我了？

Text.en: I am... open to the possibility.
Text.zh: 我……不排除这种可能。

Text.en: How gracious of you. What kind of test do you think it was, then?
Text.zh: 你可真给我面子啊。那你觉得这是什么样的考验？

Text.en: What do you suppose was the purpose of this test?
Text.zh: 你觉得这场考验的目的是什么？

Text.en: Why would they need to test me?
Text.zh: 他们为什么要考验我？

Text.en: I doubt it was their intention to test <i>{you} specifically. <continue>Rather, this battle was an obstacle placed in the path of any who would seek to access the innermost chambers.
Text.zh: 我不认为他们是要专门考验<i>{你}。更可能的是，这场战斗是一道障碍，挡在所有试图进入核心密室的人面前。

Text.en: What kind o' test would that be?
Text.zh: 那这算哪门子考验？

Text.en: I'll tell you what – you're <i>{testin}' my patience with all this bloody scepticism.
Text.zh: 我跟你说啊，你那怀疑精神倒真是在<i>{考验}我的耐心。

Text.en: I am a man of science. Would you prefer I took every claim at face value, with no attempt to corroborate the details for myself?
Text.zh: 我是个讲求科学的人。难道你希望我对所有说法都照单全收，却不花力气去证实细节吗？

Text.en: Whatever. It's not important.<continue>Let's say you're right. What's the reason for this test, then?
Text.zh: 行吧，这不重要。<continue>就算你说得对。那这场考验是为了什么呢？

Text.en: Why a test? What purpose would it serve?
Text.zh: 为什么要设置这个考验？有什么目的吗？

Text.en: A test? To what end?
Text.zh: 一场考验？为了什么呢？

Text.en: Perhaps to keep Abyssals away from the Burning Monolith while it enacted its task, or to secure the fragments in some other unknown manner. <continue>We are dealing with ancient minds that thought very differently from us. It may be impossible to truly understand them.
Text.zh: 也许是为了在燃石巨塔执行任务时，把深渊魔物挡在外面，或者是为了用某种未知的方式保护碎片。<continue>我们面对的是古老的智慧，思维方式跟我们完全不同。或许永远无法真正理解他们。

Text.en: From studying the Monolith and its murals... and this is going to sound very strange, even for me—
Text.zh: 通过研究巨塔及其壁画……这话说出来，连我自己都觉得荒唐——

Text.en: Whatever it is, I can handle it.
Text.zh: 不管是什么，我都能应付。

Text.en: Come on. Out with it.
Text.zh: 行了，别卖关子了。

Text.en: I am intrigued.
Text.zh: 我倒是挺感兴趣。

Text.en: That's saying something.
Text.zh: 能让你这么说，那可不简单。

Text.en: I'll be the judge o' that.
Text.zh: 那得我自己来判断。

Text.en: Never mind that. Lay it on me.
Text.zh: 别绕圈子了，直说吧。

Text.en: 'Strange' is a relative term. Go on.
Text.zh: “荒唐”这词可不好定义。继续说。

Text.en: Heard plenty o' strange things in my time. Don't you worry.
Text.zh: 我这辈子听过的怪事多了去了。你只管讲。

Text.en: I believe the Arbiter of Ash was <i>{grown}, not born. A great many of these creatures were... the closest word may be <i>{designed}. <continue>Only a few survived, but those that did were incredibly suited to their tasks.
Text.zh: 我认为灰烬仲裁官是被 <i>{培植}出来的，而不是天生的。许多这类生物……最贴切的词可能是被<i>{设计}出来的。<continue>只有少数能够幸存，可但凡活下来的，无一不是称职的好手。

Text.en: Tell me about this Fourth Edict.
Text.zh: 跟我讲讲这个第四律令吧。

Text.en: Tell me what you know of this Fourth Edict.
Text.zh: 你来跟我说说这个第四律令的情况吧。

Text.en: I would hear what you have to say of this 'Fourth Edict'.
Text.zh: 我想听听你对这个“第四律令”的看法。

Text.en: This Fourth Edict... what do we know about it?
Text.zh: 这个第四律令……我们对它有什么了解？

Text.en: Hopin' you have some light to shed on this 'Fourth Edict'.
Text.zh: 希望你能揭开这个第四律令的面纱。

Text.en: So, this 'Fourth Edict'... whaddaya know about it?
Text.zh: 那这个“第四律令”……你都知道些什么？

Text.en: The Fourth Edict – what do you know of it?
Text.zh: 关于第四律令，你知道些什么？

Text.en: What can ye tell me o' this 'Fourth Edict'?
Text.zh: 关于这个“第四律令”，你能告诉我些什么？

Text.en: From what we have learned, I have come to believe that the Precursors deeply trusted... well, people like me. People of science. <continue>I think about the dangers they faced, and the capabilities they had, and I know what I would have done in their place.  <continue>If the Abyssals were overrunning my people, and there was no longer any hope... <continue>I believe the Fourth Edict was not created to save the Precursors.
Text.zh: 根据我们目前掌握的信息，我开始觉得先驱者非常信任……嗯，像我这样的人——搞科研的。<continue>想想他们当时面临的危险，以及他们拥有的能力，我知道如果换作是我，我也会做出同样的选择。<continue>如果深渊魔物要吞噬我的族人，而一切希望都已破灭……<continue>我相信，第四律令并非先驱者为了自救而创造的。

Text.en: Then what was it for?
Text.zh: 那它是用来做什么的？

Text.en: Ominous. What did they intend it to do, then?
Text.zh: 听起来很不详。那他们打算用它做什么？

Text.en: A reasonable conclusion, given they are no longer here. But then... what <i>{was} its purpose?
Text.zh: 既然他们现在都不在了，这个推测倒也合理。可是……它<i>{到底}有什么用呢？

Text.en: But then what was it for?
Text.zh: 那它到底是用来干什么的？

Text.en: Why <i>{did} they create it, then?
Text.zh: 那他们<i>{当初}为什么要造它？

Text.en: Explains why they're no longer around I guess.  <continue>But that begs the question, don't it – what was it meant to do?
Text.zh: 这也就解释了他们为什么会消失。<continue>可这又引出下一个问题，它原本是用来做什么的？

Text.en: I'm not sure I understand. You said it was a contingency against the Abyssal threat.
Text.zh: 我有点没搞懂。你之前说它是用来对付深渊魔物的后备方案。

Text.en: So I did... and so it is.
Text.zh: 我确实说过……事实也是如此。

Text.en: Then what are you saying?
Text.zh: 那你说的到底是什么意思？

Text.en: Then what <i>{was} their intention?
Text.zh: 那他们<i>{是}有什么打算？

Text.en: It was created to obliterate <i>{everything}, on the slim hope that some humans would survive, somewhere... <continue>If it <i>{was} used once before, we are the legacy that remains... though it took us thousands of years to rebuild. <continue>That is my current theory.
Text.zh: 它是为了毁灭<i>{一切}，只寄希望于——也许还有少数人类能在某个地方活下来……<continue>如果它<i>{确实}被使用过一次，那么我们就是幸存的后继者……尽管我们花了数千年才重建文明。<continue>这是我目前的推测。

Text.en: If the Fourth Edict is meant to obliterate life as we know it, what good is it to the Abyssals? It would destroy them as it would us.
Text.zh: 要是第四律令是为了毁灭我们所知的一切生命，那它对深渊魔物又有什么用呢？它会像毁掉我们一样，把它们也毁了。

Text.en: Why are the Abyssals so determined to get their necrotic little hands on the Fourth Edict?
Text.zh: 深渊魔物为什么死活都要把第四律令弄到手？

Text.en: I do not relish the thought of this Edict in Abyssal hands. <continue>Why do they want it, if not to use it as the Precursors intended?
Text.zh: 我可不想看到这律令落到深渊魔物手里。<continue>如果不是打算按先驱者的初衷来用，那它们到底想拿它做什么？

Text.en: What do these Abyssals want with the Fourth Edict anyway?
Text.zh: 这些深渊魔物想拿第四律令做什么？

Text.en: What do the Abyssals want with the Fourth Edict if it'd mean their own doom?
Text.zh: 要是这第四律令也会毁掉它们自己，那深渊魔物还抢它做什么？

Text.en: Why are these Abyssals so keen to get their grubby paws on the Edict?
Text.zh: 这些深渊魔物为什么这么执着，非要把律令弄到手？

Text.en: How might the Abyssals use the Fourth Edict to further their goals?
Text.zh: 深渊魔物打算怎么利用第四律令达成他们的目标？

Text.en: What is it the Abyssals mean to do with this Fourth Edict?
Text.zh: 深渊魔物到底想拿这个第四律令做什么？

Text.en: From studying the murals we found, I believe the Abyssals were on the verge of winning the war. The living world was doomed. <continue>So the Precursors did the only thing they could... they reset the board. <continue>I imagine the Abyssals want that contingency removed from the playing field, so that their potential victory cannot be again undone. <continue>It might even be possible for them to wield that power themselves.
Text.zh: 通过研究我们找到的壁画，我相信当年深渊魔物已胜券在握。生灵世界注定灭亡。<continue>于是，先驱者做了唯一能做的事……他们重置了棋盘。<continue>依我之见，深渊魔物想把这个后备方案从棋盘上移除，好让它们稳操胜券，让对手再无翻盘机会。<continue>甚至，它们自己也想掌控这种力量。

Text.en: How many Edicts do you think there are?
Text.zh: 你觉得总共有多少个律令？

Text.en: If we're to deal with these Edicts, it might be helpful to know how many there are.
Text.zh: 既然要跟这些律令打交道，那最好先搞清楚到底有多少个。

Text.en: By your estimation, how many Edicts were there?
Text.zh: 据你估计，一共有多少个律令？

Text.en: Do we know how many edicts there are?
Text.zh: 我们知道一共有多少个律令吗？

Text.en: How many o' these Edicts d'ya think there are?
Text.zh: 你觉得这些律令有多少个？

Text.en: These Edicts... how many do you reckon there are... were... whatever?
Text.zh: 这些律令……你觉得总共有多少……曾经有多少？差不多就这意思。

Text.en: Have we ascertained how many Edicts there are?
Text.zh: 我们搞清楚律令的总数了吗？

Text.en: Can we put a number on these Edicts? How many are there?
Text.zh: 咱能给这些律令算个数吗？总共有多少？

Text.en: From what we have seen, there were four Edicts. <continue>The second was the Beast. The third was the Weapon. The fourth was the Flame Seed at the Burning Monolith. <continue>But the first... has something to do with the central tower. Its true meaning remains to be seen.
Text.zh: 按目前的状况来看，共有四个律令。<continue>第二个是邪兽。第三个是武器。第四个是燃石巨塔中的火焰之种。<continue>至于第一个……与那座中央塔楼有关。但它真正的意义尚待揭晓。

Text.en: Have you any thoughts on what that machine under the central tower does?
Text.zh: 你觉得中央塔楼下面那台机器是干什么的？

Text.en: This machine under the central tower... what do you think it does?
Text.zh: 中央塔楼下面的那台机器……你觉得它是干什么用的？

Text.en: What is that giant Precursor contraption we found?
Text.zh: 我们找到的那台巨大先驱者装置到底是什么？

Text.en: That machine we found under the central tower... what purpose could it serve?
Text.zh: 我们在中央塔楼底下找到的那台机器……会是做什么用的？

Text.en: The chamber under the central tower... what d'ya think it's for?
Text.zh: 中央塔楼下面的那个房间……你觉得是做什么用的？

Text.en: This giant Precursor doodad they've got hidden under their tower... any idea what it does?
Text.zh: 他们藏在塔下面的那个巨型先驱者玩意儿……你知道它是干什么用的吗？

Text.en: What purpose might the machine under the central tower serve?
Text.zh: 中央塔楼底下的机器有什么用途？

Text.en: This infernal contraption we found under the tower... hopin' ye've some thoughts on what it does.
Text.zh: 我们在塔下发现的那个鬼机器……希望你对它的用途能有点头绪。

Text.en: That vast machine is part of some process we have yet to understand. <continue>Now that we know the Precursors fought a war against the Abyssals, and that the fortress even held one imprisoned... I think we should complete the work. <continue>Ancient scientists started something. We should finish it.
Text.zh: 那台巨大的机器，是我们尚未理解的某个进程中的一环。<continue>既然我们知道先驱者曾与深渊魔物交战……而且那座堡垒还囚禁过一个魔物……我想我们应该完成这项工程。<continue>古代的科学家们开了头。我们应该把它完成。

Text.en: What is this <i>{thing} we've made? The one from the Precursor machine.
Text.zh: 我们到底造出了个什么<i>{东西}？就是用先驱者机器里的那个。

Text.en: Well... we combined an egg with a seed. I trust you need no explanation of the particular biological process this is imitating. Unless...?
Text.zh: 嗯……我们把一颗卵和一枚种子结合在了一起。想来不需要我解释这在模仿哪一种生物过程吧。除非……？

Text.en: I understand that perfectly, thank you. But what have we ended up with?
Text.zh: 这个我当然懂，谢谢。但最后弄出来的到底是什么？

Text.en: So I got my hands dirty with that whole seed and egg situation... but to what end?
Text.zh: 我费了好大劲处理那个卵和种子的破事……可到底有什么结果？

Text.en: What exactly have we made using this Precursor machine?
Text.zh: 我们到底用这台先驱者机器造出了什么？

Text.en: What did that machine do exactly? It made something, sure... but what is it?
Text.zh: 那机器到底做了什么？它肯定造出了什么……可究竟是什么呢？

Text.en: Ask yourself what usually happens when egg meets seed.
Text.zh: 问问自己，卵遇到种子，通常会发生什么。

Text.en: Yes, yes. I don't need <i>{that} explained to me. My question is – what purpose does it serve? What do we do with it now?
Text.zh: 行行行，我不用你讲<i>{什么}生命起源。我问的是，这东西是干什么用的？现在我们拿它怎么办？

Text.en: So we got the machine workin'... but what've we used it to make?
Text.zh: 我们确实用了那机器……可到底用它造出了个啥？

Text.en: What've we made usin' this machine... <i>{thing}? <continue>And don't say a baby.
Text.zh: 我们用这机器造了个什么……<i>{鬼东西}？<continue>别跟我说是孩子。

Text.en: It's true that the Precursor machine seems to mimic the reproductive process. For what reason, I do not know.
Text.zh: 这台先驱者机器看起来确实在模拟生殖过程。但具体目的，我还不清楚。

Text.en: Maybe these Precursors had mummy issues?
Text.zh: 兴许这些先驱者都有恋母情结？

Text.en: This object born of the Precursor machine... what do you think it is?
Text.zh: 先驱者机器诞下的这东西……你觉得它是什么？

Text.en: For the time being, that remains something of a mystery. But your choice of the word 'born' is apt.
Text.zh: 目前来说，这还是个谜。不过你说的“诞下”这个词，倒是挺贴切的。

Text.en: Usin' that infernal contraption... the whole thing causes me no end o' discomfort.
Text.zh: 使唤那台该死的机器……可把我折腾得浑身不舒服。

Text.en: Why? It is essential to our understanding of the Precursors.
Text.zh: 为什么？要理解先驱者，这东西至关重要。

Text.en: Because recklessness like yours is what caused the damn Cataclysm in the first place. How is it you're so calm about chargin' once more into the unknown?
Text.zh: 就是因为你这股莽撞劲儿，才引发了当年那场该死的灾变。现在你这么一头扎进未知里，怎么还能这么淡定？

Text.en: You underestimate the pure, unadulterated joy of discovery. And at any rate... the worst has happened. We are here. <continue>The only recourse is to move forward, without fear.
Text.zh: 你低估了发现未知带来的纯粹喜悦。而且不管怎么说……最坏的情况已经发生了，我们就身处其中。<continue>唯一的办法，就是无所畏惧地继续前进。

Text.en: Very well. In that spirit... what has this damned contraption made for us anyhow?
Text.zh: 很好。照你这么说……那台该死的机器究竟给我们造出了个什么？

Text.en: We have seen that the Precursors are no strangers to the shaping and creation of living beings. <continue>Whatever it is we completed by bringing the two components together... it may be related to that mural of the champion we saw.  <continue>That was the only mural directly depicting a living being...
Text.zh: 可以看到，先驱者对于塑造和创造生命并不陌生。<continue>无论我们将那两种物质结合后会生成什么……它很可能与我们看到的那幅描绘勇士的壁画有关。<continue>那是唯一一幅直接描绘活物的壁画……

Text.en: I have come to believe that these Arbiters were created by the Precursors to guard and operate the Edicts.<continue>You encountered the Arbiter of Ash in relation to the Fourth Edict, and the Arbiter of Divinity in relation to the First. <continue>That begs the question: were there Arbiters for the other two Edicts? If so, what happened to them?
Text.zh: 我开始相信，这些仲裁官是先驱者创造出来守护和操控律令的。<continue>你在第四律令那里遇到了灰烬仲裁官，又在第一律令那里遇到了神圣仲裁官。<continue>那问题来了，其他两个律令也有对应的仲裁官吗？如果有，它们出什么事了？

Text.en: Could they have broken? They are not entirely organic, after all.
Text.zh: 它们会不会是坏了？毕竟它们不是纯有机体。

Text.en: If there's one thing we can say with certainty about these Precursor creations, it's that they were built to last. <continue>If the remaining Arbiters are gone, it is likely they were defeated – not simply lost to time.
Text.zh: 关于这些先驱者造物，有一点可以肯定，那就是它们被造得极其耐用。<continue>要是剩下的仲裁官消失了，那么多半是被击败了——而不会只因为年头太久就坏掉。

Text.en: Wherever they are, I'm sure they're dreaming of one day being a thorn in our side. That's usually how it seems to go.
Text.zh: 不管它们在哪儿，我敢肯定它们迟早还会回来找我们麻烦。这好像就是一贯的套路。

Text.en: Perhaps they're entombed... or else dead by Abyssal hands.
Text.zh: 也许它们已经被封入墓穴……又或者死在了深渊魔物手里。

Text.en: Perhaps... though given the longevity of many Precursor creations, it is not something we should depend on.
Text.zh: 也许吧……不过考虑到很多先驱者造物都能存续极长的时间，我们不能指望它们就这么消失。

Text.en: I'm sure we'll find out sooner or later. <continue>Let's just hope – if there are more out there – that we find out <i>{before} they show up.
Text.zh: 我敢肯定，迟早会再见到它们。<continue>要是真还有的话，只希望我们能赶在它们现身<i>{之前}就发现。

Text.en: If only we knew...  <continue>Don't like the thought o' them bein' out there one bit.
Text.zh: 要是能知道就好了……<continue>一想到它们还在外面，我就浑身不自在。

Text.en: If nothing else, encountering another Arbiter may help us to better understand the Precursors and their Edicts.
Text.zh: 别的先不论，如果再遇到仲裁官，说不定能帮我们更好理解地先驱者和他们的律令。

Text.en: Aye, so says you. You're not the one fighting 'em.
Text.zh: 是啊，你说得轻巧。又不用你去跟它们打。

Text.en: You mean I'll have to fight <i>{more} o' those bloody things?
Text.zh: 你是说我还得去对付<i>{更多}那种鬼东西？

Text.en: Not necessarily... It may be that the two Arbiters we encountered are the only ones to survive the passage of time. <continue>That being said... we cannot rule it out.
Text.zh: 不一定……也许我们遇到的那两个仲裁官，是经受住时间考验而仅存的两个。<continue>话虽如此……也不能完全确定就没有更多了。

Text.en: Great. Just great.
Text.zh: 太好了，真是太好了。

Text.en: I do not know... but the thought of them out there somewhere fills me with disquiet.
Text.zh: 我可说不好……但一想到它们还在外面某个角落，我就心神不宁。

Text.en: What happened indeed. I dinnae like the thought of leavin' such a what-if unanswered.
Text.zh: 真不知道怎么回事。这种悬而未决的感觉真让人不舒服。

Text.en: All will become clear in time... whether by our will, or theirs.
Text.zh: 一切终将水落石出……不管是按我们的意愿，还是按它们的意愿。

Text.en: Aye... that's what I'm afraid of.
Text.zh: 是啊……我担心的就是这个。

Text.en: The First Divine may be the key to this mystery. Its very existence suggests the First Edict may have been... Divinity.
Text.zh: 第一神祇或许正是解开这个谜团的关键。它的存在本身就暗示着，第一律令或许就是……神性本身。

Text.en: Are you saying they invented Divinity?
Text.zh: 你是说，他们发明了神性？

Text.en: Divinity... is an invention of the Precursors?
Text.zh: 神性……是先驱者的发明？

Text.en: You're saying the First Ones – and not <i>{just} the First Ones, but any god you can think to name – only exist because of the Precursors?
Text.zh: 你是说，初生之子——而且不<i>{只是}初生之子，而是所有你能叫得出名字的神——之所以存在，全都拜先驱者所赐？

Text.en: This is my working hypothesis, yes.
Text.zh: 这是我目前的推论，没错。

Text.en: What? You mean to say the Precursors <i>{invented} the concept of godhood?
Text.zh: 什么？你是说，先驱者<i>{发明了}成神这个概念？

Text.en: Not the concept, as such. But the means through which it is attained... yes, I believe so.
Text.zh: 不是这个概念本身。而是成神的途径……对，我认为是他们发明的。

Text.en: There appears to be no shortage of catastrophes they came up with.
Text.zh: 看来他们搞出来的烂摊子还真不少。

Text.en: What do you mean? How can the Edict <i>{be} Divinity?
Text.zh: 你这话什么意思？律令怎么就<i>{成了}神性？

Text.en: I mean to say that the mechanism through which a person becomes Divine is itself a Precursor invention.
Text.zh: 我的意思是，一个人得以成神的那套机制，是先驱者的发明。

Text.en: But... how can that be?
Text.zh: 可……这怎么可能呢？

Text.en: Divinity? You sure?
Text.zh: 神性？你确定？

Text.en: Not at all. But in the absence of more elucidating information, that is my current hypothesis.
Text.zh: 一点也不确定。只是在出现更确凿的信息之前，我现阶段只能这样假设。

Text.en: In other words, you're makin' this shite up.
Text.zh: 换句话说，你就是在瞎编。

Text.en: All good science begins with – as you so eloquently put it – 'making shite up'.
Text.zh: 所有靠谱的科学，都始于——用你那精妙的说法就是——“瞎编”。

Text.en: But to invent Divinity itself... is such a thing even possible?
Text.zh: 可发明神性这种事……真的可能吗？

Text.en: Each discovery we make continues to push the bounds of what is 'possible'... so, in that sense, why not?
Text.zh: 我们每一次的新发现，都在不断拓宽“可能”的边界……所以，从这个意义上说，为什么不可能呢？

Text.en: They invented Divinity? I find that difficult to comprehend.
Text.zh: 神性是他们发明的？我实在无法理解。

Text.en: It is a terrifying concept to try and wrap one's head around. And yet, it is the best explanation I have as to how the gods came to be.
Text.zh: 确实是个让人不寒而栗的想法。然而，关于众神是如何诞生的，这是我目前能给出的最合理的解释。

Text.en: In their desperate war against the Abyssals, they resorted to extreme measures. <continue>Wraeclast may have been changed forever because of something <i>{they} did...
Text.zh: 在与深渊魔物的绝望战争中……他们诉诸了极端手段。<continue>也许正是<i>{他们}当年的所作所为，彻底改变了整个瓦尔克拉斯……

Text.en: If the Precursors created Divinity, then who first rose to godhood?
Text.zh: 如果神性是先驱者创造的，那么第一位成神的是谁？

Text.en: If the Precursors invented Divinity, then they must have chosen someone to become their first god.
Text.zh: 如果神性是先驱者发明的，那他们必定选了某个人，来当第一个神吧。

Text.en: If Divinity is a Precursor invention, then it stands to reason that the First Divine would have been a Precursor also, yes?
Text.zh: 如果神性是先驱者的发明，那么按照这个逻辑，第一神祇可能就是先驱者，对吧？

Text.en: Yes. I believe so.
Text.zh: 是的，我也是这么认为的。

Text.en: Let's say I'm on board with the idea that the Precursors created Divinity – if that's true, then which god came first?
Text.zh: 好，就算我接受“神性是先驱者搞出来的”这个说法——那第一个神是谁？

Text.en: So if Divinity's the work o' the Precursors... then the first god must've been one o' theirs.
Text.zh: 所以，如果神性是先驱者的手笔……那么第一个神肯定也是他们的人。

Text.en: I still find it hard to believe these Precursors could've <i>{created} Divinity. You're really sure that's what happened?
Text.zh: 我还是很难相信，先驱者能<i>{创造}神性。你真的确定吗？

Text.en: Not at all. But in the absence of more elucidating information, that is my current hypothesis.
Text.zh: 一点也不确定。只是在出现更确凿的信息之前，我现阶段只能这样假设。

Text.en: In other words, you're makin' this shite up.
Text.zh: 换句话说，你就是在瞎编。

Text.en: All good science begins with – as you so eloquently put it – 'making shite up'.
Text.zh: 所有靠谱的科学，都始于——用你那精妙的说法就是——“瞎编”。

Text.en: Alright, fine. Let's say I believe you. Who was the first god, then?
Text.zh: 好吧，好吧，就当我信了你。那第一个神是谁？

Text.en: If the Precursors created Divinity, then who was their 'First Divine'?
Text.zh: 如果神性是先驱者创造的，那么他们的第一神祇又是谁？

Text.en: Say I believe ye about this Divinity business... then who was the first to become Divine?
Text.zh: 就当我信了你这套神性理论……那第一个成神的是谁？

Text.en: Logic suggests that the champion we saw in the murals was the first Divine being. <continue>The Mother Soul, the First Edict... these concepts are all part of this mystery. <continue>The gods were mere myths in my era. I wish I knew more... As it stands, we must continue our search. <continue>The truth of history may reveal itself in time.
Text.zh: 按逻辑推断，我们在壁画上看到的那个勇士，就是第一位神性存在。<continue>原初之魂、第一律令……这些概念全都指向同一个谜团。<continue>在我那个时代，诸神不过是传说而已。我真希望自己知道得更多……但如今，我们只能继续寻找答案。<continue>历史的真相，也许终有一天会浮出水面。

Text.en: I have worked with Corruption my entire life. It is a disordered, chaotic energy by its very nature. <continue>The piece of the Weapon I found in my era was capable of precipitating that chaos, leaving an ordered crystalline structure.
Text.zh: 我毕生都在与腐化打交道。从其本质来说，它是一种无序、混乱的能量。<continue>我在我那个时代找到的那块武器碎片，能够促使这种混乱发生，并留下有序的晶体结构。

Text.en: Corruption can turn into crystal?
Text.zh: 腐化能变成水晶？

Text.en: You're saying the Weapon turned Corruption to crystal?
Text.zh: 你是说那件武器把腐化变成了水晶？

Text.en: Corruption and crystal seem unlikely sisters. <continue>But you're saying one becomes the other?
Text.zh: 腐化跟水晶听着八竿子打不着。<continue>但你是说，一个能变成另一个？

Text.en: The Corruption turned to crystal?
Text.zh: 腐化变成水晶了？

Text.en: The <i>{rathin} turned to crystal?
Text.zh: <i>{腐化}变成了水晶？

Text.en: What are you sayin'? That the Corruption became a crystal?
Text.zh: 你说什么呢？腐化变成了水晶？

Text.en: I don't understand. Are you suggesting that the Weapon piece somehow purified the Corruption?
Text.zh: 我不明白。你的意思是，那武器碎片把腐化净化了？

Text.en: Somethin' as rotten as Corruption can turn into crystal?
Text.zh: 腐化这种烂到骨子里的东西，也能变成水晶？

Text.en: Essentially... yes. Corruption is... captured... and deposited in physical form. <continue>The energy still exists within, but in a different pattern that <i>{should} be safer.
Text.zh: 基本上……没错。就是把腐化……捕获……并以实体形态保存下来。<continue>其中的能量依然存在，只是排列方式变了，按理说<i>{应该}更安全。

Text.en: Just how dangerous is Corruption long-term?
Text.zh: 长期来看，腐化究竟有多危险？

Text.en: As dangerous as it is in the short-term, the long-term prospects are more devastating still. <continue>Unfortunately, it is a risk we must take if we are to complete our work.
Text.zh: 短期就已经够危险了，长期累积只会更糟。<continue>可惜的是，如果我们想完成任务，就必须承担这个风险。

Text.en: So even if we destroy the Beast, the exposure I'm enduring right now will eventually mean my own death. And yours too.
Text.zh: 所以……即便我们消灭了邪兽……我眼下承受的这些腐化，早晚会要了我的命，还有你的命。

Text.en: We know Corruption is dangerous... but <i>{how} dangerous?
Text.zh: 我们知道腐化很危险……可它到底<i>{有多}危险？

Text.en: As dangerous a force as any in the known world.
Text.zh: 危险至极，不亚于世间一切已知力量。

Text.en: So, the fact I've been practically bathing in the stuff... that spells nothing good for my future health prospects. <continue>Or yours, I assume?
Text.zh: 我差不多天天泡在这东西里……看来将来我这身体怕是没指望了？<continue>而且你也好不到哪儿去？

Text.en: This constant exposure to Corruption... even if you remove the tumors, that is not a cure, is it?
Text.zh: 持续暴露在腐化中……即便你能把长出来的瘤子切了，也没法根治，对吧？

Text.en: All medicine is a stall.
Text.zh: 世上所有的医术，说到底都是缓兵之计。

Text.en: So then, with enough time, Corruption will still be the death of me.
Text.zh: 所以，只要时间够长，腐化终究还是会要了我的命。

Text.en: We already know it's dangerous to be exposed to this level of Corruption. What does that mean for my future?
Text.zh: 我们早就知道暴露在这种程度的腐化很危险了。不过那对我的将来会有什么影响？

Text.en: Your future? There will be no future for any of us if we cannot purge this world of Corruption.
Text.zh: 你的将来？要是我们无法清除这个世界的腐化，谁都别想有将来。

Text.en: Sure, but let's say we do that – am I still going to die?
Text.zh: 当然。但假设我们成功了——我该不会还是要死吧？

Text.en: Even if we succeed in riddin' the world o' <i>{rathin}... are we doomed to die a painful death after all this exposure?
Text.zh: 就算真能把全世界的<i>{腐化}都清干净……我们受了这么久的侵蚀，是不是还会死得苦不堪言？

Text.en: You said before that Corruption could give me tumors and what not. So prolonged exposure to the stuff must be pretty dangerous, yeah?
Text.zh: 你之前说过，腐化会让我长瘤子什么的。所以长时间泡在这东西里头，肯定相当危险，对吧？

Text.en: The short-term effects of Corruption are obvious... but what of the long-term? <continue>Can we expect there'll be consequences to the amount of exposure we've endured?
Text.zh: 腐化的短期危害显而易见……可长期呢？<continue>我们接触了这么久，肯定会有什么后果吧？

Text.en: All this time spent among Corruption cannae be good for our bodies... or our souls.
Text.zh: 长时间泡在腐化里，恐怕对身子骨，对灵魂都没啥好处。

Text.en: Of course. Anyone who leaves this Ziggurat for any period of time is going to die someday. Tumors, mutations, madness... <continue>Unless... we complete my prior work, and discover the secret of immortality. <continue>That is still my eventual plan, of course.
Text.zh: 当然，任何离开这座塔庙的人，时日久了，都难逃一死。肿瘤、变异、疯癫……<continue>除非……我们能完成我之前的研究，找到永生的秘密。<continue>这依然是我最终的目标。

Text.en: Well... fingers crossed you figure it out then.
Text.zh: 好吧……那就祝你好运，希望你赶紧琢磨出来。

Text.en: Do we have any hope of restoring the world as it was before the Cataclysm?
Text.zh: 我们还有可能让这世界变回灾变之前的模样吗？

Text.en: As it was? No. At least, not entirely. But there is still hope for the future.
Text.zh: 变回去？不。至少不可能完全变回去。但以后可能有希望。

Text.en: Wraeclast moves from Cataclysm to Cataclysm. At this rate... there <i>{will} come a true end of days.
Text.zh: 瓦尔克拉斯是一场灾变接着一场灾变。照这么下去……真正的末日<i>{迟早}会来。

Text.en: I disagree with your characterisation. Wraeclast does not merely spiral downward.
Text.zh: 我不同意你的说法。瓦尔克拉斯绝没有一直在走下坡路。

Text.en: Is Wraeclast forever tainted by Corruption?
Text.zh: 瓦尔克拉斯是不是被腐化彻底污染了？

Text.en: It will not be the same as it was, if that is your question. But that does not mean there can be no recovery.
Text.zh: 如果你想问的是，还能不能变回原样，那确实不会了。但这不意味着它无法复苏。

Text.en: It is hard to envision... So much has changed...
Text.zh: 好难想象……变化太大了……

Text.en: Is Wraeclast ever going to be the same again?
Text.zh: 瓦尔克拉斯还能恢复当初的模样吗？

Text.en: No – it has been irrevocably altered. But such is the nature of progress, for good or ill. The only constant is change.
Text.zh: 不能——它已经被彻底改变了。但进步的本质就是如此，无论好坏。唯一不变的，只有变化本身。

Text.en: So, what, it's just a lost cause?
Text.zh: 所以怎么着，破罐子破摔了？

Text.en: Would I be here if it was?
Text.zh: 如果真是那样，我还会站在这儿吗？

Text.en: Even if we purge every last bit o' <i>{rathin}... there's no changin' what's happened.<continue>Is Wraeclast ever goin' to be safe again?
Text.zh: 就算把最后一丁点<i>{腐化}都清干净……已经发生的事也改变不了了。<continue>这瓦尔克拉斯还能有太平的日子？

Text.en: Safe? Wraeclast has never been 'safe'.
Text.zh: 太平？瓦尔克拉斯从来就没“太平”过。

Text.en: You know what I mean. Are things ever goin' to go back to normal?
Text.zh: 你知道我什么意思。这一切还能回到正轨吗？

Text.en: Not in the sense that you seem to mean. <continue>We are in a post-Cataclysm world, and nothing will be as it was before. But then, that has already been true for over a thousand years.
Text.zh: 恐怕没法回到你说的那种“正轨”。<continue>我们早就处在后灾变时代了，一切都跟从前不一样了。不过，这情况已经持续上千年了。

Text.en: We ever gonna back to the way things were? Or is this just how things are now?
Text.zh: 我们还能回到从前吗？还是这破日子就这样了？

Text.en: In what sense?
Text.zh: 你指哪方面？

Text.en: You know... all this Corruption business. Is Wraeclast just doomed to get worse and worse until it finally keels over?
Text.zh: 你知道的……就这些腐化的事儿。瓦尔克拉斯是不是只会越来越烂，直到最后彻底崩溃？

Text.en: We rage against an endless tide. Corruption, Cataclysms... these things have plagued Wraeclast since time immemorial. Is it destined to remain this way forever?
Text.zh: 我们对抗的是一股永无止境的洪流。腐化、灾变……这些东西自古以来一直困扰着瓦尔克拉斯。它是不是注定永远如此？

Text.en: Wraeclast has been my home a long time... and never has it looked worse for wear. <continue>Is there still hope for its future? Or – even in the event we cure Corruption – does it only get worse from here?
Text.zh: 我在瓦尔克拉斯扎根很久了……它从未像现在这样破败不堪。<continue>它的未来还有希望吗？还是说，就算我们消除了腐化，情况也只会越来越糟？

Text.en: Though the task seems endless, remember the course of history. <continue>This is the third Cataclysm we know of. In both previous cases, Wraeclast eventually healed. <continue>The true challenge is whether we - or humanity itself - can survive until 'eventually.'
Text.zh: 尽管这任务看似永无止境，但回头看看历史。<continue>这是我们已知的第三次灾变。前两次，瓦尔克拉斯最终都恢复了过来。<continue>真正的挑战在于，我们——或者说人类本身——能否撑到“最终恢复”来临。

Text.en: Does sanctifying Corruption make it safer than before? Or is it still a threat?
Text.zh: 圣化后的腐化，比原来更安全吗？或者说还是很危险？

Text.en: I find it hard to believe that turning Corruption into pretty little crystals suddenly makes it safe.
Text.zh: 很难相信，把腐化变成漂亮的小水晶，就能一下子把它变得无害。

Text.en: Sanctifying Corruption and destroying it seem like two different things. Is this Sanctified Corruption safe?
Text.zh: 圣化腐化和摧毁腐化，听起来像是两码事。这种神圣腐化真的安全吗？

Text.en: So Corruption can be sanctified... but it's still in there, right? Just trapped inside the crystal.
Text.zh: 这么说腐化可以被圣化……但它其实还在里头，对吧？只是被封在水晶里了。

Text.en: Yes. It has been... neutralised, for lack of a better word. But it has not ceased to exist.
Text.zh: 没错。它已经被……中和了，暂且用这个词吧。但它并没有消失。

Text.en: So then, is it safe?
Text.zh: 那它到底安不安全？

Text.en: We've already talked about the dangers o' bein' around <i>{rathin}... is that any different, now that it's been sanctified?
Text.zh: 我们之前已经聊过接触<i>{腐化}有多危险……现在这东西被圣化了，情况会有什么不一样吗？

Text.en: This Santicified Corruption is certainly easier on the eyes... but does that mean it's safe?
Text.zh: 这圣化后的腐化看着确实顺眼多了……可这就算安全了吗？

Text.en: Does the Corruption become benign once it's been sanctified?
Text.zh: 腐化被圣化之后，就变得无害了？

Text.en: It might look pretty, but I dinnae trust this Sanctified Corruption. <continue>The rot's still in there... even if we cannae see it.
Text.zh: 看着是挺漂亮，可我信不过圣化后的腐化。<continue>那腐烂的根子还在里头……只是看不见罢了。

Text.en: It still holds power, but it will no longer mutate, or drive men insane... My initial experiments were promising. <continue>I was hoping it could be a step forward for my people. <continue>In a way, perhaps it still can be. One day.
Text.zh: 它依然蕴含着力量，但不再会引发变异，也不会再让人发疯……我最初的实验结果颇为乐观。<continue>我原本希望这能为我们族群带来进步。<continue>某种意义上，也许有一天，它终究能做到。

Text.en: What is Sanctified Corruption?
Text.zh: 什么是神圣腐化？

Text.en: Please tell me... what exactly <i>{is} Sanctified Corruption?
Text.zh: 请告诉我……神圣腐化到底<i>{是}什么？

Text.en: This 'Sanctified Corruption', as you call it... what exactly is it?
Text.zh: 你说的这个“神圣腐化”……究竟是什么？

Text.en: Sanctified Corruption – what is it?
Text.zh: 神圣腐化——是什么？

Text.en: This <i>{rathin} what's been sanctified... what's that mean? How's it different than it was before?
Text.zh: <i>{腐化}被圣化了……这是什么意思？它跟以前有什么不同？

Text.en: Fancy term, 'Sanctified Corruption'... but what's it mean?
Text.zh: 神圣腐化，挺花哨的词儿……可它到底是什么意思？

Text.en: How does Sanctified Corruption differ from the normal kind? Aside from its crystalline appearance.
Text.zh: 除了那水晶似的外表，神圣腐化跟普通腐化有什么区别？

Text.en: What can ye tell me o' this Sanctified Corruption? What even is it?
Text.zh: 你能跟我讲讲这个神圣腐化吗？它到底是什么玩意儿？

Text.en: Ah, you're asking a question of great debate. <continue>We know that there are many kinds of energy in this world. Heat, wind, gravity, belief, flesh, souls... <continue>Corruption is... a morass. A chaotic vortex of energies, broken down into unpredictable madness. <continue>Sanctifying that Corruption involves bringing order to that chaos. Depositing it into motionless crystals...
Text.zh: 啊，你问了一个极具争议的问题。<continue>我们知道这世上有许多种能量。热、风、引力、信念、血肉、灵魂……<continue>腐化……是一团混沌。各种能量混杂的混乱漩涡，最终扭曲成不可预知的疯狂。<continue>将腐化圣化，就是将秩序带入混沌，将其沉淀为静止的晶体……

Text.en: That... was not part of the plan. Have we complicated matters?
Text.zh: 那……可不在计划之内。我们是不是把事情搞复杂了？

Text.en: A Lich! Aw, why did he have to fly away?
Text.zh: 一个巫妖！唉，他为什么非得飞走？

Text.en: Wretched Abyssals! We have unleashed a terror upon Wraeclast!
Text.zh: 该死的深渊魔物！我们给瓦尔克拉斯带来了灾厄！

Text.en: Something tells me we'll regret that.
Text.zh: 我有种不好的预感，我们会后悔的。

Text.en: Made a right mess o' this, haven't we?
Text.zh: 这下可真是搞砸了，对吧？

Text.en: Oh, shite... that was bad, wasn't it?
Text.zh: 糟了……这下糟了，对吧？

Text.en: Fate conspires against us. Have we made a grave mistake?
Text.zh: 命运在跟我们作对。我们是不是犯了个天大的错误？

Text.en: Another danger loosed upon the world. And by our hand, no less.
Text.zh: 又一个危险被释放出来了，而且还是我们亲手造成的。

Text.en: We have no choice but to press on, but let us do so with extreme caution...
Text.zh: 我们别无选择，只能继续前进，但必须万分小心……

Text.en: The gate is open! And it seems the leylines within the fortress are even more powerful than the ones outside...
Text.zh: 门开了！而且堡垒里的地脉能量，似乎比外面还要强得多……

Text.en: I will calibrate the Waygate to these resonances. Perhaps we can use this new power to our advantage...
Text.zh: 我会将脉门校准到这些共振频率上。或许这股新能量可以为我们所用……

Text.en: Wait... A strange flame burns in the distance...
Text.zh: 等等……远处有团奇怪的火焰在燃烧……

Text.en: A great flame awakes. How ominous!
Text.zh: 巨大的火焰苏醒了。真是不祥……

Text.en: I see a great flame, towering over the horizon...
Text.zh: 我看到一道巨大的火焰，高耸入云……

Text.en: Hold on... there's some kind of flame, spewing out into the air.
Text.zh: 等等……那边有道火焰，正喷向空中。

Text.en: You see that flame? It makes the tower look almost small.
Text.zh: 你看到那火焰了吗？连高塔都显得小了。

Text.en: I see somethin'... A great stinkin' pillar of flame, over yonder.
Text.zh: 我看见有东西……那边有一根臭气熏天的巨大火柱。

Text.en: A column of flame erupts from the monolith. What could that mean?
Text.zh: 巨塔喷出了一道火柱。这又意味着什么？

Text.en: There's an unnatural sort o' flame in the distance. Is that another consequence of our actions?
Text.zh: 远处那火焰看着不太对劲。不会又是咱们搞出来的吧？

Text.en: That flame... that tower. They must be what this mural depicts.
Text.zh: 那火焰……那座塔。那副壁画描绘的就是它们。

Text.en: The one surrounding the caged Lich?
Text.zh: 就困住巫妖的那个？

Text.en: Yes, yes, by the caged Lich. I remember.
Text.zh: 对对，困住巫妖的那个。我记得。

Text.en: I remember. <continue>And then... the Lich was freed only moments later.
Text.zh: 我记得。<continue>然后……没过多久那巫妖就被放出来了。

Text.en: We did. Didn't have much time to decipher it before that Lich awoke from his slumber.
Text.zh: 是有这么回事。我们还来不及解读，那巫妖就苏醒过来了。

Text.en: That we did. Guess we know its meanin' now.
Text.zh: 是啊。现在总算知道那是什么意思了。

Text.en: Sure did – right before that Lich popped the lock on his cage.
Text.zh: 没错——就在那个巫妖打开自己的牢笼之前。

Text.en: I recall. The one which adorned the walls of the Lich's prison.
Text.zh: 我记得。就是刻在巫妖监牢墙上的那个。

Text.en: Aye – how could I forget? 'Twas only moments later that we encountered that ruddy Lich.
Text.zh: 是啊——我怎么可能忘记？刚看见没一会儿，就撞上那该死的巫妖了。

Text.en: I have a bad feeling about this...
Text.zh: 我有一种不详的预感……

Text.en: You must reach that Monolith as soon as possible... I doubt whatever is about to happen will be good for us.
Text.zh: 你必须尽快赶到巨塔那里……我感觉接下来要发生的事，恐怕不会是什么好事。

Text.en: Name's Farrow. Some call me Runeseeker. But Farrow'll do fine. <continue>I'm far from home. But <i>{these} lands are even more... unwelcoming than I expected.
Text.zh: 我叫法罗，也有人叫我符文猎手。不过叫法罗就行。<continue>我背井离乡来到这儿。不过<i>{这}地方比我想象中……还要不友善。

Text.en: Greetings, Farrow. I wish I could welcome you to Ogham in better times. What brought you here?
Text.zh: 幸会，法罗。要是赶在好年景，我就能在欧甘好好招待你了。你怎么会来这儿？

Text.en: Unwelcoming? Really? The dead seemed quite happy to welcome you. <continue>So why <i>{are} you so far from home, Farrow?
Text.zh: 不友善？真的吗？那些死人看着挺欢迎你的啊。<continue>你<i>{怎么会}跑到离家这么远的地方，法罗？

Text.en: This is not unique to Ogham. Wraeclast grows more hostile by the day. <continue>Now then... what brings you here, Master Farrow?
Text.zh: 不光是欧甘。瓦尔克拉斯也一天比一天危险了。<continue>那么……法罗先生，你为什么会来这里？

Text.en: They weren't always this way. Now what brings you to Ogham?
Text.zh: 这些地方以前可不这样。那你为什么会来欧甘？

Text.en: You're not the only one far from home. Hopin' you're here for a happier reason than I am.
Text.zh: 远离故乡的人可不止你一个。希望你不像我一样，是因为什么倒霉事才来到这里的。

Text.en: Can bloody well say that again. So, what brings you here?
Text.zh: 这话说得一点没错，所以你来这儿做什么？

Text.en: Yes... a shadow hangs over Ogham, blanketing all that once was. <continue>What is your reason for visiting?
Text.zh: 是啊……阴影正笼罩着欧甘，把曾经的一切都吞没了。<continue>你来这里又是为了什么？

Text.en: Aye... couldnae have picked a worse time to visit, for you and me both. <continue>What brings ye this way, anyway?
Text.zh: 是啊……咱俩都赶上了最糟的时候。<continue>话说回来，你怎么会来这儿？

Text.en: Runes... inscriptions... etched into remnants from ancient times. For whatever reason, they burn brighter in all this bedlam. <continue>Now, a smith I may have been back home, but I'm no clansman. No warrior. <continue>Perhaps... we can aid each other.
Text.zh: 符文……铭文……刻在远古遗物上的那些。不知怎的，在这片乱局里，它们反而更活跃了。<continue>我在老家算是个铁匠，但我不是部族的人，也不是战士。<continue>或许……咱们可以互相帮衬。

Text.en: A fellow smith? Heh... I've a feeling we'll get along. <continue>So... I do the fighting, you do the finding?
Text.zh: 同行啊？嘿……那咱俩应该合得来。<continue>所以……我负责打架，你负责找东西？

Text.en: I take all the risk, you bask in the rewards, I take it?
Text.zh: 我拼命冒险，你坐享其成，是这样吧？

Text.en: Let me guess: I assume the risk. You reap the rewards.
Text.zh: 让我猜猜：我负责冒险，你负责拿好处。

Text.en: Hah! No, no. We'll share those in equal parts, of course.
Text.zh: 哈！不不不。有好处我们当然会平分。

Text.en: I will overcome the danger... while you seek your runes?
Text.zh: 我去冒生命危险……而你去找你的符文？

Text.en: Need someone to watch your back while you go 'runeseeking', as you called it?
Text.zh: 需要我保护你，好让你安心“寻符文”？

Text.en: Need me to play bodyguard while you go diggin' 'round in the dirt?
Text.zh: 需要我给你当保镖，好让你安心刨土？

Text.en: I take it you need protection while you conduct your research.
Text.zh: 看来你做研究的时候需要保护。

Text.en: Think I know how this goes. I keep the beasties off your back while you find what ye're lookin' for?
Text.zh: 我好像知道套路了。我替你挡那些怪物，你找你的东西，对吧？

Text.en: That's about right. But you'll also reap rewards from the knowledge we uncover...
Text.zh: 差不多就这意思。不过，咱们找到的那些知识，也能让你得到好处……

Text.en: Ahh... a formidable inscription. <continue>But... we can add to it. Combinations of inscriptions react to the will of the inscriber. <continue>Look here. My tome contains many runeshapes. Try your hand at one.
Text.zh: 啊……这铭文可真够厉害的！<continue>但……咱们还能再添些东西。不同的铭文组合，会对刻符者的意志产生反应。<continue>看这儿，我这法典里有好多符文图样，你来试试手。

Text.en: That was impressive. The Verisium in that remnant was mighty powerful. You did well!<continue>Now, I've travelled to Ogham because I'm researching the origins of my craft. <continue>Some Ezomytes would say runeshapes were simply brought to us by the First Ones... but I reckon there's more to the tale.
Text.zh: 真了不起。那残骸里的维金能量确实强得惊人。干得漂亮！<continue>我不远万里来到欧甘，是想追寻我这门手艺的源头。<continue>有些艾兹麦人说，符文是初生之子赐予我们的……可我总觉得，这事没那么简单。

Text.en: The First Ones guide our souls... I can't speak to the rest. I admit, I am curious to learn more.
Text.zh: 初生之子指引我们的灵魂……至于其他的，我也说不好。不过我确实挺想弄明白的。

Text.en: First Ones this, First Ones that... you Ezomytes are rather dull.
Text.zh: 满嘴都是初生之子……你们艾兹麦人可真有意思。

Text.en: Oh, come now. We mean no harm.
Text.zh: 噢，别这么说，我们没有恶意。

Text.en: Mmm. There usually is. Just one reason why the Maraketh have tale-women: it helps to separate fact from fiction. <continue>Now. What would you have me do?
Text.zh: 嗯，通常都是如此。所以马拉克斯才有说书人：为了把事实和传说分开。<continue>那么，你想让我做什么？

Text.en: The First Ones gave us many things. Who's to say runes weren't among them? <continue>Wouldn't mind knowing the truth myself.
Text.zh: 初生之子赐予了我们很多东西。谁敢说符文不是其中之一？<continue>说实话，我自己也想知道真相。

Text.en: Well, only one way to figure out what's real and what's not. What can I do in that regard?
Text.zh: 嗯，只有一种办法能分辨真假。那我能帮上什么忙呢？

Text.en: You know what they say: don't believe everythin' you hear. <continue>So... where do I fit in to all this?
Text.zh: 老话说得好，耳听为虚。<continue>那么……我在其中能做些什么？

Text.en: Mysteries abound in Ezomyr. How might I assist you in learning the answer?
Text.zh: 艾兹麦到处都是谜团。我该如何协助你找出答案？

Text.en: Wish I could tell ye one way or the other. Just so happens my memory's not as sharp as it once was. <continue>Now... what else d'ye need from me?
Text.zh: 真希望我能给你个明确的答案。只是我这记性，不如从前那么灵光了。<continue>那么……你还需要我做些什么？

Text.en: Help me in my research, and I'll do what I can to aid in your... undertakings. <continue>Seek out more remnants like this one. It'll be worth your while.
Text.zh: 来帮我一起做研究。你那些……什么大业，我也尽力帮忙。<continue>多去找找类似的遗物，你肯定不亏！

Text.en: These inscriptions... I've never laid eyes on them before. Give me a moment.
Text.zh: 这铭文……我从未见过。给我点时间。

Text.en: [REDACTED]
Text.zh: [已编辑]

Text.en: Well, now we <i>{should} be able to locate more runestones like these.
Text.zh: 好了，这下咱们<i>{应该}能找到更多这种符文石了。

Text.en: [NOAUDIO] What do we do with this... runestone?
Text.zh: 这块……符文石，我们要怎么处理？

Text.en: [NOAUDIO] Well then, what do we do with it?
Text.zh: 好吧，这东西到底怎么用？

Text.en: [DNT] [UNUSED]
Text.zh: 那还用说，当然是拿你的凿子凿它啊。

Text.en: The energy... it was immense. What happened?
Text.zh: 那能量……好强。刚刚发生了什么？

Text.en: How illuminating. What just happened?
Text.zh: 真是……开眼界了。刚才是怎么回事？

Text.en: Illuminating... Heh! Good one.
Text.zh: 哈哈！开眼界！这双关不错。

Text.en: Hmph. Answer the question...
Text.zh: 哼。别打岔，回答问题……

Text.en: Such power! What did we just witness?
Text.zh: 好惊人的力量。刚刚我们看到的到底是什么？

Text.en: What was all that?
Text.zh: 刚才那是什么情况？

Text.en: What was that? Weren't expectin' such a big reaction.
Text.zh: 那是什么？真没想到会有这么大反应。

Text.en: Don't mean to alarm you, mate, but... you're <i>{glowin'.}
Text.zh: 我不是想吓你，兄弟，可你……在<i>{发光}。

Text.en: The power of these runes is plain to see. What just happened?
Text.zh: 这些符文的力量一目了然。刚才发生了什么？

Text.en: Well now... I see why ye're so taken by these runes.
Text.zh: 嗯……我算是明白你为什么这么痴迷于这些符文了。

Text.en: The energy from the runestones seems to have... <i>{moved on}. What's more, I'm picking up others in the region. <continue>If we find more like this, then I might be able to pinpoint where the energy got to.
Text.zh: 符文石的能量好像……<i>{转移了}。而且，我能感觉到这区域还有几块。<continue>再多找几块这样的符文石，说不定我就能定位那股能量跑去哪儿了！

Text.en: My tool is lighting up good and proper here. Go on. See if you can find the source.
Text.zh: 我这工具在这边反应可强烈了。来吧，看看能不能找到源头！

Text.en: I'll see what I can do.
Text.zh: 我尽力。

Text.en: Come out, come out, wherever you are... rune-thing.
Text.zh: 出来，出来，你在哪儿啊……符文那玩意儿。

Text.en: You're quite the character, you know. Endearing, actually.
Text.zh: 你这人可真有意思。还挺讨人喜欢的。

Text.en: What? Don't... say that again.
Text.zh: 什么？别……别再说这种话了。

Text.en: As you wish.
Text.zh: 没问题。

Text.en: On it.
Text.zh: 我这就去。

Text.en: Oh, we'll find it, alright.
Text.zh: 哦，我们肯定能找到。

Text.en: You got it, mate.
Text.zh: 包在我身上，伙计。

Text.en: Let us hope so.
Text.zh: 希望如此。

Text.en: Aye. As ye wish.
Text.zh: 好，就照你说的。

Text.en: Another one... and the same result. The energy has moved on. <continue>On the bright side, it seems there's only one more signature left in the region. Seek it out!
Text.zh: 又一块……结果还是一样，能量又转移了。<continue>好消息是，这一带好像只剩最后一块了。去把它找出来！

Text.en: What's there to talk about? Only one more to find. Get on with it!
Text.zh: 还有什么好说的？就剩一块了，赶紧的吧！

Text.en: Remember, we're in this together. Watch your tone.
Text.zh: 别忘了，咱俩可是一伙的，注意你的语气。

Text.en: Perhaps you'd like to try that again. With less <i>{impudence} this time.
Text.zh: 也许你该重说一遍，这次别那么<i>{无礼}。

Text.en: Watch it, Farrow. I'm here because I choose to be... and that could change at any moment.
Text.zh: 说话注意点，法罗。我是自愿来帮忙的……所以随时都有可能改变心意。

Text.en: Last I checked, Farrow, we were equals. I don't take orders from you.
Text.zh: 法罗，我没记错的话我们是平等的。我可不会听你的命令。

Text.en: Easy, mate. We're on the same side here, remember?
Text.zh: 别激动，伙计。咱们是一伙儿的，你还记得吧？

Text.en: Ah... sorry. I get a bit... overexcited about runes. In your own time, of course.
Text.zh: 啊，是……抱歉。我一碰到符文就容易激动……你按自己的节奏来就好。

Text.en: [NOAUDIO] That's better.
Text.zh: 这还差不多。

Text.en: [NOAUDIO] That's more like it.
Text.zh: 这才像话。

Text.en: Watch your tongue, Farrow, lest it forget its place in your mouth!
Text.zh: 说话注意点，法罗，小心舌头搬了家！

Text.en: You really are... Oh. You're serious. <continue>Forgive me. Just... when you have a chance.
Text.zh: 你可真……哦……你是认真的。<continue>是我失礼了。等你方便的时候再去吧。

Text.en: Come now. Such insolence is unbecoming.
Text.zh: 够了。这般无礼，有失体面。

Text.en: Now, boy... mind the words you choose.
Text.zh: 小子……说话注意点。

Text.en: Got a bit excited. Sorry about that.
Text.zh: 有点太激动了，抱歉。

Text.en: We've done it. The energies have all coalesced. The signature is far greater now.
Text.zh: 成功了！能量都汇聚到一起了。这信号可比之前强多了。

Text.en: Where is it coming from?
Text.zh: 那源头在哪儿？

Text.en: If it's <i>{so} great, where is it then?
Text.zh: 要是真有<i>{那么}强，那它到底在哪儿呢？

Text.en: Can you pinpoint where it is?
Text.zh: 能确定位置吗？

Text.en: Where's it coming from?
Text.zh: 从哪儿传来的？

Text.en: And? Where is it?
Text.zh: 然后呢？从哪儿来的？

Text.en: Where's this signature comin' from?
Text.zh: 这信号是从哪儿来的？

Text.en: Can we follow this signature?
Text.zh: 我们能追踪这个信号吗？

Text.en: From where does this signature stem?
Text.zh: 这个信号源自何处？

Text.en: Those ancient woods... That's where it's leading us.
Text.zh: 那片古老的森林……它正把我们引向那里。

Text.en: Back to the Grelwood then.
Text.zh: 那就回韧木森林吧。

Text.en: The Grelwood? Weren't we just there? <continue>Ugh. I better not be running in circles for nothing.
Text.zh: 韧木森林？我们不是刚从那儿回来吗？<continue>呃。我可不想白费力气瞎转悠。

Text.en: I did not think I'd be returning to the Grelwood so soon...
Text.zh: 没想到这么快就要回韧木森林……

Text.en: The Grelwood... <continue>Always good to return home... even if it's not what it used to be.
Text.zh: 韧木森林啊……<continue>能回家总是好的……哪怕它已不似从前。

Text.en: Guess we're not done with the Grelwood just yet.
Text.zh: 看来我们跟韧木森林的纠葛还没完结。

Text.en: Back to the Grelwood we go!
Text.zh: 回韧木森林！

Text.en: The Grelwood calls me home once more.
Text.zh: 韧木森林再次召唤我回家。

Text.en: Let's hope the Grelwood receives us with a touch more hospitality this time. <continue>Not that I'm holdin' my breath, mind.
Text.zh: 但愿这次韧木森林能对我们友善一点。<continue>不过说实话，我也没抱什么希望。

Text.en: The signature seems to be coming from <i>{this} location.
Text.zh: 这个信号看起来就是从<i>{这儿}传出来的。

Text.en: The caravan should be able to take us there.
Text.zh: 商队应该能把我们送过去。

Text.en: Then we should go there. The caravan can take us.
Text.zh: 那我们就去那里。商队可以载我们过去。

Text.en: Good. The <i>{akhara} can take us there.
Text.zh: 好。<i>{阿卡拉}能带我们过去。

Text.en: Then that's where we'll go. We can use the caravan.
Text.zh: 那就去那里吧。我们可以坐商队的篷车去。

Text.en: Guess we know where we're headed. The caravan can take us there.
Text.zh: 看来知道该往哪儿走了。商队能带我们过去。

Text.en: Now we're gettin' somewhere! Surely Asala won't mind if we uh, take the caravan?
Text.zh: 这下总算有进展了！艾萨拉应该不会介意我们用下商队的篷车吧？

Text.en: Then we know our destination. With any luck, the caravan will take us there.
Text.zh: 那目的地已经确定了。运气好的话，商队的篷车能载我们过去。

Text.en: Then we'd better investigate. It's a ways away, mind, but we can take the caravan.
Text.zh: 那我们最好去查探一下。不过路可不近，我们可以乘商队的篷车过去。

Text.en: Hmm... this is my best guess. Hope there's no damn bugs out there.
Text.zh: 嗯……八成就是那儿了。希望那儿别冒出该死的虫子。

Text.en: Everything out here seems to teem with life. I wouldn't bet on no bugs.
Text.zh: 这鬼地方，什么都能活。我可不敢说没虫子。

Text.en: Ha! Keep hoping.
Text.zh: 哈！继续做梦吧。

Text.en: I fear you're in for some disappointment.
Text.zh: 恐怕你要失望了。

Text.en: I wouldn't hold my breath.
Text.zh: 我可不抱希望。

Text.en: Wouldn't count on it. Jungle's crawlin' with 'em.
Text.zh: 别想了，林子里到处都是那玩意。

Text.en: No bugs? Ha! You'll be lucky.
Text.zh: 没虫子？哈！想得美。

Text.en: We shall see.
Text.zh: 走着瞧吧。

Text.en: Dinnae get yer hopes up, lad. I'd hate to see ye disappointed.
Text.zh: 别抱太大希望，小子。我可不想看见你失望。

Text.en: The inscriptions on the tree roots match those on the runestones we found.
Text.zh: 树干上的铭文，跟咱们找到的符文石上的铭文一模一样。

Text.en: They weren't there on the Tree of Souls earlier.
Text.zh: 之前灵魂之树上可没这些东西。

Text.en: That's odd. Someone's been carving up the Tree of Souls.
Text.zh: 真奇怪。有人在灵魂之树上乱刻。

Text.en: These inscriptions... they are new.
Text.zh: 这些铭文……是新的。

Text.en: These inscriptions are new. I'm sure of it.
Text.zh: 这些铭文是新的。我敢肯定。

Text.en: Reckon we'd have noticed if these were here before...
Text.zh: 要是之前就有，我们早该注意到了……

Text.en: I'm not the most observant bloke, but... these inscriptions weren't here before, were they?
Text.zh: 我虽然不算特别细心，可……这些铭文之前没有吧？

Text.en: I don't recall seeing these inscriptions on the tree before.
Text.zh: 我不记得之前在树上见过这些铭文。

Text.en: But where did they come from? I didnae see <i>{these} inscriptions the first time.
Text.zh: 可它们是从哪儿来的？我头一回来可没看见<i>{这些}铭文。

Text.en: Reminds me of what I've seen in the stars, more than what I've seen at the forge. <continue>Now, if this doesn't work, I'll look a right bloody fool...
Text.zh: 这让我想起天上的星象，熔炉旁可见不着这光景。<continue>要是这招不灵，我可就丢人丢大发了……

Text.en: Seems you are no bloody fool after all...
Text.zh: 看来你倒是没丢人……

Text.en: Seems you know what you're doin' after all.
Text.zh: 看来你还真有点本事。

Text.en: A Runeseeker after all, eh?
Text.zh: 真不愧是符文猎手，嗯？

Text.en: Fancy trick! I liked that one.
Text.zh: 真花哨啊！这招我喜欢。

Text.en: That was... quite spectacular!
Text.zh: 这……可真够壮观的。

Text.en: Most impressive.
Text.zh: 真厉害。

Text.en: Not bad, eh?
Text.zh: 真不赖啊。

Text.en: What in the Greatwolf's name have you done to this sacred tree?!
Text.zh: 巨狼在上，你对这颗圣树做了什么？！

Text.en: Nothing new, I assure you.
Text.zh: 是原本就该有的，我向你保证。

Text.en: What kinda magic is this?
Text.zh: 这是什么魔法？

Text.en: What foul magic have you brought upon this already cursed tree?
Text.zh: 你对这棵被诅咒的树施了什么妖法？！

Text.en: No magic of mine, I assure you.
Text.zh: 我向你保证，绝不是我施的。

Text.en: Now... best we go take a look.
Text.zh: 好了……我们最好进去看看。

Text.en: This tome... hmm. <i>{Some} of it is legible to me. <continue>'Volume One: The Runes of Aldur.' <continue>'Within the warm embrace of Aldur's teachings, he, the first Runefather, carved a future for Middengard, to liberate it from the biting frost.' <continue>Middengard? This is all... Kalguuran?
Text.zh: 这本古籍……嗯。<i>{有些}地方我还是能看懂的。<continue>“《第一卷：奥杜尔之符文》。”<continue>“在奥杜尔教义的温暖怀抱中，首位符文之父为米登加德开辟了前路，将它从刺骨的严寒中解放出来。”<continue>米登加德？这全都来自……卡古兰？

Text.en: Does this mean runes may not be of Ezomyte origin?
Text.zh: 那岂不是说，符文可能不是艾兹麦人发明的？

Text.en: That would suggest these runes are not Ezomyte at all.
Text.zh: 这意味着这些符文根本不属于艾兹麦。

Text.en: So... does this mean runes originate from Kalguur, not Ezomyr?
Text.zh: 所以……这意味着符文起源于卡古兰，而不是艾兹麦？

Text.en: Sure sounds that way, doesn't it? Then... that means these runes <i>{aren't} Ezomyte.
Text.zh: 听着像是这么回事，对吧？那……就是说这些符文<i>{不属于}艾兹麦？

Text.en: What does this mean? That the runes are not Ezomyte at all?
Text.zh: 这意味着什么？符文根本不是艾兹麦人发明的？

Text.en: Sounds like ye should've ventured to Kalguur, not Ezomyr.
Text.zh: 听起来你该去卡古兰，而不是艾兹麦。

Text.en: Uh oh. Are you about to have an identity crisis?
Text.zh: 哎呀，你是不是要面临身份危机了？

Text.en: This uh, doesn't shatter your entire worldview, does it?
Text.zh: 这呃……不会把你整个世界观都打碎了吧？

Text.en: I did suspect there was more to it. That's why I'm here. But yes, maybe runes are <i>{not} an Ezomyte invention. I need to learn more about this 'Aldur'.
Text.zh: 我早就怀疑事情没那么简单，所以我才会来这儿。不过没错，也许符文<i>{并非}艾兹麦的发明。我必须多了解一下这位“奥杜尔”。

Text.en: I'll study this text further, but yes... that's what this implies. I need to learn more about this 'Aldur'.
Text.zh: 我还得再深入研究一下这段文字，但是的……字里行间就是这个意思。我得再多了解了解这个“奥杜尔”。

Text.en: Ah! Another tome! Now let's see here... <continue>'Volume Two: The Fractured Middengard.' <continue>'After a prosperous reign, Aldur, the first King of Kalguur, joined the eternal hearth.' <continue>'Middengard quickly fractured, and her people fell into unruly despair and violence.' <continue>'To unite them anew, Aldur's Runefathers banded together to smith their greatest and most ambitious design.'
Text.zh: 啊！又一本古籍！让我看看……<continue>“《第二卷：破碎的米登加德》。”<continue>“在一段繁荣的统治之后，卡古兰的首位国王奥杜尔，归于永恒的炉火。”<continue>“米登加德迅速分崩离析，她的子民陷入了无序的绝望与暴力。”<continue>“为了重新将他们团结起来，奥杜尔的符文之父们联手，锻造了他们最伟大、最雄心勃勃的杰作。”

Text.en: I'd give anything to see such artisanship with my own eyes.
Text.zh: 只要能亲眼看看这样的技艺，让我拿什么换都行。

Text.en: Interesting. I wonder what this design might have been?
Text.zh: 有意思。我很好奇这个杰作是什么。

Text.en: You and me both.
Text.zh: 我也是。

Text.en: Whatever that is, it sounds powerful. I'd quite like to see it.
Text.zh: 不管那是什么，听起来好厉害。我倒真想瞧瞧。

Text.en: Did they now? I'd like to see such a thing.
Text.zh: 是吗？那我可真想见识见识。

Text.en: Let's make it happen, eh?
Text.zh: 那我们就该去瞧瞧，对吧？

Text.en: Sounds like something we ought to get our hands on.
Text.zh: 听起来像是我们该弄到手的东西。

Text.en: Well now... what could that mean?
Text.zh: 哎哟……这又是什么意思？

Text.en: Most ambitious, eh? I like the sound o' that!
Text.zh: 最雄心勃勃的杰作，嗯？这说法我喜欢！

Text.en: Ambition is a double-edged sword – but perhaps we should seek out this artefact all the same.
Text.zh: 雄心是把双刃剑——不过我们也许确实该去找一找这件神器。

Text.en: We need to finish the tale. I must know more about these... Runefathers.
Text.zh: 我们得把这个故事看完。我必须了解更多这些……符文之父的情况。

Text.en: A third tome! What do we have this time? <continue>'Volume Three: The Triskelion.' <continue>'To repair their broken unity, the Runefathers forged the Triskelion Flame. A monumental achievement of exquisite craftsmanship.' <continue>'The Runefather's toured Middengard with their creation. It demanded hope. It whispered a promise. It healed the people's aching hearts left in the absence of Aldur.' <continue>'The Triskelion was returned to the capital, where the Runefathers kept it, as a bastion of harmony for all Middengard.'
Text.zh: 第三本古籍！这次又有什么？<continue>“《第三卷：三耀之火》。”<continue>“为了修补破碎的团结，符文之父们锻造了三耀之火。一件技艺精湛、无与伦比的丰碑巨作。”<continue>“符文之父们携此杰作巡游米登加德。它索求希望，它低语承诺，它治愈了人们因奥杜尔离世而倍感伤痛的心。”<continue>“三耀之火被送回首都，由符文之父们守护，成为米登加德全境和谐的堡垒。”

Text.en: Incredible. Imagine inspiring an entire continent with work of the forge.
Text.zh: 太不可思议了，居然用熔炉之作鼓舞整个大陆。

Text.en: It must have been truly special, to be able to do all that they said it could.
Text.zh: 居然能做到他们说的那些事，这东西肯定非同一般。

Text.en: It's difficult to comprehend such a thing.
Text.zh: 这东西真有点难以想象。

Text.en: It 'demanded' hope? Sounds like a bit of a threat.
Text.zh: 它“索求”希望？听着怎么像是威胁。

Text.en: 'Healed the people's achin' hearts'? How could it do all that?
Text.zh: “治愈了人们倍感伤痛的心”？它怎么做到的？

Text.en: It 'whispered a promise'? What <i>{are} they on about?
Text.zh: 它“低语承诺”？他们<i>{到底}在说什么鬼东西？

Text.en: It was an interesting way to put it, I'll give you that.
Text.zh: 我得承认，这说法挺有意思。

Text.en: Hard to believe it could really do all they said it could.
Text.zh: 很难相信它真能做到他们说的那些事。

Text.en: It sounds spectacular indeed... assuming it does all they say it does.
Text.zh: 听起来确实非同小可……前提是它真能做到那些事。

Text.en: Sounds too good to be true, if I'm honest.
Text.zh: 老实说，听着好得有点不真实。

Text.en: I feel we are close to finishing this story. Let's keep on the trail!
Text.zh: 我觉得这故事快要收尾了。咱们接着找！

Text.en: Ah! Another volume! <continue>'Volume Four: The Rise of Cadigan.' <continue>'The Runefathers' rule was challenged by a claimant heir of Aldur. On his first appeal, his words fell on deaf ears. On his second appeal, he bore weapons instead.' <continue>'Cadigan took his throne as the next true King of Kalguur, and promised glory to all.' <continue>'When red adorned the sky across the sea, fear gripped Middengard once more. Cadigan assembled his finest heroes, armed them with the Triskelion Flame, and sent them to those distant shores.' <continue>'"Redeem those lands! Expand our shores! Show every Kalguuran that our prosperity is unending!"' <continue>'And so we sail on to our unknown expedition, with adventure in our hearts and fear in our throats.'
Text.zh: 啊！又找到一卷！<continue>“《第四卷：卡迪甘的崛起》。”<continue>“符文之父们的统治，受到了一个人的挑战，他自称是奥杜尔的继承人。第一次请愿时，无人理会他的言辞。第二次，他拿起了武器。”<continue>“卡迪甘登上王位，成为卡古兰新一任正统国王，并承诺给所有人荣耀。”<continue>“当血红染遍大海彼端的天空，恐惧再次攫住了米登加德。卡迪甘召集他最精锐的英雄，以三耀之火武装他们，将他们派往遥远的彼岸。”<continue>“去救赎那片土地！扩张我们的疆域！让每个卡古兰人都知道，我们的繁荣永无尽头！”<continue>“于是，我们扬帆驶向未知的远征，心中怀揣着冒险，喉头噙着恐惧。”

Text.en: Red skies... must've been the Vaal Cataclysm that brought them to Wraeclast. That confirms the Kalguurans were here in truly ancient times.
Text.zh: 血红天空……一定是瓦尔灾变把他们带到了瓦尔克拉斯。这就证实了卡古兰人确实在远古时代就来了这里。

Text.en: What they describe... it sounds like the Vaal Cataclysm. That would suggest the Kalguurans came here a long, <i>{long} time ago.
Text.zh: 他们描述的……听着像瓦尔灾变。那说明卡古兰人是在很久<i>{很久}以前就来过这里。

Text.en: That it does. I wonder what became of the Runefathers...
Text.zh: 确实。不知道那些符文之父后来怎么样了……

Text.en: So the Vaal Cataclysm brought them to Wraeclast... This Cadigan sounds like quite the over-achiever.
Text.zh: 所以是瓦尔灾变把他们引来了瓦尔克拉斯……这个卡迪甘听着还真是有抱负呢。

Text.en: So they sailed <i>{towards} the Cataclysm? At least, I think that's what those red skies are s'posed to be. <continue>Ol' King Cadigan doesn't sound too sharp.
Text.zh: 所以他们<i>{朝着}灾变的方向起航了？至少，我觉得那些血红天空应该就是这个意思。<continue>这老卡迪甘国王听着脑子不大灵光。

Text.en: That's one way to put it.
Text.zh: 倒也可以这么说。

Text.en: This tome must be truly ancient. The red sky they describe can only have been the Vaal Cataclysm.
Text.zh: 这本古籍肯定非常古老。他们描述的血红天空，只能是瓦尔灾变。

Text.en: So these fools saw the Vaal Cataclysm on the horizon... and sailed <i>{toward} it?
Text.zh: 所以这帮傻子看见地平线上的瓦尔灾变……然后就扬帆<i>{冲过去}了？

Text.en: Red sky? Do they mean... the Vaal Cataclysm? But that would mean this tome's a couple o' thousand years old!
Text.zh: 血红天空？他们是说……瓦尔灾变？那这古籍岂不是有几千年历史了！

Text.en: Could those red skies be the Vaal Cataclysm? That would make this expedition o' theirs truly ancient.
Text.zh: 血红天空会不会是瓦尔灾变？那他们的远征队可真够古老的。

Text.en: Well... what a story these tomes have made so far. <continue>There's a fair amount to mull over. Some day I'd like to learn more about these adventurers from Kalguur.
Text.zh: 嗯……目前来说，这几本古籍讲的故事可真够精彩的。<continue>有不少值得琢磨的地方。以后有机会，我倒想多了解了解这些来自卡古兰的冒险者。

Text.en: Let's see what I can make with that liquid Verisium.
Text.zh: 让我看看能用这液体维金做点什么……

Text.en: That lot you found ought to come in handy too...
Text.zh: 你找来的那堆东西应该也能派上用场了……

Text.en: Here, take this. You'll need it.
Text.zh: 来，拿着。你能用上的。

Text.en: Now, we can do something quite powerful with this anvil. A sort of... runeforging. Let's give it a try, eh?
Text.zh: 好了，我们可以用这块铁砧做点相当厉害的事。算是……符文锻造。咱们来试试，好吗？

Text.en: Well... would you look at that?
Text.zh: 嚯……你瞧这个！

Text.en: I'm sure we can push all this even further still. See? I told you, runeseeking is worth the risk!
Text.zh: 我肯定咱们还能更进一步……看吧？我跟你说什么来着？找符文这事，值了！

Text.en: I never doubted you, Farrow.
Text.zh: 我从没怀疑过你，法罗。

Text.en: Yes, yes. I suppose I see your point.
Text.zh: 行吧行吧，我懂你的意思了。

Text.en: So it would seem.
Text.zh: 看起来确实如此。

Text.en: Never said it wasn't.
Text.zh: 我又没说不是。

Text.en: I see what you mean.
Text.zh: 我明白你的意思了。

Text.en: No kiddin'!
Text.zh: 还用说嘛！

Text.en: It certainly is.
Text.zh: 确实。

Text.en: Aye, lad. So it seems.
Text.zh: 是啊，小子。看起来确实如此。

Text.en: [DNT] [UNUSED] Find more Verisium and let's put this to good use, eh?
Text.zh: 多找点维金，咱们好好利用起来，嗯？

Text.en: [DNT] [UNUSED] This method may prove quite powerful, indeed!
Text.zh: 这法子说不定真能行！

Text.en: [DNT] [UNUSED] You've still got what we found back there, yeah?
Text.zh: 咱们找到的那些东西，你还留着呢吧？

Text.en: [DNT] [UNUSED] You're quite forgetful, you know...
Text.zh: 你这记性可够差的……

Text.en: [DNT] [UNUSED] This is some serious business here. Let's get to runesmithing!
Text.zh: 这可是正经事。咱们开始打造符文吧！

Text.en: [DNT] [UNUSED] Oh yes! Take a look at that!
Text.zh: 哦耶！瞧瞧这个！

Text.en: [DNT] [UNUSED] Cor blimey... my mind is ablaze with how we could apply this knowledge further. Oh yes!
Text.zh: 天呐……为了想怎么继续利用这些知识，我的脑子都快烧坏了。没错！

Text.en: I'm just as excited as you are! There's work to be done here.
Text.zh: 我跟你一样兴奋！这里还有很多事要做。

Text.en: Ugh. Must you always be so enthusiastic?
Text.zh: 呃。你就不能别这么激动吗？

Text.en: Has anyone ever told you that you're rather... excitable?
Text.zh: 有人跟你说过你特别容易……兴奋吗？

Text.en: Well, you certainly don't lack for enthusiasm.
Text.zh: 嗯，你这股热乎劲儿倒是够足的。

Text.en: Excited, are we? Can't blame you, really.
Text.zh: 挺兴奋啊？倒也不能怪你。

Text.en: It's got potential, don't it?
Text.zh: 挺有搞头的，对吧？

Text.en: Exciting indeed.
Text.zh: 确实让人兴奋。

Text.en: It's damn impressive, I'll give ye that.
Text.zh: 我得承认，这确实挺了不起。

Text.en: Hmmm... you're onto something.
Text.zh: 嗯……你找到点门道了。

Text.en: Not too shabby, eh? If you find the resources, I can make it happen!
Text.zh: 不赖吧，嗯？只要你找到材料，我就能把它做出来！

Text.en: I admire your enthusiasm, Farrow.
Text.zh: 你这股劲头，我真是佩服，法罗。

Text.en: Your obsession with all this is certainly useful... if annoying.
Text.zh: 你对这些东西的执着确实能帮上忙……虽然有点招人烦。

Text.en: Your enthusiasm is infectious, Master Farrow.
Text.zh: 你的热情太有感染力了，法罗先生。

Text.en: 'Not too shabby' indeed.
Text.zh: “不赖”可太谦虚了。

Text.en: Gotta hand it to you, Farrow – you know what you're doin'.
Text.zh: 法罗，我得承认，你确实有本事。

Text.en: 'Not too shabby'? Try bloody brilliant!
Text.zh: “不赖”？这该叫“牛爆了”！

Text.en: You have a remarkable zest for life, Farrow.
Text.zh: 法罗，你对生活还真是充满热情。

Text.en: Ye're dead useful, Farrow. And so's all this rune business.
Text.zh: 你可真是帮了大忙了，法罗。这些符文也是。

Text.en: Absolutely.
Text.zh: 没问题。

Text.en: I didn't think it was possible for you to be more excited than last time... but here we are.
Text.zh: 我原以为你上次已经够激动了……结果现在更夸张。

Text.en: Patience, Master Farrow.
Text.zh: 耐心点，法罗先生。

Text.en: Yes, yes, I'm getting to it.
Text.zh: 好了好了，我这不正弄着呢。

Text.en: Heh. Excitable as always.
Text.zh: 呵。还是一样容易激动。

Text.en: Yeah alright, mate.
Text.zh: 行了，伙计。

Text.en: And we will.
Text.zh: 我们会的。

Text.en: Aye, lad. Keep yer hair on.
Text.zh: 行了，小子，别激动。

Text.en: Lord Dannig?
Text.zh: 丹宁大人？

Text.en: Just 'Dannig', if you will. I'm no knight. Far from it.
Text.zh: 叫我丹宁就行。我可不是骑士，差得远呢。

Text.en: Right. I must enquire... On our adventures, I learned a thing or two about a man named 'Aldur'.
Text.zh: 对。我得请教一下……我这一路冒险，知道了点关于“奥杜尔”的事。
