Text.en: Oh you have, have you? Aldur was very important to our history and people, but not really a figure known at all here on Wraeclast. <continue>How curious you should learn of him...
Text.zh: 哦，是吗？奥杜尔对我们的历史和族人至关重要，但在瓦尔克拉斯，知道他的人可不多。<continue>你居然听说过他，真是奇了……

Text.en: Did he <i>{really} invent runesmithing as we know it?
Text.zh: <i>{真}是他发明了我们所熟知的符文锻造术吗？

Text.en: He certainly did. There is no doubt that we owe everything to him and the original Runefathers. Without them, we wouldn't be where we are today.
Text.zh: 当然。我们今天的一切都归功于他和最初的符文之父们。没有他们，就不会有我们今天的成就。

Text.en: And I suppose this would have all happened... in the considerable past?
Text.zh: 那这一切……都发生在很遥远的过去吧？

Text.en: Of course. Aldur existed long before Ezomytes even breathed their first breath.
Text.zh: 当然。奥杜尔生活的年代，艾兹麦人还没出世呢。

Text.en: That's... astonishing.
Text.zh: 真是惊人。

Text.en: It seems you two are getting along quite perfectly.
Text.zh: 看来你们两个相处得不错嘛。

Text.en: Oh no. Now there's two of you.
Text.zh: 哦，不。现在有两个你了。

Text.en: It seems there's much you can learn from one another.
Text.zh: 看来你们能从彼此身上学到不少东西。

Text.en: I can't decide which of you seems more enthused by this boring conversation.
Text.zh: 我都分不出你俩谁对这无聊话题更来劲。

Text.en: Well don't you two make a fine pair o' runesmiths?
Text.zh: 你们俩还真像一对符文工匠。

Text.en: Must be nice havin' someone else to talk to about all this rune <i>{stuff}.
Text.zh: 能有个人陪你聊这些符文<i>{玩意儿}，感觉不错吧。

Text.en: Always a welcome sight, to see two like-minded people coming together.
Text.zh: 看到两个志趣相投的人走到一起，总会让人感到欣慰。

Text.en: Pleased to see you lads gettin' along.
Text.zh: 很高兴看到你俩处得来。

Text.en: In a further tome I've studied... it mentioned Aldur's death and how it deeply affected Middengard.
Text.zh: 我研读的另一本古籍里……提到了奥杜尔的离世以及这件事对米登加德造成的巨大影响。

Text.en: It threw our people into disarray. Brothers in arms became brothers at war. It was a pitiful time.
Text.zh: 那让我们的子民陷入了混乱。手足兄弟反目成仇。那段岁月真是惨不忍睹。

Text.en: And the remaining Runefathers sought to fix that?
Text.zh: 所以剩下的符文之父们就想办法补救？

Text.en: Correct. It's a tale that warms the hearts of all Kalguurans.
Text.zh: 没错。这个故事温暖了所有卡古兰人的心。

Text.en: One other thing... this tome seemed to imply that Verisium wasn't as abundant as the Runefathers had thought.
Text.zh: 还有一件事……这本古籍似乎在暗示，维金并不像符文之父们以为的那样丰富。

Text.en: They stripped the land of all the Verisium they could find. That led to... some other unfavourable developments down the line.
Text.zh: 这片土地上的维金被他们挖了个干净。这在后来导致了……一些不好的后果。

Text.en: That sounds... ominous.
Text.zh: 听起来……很不详啊。

Text.en: That's awfully vague.
Text.zh: 这说得也太含糊了。

Text.en: You are being vague with me. I do not like that.
Text.zh: 你在跟我含糊其辞……我不喜欢这样。

Text.en: Ugh. Typical.
Text.zh: 呃，果然又是这样。

Text.en: That's what happens when you mine the land for all it's worth.
Text.zh: 把地挖个底朝天，就会变成这样。

Text.en: Wouldn't be the first folk to make that mistake.
Text.zh: 他们可不是头一批犯这种错的人。

Text.en: Rather short-sighted of them.
Text.zh: 他们未免也太短视了。

Text.en: Whenever mankind delves too deep, it's always nature which pays the price.
Text.zh: 每当人类挖得太深，付出代价的总是自然。

Text.en: The third tome I found mentions an ultimate design... the Triskelion Flame.
Text.zh: 我找到的第三本古籍，提到了一个终极设计……三耀之火。

Text.en: That, Farrow... is the very reason we are even here at all.
Text.zh: 法罗……我们来这儿，正是为了它。

Text.en: I couldn't quite understand... is the Triskelion a tool for good, or evil?
Text.zh: 我有点没弄明白……三耀之火到底是好还是坏？

Text.en: Most importantly, it was a tool for <i>{hope}. A symbol of Aldur's gifts to our people. <continue>But... it reminds me of a saying my forebears passed down... 'The Will of the Inscriber may bear love in his heart, but the Will of the User may tear us apart'. <continue>Though the Triskelion Flame brought our countrymen together in our time of need... there are those who believe it was used for ill thereafter.
Text.zh: 最重要的是，它是 <i>{希望}的器物，是奥杜尔赐予我们族人的信物。<continue>但……这让我想起祖辈传下的一句话：“……刻符者之心，或存仁爱；执符者之念，能致崩坏。”<continue>尽管三耀之火在我们最需要的时候团结了同胞……但也有人认为，它后来被用在了不义之处。

Text.en: The folly of man, hard at work.
Text.zh: 人类的愚蠢，总是在作祟。

Text.en: What? How <i>{shocking}. That <i>{never} happens.
Text.zh: 什么？真是太<i>{意外}了。这种事<i>{从来没}发生过。

Text.en: Power is nothing if not corrupting.
Text.zh: 力量这东西，最容易腐化人。

Text.en: So it's not just Wraeclast that's full of power-hungry halfwits.
Text.zh: 所以不是只有瓦尔克拉斯才有追逐力量的蠢货。

Text.en: Wouldn't exactly surprise me.
Text.zh: 倒也不意外。

Text.en: Sounds about right.
Text.zh: 听着很正常。

Text.en: Such is the way of things.
Text.zh: 世事向来如此。

Text.en: There's not a powerful artefact out there that hasn't been used for ill by someone.
Text.zh: 世上的强大神器，没有一件不曾被人用来作恶。

Text.en: The final tome... it mentioned King Cadigan.
Text.zh: 最后那本古籍……提到了卡迪甘国王。

Text.en: Please, I request that we... move on from this particular topic.
Text.zh: 拜托……我不想再谈这个话题了。

Text.en: What do you mean?
Text.zh: 什么意思？

Text.en: I will not be discussing the King at any depth with you. Nor anyone, for that matter.
Text.zh: 我不想跟你深入讨论这位国王。跟谁都不想。

Text.en: Well... fine. I learned enough to know there's nothing good about him.
Text.zh: 好吧……行。我知道的也够多了，他反正不是什么好人。

Text.en: Farrow... this conversation is now on thin ice.
Text.zh: 法罗……这个话题，到此为止。

Text.en: Just tell me one thing... why would he send an expedition here with the Triskelion, if it was his most prized possession?
Text.zh: 你就告诉我……既然三耀之火是他最珍贵的宝物，那他为什么要派远征队带着它来这里？

Text.en: Some believe it was to colonise Wraeclast and thus empower Kalguur... others believe it was for... more personal reasons.
Text.zh: 有人认为是为了殖民瓦尔克拉斯，以此壮大卡古兰……也有人觉得是出于……更私人的原因。

Text.en: And what do <i>{you} believe?
Text.zh: 那<i>{你自己}怎么看？

Text.en: I believe... that we are done here.
Text.zh: 我觉得……就聊到这儿吧。

Text.en: You're lucky you got that far, Farrow.
Text.zh: 你能问到这份上，已经算走运了，法罗……

Text.en: The one time I want someone to keep talking... oh well.
Text.zh: 难得我想让别人继续说下去……算了。

Text.en: It may be best to let this one lie, Master Farrow.
Text.zh: 法罗先生，这件事或许还是别去追究了。

Text.en: You Kalguurans and your King... <continue>Give it up, Farrow. He won't move on the matter.
Text.zh: 你们卡古兰人和你们的国王……<continue>算了吧，法罗。这事他不会松口的。

Text.en: Would you look at that? You <i>{almost} got him to say summat.
Text.zh: 你瞧瞧？你<i>{差点}就撬开他的嘴了。

Text.en: Trust me, Farrow. He ain't gonna budge on this one.
Text.zh: 相信我，法罗。这事他绝不会让步。

Text.en: I'm sorry, Farrow. The Kalguurans seem quite firm on this matter.
Text.zh: 抱歉，法罗。卡古兰人在这件事上态度很坚决。

Text.en: Honestly, lad, be glad you even got that much out of him.
Text.zh: 说真的，小子，能从他嘴里撬出那么多话，你就知足吧。

Text.en: What's that you have there?
Text.zh: 你手里拿的是什么？

Text.en: What's that you have there?
Text.zh: 你手里拿的是什么？

Text.en: Whatever it is, it's incomplete. But it does have a rune on it.
Text.zh: 不管是什么，都不完整。不过，上面倒是有个符文。

Text.en: One piece of a map. Don't know where the other pieces are, but this one has a rune-thing on it.
Text.zh: 一块地图碎片。不知道其他碎片在哪儿，不过这块上有个符文模样的东西。

Text.en: It looks to be part of a map. But there's something else here too... a rune of some description.
Text.zh: 看起来是地图的一部分。但上面还有别的东西……像是某种符文。

Text.en: It's a map... or at least part of one. And there's a rune on it. Thought you might know more.
Text.zh: 是张地图……至少是其中一部分。而且上面有个符文。我想着你可能知道些什么。

Text.en: Not sure... we'd have to find the rest of it first. But this piece has some kind o' rune on it, so I thought you'd want to take a look.
Text.zh: 不确定……我们得先找到其余的碎片。不过这块上有个符文，所以我觉得你大概想瞧瞧。

Text.en: Dunno. But there's a rune on it, so figured you'd want a look.
Text.zh: 不知道。但上面有个符文，我就想着你大概会想看看。

Text.en: A map. Incomplete as it may be, it bears a rune... thought it might be of interest to you.
Text.zh: 一张地图。虽然残缺不全，但上面有个符文……我想你应该会感兴趣。

Text.en: Appears to be part of a map. I've no idea where it leads, but it is marked by a rune o' some sort.
Text.zh: 像是地图的一块。我也不知道这地图是去哪儿的，但上面标着个什么符文。

Text.en: How interesting...
Text.zh: 有意思……

Text.en: I'll hold onto this for for now. Be sure to bring further pieces my way, should you find any.
Text.zh: 我可以先帮你收着。你要是再找到别的碎片，记得拿来给我。

Text.en: That makes two... I'm afraid we'll still need more to make sense of this.
Text.zh: 这是第二块了……恐怕还得再找到更多，才能弄明白。

Text.en: There's three. Only one remains... best you find that last piece.
Text.zh: 第三块了，就差最后一块……你最好把最后那块也找到。

Text.en: Well, well... would you look at that.
Text.zh: 好家伙……看看这个。

Text.en: Go on, then. What does it depict?
Text.zh: 继续说。这上面画的是什么？

Text.en: I haven't the faintest idea.
Text.zh: 我也没有什么头绪。

Text.en: Well, I guess I just wasted my time then.
Text.zh: 好吧，那我这趟算是白忙活了。

Text.en: So I did all this for nothing, then? Someone give me better news or one of you might lose their head.
Text.zh: 所以我白忙活了一场？谁能给我点好消息，不然我怕有人的脑袋保不住了。

Text.en: Bah. Then it was all for nothing.
Text.zh: 呸。那我白忙活了。

Text.en: So I've wasted my time then?
Text.zh: 所以我白浪费时间了？

Text.en: Really? None at all?
Text.zh: 真的？一点头绪都没有？

Text.en: You're kiddin'. What'd I do all this runnin' about for then?
Text.zh: 开什么玩笑？那我跑来跑去是图什么？

Text.en: Most unfortunate, given the effort we went to in procuring them.
Text.zh: 太遗憾了，我们花了那么大力气才弄到手。

Text.en: Ach! You sure?
Text.zh: 啊！你确定？

Text.en: Not so fast. I know who <i>{will} have an idea. Take it to Rhodri. He may know where this leads.
Text.zh: 别急。我知道谁<i>{会}有头绪。拿去给罗德里看看，他也许知道这线索指向何处。

Text.en: Not so fast. I know who <i>{will} have an idea. Take it to Makoru. She may know where this leads.
Text.zh: 别急。我知道谁<i>{会}有头绪。拿去给马克鲁看看，她也许知道这线索指向何处。

Text.en: What have you got there?
Text.zh: 你找到什么了？

Text.en: I believe it's a map. Any idea where this might be?
Text.zh: 我觉得这是张地图。你知道这是哪里吗？

Text.en: A map. Please tell me you know where it leads.
Text.zh: 一张地图。拜托告诉我你知道它通向哪里。

Text.en: A map – one we went to great lengths to reassemble. <continue>Have you any idea where this might be?
Text.zh: 一张地图——我们千辛万苦才拼好的。<continue>你知道这是哪儿吗？

Text.en: It's a map. Hoping you might know where it leads.
Text.zh: 一张地图。希望你能知道它通向哪里。

Text.en: Got a map for you to look at. Recognise this at all?
Text.zh: 有张地图要给你瞧瞧。你认得这个吗？

Text.en: We've got ourselves a map. Not that I know where it leads.
Text.zh: 我们弄到了一张地图。虽然我不知道它通向哪里。

Text.en: A map – reassembled piece by piece. <continue>Now that it's whole again, it's my hope that you'll be able to read it.
Text.zh: 一张地图——一块块重新拼起来的。<continue>如今它又完整了，希望你能看懂。

Text.en: Take a look at this map, would ye? Any thoughts where this might be?
Text.zh: 帮忙看看这张地图，行吗？知道这地方是哪儿吗？

Text.en: Hmm. I have an idea or two. But only one way to find out...
Text.zh: 嗯……我倒是有几个想法。不过只有试了才知道……

Text.en: This is my best guess. And I'm not usually wrong. Shall we take a look?
Text.zh: 这是我能给出的最靠谱的推测了。我通常不会犯错。咱们去瞧瞧？

Text.en: [NOAUDIO] What's this then eh?
Text.zh: 这是什么？

Text.en: Any idea where this might be?
Text.zh: 你知道这是哪里吗？

Text.en: A map. Please tell me you know where it leads.
Text.zh: 一张地图。拜托告诉我你知道它通向哪里。

Text.en: A map – one we went to great lengths to reassemble. <continue>Have you any idea where this might be?
Text.zh: 一张地图——我们千辛万苦才拼好的。<continue>你知道这是哪儿吗？

Text.en: It's a map. Hoping you might know where it leads.
Text.zh: 一张地图。希望你能知道它通向哪里。

Text.en: Got a map for you to look at. Recognise this at all?
Text.zh: 有张地图要给你瞧瞧。你认得这个吗？

Text.en: We've got ourselves a map. Not that I know where it leads.
Text.zh: 我们弄到了一张地图。虽然我不知道它通向哪里。

Text.en: A map – reassembled piece by piece. <continue>Now that it's whole again, it's my hope you'll be able to read it.
Text.zh: 一张地图——一块块重新拼起来的。<continue>如今它又完整了，希望你能看懂。

Text.en: Take a look at this map, would ye? Any thoughts where this might be?
Text.zh: 帮忙看看这张地图，行吗？知道这地方是哪儿吗？

Text.en: [NOAUDIO] I ain't just a ship cook, and I ain't <i>{just} a pretty face neither!
Text.zh: 我可不只是船上的厨子，也不<i>{光}靠脸吃饭！

Text.en: [NOAUDIO] Accounting for curvature... give or take a little nudge here and there... yeah. About there I reckon. Wanna take a look?
Text.zh: 算上曲度……再稍微调整一下……嗯，差不多就那儿。想过去看看吗？

Text.en: Ah, it feels good to be out and about.
Text.zh: 我跟你说……出来走走，感觉真好。

Text.en: Nothing like a breeze of adventure to fill your lungs, eh?
Text.zh: 没什么比得上冒险途中的凉风，是吧？

Text.en: Quite right! Quite right, indeed.
Text.zh: 说得对！太对了。

Text.en: Now, what do we have here...
Text.zh: 现在，咱们来看看这是……

Text.en: I hoped you could tell <i>{me.}
Text.zh: 我还指望你告诉<i>{我}呢。

Text.en: That's what you're here to tell me.
Text.zh: 这该由你来告诉我吧？

Text.en: I was wondering the same. Let us think about this.
Text.zh: 我也正纳闷呢。我们好好琢磨琢磨吧。

Text.en: Enlighten me, Kalguuran.
Text.zh: 给我指点一下吧，卡古兰人。

Text.en: Precisely what I'd like to know.
Text.zh: 我也正想知道呢。

Text.en: Aye. That's what <i>{I'm} wonderin'.
Text.zh: 嗯。<i>{我}也想弄明白呢。

Text.en: Cut to the chase, mate.
Text.zh: 别卖关子了，伙计。

Text.en: Haven't the foggiest. What d'ye make of all this?
Text.zh: 我是一点头绪都没有。你怎么看？

Text.en: Makeshift lodgings... excavation markers... Yes. This looks like Gwennen's handiwork.<continue>But, she didn't finish what she started here. How odd.
Text.zh: 临时住处……挖掘标记……没错。这看着像是桂温妮的手笔。<continue>但她在这儿居然没干完就走了。真奇怪。

Text.en: What stopped her?
Text.zh: 是什么让她停了手？

Text.en: Oh my, is she dead? Where's her body?
Text.zh: 哦天哪，她死了？她的尸体呢？

Text.en: Ha! You're bloody unhinged.
Text.zh: 哈哈！你真是神经兮兮的。

Text.en: We must remain hopeful. Perhaps she just became distracted.
Text.zh: 我们得乐观点，也许她只是被什么事缠住了。

Text.en: What do you think became of Gwennen?
Text.zh: 你觉得桂温妮出什么事了？

Text.en: Where do you think she is now?
Text.zh: 你觉得她现在会在哪儿？

Text.en: Seems she ain't here to speak to why.
Text.zh: 看来她不在，没法解释原委了。

Text.en: Left without the treasure... <continue>Not a good sign.
Text.zh: 宝藏都没拿就走了……<continue>这可不是什么好兆头。

Text.en: Not one to leave a job half-done, then?
Text.zh: 她可不是那种做事半途而废的人，对吧？

Text.en: I'm sure she had her reasons to cut and run. There's all manner of monstrosities out here! <continue>In any case, it seems she left us some clues as to what's in this area. <continue>When you're ready, let's see what we can unearth. Place the first explosive and we'll get started.
Text.zh: 她肯定有不得不撒腿就跑的理由。这鬼地方什么妖魔鬼怪都有！<continue>无论如何，她似乎给咱们留了些线索，告诉我们这里有什么。<continue>准备好了咱们就挖开瞧瞧。放上第一包炸药，咱们就开工。

Text.en: You made that look easy, I dare say! <continue>You're well on your way to being a qualified archaeologist at this rate.
Text.zh: 容我说一句，你这活干得也太麻利了吧！<continue>照这个速度，你很快就能成为合格的考古学家了。

Text.en: Seems a risky occupation...
Text.zh: 这行当听着可真危险……

Text.en: The explosions were quite delightful, I must admit...
Text.zh: 老实说，这些爆炸相当带劲……

Text.en: You are quite the charmer. But yes, my heart is still racing!
Text.zh: 你可真是讨人喜欢。不过我的心都快跳出来了！

Text.en: It was quite loud... but enjoyable.
Text.zh: 声响很大……不过很过瘾。

Text.en: I'd prefer a more peaceful line of work.
Text.zh: 我宁愿干点更安稳的行当。

Text.en: Let's do that again sometime!
Text.zh: 找机会再来一次吧！

Text.en: Cheers, Dannig. I had... a blast.
Text.zh: 谢了，丹宁。我玩得……心潮澎湃。

Text.en: Done plenty o' diggin' in my time. Never like <i>{that}.
Text.zh: 我这辈子挖过不少东西。但从来没见过<i>{这阵仗}。

Text.en: Now... best you go take a look inside. I suspect it might be worth your while.
Text.zh: 行了……你最好进去看看。我琢磨着应该值得你跑一趟。

Text.en: We'll wait here and... uh...
Text.zh: 我们在这儿等着，呃……

Text.en: Oversee the situation?
Text.zh: 在这儿盯着？

Text.en: Yeah! That's it.
Text.zh: 对！就这么着。

Text.en: Well, I hope the bounty was worth the risk. I must say, it usually is...
Text.zh: 行，但愿这趟没白冒险。要我说，通常都不亏……

Text.en: It was. We've got plenty to discuss back in Kingsmarch.
Text.zh: 当然不亏。等回到君锋镇，我们可有得聊了。

Text.en: So be it. Let's regroup then. Well done, team!
Text.zh: 那就这么着。咱们先集合一下。干得漂亮，诸位！

Text.en: We have all the pieces of the broken Triskelion Flame. I still believe the Triskelion is the only way we're going to make it into that crater.
Text.zh: 三耀之火的碎片我们全都收集齐了。我还是觉得，要进那个陨石坑，只能靠它了。

Text.en: Is there any hope, if it is broken?
Text.zh: 都碎成这样了，还有指望吗？

Text.en: Very optimistic of you, considering it's broken.
Text.zh: 都碎成这样了，你倒是挺乐观。

Text.en: You are rather <i>{hopeful}... given the state of the Flame.
Text.zh: 三耀之火都这惨状了，你倒还挺有……<i>{信心}。

Text.en: But you said it yourself – it's broken. Unless you think you can fix it?
Text.zh: 可你自己也说了——它碎了。除非你觉得你能修好它？

Text.en: Can you fix it somehow? Piece it back together, like?
Text.zh: 你能修好它？把它拼回去？

Text.en: How're we gonna do that if it's broken? <continue>Unless... is there some hope of fixin' it?
Text.zh: 都碎了，还怎么搞？<continue>除非……还能修好？

Text.en: You seem remarkably hopeful, given the circumstances.
Text.zh: 就这情况，你倒是乐观得可以。

Text.en: Have ye any hope of fixin' it?
Text.zh: 你还指望能修好它？

Text.en: Yes. I have hope. And not only that... I have <i>{help}. <continue>It's our time to shine, Farrow.
Text.zh: 有。我心里有底。不单有底……我还有<i>{帮手}。<continue>法罗，咱俩露脸的时候到了。

Text.en: Uh... excuse me?
Text.zh: 呃……你说我？

Text.en: You and I... are going to rebuild the Triskelion Flame. Right here. At the forge.
Text.zh: 你和我……咱俩要在这里，在这座熔炉前，把三耀之火重铸出来。

Text.en: <i>{What?} How the bloomin' heck are we gonna do that?
Text.zh: <i>{什么？} 咱俩怎么可能做得到？

Text.en: Well, the same way our ancient Runefathers did, I suspect. With passion! Dedication! Belief! And of course, skill.
Text.zh: 那些远古的符文之父怎么做，咱们就怎么做。凭热血。凭专注。凭信念。当然，还有手艺。

Text.en: I worry you are overestimating my abilities, but... ah, who am I kidding? I'm in. Let's see what we can do.
Text.zh: 恐怕你有点高估咱俩了，不过……啊，我还犹豫啥？算我一个。试试看能做成什么样。

Text.en: The Triskelion... is reforged!
Text.zh: 三耀之火……重铸了！

Text.en: Hah! Look at us! Aldur's very own flame burns within our hearth!
Text.zh: 哈！瞧瞧咱俩！奥杜尔的神火，在咱俩的炉膛里烧起来了！

Text.en: Well done, both of you. It is a remarkable achievement... but now what do we do?
Text.zh: 干得好，二位。这可太了不起了……可接下来呢？

Text.en: Yes, yes, we're all <i>{very} excited. Now what?
Text.zh: 行了行了，大家都<i>{很}激动。那之后怎么办？

Text.en: You should be proud – the both of you. Now, what is our next move?
Text.zh: 你们该为自己骄傲——二位都是。可下一步该怎么走？

Text.en: I must admit, I'm impressed. Wasn't sure you had it in you. <continue>But now that it's reassembled... what do we do with it?
Text.zh: 不得不说，我心悦诚服。本来还怕你不行呢。<continue>可既然拼好了……那我们该拿它怎么办？

Text.en: You must be over the moon! But what do we do now?
Text.zh: 你怕是高兴坏了吧！可接下来该怎么办？

Text.en: Blimey! Wouldn't have thought it was possible if I hadn't seen it with me own eyes. <continue>Still though... now what?
Text.zh: 天呐！要不是亲眼瞧见，打死我也不敢信。<continue>可话说回来……然后呢？

Text.en: A spectacular achievement... though we've little time to celebrate. As we speak, your King draws ever closer.
Text.zh: 太了不起了……不过我们可没工夫庆祝。就在这会儿，你们那位国王可就要来了。

Text.en: Yes, well, on that subject...
Text.zh: 是啊，说到这事……

Text.en: Hate to cut short the celebrations, but what do we do now?
Text.zh: 我本不想扫兴，可接下来该怎么办？

Text.en: We've a rather... unorthodox idea.
Text.zh: 我们有个……不太寻常的想法。

Text.en: I'm listening...
Text.zh: 我听着呢……

Text.en: Go on then. Out with it.
Text.zh: 接着说，别卖关子了。

Text.en: Continue.
Text.zh: 继续说。

Text.en: Here we go...
Text.zh: 那就说吧……

Text.en: Do you now?
Text.zh: 你有想法了？

Text.en: Ugh, don't keep me waitin'! What is it?
Text.zh: 那就别让我干等着！到底是什么？

Text.en: Go on.
Text.zh: 请讲。

Text.en: Oh, aye? And what would that be?
Text.zh: 哦，是吗？什么想法？

Text.en: We think you ought to... wear it to the crater.
Text.zh: 我们觉得你应该……把它背到那个陨石坑去。

Text.en: Wear it? How? It's huge!
Text.zh: 背着它？怎么背？这玩意儿大得离谱！

Text.en: What? Have you lost your mind?
Text.zh: 什么？你疯了吧？

Text.en: That cannot be your grand plan. <continue>Consider my size, and then consider the size of the Flame!
Text.zh: 这不会就是你的大计划吧。<continue>先看看我的体型，再想想那三耀之火有多大。

Text.en: <i>{That's} your big idea? I couldn't carry that if there were three of me.
Text.zh: <i>{这}就是你的妙计？就算有三个我，也搬不动那玩意儿。

Text.en: You what? How am I s'posed to do that?
Text.zh: 你说什么？我怎么可能办得到？

Text.en: We talkin' about the same Flame here? Thing's bloody massive!
Text.zh: 我们说的是同一个三耀之火吗？那玩意儿可大得吓人！

Text.en: I fear you may be overestimating my strength.
Text.zh: 恐怕你高估了我的力气。

Text.en: Come now, lads... I may be a Druid, but I'm no pack mule.
Text.zh: 得了吧，伙计们……我确实是德鲁伊，但不是驮货的牲口。

Text.en: The only way would be to strap it to your back. And trust me, we'll fit to you the best way we can. It'll be as optimal as possible.
Text.zh: 唯一的法子就是把它绑在你背上。放心，我们会尽量绑得贴身点，怎么舒服怎么来。

Text.en: Then, you'll be able to reach the crater. <i>{And...} the Verisium. Well, you get the picture.
Text.zh: 然后你就能去陨石坑了。<i>{还有……}那些维金。你明白的吧？

Text.en: Be careful. There's no shame in running if it all turns to shite.
Text.zh: 路上小心。真要出事了，逃跑也不丢人。

Text.en: We'll be watching... and waiting. Good luck.
Text.zh: 我们会在这边看着……等你回来。祝你好运。

Text.en: These ancient remnants... we've nothing like them back home. They're quite the sight, don't you think?
Text.zh: 这些远古的遗物……我们老家可没有。真是一道奇景，你说呢？

Text.en: They are impressive, absolutely. But what causes them to reveal themselves now?
Text.zh: 确实很惊人。可它们为什么偏偏这时候冒出来？

Text.en: They're fine, I suppose. But why do they only reveal themselves <i>{now?}
Text.zh: 还算不错吧。不过为什么偏偏<i>{现在}才出现？

Text.en: Indeed. But there's one thing I don't understand... <continue>If they are so ancient, why weren't they visible until recently?
Text.zh: 确实。但有一件事我不明白……<continue>如果它们真这么古老，为什么直到最近才出现？

Text.en: It's certainly strange, to see them appear where once there was nothing. Why couldn't we see them before?
Text.zh: 明明之前什么都没有，现在突然冒出来，确实有点怪。为什么以前看不到？

Text.en: No doubt they're impressive. But... why can we see 'em now, when we couldn't before?
Text.zh: 确实很惊人。但……为什么现在能看到，以前却不见它们的踪迹？

Text.en: Sure, but where'd they come from? One minute there's nothin'. The next, there they are.
Text.zh: 对，可它们是从哪而来的？前一秒还什么都没有，下一秒就冒出来了。

Text.en: They certainly are. But why have they only revealed themselves now?
Text.zh: 确实是奇景。但为什么偏偏现在才显现？

Text.en: I'm less concerned by their appearance than how they came to be.
Text.zh: 比起它们长什么样，我更在意它们是怎么出现的。

Text.en: Perhaps they always were here... and we only see them now because the world's gone to shite. I can't be sure of the answer. <continue>Either way... I'm glad to see them.
Text.zh: 也许它们一直都在……只是眼下世道乱了，我们才能瞧见。这我说不准。<continue>不管怎样……能见到它们，我就高兴。

Text.en: Our inscriptions go way back to the beginning of Ezomyte history. <continue>Pay no mind to commonfolk babble. They'd have you believe the First Ones bore us from mud and slime. Load of rubbish. <continue>I know runic inscriptions like the back of my hand. But of late... I've begun seeing them in the night skies.
Text.zh: 咱们的铭文可以说是历史悠久，从艾兹麦人一出现就有了。<continue>别听那些市井传言胡说八道。他们非说咱们是初生之子用泥巴和黏液捏出来的。真是一派胡言。<continue>我对符文铭文那可是了如指掌。可最近……我居然在夜空中看见了它们。

Text.en: Not all that is said about the First Ones is meant to be taken so literally.
Text.zh: 关于初生之子的传说，也不是什么都要按字面意思理解。

Text.en: A convenient excuse...
Text.zh: 这像是随手编造的托词……

Text.en: Regardless... are you saying you've seen the shape of these inscriptions in the stars?
Text.zh: 先不说这个……你是说你在群星里看到了这些铭文的形状？

Text.en: Oh dear. I knew you were... unwell. You're seeing things? In the stars?
Text.zh: 哦，老天。我早就觉得你……不大正常。你看到了东西？在群星里？

Text.en: As in... you're seeing their shape in the constellations?
Text.zh: 你是说……你从群星里看到了它们的形状？

Text.en: Mind what you say about the First Ones. Maybe they didn't 'bear us from mud and slime,' but they still gave us much to be grateful for.
Text.zh: 说话当心点，别乱说初生之子的不是。也许他们确实不是用泥巴和黏液把我们捏出来的，可他们留给我们的恩赐，仍值得感激。

Text.en: Did they? Or were they just a bunch of mythical animals?
Text.zh: 是吗？或者说它们不过是神话中的动物？

Text.en: Why would you think such a thing?
Text.zh: 你怎么会这么想？

Text.en: I've seen how tales can grow in the telling. Have you ever seen a First One yourself?
Text.zh: 我亲眼见识过传说是如何越传越离谱的。你亲眼见过初生之子吗？

Text.en: Fine, believe what you like – or don't, in this case. <continue>But what is this you're saying about runes? You've seen their shape in the stars?
Text.zh: 行，爱信不信——现在看来是不信。<continue>但你刚才说的符文是怎么回事？你在群星中看到了它们的形状？

Text.en: You see their shape in the stars?
Text.zh: 你在群星中看到了它们的形状？

Text.en: How d'ya mean? Like, you see their shape in the stars?
Text.zh: 什么意思？你是说在群星里看到了它们的形状？

Text.en: You've seen these inscriptions in the stars themselves?
Text.zh: 你在群星本身之中看到了这些铭文？

Text.en: Ye've been seein' their shape in the stars?
Text.zh: 你在群星里看到了它们的形状？

Text.en: That's right. At first I thought I was just enjoying too much faeleaf, but... even abstaining from the pipe didn't change what I saw. <continue>Runeshapes, in the stars, clear as the Greatwolf's howl.
Text.zh: 没错。起初我以为是自己抽菲叶抽多了，可……后来我把烟戒了，还是能看见。<continue>符文的形状……就在星星里，清楚得跟巨狼的嚎叫似的。

Text.en: The Greatwolf's howl shouldn't be different to any other, if what you say about the First Ones is true.
Text.zh: 要是你关于初生之子的说法是真的，那巨狼的嗥叫应该跟普通的狼没区别才对。

Text.en: Heh. It's a figure of speech, m'lady.
Text.zh: 哈。那就是个比喻，女士。

Text.en: What do you think it means, seeing runeshapes in the stars above?
Text.zh: 在群星中看见符文形状，你觉得这意味着什么？

Text.en: And you think there's some significance to seeing runes in the night sky? <continue>Assuming you're <i>{not} just hallucinating, that is.
Text.zh: 在夜空中看见符文，你觉得这有什么深意？<continue>当然，前提是你<i>{没有}产生幻觉。

Text.en: You say you see runeshapes in the stars above. What does that mean?
Text.zh: 你是说你在头顶的群星中看见了符文形状，这意味着什么？

Text.en: Okay, so you've seen runeshapes in the night sky... but what does that mean, exactly?
Text.zh: 好吧，你在夜空中看见了符文形状……可这到底意味着什么？

Text.en: What's it mean to see runeshapes in the night sky?
Text.zh: 在夜空中看见符文形状，这意味着什么？

Text.en: These runeshapes you see in the sky... you sure they're really there? <continue>What if you just spent too long with your head in a tome?
Text.zh: 你在天空中看见的那些符文形状……你确定它们真的存在？<continue>会不会是你埋头看古籍看得眼花了？

Text.en: I wonder why it is that you can now see runeshapes in the stars.
Text.zh: 我很好奇，你怎么突然就能在群星里看到符文形状了。

Text.en: These runeshapes ye see in the stars... what's the significance o' that, I wonder?
Text.zh: 你在群星里看见的那些符文形状……到底有什么深意呢？

Text.en: Well, I asked around. Not many runesmiths left in Ezomyr to ask, mind you. Not much of Ezomyr left at all, for that matter. <continue>There's one fella... drunk as the day is long. I asked him: 'Bertram, you ever seen runeshapes up in the stars?'<continue>He grumbled, and threw up on himself. That's when I knew I wasn't goin' to get any answers sittin' on my arse.<continue>I see them up there... some just tweaks on existing inscriptions. Others, entirely new. <continue>I set my chisel to work. And... had some <i>{unique} results. It was then that I decided to learn all I could. To become... a Runeseeker.
Text.zh: 后来我四处去打听了。艾兹麦那地方剩下的符文匠也不多了。其实，艾兹麦本身也没剩多少了。<continue>有个家伙……成天醉醺醺的。我问他：“伯特伦，你在星星里看见过符文的形状吗？”<continue>他咕哝了几声，然后吐了自己一身。那一刻我就知道，干坐着是找不着答案的。<continue>我在天上看见的符文……有些是在原有符文上略有改动，有些则是全新的。<continue>于是我就拿出凿子试试。然后……确实出现了<i>{特别}的成果。打那会儿起，我就决定，要把这本事学到手，成为一个……符文猎手。

Text.en: I heard a lot about Ezomyr back home... but what can you tell me about what happened there?
Text.zh: 我在老家听说过很多关于艾兹麦的事……不过你能跟我讲讲那儿到底发生了什么吗？

Text.en: I've heard bits and pieces about Ezomyr, but I'd like to hear it from someone who lived there.
Text.zh: 我零零碎碎听过些艾兹麦的事，但我想听听在当地生活过的人怎么说。

Text.en: I suppose you would have, growing up in Phaaryl! Well now... where to begin?
Text.zh: 你在法瑞欧长大，听说过也不奇怪！嗯……这要从何说起呢？

Text.en: What befell Ezomyr? You make it sound like a wasteland.
Text.zh: 艾兹麦出什么事了？听你的口气，那里简直像一片废土。

Text.en: The way you describe Ezoymr, it sounds like a sad shadow of its former self.
Text.zh: 照你这么说，艾兹麦好像已经面目全非了。

Text.en: You said there's not much of Ezomyr left. What happened to it?
Text.zh: 你说艾兹麦已经没剩多少了。那里到底怎么了？

Text.en: Sounds like Ezomyr's seen better days. What happened?
Text.zh: 听起来艾兹麦风光不再了。出什么事了？

Text.en: You mentioned Ezomyr is all but gone. Can you explain?
Text.zh: 你提到艾兹麦差不多全毁了。能详细说说吗？

Text.en: Spent plenty o' time in Ezomyr myself. Once was, I was known as a Druid of Skothe.
Text.zh: 我自己就在艾兹麦待过很长一段时间。那会儿，我还是斯科提的德鲁伊。

Text.en: Ah, yes! I've heard of your kind. Though... I gather things didn't end well for your people. <continue>My condolences.
Text.zh: 啊，没错！我听说过你们这一派。不过……你们的结局似乎不太好。<continue>请节哀。

Text.en: The past is the past. And Skothe's not unique in that regard. <continue>From what I hear, they had it even worse in certain parts of Ezomyr.
Text.zh: 过去的事就让它过去吧。也不是就斯科提一个地方遭殃了。据我所知，艾兹麦有些地方还要惨得多。

Text.en: You don't need to tell me. I'm from Skothe myself.
Text.zh: 这还用你说。我就是斯科提人！

Text.en: Ezomyr stretches a great distance. And yet... many of the clans are scattered, dead, or otherwise unknown. It's a far cry from what we once were. <continue>Where I'm from, the Isles of Skothe... well, we've done alright.
Text.zh: 艾兹麦幅员辽阔。可如今……许多氏族早已四散，消亡，或是下落不明。跟以前比，差得太远了。<continue>我老家斯科提岛……倒还凑合。

Text.en: Our current thane, Dermon, is a good man. Keeps us out of trouble, for the most part. But of late, well... <i>{the world's turning ill.} Just look around us. <continue>The Isles are no longer a pristine sanctuary.
Text.zh: 咱们现在的领主德蒙是个好人。大多数时候，都能让咱们不招惹上麻烦。可近来，唉……<continue><i>{世道不太平了。}看看周围就知道了。<continue>斯科提岛也早就不是什么净土了。

Text.en: That poor girl, Leitis. To see your beloved, killed before your eyes.... Monstrous. <continue>Even turned mad, I can't see our Thane doing such a thing. He just doesn't have it in him.
Text.zh: 那可怜的姑娘，莱蒂斯。眼睁睁看着心上人被杀……真是残忍。<continue>就算是发疯了，咱们领主也干不出这种事来。他不是那种人。

Text.en: I've never understood why we in Ogham have a Count, while you back in Ezomyr have Thanes.
Text.zh: 我一直搞不懂，为什么我们欧甘有伯爵，而你们艾兹麦那边是领主。

Text.en: Ogham has a Count... but <i>{you} have Thanes. You Ezomytes need to get your story straight.
Text.zh: 欧甘有伯爵……而<i>{你们}有领主。你们艾兹麦人真得把说法统一一下。

Text.en: Why do you have a Thane, but Ogham has a Count?
Text.zh: 为什么你们有领主，但欧甘却有伯爵？

Text.en: Why do we have a Count here in Ogham, but you have Thanes back in Ezomyr?
Text.zh: 为什么我们欧甘这儿有伯爵，而你们艾兹麦那边有领主？

Text.en: So you have a Thane? How's that different from the Count they have here in Ogham?
Text.zh: 所以你们有领主？那跟欧甘这里的伯爵有什么区别？

Text.en: Thanes, Counts... I can't keep all this shite straight. Why've you got <i>{one}, and Ogham's got the <i>{other}?
Text.zh: 领主，伯爵……这些乱七八糟的我根本分不清。为什么你们有<i>{这个}，而欧甘有<i>{那个}？

Text.en: Why does Ogham have a Count, while you have Thanes?
Text.zh: 为什么欧甘有伯爵，而你们有领主？

Text.en: I wouldnae be so sure o' that. But tell me – why do <i>{they} have Counts, and <i>{you} have Thanes?
Text.zh: 我可不敢那么肯定。不过告诉我——为什么<i>{他们}有伯爵，而<i>{你们}有领主？

Text.en: Phaaryl and Ezomyr have their differences. Phaaryl's got <i>{some} Thanes out here, but the title of Count is considered '<i>{better}'.<continue>I guess this all goes to show... that's a load of shite.
Text.zh: 法瑞欧和艾兹麦不一样。法瑞欧这边也有<i>{几个}领主，但他们觉得伯爵这个头衔“<i>{更体面}”。<continue>说到底嘛……这些头衔也都是扯淡。

Text.en: The way the Maraketh have treated the Faridun... is it really any surprise they behave like this?
Text.zh: 马拉克斯人那样对待法里顿人……他们现在变成这样，一点也不意外。

Text.en: The Faridun are not exactly innocent.
Text.zh: 法里顿人也不见得有多清白。

Text.en: So the Maraketh are free to treat them as they please?
Text.zh: 所以马拉克斯人就可以随心所欲地对待他们了？

Text.en: That's not what I said. All I'm saying is it's not so simple.
Text.zh: 我没这么说。我只是说，事情没那么简单。

Text.en: Can't say I wouldn't do the same. But the Faridun don't do themselves any favours.
Text.zh: 换作是我，说不定也会这么做。但法里顿人自己也没少找麻烦。

Text.en: You speak of the Faridun as though they are innocent. <continue>The Maraketh are a proud people. A <i>{strong} people. And that is only because we do not abide the weakness inherent in the Faridun.
Text.zh: 你说得像是法里顿人有多无辜似的。<continue>马拉克斯是个骄傲的民族。一个<i>{强大的}民族。而我们之所以强大，是因为我们绝不姑息法里顿人骨子里的那种软弱。

Text.en: That may be true...
Text.zh: 这点或许没错……

Text.en: Seems to me there's fault on both sides.
Text.zh: 照我看，双方都有问题。

Text.en: That's lettin' them off light. There's no justification for the Maraketh's actions.
Text.zh: 这么说也太轻巧了。马拉克斯人犯下的暴行，根本没法正当化。

Text.en: No, there isn't... just as there's no justification for what the Faridun have done in return.
Text.zh: 是没办法……可法里顿人回敬的手段，也一样没法正当化。

Text.en: I understand the Faridun wantin' to rise up. <continue>What I don't understand is how they choose to go about it.
Text.zh: 我能理解法里顿人想反抗。<continue>但我不理解他们选择的手段。

Text.en: What would you have them do? Revolution is never bloodless.
Text.zh: 那你要他们怎么做？革命哪有不流血的。

Text.en: True enough. Still doesn't justify raisin' the dead.
Text.zh: 这话倒没错。但复活死人这事还是说不过去。

Text.en: From where I'm standin', they both deserve each other.
Text.zh: 依我看，他们是半斤八两，活该凑一块儿。

Text.en: But who cast the first stone? The Maraketh made the Faridun what they are.
Text.zh: 可谁先动的手？是马拉克斯人把法里顿人逼成了现在这副模样。

Text.en: Well, maybe they did. All I'm sayin' is, the Faridun have sunk lower than the Maraketh ever did.
Text.zh: 也许吧。我只是说，相比于马拉克斯人，法里顿人堕落的程度，有过之而无不及。

Text.en: Perhaps not. But the Faridun are far from innocent.
Text.zh: 没有吧。不过法里顿人绝对算不上清白无辜。

Text.en: The Faridun want respect. They want fair treatment. <i>{That} I understand.<continue>But they've crossed many a line in their war against the Maraketh.
Text.zh: 法里顿人想要尊严，想要公平，<i>{这}我能理解。<continue>但他们在对抗马拉克斯人的战争中，早已越过了无数条底线。

Text.en: True enough. And I can't make sense of either...
Text.zh: 说得对。我也搞不清这两边到底在搞什么……

Text.en: But treat people like dogs long enough, they're going to bite back.
Text.zh: 可把人当成狗使唤久了，他们迟早会反咬一口。

Text.en: I wonder why the King of Kalguur has such an interest in Vaal artefacts...
Text.zh: 卡古兰的国王怎么对瓦尔的遗物这么上心……

Text.en: The rich all have their obsessions, I suppose.
Text.zh: 有钱人嘛，都有自己的怪癖。

Text.en: I've learned not to try to understand the minds of those types. They're much more pleasant without breath in their lungs.
Text.zh: 我早就不去琢磨那种人的心思了。他们没气的时候反而顺眼些。

Text.en: The rich love their idle obsessions. It's not worth the time you'd waste trying to make sense of them.
Text.zh: 有钱人总有些无聊的怪癖。想弄明白纯属浪费时间。

Text.en: The rich need hobbies. <continue>If not, they'd have nothing to distract themselves from what awful people they are.
Text.zh: 有钱人总得找点爱好。<continue>不然的话，他们怎么转移注意力，好忘掉自己有多烂。

Text.en: Makes sense to me. Those who hoard wealth will always want more. It's practically a hobby of theirs.
Text.zh: 挺合理的。守着财富的人永远想要更多，这差不多就是他们的消遣。

Text.en: Ah, rich people, eh? Who knows what goes through their minds... Maybe it's a hobby o' his.
Text.zh: 有钱人嘛，谁知道他们脑子里都想些什么……也许这就是他的爱好吧。

Text.en: Those who covet riches are not so easily satisfied. To them, amassing wealth is but a game.
Text.zh: 贪恋财富的人可没那么容易满足。对他们来说，堆积财富就是场游戏。

Text.en: Rich folk and their trinkets. They'd lead hollow lives if not for their little 'hobbies'.
Text.zh: 有钱人和他们的小玩意儿。要不是有这点“小爱好”，他们的生活得多空虚。

Text.en: Quite the extreme to go to for a hobby... I don't know. I get the idea it's likely something... <i>{pretty odd.}
Text.zh: 为了点兴趣，就搞这么夸张……我说不好。我总觉得……<i>{不太对劲。}

Text.en: So, got to assemble a crew to save the world, eh? That's a lot on your shoulders. <continue>For what it's worth... I'm on your side too.
Text.zh: 所以要召集一帮人去拯救世界，嗯？嘿。这担子可不轻啊。<continue>不管怎么说……我站在你这边。

Text.en: I managed to make out a bit more of that tome we found. Ah yes, here it is. <continue>'Aldur gazed at the pristine stars and there he saw meaning. Those shapes he wrought into our bedrock. His very Will gave the shapes life.' <continue>'And so, we began to know prosperity, and the beginnings of Kalguur was born.' <continue>Something to chew on, isn't it?
Text.zh: 那本古籍我总算又破译了一点。啊，对，就是这段。<continue>“奥杜尔凝视着纯净的星辰，从中读出了意义。他将那些形状刻入大地的基岩。他的意志，赋予了那些形状生命。”<continue>“于是，我们开始走向繁荣，卡古兰也由此诞生。”<continue>挺耐人寻味的，对吧？

Text.en: I found this section rather interesting in that second tome. <continue>'The Runefathers were Aldur's chosen apprentices. Artisans of runesmithing. They saw to Middengard's every need.' <continue>'The abundance of Verisium in their volcanic ranges meant they would never exhaust their capacity for progress... or so they thought.' <continue>Oh boy. It's all going to go bad, isn't it?
Text.zh: 第二本古籍里，这段挺有意思。<continue>“符文之父是奥杜尔亲选的学徒，他们是符文锻造的工匠。他们照料着米登加德的一切需求。”<continue>“火山山脉里取之不尽的维金，意味着进步永远不会受限……至少他们当时是这么想的。”<continue>哦，天哪。感觉后面要出事了，对吧？

Text.en: There's a little more on the Triskelion Flame here... <continue>'The Runefathers' conquest came from their incredible design. They toiled and poured their Will into the most elegant combination of complex runeshapes that ever existed.' <continue>'But not every Runefather's ambition was sated.' <continue>Yes, of course... there's always some greedy bugger who desires more.
Text.zh: 这儿还有一点关于三耀之火的内容……<continue>“符文之父的征服，源于他们无与伦比的杰作。他们倾注心血，将自己的意志灌注于有史以来最精妙复杂的符文组合。”<continue>“但并非所有符文之父都满足于此。”<continue>当然……总有些贪心的家伙索求无度。

Text.en: This portion seems to dig a little deeper into Cadigan... <continue>'Cadigan was not a name anyone knew at the time of his claim, now it is a name we shall never forget.'<continue>'His persuasion rallied some. Terrified others. The shrieks of the Triskelion Flame during his coronation were reported across the capital.' <continue>'We feared he controlled it. We feared it commanded our obedience. And yet here we stand, aboard creaking ships... the Flame's warmth gently burning to keep us alive.' <continue>Wait... what does this mean? The Triskelion's inscriptions... I <i>{must} study them someday!
Text.zh: 这段似乎深入介绍了卡迪甘……<continue>“在卡迪甘宣称继承权时，没人听说过这个名字。而如今，我们再也不会忘记。”<continue>“他的劝说拉拢了一些人，也吓坏了另一些人。在他加冕时，三耀之火的尖啸响彻整个首都。”<continue>“我们害怕他掌控了它，也害怕它命令我们臣服。然而我们此刻，却站在嘎吱作响的船上……靠三耀之火散发的温热勉强活着。”<continue>等等……这是什么意思？三耀之火的符文……我<i>{一定}要找机会好好研究一下！

Text.en: Who'd have thought this tree would have such hidden secrets?
Text.zh: 谁能想到这树下还藏着这种秘密？

Text.en: Even those of us in Ogham didn't know the tree's true nature. I suppose this was the source of its power all along.
Text.zh: 就连我们这些住在欧甘的人，都不知道那棵树的真正面目。想来，它的力量之源自始至终都是这里吧。

Text.en: Well, they <i>{do} call it the Tree of Souls... though I suppose even the locals were ignorant as to where its power came from.
Text.zh: 它<i>{确实}被叫作灵魂之树……不过我想就连本地人都不清楚它的力量来自何处。

Text.en: Yes... though I sensed its power, I knew not where it came from. I suppose we've answered that mystery.
Text.zh: 是的……我虽能感受到它的力量，却不知这力量从何而来。我想我们算是解开了这个谜团。

Text.en: Not me – and I have lived my whole life in this wood.
Text.zh: 我想到了，我都在这片林子里住了一辈子了。

Text.en: To think its power could have gone undetected for so long...
Text.zh: 想想看，它的力量竟然隐藏了这么久都没被察觉……

Text.en: We always knew there was <i>{something} special about this tree – we just didn't know what. <continue>I guess this was that 'something' all along.
Text.zh: 我们一直都知道这棵树有点特别——只是说不出到底哪里特别。 <continue>现在看来，那“特别之处”一直就是它。

Text.en: Could tell it were no normal tree... Suppose now we know the source of its power.
Text.zh: 一看就知道那不是普通的树……现在总算闹明白它的力量是怎么来的了。

Text.en: And here I thought a tree was just a tree! Guess we know what makes this one so special.
Text.zh: 我以前还以为树就只是树呢！这下可知道这棵为什么如此特别了。

Text.en: The Tree of Souls holds many a mystery in its branches. It seems we've answered where its power came from.
Text.zh: 灵魂之树的枝丫间藏着许多奥秘。看来我们已经搞清楚它力量的来源了。

Text.en: They dinnae call it the Tree o' Souls for nothin'... These runes must be the source of its power.
Text.zh: 灵魂之树可不是白叫的……这些符文一定就是它力量的源泉。

Text.en: Most likely. Though I'm still not entirely sure what <i>{that} is.
Text.zh: 很可能是这样。但我还是不太确定<i>{那}到底是什么。

Text.en: What do you make of this forge?
Text.zh: 你觉得这熔炉怎么样？

Text.en: Well now... any thoughts?
Text.zh: 好了……有什么想法吗？

Text.en: What can we learn from this place?
Text.zh: 我们能从这个地方了解到什么？

Text.en: What do you make of this?
Text.zh: 你对此怎么看？

Text.en: What d'ya make of all this, then?
Text.zh: 那么，你怎么看这一切？

Text.en: So... a gold coin for your thoughts?
Text.zh: 来……一枚金币换你的高见？

Text.en: What can we glean from all this?
Text.zh: 我们能从这一切中收集到什么信息？

Text.en: Come on then. Let's hear yer thoughts.
Text.zh: 来吧，说说你的想法。

Text.en: I suspect some rather bad things happened here. <continue>The Kalguurans were far from home... and potentially far from their own values.
Text.zh: 我猜这儿发生过一些相当惨烈的事。<continue>那些卡古兰人当时背井离乡……或许也背离了他们自己的准则。

Text.en: A strange lot this bunch. What did they even <i>{do} in here?
Text.zh: 这群人真奇怪。他们到底在这儿<i>{做}了什么？

Text.en: They seemed like mystics of some kind.
Text.zh: 他们看着像什么秘术师。

Text.en: Whatever they did, it feels imbued with delicious mysticism.
Text.zh: 不管他们做了什么，这儿都透着一股诱人的神秘气息。

Text.en: Whatever it was, it was mysterious. It feels... arcane. Brutal.
Text.zh: 不管做了什么，都很神秘。感觉……很玄妙，也很残酷。

Text.en: Not sure. I expected something more... scientific.
Text.zh: 不知道。我还以为会更……科学一点。

Text.en: Strange is right. They weren't exactly scientists, that's for sure.
Text.zh: 奇怪就对了。他们肯定不是那种科学家。

Text.en: Dunno, but it all seems more... <i>{mystical} than I expected.
Text.zh: 不知道，但这一切比我预想得更……<i>{神秘}。

Text.en: I imagine they were mystics, based on what we see here.
Text.zh: 依我们在此之所见，我估计他们是秘术师。

Text.en: Cannae talk specifics... but I know mysticism when I see it.
Text.zh: 说不清具体细节……但我看得出这股神秘气息。

Text.en: Yes, I'm surprised to see that. The Kalguurans seemed very practical to me. This suggests there's more to it.
Text.zh: 是啊。看到这个我也很惊讶。我本以为卡古兰人都很务实。看来事情没那么简单。

Text.en: It's... unreal to see it like this. <continue>When I first laid eyes on Kingsmarch, I felt like I saw my future in front of me. Another new beginning... <continue>And now... it's all rubble.
Text.zh: 这副景象……看着太不真实了。<continue>我第一次见到君锋镇时，还以为未来的画卷在我眼前展开了，还以为那是又一个崭新的开始。<continue>而现在……全都成了废墟。

Text.en: Buildings can be rebuilt. It's no reason to give up on your dreams.
Text.zh: 建筑毁了可以重建。这不该成为放弃梦想的理由。

Text.en: You're annoyingly enthusiastic at the best of times, and well... dare I say, we likely need some of that now.
Text.zh: 你平时就已经热情得够烦人的了，而且……恕我直言，我们现在或许正需要一点这种劲头……

Text.en: Kingsmarch has been devastated, yes... but you are still here. Your quest remains the same.
Text.zh: 君锋镇是被毁了，没错……可你还活着。你的目标也没变。

Text.en: You still have your health. We all do. As long as we have that, we have hope.
Text.zh: 你还健健康康的。大家都还活着。只要还活着，就还有希望。

Text.en: It won't be easy, but all o' this can be rebuilt. Don't give up now.
Text.zh: 重建肯定不容易，但这一切总能重新站起来。现在不该放弃。

Text.en: I won't lie to ya, mate – it's bloody bleak. But we're still here, ain't we? That's gotta count for somethin'.
Text.zh: 伙计，我不跟你扯谎——情况确实惨到家了。可我们还活着，不是吗？这总得算点什么。

Text.en: It is tragic, undoubtedly. But let the dying embers of old Kingsmarch be the spark which lights a new flame in your heart.
Text.zh: 这无疑是场悲剧。但就让旧君锋镇那将熄的余烬，点燃你心中新的火花吧。

Text.en: The folk who built it yet live – as do you, lad. What is destroyed can be rebuilt, so as long as you dinna sink into despair.
Text.zh: 建造它的人还活着——你也一样，小子。只要别被绝望压垮，毁掉的东西就都能重建。

Text.en: You know what? You're right. Thanks. I needed to hear that.
Text.zh: 嗯，你说得对，谢谢。这话真是说到我心里去了。

Text.en: Don't mention it. No, seriously. Speak of my words to no one.
Text.zh: 别提了。不，说真的。我这话你可别告诉任何人。

Text.en: I really hope Captain Makoru is right. <continue>I've seen how worried Rog has been. Just imagine his conversations with his children at night. <continue>Please... help find Gwennen.
Text.zh: 我真希望马克鲁船长是对的。<continue>我看罗格一直忧心忡忡。想想他晚上要怎么跟孩子们开口吧。<continue>拜托了……帮忙把桂温妮找回来。

Text.en: You bloody legend! You did it! <continue>The big man hasn't stopped smiling. It's a beautiful sight. <continue>You have my thanks, as well.
Text.zh: 你这家伙真了不起！你做到了！<continue>那大个子一直笑得合不拢嘴，这画面看着真不错。<continue>我也要谢谢你。

Text.en: It's comforting to hear the Kalguurans talk about firing up their old quest once more. <continue>I could see it in Dannig's eyes at the forge before all this... He longed for adventure. To relive that part of his life.
Text.zh: 听到卡古兰人说要重拾他们之前的使命，真是令人欣慰。<continue>在这之前，我在熔炉那儿就看得出来，丹宁眼里一直有那股劲……他渴望冒险，渴望重温那段岁月。

Text.en: I'm glad they have a reason to carry on, if nothing else. If not for their quest, who knows how much harder Dannig might have taken the loss of Kingsmarch?
Text.zh: 不管怎么说我还是挺高兴的，他们总算又有继续前进的奔头了。要不是有这个使命，谁知道丹宁失去君锋镇之后会消沉成什么样。

Text.en: That's a nice way of looking at it.
Text.zh: 这么想也挺好的。

Text.en: Well, it seems he got what he wanted... if not quite how he imagined it.
Text.zh: 嗯，看来他得偿所愿了……虽说跟他想象的不太一样。

Text.en: I saw it too. He was made for adventuring, not running a port town. He is meant for so much more.
Text.zh: 我也看出来了。他天生就是冒险的料，根本不适合管理港口小镇。他的天地远不止于此。

Text.en: Careful what you wish for, I guess.
Text.zh: 我只能说，许愿可得谨慎些啊。

Text.en: Yes... a shame such dire circumstances forced them back on their quest.
Text.zh: 是啊……只可惜是这恶劣的形势逼迫他们重拾伟大使命。

Text.en: Maybe this is their chance to put the disappointment of Kingsmarch's destruction behind them.
Text.zh: 也许这是个机会，能让他们把君锋镇覆灭的失落抛在脑后。

Text.en: Perhaps you're right.
Text.zh: 也许你说得对。

Text.en: Aye. Could tell he wanted to get back out there... only, he didn't know how.
Text.zh: 是啊。我看得出来他想重新上路了……只是苦于不知道该怎么开始。

Text.en: I guess he got his answer, didn't he?
Text.zh: 这下倒好，老天替他做了决定，对吧？

Text.en: I guess he did.
Text.zh: 看来是的。

Text.en: Guessin' this ain't quite how he imagined it.
Text.zh: 我估摸着，这跟他当初想的可不一样。

Text.en: No, I'd wager not... but then, it never is, is it?
Text.zh: 是，我猜也是……不过世事不都是如此吗？

Text.en: Can say that again. Just gotta roll with the punches, you know? Dust yourself off and get back out there.
Text.zh: 这话不假。拳头砸过来，也只能受着，对吧？拍拍灰继续往前走。

Text.en: Fate works in strange ways. Perhaps the loss of Kingsmarch was the impetus he needed to take up the quest once more.
Text.zh: 命运弄人。说不定，失去君锋镇反而成了他重新开启使命的契机。

Text.en: A cruel twist o' fate, that this is the manner in which he renews his quest. <continue>But there's no changin' the past, so might as well make the most of it.
Text.zh: 以这种方式重启使命，命运还真是残酷。<continue>可过去没法改变，不如顺势而为。

Text.en: I hope, whatever happens, it brings him peace. That's all, really.
Text.zh: 不管怎样，希望这能带给他安宁。这就够了。

Text.en: Things really never let up around here, do they? First, a potentially world-ending Cataclysmic event... <continue>Now, a giant comet crashes down on our doorstep. <continue>Dannig claims it's pure Verisium. The waters out there around it... they've gone really mad.
Text.zh: 这地方的事真是没完没了，对吧？先是差点毁灭世界的灾变……<continue>现在又有一颗巨大的彗星直接砸到家门口。<continue>丹宁说那是纯净维金。而且它周围的水域……已经彻底失控了。

Text.en: Mad? What do you mean?
Text.zh: 失控了？你什么意思？

Text.en: Mad? In what sense?
Text.zh: 失控了？怎么个失控？

Text.en: Mad? How so?
Text.zh: 失控了？怎么说？

Text.en: What do you mean, 'gone mad'?
Text.zh: 你说“失控了”是什么意思？

Text.en: Mad? How so?
Text.zh: 失控了？怎么说？

Text.en: Eh? What d'ya mean?
Text.zh: 嗯？这话什么意思？

Text.en: I'm not sure what you mean.
Text.zh: 我不太明白你的意思。

Text.en: Not sure I catch yer meanin'. How has the water 'gone mad'?
Text.zh: 我没太明白你的意思。水怎么能“失控”？

Text.en: Verisium is a mystery I'm still learning more about. But Dannig is certain... the potential of this <i>{particular} Verisium... well, it's unmatched. <continue>Whatever <i>{it} is... it's potent. It's powerful.
Text.zh: 维金的奥秘，我至今还在探索学习中。但丹宁很肯定……这块<i>{特别的}维金……有着无可匹敌的的潜力。<continue>不管<i>{它}是什么……它都蕴含着巨大的能量，无比强大。

Text.en: It's strange really... I didn't fully know what my hands were doing, but Dannig and I worked as if it were almost a well-rehearsed dance. <continue>I'm not a pious man, but the whole thing felt almost... guided somehow.
Text.zh: 说来也怪……我当时都不太清楚自己的手在做什么，但丹宁和我配合起来，就像排练过无数次一样。<continue>我并不信神，但整个过程，确实有种被指引的感觉……

Text.en: Perhaps the Triskelion <i>{itself} guided you.
Text.zh: 也许是三耀之火<i>{亲自}指引了你。

Text.en: The way Dannig talks about it, you'd think the Flame could spin gold from straw. Perhaps the Flame itself <i>{did} help you.
Text.zh: 照丹宁的说法，那三耀之火简直能点石成金。说不定<i>{真是}火焰本身在指引你？

Text.en: Perhaps, even in its fractured state, the Triskelion was able to influence you.
Text.zh: 也许，哪怕在破损状态下，三耀之火依然能影响你。

Text.en: Dannig seems to think the Flame is the most powerful artefact in Kalguuran history. <continue>Maybe it used some of that power to, I don't know... inspire you.
Text.zh: 丹宁把那火焰说得像是卡古兰历史上的最强神器。<continue>也许它用了点力量……怎么说呢……启发了你。

Text.en: Spent half of your life studyin' runes, haven't ya? Maybe some o' that study paid off in ways you weren't even aware of.
Text.zh: 你不是研究符文大半辈子了吗？说不定那些研究早就回报了你，只是你自己都没察觉。

Text.en: This Triskelion's s'posed to be dead powerful, right? Well maybe it, you know... <i>{helped} you somehow.
Text.zh: 这三耀之火据说强得不得了，不是吗？也许它……用什么法子<i>{帮了}你一把。

Text.en: One need not be pious to recognise there are larger forces at work. Perhaps one such force guided your hand.
Text.zh: 即便不信神，也能看出有更强大的力量在运作。也许正是那种力量指引了你的手。

Text.en: Fate's a curious thing. Mayhap you were fated to reforge the Flame?
Text.zh: 命运这东西很奇妙。说不定你命中注定要重铸三耀之火？

Text.en: For all I know, that could be the truth.
Text.zh: 就我所知，说不定还真是这么回事。

Text.en: That giant thing, lurking in the crater... what even <i>{was} it?
Text.zh: 那个躲在陨石坑的庞然大物……到底<i>{是}什么东西？

Text.en: Some sort of mutation... a fusion of Corruption and Verisium. Both at their worst.
Text.zh: 某种变异……腐化和维金融合出来的产物，两者最糟糕的部分搅在了一起。

Text.en: The result of some strange cocktail of Corruption and Verisium, I gather.
Text.zh: 看起来像是腐化和维金混合出来的怪东西。

Text.en: It seemed an abomination born of both Corruption and Verisium.
Text.zh: 看着像是腐化和维金共同孕育出来的怪胎。

Text.en: I don't know. I'm guessing that's what happens when you're hit full force with both Corruption and Verisium.
Text.zh: 我也不清楚。我猜同时被腐化和维金正面砸脸，大概就会变成这样。

Text.en: A strange mutation of some sort... one caused by the fusion of <i>{rathin} and Verisium.
Text.zh: 某种奇怪的变异物……<i>{腐化}和维金融合后催生出来的。

Text.en: Everythin' bad about Corruption and Verisium, rolled into one big ugly beast.
Text.zh: 把腐化和维金所有糟糕的玩意儿，全塞进一头丑八怪里了。

Text.en: The entire crater was smothered in Corruption and Verisium both. I imagine that was the reason for its mutations.
Text.zh: 整个陨坑都闷在腐化和维金的双重包裹里。我想这就是变异的原因。

Text.en: Hard to say. It seemed heavily mutated... likely due to all that Corruption and Verisium.
Text.zh: 很难说。变异得很厉害……多半是因为那些腐化和维金。

Text.en: Strange. It almost sounds like Verisium and Corruption... combined. Catalysing one another to create whatever <i>{that} was.
Text.zh: 真奇怪。听着就像是维金和腐化……融合在一起，然后互相催化生出了<i>{那种}东西。

Text.en: Yes, it seems that way.
Text.zh: 对，看来是那样。

Text.en: Perhaps... but I don't really like to think about it.
Text.zh: 也许吧……但我不太愿意去想这事。

Text.en: Yes. It appears so.
Text.zh: 对。看来是这样。

Text.en: I think you might be right.
Text.zh: 我想你可能是对的。

Text.en: Aye. Seems that way.
Text.zh: 嗯。看来是那样。

Text.en: Seems that way, don't it?
Text.zh: 看着是那样，对吧？

Text.en: It does seem that way.
Text.zh: 看着确实是那样。

Text.en: Ach... that's just what we need. More ways for Corruption to make a right mess o' things.
Text.zh: 啊……真是雪上加霜。腐化又学了新门道。

Text.en: Not born of the stars... but neither born here. It simply came to be at the moment the comet hit.
Text.zh: 不是生于群星……也并非诞于此地。它只是在彗星撞击的那一刻，就这么出现了。

Text.en: I said I don't want to think about–
Text.zh: 我说了我不想……

Text.en: This is why I continue to believe the mysteries of life may be answered in what we see... above us.
Text.zh: 正因如此，我才始终相信，生命的秘密也许就在……我们的头顶。

Text.en: <i>{Please} be quiet, Farrow!
Text.zh: 法罗，<i>{麻烦}你闭嘴！

Text.en: The Kalguurans intend to overthrow their king. And I, for one, can't blame them.
Text.zh: 卡古兰人打算推翻他们的国王。说实话，我完全能理解他们。

Text.en: They intend to overthrow the King? To overthrow Cadigan?
Text.zh: 他们要推翻国王？推翻卡迪甘？

Text.en: Finally found the courage to fight back, have they?
Text.zh: 他们终于鼓起勇气反抗了，是吗？

Text.en: It wasn't so long ago that the mention of their king caused them great distress. But now they intend to overthrow him?
Text.zh: 不久前，光是提到那位国王，他们都会吓得发抖。现在竟然想推翻他？

Text.en: About time they found their courage.
Text.zh: 也该是他们硬气一回的时候了。

Text.en: Aye, good on 'em. <continue>That bloody tyrant's tormented 'em for too long.
Text.zh: 是啊，好样儿的。<continue>那该死的暴君折磨他们太久了。

Text.en: Used to be, they wouldn't even say the bloke's name. Now, they're ready to overthrow 'im!
Text.zh: 以前他们连那家伙的名字都不敢提。现在居然要推翻他！

Text.en: They would rise up against their king?
Text.zh: 他们要反抗自己的国王？

Text.en: Nor can I. Only wonder what took 'em so long.
Text.zh: 我也搞不懂，只是纳闷他们怎么拖了这么久才想起动手。

Text.en: Yes. And I've decided... I'll join them. How can I not? An opportunity to see Middengard with my own eyes. <continue>I almost feel this choice was made for me, on the very first day we met. <continue>And, well... I've got an awful lot to thank you for. So, thank you, truly.
Text.zh: 是的。我也决定了……我要加入他们。怎么能不去呢？这可是亲眼见证米登加德的机会。<continue>我甚至觉得，这选择从咱们第一次见面那天起，就已经注定了。<continue>还有啊……我有太多事要感谢你了。所以，真心谢谢你。

Text.en: You're most welcome. And thank you, Farrow.
Text.zh: 不必客气。也谢谢你，法罗。

Text.en: Don't get all gooey on me now. But... you're welcome, I suppose.
Text.zh: 少跟我这么肉麻。不过嘛……不用客气。

Text.en: You are more than welcome, Master Farrow.
Text.zh: 这是我的荣幸，法罗先生。

Text.en: Don't mention it. Happy to help a friend.
Text.zh: 不用客气，帮朋友嘛，我很乐意。

Text.en: Did you just call me a... friend?
Text.zh: 你刚刚是叫我……朋友？

Text.en: Don't make a big deal of it, or else I'll take it back.
Text.zh: 别小题大做，不然我就收回这话了。

Text.en: Ha! As you wish... friend.
Text.zh: 哈！遵命……朋友。

Text.en: The pleasure's all mine, Farrow.
Text.zh: 这是我的荣幸，法罗。

Text.en: Well, hey – what are friends for?
Text.zh: 嘿——朋友之间不就是这样吗？

Text.en: Friends? Well, yes... I suppose we are, aren't we?
Text.zh: 朋友？嗯，是啊……好像确实是这么回事。

Text.en: You are most welcome.
Text.zh: 千万别客气。

Text.en: Aye, lad. And thank you as well.
Text.zh: 是啊，小子。也谢谢你。

Text.en: Well, this is significant! More runeshapes to add to our records. And more power to add to your payload!<continue>I guess this means there's even more out there, eh? Keep an eye out!
Text.zh: 嗯，这可了不得！又有新的符文形状可以收录了，还能给你的装备再添点法力！<continue>看来外面肯定还有更多，对吧？记得多留意！

Text.en: I will. And thanks for all you do.
Text.zh: 我会的，也谢谢你所做的一切。

Text.en: Well, I'm never one to say no to more power...
Text.zh: 嗯，送上门来的力量，我可从来不会说不……

Text.en: As always, you've been most helpful. Thank you.
Text.zh: 你总是这么肯帮忙，多谢了。

Text.en: I admit, I had my reservations about you... but you've certainly been useful to have around.
Text.zh: 说实话，起初我对你还有点怀疑……但有你在身边，真是帮了大忙。

Text.en: Coming from you, that was almost a proposition...
Text.zh: 能听见你这么说，简直跟求婚差不多了！

Text.en: It's nothing of the sort!
Text.zh: 才不是那回事！

Text.en: Hah! I know, darlin'... just riling you up.
Text.zh: 哈！我清楚得很，亲爱的……逗你玩的。

Text.en: You're dead useful to have around, you know that?
Text.zh: 你还真是个好帮手，知道吗？

Text.en: Heh... what would I do without ya?
Text.zh: 嘿……少了你我该怎么办哟。

Text.en: Your assistance is much appreciated. Thank you.
Text.zh: 承蒙相助，不胜感激。

Text.en: Good to see ye as always, Farrow.
Text.zh: 每次见到你都很高兴，法罗。

Text.en: But regardless... Just pleased I can help.
Text.zh: 不过话说回来……我很高兴我能帮上忙。

Text.en: Just pleased I can help.
Text.zh: 能帮上忙我就很开心了。

Text.en: Where did you get that tool you carry?
Text.zh: 你随身带的那个工具，是从哪儿弄来的？

Text.en: What is that toy you keep waving around?
Text.zh: 你一直挥来挥去的那个玩具是什么？

Text.en: What is this tool you carry, Master Farrow?
Text.zh: 你带的这个工具是什么，法罗先生？

Text.en: What is this tool of yours?
Text.zh: 你这工具是什么？

Text.en: What's this tool I see you usin'? Seems right useful.
Text.zh: 你用的这工具是什么东西？看起来很好用。

Text.en: What's that tool you got there?
Text.zh: 你手里那工具是什么？

Text.en: This tool you carry – what is it?
Text.zh: 你带的这个工具，是什么东西？

Text.en: That tool o' yours has come in handy. Where'd ye get it?
Text.zh: 你那工具可真帮了不少忙。你从哪儿得来的？

Text.en: Oh, this old thing? I smithed it myself back home. Quite the tool. <continue>I can attune it to the signature of runes and, well... the brighter it glows, the closer they are. <continue>Should come in handy for tracking down more of these. <continue>And... it has a certain <i>{persuasion} over Verisium that I've yet to fully understand.
Text.zh: 哦，这老家伙？这是我在老家自己打造的。挺好用的。<continue>我可以让它与符文的特征产生共鸣，符文离得越近，它就越亮。 <continue>用来追踪这种遗迹特别方便。<continue>而且……它对维金有种<i>{影响力}，只是我还没完全搞明白。

Text.en: Who the heck was that fellow then?
Text.zh: 刚才那家伙到底是谁？

Text.en: A knight – and he certainly fought like one. He seemed to be protecting a trove of Verisium.
Text.zh: 一位骑士，打起仗来也确实不辱骑士之名。他似乎在看守一批维金。

Text.en: He was a knight. Brave. Strong. Now dead. He was watching over some Verisium.
Text.zh: 他是位骑士。英勇、强壮。如今死了。当时他正在看守一批维金。

Text.en: A warrior of some skill – though not enough to save him. He appeared to be protecting some Verisium.
Text.zh: 一个有点本事的战士，尽管还不足以保住自己的命。他应该是在看守一批维金。

Text.en: Someone with an inflated sense of self-worth.
Text.zh: 一个自我感觉过分良好的人。

Text.en: Oh? What makes you say that?
Text.zh: 哦？为什么这么说？

Text.en: Well, he's a knight, isn't he? That's sort of their thing. In any case, he was watching over some Verisium.
Text.zh: 呃，他不是骑士吗？骑士差不多都那样。总之，他当时在看守一批维金。

Text.en: How should I know? We didn't exactly exchange pleasantries. He were too busy watching over some Verisium.
Text.zh: 我怎么知道？我们又没寒暄过。他正忙着看守那堆维金呢。

Text.en: Wish I could tell ya, mate. Seemed like a knight of some kind. He was very invested in his collection of Verisium.
Text.zh: 我倒是想告诉你，伙计。看着像个骑士。他对自己的维金收藏可上心了。

Text.en: A brave warrior. Though his bravery did not save him in the end. He cared too much for his Verisium.
Text.zh: 一位勇敢的战士。虽然那份勇敢还是没能救下他。他太在乎自己的维金了。

Text.en: A knight o' some sort. Guessin' he was Kalguuran. Mad for his damn Verisium.
Text.zh: 骑士之类的人物。估计是卡古兰人。为了那些该死的维金连命都不要了。

Text.en: He was an ancient Kalguuran safeguarding Verisium? <continue>Any other clues about him?
Text.zh: 一个看守维金的古代卡古兰人？<continue>还有别的线索吗？

Text.en: He was well-runed, and well-equipped.
Text.zh: 他浑身都是符文，而且装备精良。

Text.en: The man had enough runes about him to take your breath away.
Text.zh: 那人身上的符文，多得能让你气都喘不过来。

Text.en: The place was a treasure trove of runes.
Text.zh: 那地方简直就是符文的宝库。

Text.en: He had runes. Lots of them.
Text.zh: 他有很多符文，非常非常多。

Text.en: It weren't just Verisium about. He had a number o' runes as well.
Text.zh: 那儿不光有维金。他还有好些符文哩。

Text.en: There was a whole boatload o' runes about.
Text.zh: 那儿有一大堆符文。

Text.en: There were runes. Many of them.
Text.zh: 那里有符文，多得很。

Text.en: He had near enough runes to make a man like you weep.
Text.zh: 他身上的符文多到能让你这种人高兴得哭出来。

Text.en: Sounds like someone of significance. Could he have come with the original Expedition? Or... perhaps even earlier?
Text.zh: 听起来像是个有身份的人。他有没有可能是第一批远征队的成员？或者……还要更早？

Text.en: I've been thinking... we ought to go check on our Kalguuran friends.
Text.zh: 我一直在想……咱们该去看看那帮卡古兰朋友了。

Text.en: I'm worried what might have happened to Kingsmarch in all this chaos. I'm going to head south and check for myself.
Text.zh: 这阵子乱成这样，我很担心君锋镇可能出了什么事。我打算往南走，亲自去看看。

Text.en: Wait. What's that you've got there?
Text.zh: 等等，你手里拿的是什么？

Text.en: Hmm... that rune is rare indeed. Strangely, it seems like it is desperate for power.
Text.zh: 唔……这符文可真稀罕。奇怪的是，它似乎很渴求力量。

Text.en: I have a feeling... you could fill it, if you had enough power to do so.
Text.zh: 我有种感觉……如果你有足够的力量，就可以给它充能……

Text.en: Providing you're willing to take the risk.
Text.zh: 前提是你愿意冒这个险。

Text.en: Wait. What's that you've got there?
Text.zh: 等等，你手里拿的是什么？

Text.en: Hmm... That rune is rare indeed. And my tool... It's resonating strangely in its presence.
Text.zh: 唔……这符文可真稀罕，而且我的工具……对这东西产生了奇怪的共鸣。

Text.en: Well, look... I've decided. You should take it. I've a feeling you'll be able to harness its true potential.
Text.zh: 嗯，听着……我决定了。你该拿上它。我有种预感，你能发挥它真正的力量。

Text.en: I tried once, and fell asleep for two days straight. Be careful with it!
Text.zh: 我试过一次，结果一连昏睡了两天。千万小心！

Text.en: My word, you did it. That rune is incredible!
Text.zh: 我的天，你做到了。这符文太不可思议了！

Text.en: Well, look... I've decided. You should take it. I've a feeling you'll be able to harness its true potential.
Text.zh: 嗯，听着……我决定了。你该拿上它。我有种预感，你能发挥它真正的力量。

Text.en: I tried once, and fell asleep for two days straight. Be careful with it!
Text.zh: 我试过一次，结果一连昏睡了两天。千万小心！

Text.en: That Farrow a friend o' yours?
Text.zh: 那个法罗，是你朋友吗？

Text.en: No.
Text.zh: 不是。

Text.en: But did he come here with you?
Text.zh: 那他是跟你一起来的？

Text.en: Yes, we travelled together.
Text.zh: 对，我们一起旅行过。

Text.en: Who?
Text.zh: 谁？

Text.en: The excitable one with the tattoos.
Text.zh: 就是那个有点兴奋，身上有纹身的家伙。

Text.en: Oh, him? Yeah, I- I suppose we're, uh... acquainted.
Text.zh: 他？嗯，算是……认识吧。

Text.en: He's come here with me, if that's what you're getting at.
Text.zh: 他跟我一起来的，你要是想问这个的话。

Text.en: We have been travelling together.
Text.zh: 我们一直结伴同行。

Text.en: I wouldn't say 'friend', but I've allowed him to tag along, yes.
Text.zh: 算不上“朋友”，只是让他跟着罢了。

Text.en: He is my companion, yes.
Text.zh: 对，他是我的同伴。

Text.en: Yes, why?
Text.zh: 是啊，怎么了？

Text.en: Aye. Us wanderin' Ezomytes gotta stick together!
Text.zh: 对。咱们这些四处漂泊的艾兹麦人总得互相照应！

Text.en: Heh. Thought so. You both got some... choice attire. <continue>Tell him to stop stickin' his face in the forge, will ya? Unless he <i>{wants} to get burnt.
Text.zh: 嘿，我就知道。你们俩打扮得……挺有风格啊。<continue>跟他说一声，别老把脸往熔炉里凑，行吗？除非他<i>{想}被烫着。

Text.en: He's an Ezomyte runesmith. He's interested in learning Kalguuran methods.
Text.zh: 他是艾兹麦的符文匠，想学学卡古兰人的手艺。

Text.en: He's come from the Isles of Skothe, studying ancient Ezomyte runes.
Text.zh: 他从斯科提岛来，一直在研究远古艾兹麦符文。

Text.en: The Ezomytes have runes? Really? Are they anything like ours?
Text.zh: 艾兹麦人也有符文？真的假的？跟我们的像吗？

Text.en: They look similar. They may even be one and the same. Farrow could explain.
Text.zh: 看着挺像。说不定就是同一种东西。法罗能说明白。

Text.en: You'll have to ask Farrow.
Text.zh: 这个你得去问法罗。

Text.en: As he won't shut up about, he's travelled all the way from Ezomyr to study runes! He's right where he wants to be.
Text.zh: 他自己天天唠叨个没完，说自己不远万里从艾兹麦跑来研究符文！这地方正和他胃口。

Text.en: He is seeking the origins of his runes. He has travelled far, from Ezomyr to Kingsmarch, in his pursuit.
Text.zh: 他在追寻符文的起源。为了这个目标，他横穿整片大陆，从艾兹麦一直到君锋镇。

Text.en: He's just curious, like. He's a runesmith from Ezomyr – wants to know all there is to know about 'em.
Text.zh: 他就是单纯好奇而已。他是从艾兹麦来的符文工匠，一门心思想搞懂符文。

Text.en: Tell him yourself. The bloke's interested in runes, see. Travelled all the way here from Ezomyr to learn about 'em.
Text.zh: 你自己问他吧。这家伙对符文着迷得很。大老远从艾兹麦跑来这里，就为了研究这些玩意儿。

Text.en: Farrow knows enough to not get hurt. He is an avid runesmith, all the way from Ezomyr.
Text.zh: 法罗知道怎么保护自己。他是个热心的符文匠，从艾兹麦远道而来。

Text.en: The man knows what he's doin'. Could tell ye more about runes than I could tell ye about damn near anythin'.
Text.zh: 这家伙很懂行。说到符文，他知道的比我知道的多太多了。

Text.en: Really? Wonder how he knows about runes...
Text.zh: 是吗？他怎么会知道符文的事……

Text.en: If Dannig's fine with it... I'll just keep an eye on him.
Text.zh: 只要丹宁没意见……我就先盯着他点。

Text.en: I've got workers restoring the forge. Tujen, we'll need materials. Rog, we'll need schematics. And this time, we should reinforce the foundations.
Text.zh: 我已经让人去修熔炉了。图金，材料就靠你了。罗格，你负责图纸。这次，还得把地基加固一下。

Text.en: It's sad to see Kingsmarch like this...
Text.zh: 看到君锋镇变成这样，真叫人难过……

Text.en: Kingsmarch has seen better days.
Text.zh: 君锋镇现在这副样子可真够惨的。

Text.en: I am truly sorry for what has become of your home.
Text.zh: 看到你们的家乡落到这般田地，我真的很痛心。

Text.en: It still has a certain... charm.
Text.zh: 倒也还有几分……韵味。

Text.en: Never thought I'd see Kingsmarch in such a state.
Text.zh: 真没想到君锋镇会变成这副模样。

Text.en: It saddens me to see Kingsmarch in such a state.
Text.zh: 看到君锋镇破败成这个样子，我心里真不好受。

Text.en: I'm sorry, lads. No one deserves to lose their home this way.
Text.zh: 真是遗憾，兄弟们。谁也不该这样失去自己的家园。

Text.en: Blimey... All your hard work... down the gutter...
Text.zh: 天哪……你这些年所有的心血……全打水漂了……

Text.en: It was terrible, and so... sudden. Felt like the world might break in half.
Text.zh: 太可怕了，而且……来得那么突然，感觉世界都要裂成两半了。

Text.en: Kingsmarch... torn apart in an instant.
Text.zh: 君锋镇……一瞬间就毁了。

Text.en: We weren't prepared... but then again, what would it have changed?
Text.zh: 我们根本来不及准备……不过说实话，就算准备了又能怎样呢？

Text.en: And we can't even drink, cause the tavern is rubble.
Text.zh: 连喝一杯都不成了，酒馆都成废墟了。

Text.en: For whatever it's worth... I'm sorry.
Text.zh: 我知道说什么都没用……可我还是想说，我很遗憾。

Text.en: That means a lot, coming from you.
Text.zh: 你能这么说，我心里好受多了。

Text.en: Aye... talk about salt in the wound...
Text.zh: 是啊……真是往伤口上撒盐啊……

Text.en: Twenty years of progress... undone in the blink of an eye.
Text.zh: 二十年的成果……眨眼之间化为乌有。

Text.en: No sarcasm, little one? This <i>{is} truly dire, then.
Text.zh: 小家伙，怎么没嘲讽两句？看来这次真<i>{是}糟透了。

Text.en: It may not mean much... but I will do what I can to help you rebuild.
Text.zh: 这也许算不上什么……但我会尽力帮你们重建。

Text.en: I wish I could have done something to prevent it.
Text.zh: 真希望我当时能做点什么来阻止这一切。

Text.en: Ach... as if things weren't dire enough already.
Text.zh: 唉……情况已经够糟的了。

Text.en: The world's got a sick sense o' humour, don't it?
Text.zh: 命运还真爱捉弄人，不是吗？

Text.en: Dark times. We won't be trading any time soon. And worst of all, still no word from Gwen!
Text.zh: 这日子真难。一时半会儿是没法做生意了。最糟的是，桂温妮还是没消息！

Text.en: Oh, Gwennen... I hope you're okay.
Text.zh: 哦，桂温妮……希望你平安无事。

Text.en: Oh, sorry Rog. I'm sure she's fine.
Text.zh: 啊，抱歉，罗格。她一定没事的。

Text.en: You're all together... good! I've got great news. <continue>I think I know where Gwennen is!
Text.zh: 你们都在……太好了！我有个天大的好消息。<continue>我好像知道桂温妮在哪儿了！

Text.en: Hail, Captain! That's wonderful news!
Text.zh: 幸会，船长！这真是天大的好消息！

Text.en: [NOAUDIO] It's my logbook, where I note landmarks I plan on revisiting.
Text.zh: 这是我的日志，我会把想重游的地方都记在上面。

Text.en: Oh, do you really think you've found her?
Text.zh: 你真觉得你找到她了？

Text.en: I'm almost positive. I've seen signs of Gwennen in a few locations. All noted down here, in this logbook. <continue>The only problem is, these are dangerous areas...
Text.zh: 我几乎能肯定。我在好几个地方都发现了桂温妮的踪迹。全记在这本日志里了。<continue>唯一的麻烦是，这些地方都很危险……

Text.en: Good thing I'm used to danger, then.
Text.zh: 那还好，我早就习惯了危险。

Text.en: Lucky for you lot, I'm not averse to a little danger.
Text.zh: 算你们走运，我可不怕冒点风险。

Text.en: Then allow me to brave that danger.
Text.zh: 那就让我去面对这危险吧。

Text.en: Allow me to skip ahead to where we all know this conversation is headed. <continue>You all stay. <i>{I'll} go do the hard parts.
Text.zh: 反正我们都知道最后会怎样，就由我替大家把话说完吧。<continue>你们都留下。<i>{我}去干那些脏活累活。

Text.en: This is Wraeclast. If we didn't venture to dangerous areas, we'd be standing in place.
Text.zh: 这里可是瓦尔克拉斯。不敢去危险的地方，那就只能在原地踏步。

Text.en: I'll go. One of us has to... It might as well be me.
Text.zh: 我去吧。总得有人去……不如让我去。

Text.en: Since when have we let that stop us?
Text.zh: 我们什么时候被危险吓退过？

Text.en: If it's danger you're worried about, I'm your man.
Text.zh: 要是担心危险，那就交给我吧。

Text.en: I would go too, but, uh...
Text.zh: 我倒想去，可是……

Text.en: The kids, Rog. You're right where you should be for now.
Text.zh: 孩子要紧，罗格。眼下你就该守在这儿。

Text.en: We must stay here. Kingsmarch and its people need our immediate attention.
Text.zh: 我们必须留下。君锋镇和这里的人都指望着我们呢。

Text.en: It's settled then. I'm ready when you are!
Text.zh: 那就这么定了。我随时可以出发！

Text.en: Oh, it's been too long! I'm so happy to see you all.
Text.zh: 噢，好久不见了！见到你们真高兴。

Text.en: Ha! I knew you were alive! Never doubted for a second.
Text.zh: 哈！我就知道你还活着。我压根儿就没怀疑过。

Text.en: Oh, <i>{darling!} What happened? Are you hurt?
Text.zh: 噢， <i>{亲爱的！}出什么事了？你受伤了吗？

Text.en: I'm fine, Rog. Luck is always on my side! And... I've discovered something extraordinary... <continue>I found Medved himself!
Text.zh: 我没事，罗格。运气向来都眷顾我！而且……我有一个惊天大发现……<continue>我找到梅德维德本人了！

Text.en: What? His... remains, you mean?
Text.zh: 什么？你是说……他的遗骸？

Text.en: No. He was undead... and he put up quite the fight.
Text.zh: 没有。他是亡灵……而且还挺能打的。

Text.en: His <i>{reanimated} remains. He was undead.
Text.zh: 是他<i>{复生的}遗骸。他是亡灵。

Text.en: He's naught but remains <i>{now.} He was very violent – and very undead – when we found him.
Text.zh: <i>{如今}确实是遗骸了。可我们找到他的时候，他可是活蹦乱跳的——虽说是亡灵。

Text.en: Not quite... unless you count being undead as 'remains'.
Text.zh: 那倒不是……除非你把亡灵也算作“遗骸”。

Text.en: If only... He were undead. We had to put 'im down.
Text.zh: 要是那样就好了……他是亡灵。我们只能把他解决了。

Text.en: Well, he's <i>{dead}-dead now. Wasn't that way when we found him, though.
Text.zh: 嗯，他现在是彻底<i>{死透了}。我们找到他的时候，可不是这样。

Text.en: Not so. He may have 'died' many years ago, but he lived on in undeath – until we put him down.
Text.zh: 不是的。他也许很多年前就“死了”，但一直以亡灵状态活着——直到我们把他了结了。

Text.en: Nay, lad. His spirit remained bound to its fleshy prison, same as it ever was.
Text.zh: 不是的，小子。他的灵魂一直困在那具血肉牢笼里，和以往没什么两样。

Text.en: That doesn't bode well...
Text.zh: 这可不是什么好兆头……

Text.en: I uncovered writings of a Remembrancer who was tracking Medved, after he'd... fallen. <continue>After many failed attempts, I finally found Medved's tomb. But before I could enter, I was swarmed by monsters. <continue>I hid. Couldn't reach my boat, couldn't reach the tomb. Surviving on rainwater and <i>{hope}... until your local friend arrived just in time.
Text.zh: 我找到了一位文书的手稿，他一直在追踪梅德维德，就在梅德维德……堕落之后。<continue>我失败了好多次，终于找到了梅德维德的墓地。可我还没进去，就被一群怪物团团围住。<continue>我只好躲起来。没法回船，也进不了墓地。靠雨水和<i>{希望}撑着……直到你那本地朋友及时赶到。

Text.en: I'm just glad I could help.
Text.zh: 能帮上忙就好。

Text.en: Saving people is becoming a bad habit of mine.
Text.zh: 这都快成我的老本行了。

Text.en: I cannot claim credit. Were it not for these men, I might never have found you.
Text.zh: 我不敢居功。要不是这几位，我可能永远也找不到你。

Text.en: I seem to do a lot of that these days.
Text.zh: 我最近好像老干这种事儿。

Text.en: It's Makoru who deserves the credit, really. She were the one what pointed us in the right direction.
Text.zh: 其实马克鲁才是最大的功臣。是她指引我们找对了方向。

Text.en: It would be remiss of me to take all the credit. Everyone here played their part – and Makoru as well.
Text.zh: 我可不能把功劳全揽在自己身上。在场的各位都出了力——马克鲁也是。

Text.en: I must remember to thank her!
Text.zh: 我一定要好好谢谢她！

Text.en: You've got all these blokes in your corner. Couldn't have found you without 'em.
Text.zh: 都要感谢你这帮兄弟的帮衬。没他们，还真找不到你。

Text.en: Aye, lass. You were in a tight spot. Just glad I found ye when I did.
Text.zh: 是啊，姑娘。你当时可真是身处险境‌。幸好我及时找到了你。

Text.en: On the bright side... we found a logbook written by Vorana herself!
Text.zh: 好消息是……我们找到了沃拉娜亲笔写的航海日志！

Text.en: Really? Remarkable!
Text.zh: 真的？太了不起了！

Text.en: Oh, I wish you were coming home to better circumstances, Gwen.
Text.zh: 要是你回来时这儿还好好的就好了，桂温妮。

Text.en: Look at the state of it here... we're basically starting from scratch!
Text.zh: 看看这儿成什么样了……咱们差不多得从头开始了。

Text.en: Yes! It's almost too perfect! Our good luck strikes again.
Text.zh: 是啊……简直太完美了！咱们的好运气又来了。

Text.en: Perfect? In what sense?
Text.zh: 太完美了？这话怎么说？

Text.en: That's a new one. Perfect how?
Text.zh: 这可真新鲜，哪里“完美”了？

Text.en: I'm afraid I do not follow.
Text.zh: 恐怕我没太明白你的意思。

Text.en: Did you hit your head?
Text.zh: 你是撞到脑袋了吗？

Text.en: I'm sorry... did you say <i>{good} luck?
Text.zh: 抱歉……你刚才是说<i>{好}运气？

Text.en: Eh? You feelin' alright, miss?
Text.zh: 嗯？你没事吧，小姐？

Text.en: How so?
Text.zh: 这话怎么讲？

Text.en: Seems like the opposite o' good luck from where I'm standin'.
Text.zh: 在我看来，这更像是倒霉透顶。

Text.en: Hmmm... I suppose if there's no Kingsmarch, there's no King's charter to uphold.
Text.zh: 嗯……要是君锋镇不在了，那也用不着什么国王特许经营权了吧。

Text.en: Exactly!
Text.zh: 没错！

Text.en: Oh, I don't know, Gwen. What about the kids? Isn't this a bit risky?
Text.zh: 哦，这我可说不好，桂温妮。孩子们怎么办？是不是太冒险了？

Text.en: What else should we do? None of us will ever truly be safe from <i>{him}, we all know that... <continue>And with this new information, there's a chance we could actually find the Triskelion Flame! <continue>The Remembrancers claimed Uhtred stole the Triskelion. And Vorana was the last to see him.
Text.zh: 那我们还能怎么办？你我都清楚，没人能完全躲开<i>{他}……<continue>而且有了这条新线索，咱们说不定真能找到三耀之火！<continue>那些文书说，是乌崔德偷走了三耀之火，而沃拉娜是最后一个见到他的人。

Text.en: ... so Vorana might lead us to Uhtred. And perhaps... to the Triskelion itself.
Text.zh: ……所以，沃拉娜能指引我们找到乌崔德。甚至可能……找到三耀之火。

Text.en: <i>{Finally!} I'm tired of waiting around. We were meant for adventure!
Text.zh: <i>{终于！}我可等烦了。咱们生来就该去冒险！

Text.en: Then it's settled. The grand quest is reignited!
Text.zh: 那就这么定了。伟大使命要重启了！

Text.en: Tujen, take Vorana's logbook. You and the local should seek her out. I'm sure you'll find more answers there.
Text.zh: 图金！你拿着沃拉娜的日志。你和这位当地朋友去找她。我敢说，你们能从那儿解开更多疑惑。

Text.en: Alright! You and me, we're gonna track down Vorana. This logbook has all the information we need to find her. <continue>You thought Medved was fearsome? Ha! Vorana led the Black Scythes! A legendary warrior, and a master tactician.
Text.zh: 好了！就咱俩，去把沃拉娜找出来。要想找到她，这本日志里的线索足够了。<continue>你觉得梅德维德很可怕？哈！沃拉娜统领的可是漆黑之镰！她是传奇战士，也是战术大师。

Text.en: Then let this be a fight to remember.
Text.zh: 那就让此战，成为一段难忘的传奇吧。

Text.en: All fine things to put on her tombstone.
Text.zh: 这些话刻在她墓碑上倒不错。

Text.en: A true <i>{dekhara} – but even the mightiest can be beat.
Text.zh: 一个真正的<i>{德卡拉}——但再强的人也会倒下。

Text.en: Tactics can only get you so far.
Text.zh: 战术再高明，也总有极限

Text.en: Every warrior's gotta lose sometime.
Text.zh: 每个战士都有败北的时候。

Text.en: Sounds like she'll be a kill worth boastin' about, then.
Text.zh: 听起来，她会是个值得吹嘘的战绩。

Text.en: She will make for a worthy opponent.
Text.zh: 她会是个值得尊敬的对手。

Text.en: Let's see if she can keep to her tactics with us bearin' down on her.
Text.zh: 看看我们杀到她面前时，她还能不能坚持那些战术。

Text.en: [DNT]
Text.zh: 无所谓，任谁都会流血。

Text.en: Gather your supplies. You'll want to be prepared for this one.
Text.zh: 收拾好你的装备。这一趟，得做足准备。

Text.en: This is it! I think. While you were loafin' about on the ship, I set up <i>{the explosives.}
Text.zh: 我猜就是这儿！你在船上闲逛那会儿，我把<i>{炸药}埋好了。

Text.en: Should pop the lid right open, so you can jump inside and size the place up.
Text.zh: 你得把这盖子整个掀开，才能跳进去把里面打探清楚。

Text.en: What is this place?
Text.zh: 这是什么地方？

Text.en: And you're sure this is the right place? What is it?
Text.zh: 你确定是这儿？这是什么地方？

Text.en: And you're sure this is the place?
Text.zh: 你确定是这地方？

Text.en: It's an educated guess from an uneducated man.
Text.zh: 这是一个没文化的人做出的有文化的猜测。

Text.en: Where exactly are we?
Text.zh: 我们究竟在哪儿？

Text.en: What can you tell about this place?
Text.zh: 关于这地方，你能看出什么？

Text.en: What's all this, then?
Text.zh: 这里到底什么情况？

Text.en: What is this place?
Text.zh: 这地方是哪儿？

Text.en: What d'ye know o' this place?
Text.zh: 你对这地方有什么了解？

Text.en: [DNT]
Text.zh: 所以这里究竟是什么地方？

Text.en: Looks to be a Kalguuran fortification. Or it could be an outhouse! I told you, I'm not a tour guide!
Text.zh: 看着像是卡古兰的堡垒，也可能是茅房！我说了，我又不是向导！

Text.en: I did not mean to offend you.
Text.zh: 我不是有意冒犯你的。

Text.en: Touchy today, aren't we?
Text.zh: 你今天火气挺大啊？

Text.en: Your belligerence helps no one.
Text.zh: 你这杠精性格对谁都没好处。

Text.en: Excuse me for asking a simple question.
Text.zh: 我就问个问题而已。

Text.en: Right then. Forget I asked...
Text.zh: 行吧，当我没问……

Text.en: Well, great. Good talk.
Text.zh: 很好，你太会说话了。

Text.en: Infighting only benefits our foes.
Text.zh: 内讧只会便宜敌人。

Text.en: Speak to me like that again, lad, and the next outhouse I see, I'm shovin' you in it.
Text.zh: 小子，你要再敢这么说话，待会看到茅坑，我就把你塞进去。

Text.en: Ugh. I know... I'm just... nervous. Okay? <i>{Happy?} Let's get this over with.
Text.zh: 唉，我知道……我就是……太紧张了。行了吧？<i>{满意了？}赶紧了结这事吧。

Text.en: What in the world became of her?
Text.zh: 她这是怎么了？

Text.en: Even in death, she fought well.
Text.zh: 哪怕是死了，她也这么能打。

Text.en: She fought well. Just not well enough.
Text.zh: 她打得很漂亮，只是还不够漂亮。

Text.en: The tales were true. Even in death, she made for a mighty <i>{dekhara.}
Text.zh: 传说都是真的。即便在死后，她仍是一位强大的<i>{德卡拉}。

Text.en: She sure didn't make it easy on us.
Text.zh: 赢下她可一点不轻松。

Text.en: She put up a fight, I'll give her that.
Text.zh: 不得不承认，她打得很拼。

Text.en: She went down swingin', that's for sure.
Text.zh: 她拼到了最后一刻才肯倒下。

Text.en: She fought valiantly.
Text.zh: 这一战，她战得很英勇。

Text.en: She fought well for a dead lass.
Text.zh: 哪怕是死了，她也一样能打。

Text.en: I expected nothin' less. But to finally reach her, and see her like this... <continue>It's chilling, is all.
Text.zh: 我早就料到会这样。可真找到她，看到她这副样子……<continue>还真叫人后背发凉。

Text.en: Did you find anything?
Text.zh: 你找到什么了吗？

Text.en: I have Uhtred's logbook.
Text.zh: 我拿到了乌崔德的日志。

Text.en: Yes! I found Uhtred's diary. Logbook. Same thing.
Text.zh: 对！我找到了乌崔德的日记。日志。大差不差啦。

Text.en: Indeed I did – Uhtred's logbook.
Text.zh: 没错，乌崔德的日志。

Text.en: Pretty sure this is Uhtred's logbook.
Text.zh: 我很确定，这就是乌崔德的日志。

Text.en: Aye. I've got Uhtred's logbook.
Text.zh: 对，乌崔德的日志到手了。

Text.en: Sure did. Feast your eyes on Uhtred's logbook.
Text.zh: 没错，睁大眼睛瞧好了——乌崔德的日志。

Text.en: Thankfully so. I found Uhtred's logbook.
Text.zh: 万幸。我找到了乌崔德的日志。

Text.en: Aye, lad, I did. Got Uhtred's logbook right here.
Text.zh: 对，小子，我找到了。乌崔德的日志就在这儿。

Text.en: Well, that's a scrap of good news. Best take it to Rog. Uhtred's his domain.
Text.zh: 好歹也算是好消息。最好拿去给罗格。乌崔德的事都归他管。

Text.en: You better grab that bloody logbook on the floor!  Y'know, it's the whole reason we're here! <continue>Once you have it, best go speak with Rog.
Text.zh: 你最好把那本破日志从地上捡起来，别忘了，咱们可是为了它来的！<continue>拿到手之后，赶紧去找罗格吧。

Text.en: You still have not heard from Gwennen?
Text.zh: 还是没有桂温妮的消息？

Text.en: Heard from your missing companion yet?
Text.zh: 你那位失踪的同伴还没消息吗？

Text.en: I see the worry on your face. Still nothing of your Gwennen?
Text.zh: 我看得出你很担心。桂温妮还没消息吗？

Text.en: In these conditions, I understand your concern for Gwen. You've received no word?
Text.zh: 这种情况下，我理解你担心桂温妮。现在还没消息？

Text.en: Still no word from your Gwen?
Text.zh: 你家那位桂温妮还是没消息？

Text.en: Any word yet from Gwen?
Text.zh: 桂温妮有消息了没？

Text.en: Are you still waiting to hear from Gwennen?
Text.zh: 你还在等桂温妮的音信吗？

Text.en: Still no word from Gwennen, I take it?
Text.zh: 我猜，还是没有桂温妮的消息？

Text.en: No word from Gwen at all. With the state of things... I don't even want to think about it! <continue>She was out there, carryin' on our grand quest. Used to give her shite about that, heh. <continue>Makoru believes she can find her. If anyone can, it's her.
Text.zh: 桂温妮一点消息都没有。这世道……我都不敢想！<continue>她还在外面，继续着咱们那伟大使命。我以前还老拿这事儿笑话她，嘿嘿。<continue>马克鲁说能找到她。能办成这个事的，也就是她了。

Text.en: Who is Gwen?
Text.zh: 桂温妮是谁？

Text.en: Who is this Gwendolyn? Or whatever her name is.
Text.zh: 这位格温多琳……管她叫什么呢……到底是谁？

Text.en: Who is this Gwennen you all speak of?
Text.zh: 你们老提到的这个桂温妮是谁？

Text.en: Who is Gwennen?
Text.zh: 桂温妮是谁？

Text.en: Who is this Gwennen o' yours, anyway?
Text.zh: 你的这位桂温妮到底是谁？

Text.en: I haven't seen this Gwennen. Who is she?
Text.zh: 我还没见过这位桂温妮。她是什么人？

Text.en: Who is this Gwennen?
Text.zh: 这“桂温妮”又是哪位？

Text.en: I keep hearin' the name Gwennen, but I'm yet to meet the lass.
Text.zh: 我老听人提起桂温妮这个名字，但还没见过这位姑娘呢。

Text.en: Gwennen? She's the fourth member of our crew. Bravest of us all. <continue>She's been missin' for a while. She didn't love Kingsmarch, but she'd come around, for our sakes.
Text.zh: 桂温妮？她是我们船上的第四个成员，也是最勇敢的那一个。<continue>她失踪好些日子了。她其实并不喜欢君锋镇，不过为了我们，她还是留下来了。

Text.en: And you have not heard from her?
Text.zh: 然后你们一直没收到她的消息？

Text.en: You haven't heard anything?
Text.zh: 一点消息都没有？

Text.en: And you've received no word on where she's gone?
Text.zh: 你们就一点也没听说她去了哪里？

Text.en: But you haven't heard from her at all?
Text.zh: 可你完全没她的消息？

Text.en: No word on where she is?
Text.zh: 不知道她在哪儿？

Text.en: So that's it? No word from her?
Text.zh: 就这样？没有她的消息？

Text.en: She's sent no word on her whereabouts?
Text.zh: 她没告知你她的去向？

Text.en: I take it ye haven't heard from her, then.
Text.zh: 我估摸着，之后你就没收到她的消息了。

Text.en: No word from Gwen at all. With the state of things... I don't even want to think about it! <continue>She was out there, carryin' on our grand quest. Used to give her shite about that, heh. <continue>Makoru believes she can find her. If anyone can, it's her.
Text.zh: 桂温妮一点消息都没有。这世道……我都不敢想！<continue>她在外面，继续着咱们那伟大使命。我以前还老拿这事儿笑话她，嘿嘿。<continue>马克鲁说能找到她。能办成这个事的，也就是她了。

Text.en: Ohh, what a relief. I was starting to fear the worst. Couldn't let Rog see that, though. <continue>Thank you for bringin' her back. Gwen's tough, but... she was lucky you found her. <continue>Don't tell her I said she was lucky! She'll never shut up about it.
Text.zh: 总算松了口气！我先前都怕她出事了。可不能让罗格看出来。<continue>多谢你把她带回来。桂温妮真够硬气的，可……遇上你，也是她走运。<continue>别告诉她我说她走运！不然她得念叨个没完。

Text.en: You should let people see this softer side of you more often.
Text.zh: 你真该多让别人看看你这温柔的一面。

Text.en: What's happened to you, little one? Compared to your usual self, you seem positively gooey. <continue>I hate it.
Text.zh: 你这是怎么了，小家伙？跟平时比起来，你现在简直黏糊得不行。<continue>真是烦人。

Text.en: Master Tujen... I have never seen this gentle side to you before.
Text.zh: 图金先生……我还是头一次见到你这么温柔。

Text.en: Look at you, caring about the wellbeing of other people.
Text.zh: 瞧瞧你，还会关心别人。

Text.en: You know, it wouldn't hurt to show this softer side every once in a while...
Text.zh: 说真的，偶尔露一下这副软心肠也好，又不会少块肉……

Text.en: Well, well. Never took you for such a softy!
Text.zh: 哎哟，真没想到你是软心肠的汉子。

Text.en: It seems even you have a gentler side.
Text.zh: 看来你也有温柔的一面。

Text.en: Could this be a rare moment o' sentimentality from our resident grouch?
Text.zh: 咱们这位臭脾气先生也有多愁善感的时候？真是稀奇。

Text.en: Don't go on about it, will ya?
Text.zh: 别没完没了了，行不行？

Text.en: This logbook is written by Vorana herself! Usually, a Remembrancer would write these. A precious artefact, this. <continue>Us Kalguurans like to chronicle our lives. Leave a legacy for our descendants, you know? If you're lucky enough to have 'em.
Text.zh: 这本日志，是沃拉娜亲笔写的！一般来说这都是文书的事。这可是件宝贝。<continue>我们卡古兰人，喜欢记录生平，给后代留点什么，你懂吧？前提是你有后代。

Text.en: Like a diary? What does it say?
Text.zh: 像本日记？上面写什么了？

Text.en: What, like a diary?
Text.zh: 什么，像一本日记？

Text.en: No, like an important historical text! Listen up.
Text.zh: 不，像是重要的历史文献！认真听！

Text.en: That is a sound practice. What has she written?
Text.zh: 这习惯很好。她写了些什么？

Text.en: A wonderful tradition. Similar to our tale-women. What does it say?
Text.zh: 很好的传统。跟我们的说书人类似。她写了什么？

Text.en: What's in the logbook?
Text.zh: 日志里写了什么？

Text.en: Good thing we found it then. What's it say?
Text.zh: 幸亏我们找到了。上面说什么？

Text.en: What does Vorana's logbook say?
Text.zh: 沃拉娜的日志里写了什么？

Text.en: We're the lucky ones here. Leaves us a trail to follow. <continue>Now what does it say?
Text.zh: 咱们运气不错，至少给我们留了条线索。<continue>说说吧，上面写了什么？

Text.en: I'll read it for ya. Ahem.
Text.zh: 我念给你听。咳咳。
