Text.en: 'My messenger has not returned from Uhtred's hiding place. Now the starlight barrier has vanished. What has that traitor done with the Triskelion Flame?' <continue>'In my dreams, I relive that moment. Medved, ordering monstrosities to tear my men apart. <i>{Our} men.' <continue>'In his eyes, I saw nothing of the man he once was. I know what I must do.' <continue>'The pain of driving gems into my flesh was a welcome reprieve from this heartache. The rush of power... it's overwhelming. I must find Medved, before I lose myself.' <continue>Oh, oh, hang on. The penmanship gets a bit rough at this point. Ahem. <continue>'How can this be? The monsters are still organised, yet Medved remains trapped. Could Uhtred speak true? Are Olroth and Medved both traitorous wretches?' <continue>'I've tracked a rank fallen to an old scouting fortification. They appear to be using Kalguuran sites for their own depraved plans.' <continue>'My dear Medved. I will save us all. Come find me, where the lavender climbs and the waves carve through stone. The Flame will purify us. I promise.' <continue>That's the end of it. The rest is just meaningless scratches, so...
Text.zh: “我派去乌崔德藏身处的信使至今未归。如今星光屏障已经消失。那个叛徒，到底把三耀之火怎么样了？”<continue>“梦里，我一遍遍回到那一刻。梅德维德命令那些怪物撕碎我的部下。<i>{我们的}部下。”<continue>“他的眼神中，我再也看不到从前的他。我知道自己该做什么。”<continue>“将宝石刺入血肉的痛楚，反倒让我暂时忘了内心的煎熬。力量的奔涌……难以抗拒。我必须找到梅德维德，趁我还没迷失自我。”<continue>哦，等等。这里开始字迹有点潦草了。咳咳。<continue>“怎么会这样？怪物依然秩序井然，可梅德维德明明还被困着。难道乌崔德说的是真的？难道欧罗什和梅德维德，都是叛徒？”<continue>“我追踪一个小队，到了个旧侦察碉堡。他们似乎在利用卡古兰的据点，搞那些见不得人的勾当。”<continue>“我亲爱的梅德维德。我会拯救我们所有人。来找我吧，在藤蔓疯长、海浪蚀穿岩石的地方。三耀之火会净化我们。我保证。”<continue>没了。剩下的就是一些看不懂的涂写了……

Text.en: You want to know more about Vorana? I'll give you the highlights. <continue>Vorana was chosen for the first expedition because she was one of the most fearsome warriors of Kalguur. <continue>She had refused to serve in the King's army, and killed every man the King sent to arrest her.
Text.zh: 你想多了解了解沃拉娜？我给你说说关键的。<continue>沃拉娜能被选入第一次远征，就是因为她是卡古兰最可怕的战士之一。<continue>她拒绝在国王军中效力，还把国王派去抓她的人全都杀了。

Text.en: Did she travel to Wraeclast to escape the King's wrath, then?
Text.zh: 所以，她是为了逃避国王的怒火，才来到了瓦尔克拉斯？

Text.en: What happened then? Did she flee? Did she kill the King?
Text.zh: 然后呢？她逃走了？她把国王杀了？

Text.en: Actually, no.
Text.zh: 实际上，并没有！

Text.en: Well, that can't have ended well for her.
Text.zh: 嗯，那她的下场恐怕好不了。

Text.en: So the lass was exiled for her crimes?
Text.zh: 这么说，那姑娘是因为犯了事才被流放？

Text.en: Ha! Not quite. You won't believe this!
Text.zh: 哈！差远了。你绝对不会信！

Text.en: That's very impressive. I like this Vorana.
Text.zh: 厉害啊。我喜欢这个沃拉娜。

Text.en: Good for her. She must've been strong.
Text.zh: 她可真行。她一定很强。

Text.en: That's no small feat.
Text.zh: 那可不是小本事。

Text.en: She must have been a remarkable warrior.
Text.zh: 她一定是一位非凡的战士。

Text.en: Right? She was as tough as they come!
Text.zh: 对吧？她可真是个硬茬子。

Text.en: She defied the King, and not only lived - she was rewarded. Can you imagine? <i>{That's} how strong she was. <continue>Her mercenaries could wipe out entire regions in a single day. But she wasn't <i>{all} violence. <continue>She stood up for what's right. She protected the weak. And she was a loving mother. <continue>Vorana and Medved's daughter, Annest, was sent back to Kalguur once the expedition went south. <continue>Gwennen and I are actually descendants of Annest.
Text.zh: 她违抗王命，不仅没死，反而还得了赏赐。你敢相信吗？她就厉害到<i>{这份上}。<continue>她的佣兵团，一天之内能踏平一整片区域。不过她也不 <i>{光}会打打杀杀。<continue>她为正义出头，会保护弱者，而且还是个慈爱的母亲。<continue>远征队出事之后，沃拉娜和梅德维德的女儿安妮斯特，就被送回了卡古兰。<continue>我和桂温妮，其实就是安妮斯特的后人。

Text.en: I understand wanting to know more about your ancestors.
Text.zh: 我明白，所以你想多了解自己祖先。

Text.en: I see. So you have a personal interest in learning her fate.
Text.zh: 我明白了。所以你对她的命运也格外在意。

Text.en: So you're personally invested in this?
Text.zh: 所以这事对你来说很私人啊？

Text.en: So you want to know more about your ancestor, then?
Text.zh: 所以，你是想多了解自己的祖先？

Text.en: I see. So you seek to learn more about your lineage.
Text.zh: 我懂了。你是在追寻自己的血脉根源。

Text.en: Makes sense why you want to know what became o' her.
Text.zh: 怪不得你想知道她后来怎么样了。

Text.en: It's one thing to read about her, another to see it for yourself.
Text.zh: 在书里读到是一回事，亲眼见到又是另一回事。

Text.en: Gwennen's your sister? I can't say I see the resemblance.
Text.zh: 桂温妮是你妹妹？我是真没看出来。

Text.en: Hang on... Gwennen's your sister?
Text.zh: 等等……桂温妮是你妹妹？

Text.en: [DNT]
Text.zh: 那桂温妮岂不是你妹妹？

Text.en: No, she's my cousin! Is that <i>{really} the part you have questions about?
Text.zh: 不，她是我表妹！你<i>{真的}在纠结这个？

Text.en: We came to Wraeclast to discover the true history of that first expedition, and our ancestors. <continue>I want to learn how Vorana died. I bet she went down in a blaze of glory.
Text.zh: 我们来瓦尔克拉斯，就是想弄清楚第一次远征的真相，还有我们祖先的历史。<continue>我一直想知道沃拉娜是怎么死的。我猜她肯定是轰轰烈烈战死的。

Text.en: So tell me... what <i>{really} happened in there?
Text.zh: 那你跟我说说……里面<i>{到底}发生了什么？

Text.en: She attacked me with such fury, like she thought I was someone else. <continue>There was no reasoning with her. May her soul finally rest.
Text.zh: 她像是把我当成了别人，疯了一样扑上来。<continue>根本没法跟她讲道理。愿她的灵魂安息。

Text.en: Her soul? So she <i>{was} alive?
Text.zh: 灵魂？所以她<i>{当时}还活着？

Text.en: Not alive. She had risen, like Medved.
Text.zh: 不是活着，是复生，跟梅德维德一样。

Text.en: It seemed like she'd been trapped for ages and had gone a bit mad. <continue>I can't blame her. Being undead <i>{and} alone for that long is quite sad.
Text.zh: 看来她被关了太久，有点失心疯了。<continue>也怪不得她。变成了亡灵，<i>{还}独自待了那么久，真是可怜。

Text.en: She was undead? Are you sure?
Text.zh: 她是亡灵？你确定？

Text.en: Oh, yes. Very sure.
Text.zh: 是的，非常确定。

Text.en: It appears Vorana's tactics were used against her. Someone trapped her in there.
Text.zh: 看来沃拉娜最擅长的战术，最后反而害了她。有人把她困在了里面。

Text.en: Trapped her in there? How was she alive?
Text.zh: 把她困在里面？那她怎么还活着？

Text.en: Wraeclast has a strange sense of humour. She was not alive. She was risen, as her lover was.
Text.zh: 瓦尔克拉斯这地方还真爱捉弄人。她不是活人，是复生了，就像她的爱人一样。

Text.en: She gave me no choice. She was looking for a fight. <continue>I've fought many corpses. She was one of the strongest, if that's any consolation.
Text.zh: 我别无选择，她就是奔着干架来的。<continue>我解决过不少活死人。她算是最难缠的那种，不知道这能不能让你心里好受点。

Text.en: Not really, no. Wait, she was undead?
Text.zh: 完全不会！等等，她是亡灵？

Text.en: Aye, we fought summat fierce. Every bit a warrior as you proclaimed. <continue>I wonder if she was more fearsome in life, or in death.
Text.zh: 对，我们狠狠干了一架。她真是你夸的那样，十足的狠角色。我不禁想，她是活着的时候更可怕，还是死了之后。

Text.en: What? In death?
Text.zh: 什么？死了之后？

Text.en: In <i>{undeath,} more like.
Text.zh: 更准确地说，是<i>{不死亡灵}。

Text.en: Went about the same as these things often go. I enter a room, and the women attack me. <continue>They're usually not so scary. And usually alive.
Text.zh: 过程跟那事儿差不多。我进了屋子，女人们开始打我。<continue>只不过这次特别吓人。而且她们通常是活的。

Text.en: Oh, <i>{very} funny! I shouldn't have even asked.
Text.zh: 哈，<i>{真}有意思！我就不该问。

Text.en: Let me rephrase. Vorana was a risen corpse, and I had to fight her.
Text.zh: 换个说法。沃拉娜成了不死亡灵，我不得不跟她交手。

Text.en: You better not be joking. <i>{She} was a monster too?
Text.zh: 你最好不是在开玩笑。<i>{她}也成了怪物？

Text.en: It was regrettable. She spoke, but her words were for others. She cursed Olroth, and grieved Medved. <continue>My words could not reach her. She was blinded by fury.
Text.zh: 很遗憾。她说了很多话，但是对别人说的。她咒骂欧罗什，哀悼梅德维德。我的话根本传不到她耳朵里。她已经被狂怒吞没了。

Text.en: And that made you fight her? What am I missing?
Text.zh: 所以你就跟她打起来了？我是不是漏听了什么？

Text.en: No. I'm sorry, Tujen. She was a risen horror, as Medved was.
Text.zh: 你没有。抱歉，图金。她像梅德维德一样，是复生的怪物。

Text.en: The stories of her prowess are all true. She fought me like her life depended on it! <continue>Killin' her was a mercy. No one deserves that fate.
Text.zh: 关于她勇武过人的传闻全都是真的。她拼了命一样跟我战斗！<continue>把她杀了反而是种慈悲。没人该落得那种下场。

Text.en: You killed her? What, so she was alive?
Text.zh: 你把她杀了？所以她之前还活着？

Text.en: Ach, no. She was a shambler. For a long time, I'd guess.
Text.zh: 啊，也不是。她是行尸走肉。我猜，已经很久了。

Text.en: Ugh... I don't get it. She used gems, sure, but you use 'em, and you're no abomination. Not like that, anyway. <continue>How did <i>{she} become one of <i>{them?}
Text.zh: 唉……我真搞不懂。她是用过宝石没错，可你也在用啊，你不也没变成怪物。至少没变成那样。<continue><i>{她}怎么就成了<i>{那种东西}呢？

Text.en: Could she have found Olroth? Maybe he killed her.
Text.zh: 她是不是找到欧罗什了？可能欧罗什杀了她。

Text.en: She was tracking Olroth. If she found him, she may have simply lost that fight.
Text.zh: 她一直在追踪欧罗什。要是找到了，可能就是打输了。

Text.en: She was one woman taking on hordes of monsters. She was cornered, outnumbered, and killed.
Text.zh: 她一个人对抗成群的怪物，被逼到绝路，寡不敌众，然后被杀死了。

Text.en: I'd like to believe that. But why bother trapping her after? It doesn't make sense.
Text.zh: 我倒是愿意信这个。可那之后为什么还要把她困起来？说不通啊。

Text.en: It wasn't a Necromancer's doing. Vorana still had her own will. <continue>But, as they say: 'No one stays dead on Wraeclast for long.'
Text.zh: 这不像是死灵法师干的。沃拉娜当时还有自己的意志。<continue>不过大家不都说了嘛，“在瓦尔克拉斯，没人能死太久。”

Text.en: She steeped herself in Corruption to fight Corruption. It was a fitting end... only miserable.
Text.zh: 为了对抗腐化，她把自己浸染在腐化之中。结局也很合理……只是很可悲。

Text.en: The power o' <i>{rathin} has its price.
Text.zh: <i>{腐化}的力量，总要付出代价。

Text.en: She would not be the first to succumb to the overwhelming power of Corruption.
Text.zh: 她不是第一个被腐化吞噬的人。

Text.en: Any number o' ways, really. Corruption's rife on Wraeclast. As is necromancy.
Text.zh: 说真的，办法太多了。瓦尔克拉斯上腐化横行，更别提死灵法术了。

Text.en: So she would become a monster, no matter how she died?
Text.zh: 所以不管她怎么死的，最后都会变成怪物？

Text.en: She deserved better. Those damn gems allowed Corruption to twist her into the very thing she was fighting. <continue>Will you <i>{all} end up like that? Ugh. Actually, don't answer that.
Text.zh: 她不该落得这般下场。那些破宝石，让腐化把她变成了她曾经对抗的那种东西。<continue>你们<i>{都}会变成那样吗？唉，算了，当我没问。

Text.en: Honestly, it would've been easier for me to accept that the Triskelion Flame was lost forever. <continue>To have such an important part of your history, your legacy, broken like that? <continue>I don't feel much like talking. Gotta figure out another way to get to the crater.
Text.zh: 说实话，要是三耀之火永远找不回来了，我心里反倒好受些。<continue>你们历史中那么重要的东西，传承下来的遗产，就这么碎了？<continue>我现在没心情多说了。得想别的法子去陨石坑才行。

Text.en: Unbelievable! Dannig and Farrow did it! The Triskelion Flame is restored!
Text.zh: 难以置信！丹宁和法罗居然成功了！三耀之火被重铸了！

Text.en: Yes! They put all the pieces together, and made me carry it.
Text.zh: 对！他们把碎片拼起来了，还让我背着它。

Text.en: Yeah... Restored, and heavy as anythin'. They made me carry the damn thing.
Text.zh: 对……修好了，而且沉得要命。那些家伙居然让我一路背回来。

Text.en: Hahahahah! Even better.
Text.zh: 哈哈哈哈！甚好甚好。

Text.en: It is amazing craftsmanship. They are master runesmiths.
Text.zh: 这工艺当真了不起。他们真是大师级的符文工匠。

Text.en: It is quite impressive, yes.
Text.zh: 确实相当厉害。

Text.en: Surprised they could put it back together at all, given how ancient it is.
Text.zh: 这么古老的物件，他们居然还能拼回去，真叫人吃惊。

Text.en: Done themselves proud, they have.
Text.zh: 他们干得很漂亮。

Text.en: It is quite the accomplishment.
Text.zh: 这确实是了不起的成就。

Text.en: Aye, can scarce believe it meself. We're lucky we ran into Farrow when we did.
Text.zh: 是啊，连我自己都不敢相信。幸亏我们遇见了法罗。

Text.en: Ah, it's brilliant! They're like the Runefathers of old. <continue>That Ezomyte friend of yours is a legend. Absolute <i>{legend!} Ha! <continue>Ahh... Damn! We gotta build that tavern, quick. I need to buy <i>{him} a drink!
Text.zh: 啊，太厉害了！他们就像古代的符文之父一样。<continue>你那个艾兹麦朋友真是个传奇人物。真<i>{神了}！<continue>啊……该死！咱们得赶紧把酒馆建起来。我得请<i>{他}喝一杯！

Text.en: No! No! I will not cut their throats...
Text.zh: 不！不行！我不能割他们的喉咙……

Text.en: They are but babes!
Text.zh: 他们都还是小孩！

Text.en: Old man... are you okay?
Text.zh: 老人家……你还好吗？

Text.en: Is this a game?
Text.zh: 这是在玩什么把戏？

Text.en: What are you talking about?
Text.zh: 你在说什么？

Text.en: What are you raving about?
Text.zh: 你在胡言乱语什么？

Text.en: I don't see any babes...
Text.zh: 我没看见什么小孩啊……

Text.en: What's goin' on?
Text.zh: 怎么回事？

Text.en: Are you alright?
Text.zh: 你没事吧？

Text.en: What are ye talkin' about?
Text.zh: 你在说什么？

Text.en: The only judgement I face is that of the Spirit! <continue>The Winter Owl sees <i>{me} for who I am!
Text.zh: 我只接受万灵的裁决！<continue>凛冬之枭看得见<i>{我}的本心！

Text.en: Hmm... seems not.
Text.zh: 嗯……我看未必吧。

Text.en: I don't get it.
Text.zh: 我听不懂。

Text.en: He does not hear me...
Text.zh: 他听不见我说话……

Text.en: Strange. It's like I'm not even here...
Text.zh: 奇怪，就好像我根本不存在似的……

Text.en: Aye, the Spirit judges us all.
Text.zh: 是啊，万灵会裁决我们所有人。

Text.en: Mate... you lost your mind?
Text.zh: 兄弟……你疯了吧？

Text.en: Judgement? What do you mean?
Text.zh: 裁决？什么意思？

Text.en: He's gone stark ravin' mad.
Text.zh: 他是彻底疯了。

Text.en: The Spirit connects our paths once more.
Text.zh: 万灵再次让我们相逢。

Text.en: Thank you. I know not where I was. <continue>You saved me from utter madness.
Text.zh: 多谢。我方才迷失了神智。<continue>是你把我从彻底发疯的边缘拉了回来。

Text.en: We meet again, Elder Madox. Are you alright?
Text.zh: 又见面了，马多克斯长老。你还好吗？

Text.en: It is an honour, Elder. Are you well enough to talk?
Text.zh: 幸会，长老。您身体怎么样，方便谈谈吗？

Text.en: Yes, I'm alright. But I bring grim news.
Text.zh: 嗯，我没事。但我带来了坏消息。

Text.en: You're rather far from the mountains, Elder. Are you lost?
Text.zh: 你怎么远离群山跑到这儿来了，长老。是迷路了吗？

Text.en: Admirin' your own reflection, Elder?
Text.zh: 长老，看自己看入迷了？

Text.en: What? No, I'm not... now listen.
Text.zh: 什么？不是，我……你听我说。

Text.en: Alright, Elder? Has observation brought ya here?
Text.zh: 好吧，长老。你是因为在察觉到什么才来这里的吗？

Text.en: I can scarce believe my eyes. What drew ye from yer mountain perch?
Text.zh: 真不敢相信我的眼睛，你怎么从山上的老窝跑到这儿来了？

Text.en: The Spirit connects our paths once more.
Text.zh: 万灵再次让我们相逢了。

Text.en: Last I saw you... I was sure you'd have died of chill in those mountains.
Text.zh: 上次见你……我还以为你会冻死在山里。

Text.en: Elder, are you well? Why have you left your mountain?
Text.zh: 长老，你还好吗？你怎么抛下山里的事务来到这儿了？

Text.en: Grave news called me here.
Text.zh: 有紧急情况，我不得不过来。

Text.en: I wasn't sure what I was seeing... but I'm glad to see you well.
Text.zh: 我之前还不确定看到的是不是你……但看到你没事我很高兴。

Text.en: I have you to thank for that! Now...
Text.zh: 这还要多谢你！现在……

Text.en: Saving people seems to be what I do now. And you are?
Text.zh: 看来如今救人成了我的本行。你是哪位？

Text.en: It would be dishonourable to have left you in such a state.
Text.zh: 把你扔在那儿不管，那可太不体面了。

Text.en: Dishonourable... and yet, expected. <continue>Now, allow me to introduce myself.
Text.zh: 不体面……却也情理之中。<continue>好了，容我自我介绍。

Text.en: Used to be, I would've left you alone. <continue>But we live in strange times. We should help those who need it.
Text.zh: 换作以前，我可能就不管你了。<continue>不过现在世道不一样了，该帮的人还是得帮。

Text.en: That we should. Now, allow me to introduce myself.
Text.zh: 说得对。好了，容我自我介绍。

Text.en: You have the look of an Azmeri Elder. But so far from home?
Text.zh: 你看着像阿兹莫里的长老。可怎么会跑这么远？

Text.en: Ah, a fellow mountain-born. I greet you.
Text.zh: 啊，山民老乡。幸会。

Text.en: Respectfully, old man, what <i>{were} you doin'?
Text.zh: 恕我冒昧，老先生，您刚才<i>{在}做什么？

Text.en: Sadly... not much of anything, other than trying to escape. Now listen...
Text.zh: 说来惭愧……我什么都没做，只顾着逃命了。你听我说……

Text.en: I am glad to hear you are well. But what are you doing out here?
Text.zh: 我很高兴您安好。可您跑到这外面来做什么？

Text.en: Firstly...
Text.zh: 首先……

Text.en: I know not how I saved ye, but I'm glad it is so.
Text.zh: 我也不知道怎么救的你，但好在是救下了。

Text.en: As am I! Now...
Text.zh: 我也是这么想的！那么……

Text.en: I am Elder Madox of the Azmeri of Mount Kriar.
Text.zh: 我是克里亚山的阿兹莫里长老，马多克斯。

Text.en: The Winter Owl spoke to me. His enemy returns. <continue>The Raven Trickster... He of many names and many faces.
Text.zh: 凛冬之枭向我发出了警示。他的敌人回来了。<continue>黑鸦欺诈者……那位千面千名者。

Text.en: Tekaru. Yes... I may have something to do with that.
Text.zh: 黑鸦。是啊……那可能跟我有点关系。

Text.en: Oh! The god in the fog... this is a touch awkward. Would you be upset if I told you I <i>{may} have freed him?
Text.zh: 哦！雾中之神……真是尴尬。要是我告诉你<i>{可能}是我干的，你会不会生气？

Text.en: Regrettably so. I have seen him before with my own eyes.
Text.zh: 不幸的是，我遇见过他。

Text.en: Yes. I've had the misfortune of encountering him already.
Text.zh: 是的。我已经很不幸地遇到过他了。

Text.en: It pains me to say, Elder, but I've seen it meself.
Text.zh: 长老，说来惭愧，我也见过他。

Text.en: Yeah, well... I, uh... <i>{might've} had somethin' to do with that.
Text.zh: 是啊，那个……我，呃……<i>{可能}跟我多少有点关系。

Text.en: I regret to say that I had a hand in freeing him.
Text.zh: 很遗憾，是我帮着他逃脱的。

Text.en: Aye... the Owl speaks true. I freed the Trickster myself... to my shame.
Text.zh: 是啊……凛冬之枭没说错。是我亲手放走了那骗子……真丢人。

Text.en: I am probably best not to ask how. I suppose... it was only a matter of time.
Text.zh: 至于你是怎么放走他的，我还是不问了。想来……也是迟早的事。

Text.en: I have heard of this being, 'Tekaru'. He is a great threat.
Text.zh: 我听说过这个家伙，“特卡鲁”。他非常危险。

Text.en: Tales are told of this Raven. He has returned?
Text.zh: 关于这只黑鸦的传说可不少。他回来了？

Text.en: If the Raven Trickster is free, we are in grave danger.
Text.zh: 如果黑鸦欺诈者重获自由了，那我们就大难临头了。

Text.en: The Raven Trickster? That's not good.
Text.zh: 黑鸦欺诈者？这可不太妙。

Text.en: Who's that? Some kinda charlatan or somethin'?
Text.zh: 那是谁？是什么江湖骗子之类的吗？

Text.en: I have heard his name in the wind. He seems a force to be reckoned with.
Text.zh: 我曾听过他的传闻。他似乎是个不可小觑的狠角色。

Text.en: Now there's a name I'd hoped never to hear again. If ye speak true, the world is in grave danger.
Text.zh: 这名字，我这辈子都不想再听到了。如果你说的是真的，那这个世界可就危险了。

Text.en: Indeed.
Text.zh: 确实。

Text.en: And what does that have to do with anything?
Text.zh: 可这跟我们有什么关系？

Text.en: Please, heed my words!
Text.zh: 请听我说！

Text.en: With the land now tormented, his power will only grow. <continue>He must be stopped.
Text.zh: 如今这片土地饱受折磨，他的力量只会日益增长。<continue>必须阻止他。

Text.en: What must be done?
Text.zh: 那该怎么做？

Text.en: Fine, I'll help. What do you need?
Text.zh: 好吧，我来帮忙。你需要我做什么？

Text.en: I see. How can I assist you?
Text.zh: 我明白了。我该怎么帮你？

Text.en: Agreed. But how?
Text.zh: 说得对。但要怎么做？

Text.en: I'll do what I can for ya.
Text.zh: 能帮的我一定帮。

Text.en: Alright, I'm in. Where do we start?
Text.zh: 行，算我一个。从哪儿着手？

Text.en: Then stop him we shall.
Text.zh: 那我们就去阻止他。

Text.en: What d'ye need from me?
Text.zh: 需要我做什么？

Text.en: Your aid would be most welcome. <continue>I am retracing the Raven Trickster's tale, 'The Raven and the Hare'. Not all the Elders believe it is a true retelling of his story...
Text.zh: 你的帮助对我来说非常重要。<continue>我正在追溯黑鸦欺诈者的故事，《黑鸦与野兔》。并非所有长老都相信这故事是他的亲身经历……

Text.en: Your aid would be most welcome. <continue>I am retracing the Raven Trickster's tale, 'The Raven and the Hare'. Not all the Elders believe it is a true retelling of his story... <continue>But I believe it, because I have <i>{seen} where it took place.
Text.zh: 你的帮助对我来说非常重要。<continue>我正在追溯黑鸦欺诈者的故事，《黑鸦与野兔》。并非所有长老都相信这故事真的发生过……<continue>但我相信，因为我<i>{亲眼见过}故事发生地。

Text.en: We must go to that original site. There, the Winter Owl will reveal more to us, I know it.
Text.zh: 我们必须去那个最初的地点。在那里，凛冬之枭会给我们更多指引，我很确定。

Text.en: Not to worry, Elder. I can accompany you.
Text.zh: 别担心，长老，我愿随你同去。

Text.en: I'm positively <i>{dying} to hear what he has to say.
Text.zh: 我正<i>{巴不得}听听他要说什么呢。

Text.en: I would gladly entreat his wisdom.
Text.zh: 我很乐意聆听他的智慧。

Text.en: Alright then. I'm in.
Text.zh: 好啊，算我一个。

Text.en: Then I welcome his guidance.
Text.zh: 我愿听从他的指引。

Text.en: A talkin' owl? This I have to see.
Text.zh: 一只能说话的枭？我倒要见识见识。

Text.en: You will not hear his words. But there is still much you can learn from him.
Text.zh: 你听不到他说话。但可以从他身上学到许多。

Text.en: Bollocks... Had me excited there for a minute.  <continue>Ah, well. Guess I'm in anyway.
Text.zh: 啧……让我白激动一场。<continue>唉，算了。反正我也会去。

Text.en: If this owl has wisdom to share, I would gladly hear it.
Text.zh: 如果这头枭还真有智慧，我愿洗耳恭听。

Text.en: So this is what it's come to... takin' advice from a ruddy owl. <continue>But if he's wisdom to offer, then so be it.
Text.zh: 所以到头来……还得听一只该死的枭的指点。<continue>但它若真有智慧，那也只能这样了。

Text.en: Yes... this is it. The Withered Willow. This is where I believe the Raven Trickster was... born.
Text.zh: 是的……就是这里。萎败细柳。我相信，黑鸦欺诈者就是在此处……诞生的。

Text.en: Hm. What makes you think so?
Text.zh: 嗯……何以见得？

Text.en: [NOAUDIO] It's a rather ordinary looking tree.
Text.zh: 这树看起来挺普通的嘛。

Text.en: Here? Are you certain?
Text.zh: 这儿？你确定？

Text.en: Never seen a tree with such an ominous energy.
Text.zh: 我从没见过这么不详的树。

Text.en: Oh, how'd you know?
Text.zh: 你怎么知道？

Text.en: Well, it's definitely a tree.
Text.zh: 嗯，确实是棵树。

Text.en: How can you tell this is the right tree?
Text.zh: 你怎么断定就是这棵树？

Text.en: Aye, there's somethin' terrible about this one.
Text.zh: 是啊，这棵树确实有点邪门。

Text.en: The tale speaks of a tribe driven mad long ago. Such a tribe was near to these parts. <continue>And this tree... it holds a secret, I am sure of it. Let us see.
Text.zh: 传说中，有一个部族很久以前陷入了疯狂。那部族就在这附近。<continue>而这棵树……我敢肯定它藏着一个秘密。我们来看看吧。

Text.en: Behold! The tree bore an illusion! Hmm... but this mirror... misses a piece. <continue>If we restore this veiled glass, we might learn the truth.
Text.zh: 看！这棵树显现过幻象！嗯……可一面镜子……好像缺了一块。<continue>如果我们修复这面被遮蔽的镜子，或许就能知晓真相。

Text.en: Another mirror? How will this help?
Text.zh: 又一面镜子？这能有什么用？

Text.en: What makes <i>{this} mirror different?
Text.zh: <i>{这面}镜子到底有什么不同？

Text.en: Does this mirror connect to another realm?
Text.zh: 这面镜子能通向另一个世界吗？

Text.en: Patience! Soon you will understand.
Text.zh: 耐心些！你很快就会明白。

Text.en: Beyond the glass, ye say? Then stand clear. I'll give it a good wallop!
Text.zh: 你是说在玻璃后面？那你闪开，我给它来一记狠的！

Text.en: That is not what I meant! Focus now.
Text.zh: 我不是这个意思！集中精神。

Text.en: We <i>{might}? I had hoped for a bit more certainty than that.
Text.zh: <i>{或许}？我还以为能听到更确切的答案。

Text.en: Might? Have you no more surety than that?
Text.zh: 或许？你就这么不确定吗？

Text.en: Might? So... you don't know?
Text.zh: 或许？所以……你也不知道？

Text.en: Were you expecting a centuries-old mystery to have a straightforward answer?
Text.zh: 你真以为一个数百年的谜团，能有直接了当的答案？

Text.en: I sense wickedness in this mirror.
Text.zh: 我能感觉到这镜子里有邪气。

Text.en: Now that the Raven is free, he could plunge all the world into madness. Spirit, guide us. <continue>If we've any chance of defeating him, we must uncover the truth beyond the tale of 'The Raven and the Hare' as we know it. <continue>Seek the Raven Trickster within his fog. We must shatter his illusions to see his truths.
Text.zh: 如今黑鸦已获自由，他随时都可能让整个世界陷入疯狂。万灵啊，指引我们吧。<continue>若我们还有机会击败他，就必须揭开我们所知的《黑鸦与野兔》故事背后的真相。<continue>要在他的迷雾中寻找黑鸦欺诈者。我们就必须击碎他的幻象，才能看清他的真相。

Text.en: Seek the Raven Trickster within his fog. We must shatter his illusions to see his truths.
Text.zh: 在他的迷雾中寻找黑鸦欺诈者。我们必须击碎他的幻象，才能看清他的真相。

Text.en: I didn't expect this to be simple, but time is against us. What have you learned?
Text.zh: 我本就没指望这事会简单，但时间不等人。你了解到什么情况了？

Text.en: Oh, you know. His deepest fears, his true name, his favourite food...
Text.zh: 哦，就那些，他内心深处的恐惧，他的真名，他最爱的食物……

Text.en: So you've learned nothing, I gather.
Text.zh: 看来你什么也没了解到。

Text.en: He clearly does not want to be followed.
Text.zh: 他显然不想被人跟踪。

Text.en: His insults are getting more pointed and less funny.
Text.zh: 他的嘲弄越来越刻薄，也越来越不好笑了。

Text.en: It may sound strange, but that bodes well for us.
Text.zh: 这话听着可能奇怪，但这对我们来说是好事。

Text.en: I've followed Tekaru, but I cannot catch him.
Text.zh: 我一直在追特卡鲁，但抓不住他。

Text.en: I'm afraid to say, I didn't find much on my hunt.
Text.zh: 说来惭愧，我一路追查下来也没发现什么。

Text.en: Not much. More elusive than a fox, that one.
Text.zh: 没什么收获。那家伙比狐狸还狡猾。

Text.en: Regrettably little. As much as I pursue him, he remains one step ahead.
Text.zh: 可惜收获不多。不管我怎么追，他都快我一步。

Text.en: He's got a mouth on him, that one. But not the stones to say it to my face.
Text.zh: 那家伙嘴真毒，但他没胆量当面说给我听。

Text.en: Either way, the Winter Owl believes you've begun to ruffle the Raven's feathers.
Text.zh: 不管怎么样，凛冬之枭认为，你已经惹恼乌鸦了。

Text.en: Are you following me, Elder? I'm flattered. Would you like to join my flock?
Text.zh: 长老，你是在跟踪我吗？我真是受宠若惊。你想加入我的鸦群吗？

Text.en: The Raven speaks. It appears we are on the right path. But... what is this damn thing?
Text.zh: 黑鸦在说话。看来我们的方向是对的。可……这鬼东西到底是什么？

Text.en: Hmm. One moment.
Text.zh: 嗯。稍等片刻。

Text.en: Very clever! You are a quick study.
Text.zh: 真聪明！学得倒挺快。

Text.en: My word... what am I seeing? Utter madness... misery... despair!
Text.zh: 天哪……我看到了什么？彻底的疯狂……痛苦……绝望！

Text.en: Don't look at it too long then, old man.
Text.zh: 那就别盯着它看太久，老人家。

Text.en: Get a hold of yourself, would you? Tell me what you see.
Text.zh: 振作点，行吗？告诉我你看到了什么。

Text.en: Focus. What exactly do you see?
Text.zh: 集中精神。你到底看到了什么？

Text.en: But what <i>{is} it?
Text.zh: 但这到底<i>{是}什么？

Text.en: Hold fast Elder! What do you see?
Text.zh: 稳住，长老！你看到了什么？

Text.en: Stay with me, old fella! What's happenin'?
Text.zh: 挺住啊，老爷子！发生了什么？

Text.en: Remain calm. Tell me what you see.
Text.zh: 冷静点。告诉我你看到了什么。

Text.en: It's alright, Elder. I'm here. Just keep talkin'.
Text.zh: 没事的，长老。我在这儿呢，继续说。

Text.en: It seems this is a decanter of sorts. Clarifying all manner of horrors within, into a kind of... essence. <continue>I can see emotions... swirling in the liquid.
Text.zh: 看来这是个精魂之瓶。将各色恐怖之物精炼提纯，凝成某种……精华。<continue>我能看到……那液体里翻涌的情绪。

Text.en: Liquid emotions? Hmm. I found something similar from the fog demons.
Text.zh: 液化情绪？嗯。我在雾魔那儿也找到过类似的东西。

Text.en: Hmm. I plucked something similar off the corpse of a fog demon.
Text.zh: 嗯。我从雾魔的尸体上也捡到过类似的东西。

Text.en: Perhaps that explains what I found earlier. It is a similar liquid to what you describe.
Text.zh: 这或许能解释我之前找到的东西。它跟你描述的液体很像。

Text.en: Now that you mention it, I did find something similar earlier.
Text.zh: 你这么一说，我先前也找到了类似的东西。

Text.en: I found this strange liquid earlier. Reckon it's the same thing?
Text.zh: 我之前也找到过这种奇怪的液体。估计就是你说的那东西？

Text.en: Strangely enough, I know exactly what you're talkin' about. I found one earlier.
Text.zh: 说来也怪，我完全明白你在说什么，我之前也捡到过一个。

Text.en: Hmm. During my quarrel with the fog demons, I found something similar.
Text.zh: 嗯。我跟那些雾魔交手时，也找到了类似的东西。

Text.en: Think I found just the same after my fight with the fog demons.
Text.zh: 我跟雾魔打完架后，好像也捡到了差不多的东西。

Text.en: What? You <i>{definitely} should stop looking at it...
Text.zh: 什么？你<i>{千万}别再盯着它看了……

Text.en: If you think you're <i>{seeing} emotions, old man, you may be a lost cause.
Text.zh: 你要是觉得自己能<i>{看见}情绪，老头，那可能是没救了。

Text.en: I fear this decanter may have... damaged your mind.
Text.zh: 恐怕这个精魂之瓶……伤了你的脑子。

Text.en: You can <i>{see} emotions?
Text.zh: 你能<i>{看见}情绪？

Text.en: I... don't understand...
Text.zh: 我……不明白……

Text.en: Sounds like my last visit to an opium den.
Text.zh: 听起来就跟我上次去鸦片馆一样。

Text.en: I am not sure I understand.
Text.zh: 我不太懂你的意思。

Text.en: Ye're not making any sense.
Text.zh: 你说话没头没脑的。

Text.en: How... curious. I've a thought. Take this, then approach the decanter.
Text.zh: 真是……奇了。我有个想法。拿着这个，然后靠近那个精魂之瓶。

Text.en: The fog demons you faced earlier dropped something similar... I've a thought. Take this, then approach the decanter.
Text.zh: 你先前对付的那些雾魔掉落过类似的东西……我有个想法。拿着这个，然后靠近那个精魂之瓶。

Text.en: Place the amulet. Go on.
Text.zh: 把护身符放上去。对。

Text.en: Now... pour the distilled emotions into the decanter. Observe what manifests.
Text.zh: 现在……把凝练的情绪倒进精魂之瓶里。看看会出现什么。

Text.en: Such beautiful madness... The first to be distilled was unwilling, but the rest sought out their fate.
Text.zh: 真是美丽的疯狂……第一个被凝炼者并非自愿，但余下的都自愿接受了命运。

Text.en: Hold your tongue, Raven!
Text.zh: 住口，黑鸦！

Text.en: Hmm. Most ponderous, indeed. The liquids... they are gone. Imbued upon the very amulet itself.
Text.zh: 嗯。确实耐人寻味。那些液体……不见了，都已融入护身符。

Text.en: Adorn it. Let us see what happens.
Text.zh: 戴上它，看看会发生什么。

Text.en: The amulet reveals a story. A tale lost to time and delirium. This tale's ending... it may not be to your liking.
Text.zh: 这护身符揭开了一个故事。一个被时光与癫狂所掩埋的故事。这故事的结局……未必合你的心意。

Text.en: I suspect the selection and arrangement of emotions is important to the story.
Text.zh: 我猜想，情绪的选择与排列，对故事的走向至关重要。

Text.en: Keep your eye sharp for these 'liquid emotions' from the fog demons. Perhaps, they will aid us on the journey.
Text.zh: 留意这些雾魔身上的“液化情绪”。或许，它们能助我们一臂之力。

Text.en: Now... I must tell you. The Winter Owl has shown me... The Raven's curse spreads.
Text.zh: 现在……我必须告诉你们。凛冬之枭向我示警……黑鸦的诅咒正在蔓延。

Text.en: Here... his curse grows heavier.
Text.zh: 这里……他的诅咒愈发沉重了。

Text.en: Go. Observe. But you must be strong... and do not listen to the Raven Trickster's words.
Text.zh: 去吧。去观察。但你们必须坚强……切勿听信黑鸦欺诈者的言语。

Text.en: His power grows, but so does yours.
Text.zh: 他的力量在增长，但你的力量也在增长。

Text.en: He's frightful, aye, but huntin' <i>{rathin} is in me blood.
Text.zh: 他确实吓人，可猎杀<i>{腐化}，是我的天性。

Text.en: He's trying to get into my head. But I remain grounded.
Text.zh: 他想钻进我脑子里，可我清醒得很。

Text.en: Finally some progress. I had begun to feel hopeless.
Text.zh: 总算有点进展了。我之前都快觉得没指望了。

Text.en: He tests my resilience with each and every taunt.
Text.zh: 他每一次冷嘲热讽，都像是在挑战我的耐性。

Text.en: Yes, he seems to have forgotten his mirror. And his manners.
Text.zh: 是啊，他好像把自己的镜子忘了。礼数也忘了。

Text.en: If wars could be won with words, he would still lose to me. When I lose my temper, I can speak quite ill.
Text.zh: 要是光靠嘴皮子就能打赢仗，那他照样得输给我。

Text.en: I've felled my fair share of silver-tongued villains.
Text.zh: 能说会道的恶徒，我收拾过不少。

Text.en: He ran off right quick. Happy to run his mouth, mayhaps, but he wouldnae face me.
Text.zh: 他跑得倒是快。嘴皮子耍得厉害，但他不敢真正面对我。

Text.en: The Raven's words indeed bite, like a dog defending its bone. <continue>Wait... what is that within the mirror? Do you see it?
Text.zh: 黑鸦的话确实刺人，就像护食的狗一样。<continue>等等……镜子里那是什么？你们看见了吗？

Text.en: I see... huh? What is that?
Text.zh: 我看见了……嗯？那是什么？

Text.en: That strange little thing?
Text.zh: 那个奇怪的小东西？

Text.en: I am not sure what it is I see. A small, dark object of some sort.
Text.zh: 我不确定看见了什么。像是某种又小又黑的东西。

Text.en: I see something... but what is it?
Text.zh: 我看见了……但那是什么？

Text.en: Aye, Elder. I see it.
Text.zh: 是的，长老。我看见了。

Text.en: What, that shrivelled little thing?
Text.zh: 什么，那个干瘪的小东西？

Text.en: Yes. I see it.
Text.zh: 是的，我看见了。

Text.en: These old eyes see somethin' or other...
Text.zh: 我这双老眼也瞅见个什么东西……

Text.en: Forgive me such an odd instruction, but I think you must reach in and take it. <continue>I would, but I'm... old and tired.
Text.zh: 恕我冒昧，这个要求可能有些古怪，但我认为你必须伸手进去，把它拿出来。<continue>我本想自己去，可我……老了，没力气了。

Text.en: It's soft... like a fruit.
Text.zh: 软软的……像是果子。

Text.en: It's a fruit. Although not one I'd like to eat.
Text.zh: 是果子。不过这种我可不想吃。

Text.en: It's a fruit. Cursed and blackened.
Text.zh: 是果子。又邪门又黑。

Text.en: It's a fruit of some kind. Old. Rotten.
Text.zh: 算是一种果子吧。又老又烂的。

Text.en: It's a fruit. Not any fruit I've seen before, mind.
Text.zh: 是果子。不过，我可没见过这种果子。

Text.en: Were you hungry? I think it's a fruit.
Text.zh: 你饿了？我觉得是果子。

Text.en: It appears to be a rotten fruit.
Text.zh: 看起来是个烂果子。

Text.en: Looks like a fruit... or the withered remains of one.
Text.zh: 看着像果子……或者说是干瘪的果子。

Text.en: Can it be? I have read of this dark fruit. <continue>The Raven's tribe once believed it gifted them 'dark premonitions'. <continue>The tale of 'The Raven and the Hare' may be disputed, but his tribe was very real. <continue>I wonder...   <continue>Yes. The Winter Owl agrees. Eat the fruit.
Text.zh: 难道真是它？我在书里读到过这种黑暗果实。<continue>黑鸦的部族曾相信，它能赐予他们“黑暗的预兆”。<continue>“黑鸦与野兔”的故事或许有争议，但他的部族是真实存在过的。<continue>我在想……<continue>对。凛冬之枭也赞同。吃下这果子吧。

Text.en: Respectfully, Elder, it's gone bad...
Text.zh: 长老，恕我直言，它已经烂了……

Text.en: You must be joking?
Text.zh: 你在开玩笑吧？

Text.en: Are you serious? I would rather drink sand.
Text.zh: 你是认真的吗？我宁愿喝沙子。

Text.en: Is there no other way?
Text.zh: 没别的法子了吗？

Text.en: This rotten thing? Ugh. For a million gold pieces, I'd consider it.
Text.zh: 这烂东西？哪怕给我一百万金币，我都得考虑一下。

Text.en: I feared ye'd gone mad. Now I <i>{know} ye have.
Text.zh: 我之前还怕你疯了。现在我<i>{敢肯定}你确实疯了。

Text.en: Is this truly the moment you give up? Eat it.
Text.zh: 你真要在这种时候退缩吗？吃下去。

Text.en: I've eaten worse, I suppose.
Text.zh: 好吧，又不是没吃过更难吃的。

Text.en: I am ready, Elder.
Text.zh: 我准备好了，长老。

Text.en: Just be prepared for what you might see.
Text.zh: 做好心理准备，可能会看见些东西。

Text.en: Well, if there's truly no other way...
Text.zh: 好吧，要是真没别的法子……

Text.en: If I start choking, I'll have your head.
Text.zh: 我要是噎着了，那可都怪你。

Text.en: Ugh... if you insist. I place my trust in you.
Text.zh: 呃……如果你坚持的话。我信你就是。

Text.en: Ugh. Fine.
Text.zh: 呃，行吧。

Text.en: [NOAUDIO] Wish me luck!
Text.zh: 祝我好运吧！

Text.en: Oh, alright then. Down the hatch!
Text.zh: 哦，好吧。一口闷！

Text.en: Let us see if your Owl speaks true.
Text.zh: 让我们看看你的那只枭说得准不准。

Text.en: Ugh... well let's get on with it.
Text.zh: 唉……行了，那就开始吧。

Text.en: Now, tell me... how do you feel?
Text.zh: 快跟我说说……你感觉怎么样？

Text.en: I feel... out of sorts.
Text.zh: 我感觉……不太对劲。

Text.en: This. Is. Incredible.
Text.zh: 这。感。觉。太。棒。了。

Text.en: You... enjoy this sensation? That is not what I expected.
Text.zh: 你……很享受这种感受？这倒出乎我的意料。

Text.en: I feel nothing. Should I feel something? Wait... who are you?
Text.zh: 我什么感觉都没有。我应该有感觉吗？等等……你是谁？

Text.en: It is I, Madox! We've been talking the whole time.
Text.zh: 是我，马多克斯！我们一直在说话啊。

Text.en: I... can't move... my legs.
Text.zh: 我的腿……动不了。

Text.en: I'm seein' double. Hold still, old man!
Text.zh: 我看东西重影了。别动，老爷子！

Text.en: Whoa... and I thought the opium dens in Trarthus were rough...
Text.zh: 哇……我还以为特拉特斯的鸦片馆就已经够狠了……

Text.en: I've eaten my fair share of odd mushrooms in my day, but ach... this is <i>{somethin'} <i>{else.}
Text.zh: 我年轻时也吃过不少稀奇古怪的蘑菇，可这……这真是<i>{另一码}<i>{事}。

Text.en: I see the Dreamer!
Text.zh: 我看见幻梦者了！

Text.en: Yes, yes. Calm down.
Text.zh: 好了，好了。别激动。

Text.en: This feeling ought to pass... eventually. <continue>But now, the Winter Owl believes you are ready to chase down the Raven. <continue>Let his madness guide you.
Text.zh: 这感觉……应该迟早会过去的。<continue>但现在，凛冬之枭相信，你现在可以去追捕黑鸦了。<continue>让他的疯狂指引你。

Text.en: I see something. A path of sorts.
Text.zh: 我看到什么了，像是一条路。

Text.en: Well, well... I see a path! I think.
Text.zh: 嚯，嚯……我看到一条路。

Text.en: There it is! I see a path unfurl before us.
Text.zh: 就在那儿！一条路在我们眼前铺开了。

Text.en: It worked. I see a path.
Text.zh: 成了。我看见路了。

Text.en: Well, whaddaya know? <continue>Let's see the Raven try to hide me from now.
Text.zh: 嘿，你看这事儿！<continue>这下看那黑鸦还怎么躲我。

Text.en: Well, looky here. We've a breadcrumb trail to follow.
Text.zh: 嘿，瞧啊。顺着这条路走就是了。

Text.en: I see it. A new way forward.
Text.zh: 我看见了。一条新路。

Text.en: Aye, that's done it. We've a trail to follow.
Text.zh: 嗯，这下成了。有条路可以走。

Text.en: Good, but be careful when you find him. A beast is most dangerous when it's cornered.
Text.zh: 好，但找到他的时候要小心。困兽之斗，最为凶险。

Text.en: You return. What did the fruit show you?
Text.zh: 你回来了。那果子让你看到了什么？

Text.en: I saw everyone I know, dead. He said it was my fault.
Text.zh: 我看见我认识的所有人都死了。他说那都是我的错。

Text.en: He implied I was irredeemable. As if I haven't heard that one before. <continue>Oh, and there were dead people. Quite a lot of them.
Text.zh: 他暗示我无可救药。好像这种话我还没听够似的。<continue>哦，对了，还有死人。一大堆死人。

Text.en: That demon... he is too wicked for words. I refuse to believe what I saw. It was some sort of twisted reality.
Text.zh: 那恶魔……恶毒得没法形容。我绝不相信我看到的是真的。那就是被歪曲的现实。

Text.en: The Raven... he was inside my head. He showed me terrible things.
Text.zh: 黑鸦……他钻进我的脑子里，给我看了些可怕的东西。

Text.en: That Raven is full o' nothin' but bluster an' blame.
Text.zh: 那黑鸦，除了虚张声势和推卸责任，什么都不会。

Text.en: A whole load o' things I'd rather not repeat.
Text.zh: 一堆破事，我都不想说第二遍。

Text.en: A series of visions, each one more terrifying than the last.
Text.zh: 一连串幻象，一个比一个吓人。

Text.en: One o' the worst voyages I ever had, that. Saw death 'round every bend.
Text.zh: 那是我经历过的最糟心的旅程之一，到处都是死亡。

Text.en: I see. So, it was no premonition after all. <continue>Though you may see truth in the Raven's illusions, they are not real.
Text.zh: 明白了。所以它终究不是预言。<continue>就算你在黑鸦的幻象中看到了真相，它们本身并不是真的。

Text.en: Wait... there was another mirror, but in it, I saw a boy.
Text.zh: 等等……那里还有一面镜子，我在里面看到一个男孩。

Text.en: What about the boy? Was he real?
Text.zh: 那男孩呢？他是真的吗？

Text.en: I did look in his mirror. I think I saw the boy from your tale.
Text.zh: 我确实瞧了瞧他的镜子。我想我看见了，就是你故事里的那个男孩。

Text.en: The Raven Trickster were gazin' at a boy in the mirror.
Text.zh: 那个黑鸦欺诈者，他正盯着镜子里的一个男孩看。

Text.en: It's a pretty good parlour trick. You don't think any of it was real? <continue>What about the boy? Or the, uh, pile of gold?
Text.zh: 挺不错的戏法。你该不会觉得那些都是真的吧？<continue>那男孩呢？那堆金币呢？

Text.en: It was not all illusion. I saw the boy you speak of.
Text.zh: 不全是幻象。我看见了你说的那个男孩。

Text.en: Yes, I know. He got one thing wrong, though...  <continue>At the end of his torments, he showed me a boy. But I do not have a son.
Text.zh: 对，我知道。不过有一件事他搞错了……<continue>在折磨快结束的时候，他给我看了个男孩。不过我根本没有儿子。

Text.en: A son? Wait...
Text.zh: 儿子？等等……

Text.en: Some of it were true. My Aoife... I know she is already dead.
Text.zh: 有些是真的。我的艾欧菲……我知道她已经死了。

Text.en: I'm sorry to hear that.
Text.zh: 听到这个我很难过。

Text.en: The wee lad in the mirror may well've been dead too.
Text.zh: 镜子里那个小家伙，可能也早就死了。

Text.en: You saw the boy? You saw past your own reflection and into the Raven's! <continue>For an innocent child to become such a monster... there must be more to this tale.
Text.zh: 你看见了那个男孩？你透过自己的倒影，看穿了黑鸦的内心！<continue>一个无辜的孩子竟能变成这般怪物……这故事背后，必有隐情……

Text.en: I tried talking to the boy. The mirror shattered and I grabbed this.
Text.zh: 我试着跟那男孩说话，然后镜子就碎了，我抓到了这个。

Text.en: He can't be all bad. He left me a present!
Text.zh: 他未必就坏透了。他还给我留了件东西！

Text.en: We will learn the truth... and perhaps this will help.
Text.zh: 我们会查明真相的……也许这个能帮上忙。

Text.en: Few people in this world are truly innocent. <continue>And there was something else. Before I came to, I picked this up.
Text.zh: 这世上真正无辜的人屈指可数。<continue>还有一件事。我清醒之前，捡到了这个。

Text.en: Aye, must be. And I think I found a clue.
Text.zh: 对，肯定有。而且我好像还发现了一条线索。

Text.en: If the boy was real, then this might be real too.
Text.zh: 如果那男孩是真的，那这东西也可能是真的。

Text.en: As the vision faded, I took this. It called to me.
Text.zh: 幻象消散时，我拿起了这个。它在呼唤我。

Text.en: And what do ye make o' this?
Text.zh: 你觉得这玩意儿是啥？

Text.en: Let me see that! Is that the missing piece from the mirror? <continue>Hmm... approach the tree. Complete his mirror. Find the boy, and learn the truth.
Text.zh: 让我看看！这是不是那面镜子上缺的那一块？<continue>嗯……到树那里去。把他的镜子拼好。找到那个男孩，弄清楚真相。

Text.en: What happened to you, boy?
Text.zh: 孩子，你遭遇了什么？

Text.en: I was chasin' a hare... and... and a monster found me and it... it...
Text.zh: 我当时在追一只野兔……然后……然后有个怪物发现了我，然后它……它……

Text.en: A monster? Tell me what you remember!
Text.zh: 怪物？告诉我你还记得什么！

Text.en: It showed me... how to prolong their suffering.
Text.zh: 它教我……怎么延长他们的痛苦。

Text.en: What? You're not well, boy. But the Spirit can still save you.
Text.zh: 什么？你脑子不清醒了，孩子。但万灵还能拯救你。

Text.en: Your Spirit led me to the monster, and left me to die.
Text.zh: 是万灵把我引到怪物面前，然后把我扔在那儿等死。

Text.en: No, that can't be right. All children are sacred to the Spirit!
Text.zh: 不，不可能。万灵视所有孩子为神圣！

Text.en: Are you quite certain of that?
Text.zh: 你确定吗？

Text.en: You are a greater fool than I imagined. Until next time...
Text.zh: 你比我想象的还要愚蠢。后会有期吧……

Text.en: A chilling tale. But how can you be sure this boy became... so much more?
Text.zh: 很阴暗的故事。但你怎么能肯定，这个男孩就变成了……那般存在？

Text.en: And you believe this boy became the Raven Trickster? How?
Text.zh: 所以你认为这个男孩变成了黑鸦欺诈者？怎么会？

Text.en: Maybe I missed it, but how did the boy become Tangmazu?
Text.zh: 可能是我没听明白，那个男孩是怎么变成唐玛祖的？

Text.en: I can't be sure. But I trust in the Winter Owl's wisdom. And he tells me this boy became the Raven.
Text.zh: 我也说不准。但我相信凛冬之枭的智慧。他告诉我，这个男孩变成了黑鸦。

Text.en: It's a nice story, but how does it connect to old 'Fog God'?
Text.zh: 故事倒是不错，可这跟老“迷雾之神”有什么关系？

Text.en: I don't understand. The child was cursed! The tribe did what was necessary.
Text.zh: 我不明白。那个孩子被诅咒了！部族也只能这么做了。

Text.en: And I will never understand how the Maraketh could... hmph. That is not the point.
Text.zh: 我永远也无法理解马拉克斯人怎么能……哼。这不是重点。

Text.en: The tale is not sound. The boy became the Raven. That means he lived.
Text.zh: 这故事说不通。男孩成了黑鸦。那说明他活下来了。

Text.en: Spirit's revenge on those who kill a child. But he must've survived, if he became the Raven?
Text.zh: 万灵会报复杀害孩子的人。可他若成了黑鸦，那肯定是活下来了。

Text.en: You are wise. Why would the Spirit punish the Raven's tribe if the boy survived?
Text.zh: 你说得有道理。如果那个男孩活了下来，万灵为何还要惩罚黑鸦的部族？

Text.en: I never knew the Spirit to be so vengeful. There's many an ill omen out in the woods...
Text.zh: 我从不觉得万灵会如此睚眦必报。森林里不详之物多了去了……

Text.en: Yes... I feel there were other forces at play. But the tale has no mention of them.
Text.zh: 是啊……我觉得还有其他力量在作祟。但故事里没有提到。

Text.en: I'm not one to question the Spirit's will, but his tribe's suffering went beyond simple justice. <continue>Who was the boy? What was his tribe's curse? These are the answers we seek.
Text.zh: 我从不质疑万灵的意志，可他的部族所受的苦难，已超出了单纯的惩戒。<continue>那男孩是谁？他的部族又背负着怎样的诅咒？这些才是我们要找的答案。

Text.en: The Karui call him Tekaru. The Ezomytes call him the Black Crow. Are you sure he is Azmeri?
Text.zh: 卡鲁人称他为特卡鲁。艾兹麦人称他为黑鸦。你确定他是阿兹莫里人吗？

Text.en: The Winter Owl believes it is so.
Text.zh: 凛冬之枭是这么认为的。

Text.en: How could he be in so many places at once?
Text.zh: 他怎么可能同时出现在那么多地方？

Text.en: Origins can be lost to time, but... his presence in so many tales <i>{is} strange.
Text.zh: 他的来历或许会湮没在时间长河中，但……他出现在如此多的故事里，<i>{确实}有点奇怪。

Text.en: Tangmazu, Tekaru, the Raven Trickster... personally, I prefer 'Fog God'. Either way, he's quite the celebrity!
Text.zh: 唐玛祖、特卡鲁、黑鸦欺诈者……我个人更喜欢“迷雾之神”这名字。不管怎样，他可真有名啊！

Text.en: This Raven Trickster does get around.
Text.zh: 这个黑鸦欺诈者，还真是无处不在。

Text.en: He is not the only god known across cultures. Our Last Children are rightfully honoured! But that is different.
Text.zh: 他不是唯一一个被不同文化所知的神祇。我们的终末之子理应受到尊崇！但性质不一样。

Text.en: The Karui call him Tekaru. Some call him Tangmazu. Why is that?
Text.zh: 卡鲁人称他为特卡鲁。而有些人叫他唐玛祖。这是为什么？

Text.en: He has darkened many a sky on Wraeclast.
Text.zh: 他曾让瓦尔克拉斯的天空无数次陷入黑暗。

Text.en: Poetic. Is that why you call him the Raven?
Text.zh: 真是诗意。所以你们才叫他黑鸦？

Text.en: The name came first, I believe. Even when he was a boy.
Text.zh: 我想，这个名字一开始就有，甚至在他还是个孩子的时候。

Text.en: Does every culture have their own Trickster?
Text.zh: 每个文化都有自己的欺诈者吗？

Text.en: It is always the same being. He of many names and many faces.
Text.zh: 其实一直都是同一个存在，那位千面千名者。

Text.en: How is that possible? How could he be in so many places?
Text.zh: 这怎么可能？他怎么能同时存在于那么多地方？

Text.en: I'm not sure how, but he travelled great distances to spread his madness.
Text.zh: 我也不清楚，但他确实跨越了漫长的距离，散播他的疯狂。

Text.en: The Raven Trickster seems to be everywhere now. Uncanny, that one.
Text.zh: 这黑鸦欺诈者现在似乎无处不在。真是邪门。

Text.en: Yes, child. That is why so many people tell tales of his misdeeds.
Text.zh: 是啊，孩子。正因为如此，才有那么多关于他恶行的传说。

Text.en: Always assumed some o' the tales must be false. Now, I don't know.
Text.zh: 我以前总觉得，那些传说里肯定有些是假的。现在，我拿不准了。

Text.en: I had never heard of your Raven. Yet he is known by the Karui and the Azmeri. <continue>How many cultures revere him as a god?
Text.zh: 我从未听说过你们的黑鸦。可卡鲁人和阿兹莫里人都知道他。<continue>到底有多少文化将他奉为神明？

Text.en: He is known by many, Monk. But I would not say he is 'revered'.
Text.zh: 很多族群都知道他，行者。但说“奉为神明”，怕是谈不上。

Text.en: The Raven Trickster... now that he's free, cannae imagine what he might do here.
Text.zh: 黑鸦欺诈者……如今他重获自由，真不敢想他会在这儿干出什么事。

Text.en: In ancient times, the Raven's wrath was not limited by distance.
Text.zh: 远古时代，黑鸦的怒火便不受距离所限。

Text.en: I suppose we should be thankful there's no more gods he can bed.
Text.zh: 我看，咱们该庆幸已经没有多少神明能让他祸害了。

Text.en: Yes...uh, well... there's other ways he can harm us.
Text.zh: 是啊……呃……不过他害人的法子可多着呢。

Text.en: The Raven Trickster's notoriety only proves how large a threat he is.
Text.zh: 黑鸦欺诈者臭名远扬，恰恰证明了他有多危险。

Text.en: Perhaps we could imprison him again?
Text.zh: 或许我们可以再次将他囚禁？

Text.en: Could we not catch him again? I've caught bigger game.
Text.zh: 我们就不能再把他抓住吗？比这更大的猎物我都抓过。

Text.en: I fear he won't fall for such a trap again.
Text.zh: 恐怕他不会再落入同样的陷阱了。

Text.en: How is he different from your other gods, then?
Text.zh: 那他和你们其他的神又有什么不同？

Text.en: Some gods show mercy. He is not capable.
Text.zh: 有些神会施以仁慈。而他不会。

Text.en: Yes, well... there's always a threat somewhere, I suppose.
Text.zh: 是啊……这世上总有各种威胁。

Text.en: A threat, maybe... but not invulnerable. We will triumph, in the end.
Text.zh: 也许确实是威胁……但并非不可战胜，我们终将获胜。

Text.en: We already know imprisoning him doesn't work. <continue>That calls for a more... <i>{permanent} solution.
Text.zh: 我们已经知道囚禁他不管用了。<continue>这次需要更……更<i>{一劳永逸的}办法。

Text.en: Indeed.
Text.zh: 确实。

Text.en: We have the Spirit on our side. We won't fail.
Text.zh: 我们有万灵相助。我们不会失败。

Text.en: Sounds like it's time we put 'im in the dirt.
Text.zh: 听起来，是时候把他狠狠干掉了。

Text.en: We must succeed. The Raven is not content to torture the few or the weak. <continue>He turned gods on each other. <continue>Stopping him would be a great boon to this world.
Text.zh: 我们必须成功。黑鸦可不会只折磨少数人或弱者。<continue>他甚至能挑拨众神互相争斗。<continue>若能阻止他，将是这世间莫大的福祉。

Text.en: The Winter Owl is neither animal, nor god. He is one of the Spirit's guardians.
Text.zh: 凛冬之枭既非野兽，亦非神明。他是万灵的守护者之一。

Text.en: Aye, I've seen these guardians hunt <i>{rathin} marked by the Spirit's wisps.
Text.zh: 是啊，我见过这些守护者猎杀被万灵的鬼灵标记的<i>{腐化}。

Text.en: Yes. The wisps and the guardians work together to heal this land.
Text.zh: 是的。鬼灵与守护者携手合作，修复这片土地。

Text.en: I have seen wisps that closely resemble the Owl. Are they the same?
Text.zh: 我见过一些长得很像枭的鬼灵。它们是同一种东西吗？

Text.en: One of... then who are the others? Those wisps I've seen around?
Text.zh: 之一……那还有其他的守护者？我见过的那些鬼灵？

Text.en: How does he differ from the wisps I've seen? They certainly share a likeness.
Text.zh: 他跟我见过的鬼灵有什么区别？它们确实长得很像。

Text.en: I've seen something like your Winter Owl before. Are these guardians also known as wisps?
Text.zh: 我以前见过类似你那凛冬之枭的东西。这些守护者也叫鬼灵吗？

Text.en: So he's like one o' those wisps I've seen about? Sure looks like one.
Text.zh: 所以，他就像是我见过的那些鬼灵？看着确实差不多。

Text.en: What of the wisps? Are they and these 'guardians' one and the same?
Text.zh: 那些鬼灵呢？它们和这些“守护者”是一种东西吗？

Text.en: Who are these guardians? Do they all take the form of a wisp?
Text.zh: 这些守护者到底是什么？它们都会以鬼灵的形态出现吗？

Text.en: The wisps mark creatures for the guardians to hunt. They work in harmony, but are not the same.
Text.zh: 鬼灵为守护者标记猎物。它们协同工作，但并非同一种东西。

Text.en: The guardians embody aspects of life. The Winter Owl, for example, represents wisdom. <continue>Our actions and beliefs inform which guardian we follow. <continue>We Azmeri believe in balance. While I observe, there are others better suited to act. <continue>Perhaps a warrior, guided by the Frenzied Boar. Or a leader, guided by the Noble Wolf.
Text.zh: 守护者代表的是生命的不同侧面。比如，凛冬之枭代表智慧。<continue>我们选择追随哪位守护者，取决于我们的行为和信念。<continue>我们阿兹莫里人信奉平衡。我负责观察，自有更适合行动的人。<continue>比如说战士，就更有可能受凶狂野猪指引。而领袖，则会受显贵头狼的指引。

Text.en: I know of the guardians, but I've never heard any of them speak.
Text.zh: 我听说过守护者，但从没听他们说过话。

Text.en: You will not hear their words until you have travelled far with them. Which guardian do you follow?
Text.zh: 你与他们同行久了，自会听到他们的话语。你追随哪一位守护者？

Text.en: I've not chosen. How did you know the Winter Owl was your guardian?
Text.zh: 我尚未选择。你怎么知道凛冬之枭就是你的守护者？

Text.en: But to be clear, he's not a talkin' owl?
Text.zh: 不过我先确认一下，他不是会说话的枭吧？

Text.en: Just because you cannot hear him, does not mean he cannot speak.
Text.zh: 你听不到他说话，不代表他不会说。

Text.en: Can't you just... ask him to talk to me, then?
Text.zh: 那你就不能……让他跟我说两句吗？

Text.en: That's not how it works! You are not ready for his words.
Text.zh: 不是这么回事！你还没到能听懂他话的时候。

Text.en: Then you converse with these guardians?
Text.zh: 所以，你能与这些守护者交谈？

Text.en: I can. But that is not the only shape their guidance takes.
Text.zh: 我能。但他们的指引并不只有说话这一种方式。

Text.en: But <i>{how} do they guide you? They don't even talk.
Text.zh: 那它们到底<i>{如何}指引你？它们又不说话。

Text.en: Oh, they are not mutes. Merely selective about when they choose to speak.
Text.zh: 哦，他们并不是哑巴，只是会挑选开口的时机而已。

Text.en: You're telling me this Owl is holding out on us? Let's make it squawk.
Text.zh: 你是说这只枭只是故意不跟我们说话？让它叫两声。

Text.en: The Winter Owl represents wisdom... yet does not speak?
Text.zh: 凛冬之枭代表智慧……却不肯开口？

Text.en: He <i>{can} speak... when he chooses to. <continue>Sometimes it is wisest to say nothing at all.
Text.zh: 他<i>{能}说话……只是挑时候。<continue>有时，什么都不说，才是最明智的。

Text.en: Sometimes... but not always. Silence seems a poor form of guidance.
Text.zh: 有时候吧……但也不总是。一直沉默听起来可不像什么好指引。

Text.en: It's an aspect of life, yet you claim to hear its words. <continue>How does this Winter Owl speak to you?
Text.zh: 它只是生命的一个侧面，你却说自己能听到它说话。<continue>那凛冬之枭到底是如何对你说话的？

Text.en: You speak of them like shepherds... yet they do not speak. How do they impart their wisdom?
Text.zh: 你把它们说得像牧羊人……可它们又不说话。那它们怎么传授智慧？

Text.en: I've been around enough boars to know I wouldnae turn to one for guidance. <continue>But I'm guessin' there's more to these guardians than meets the eye.
Text.zh: 我见过的野猪可不少，反正我是不会找它们求指引的。<continue>但我猜，这些守护者，绝非表面看起来那么简单。

Text.en: How to explain... The Winter Owl has guided me throughout my life, starting more subtly. <continue>Feelings, at first. Then glimpses of his form. Whispers on the wind. Now, a constant companion. <continue>The less time I have in this world, the clearer his words become.
Text.zh: 这个嘛……怎么说呢。我这一生都有凛冬之枭的指引，只不过一开始很隐晦。<continue>最初是一些感觉，然后是瞥见他的影子，还有风中的低语。如今，已是常伴身旁的挚友。<continue>我在这世上的时日越少，他的话语便愈发清晰。

Text.en: What made you leave Mount Kriar? I got the impression you'd planned to stay.
Text.zh: 你为什么会离开克里亚山？我还以为你打算在那里终老。

Text.en: You left your mountain perch. Why was that, exactly?
Text.zh: 你离开了山上的老窝。这究竟是怎么回事？

Text.en: You've travelled far from your mountain. I thought you intended to see out your days there.
Text.zh: 你这是跋山涉水远离群山啊。我还以为你打算在那里度过余生。

Text.en: You told me you would observe from the mountain peak for the rest of your days. <continue>What made you leave?
Text.zh: 你曾告诉我，你将在山巅观察直到生命终结。<continue>那你为什么要离开？

Text.en: Did you observe all you needed to on Mount Kriar then?
Text.zh: 你在克里亚山上已经把一切都观察透了吗？

Text.en: No. I was needed here.
Text.zh: 不，是这里需要我。

Text.en: I was needed here.
Text.zh: 是这里需要我。

Text.en: Why'd you leave Mount Kriar? Kinda figured that'd be your home until you croaked.
Text.zh: 你为什么离开克里亚山？我还以为你会在那里一直待咽气呢。

Text.en: When first we met, you said you would remain on Mount Kriar until your death.Yet here you are.
Text.zh: 我们初次见面时，你说你会留在克里亚山直到死去。可现在你却在这里。

Text.en: Once was, you planned to die on that mountain o' yours. Seems you found another callin'.
Text.zh: 之前，你本打算死在你那座山上。看来你找到了新的使命。

Text.en: During my observation, the Winter Owl told me of the Raven Trickster's return. <continue>I have spent much of my life studying the history of the Azmeri. I know how large a threat he poses.
Text.zh: 在我观察的过程中，凛冬之枭告诉我黑鸦欺诈者归来了。<continue>我一生大部分时间都在研究阿兹莫里人的历史。我知道他的威胁有多大。

Text.en: Noble of you, to answer such a calling. Especially after the loss of your tribe.
Text.zh: 能响应这样的召唤，你真是高尚。尤其是在失去族人之后。

Text.en: Ahh, this is your redemption for not saving your tribe! Sad...
Text.zh: 啊，你这是想弥补当年没救下族人的过失啊。真可惜……

Text.en: There is no balm for the sorrow I feel.
Text.zh: 我心中的悲痛，无药可医。

Text.en: Then it was the Winter Owl who bid you fight back?
Text.zh: 是凛冬之枭让你奋起反击的？

Text.en: So, the Winter Owl asked you to abandon your observation?
Text.zh: 所以，是凛冬之枭让你放弃了观察？

Text.en: So you're here doin' the Owl's biddin'?
Text.zh: 所以你是在执行那只枭的命令？

Text.en: It was my choice to come here, as it was my choice to observe there.
Text.zh: 来这里是我的选择，留在山上观察也是我的选择。

Text.en: The Winter Owl was wise to guide you here.
Text.zh: 凛冬之枭指引你来到这里，真是明智。

Text.en: Not just to the Azmeri, but to us all.
Text.zh: 不仅对阿兹莫里人，对我们所有人都是。

Text.en: Indeed.
Text.zh: 确实。

Text.en: And you intend to answer that threat.
Text.zh: 而你打算去应对这威胁。

Text.en: I do... while I still can.
Text.zh: 我确实打算……趁我还有能力。

Text.en: Gazing toward the past... I was too distracted to prevent the carnage on Mount Kriar. <continue>Now, the wisdom I've gathered might prevent even greater evils.
Text.zh: 追忆往昔……我当时心思在别处，未能阻止克里亚山上的屠杀。<continue>如今，我积累的智慧或许能阻止更大的灾祸。

Text.en: I never questioned how the Raven Trickster became a god. <continue>I believed the tribe was to blame. The boy was a scapegoat, made real by their fear. <continue>But I'd hoped, in learning the truth behind his tale, we could try to reason with him.
Text.zh: 我从未深究过黑鸦欺诈者是怎么变成神的。<continue>我一直认为是那个部族的错。那男孩是替罪羊，是他们恐惧的产物。<continue>但我曾希望，弄清故事背后的真相之后，或许就能跟他讲讲道理。

Text.en: From what I saw, the Raven Trickster is no god.
Text.zh: 依我所见，黑鸦欺诈者并非神祇。

Text.en: The Raven's a special kind o' evil. He makes my blood run cold.
Text.zh: 那黑鸦身上的恶非同一般。他让我不寒而栗。

Text.en: Plenty of evil gods. He's no different there.
Text.zh: 邪恶的神多的是，他也没什么特别的。

Text.en: I still don't understand. How did the boy become Tekaru, the Deceiver?
Text.zh: 我还是不明白。那男孩是怎么变成狡徒特卡鲁的？

Text.en: Did you learn what you need? Can you stop the Raven Trickster?
Text.zh: 你得到需要的信息了吗？你能阻止黑鸦欺诈者吗？

Text.en: I am not sure. I am trying to understand what just happened.
Text.zh: 我不确定。我正在努力理解刚才发生的事。

Text.en: He doesn't seem the reasonable sort. Even for a god. <continue>Or is he a demon?
Text.zh: 他看起来不像是通情达理的类型。哪怕是神也一样。<continue>还是说他是恶魔？

Text.en: Always thought Tangmazu was a strange one, even among gods. <continue>This 'origin story' only confirms that.
Text.zh: 我一直觉得唐玛祖很奇怪，即使在众神之中也是如此。<continue>这个“起源故事”更加印证了这一点。

Text.en: I know many tales of gods' ascensions, but the Raven's is unlike any I've heard.
Text.zh: 我知道许多神祇升格的故事，但黑鸦的故事与我所知的完全不一样。

Text.en: Is the Raven even a god? Never seen someone worship him. <continue>And I've seen folk worship some dark forces.
Text.zh: 黑鸦算是神吗？我从没见过有人供奉他。<continue>有些黑暗力量，我都看见过有人供奉。

Text.en: I thought 'The Raven and the Hare' described the foundation of his eventual ascension. <continue>But how did the boy live long enough to ascend, if the Darkness caught him?  <continue>If this is how the Raven Trickster was born... well, I've never heard of a child becoming a god.
Text.zh: 我曾以为《黑鸦与野兔》描述的是他最终升格为神的起点。<continue>但如果黑暗抓住了那个男孩，他又怎能活到升格之时？<continue>如果黑鸦欺诈者就是这样诞生的……好吧，我从没听说过孩子能变成神。

Text.en: How do we know it's the right boy? I saw the Darkness attack him.
Text.zh: 我们怎么知道这是那个男孩？我看见黑暗袭击了他。

Text.en: If he was attacked, the boy would have died. The Darkness spared him.
Text.zh: 如果他遭受了袭击，那男孩应该已经死了。黑暗饶过了他。

Text.en: If the Darkness made him Tekaru, I would not consider him 'spared'.
Text.zh: 如果黑暗造就了特卡鲁，我可不认为那是“饶过”。

Text.en: It must be a lonely existence. He'd be the only one of his kind.
Text.zh: 那一定是一种无比孤独的存在。这世上，再无同类。

Text.en: You said the Darkness opened the boy's head?
Text.zh: 你说黑暗打开了那男孩的脑袋？

Text.en: It scratched his forehead open and poured something into him. Something potent.
Text.zh: 它划开了他的前额，往里面倒了什么东西。某种强大的东西。

Text.en: Then the boy was a vessel already capable of receiving the Darkness's gift.
Text.zh: 这说明男孩当时就是可以接受黑暗恩赐的容器了。

Text.en: Could that gift be the source of Tangmazu's power?
Text.zh: 那恩赐会是唐玛祖力量的来源吗？

Text.en: Perhaps the boy was cursed after all.
Text.zh: 或许那男孩原本就被诅咒了。

Text.en: No child is born evil.
Text.zh: 没有孩子生来就是邪恶的。

Text.en: On this, we disagree. The Darkness chose him. It saw his evil and embraced him.
Text.zh: 关于这一点，我们看法不同。黑暗选中了他。黑暗看到了他的邪恶，并接纳了他。

Text.en: It is possible that the boy was already chosen. That, I will concede.
Text.zh: 有可能那男孩早已被选中，这点我可以承认。

Text.en: The Raven Trickster could be a servant of this Darkness.
Text.zh: 黑鸦欺诈者可能是这黑暗的仆从。

Text.en: That rings true. But there must be more to it.
Text.zh: 这话有道理。但肯定不止于此。

Text.en: What makes you think so?
Text.zh: 你为什么这么想？

Text.en: The Raven Trickster causes madness. I don't see how that benefits the Darkness.
Text.zh: 黑鸦欺诈者会制造疯狂。我不明白这对黑暗有什么好处。

Text.en: T'were not the Spirit that punished his tribe, but the Raven. And Darkness aided him.
Text.zh: 惩罚他的部族的不是万灵，而是黑鸦。黑暗助了他一臂之力。

Text.en: Perhaps. But why did the Darkness choose this child?
Text.zh: 或许吧。但黑暗为什么会选这个孩子？

Text.en: Aye, a fair question. I've never heard tell o' Darkness that far from the Wildwood.
Text.zh: 是啊，问得好。我从没听说过黑暗会出现在离荒林那么远的地方。

Text.en: You're right... Maybe that's where the hare was headed?
Text.zh: 你说得对……也许那野兔就是往那个方向跑的？

Text.en: Do you still believe it was the tribe's fear that made him a god? <continue>Awful long time to hold a grudge against a boy, cursed or not.
Text.zh: 你还是觉得是部族的怨恨让他成了神吗？<continue>对一个男孩心怀怨恨那么久，不管他是不是被诅咒了，都说不过去。

Text.en: No... I do not. The Darkness seems to have chosen the boy.
Text.zh: 不……我不这么认为了。看来是黑暗选中了那个男孩。

Text.en: Unlucky kid. But better kids have experienced worse, I'm afraid.
Text.zh: 可怜的孩子。但恐怕比他惨的无辜孩子多的是。

Text.en: Yes, but this is not the same. The Spirit should have protected him.
Text.zh: 是的，但这不一样。万灵本该保护他的。

Text.en: Can gods be made?
Text.zh: 神祇可以被创造吗？

Text.en: In what sense? The divine spark once molded men into gods.
Text.zh: 什么意义上的造神？曾经神性火花能把凡人塑造成神。

Text.en: The Darkness gave something to the boy. Could it have been this 'divine spark'?
Text.zh: 黑暗给了男孩什么东西。那东西会是这个“神性火花”吗？

Text.en: No. The Darkness is not of Divinity, nor of Corruption. It is something else...
Text.zh: 不。黑暗既不属于神性，也不属于腐化。它是别的东西……

Text.en: In the Wildwood, ideas can become gods. And quick, too.
Text.zh: 在荒林中，意念可以成为神。而且很快。

Text.en: That's true. But they cannot exist outside the Wildwood.
Text.zh: 没错。但它们无法在荒林之外存在。

Text.en: Ach. That's where you're wrong. They can, and they do.
Text.zh: 唉。这你可就错了。它们可以，而且确实存在于外界。

Text.en: Hmm. You've given me much to consider. <continue>The Winter Owl has gone silent. Perhaps this is something we should not know.
Text.zh: 嗯，你说的那些话我得好好想想。<continue>凛冬之枭沉默了。或许有些事情，我们不该知道。

Text.en: [DNT]
Text.zh: 你之前不是问过关于原初之魂的事吗？<continue>在大焚毁之前，原初之魂将我们所有人团结在一起。它护佑着我们，启迪着我们。<continue>后来，我们的族人与原初之魂分离，个中缘由已湮没在时间之中。

Text.en: [DNT]
Text.zh: 这些问题，说不定你还能找到答案。<continue>我和我的盟友已经找到了原初之魂。

Text.en: [DNT]
Text.zh: 也许下次你应该找得再仔细点。<continue>我找到了原初之魂。举手之劳而已。

Text.en: [DNT]
Text.zh: 说出来你可能不信，我找到了原初之魂。

Text.en: [DNT]
Text.zh: 他们分离的原因或许已无从知晓，但原初之魂本身并没有消失。

Text.en: [DNT]
Text.zh: 你为什么这么说？

Text.en: [DNT]
Text.zh: 这个嘛……我找到了它。

Text.en: [DNT]
Text.zh: 它如今又回到了我们族人的手中。<continue>准确地说，是在我手里。

Text.en: [DNT]
Text.zh: 嘿，坐稳了，老爷子，这玩意儿还真让我给找着了！

Text.en: [DNT]
Text.zh: 可能也没有湮灭吧。

Text.en: [DNT]
Text.zh: 怎么讲？

Text.en: [DNT]
Text.zh: 原初之魂依然存在。我亲眼所见。

Text.en: [DNT]
Text.zh: 也许它能再次将我们团结起来。

Text.en: [DNT]
Text.zh: 恐怕我对这件事不抱什么希望，德鲁伊。

Text.en: [DNT]
Text.zh: 希望不希望的，根本不重要。原初之魂是真实存在的。我找到了。

Text.en: [DNT]
Text.zh: 什么？你胡说什么！原初之魂不是一件实体，它是一份承诺！<continue>你是不是中了黑鸦的疯病了？

Text.en: [DNT]
Text.zh: 长老，我没有骗你。我亲手拿过原初之魂。

Text.en: [DNT]
Text.zh: 你爱装糊涂就装吧。但它是个真实存在的实体。

Text.en: [DNT]
Text.zh: 这既非妄想，也非戏言。原初之魂确确实实是个实体。

Text.en: [DNT]
Text.zh: 我向你保证，它<i>{就是}个实体。

Text.en: [DNT]
Text.zh: 我是认真的，长老。我亲眼所见。

Text.en: [DNT]
Text.zh: 我跟你说，我亲手拿过那该死的东西！

Text.en: [DNT]
Text.zh: 我向你保证，我神志清醒。

Text.en: [DNT]
Text.zh: 一开始我也不敢相信，但我跟你保证……我说的都是真的。

Text.en: [DNT]
Text.zh: 够了！我在这世上的时日已经不多了，别再让我折寿了。

Text.en: I'd say 'Runeseeker' is an apt moniker. Farrow's a quick learner.
Text.zh: 要我说，“符文猎手”这名字还真贴切。法罗这人学东西真快。

Text.en: Perhaps that's due to his teacher.
Text.zh: 也许是因为有这么一个老师。

Text.en: Oh, no, I couldn't possibly take credit!
Text.zh: 哎呀，不不不，我可不敢居功！

Text.en: But you are teaching him what you know, yes?
Text.zh: 可你不是在把自己的本事传授给他吗？

Text.en: I don't know... he gets under my skin at times.
Text.zh: 我也不知道……而且他总是能招惹到我……

Text.en: That's a surprise! What is it about him that bothers you?
Text.zh: 这倒新鲜！他哪儿招惹你了？

Text.en: Oh, it's just his... <i>{everything.} And... he looks at me... often. <continue>Anyway. You're teaching him rune... <i>{things.}
Text.zh: 唉，就是他……<i>{整个人}都招我烦。还有就是……他老是……盯着我看。<continue>不管怎样，你在教他符文……<i>{那些东西。}

Text.en: I gather that you're instructing him on runes and smithing.
Text.zh: 我猜你在教他符文和锻造。

Text.en: Seems you have a lot you could teach him. <continue>Assuming you want to do such a thing?
Text.zh: 你应该有很多可以教他的。<continue>前提是你愿意教他？

Text.en: So you're teachin' him about runes and the like?
Text.zh: 所以你是在教他符文之类的东西？

Text.en: You seem to have become fast friends.
Text.zh: 看来你们俩很快就混熟了。

Text.en: You could say that. <continue>We don't yet know each other well, but we do have a great deal in common.
Text.zh: 可以这么说。<continue>我们还没到知根知底的地步，但确实有很多共同点。

Text.en: Then I take it you've enjoyed teaching him about runesmithing?
Text.zh: 这么说，你很乐意教他符文锻造了？

Text.en: How do ya know? He's learnin' from you?
Text.zh: 你怎么知道？他在跟你学？

Text.en: It's good o' you, to teach him what ye know.
Text.zh: 你愿意把本事教给他，这点挺难得的。

Text.en: Of course! It's the duty of every smith to pass down the art. <continue>Although, I do feel like I'm learning things as well. <continue>The Ezomyte and Kalguuran runesets are not <i>{exactly} the same. Each has a few symbols the other does not. <continue>Considering how established our science is, I find that... curious... and disturbing.
Text.zh: 那当然！把手艺传下去，是每个铁匠的本分。<continue>不过，我感觉自己也学到了新东西。<continue>艾兹麦和卡古兰的符文体系并不完全相同。两边都有一些对方没有的符号。<continue>一想到我们的学问已经发展得很成熟了，我就觉得……这事既让人好奇，又让人不安。

Text.en: Farrow is voracious for knowledge. He never lets up.
Text.zh: 法罗对知识简直是贪得无厌，一刻都不消停。

Text.en: Sounds like you've found a fellow enthusiast... or a rival.
Text.zh: 看来你找到了志趣相投的人……或者说，竞争对手。

Text.en: I wish he would.
Text.zh: 我倒希望他能消停点。

Text.en: Please understand. It's not a knock against him!
Text.zh: 别误会，我不是在贬低他！

Text.en: Of course <i>{you} find that quality endearing.
Text.zh: <i>{你}当然会觉得这样挺讨人喜欢的。

Text.en: A like-minded fellow, then.
Text.zh: 那就是志同道合的人咯。

Text.en: Is that a compliment, or a complaint?
Text.zh: 你这是夸他，还是损他呢？

Text.en: That some grudgin' respect I hear?
Text.zh: 我怎么听着像是有点不情愿的赞许呢？

Text.en: It seems he's earned your respect.
Text.zh: 看来他赢得了你的尊重。

Text.en: Like two peas in a bloody pod, you are!
Text.zh: 你们俩简直一个模子刻出来的！

Text.en: Somethin' of a backhanded compliment, wouldn't ye say?
Text.zh: 这算是那种“话里带刺”的夸奖吧？

Text.en: Oh, I like the lad, I'll admit that. I just can't let him know the full truth just yet... <continue>He has the drive to rival a Runefather someday. <continue>That's the kind of thing you can't tell a young man without setting him up for failure. I know from experience...
Text.zh: 好吧，我承认，我是喜欢那小子。只是现在还不能全都告诉他……<continue>看他那股劲头，有朝一日绝对能比肩符文之父。<continue>这种话，你要是跟一个年轻人说了，反而会害了他。我有过切身体会……

Text.en: You and Farrow have done yourselves proud. You did as you set out to do, and reforged the Triskelion Flame.
Text.zh: 你和法罗这次干得真漂亮。你们说到做到，把三耀之火重铸出来了。

Text.en: If not for you and Farrow, your precious Triskelion Flame would remain in pieces. <continue>You must be pleased with yourself.
Text.zh: 要不是你和法罗，你那宝贝三耀之火现在还只是碎片呢。<continue>你现在一定挺得意的吧。

Text.en: Your life's work is complete. The Triskelion Flame is whole once more.
Text.zh: 你这一辈子的心血，总算圆满了。三耀之火完好如初了。

Text.en: Well, I'd like to think I've a bit more work in me yet!
Text.zh: 嗯，我倒觉得我这心血，还能再折腾出点东西！

Text.en: Still... you must be thrilled.
Text.zh: 不管怎么说……你一定高兴坏了吧。

Text.en: How's it feel to have completed your quest after so long?<continue>You not only found the Flame, but reforged it.
Text.zh: 过了这么久，终于完成了心愿，感觉如何？<continue>你不仅找到了三耀之火，还重铸了它。

Text.en: I can't take all the credit. You did the hard part.
Text.zh: 我可不敢把功劳全揽在自己身上。那些脏活累活都是你干的。

Text.en: Either way... it's finished. You won.
Text.zh: 不管怎样……总算是完成了。你成功了。

Text.en: So... is it everythin' you imagined?
Text.zh: 那么……这一切跟你想象的一样吗？

Text.en: Come again?
Text.zh: 你说什么？

Text.en: The Triskelion Flame! How does it feel to have finally completed your quest?
Text.zh: 三耀之火啊！终于完成了心愿，你感觉如何？

Text.en: It would seem congratulations are in order – for you and Farrow both. <continue>By your combined efforts, the Triskelion Flame has been remade.
Text.zh: 看来确实该恭喜你了，还有法罗。<continue>你们俩合力让三耀之火重获新生。

Text.en: So, you and Farrow, eh – who'd have thunk it? You only went and reforged the Triskelion Flame!
Text.zh: 你和法罗真是——谁能想到呢？你俩还真就把三耀之火重铸出来了！

Text.en: Ye've the look of a man content with his lot – and rightly so. <continue>I know what it means to ye to have reforged the Triskelion Flame.
Text.zh: 看你这神情，像是心满意足了，也确实该这样。<continue>我知道重铸三耀之火对你来说意义重大。

Text.en: Yes. Incredible! I never thought this day would come. In truth, part of me always thought the quest doomed. <continue>Even if I found the Triskelion Flame, I was certain Kalguuran smithing could not fully reforge it. <continue>I was right. I couldn't have done it without Farrow. The final two runes we imbued were from Ezomyte runeshapes.
Text.zh: 是啊。太不可思议了！我从没想过会有这一天。说实话，我心里一直觉得这个心愿没指望了。<continue>就算找到了三耀之火，我也觉得凭卡古兰的锻造术，不可能把它完全重铸。<continue>结果证明我没错。没有法罗，我根本做不到。我们最后灌注的两枚符文，来自艾兹麦的符文体系。

Text.en: What does that mean for the Flame?
Text.zh: 那对三耀之火来说意味着什么？

Text.en: What difference does that make?
Text.zh: 那会带来什么不同？

Text.en: What does that mean for the Flame?
Text.zh: 那对三耀之火来说意味着什么？

Text.en: Does that alter the Flame in some way?
Text.zh: 那会不会让三耀之火产生变化？

Text.en: So the Flame's different to how it were before?
Text.zh: 所以三耀之火跟以前不一样了？

Text.en: Might that affect the Flame?
Text.zh: 那会不会影响三耀之火？

Text.en: Is that bad?
Text.zh: 这算是坏事吗？

Text.en: I wouldn't say <i>{'bad'.}
Text.zh: 我倒不觉得是<i>{“坏事”。}

Text.en: Oh. So... it's good, then?
Text.zh: 哦。那就是……好事？

Text.en: But the Flame's still as it was before?
Text.zh: 那三耀之火还跟以前一样吗？

Text.en: I'm not sure... It's like writing a poem in another language. <continue>It sounds beautiful to me, even if there's context I might be missing... <continue>With runecraft, it's the will behind it that truly matters.
Text.zh: 我不确定……这就像用另一种语言写诗。<continue>在我听来还是很美，但可能错过了一些意境……<continue>符文工艺嘛，真正重要的是背后的意志。

Text.en: Hail! Fortuitous timing. Don't suppose you two saw the comet? Scared us half to death!
Text.zh: 幸会！来得真凑巧。你俩不会也瞧见那彗星了吧？把我们吓个半死！

Text.en: Our timing is no coincidence. Uhtred <i>{somehow} pulled that comet down himself. <continue>But... no sign of the Triskelion Flame. Olroth must have it.
Text.zh: 可不是凑巧。乌崔德<i>{不知用了什么法子}把那颗彗星拽下来了。<continue>不过……还是没找到三耀之火的踪迹。准是在欧罗什手里。

Text.en: Then the adventure continues. But for now, this comet demands our immediate attention.
Text.zh: 那冒险还得继续。不过眼下，这颗彗星才是当务之急。

Text.en: We sent scouts to the crater... the comet's pure Verisium!
Text.zh: 我们派人去陨石坑查探过……那彗星是纯净维金！

Text.en: But a scant glimpse of it is all we managed. We can't get any closer due to an impenetrable barrier and... <continue>Lots of... crawlers.
Text.zh: 不过也就远远看了一眼。我们没法靠近，有一道打不破的屏障，而且……<continue>满地都是……爬虫。

Text.en: Surely there can't be <i>{that} many crawlers.
Text.zh: 不至于有<i>{那么}多爬虫吧。

Text.en: Have a look for yourself.
Text.zh: 你自己去看看就知道了。

Text.en: Pure Verisium? Recovering that would be invaluable.
Text.zh: 纯净维金？要是能弄到手，那可真是无价之宝。

Text.en: A whole comet of Verisium? That's gotta be worth a lot.
Text.zh: 一整颗都是维金的彗星？那得值多少钱啊。

Text.en: Never seen that much Verisium in my life. It's quite a sight.
Text.zh: 我这辈子都没见过那么多维金。真是开眼了。

Text.en: And what are you going to do with all that Verisium?
Text.zh: 那么大一块维金，你们打算怎么用？

Text.en: You could achieve a great deal with that amount of Verisium.
Text.zh: 那么大一块维金，能做成不少大事。

Text.en: It cannot be overstated. The possibilities are endless!
Text.zh: 怎么说都不夸张，这真是有无穷的可能性啊！

Text.en: What kind of barrier?
Text.zh: 什么样的屏障？

Text.en: Reckon I can handle the crawlers... but what's this about a barrier?
Text.zh: 爬虫我倒能对付……可那屏障是怎么回事？

Text.en: It seems to be magic in origin... but beyond that we know little. Only that it seems impassable in its current state.
Text.zh: 看起来像是魔法的产物……但除此之外，我们也不清楚。只知道目前这个状态根本过不去。

Text.en: Never easy, is it?
Text.zh: 就没顺当过，是吧？

Text.en: Would that it were. This is a delay we cannot afford.
Text.zh: 要是能顺顺当当的就好了。我们可耽误不起。

Text.en: We must harvest that Verisium, and quickly. With it, we will forge the finest arsenal Wraeclast has ever seen!
Text.zh: 我们得赶紧把那维金弄到手。有了它，我们就能打造出瓦尔克拉斯有史以来最精良的武器库！

Text.en: The King has a way of knowing about these things. If the King gets there before we can...
Text.zh: 这种事，国王肯定听到风声了。要是他抢先下手……

Text.en: We simply can't let that happen.
Text.zh: 绝不能让他得逞。

Text.en: Then we'd better get a move on.
Text.zh: 那我们最好快点行动。

Text.en: Unless you have a plan to stop him, all of this is moot.
Text.zh: 除非你有办法拦住他，不然说再多都没用。

Text.en: Agreed. But I would imagine that the King's resources outnumber our own...
Text.zh: 同意。可我敢打赌，国王的势力可比咱们大得多……

Text.en: Easy to say. Harder to do.
Text.zh: 说起来容易，做起来难。

Text.en: Alright, so what's the plan?
Text.zh: 好吧，那你有什么计划？

Text.en: Great... How do we do that?
Text.zh: 好极了……那具体怎么做？

Text.en: It is no simple task. We number so few... and his resources are many.
Text.zh: 这事可不简单。咱们就这么几个人……而他的势力那么庞大。

Text.en: Well then I hope you've a plan.
Text.zh: 好吧，希望你已经有主意了。

Text.en: We'll figure something out. We're a resourceful bunch.
Text.zh: 总会有办法的。我们这帮人点子多。

Text.en: I have an idea... The Triskelion Flame was said to ward off all manner of threats.
Text.zh: 我有个主意……据说三耀之火能抵御一切威胁。

Text.en: See? He figured it out already!
Text.zh: 瞧见没？他这就想出来了！

Text.en: We've even more reason to complete our grand quest now! Are you ready, local?
Text.zh: 这下咱们更应该去完成那伟大使命了！准备好了吗，本地朋友？

Text.en: This logbook... Olroth is our last chance of finding the Triskelion Flame.<continue>It pains me to imagine my ancestor could do such a thing. Yet I am overcome with excitement!  <continue>This is it! With this logbook, we will right the wrongs of the past, and bring about a glorious future!
Text.zh: 这本日志……欧罗什是我们找到三耀之火的最后希望了。<continue>想到我的祖先可能做过这种事，真让人难以接受。可我又激动得不行！<continue>就是它了！有了这本日志，我们就能纠正过去的错误，开创一个光明的未来！

Text.en: You're certain this is the one?
Text.zh: 你确定就是这本？

Text.en: So sure, are we?
Text.zh: 真的？你确定？

Text.en: This one? You're quite sure?
Text.zh: 就这本？你确定？

Text.en: How can you be sure?
Text.zh: 你怎么能确定？

Text.en: And you're sure about this?
Text.zh: 你这么有把握？

Text.en: Are you certain?
Text.zh: 你确定吗？

Text.en: This one? You sure?
Text.zh: 这本？你确定？

Text.en: Ye seem rather sure o' that.
Text.zh: 你看着挺自信的啊。

Text.en: Yes! Are you not? I feel it in my bones! <continue>Prepare yourself. We're about to make history!
Text.zh: 当然！你们不觉得吗？我这直觉错不了！<continue>做好准备吧。我们要创造历史了！

Text.en: It's all ready to go. I've got a good feeling about this one.
Text.zh: 都准备好了。我有预感，这次准能成。

Text.en: What do you mean to do with Olroth once we find him?
Text.zh: 找到欧罗什之后，你打算怎么处理他？

Text.en: If and when we find Olroth, what is it you want to do with him?
Text.zh: 要是找到了欧罗什，你想怎么处理他？

Text.en: Should we find Olroth, what do you intend we do?
Text.zh: 如果真的找到欧罗什，你打算怎么办？

Text.en: Say we find Olroth – what then?
Text.zh: 假设我们找到了欧罗什——然后呢？

Text.en: If Olroth's here, what do you mean to do with him?
Text.zh: 要是欧罗什在这儿，你打算拿他怎么办？

Text.en: If Olroth's actually here, what do we do with him?
Text.zh: 如果欧罗什真在这儿，我们该怎么处理？

Text.en: Assuming Olroth is indeed here... what do we do once we find him?
Text.zh: 假设欧罗什真在这儿……找到他之后该怎么做？

Text.en: If ye speak true, and Olroth is here... what happens once we find him?
Text.zh: 如果你说得没错，欧罗什在这儿……找到他之后怎么办？

Text.en: Well, if he has the Triskelion Flame... something tells me he won't relinquish it without a fight.
Text.zh: 嗯，如果三耀之火真在他手里……我总觉得他不会乖乖交出来。

Text.en: Same old story then.
Text.zh: 又是老一套。

Text.en: Dealing death is sort of my day to day at this point.
Text.zh: 杀人这事，对我来说现在都快成日常了。

Text.en: Then a fight he shall have.
Text.zh: 那就狠狠干一架吧。

Text.en: Do they ever?
Text.zh: 他们哪次不是这样？

Text.en: Wouldn't have it any other way.
Text.zh: 不打反倒不习惯了。

Text.en: Heh... what else is new?
Text.zh: 呵，还能有什么新花样。

Text.en: I expected as much.
Text.zh: 我早料到了。

Text.en: No different than usual, then.
Text.zh: 那跟平时也没什么两样。

Text.en: That's Wraeclast for you! If there's one thing I've learned – this land loves a good battle. <continue>Let's not disappoint her.
Text.zh: 瓦尔克拉斯就这样！要说我学到了什么——那就是这片土地好战得很。<continue>我们可别让它失望。

Text.en: He appears... rather monstrous.
Text.zh: 他看着……挺像怪物的。

Text.en: He commanded the Triskelion Flame... but it was destroyed in the fight.
Text.zh: 他确实掌控过三耀之火……可在战斗中被毁了。

Text.en: And now, he's just as broken as the Flame he wielded against me.
Text.zh: 如今，就像他用来对付我的三耀之火一样，他也支离破碎了。

Text.en: As expected, he had the Triskelion Flame... He even tried to use it against me. <continue>I'm afraid the Flame did not survive the fight.
Text.zh: 不出所料，三耀之火确实在他手里……他甚至想用它来对付我。<continue>可惜那东西在那场战斗中被毁了。

Text.en: You were right about one thing... Olroth had the Flame alright.
Text.zh: 有一件事你说对了……欧罗什确实拿着三耀之火。

Text.en: He did? Well, it's not all bad news.
Text.zh: 拿着？那也不全是坏消息。

Text.en: Let me finish. He <i>{had} the Flame... but tried to use it against me. <continue>You can probably guess what happened next.
Text.zh: 听我说完。他<i>{拿着}三耀之火……但拿来对付我了。<continue>结果怎样，你大概能猜到。

Text.en: Fought like a monster too. Even used that Flame o' yours to try and kill me. <continue>I held me own... but the Flame didn't survive the fight.
Text.zh: 他打起架来简直像个怪物，还想用你那把火焰杀死我。<continue>我倒是撑住了……可那火焰没撑住。

Text.en: You were right about him havin' the Flame. Bastard tried to kill me with it. <continue>Got more than he bargained for, as you can see... but the Flame died with him.
Text.zh: 你说得没错，他确实有三耀之火。那混蛋想用它杀我。<continue>结果你也看见了，他自找苦吃……可火焰也跟着他一起完蛋了。

Text.en: Olroth tried to wield the Triskelion Flame against me. <continue>It was destroyed in the fight... and he along with it.
Text.zh: 欧罗什想用三耀之火对付我。<continue>结果在战斗中被毁了……他也跟着一起完蛋了。

Text.en: That ancestor o' yours didnae take kindly to my intrusion. <continue>He tried to wield the Flame against me... and it was destroyed in the fight that followed.
Text.zh: 你那位祖先可不欢迎我闯进去。<continue>他想用三耀之火对付我……结果在战斗中被毁了。

Text.en: He used it <i>{against} you? I think we keep that part to ourselves... <continue>To come all this way, only for the Triskelion Flame to be destroyed as we finally reach it...  <continue>What unbearable luck.
Text.zh: 他用火焰<i>{对付}你？我看这事儿我们就别往外说了……<continue>费了这么大劲，结果三耀之火刚到手就毁了……<continue>这运气也太背了。

Text.en: I'm sorry, Dannig. There was nothing I could have done.
Text.zh: 抱歉，丹宁。我也是没办法。

Text.en: It was unavoidable.
Text.zh: 这事真避免不了。

Text.en: I understand your disappointment all too well.
Text.zh: 你的失望，我完全能理解。

Text.en: I wish it could've been avoided.
Text.zh: 真希望这事能避免。

Text.en: I'm sorry, Dannig. Was fightin' for me life back there. Could hardly spare a thought for preservin' the Flame.
Text.zh: 抱歉，丹宁。我当时只顾着拼命活下来，哪还顾得上保住那火焰。

Text.en: I'm sorry, mate. Wish I coulda done more.
Text.zh: 抱歉，兄弟。真希望我能保住它。

Text.en: My apologies, Dannig. If I could have preserved it, I would have.
Text.zh: 抱歉，丹宁。要是能保住它，我一定保。

Text.en: I'm sorry, lad. I'm afraid there was no other way.
Text.zh: 抱歉，小子。当时真是没办法了。

Text.en: Hmm... I've a mad thought. Utterly mad. And yet, we have nothing to lose.<continue>Take what you can of the shattered Triskelion, then let us return to Kingsmarch!
Text.zh: 嗯……我有个疯狂的念头，真的很疯狂。不过我们也没什么可失去的了。<continue>把三耀之火的碎片尽量收好，然后我们回君锋镇！

Text.en: Amazingly... we've done it!
Text.zh: 真不敢相信……我们居然做到了！

Text.en: And with all that Verisium...
Text.zh: 有了这么多维金……

Text.en: ... we could change the course of Middengard's future.
Text.zh: ……咱们就能改变米登加德的未来了。

Text.en: Someone's gotta do it sometime. May as well be us...
Text.zh: 这事总得有人去做。不如就我们来吧……

Text.en: We must act fast. As we speak the King is likely sending ships here to investigate the crater.
Text.zh: 咱们得快点。说不定国王已经派船来探查陨石坑了。

Text.en: Logistics man here, at your service. I'll get it done.
Text.zh: 后勤专员在此，随时为您效劳。这事包在我身上。

Text.en: What will you do with the Triskelion Flame?
Text.zh: 你们打算怎么处理三耀之火？

Text.en: Your big bad king will want to get his hands on the Flame, I suspect. What are you going to do with it?
Text.zh: 我猜你们那位厉害的国王肯定想拿到三耀之火。你们打算怎么处理它？

Text.en: What of the Triskelion Flame? With the King's ships on the way, will you try to hide it?
Text.zh: 三耀之火呢？国王的船就要来了，你们想把它藏起来？

Text.en: Uhh... quite the opposite, actually!
Text.zh: 呃……恰恰相反！

Text.en: I am not sure what you mean.
Text.zh: 我不明白你的意思。

Text.en: What next for the Flame? Now that you've finally got your hands on it.
Text.zh: 那火焰接下来怎么处理？你们好不容易拿到手了。

Text.en: What are your plans for the Triskelion Flame?
Text.zh: 你们打算怎么处理三耀之火？

Text.en: What are you gonna do with the Triskelion Flame? Guessin' you can't leave somethin' like that just lyin' around.
Text.zh: 你们打算拿三耀之火怎么办？我猜那种东西不能这么随便放着吧。

Text.en: I suspect the King will have interest in more than just the crater – he's sure to come for the Flame as well.
Text.zh: 我猜国王不只盯上了那个陨石坑，他肯定也会来抢火焰。

Text.en: With any luck, he won't yet know about that particular development.
Text.zh: 但愿他还不知道这事儿。

Text.en: Not yet, perhaps. But when he does...
Text.zh: 也许现在还不知道。可一旦他知道……

Text.en: Ye've spent half yer life chasin' that Flame... What do you plan to do with it now?
Text.zh: 你半辈子都在追查那三耀之火……现在到手了，打算怎么办？

Text.en: Well, in case you didn't notice... they've strapped it to my mast.
Text.zh: 你该不会没看见吧？他们把那玩意儿绑在我的桅杆上了。

Text.en: If sailing to Kalguur with the Triskelion Flame burning bright doesn't rally our people to action... nothing will.
Text.zh: 要是举着熊熊燃烧的三耀之火驶向卡古兰，都唤不起族人的斗志……那真是无计可施了。

Text.en: It's decided then. Let's move our arses! <continue>For the Kalguur!
Text.zh: 就这么定了。赶紧动身！<continue>为了卡古兰！

Text.en: For the Kalguur!
Text.zh: 为了卡古兰！

Text.en: For the... ah bugger I missed it.
Text.zh: 为了卡……啊，该死，没跟上。

Text.en: Our runic wards weren't enough. <continue>Corruption itself was held at bay, but the shockwave was overwhelming.<continue>The people of Kingsmarch survived. None of us sick, nor insane. The rest... <continue>It's all been destroyed.
Text.zh: 我们的符文结界还是不够强。<continue>腐化本身是被挡住了，可那冲击波太过猛烈。<continue>君锋镇的人都活了下来。没人染病，也没人发疯。可其他的……<continue>全都毁了。

Text.en: At least you have your health. But what do you plan to do now?
Text.zh: 至少人都没事。不过你接下来打算怎么办？

Text.en: If it were me, I'd probably cut and run. But knowing you, you'll be infuriatingly positive about the whole thing.
Text.zh: 要是我的话，恐怕早就跑路了。可我知道你，肯定还是会乐观得让人恼火。

Text.en: I've no intention of giving up, if that's what you mean.
Text.zh: 我可没打算放弃，如果你是这意思的话。

Text.en: I am sorry, Dannig. Will you return home?
Text.zh: 真是遗憾，丹宁。你会回故乡吗？

Text.en: To Kalguur? This may be dire... but returning home presents its own problems.
Text.zh: 回卡古兰？眼下是够糟的……但回去肯定也有别的烦心事。

Text.en: Yet another thing the Beast has to answer for. What will you do now?
Text.zh: 又是一笔邪兽欠下的血债。你现在打算怎么办？

Text.en: The fact you're alive at all is summat to be thankful for. <continue>Still, though... I feel for ya. What'll you do now?
Text.zh: 你们还能活着，就该谢天谢地了。<continue>可话说回来……我真替你难过。接下来你打算怎么办？

Text.en: That's rotten luck, mate. But hey – can't keep a good man down, right?
Text.zh: 真是倒霉透了，兄弟。不过嘛——好汉可不会轻易被打倒，对吧？

Text.en: Well said.
Text.zh: 说得好。

Text.en: The destructive power of the Beast once again rears its ugly head. What will you do next?
Text.zh: 邪兽那可怕的破坏力又卷土重来了。你下一步打算怎么办？

Text.en: Sounds like yer wards <i>{were} enough. <continue>Not to save yer buildings, aye... but the folk inside 'em owe you their lives.
Text.zh: 听起来你们的结界<i>{还是}挺管用的。<continue>虽然没保住房子，对……可房子里的人能活下来，都是多亏了你。

Text.en: Yes... We can be thankful that more lives weren't lost, if nothing else.
Text.zh: 是啊……至少没死更多人，这也算是不幸中的万幸了。

Text.en: We'll have to rebuild, from the ground up. <continue>We did it once. We can do it again!
Text.zh: 我们只能重建，从头再来。<continue>我们建过一次，就能再建一次！

Text.en: Where is this mysterious Gwennen, anyway?
Text.zh: 话说回来，那位神秘的桂温妮到底在哪儿？

Text.en: This... <i>{Gwendolyn,} or whatever her name is...
Text.zh: 那个……叫什么来着，<i>{桂温多琳}什么的……

Text.en: You mean Gwennen?
Text.zh: 你是说桂温妮？

Text.en: Yes, her... where is she?
Text.zh: 对……她……在哪儿？

Text.en: This Gwennen of yours... where is she?
Text.zh: 你们说的那个桂温妮……她在哪儿呢？

Text.en: Where's this Gwennen I've heard so much about?
Text.zh: 老听人提起那个桂温妮，她在哪儿呢？

Text.en: Where's this Gwennen o' yours, anyway?
Text.zh: 你们那位桂温妮到底在哪儿？

Text.en: So where's this Gwennen got to? I thought I mighta seen her by now.
Text.zh: 那位桂温妮跑哪儿去了？我还以为早该见到她了。

Text.en: Where is this ally of yours – the one you call Gwennen?
Text.zh: 你那位盟友——就是叫桂温妮的那个，她在哪儿？

Text.en: I keep hearin' the name Gwennen, but I'm yet to meet the lass.
Text.zh: 我老是听见桂温妮这名字，可还没见过这姑娘呢。

Text.en: She's out adventuring, though I'll admit, it's been longer than usual. <continue>Might be time to start worrying...
Text.zh: 她外出冒险了，不过说实话，这次确实比平时久。<continue>没准真得开始担心了……

Text.en: Gwennen's back, safe and sound, but we can't play it cautiously anymore. <continue>She's right. We came here on a quest, and we need to push forward.
Text.zh: 桂温妮回来了，平安无事，可咱们不能再谨慎行事了。<continue>她说得对。咱们是带着使命来的，得继续往前推进。

Text.en: Very well. What are you going to do?
Text.zh: 好吧。你们打算怎么做？

Text.en: And how do you intend to do that?
Text.zh: 你们打算怎么推进？

Text.en: Push forward how?
Text.zh: 怎么继续推进？

Text.en: Alright... so what are you going to do?
Text.zh: 行……那你们打算怎么做？

Text.en: Time to dust off the ol' gear, eh?
Text.zh: 该把老装备拿出来掸掸灰了，嗯？

Text.en: Feelin' the call of adventure, are ye?
Text.zh: 感受到冒险的召唤了，是吗？

Text.en: Right you are!
Text.zh: 说得对！

Text.en: Spoken like a man with a plan.
Text.zh: 听起来像是心里有谱了。

Text.en: Have you a plan?
Text.zh: 你有计划吗？

Text.en: Not exactly, but... we've rested on our laurels long enough.
Text.zh: 也不完全是，不过……我们安于现状太久了。

Text.en: It's time to get back on the trail. <continue>We must finally find... the Triskelion Flame!
Text.zh: 该重新上路了。<continue>我们必须找到……三耀之火！
