Text.en: Curious about our ancestors, are you? Ha! So are we. That's why we came here! <continue>I am a descendant of Olroth, who led the valiant Knights of the Sun! <continue>The Knights were the most honourable Kalguuran soldiers. They were the right arm of the King.
Text.zh: 对我们的祖先感兴趣？哈！我们也是。所以才跑到这儿来！<continue>我是欧罗什的后代，他曾率领英勇的烈日骑士！<continue>烈日骑士是卡古兰最可敬的战士。他们曾是国王的左膀右臂。

Text.en: Are the Knights of the Sun still around?
Text.zh: 烈日骑士还在吗？

Text.en: The Knights 'were' the right arm of the King? What happened to them?
Text.zh: 他们“曾是”国王的左膀右臂？后来出什么事了？

Text.en: Are these 'knights' of yours still a thing?
Text.zh: 你们那些“骑士”现在还在吗？

Text.en: Do the Knights of the Sun still fight in Kalguur?
Text.zh: 烈日骑士还在卡古兰作战吗？

Text.en: The Knights still exist, but they're all nobles and loyalists. Not much honour among them.
Text.zh: 骑士团还在，不过现在都是些贵族和保皇派，哪还有什么可敬的地方。

Text.en: Do you hate the Knights like you hate your king?
Text.zh: 你像讨厌国王那样讨厌烈日骑士吗？

Text.en: I don't know what gave you that impression, dangerous one!
Text.zh: 我不明白你怎么会有这种印象，危险分子！

Text.en: You look like the type to fight for a king.
Text.zh: 你看起来像是会为国王而战的那种人。

Text.en: Do I? That's... odd.
Text.zh: 是吗？那可真……奇怪。

Text.en: Are you a knight, then?
Text.zh: 那你是骑士吗？

Text.en: A runesmith and a knight! Yer an impressive one!
Text.zh: 符文匠兼骑士！你可真了不起！

Text.en: I am no knight, but I respect the knights of old.
Text.zh: 我不是骑士，但我敬重古代的骑士。

Text.en: Olroth, by all accounts, was a beloved leader and a storied fighter.<continue>But he was also the first among that ancient expedition to begin using the forbidden gems. <continue>He believed it was necessary to protect his people. That decision cost him greatly.  <continue>Olroth was ambushed, and almost died. His body was preserved in glass to prevent his death. <continue>Yet when the hour was most dire, and Olroth was needed, his body was gone. <continue>His fate remains unknown.
Text.zh: 从各种记载来看，欧罗什是一位受人爱戴的领袖，也是战功赫赫的斗士。<continue>可他也是那支远古远征队中，第一个开始使用禁忌宝石的人。<continue>他相信这是保护子民的必要之举。可这个决定让他付出了惨重代价。<continue>欧罗什遭到伏击，差点丧命。他的身体被封在玻璃中以防死亡。<continue>可到了最危急、最需要欧罗什的时候，他的身体却不见了。<continue>他的下落至今不明。

Text.en: That is... troubling.
Text.zh: 这……听着可不太妙。

Text.en: Ominous indeed.
Text.zh: 真不是什么好兆头。

Text.en: That's... curious.
Text.zh: 这可……真蹊跷。

Text.en: Rather ominous.
Text.zh: 真是太不吉利了。

Text.en: It is, isn't it? Perhaps you now understand why I'm so desperate to know what became of him.
Text.zh: 是吧？也许你现在能明白，我为什么那么急切地想知道他的下落了。

Text.en: Ooh, I love a good mystery!
Text.zh: 哦，这种谜团我最喜欢了！

Text.en: Don't we all? This is just one mystery I was hoping to have solved by now.
Text.zh: 谁不是呢？可这个谜团，我原本希望能早点解开的。

Text.en: Could someone have stolen his body?
Text.zh: 会不会有人偷走了他的身体？

Text.en: You think someone stole it? His body, I mean.
Text.zh: 你觉得会不会是有人……偷走了它？我是说他的身体。

Text.en: That's the great mystery, isn't it? I only hope that one day soon we'll know the answer.
Text.zh: 这不就是最大的谜团吗？我只希望有朝一日能知道答案。

Text.en: Certainly curious. Would love to know what happened to the man.
Text.zh: 确实蹊跷。真想弄清楚这个人的下落。

Text.en: You and me both, friend.
Text.zh: 咱们都一样，朋友。

Text.en: What does Olroth's logbook say?
Text.zh: 欧罗什的日志里写了什么？

Text.en: So then, what's in Olroth's logbook?
Text.zh: 欧罗什的日志里写了什么？

Text.en: What does Olroth's logbook tell us?
Text.zh: 欧罗什的日志都记了些什么？

Text.en: What does Olroth's logbook say?
Text.zh: 欧罗什的日志里写了什么？

Text.en: What does Olroth's logbook say?
Text.zh: 欧罗什的日志里写了什么？

Text.en: What does Olroth's logbook say?
Text.zh: 欧罗什的日志里写了什么？

Text.en: So... what's Olroth's logbook got to say?
Text.zh: 所以……欧罗什的日志里写了什么？

Text.en: What does Olroth's logbook say?
Text.zh: 欧罗什的日志里写了什么？

Text.en: Curiously, it was not written by Olroth himself. He was out of sorts at the time, I believe.<continue>This logbook was written by a Remembrancer, tasked by Uhtred to follow Olroth's movements.
Text.zh: 有意思的是，这本日志其实不是欧罗什本人写的。我想他那会儿已经神志不清了。<continue>这本日志是一位文书写的，他当时被乌崔德派去跟踪欧罗什。

Text.en: Were they spying on him?
Text.zh: 他们在监视他？

Text.en: In other words... they were spying on him.
Text.zh: 换句话说……他们在监视他。

Text.en: So this Remembrancer was spying on him?
Text.zh: 所以这个文书在监视他？

Text.en: They were spying on him?
Text.zh: 他们在监视他？

Text.en: Sounds like they were spyin' on him.
Text.zh: 听起来他们在监视他。

Text.en: So they were, what, spyin' on the guy?
Text.zh: 所以怎么着，他们在监视那家伙？

Text.en: He was surveilling Olroth?
Text.zh: 他在监视欧罗什？

Text.en: Seems like this Remembrancer turned spy for Uhtred.
Text.zh: 看来这位文书成了乌崔德的眼线。

Text.en: It seems so. Let's see what he found. <continue>'Day one. I have found Olroth. He is no longer within his glass case. He appears unwell. His skin is sunken. His eyes are closed, yet he walks with purpose. To where... I do not know.' <continue>'Day three. He does not stop to eat, or sleep. It is hard to keep pace. There have been no deviations in his path and I fear he is headed toward our main settlement.' <continue>'Day four. I have found some reprieve. Olroth will only walk through the villages at night. During the day, I rest as he stands at their borders, waiting. He has not spoken a word.'<continue>'Day six. Olroth has stolen the Triskelion Flame and burned every ship in our harbour, save one, which he took. I made it on board... but I think he knows I am here.'  <continue>'I know not the day. We've stopped... I'm uncertain where, or why. It sounds like he's... '<continue>That's it. There's ancient dried blood on the page. A grim portent. <continue>But why did Olroth do this? What was he doing with the Flame?
Text.zh: 看起来确实如此。来看看他都记录了什么。<continue>“第一天。我找到了欧罗什。他已不在玻璃容器里了。他的状态很不对劲，皮肤干瘪，双目紧闭，却步态坚定地走着。至于要去哪儿……我不知道。”<continue>“第三天。他不吃不睡，我快跟不上他了。他一路没有偏离方向。我担心他是要去我们的主要定居点。”<continue>“第四天。我终于能稍事休息了。欧罗什只在夜间穿过村庄，白天则站在村庄边界等待，我便趁机休息。他一句话也没说过。”<continue>“第六天。欧罗什偷走了三耀之火，烧毁了港口所有的船，只留下一艘自己开走。我设法混上了船……但我感觉他知道我在船上。”<continue>“我不知道今天是第几天。我们停下来了……我不知道这是哪儿，也不知道为什么停。听上去他好像在……”<continue>就这些了。这一页上有一块干涸已久的血迹，看着不太吉利。<continue>可欧罗什为什么要这么做？他要三耀之火做什么？

Text.en: So this is how the story finally ends. <continue>Why do I feel like there's still something missing? <continue>Olroth stole the Triskelion Flame, burned all the other ships, and was heading... <i>{somewhere}...
Text.zh: 看来故事就这么结束了。<continue>可为什么我总觉得还是少了点什么？<continue>欧罗什偷走了三耀之火，烧毁了所有其他船只，然后去了……<i>{某个地方}……

Text.en: When Karui are exposed to Corruption, they develop blood fever and are driven mad.
Text.zh: 卡鲁人一旦接触腐化，就会患上血热病，然后发疯。

Text.en: Yes... as happened on Ngakanu. You think Olroth was mad with blood fever?
Text.zh: 是啊……就像纳卡努那次一样。你觉得欧罗什是得了血热病，然后疯了？

Text.en: It is possible. Or Kalguurans have their own 'blood fever', and that was the cause.
Text.zh: 有可能。或者卡古兰人有他们自己的“血热病”，那玩意儿就是祸根。

Text.en: It seems he went mad. Corruption tends to do that to folks.
Text.zh: 看来他确实是疯了。腐化能让人发疯。

Text.en: Why not burn <i>{all} the ships, if it was only madness? He had some purpose.
Text.zh: 如果只是疯了，为什么不把<i>{所有}船都烧掉？他肯定是有目的的。

Text.en: Sure. But what if he was being controlled by someone?
Text.zh: 有道理。可万一他是被什么人控制了呢？

Text.en: What if Olroth was working with someone? Who are the allies of the Kalguur?
Text.zh: 万一欧罗什在和什么人合作呢？卡古兰有什么盟友吗？

Text.en: We had a few allies on Wraeclast, and none on Middengard. Kalguuran history is fraught with conflict.
Text.zh: 我们在瓦尔克拉斯没什么盟友，在米登加德更是一个都没有。卡古兰的历史就是一路打打杀杀。

Text.en: Then he was returning home. It must be so.
Text.zh: 那他是想回家。肯定是这样。

Text.en: You don't think that cove was where he meant to go?
Text.zh: 你不觉得那个海湾就是他要去的地方吗？

Text.en: I don't. Stealing the Flame, only to hide away... It makes little sense.
Text.zh: 我不觉得。偷了三耀之火，却只是藏起来……说不通。

Text.en: Could he have been returning to Kalguur?
Text.zh: 他会不会是要回卡古兰？

Text.en: Gems and <i>{rathin} drove him to madness. It's as simple as that.
Text.zh: 宝石和<i>{腐化}让他发了疯，也许事情就这么简单。

Text.en: There must be more to it. Otherwise, you would be mad as well.
Text.zh: 肯定不止于此。不然你现在也该疯了才对。

Text.en: Who says I'm not? Madness does not mean he was mindless.
Text.zh: 谁说我还没疯？发疯不代表他没有理智。

Text.en: If it were me, I'd be off trying to find the highest bidder for that Flame.
Text.zh: 要是我的话，我早就拿着那火焰去找出价最高的人了。

Text.en: That's... bleak. Who could put a price on something so important?
Text.zh: 这……真可悲。那么重要的东西，怎么能用价钱衡量？

Text.en: The Trarthans, the Brinerots, the Eternals, the Templars... It's not a question of who would, but who was around to buy it.
Text.zh: 特拉特斯人、布林洛特人、永恒帝国、圣堂武僧……问题不是谁会买，而是当时谁在附近。

Text.en: It seems he understood something about the Flame that others did not.
Text.zh: 也许他对这火焰的理解，比其他人更深。

Text.en: What makes you think that?
Text.zh: 你为什么这么觉得？

Text.en: Olroth made a decision to hide the flame, and prevent others from following him. <continue>Perhaps it is best we do not know his reasons.
Text.zh: 欧罗什决定藏起火焰，还阻止别人跟踪他。<continue>也许他做这事的理由，我们还是不知道为好。

Text.en: Took the Flame and ran... Sounds to me like he were escaping from the mess he made.
Text.zh: 拿着三耀之火跑了……在我看来，他像是在逃避自己闯下的祸。

Text.en: That's not possible. Olroth was many things, but he was not a coward.
Text.zh: 不可能。欧罗什再怎么样，也绝不会是懦夫。

Text.en: Oh! The whinging nursemaid of Kingsmarch thinks good men always act bravely? Ha! He fled.
Text.zh: 哦！君锋镇那个喋喋不休的保姆居然觉得好人一定会英勇到底？哈！他就是逃跑了。

Text.en: Perhaps... hmm. Or maybe I just don't want this particular journey to end.
Text.zh: 也许吧……唉。可能我只是不想让这段旅程就这么结束。

Text.en: I may keep searching, until I discover where Olroth was headed... and <i>{why}. <continue>For centuries, we thought Uhtred was the traitor. We were wrong. <continue>Maybe, in thinking Olroth went mad... we're still wrong.
Text.zh: 我会继续查下去，直到弄清楚欧罗什要去哪里……以及 <i>{为什么}。<continue>数个世纪以来，我们一直以为乌崔德是叛徒。可我们错了。<continue>或许认定欧罗什疯了……也是错的。

Text.en: It was a whisper of a hope when we first came to Wraeclast, but we finally have what we need. <continue>We have the resources to return home. To fight back.
Text.zh: 当初我们刚到瓦尔克拉斯时，希望如游丝般微弱。可如今，我们终于有了所需的一切。<continue>我们有了回家的资本，有了反击的力量。

Text.en: Against your king, you mean?
Text.zh: 你是说，对抗你们的国王？

Text.en: So no more cowering at the mention of your king, I take it?
Text.zh: 那以后不会一提到你们的国王就畏畏缩缩了？

Text.en: You would rise up against your king?
Text.zh: 你要起来反抗你们的国王？

Text.en: Against the King?
Text.zh: 对抗国王？

Text.en: Against the King?
Text.zh: 对抗国王？

Text.en: Oho! Sounds like ol' Cadigan's days are numbered!
Text.zh: 哦嚯！看来那个老卡迪甘的好日子到头了！

Text.en: You mean to take up arms against your king?
Text.zh: 你们要拿起武器对抗你们的国王？

Text.en: Finally found yer appetite for revolution, I see.
Text.zh: 看来你终于下定决心造反了。

Text.en: Yes. Our people have lived in fear for too long. Too afraid to even speak his name half a world away.
Text.zh: 是的。我们的人民活在恐惧里太久了，甚至远在半个世界之外，都不敢提他的名字。

Text.en: With the Triskelion Flame... the Verisium we've found, and all our new allies... <continue>I am going to set right what once went wrong. <continue>For everything he has done to us... for all the pain he's caused... Cadigan must die.
Text.zh: 有了三耀之火……我们找到的维金，还有所有这些新盟友……<continue>我要纠正过去的错误。<continue>为了他对我们做的一切……为了他造成的所有痛苦……卡迪甘必须死。

Text.en: Are you with me?
Text.zh: 你愿意跟我一起上吗？

Text.en: You know that I am.
Text.zh: 你知道我愿意的。

Text.en: I do, indeed. Brothers-in-arms!
Text.zh: 我当然知道，并肩作战的兄弟！

Text.en: Yes. I like the sound of that.
Text.zh: 好，这话我爱听。

Text.en: This <i>{Cadigan}... would he bow to me, if I showed him my power?
Text.zh: 这位<i>{卡迪甘}……要是我向他展示力量，他会不会向我低头？

Text.en: Never. His arrogance is the only thing that outshines his cravenness.
Text.zh: 永远不会。他那股子傲慢，比他的怯懦还扎眼。

Text.en: Alright then. I'll destroy him for you.
Text.zh: 那好。我替你除掉他。

Text.en: I was hoping you'd say that.
Text.zh: 我就知道你会这么说。

Text.en: Until the end.
Text.zh: 奉陪到底。

Text.en: That means more to me than you know.
Text.zh: 这句话对我的意义，你无法想象。

Text.en: Of course. Only one thing to do with a tyrant like him.
Text.zh: 当然。像这种暴君，只有一个下场。

Text.en: And at last, we have the means to do it.
Text.zh: 现在，我们终于有能力做到这一点了。

Text.en: Been with you this far, haven't I? Not about to back out now.
Text.zh: 我都陪你走到这一步了，还能临阵退缩不成？

Text.en: Magnificent! That's what I like to hear!
Text.zh: 太棒了！这话我爱听！

Text.en: Am I with you? I'll stick a blade in his gut myself if that's what it comes down to.
Text.zh: 跟你一起上吗？要真到那一步，我亲手把刀捅进他肚子里。

Text.en: You're a true friend.
Text.zh: 你真是够朋友。

Text.en: Yes. I am with you.
Text.zh: 是的，我愿意跟你一起。

Text.en: How lucky we are to have met you. It's hard to comprehend how different things would've been had you never found your way to Kingsmarch.
Text.zh: 能遇见你，我们何其幸运。难以想象，如果你当初没来君锋镇，一切会变成什么样。

Text.en: You are too kind.
Text.zh: 你太客气了。

Text.en: Aye, lad. No man deserves to live beneath the bootheel of a tyrant like him.
Text.zh: 没错，小子。没人该活在这种暴君的压迫之下。

Text.en: Couldn't have said it better myself.
Text.zh: 你说得太好了，我都说不出来。

Text.en: Plans will be made in secret... It will take many months to prepare.  <continue>I'll let you know when the time is right.
Text.zh: 我们要暗中筹划……准备工作可能要花上好几个月。<continue>等时机成熟，我会通知你。

Text.en: Fancy seeing you out here. How've you been?
Text.zh: 没想到在这儿还能碰见你，最近怎么样？

Text.en: Fancy seeing you out here, mountain-born. How are ya?
Text.zh: 没想到在这儿还能碰见你，山民老乡。最近过得咋样？

Text.en: Good day to ya. I'm Hilda.
Text.zh: 你好，我是希尔达。

Text.en: Good day, fellow mountain-born. I'm Hilda.
Text.zh: 你好啊，山民老乡，我是希尔达。

Text.en: Been roaming the world for some time now, hunting all manner of beasts...
Text.zh: 我在外游历有些时日了，狩猎各种猛兽……

Text.en: I'm alive, so that's something. What are you doing out here, Hilda?
Text.zh: 我还活着，这就算不错了。你在这儿干嘛呢，希尔达？

Text.en: I'm on top of the world... or at least what's left of it. Now tell me... what <i>{are} you doing, Hilda?
Text.zh: 我好得快上天了……尽管这片天早就塌得差不多了。话说……你<i>{到底}在干嘛呢，希尔达？

Text.en: The world has fallen to madness, but I live on. I am grateful for that. <continue>Hilda, I must ask... what exactly are you doing?
Text.zh: 这世界已经乱成一团了。但我还活着，对此我心怀感激。<continue>我想问问你，希尔达，你在做什么？

Text.en: Wraeclast is ravaged. But... we survive. What are you doing, Hilda?
Text.zh: 瓦尔克拉斯都被折腾成这样了。但……我们活下来了。你在这儿做什么呢，希尔达？

Text.en: Well... what's it look like I'm doing?
Text.zh: 嗯……你看我像是在干什么？

Text.en: My journey continues. Looks like you've been huntin'...
Text.zh: 我的旅途还在继续。看来你一直在狩猎……

Text.en: All things considered, I'm just happy to be breathin'. You've been busy it seems...
Text.zh: 想想现在这情况，能活着就不错了。你好像挺忙的……

Text.en: I continue on the Dreamer's path. It seems your path has been fruitful...
Text.zh: 我还在追寻幻梦者的路。看来你这条路收获不少啊……

Text.en: I still meander through all this wretched madness. Is this your kill, Hilda?
Text.zh: 我还在这片乱七八糟的世界里晃悠。这是你打的猎物，希尔达？

Text.en: That's right!
Text.zh: 没错！

Text.en: This great beast were me latest target... now it's me trophy. <continue>And I tell you what, it does more than just line me pocket...  <continue>It's a real rush!
Text.zh: 这头大家伙是我最近的目标……现在是我的战利品了。<continue>我跟你说，它可不光是能换钱……<continue>那种刺激，真没得说！

Text.en: Hmm... Come to think of it, I bet you'd make a fine trophy hunter!
Text.zh: 嗯……说起来，我觉得你也挺适合去当战利品猎人的。

Text.en: Hmm... and seeing you've made it this far... with the world gone mad and all... maybe you've got what it takes to be a trophy hunter!
Text.zh: 嗯……在这乱七八糟的世界里……你都能活到现在……说不定你还真够格当个战利品猎人！

Text.en: I could use a distraction. What does it entail?
Text.zh: 我正想找点事消遣消遣……具体要做什么？

Text.en: More killing, you say? I'm in. What do I do?
Text.zh: 你是说多杀点东西？我喜欢。该怎么做？

Text.en: Perhaps this would be a welcome change. Explain more to me.
Text.zh: 这倒是个不错的转变。你详细说说。

Text.en: I sure could use more time to myself... tell me what to do.
Text.zh: 我确实想一个人清静清净……告诉我该怎么做吧。

Text.en: A hunt? You're speakin' my language now! Tell me more.
Text.zh: 狩猎？这我可太熟了！快说详细点！

Text.en: A job's a job. I'm your man. What do I do?
Text.zh: 有活干就行。我接了。怎么做？

Text.en: I will consider it. What does it involve?
Text.zh: 我会考虑的。具体要做些什么？

Text.en: Must I be everybody's lackey? Though... some more time in the woods wouldn't hurt. Go on then. Tell me more.
Text.zh: 我非得到处给人跑腿啊？不过……在林子里多待一阵倒也没坏处。行吧，你接着说。

Text.en: It's dead simple really. You get a target... <continue>You hunt it...  <continue>You kill it. <continue>You keep the rewards and notoriety, while I keep the carcass... <continue>Or at least what's left of it.
Text.zh: 其实简单得要命。你接个目标……<continue>然后去追踪它……<continue>干掉它。<continue>你拿奖励和名声，尸体归我……<continue>或者说剩下那点归我。

Text.en: Now... last I heard, a great beast was roaming through that area. <continue>If you can track it down, and put it down... well then that's all there is to it. <continue>It's worth noting... none of these beasts will go down easy. <continue>So you best bring all you've got to the hunt!
Text.zh: 嗯……我上次听说……有一头大家伙在那片区域游荡。<continue>你要是能把它找出来，再把它干掉……那就完事了。<continue>提醒你一句……这些野兽可都不好对付。<continue>所以你最好使出全力！

Text.en: Right... any questions?
Text.zh: 行……还有什么问题吗？

Text.en: Find beast. Kill beast. Get reward. Simple enough.
Text.zh: 找野兽，杀野兽，拿奖励。够简单。

Text.en: Kill animals for fun? People have been doing that forever. Seems straightforward enough.
Text.zh: 杀野兽找乐子。大家一直都这么干。听起来简单又直接。

Text.en: Locate the beast and end its life. This is all?
Text.zh: 找到野兽，然后终结它。就这些？

Text.en: Track down trophy and bring it down. I got it.
Text.zh: 追踪猎物，把它拿下。明白了。

Text.en: Hunting's in me blood. I know what to do.
Text.zh: 打猎这事儿我熟，我知道该怎么干。

Text.en: I never doubted you, mountain-born.
Text.zh: 我就知道你行，山民老乡。

Text.en: Run around the world risking my neck for some hide? Easy enough, love.
Text.zh: 满世界乱跑，把命豁出去，去换几张兽皮。小事一桩，亲爱的。

Text.en: I am not stupid. Kill the beast. Simple enough.
Text.zh: 我又不傻。杀掉野兽。简单得很。

Text.en: I understand, lass. Hunt the beast, kill the beast.
Text.zh: 我听明白了，姑娘。猎野兽，杀野兽。

Text.en: That's it!
Text.zh: 就是这么回事！

Text.en: You've done a fine job! Dare I say, this beast looks even more formidable than last I saw it. <continue>Well, you've earned your Trophy Hunter badge today, that's for sure.
Text.zh: 干得漂亮！我敢说这头野兽比我上次见的时候还要猛。<continue>不过嘛，你今天的表现绝对配得上战利品猎人的称号。

Text.en: Now, you up for another challenge?<continue>I <i>{think} you might be ready for this one. Only one way to find out...
Text.zh: 好了，想不想再来挑战一次？<continue>我<i>{觉得}你差不多可以试试这个了。不过嘛，试试才知道……

Text.en: [NOAUDIO] And remember: try to keep it in one piece, if you can.
Text.zh: 记住啊，可以的话，尽量留个全尸。

Text.en: I'll do my best.
Text.zh: 我会尽力的。

Text.en: I'll handle it just fine, thank you.
Text.zh: 我能搞定的，多谢。

Text.en: Indeed.
Text.zh: 当然。

Text.en: I'm more than ready.
Text.zh: 我早就准备好了

Text.en: Reckon I can handle it.
Text.zh: 这点事我能应付。

Text.en: Yeah, yeah. I know the drill.
Text.zh: 行啦行啦，规矩我都懂。

Text.en: Consider it dead.
Text.zh: 就当它已经死了。

Text.en: Suppose we'll see, won't we?
Text.zh: 走着瞧呗，对吧？

Text.en: This makes two. You're on the up and up! Did you even break a sweat?
Text.zh: 这是第二头了。你越来越厉害了！连汗都没出吧？

Text.en: It's dead. I'm not. That's all that matters.
Text.zh: 它死了，我没死。这就够了。

Text.en: Me? Sweat? Of course not. It was quite a bore, really.
Text.zh: 我？出汗？怎么可能。不过有点无聊。

Text.en: You're an inspiration!
Text.zh: 你可真行！

Text.en: It fought well, I assure you.
Text.zh: 说真的，它也挺能打。

Text.en: I didn't stop to check. Main thing is... it went down.
Text.zh: 我没空停下来看。关键是……它已经倒了。

Text.en: Me? Come on, Hilda. I know what I'm doin'.
Text.zh: 我？拜托，希尔达。我有分寸。

Text.en: Right you are, mountain-born.
Text.zh: 说得对，山民老乡。

Text.en: Getting into fights is all I do these days. It went down easy enough.
Text.zh: 我现在天天都在打架，这种算轻松的。

Text.en: No. I am well disciplined and balanced in combat.
Text.zh: 当然，我在战斗中一向冷静克制。

Text.en: All I ever do is bloody well sweat. I'm getting too old for this shite...
Text.zh: 我倒是一直在拼命流汗。老了，真有点吃不消这活了……

Text.en: Of course, of course...
Text.zh: 当然，当然……

Text.en: Time to up our game. This one might just test you. But that's half the appeal, right? <continue>Keep your eyes open and your mind focused!
Text.zh: 是时候提高难度了。这头可能会让你吃点苦头。但这不就是它好玩的地方吗？<continue>睁大眼睛，集中精神！

Text.en: Don't go in there overconfident now, alright?
Text.zh: 别太自信了，好吗？

Text.en: And that's three then. You're making it look bloody easy.
Text.zh: 这是第三头了。你打得也太轻松了吧。

Text.en: Now, I must warn you... this last beast is utterly ferocious. <continue>Are you sure you're up to it?
Text.zh: 不过我得提醒你……最后一头可是凶得要命。<continue>你确定你应付得来？

Text.en: I look forward to the challenge.
Text.zh: 这种挑战，我正求之不得呢。

Text.en: Come now, dear... It may as well already be dead.
Text.zh: 拜托，亲爱的……它死定了。

Text.en: The beast should be the one to fear me.
Text.zh: 该害怕的是它。

Text.en: I do not fear it. Not at all.
Text.zh: 我一点都不怕它。

Text.en: Should ask the beast that. Not me.
Text.zh: 你该去问那畜生，不该问我。

Text.en: I'll have its head. Don't you worry.
Text.zh: 我会把它的头带回来，你放心。

Text.en: It'll be a quick kill, I am sure.
Text.zh: 我保证很快就能搞定。

Text.en: It won't know what hit it...
Text.zh: 它连怎么死的都不知道……

Text.en: Hah! You're enjoying this, I can tell!
Text.zh: 哈！我就知道你乐在其中！

Text.en: Get on the trail. I know you can do it!
Text.zh: 上路吧。我知道你行！

Text.en: Well, that there... that's the last of the beasts I've been tracking. <continue>You're a damn well qualified trophy hunter, in my book!
Text.zh: 好了……这就是我一直在追踪的最后一头野兽了。<continue>在我眼里，你已经是非常合格的战利品猎人了！

Text.en: Perhaps in the future, we can... find some more tails to chase, eh?
Text.zh: 干得好。以后有机会……我们再去追追别的猎物，怎么样？

Text.en: It was a good run, Hilda. I enjoyed it.
Text.zh: 这一趟很过瘾，希尔达。我很享受。

Text.en: Dare I say... I've learned a thing or two.
Text.zh: 说真的……我也学到了不少。

Text.en: It has been good working with you.
Text.zh: 跟你合作很愉快。

Text.en: I look forward to that.
Text.zh: 我很期待。

Text.en: It's been my pleasure, Hilda. Truly.
Text.zh: 这是我的荣幸，希尔达。真心话。

Text.en: Chasing tail? Heh. Yeah, alright.
Text.zh: 追猎物？嘿。行吧。

Text.en: Of course. Just let me know.
Text.zh: 当然。有活随时叫我。

Text.en: For now... I can rest a little. Pfft. Who am I kiddin'?
Text.zh: 现在……我可以稍微歇一歇了。唉，我这是糊弄谁呢。

Text.en: Remember what you've learnt on the hunt. I'm sure it'll come in handy down the road.
Text.zh: 记住你在狩猎中学到的东西。以后肯定会派上用场。

Text.en: I mean, just look around... <continue>Where's the precious Spirit now? Is it flockin' to our aid? No!
Text.zh: 你自己看看这周围…… <continue>那个所谓的万灵现在在哪儿？它跑来帮我们了吗？没有！

Text.en: The same can be said for many things. The First Ones also stay silent.
Text.zh: 很多事都是这样。初生之子不也这样沉默不语嘛。

Text.en: Surprise, surprise... even the ephemeral are selfish and self-serving.
Text.zh: 哎呀，真稀奇……连那些虚无缥缈的东西都只顾自己。

Text.en: I do not know enough about such matters. But I understand your disdain.
Text.zh: 我对这些事不太懂。但我明白你为啥瞧不上它。

Text.en: No one and nothing is coming to save us. We must save ourselves.
Text.zh: 没有谁，也没有什么东西会来救我们。我们得靠自己。

Text.en: It has its reasons, I'm sure. Perhaps it's weakened by the state of the world. We must not lose hope.
Text.zh: 我相信它有它的理由。也许是这个世界的状况让它变弱了。我们不能失去希望。

Text.en: The way I see it... it's all on us. We get by, we survive. Can't be waiting on miracles.
Text.zh: 依我看……只能靠咱们自己。活下去，挺过去。不能指望什么奇迹。

Text.en: Without direction... we are hopeless. The Dreamer provides. Perhaps, you should listen.
Text.zh: 没了指引……我们就毫无希望。幻梦者会指引我们，也许你该听听。

Text.en: The Draíocht have purpose: to show us our true potential. Not to give us all the answers.
Text.zh: 德莱契自有其意义。它会让我们看清自己的潜力，而不是替我们解答一切。

Text.en: All I know is I'm happiest with me axe in hand and me eyes on my target. The rest be damned.
Text.zh: 我只知道，手里握着斧子，眼睛盯着猎物，这时候我最痛快。其他的，爱咋咋地。

Text.en: Well... here and now, it's just you and me. But one day, it could be so much more. <continue>It's a dream of mine. Just... just imagine it. <continue>Great warriors, seeking out the world's most dangerous beasts. Competing to slay the deadliest of foes. <continue>It'd be lucrative for sure... but also, it'd be damn well glorious!
Text.zh: 好吧……此时此刻，只有你和我。但总有一天……这能扩张成更大的事业。<continue>这是我的一个梦想……你想象一下。<continue>一群顶尖的战士，满世界追寻最危险的野兽，看谁能干掉最致命的猎物。<continue>那肯定能赚大钱……而且那也绝对会是无上的荣耀！

Text.en: The 'Spirit' is a load of codswallop, if you ask me. I've nothing more to say on it.
Text.zh: 要我说，那些关于“万灵”的说法纯粹是一派胡言。这事我不想再说了。

Text.en: Best not ask me about that codswallop, friend. I fear you'll not think too kindly about what I have to say...
Text.zh: 朋友，你最好别问我那些乱七八糟的事。我怕你不会喜欢我的说法……

Text.en: Alright then, ya nosy blighter! Let me tell it as it is. <continue>I'm a hunter, aye. I run the woods, the fields and I've tracked many beasts. <continue>Yet I've never heard a voice, or saw a light leading me. <continue>It's always been just me. Alone. <continue>The Spirit stayed silent when my dear brother lay starving. <continue>Nor did it light up the night when my ailing mother was lost to the wilderness. <continue>Even if it does exist, what use is it? In a world brimmin' with pain and misery? <continue>For nothin'!
Text.zh: 好吧，你这爱打听的讨厌鬼！我就实话实说吧。<continue>老子是个猎手，这没错。我穿梭树林荒野，追踪过无数野兽。<continue>可从来没听过什么指引之声，没见过什么指路之光。<continue>从来都是老子独自一人。<continue>我亲弟弟饿得皮包骨头时，万灵屁都不放一个。<continue>我老娘病重时在那林子里走丢，它也没把黑夜照亮半分。<continue>就算这玩意儿真存在，在这满是痛苦悲惨的世道里能有什么用？<continue>屁用没有！

Text.en: I've read Uhtred's logbook a dozen times... So, I'm as ready as I'll ever be.
Text.zh: 乌崔德的日志我都看了十几遍了……我准备得没法再充分了。

Text.en: Are you alright? You don't seem excited.
Text.zh: 你没事吧？看起来不怎么兴奋啊。

Text.en: You don't <i>{sound} ready.
Text.zh: 你听起来不像准备好了。

Text.en: Are you not excited? You are retracing the tale of your lineage!
Text.zh: 你不兴奋吗？你正在追溯你家族的故事啊！

Text.en: Shouldn't you be, y'know, excited?
Text.zh: 这种时候你不是应该很兴奋吗？

Text.en: You seem... subdued. I'd have thought you'd be more excited.
Text.zh: 你看起来……挺平静的。我还以为你会更兴奋一些。

Text.en: Oh, I am! It's just, y'know... uh, complicated.
Text.zh: 我兴奋啊！只是……说来话长。

Text.en: Then let's get going.
Text.zh: 那咱们就走吧。

Text.en: Alright then. Let's get a move on.
Text.zh: 好吧。那赶紧出发。

Text.en: Then let us not waste any time.
Text.zh: 那就别浪费时间了。

Text.en: Right. This is my opportunity to learn the truth... no matter what it is.
Text.zh: 对。这是我查明真相的机会……不管真相是什么。

Text.en: My ancestor <i>{was} a traitor. Uhtred stole the Flame, dooming that first expedition.<continue>I am his lineage, but I could never do that to my friends. No one even knows <i>{why} he did it. <continue>I <i>{do} know that Uhtred never used your 'gems'. <continue>Maybe we won't have to fight him? If that's why the others all... y'know. Came back.
Text.zh: 我的祖先<i>{是}叛徒。乌崔德偷走了三耀之火，害得第一次远征全军覆没。<continue>我是他的血脉，但我绝不可能那样对我的朋友。而且没人知道他<i>{为什么}要那么做。<continue>但我<i>{确实}知道，乌崔德从来没用过你们那些“宝石”。<continue>说不定我们不用和他动手？如果那是其他人……你懂的……“活过来”的原因。

Text.en: It's all set up. Everything's going as planned. We should be fine! Right? Hah.<continue>But, uh... do you feel that? This place... I can <i>{feel} something.
Text.zh: 都安排好了。一切按计划进行。我们不会有事的！对吧？哈。<continue>不过，呃……你感觉到了吗？这个地方……我好像能<i>{感觉到}点什么。

Text.en: I don't feel anything.
Text.zh: 我啥也没感觉到。

Text.en: That makes one of us.
Text.zh: 看来只有你一个人有感觉。

Text.en: Yeah, I think I do! <continue>Wait, no... Just had somethin' in me boot.
Text.zh: 嗯，我好像也感觉到了！<continue>等等，没有……只是鞋里进了个东西。

Text.en: Oh, okay. Maybe I'm just imagining it then, based on what I've read.
Text.zh: 哦，好吧。那可能是我受那些记录影响，想太多了。

Text.en: Yes... I do. Something strong. Something ancient.
Text.zh: 对……我感觉到了！某种强大而古老的东西。

Text.en: I feel it. An ancient power dwells in this place.
Text.zh: 我感觉得到。一种古老的力量栖息在此地。

Text.en: I do... There's summat here, alright.
Text.zh: 我也感觉到了……这地方确实有点东西。

Text.en: You're not alone... I feel it too.
Text.zh: 不止你一个……我也感觉到了。

Text.en: Aye, lad. I feel it too.
Text.zh: 是啊，小子。我也感觉到了。

Text.en: Then we must be at the right spot.
Text.zh: 那我们应该是找对地方了。

Text.en: Every account of Uhtred's history agrees: he went into hiding at 'an ancient site of power'. <continue>Good luck. Let me know when it's safe, and I'll join you. I want to know what's down there.
Text.zh: 所有关于乌崔德的记载都提到，他躲进了“一个古老的能量之地”。<continue>祝你好运。等你确认安全了再叫我。我想知道下面到底有什么。

Text.en: I just saw a bloody comet fall right out of the sky!
Text.zh: 我刚看到一颗该死的彗星从天而降！

Text.en: Your ancestor... <i>{he} pulled that comet down.
Text.zh: 你的祖先……是<i>{他}把那颗彗星拽下来的。

Text.en: Your ancestor summoned that comet. Did you not know he could do that?
Text.zh: 是你的祖先把那颗彗星召唤下来的。你不知道他有这个能耐？

Text.en: Uhtred called down the comet himself. If I had not witnessed it, I would think it absurd.
Text.zh: 乌崔德亲自把那颗彗星召唤下来的。要不是我亲眼看到，肯定不敢相信。

Text.en: You're not going mad. It was Uhtred who called down the comet.
Text.zh: 你没疯。那彗星是乌崔德召唤下来的。

Text.en: That comet were all Uhtred's doin'. He ripped it from the very stars.
Text.zh: 那颗彗星是乌崔德干的好事。他直接把它从天上扯下来了。

Text.en: We may both be mad... I saw Uhtred call down the comet himself!
Text.zh: 咱俩可能都疯了……我亲眼看到乌崔德把彗星召唤下来了！

Text.en: Yes. Uhtred summoned the comet of his own accord.
Text.zh: 对，是乌崔德主动召来了彗星。

Text.en: It was he who called down the comet... yer man Uhtred.
Text.zh: 是他召唤了彗星……你家那位乌崔德。

Text.en: What? Really? Hmm... Uhtred's disciples <i>{studied} the stars.... but they were scientists, not magicians. <continue>Some older texts do describe them altering the firmament... I thought that was just a load of poetic fluff! <continue>But now, feeling the power emanating from that mirror... it is like the stars themselves observe this place.
Text.zh: 什么？真的吗？嗯……乌崔德的门徒确实<i>{研究}过星象……但他们是学者，不是魔法师。<continue>有些古老的文献确实说过他们可以改变苍穹……我还以为那只是一堆诗意的大话。<continue>但现在，感受着那面镜子散发出来的力量……就好像群星本身在注视着这里。

Text.en: What's that? Another logbook? Would you mind grabbing that for me?
Text.zh: 那是什么？又是一本日志？你能帮我拿过来吗？

Text.en: Wait. Where is the Triskelion Flame?
Text.zh: 等等。三耀之火在哪儿？

Text.en: If he had the Flame, I did not see it. Just this logbook.
Text.zh: 就算他拿着三耀之火，我也没看见。我只找到这本日志。

Text.en: This logbook's the only thing he left behind. <continue>Well, that and his remains.
Text.zh: 他就留下了这本日志。<continue>嗯，还有他的遗骸。

Text.en: If Uhtred had the Flame, he hid it well. I found only this logbook.
Text.zh: 就算乌崔德拿着三耀之火，他也藏得很好。我只找到了这本日志。

Text.en: Not here. If Uhtred had it, you'd think he'd have used it to defend himself.
Text.zh: 不在这儿。如果乌崔德真有三耀之火，他肯定会用它来防身。

Text.en: Beats me. All he had was that logbook.
Text.zh: 这我可不知道，他身上只有那本日志。

Text.en: Wherever it is, it ain't here.
Text.zh: 不管在哪儿，反正不在这儿。

Text.en: I can only assume it lies elsewhere. Uhtred did not have it on him.
Text.zh: 我只能推测它在别的地方。乌崔德身上没有。

Text.en: I wondered the same. If Uhtred once had the Triskelion, that time seems long since past.
Text.zh: 我也在想同样的问题。就算乌崔德曾经拥有过三耀之火，那也是很久以前的事了。

Text.en: Does that mean Uhtred was <i>{not} a traitor? He didn't steal the Flame after all? <continue>Then there's only one person who might have it... Olroth. <continue>We'll have to talk to Dannig about that one.
Text.zh: 那是不是说明乌崔德<i>{不是}叛徒？他其实并没有偷走三耀之火？<continue>那现在有可能拿着它的人就只剩一个了……欧罗什。<continue>我们得跟丹宁谈谈这事。

Text.en: Do you know where Gwennen might be?
Text.zh: 你知道桂温妮可能在哪儿吗？

Text.en: Where was this elusive Gwennen last seen?
Text.zh: 那个行踪不定的桂温妮最后一次出现是在哪儿？

Text.en: Where might Gwennen be?
Text.zh: 桂温妮可能在哪里？

Text.en: Do you know where Gwennen could've gone?
Text.zh: 你知道桂温妮可能去了哪里吗？

Text.en: Any ideas where Gwennen is?
Text.zh: 知道桂温妮在哪儿吗？

Text.en: You must have <i>{some} idea where Gwennen is... no?
Text.zh: 桂温妮在哪儿，你总该<i>{有点}头绪吧……没有吗？

Text.en: Where do you think Gwennen might be?
Text.zh: 你觉得桂温妮可能在哪里？

Text.en: Have ye any idea where Gwennen might be?
Text.zh: 你知不知道桂温妮可能在哪里？

Text.en: I wish I knew. When Gwen's away, she sends messages with updates on her adventures. <continue>We checked the last location she'd mentioned, and she wasn't there. We've found her handiwork around, but not her. <continue>I try not to worry too much, but uh... our kids are starting to ask questions.
Text.zh: 我要是知道就好了。桂温妮每次外出都会寄消息回来，讲讲她冒险的近况。<continue>我们去过她提及的最后一个地点，但她不在那儿。我们在附近找到了她干活留下的痕迹，但没找到她本人。<continue>我尽量不让自己太担心，但是……我们的孩子已经开始问这问那了。

Text.en: Who is this Gwendolyn? Or whatever her name is.
Text.zh: 这位格温多琳……管她叫什么呢……到底是谁？

Text.en: You mean Gwennen?
Text.zh: 你是说桂温妮？

Text.en: Who is Gwen?
Text.zh: 桂温妮是谁？

Text.en: Who is this Gwennen you all speak of?
Text.zh: 你们老提到的这个桂温妮是谁？

Text.en: Who's Gwennen?
Text.zh: 桂温妮是谁？

Text.en: Who is this Gwennen o' yours, anyway?
Text.zh: 你们说的桂温妮到底是谁啊？

Text.en: I haven't seen this Gwennen. Who is she? Is she a looker?
Text.zh: 我没见过这个桂温妮。她是谁？长得好看吗？

Text.en: That's... my wife. The four of us came from Kalguur together.
Text.zh: 那是……我妻子。我们四个一起从卡古兰过来的。

Text.en: Oh... heh. Sorry about that.
Text.zh: 哦……抱歉。

Text.en: But yes, if you must know, she is a 'looker'.
Text.zh: 不过，你要是非知道不可的话，她确实“挺好看的”。

Text.en: Who is this 'Gwennen'?
Text.zh: “桂温妮”是谁？

Text.en: I keep hearin' the name Gwennen, but I'm yet to meet the lass.
Text.zh: 我老听人提起桂温妮这个名字，但还没见过这位姑娘呢。

Text.en: She's my wife. The four of us came from Kalguur together, seeking adventure.
Text.zh: 她是我的妻子。当初我们四个一起从卡古兰出来闯荡。

Text.en: She was never content to stay in one place, Gwen. Especially not Kingsmarch.
Text.zh: 桂温妮从来都不愿意待在一个地方，尤其是君锋镇。

Text.en: Do you know where she might be?
Text.zh: 你知道她可能在哪里吗？

Text.en: Where was she last?
Text.zh: 她最后一次出现是在哪儿？

Text.en: Where have her travels taken her?
Text.zh: 她这次跑去哪儿了？

Text.en: I know how she feels.
Text.zh: 我懂她的感受。

Text.en: I'll try not to take that personally.
Text.zh: 没事，我不会觉得你是在说我。

Text.en: Not about Kingsmarch specifically. Just civilisation in general... it's not for me.
Text.zh: 倒不是针对君锋镇。就是我对整个人类文明……都不太感冒。

Text.en: Not to worry. I'm only jokin'.
Text.zh: 别在意，我开玩笑的。

Text.en: Well if she's not here, then where is she?
Text.zh: 那她要是不在这儿，她能在哪儿？

Text.en: So she's off adventurin' now then?
Text.zh: 那她现在出去冒险了？

Text.en: That she is.
Text.zh: 没错。

Text.en: Where?
Text.zh: 去哪儿了？

Text.en: So she ain't here? Then where is she?
Text.zh: 所以她不在？那她在哪儿？

Text.en: Where is she now?
Text.zh: 她现在在哪儿？

Text.en: I'm a bit of a wanderer myself... Where has she wandered off to this time?
Text.zh: 我自己也有点爱到处跑……她这次跑哪儿去了？

Text.en: I wish I knew. When Gwen's away, she sends messages with updates on her adventures. <continue>We checked the last location she'd mentioned, and she wasn't there. We've found her handiwork around, but not her. <continue>I try not to worry too much, but uh... our kids are starting to ask questions.
Text.zh: 我要是知道就好了。桂温妮每次外出都会寄消息回来，讲讲她冒险的近况。<continue>我们去过她提及的最后一个地点，但她不在那儿。我们在附近找到了她干活留下的痕迹，但没找到她本人。<continue>我尽量不让自己太担心，但是……我们的孩子已经开始问这问那了。

Text.en: Oh, I'm gonna miss it. My shop was my dream, but it was more than that... <continue>It was the one thing keeping the kids and I distracted from Gwennen's absence.
Text.zh: 我会怀念它的。那间店曾是我的梦想，但它的意义不止于此……<continue>在桂温妮不在时，它也是我和孩子们用来分散注意力的唯一寄托。

Text.en: You can always rebuild. You will get through this.
Text.zh: 你随时可以重建。你会挺过去的。

Text.en: You built your shop from nothing. There is no reason to say you can't do it again.
Text.zh: 你当初白手起家把店建起来，再来一次也不是不可能。

Text.en: Thank you, friend. I appreciate it.
Text.zh: 谢谢你，朋友。我很感激。

Text.en: Chin up, fat one. At least you're not dead!
Text.zh: 打起精神来，胖子。至少你还活着！

Text.en: You're alive. That's something.
Text.zh: 你还活着，这就已经很好了。

Text.en: I know there are more important things. People's homes were ruined, too.
Text.zh: 我知道还有更重要的事，很多人的家也被毁了。

Text.en: Your children were unharmed. We can find solace in that.
Text.zh: 至少你的孩子们平安无事，这就够让人庆幸的了。

Text.en: But the wee 'uns are okay, at least?
Text.zh: 但至少小家伙们都还好吧？

Text.en: Yes... and for that, I'm eternally grateful.
Text.zh: 是的……光这一点，我就已经无比感恩了。

Text.en: We'll just have to find her then, won't we?
Text.zh: 那我们就必须找到她，对吧？

Text.en: Don't worry, mate. We'll find her.
Text.zh: 别担心，哥们儿。我们会找到她的。

Text.en: I can only hope.
Text.zh: 也只能这么希望了。

Text.en: Oh well. It's not the first time we've had to start over. <continue>I'll call my new shop... <i>{Rog's Reactivation}. Heh, yeah. Or <i>{Rog's Return}. Yeah. Or <i>{Rog's Revival?} Hmm...
Text.zh: 唉，算了。也不是第一次从头再来了。<continue>新店就叫……<i>{罗格再出发}。嘿嘿，不错。或者<i>{罗格回归}。对。或者<i>{罗格重振？}嗯……

Text.en: She really did it. She found Medved! My Gwennen can do <i>{anything.}
Text.zh: 她真的做到了。她找到了梅德维德！我的桂温妮<i>{无所不能。}

Text.en: Your support means everything. Getting home to you and the kids is what kept me going.
Text.zh: 你的支持对我来说太重要了。就是想着可以回家见到你和孩子们，我才能坚持下去。

Text.en: I am glad to see you both happy.
Text.zh: 看到你们都好好的，我真高兴。

Text.en: Ew.
Text.zh: 真肉麻。

Text.en: It is wonderful to see lovers reunite.
Text.zh: 看到有情人重逢，真是太美好了。

Text.en: Alright then...
Text.zh: 好吧……

Text.en: Returnin' home is a blessin'.
Text.zh: 能回家就是福气。

Text.en: Yeah, don't forget, I helped too!
Text.zh: 对，别忘了，我也帮忙了！

Text.en: It is good you are safe.
Text.zh: 你平安就好。

Text.en: A beautiful reunion. Brings a tear to my eye.
Text.zh: 多美好的重逢啊。看得我都要掉眼泪了。

Text.en: Although... my adventure would have gone perfectly if I'd had <i>{your} plans, logistics man.
Text.zh: 不过……要是当初有<i>{你}这个后勤专员替我筹划，我的冒险肯定能完美无缺。

Text.en: I do know how to make a good plan or two, heh. In fact, I've got a couple of plans for us later...
Text.zh: 嘿嘿。我确实是做计划的一把好手。说起来，我晚点还有几个“计划”想跟你聊聊……

Text.en: How sweet. May I see the amulet he has made you?
Text.zh: 真甜蜜。我能看看他给你做的护身符吗？

Text.en: What? I don't wear jewellery. He made you a necklace?
Text.zh: 什么？我可不戴首饰。他给你做了条项链？

Text.en: He did not intend romance, though... I would have accepted. I imagine his touch is quite delicate, like the breeze.
Text.zh: 他倒不是想搞浪漫，不过……就算他是，我倒也不介意。我猜他手艺一定很灵巧，像微风一样轻柔。

Text.en: Uh, that's about enough I think... Sorry, we'd like to be alone now, if you don't mind.
Text.zh: 呃，差不多得了……不好意思，可以的话，我们想单独待会儿。

Text.en: You'd best start explaining the necklace.
Text.zh: 你最好给我解释解释那条项链。

Text.en: Yes, dear.
Text.zh: 是，亲爱的。

Text.en: First Ones save me... I'm still here, you know.
Text.zh: 初生之子在上……我还在这儿呢。

Text.en: Go on.
Text.zh: 你们继续。

Text.en: We'd like a minute to ourselves, friend.
Text.zh: 我们想单独待会儿，朋友。

Text.en: [NOAUDIO] Fine by me.
Text.zh: 我无所谓。

Text.en: Fine. But I'll be back!
Text.zh: 行。但我还会回来的！

Text.en: Ach! To be young again. Me and my Aoife would make the wolves howl...
Text.zh: 啊！真想再年轻一回。我和我的艾欧菲当年都能让狼跟着嚎起来……

Text.en: I... don't know what that means.
Text.zh: 我……不太明白你这话什么意思。

Text.en: You'll have to tell us later. We'd like to be alone now.
Text.zh: 回头再跟我们讲吧，我们现在想单独待会儿。

Text.en: I... think I should go.
Text.zh: 我……觉得我该走了。

Text.en: Oh, please don't. I'm already seasick.
Text.zh: 别别别，我已经够晕船了。

Text.en: How sweet. I will return later.
Text.zh: 真甜蜜。我等会儿再回来。

Text.en: A proper celebration is in order!
Text.zh: 该好好庆祝一下！

Text.en: I am... needed elsewhere.
Text.zh: 我……还有别的事要忙。

Text.en: It must be hard to leave Kingsmarch behind after all the time you've spent here.
Text.zh: 在君锋镇待了这么久，要离开一定很难受吧。

Text.en: You lot are starting up the adventure again, I see. What of Kingsmarch?
Text.zh: 你们这帮人又要重新去冒险了，是吧？那君锋镇怎么办？

Text.en: Kingsmarch has been your home for so long. How do you feel about leaving it?
Text.zh: 君锋镇已经是你的家了。要离开这里，你们心里什么感觉？

Text.en: Are you nervous about leaving Kingsmarch behind?
Text.zh: 要离开君锋镇，你们不紧张吗？

Text.en: You spent so long buildin' Kingsmarch up. Can't be easy now to leave.
Text.zh: 你们花了那么大的心血把君锋镇建起来。现在要离开，肯定不容易吧。

Text.en: Must be strange, movin' away from Kingsmarch after all this time.
Text.zh: 在君锋镇待了这么久，现在要离开，一定很别扭吧。

Text.en: Any doubts about leaving Kingsmarch?
Text.zh: 要离开君锋镇了，你有没有犹豫？

Text.en: How do ye feel about leavin' Kingsmarch?
Text.zh: 要离开君锋镇了，你有什么感受？

Text.en: Of course I'm scared, but Gwen's right. We cannot continue to live under the King's scrutiny. <continue>Kingsmarch was built under the King's charter, but we are no longer bound to uphold it.<continue>And our kids will have better futures if we succeed <i>{this time}.
Text.zh: 我当然害怕，但桂温妮说得对。我们不能再活在国王的监视下了。<continue>君锋镇是靠国王特许经营权建立起来的，但现在我们不用再受这个束缚了。<continue>而且如果<i>{这次}我们成功了，那我们的孩子们也会有更好的未来。

Text.en: This time?
Text.zh: 这次？

Text.en: This time? Tell me more.
Text.zh: 这次？你再说详细点。

Text.en: There was another time?
Text.zh: 还有上一次？

Text.en: This time?
Text.zh: 这次？

Text.en: I'm sorry – this time?
Text.zh: 不好意思，你说“这次”？

Text.en: Whaddaya mean 'this time'?
Text.zh: 你说“这次”是什么意思？

Text.en: This time?
Text.zh: 这次？

Text.en: Did ye say 'this time'?
Text.zh: 你刚才是说“这次”吗？

Text.en: What? No. No, that's... that's not what I said.
Text.zh: 什么？不。我……我不是那个意思。

Text.en: Uhtred the Traitor... that's what they called him back home, anyway. I'm his descendant. <continue>Uhtred was High Priest of the Order of the Chalice. They interpreted the stars, among other things.
Text.zh: 叛徒乌崔德……反正老家那边都这么叫他。我是他的后裔。<continue>乌崔德曾是圣杯教团的高阶祭司。他们会解读星象之类的东西。

Text.en: Priest? I thought Kalguurans did not have religion.
Text.zh: 祭司？我还以为卡古兰人不信宗教。

Text.en: Didn't pin you for a religious man, Rog.
Text.zh: 罗格，真没看出来你是信教的人。

Text.en: A Kalguuran religion? Without gods, what do you worship?
Text.zh: 卡古兰的宗教？连神都没有，你们崇拜什么？

Text.en: A priest? Didn't think you had those in Kalguur.
Text.zh: 祭司？我还以为你们卡古兰没有这种东西。

Text.en: Didn't think you lot had priests.
Text.zh: 没想到你们也有祭司。

Text.en: I didn't think you Kalguurans bothered with all that religious carry-on.
Text.zh: 我还以为你们卡古兰人不搞这些宗教玩意儿。

Text.en: Kalguur has priests, but no gods?
Text.zh: 卡古兰有祭司，却没有神？

Text.en: A priest, eh? Didnae think you Kalguurans were fond o' religion.
Text.zh: 祭司，嗯？我还以为你们卡古兰人不信教呢。

Text.en: It's not like religion on Wraeclast. <i>{Knowledge} is what the Order considers 'sacred'. <continue>The Order used scientific instruments to measure and translate celestial movements. <continue>For example, the Order determines when certain stars align, allowing Runesmiths to craft more potent runes.
Text.zh: 跟瓦尔克拉斯那种宗教不一样。<i>{知识}才是教团的“圣物”。<continue>教团使用科学仪器来测量和解读天体的运动。<continue>比如说，教团可以测定出某些星辰何时抵达特定位置，好让符文匠打造出更强大的符文。

Text.en: Runes are based on the Order's reading of the stars? That is amazing.
Text.zh: 符文是基于教团对星象的解读制造出来的？太了不起了。

Text.en: And it's not just runes! Many practices benefit from interpreting the stars.
Text.zh: 不光是符文！很多技术都受益于对星象的解读。

Text.en: My mother told me that ancient Ezomytes could read the night sky.
Text.zh: 我母亲告诉我，古代的艾兹麦人也能解读夜空。

Text.en: Fascinating stuff. I wonder how similar their interpretations were to the Order's...
Text.zh: 真有意思。不知道他们的解读跟教团的会有多像……

Text.en: More delightful Kalguuran magic!
Text.zh: 又是卡古兰那套神奇魔法！

Text.en: Your people's magic is a true wonder.
Text.zh: 你们族人的魔法真是了不起。

Text.en: Never heard of that kind of magic.
Text.zh: 从没听说过那种魔法。

Text.en: That's a new magic, at least.
Text.zh: 起码算是种新魔法。

Text.en: Kalguuran magic is fascinating.
Text.zh: 卡古兰的魔法真是迷人。

Text.en: What a strange brand of magic.
Text.zh: 这魔法还真是奇怪。

Text.en: It's not magic! It's repeatable, and it's real. It's <i>{science.}
Text.zh: 那不是魔法！它是可重复的，是真实的。它是<i>{科学。}

Text.en: The Order requires rigorous discipline. The best became priests of the Order. Beneath them, disciples. As for Uhtred, well he was as devout as they come. <continue>During that ancient expedition, he never touched your gems. He remained pure.
Text.zh: 教团纪律要求极为严格。最优秀的人成为教团的祭司，其下是门徒。至于乌崔德嘛，他可以说是最虔诚的那种人。<continue>在那次古老的远征中，他从没碰过你们的宝石。他一直保持纯洁。

Text.en: Until the very end... when he stole the Triskelion Flame, making him a traitor.
Text.zh: 直到最后一刻……他偷走了三耀之火，成了叛徒。

Text.en: He was labeled a traitor for stealing the Triskelion Flame. Now we know that's not true. <continue>You've wiped clean that stain on my lineage. Thank you.
Text.zh: 他是因为偷走三耀之火才被扣上了叛徒的帽子。现在我们知道那不是真的。<continue>你帮我洗刷了家族血脉上的这个污点。谢谢你。

Text.en: If you'd like, I'll read Uhtred's logbook for you. Yeah! Ahem. <continue>'Vorana. I pray this message finds you. Our time is running out.' <continue>'I know you think me a monster, but we must work together. It is our only hope of survival.' <continue>'I do not delight in treachery. My disciples did what was necessary.' <continue>'Olroth is a fiend. He is pure evil.'<continue>'I only regret that their blades did not finish the job.'
Text.zh: 要不要我给你念一段乌崔德的日志。对！咳咳。<continue>“沃拉娜。希望你能收到这条消息。我们的时间不多了。”<continue>“我知道你觉得我是个怪物，但我们必须合作。这是我们活下去的唯一希望。”<continue>“我并不以背叛为乐。我的门徒只是做了必要之事。”<continue>“欧罗什是个怪物，纯粹的邪恶。”<continue>“我唯一的遗憾，就是他们没能彻底解决他。”

Text.en: What does that mean? He sent men to kill Olroth?
Text.zh: 什么意思？他派人去杀欧罗什？

Text.en: You never mentioned that part! Uhtred stabbed Olroth in the back?
Text.zh: 你之前可没提过这段！乌崔德从背后捅了欧罗什一刀？

Text.en: Did I hear that right? Uhtred's followers tried to kill this Olroth?
Text.zh: 我没听错吧？乌崔德的门徒想杀欧罗什？

Text.en: Your ancestor got his men to do his dirty work for him?
Text.zh: 你的祖先让别人替他干脏活？

Text.en: Am I hearin' you right? His men tried to kill Olroth?
Text.zh: 我没听错吧？他的人要杀欧罗什？

Text.en: Your ancestor's a dirty backstabber, then?
Text.zh: 这么说，你的祖先是个背后捅刀的小人？

Text.en: Pardon? His men attacked Olroth?
Text.zh: 什么？他的人袭击了欧罗什？

Text.en: Wait a minute, lad. Your Uhtred tried to have Olroth killed?
Text.zh: 等等，小子。你家那位乌崔德想弄死欧罗什？

Text.en: Right... Uhtred's disciples stabbed Olroth. He almost died, but he was put in a glass case to preserve him. <continue>That's another reason he's called a traitor, I suppose. Still more to read here... <i>{uhh, where are we now?} Yes.<continue>'Olroth has turned his back on the stars. By day, he leads us. By night, he commands nightbreeds to slaughter us. I know not his reasons, but it is so.' <continue>'I need your help, Vorana. Guide any survivors to me. Do so with discretion. Olroth cannot know what you are doing, or my location.'<continue>'I am within the ancient temple under the earth. You may not trust me... but you know I am right.' <continue>Hmm. Heavy accusations, that. It's hard to say if Uhtred was telling the truth, or trying to shift the blame. <continue>Either way... gives me a lot to think about.
Text.zh: 没错……乌崔德的门徒确实刺伤了欧罗什。他差点死了，但被放进玻璃容器里保命。<continue>我想这也是他被称为叛徒的另一个原因吧。不过这里还有后文……<i>{嗯，读到哪儿了？}对。<continue>“欧罗什已经背弃星辰。白天，他领导我们。夜晚，他指挥黑夜后裔屠杀我们。我不知道原因，但事实就是如此。”<continue>“我需要你的帮助，沃拉娜。把幸存者带来这里找我。行事要谨慎。别让欧罗什知道你的行动，也别让他知道我在哪儿。”<continue>“我在地下那座远古神庙里。你也许不信任我……但你知道我是对的。”<continue>嗯。很严重的指控啊。很难说乌崔德是在说实话，还是在推卸责任。<continue>不管怎样……这事值得我好好想一想。

Text.en: Uhtred's name is cleared, at least of stealing the Flame. I have you to thank for that. <continue>But after seeing the temple, and his body... I still have questions about his fate. <continue>Why was Uhtred undead, if he never touched the forbidden gems?
Text.zh: 乌崔德至少洗清了偷三耀之火的罪名，这还得谢谢你。<continue>但看到那座神庙和他的遗体之后……我对他的命运仍然有疑问。<continue>要是乌崔德没用过那些禁忌宝石，他为什么会变成亡灵？

Text.en: Gems are not the only source of Corruption. It may have affected him over time.
Text.zh: 宝石不是腐化的唯一来源。也许他是时间长了受到了影响。

Text.en: You think it's just because of <i>{exposure?} He was alive on this continent for less time than <i>{I've} been here.
Text.zh: 你觉得就……因为<i>{暴露}在腐化中？可他在这片大陆待的时间还没有<i>{我}长。

Text.en: When you put it that way, it does seem strange.
Text.zh: 你这么一说，确实有点不对劲。

Text.en: In my expert opinion, Uhtred was not <i>{really} undead.
Text.zh: 以我的专业判断，乌崔德其实不算是<i>{真正的}亡灵。

Text.en: Wh... W-what do you mean?
Text.zh: 什……什么意思？

Text.en: He seemed more... <i>{transformed.} Kept muttering about the 'beyond' and the stars.
Text.zh: 我觉得他更像是<i>{被转化了。}一直嘟囔着什么“彼岸”，还有星辰。

Text.en: Well, he did study the stars, but...
Text.zh: 嗯，他确实研究星辰，但是……

Text.en: There are other ways to avoid death. The djinn are one example.
Text.zh: 也有其他方式可以逃避死亡，比如说巨灵。

Text.en: Do you think that's what happened? Uhtred was turned into a djinn?
Text.zh: 你觉得是那样吗？乌崔德变成了巨灵？

Text.en: It is possible... but unlikely. He did not resemble any djinn I've seen. And I saw no <i>{barya.}
Text.zh: 有可能……但不太像。他跟我见过的任何巨灵都不一样。而且我没看到什么<i>{硬币。}

Text.en: I can't read them, but I saw glowing runes carved into his limbs.
Text.zh: 我看不懂那些符文，但我看到他四肢上刻着发光的符文。

Text.en: I saw those as well. It's not a practice of the Order, or any Kalguurans... that I know of.
Text.zh: 我也看到了。据我所知，这不是教团或是卡古兰人的常规做法。

Text.en: Perhaps your 'High Priest' messed with more nefarious magics, then?
Text.zh: 那也许你的“高阶祭司”沾染了更邪恶的魔法？

Text.en: He <i>{were} different than the others. He came out of that strange mirror.
Text.zh: 他<i>{确实}跟其他亡灵不一样。他是从那面奇怪的镜子里出来的。

Text.en: How do you mean? He was <i>{behind} the mirror? But it was intact?
Text.zh: 什么意思？他在镜子<i>{后面}？但镜子是完好的啊？

Text.en: He came through it like it were liquid. It were a frightful sight. <continue>Beyond that, he seemed as undead as the rest.
Text.zh: 他像穿过水一样从镜子里走出来的，挺吓人的。<continue>除此之外，他跟其他亡灵看起来没什么两样。

Text.en: How can you be so sure he never used gems?
Text.zh: 你怎么能肯定他没用过宝石？

Text.en: Why spare a traitor's reputation by not mentioning that? And I saw no gems on him.
Text.zh: 大家没必要替一个叛徒遮掩吧？而且我在他身上没看到宝石。

Text.en: The man had other secrets. Maybe he just hid the gems well.
Text.zh: 那人肯定还有别的秘密，说不定他把宝石藏起来了。

Text.en: It is hard to say. There are many mysterious forces on Wraeclast.
Text.zh: 很难说。瓦尔克拉斯充满各种神秘力量。

Text.en: You're not wrong, friend. But Uhtred made a point to <i>{not} use them.
Text.zh: 你说得没错，朋友。但乌崔德刻意强调自己<i>{不用}那些东西。

Text.en: He used this mirror. As we fought, he travelled through it like a veil.
Text.zh: 他用了这面镜子。我们打斗的时候，他像穿帘子一样来回穿梭。

Text.en: Really? Maybe this mirror... changed him? But how?
Text.zh: 真的吗？也许这面镜子……改变了他？但怎么会呢？

Text.en: That place is powerful... terribly so. We both felt that.
Text.zh: 这地方的力量……太强了，我们都感觉得到。

Text.en: That is true. Do you think it turned him into... a monster?
Text.zh: 没错。你觉得是它把他变成了……怪物？

Text.en: Could be he died, and the temple is keeping his spirit there... why, I dinnae know.
Text.zh: 可能他死了，而这座神庙将他的灵魂困在了这里……至于为什么，我也不知道。

Text.en: There must be <i>{something} we're missing. <continue>New dangers are discovered on this damn continent every day... <continue>Makes me miss Middengard! Heh. Well, almost.
Text.zh: 肯定<i>{还有什么}我们没搞明白。<continue>在这个鬼地方，每天都有新危险冒出来……<continue>我真是想念米登加德！嗯，也没那么想。

Text.en: A comet that size... summoned by Uhtred himself. It's hard to believe.
Text.zh: 那么大一颗彗星……是乌崔德亲自召唤过来的。真是难以置信。

Text.en: It was an awe-inspiring sight.
Text.zh: 那场景确实很震撼。

Text.en: Well, believe it. I saw it with my own eyes.
Text.zh: 信不信由你，我亲眼所见。

Text.en: It was certainly... unexpected.
Text.zh: 这确实……出乎意料。

Text.en: Not sure I <i>{would} believe it, if I hadn't seen it for myself.
Text.zh: 要不是亲眼看见，我还真不确定自己<i>{会不会}相信。

Text.en: Never seen anythin' like it.
Text.zh: 从没见过那种景象。

Text.en: I'm still not sure I believe it and I <i>{saw} the bloody thing.
Text.zh: 我亲眼<i>{看见了}都还有点不敢信。

Text.en: It was a sight to behold.
Text.zh: 那场面真是壮观。

Text.en: Aye, lad. Damn near took my breath away.
Text.zh: 是啊，小子。差点让我喘不过气来。

Text.en: I've read some... dubious accounts, describing an ancient 'starlit rite' of the Order. I suppose some of it was true. <continue>Uhtred was the first High Priest to perform the rite. He and his disciples drank liquid Verisium, to absorb its resonance. <continue>Some claimed they could pull Verisium from the firmament... others claimed they could speak with the stars. It all seemed... well, quite mad. <continue>The modern Order can track Verisium and other celestial bodies, but they work in laboratories... as far as I know. <continue>So how did he do it? I didn't see any equipment... just that strange mirror.
Text.zh: 我读过一些……不太靠谱的记载，说教团有一种古老的“星光仪式”。现在看来可能不全是胡说。<continue>乌崔德是第一位举行该仪式的高阶祭司。他和门徒饮用了液态维金，让身体与其产生共振。<continue>有些人声称他们能从天空中把维金拉下来……另一些人声称他们能与星辰对话。这一切听起来……嗯，相当疯狂。<continue>现代的教团能够追踪维金和其他天体，但他们是在实验室里进行追踪……据我所知是这样。<continue>那他到底是怎么做到的？我没看到任何设备……只有那面奇怪的镜子。

Text.en: The mirror... do you know anything about it?
Text.zh: 关于那面镜子……你知道点什么吗？

Text.en: You didn't recognise that mirror? I assumed it was some Order artefact.
Text.zh: 你不认得那面镜子？我还以为是什么教团的古物呢。

Text.en: The mirror that Uhtred used – does that belong to the Order?
Text.zh: 乌崔德用的那面镜子属于教团吗？

Text.en: Is that mirror we found somehow related to the Order?
Text.zh: 我们发现的那面镜子，跟教团有什么关联吗？

Text.en: Reckon this mirror has summat to do with the Order?
Text.zh: 你觉得那面镜子和教团有关系吗？

Text.en: This mirror thing... you think it's related to the Order?
Text.zh: 这面镜子……你觉得跟教团有关吗？

Text.en: What do you know of the mirror we found?
Text.zh: 你对我们找到的那面镜子有什么了解？

Text.en: This mirror we found... what is it?
Text.zh: 我们发现的那面镜子……到底是什么？

Text.en: The Order of the Chalice has some covert practices. High Priests and disciples swear lifelong oaths of secrecy. <continue>But I'm pretty sure their celestial instruments do not include ancient underground mirrors.<continue>Besides, that mirror must have been there long before Uhtred was. It looked like the temple was built around it.
Text.zh: 圣杯教团确实有一些隐秘的活动。高阶祭司和门徒都要发誓终身保密。<continue>但我很确定，他们用的那些天文仪器里，不包括埋在地下的古老镜子。<continue>再说了，那面镜子明显在乌崔德来之前就存在很久了。看起来整座神庙都是围绕它建起来的。

Text.en: So what do <i>{you} think happened?
Text.zh: 那<i>{你}觉得是怎么回事？

Text.en: Uhtred came out of the mirror to fight me, so he was quite familiar with it.
Text.zh: 乌崔德从镜子里钻出来跟我打架，所以他肯定很熟悉这东西。

Text.en: You must have <i>{some} idea of what it is.
Text.zh: 你总该<i>{有点}头绪，知道它是什么吧。

Text.en: Then... what <i>{is} it?
Text.zh: 那……它到底<i>{是}什么？

Text.en: If it ain't Uhtred's... then what's that mean?
Text.zh: 如果不是乌崔德的……那又意味着什么？

Text.en: Then why was it there? And why was he?
Text.zh: 那它为什么在那里？他又为什么在那里？

Text.en: Well then what do you think? It can't have just been there by coincidence.
Text.zh: 那你觉得呢？总不能只是碰巧出现在那里吧。

Text.en: Actually, I think it's Uhtred who wasn't there by coincidence.
Text.zh: 实际上，我觉得并非碰巧出现在那里的，反而是乌崔德。

Text.en: In that case... might he have sought the mirror out?
Text.zh: 那样的话……他是不是冲着这面镜子去的？

Text.en: Certainly seems that way, doesn't it?
Text.zh: 看起来确实是这样，不是吗？

Text.en: If Uhtred was able to pull down Verisium himself, he could do that anywhere. But he is here... <continue>He chose to come here, at the end of that first expedition. He needed the mirror... for something else. <continue>A mirror, considered ancient even when Uhtred was alive, almost two thousand years ago...
Text.zh: 如果乌崔德能凭一己之力把维金拉下来，那他随便在哪儿都能做这事。但他偏偏要在这里……<continue>在第一次远征的最后，他特意选择来到这里。他需要这面镜子……用来做别的事情。<continue>而这面镜子，即使在大约两千年前乌崔德活着的时候，就已经被视为远古之物了……

Text.en: Amazing! We're really making history here.<continue>This may be the first time Ezomyte and Kalguuran runesmiths have crafted together! <continue>And to remake the Triskelion Flame, the most storied runecraft of Kalguur... It's... it's inspiring. <continue>The Flame reforged... a symbol of our <i>{own} reformation.
Text.zh: 太棒了！我们真的在创造历史。<continue>这可能是艾兹麦和卡古兰的符文匠首次携手合作！<continue>而且是要重铸三耀之火，卡古兰最富传奇色彩的符文造物……真是……太振奋人心了。<continue>重铸三耀之火……象征着我们<i>{自己的}重生。

Text.en: Are you alright, Rog?
Text.zh: 你没事吧，罗格？

Text.en: Oh, no... You're not about to start crying, are you?
Text.zh: 哦，不……你该不会要哭了吧？

Text.en: It is quite moving. Do you need a moment?
Text.zh: 真是感人。你需要缓一缓吗？

Text.en: I think that's my cue to get going...
Text.zh: 看来我该走了……

Text.en: Would you, uh... prefer if I left you alone?
Text.zh: 要不我先回避一下，让你自己待会儿？

Text.en: You alright, mate?
Text.zh: 你没事吧，哥们儿？

Text.en: Can I fetch you something?
Text.zh: 要不要我给你拿张纸巾？

Text.en: Ach... mayhap there's someone else you'd rather have this conversation with...
Text.zh: 啊……也许你更想跟别人聊这个……

Text.en: I'm fine! Yeah, nah, I'm good. I— I haven't felt this good in a while, actually.
Text.zh: 我没事！嗯，没事，我好得很。我——我已经很久没这么痛快过了。

Text.en: Not so fast. I know who <i>{will} have an idea. Take it to Rhodri. He may know where this leads.
Text.zh: 别急。我知道谁<i>{会}有主意。拿去给罗德里看看，他也许知道这里通往什么地方。

Text.en: What've you got there, eh? Let's take a look.
Text.zh: 你这拿的是什么？来，我看看。

Text.en: Hmm... well, if that's Dead Man's Reef... <continue>Then that must be Sorrow's Point. And that'll mark the Dreadmire Current. <continue>Yeah. I know where this leads.
Text.zh: 嗯……如果这是死人礁…… <continue>那这肯定就是悲恸角了。那边则是恐沼洋流。对，我知道这里通往什么地方。

Text.en: You made that sound easy, Rhodri.
Text.zh: 罗德里，你说得像这事很简单似的。

Text.en: So there is something more to you than that inedible soup you make...
Text.zh: 看来你不只会熬那些没法下咽的汤……

Text.en: You are gifted, Seamaster Rhodri.
Text.zh: 你真有一套，海上霸主罗德里。

Text.en: Nice work.
Text.zh: 干得漂亮。

Text.en: It's a mystery to me how you did that so fast.
Text.zh: 我真搞不懂你怎么能这么快就看明白了。

Text.en: Good job, mate.
Text.zh: 好样的，哥们儿。

Text.en: You made short work of that. Excellent.
Text.zh: 这么快就搞定了，厉害。

Text.en: Rhodri... you're sharper than you first appear.
Text.zh: 罗德里……你可比看上去聪明多了。

Text.en: Not <i>{just} the ship's cook, after all, eh? <continue>We can head there whenever you like. What do you say?
Text.zh: 毕竟我可不<i>{只是}船上的厨子，对吧？<continue>我们随时可以出发。你怎么说？

Text.en: Well, here we are then. Got ya here in one piece, no less. <continue>I'll have you know, we crossed some pretty rugged sea to get here... <continue>So whatever you find, you better share some of it with me... alright?
Text.zh: 好了，我们到了。还把你们全须全尾带到了。<continue>我跟你说，这一路上的海况可真是风高浪急……<continue>所以你们不管找到什么宝贝，最好也分我一点……行吧？

Text.en: Didn't know we were coming out here to blow the island to smithereens. But hey... got the old heart racin'. <continue>I'll let you decide if you're interested in sharing a little of what you've unearthed... if you catch me drift.
Text.zh: 没想到咱们出来这一趟，是要把整座岛都给炸上天。不过嘿……倒也挺刺激的。<continue>至于你要不要分我点你挖出来的东西……你懂我意思吧。

Text.en: Matiki! You're alive! Makoru would be pleased to see it.
Text.zh: 玛提吉！你还活着！马克鲁见到你一定很高兴。

Text.en: Yes. They told me I was too old to keep taking out my canoe... Maybe they were right.
Text.zh: 对，他们说我太老了，不该总划着独木舟出海……也许他们说得对。

Text.en: From canoe to a cage. What happened out there?
Text.zh: 从泛舟到被囚，这到底怎么回事？

Text.en: You went out on a canoe... and ended up <i>{there}?
Text.zh: 你划着小舟出海……结果跑到<i>{那种地方}去了？

Text.en: Tell us, Matiki... how would you find yourself caged?
Text.zh: 告诉我们，玛提吉……你怎么会被关在笼子里？

Text.en: From canoeing to trapped in a cage... Explain yourself.
Text.zh: 从划独木舟到被困笼中……你具体讲讲吧。

Text.en: Sounds like you had a rough day at sea.
Text.zh: 听起来你在海上过得可不怎么顺。

Text.en: [NOAUDIO] Matiki... how'd you end up caged like that?
Text.zh: 玛提吉……你怎么会落到被人关笼子的地步？

Text.en: Went out for a nice row, then found yourself caged up, right?
Text.zh: 本想惬意地划个船，结果却成了阶下囚，对吧？

Text.en: Rowed your canoe right into a cage, did ye?
Text.zh: 划着独木舟自投罗网，对吧？

Text.en: It is quite the tale... <continue>... and I was weak. I admit. <continue>When the fog swept over the water... I should've known what I saw wasn't real! <continue>But there she was. My Rakana. Sweet and strong as the day we met. <continue>She plowed on through the fog. And I chased her like she was alive! Like we were young again. <continue>Then... crash! Next thing I know... I'm wet as anything and preparing to be bird food!
Text.zh: 这说来话长了……<continue>……我那时很脆弱，我承认这一点。<continue>当迷雾笼罩海面时……我本该知道眼前所见并非真实！<continue>但她就在那里，我的拉卡娜。美丽而坚毅，如同我们初遇时一般。<continue>她穿透迷雾而来。而我如痴如醉地追随着她，仿佛她依然在世！仿佛我们重回青春年华。<continue>然后……轰然一击！等我回过神来……全身湿透，简直像是专程来给海鸟加餐的！

Text.en: It is quite the tale... <continue>... and I was weak. I admit. <continue>When the fog swept over the water... I should've known what I saw wasn't real! <continue>But there she was. My Rakana. Sweet and strong as the day we met. <continue>She plowed on through the fog. And I chased her like she was alive! Like we were young again. <continue>Then... crash! Next thing I know... I'm wet as anything and cooped up in that bloody cage.
Text.zh: 这说来话长了……<continue>……我那时很脆弱，我承认这一点。<continue>当迷雾笼罩海面时……我本该知道眼前所见并非真实！<continue>但她就在那里，我的拉卡娜。美丽而坚毅，如同我们初遇时一般。<continue>她穿透迷雾而来。而我如痴如醉地追随着她，仿佛她依然在世！仿佛我们重回青春年华。<continue>然后……轰然一击！等我回过神来……全身湿透，被困在那可恶的牢笼之中。

Text.en: It is quite the tale... <continue>... and I was weak. I admit. <continue>When the fog swept over the water... I should've known what I saw wasn't real! <continue>But there she was. My Rakana. Sweet and strong as the day we met. <continue>She plowed on through the fog. And I chased her like she was alive! Like we were young again. <continue>Then... crash! Next thing I know... I'm inside that damn prison counting my blessings!
Text.zh: 这说来话长了……<continue>……我那时很脆弱，我承认这一点。<continue>当迷雾笼罩海面时……我本该知道眼前所见并非真实！<continue>但她就在那里，我的拉卡娜。美丽而坚毅，如同我们初遇时一般。<continue>她穿透迷雾而来。而我如痴如醉地追随着她，仿佛她依然在世！仿佛我们重回青春年华。<continue>然后……轰然一击！等我回过神来……我已被关在那该死的牢笼里，庆幸自己捡回条命！

Text.en: It is quite the tale... <continue>... and I was weak. I admit. <continue>When the fog swept over the water... I should've known what I saw wasn't real! <continue>But there she was. My Rakana. Sweet and strong as the day we met. <continue>She plowed on through the fog. And I chased her like she was alive! Like we were young again. <continue>Then... crash! Next thing I know... I'm wet as anything and held prisoner in that bloody cave. <continue>And let me tell you... it was hard to keep my eyes to myself, heh.
Text.zh: 这说来话长了……<continue>……我那时很脆弱，我承认这一点。<continue>当迷雾笼罩海面时……我本该知道眼前所见并非真实！<continue>但她就在那里，我的拉卡娜。美丽而坚毅，如同我们初遇时一般。<continue>她穿透迷雾而来。而我如痴如醉地追随着她，仿佛她依然在世！仿佛我们重回青春年华。<continue>然后……轰然一击！等我回过神来……全身湿透，被困在那可恶的洞里。<continue>说实话……在那种地方管住自己的眼睛可真不容易，呵呵。

Text.en: Well, it's a good thing our friend here got to ya when they did.
Text.zh: 幸好我们这位朋友及时找到了你。

Text.en: Yes! And about that... <continue>There is the small matter of what I've promised our hero here...
Text.zh: 是的！说到这个……<continue>关于我向我们的英雄许下的承诺，还有个小小的麻烦……

Text.en: Oh dear. What's that then?
Text.zh: 哎呀，什么麻烦？

Text.en: There's a place I want to take our friend, when they have the time.
Text.zh: 有个地方，等我们的朋友有空时，我想带他们去。

Text.en: Yes... there it is. The Eye of Hinekora. Gateway... to the Halls of the Dead!
Text.zh: 是的……就在那里。辛格拉之眼。通往……亡者之境的门扉！

Text.en: You want us and our merry crew to sail to the Karui's most sacred place? You've lost it, mate.
Text.zh: 你居然想让我们这一船人航行到卡鲁最为神圣的圣地？你疯了吧，哥们儿。

Text.en: Trust me. My hero here... is special. They deserve a chance to prove themselves to our ancestors.
Text.zh: 相信我。我们的英雄……与众不同。他们理应有机会向我们的先祖证明自己。

Text.en: Well, it's your head if Tavakai finds out. I just drive the boat...
Text.zh: 要是塔瓦凯知道了这事，他会气疯的……

Text.en: Ah. Well, perhaps in this case it's best for him to only hear what he needs to hear.
Text.zh: 唉。或许在这种情况下，最好只让他知道该知道的事情。

Text.en: Fine. But afterwards... I'm sailing you back to Ngakanu.
Text.zh: 好吧，不过事后我得带你回纳卡努。

Text.en: Yes, yes... that's fine.
Text.zh: 好的，好的……没问题。

Text.en: This logbook contains all the clues I've gathered about Gwennen's location.
Text.zh: 这本航海日志里记录了我收集到的所有关于桂温妮下落的线索。

Text.en: We just need to chart a course.
Text.zh: 我们只需要规划一条航线。

Text.en: Gwennen's out there. I know it. Now... all that's left is to set sail!
Text.zh: 桂温妮肯定在那里。我很确定。现在……只剩扬帆起航了！

Text.en: It's a miracle any of us made it out of Oriath alive...
Text.zh: 我们这些人还能活着离开奥瑞亚，简直是奇迹……

Text.en: Bit of a pickle we're in, eh? Things in Wraeclast only ever go from bad to worse. <continue>I'll never forget the sight of the Cataclysm.
Text.zh: 现在的处境有点棘手，是吧？瓦尔克拉斯的情况永远只会从糟糕变得……更糟。<continue>我永远都忘不了那场灾变的景象。

Text.en: How did the ship survive the blast?
Text.zh: 那艘船究竟是怎么经受得住爆炸的？

Text.en: What in the world held your ship together?
Text.zh: 你的船究竟是怎么撑住没散架的？

Text.en: I am pleased to see the Aurora May lives on.
Text.zh: 看到极光五月号还能继续航行，我真的很高兴。

Text.en: Your ship still floats. That's truly something.
Text.zh: 你的船居然还浮着，这可太离谱了。

Text.en: Glad to see your ship is still seaworthy.
Text.zh: 很高兴看到你的船还能出海。

Text.en: You'll have to tell me how you lot made it out sometime.
Text.zh: 有空你得跟我讲讲你们是怎么活下来的。

Text.en: You yet again prove your prowess on the sea, Makoru.
Text.zh: 马克鲁，你又一次证明了你在海上的本事。

Text.en: Yer mastery of the water is unmatched, dear Makoru.
Text.zh: 你对海洋的掌控无人能及，亲爱的马克鲁。

Text.en: The Aurora May has overcome many obstacles in her time. But yes... that <i>{was} a close one.
Text.zh: 极光五月号这些年经历过不少风浪。但没错……那次<i>{确实}挺悬的。

Text.en: A storm or two, sure... but a bloody Cataclysm? <continue>I'd say we're damn lucky to be alive.
Text.zh: 经历一两次风暴倒没什么……可该死的灾变？<continue>我觉得我们能活下来简直是走了狗屎运。

Text.en: It's a right shame to see Kingsmarch like this. The folk here didn't deserve such a fate.
Text.zh: 看到君锋镇变成这样，真让人心痛。这里的人不该落得这种下场。

Text.en: I have a feeling they'll be alright. They are strong. They'll persevere and move forward.
Text.zh: 我总觉得他们会挺过去的。他们很坚强，会咬牙撑下去，继续往前走。

Text.en: What's become of your tribe, Makoru?
Text.zh: 马克鲁，你的部落怎么样了？

Text.en: So... what's left of your tribe at this point, Makoru?
Text.zh: 所以……你们部落到现在还剩下多少人？

Text.en: Makoru, I must ask... are your people safe?
Text.zh: 马克鲁，我得问一句……你的人还活着吗？

Text.en: And what of your own people?
Text.zh: 那你自己的人呢？

Text.en: And what about your tribe, Makoru?
Text.zh: 马克鲁，你的部落怎么样了？

Text.en: And what about your tribe, Captain?
Text.zh: 船长，你的部落怎么样了？

Text.en: I am curious. What became of your tribe, Makoru?
Text.zh: 我很好奇，马克鲁，你的部落后来怎样了？

Text.en: And what of your kin, Makoru?
Text.zh: 马克鲁，你的族人呢？

Text.en: We've lost many brave warriors. Our time of mourning has begun. But... we know the tide doesn't stop to wait for us.
Text.zh: 我们失去了很多勇猛的战士。哀悼的时刻已经到来。但我们知道……潮水不会停下来等我们。

Text.en: No... there's no denying. The Karui spirit is stronger than any of us.
Text.zh: 没错，毋庸置疑。卡鲁人的意志比我们任何人都要坚韧。

Text.en: You don't know her? <continue>Gwennen is one of the Kalguurans here. She's an adventurer and a woman after my own heart. <continue>Since we got back, we've been chasing the wind, trying our best to find anyone that may have been displaced since the Cataclysm. <continue>A particular group of islands stuck out to me. <continue>On the horizon I saw smoke, but by the time we arrived, it was gone.
Text.zh: 你不知道她？<continue>桂温妮是这里的一个卡古兰人。她是个冒险家，也是个很对我脾气的女人。<continue>自从我们回来后，就一直在四处奔波，尽最大努力寻找在灾变后流离失所的人。<continue>有一片群岛引起了我的注意。<continue>我在地平线上看到了烟雾，但等我们赶到时，烟雾已经散了。

Text.en: Why did you think it was Gwennen?
Text.zh: 你为什么觉得那是桂温妮？

Text.en: Why would you suspect this is where lonesome Gwennen would be?
Text.zh: 你为什么觉得孤身一人的桂温妮会去那地方？

Text.en: And somehow you thought this would belong to Gwennen?
Text.zh: 那你怎么会觉得这是桂温妮留下的线索？

Text.en: Why would you assume it was Gwennen?
Text.zh: 可你为什么觉得那是桂温妮呢？

Text.en: But why would you think that was Gwennen?
Text.zh: 但你为什么会觉得那是桂温妮？

Text.en: Something doesn't add up. Why would you think it was her out there?
Text.zh: 有些地方说不通。你为什么会觉得她在那儿？

Text.en: You're not being entirely forthcoming here.
Text.zh: 你话没说全吧。

Text.en: Speak plain, lass. What aren't you telling me?
Text.zh: 直说吧，姑娘。你到底隐瞒了什么？

Text.en: Well, I <i>{was} aware of some of her previous excursions... in the general area.
Text.zh: 嗯，我<i>{确实}知道她之前去过……那一片。

Text.en: So you knew where she was all along?
Text.zh: 所以你一开始就知道她在哪儿？

Text.en: So you knew where she was all along?
Text.zh: 所以你一开始就知道她在哪儿？

Text.en: So you knew where she was all along?
Text.zh: 所以你一开始就知道她在哪儿？

Text.en: So you knew where she was all along?
Text.zh: 所以你一开始就知道她在哪儿？

Text.en: So you knew where she was all along?
Text.zh: 所以你一开始就知道她在哪儿？

Text.en: So you knew where she was all along?
Text.zh: 所以你一开始就知道她在哪儿？

Text.en: So you knew where she was all along?
Text.zh: 所以你一开始就知道她在哪儿？

Text.en: So you knew where she was all along?
Text.zh: 所以你一开始就知道她在哪儿？

Text.en: Not exactly...
Text.zh: 也不算吧……

Text.en: I think the budgie's outta the barrel at this point, Makoru...
Text.zh: 马克鲁，我看这纸里已经包不住火了……

Text.en: Fine, fine. Yes. I was assisting her here and there. Supplies. Transport. That sort of thing. <continue>But one night, she never showed up at our meeting place. <continue>She made me promise, though - no matter what, don't get involved. She's very protective.
Text.zh: 好吧，好吧。是的。我一直时不时帮衬她一下。给点补给、运送东西什么的。<continue>可有一天晚上……她没来我们约好的地点。<continue>但她让我保证，无论发生什么都别插手。她这个人老想着保护别人。

Text.en: The last thing she wanted was the King's Hand catching wind of any of it.
Text.zh: 她最不想的，就是被国王之手察觉到这件事。

Text.en: She's a true friend. And so I owe it to her, to seek her out and make sure she's safe.
Text.zh: 她这人很重情义。所以我必须找到她，确保她平安无事，这是我欠她的。

Text.en: So tell me... have you heard of the Kalguurans' plans?
Text.zh: 说起来……你听说过卡古兰人的计划吗？

Text.en: Well, you must have heard what the Kalguurans are plotting...
Text.zh: 你肯定已经听说卡古兰人在搞什么大事了吧……

Text.en: Makoru, have you heard what the Kalguurans plan to do?
Text.zh: 马克鲁，你听说卡古兰人打算做什么了吗？

Text.en: Have you heard of the Kalguurans' rebellion plans?
Text.zh: 你听说过卡古兰人的叛乱计划吗？

Text.en: Have you heard the Kalguurans' plan to take the fight to their king?
Text.zh: 你听说卡古兰人打算跟他们的国王狠狠干一架了吗？

Text.en: Have you heard the latest from the Kalguurans? There's a fight I wanna see.
Text.zh: 你听说卡古兰人的最新消息了吗？我倒想见识一下这场较量。

Text.en: Have you heard what the Kalguurans are planning?
Text.zh: 你听说卡古兰人在筹划什么了吗？

Text.en: Have you caught wind of the Kalguurans' intentions?
Text.zh: 你听说卡古兰人的打算了吗？

Text.en: I have, yes.
Text.zh: 听说了，没错。

Text.en: And...
Text.zh: 然后呢？

Text.en: I answered the question.
Text.zh: 我已经回答了你的问题。

Text.en: You know there's more to the answer.
Text.zh: 你知道我问的不止这个。

Text.en: Well, not that you asked but... yes. I'll be sailing them home.
Text.zh: 好吧……虽然你没问，不过……对，我会把他们送回家。

Text.en: You're joining their fight?
Text.zh: 你要加入他们的战斗？

Text.en: You'd risk your life... for <i>{them}?
Text.zh: 你愿意拿命去冒险……为了<i>{他们}？

Text.en: For those who are not your own? You would risk it all to help them?
Text.zh: 为了那些不是你同胞的人？你愿意为了他们赌上一切？

Text.en: So you are joining their rebellion?
Text.zh: 所以你要加入他们的叛乱？

Text.en: So you'll join them in their fight?
Text.zh: 所以你会跟他们一起战斗？

Text.en: Ah! So you're onboard with their revolution?
Text.zh: 啊！所以你支持他们革命？

Text.en: So... you are joining them in their fight.
Text.zh: 所以……你是要加入他们的战斗。

Text.en: Careful, lass... It is not your war.
Text.zh: 小心点，姑娘……这场战争与你无关！

Text.en: I suppose you could think of it that way. But really, I see it as helping my friends. And... <continue>Adventure is addictive.
Text.zh: 你要这么想也行。但在我看来，我只是在帮朋友。而且……<continue>冒险这东西会上瘾。

Text.en: Makoru is the best Captain this port has ever seen! That much is clear. <continue>Just imagine... sailing across the sea to Middengard, the Triskelion Flame brandished upon the Aurora May's mast... well... <continue>There is no better person to have at the helm.
Text.zh: 马克鲁是这个港口有史以来最好的船长！这谁都看得出来。<continue>你想想看……我们横渡大洋直奔米登加德，极光五月号的桅杆上高高飘扬着三耀之火……嗯……<continue>再没有人比她更适合掌舵的人了。

Text.en: That's right. Foes call me Fleabag. Friends call me Finnegan. Most call me both. <continue>Well, I found meself out here after a rather... long job. Tired, I was. All alone. But alone no longer it seems... <continue>Yes, let's see what magic we can make on the open market, eh? <continue>I won't ask how you got your coin... if you don't ask how I move your goods... hehehe!
Text.zh: 没错。敌人叫我“跳蚤”，朋友叫我芬尼根。大多数人两个都叫。<continue>我干了一票……挺漫长的活儿，之后就跑到这儿来了。那时候多累啊，又是孤零零一个人。但看来现在不是一个人了……<continue>咱看看在这自由市场上能搞出点什么名堂，嗯？<continue>我不问你钱是怎么来的……你也别问我货是怎么出手的……嘿嘿嘿！

Text.en: You want to hear me tale, eh? I'm... shocked. You seemed more a... strictly business type. <continue>So imagine, there I was... prime of me life... full of confidence... full of food even... and best of all: full of dreams. <continue>The lass I'd me eye on for a long while, stood out in the cold of night, waiting for her carriage home. <continue>It struck old Finnegan. Offer the lady your coat. Warm her, as she's warmed your heart. <continue>I moved over to her. She looked at me and... fear caught her lip. It quivered, but not from the cold. <continue>It struck me like a bolt in the chest. What was I to do? <continue>Did she thought me a monster? I only wanted to offer her my coat...  <continue>Suddenly... I were tossed aside. And upon her... was a fearsome wolf of white, ripping and tearing at her. <continue>I rejoiced at the fool I was. It were not me she feared! It were the damn lunging beast behind me! Hahaha! <continue>The poor lass... within an instant, she were a jewel no longer. I mean, seriously. She were right bloody massacred. Unsightly even. <continue>But now, hear this... the moral of me story... <continue>Oooo... hang on now... <continue>Wait... what was I on about?
Text.zh: 你想听我的故事，是吧？我……还挺意外。你看起来更像是……那种只谈生意的人。<continue>那你想象一下啊，我那时候……风华正茂，浑身是胆，肚子也填得满满的……最棒的是，满怀梦想。<continue>我盯了好久的那个姑娘，大冷天的夜里站在外面，等马车接她回家。<continue>老芬尼根我心里一动，把外套给人家女士披上，给她暖和暖和，毕竟她也暖了你的心。<continue>我挪到她跟前。她看了我一眼。然后……恐惧攫住了她的嘴唇。她嘴唇发抖，可不是因为天冷。<continue>那一刻我整个人像被雷劈了一样。我该怎么办？<continue>她是不是觉得我是个怪物？我只是想把外套给她啊……<continue>突然……我被撞开了。而她身上……扑着一头凶恶的白狼，撕咬不止。<continue>我笑骂自己是个傻瓜。她怕的不是我！是我背后那头畜生！哈哈哈！<continue>可怜的姑娘……一眨眼的功夫，她就不再是珍宝了。我是说真的，她被撕得血肉模糊，简直没法看。<continue>不过你听好了……我故事的道理是……<continue>哦……等一下…… <continue>等等……我刚才说到哪儿了？

Text.en: Thank you. Those monsters were relentless! <continue>Would you do me a favour? Can you check those explosives for me?
Text.zh: 谢谢你！那些怪物简直没完没了！<continue>你能帮我个忙吗？替我去检查一下那些炸药？

Text.en: I could try.
Text.zh: 我可以试试。

Text.en: I love a domineering woman. Direct me to them!
Text.zh: 我就喜欢强势的女人。告诉我它们在哪儿！

Text.en: I'll do what I can.
Text.zh: 我尽力。

Text.en: Wonderful! I hope the rain didn't affect them.
Text.zh: 太好了！但愿雨水没把它们弄坏。

Text.en: Do you often make demands of people you've just met?
Text.zh: 你经常让刚认识的人帮你干活吗？

Text.en: Is that wise? Offering explosives to strangers?
Text.zh: 这样明智吗？把炸药交给陌生人？

Text.en: I am no pet to be commanded.
Text.zh: 我又不是你的宠物，别来命令我。

Text.en: Mind tellin' me who you are first?
Text.zh: 你能先告诉我你是谁吗？

Text.en: Are ye daft? Do I look like I know anythin' about explosives?
Text.zh: 你疯了吗？我看起来像是懂炸药的人吗？

Text.en: Oh! Where are my manners? I am Gwennen, and I am about to make a momentous discovery!
Text.zh: 哎呀！真是失礼了。我是桂温妮，而且我马上就要有划时代的发现了！

Text.en: Gwennen - yes. You are the one the Kalguurans seek.
Text.zh: 桂温妮——没错。你就是卡古兰人在找的那个人。

Text.en: Hold a moment... I know you. You're the one they're looking for!
Text.zh: 等一下……我认得你。你就是他们在找的人！

Text.en: Oh... really? May I ask who, specifically?
Text.zh: 哦……真的吗？我能问一下，具体是谁在找我吗？

Text.en: The small one, the big one, and the cheery one.
Text.zh: 那个小个子、那个大个子，还有那个乐呵呵的家伙。

Text.en: The Kalguuran folk, back in Kingsmarch.
Text.zh: 君锋镇的那些卡古兰人。

Text.en: I thought you might be. It is nice to meet you, Gwennen. I have been asked to find you.
Text.zh: 我就知道是你。很高兴见到你，桂温妮。有人托我来找你。

Text.en: Guess I can't leave you behind then, Gwenny! Your friends sent me to find you.
Text.zh: 看来我是没法把你丢下了，桂温妮！你的朋友们派我来找你。

Text.en: Oh... <i>{you're} Gwennen. Makoru is a skilled tracker. We're here to bring you home.
Text.zh: 哦……<i>{你}就是桂温妮。马克鲁追踪人真有一套。我们是来接你回家的。

Text.en: What luck! I've been looking for ya. The folk in Kingsmarch sent me to retrieve ya.
Text.zh: 真巧！我一直在找你。君锋镇的人让我把你带回去。

Text.en: Just the woman I'm looking for! Your friends tasked me with savin' ya.
Text.zh: 我正要找你呢！你的朋友们托我来救你。

Text.en: Gwennen... I have come with Makoru, by request of your friends, to find <i>{you.}
Text.zh: 桂温妮……我是受你朋友之托，跟马克鲁一起来找你的。

Text.en: If my friends sent you, you must be tough. Do you know anything about our grand quest?<continue>Inside this tomb lies Medved, an ancient Kalguuran hero. I'm absolutely positive! <continue>We'll have time for small talk later. When you're ready, I'll blow open the door, and you'll go inside.
Text.zh: 既然是我的朋友们派你来的，那你肯定有两下子。你知道我们的伟大使命吗？<continue>这座墓穴里安葬着梅德维德，一位卡古兰的古代英雄。我非常确定！<continue>我们待会儿再闲聊。你准备好了，我就把门炸开，然后你进去。

Text.en: If that's what it takes to see you home safely.
Text.zh: 只要能把你安全带回去就行。

Text.en: What if I say no? Is that an option? <continue>Alright. Fine.
Text.zh: 要是我说不呢？有这选项吗？<continue>行，好吧。

Text.en: You are full of surprises.
Text.zh: 你还真是让人意外。

Text.en: Let's make this quick. Makoru's waiting for us.
Text.zh: 咱们速战速决。马克鲁还在等我们。

Text.en: Well then. Suppose I don't have a choice.
Text.zh: 好吧。看来我也没得选。

Text.en: There better not be something dangerous down there.
Text.zh: 希望下面别有什么危险的东西。

Text.en: I will do what is necessary.
Text.zh: 必要之事，我自会去做。

Text.en: Ye've done this afore, I hope?
Text.zh: 你以前干过这事吧？

Text.en: You survived! Good work. Sounds like you two had quite the battle! <continue>Wow! This... is definitely Medved. Look! He's still got his twin axes.
Text.zh: 你居然活着出来了！干得不错。听声音你们打得挺激烈啊！<continue>哇。这……确实是梅德维德。你看，他那对双斧还在。

Text.en: Did you send me in here knowing I'd have to kill him?
Text.zh: 你派我进去的时候，就知道我得把他杀掉？

Text.en: Cheeky. You said Medved 'lies' in this tomb. Did you know he was undead?
Text.zh: 真够贼的。你刚才说梅德维德“安息”在这墓里。你早就知道他变成亡灵了吧？

Text.en: I was aware he might put up a fight. I thought you could handle it - and I was right!
Text.zh: 我料到他可能会动手。但我觉得你能应付，看来我没想错！

Text.en: Your ancestor was in a monstrous state, yet you do not seem surprised.
Text.zh: 你的祖先都变成那副怪物模样了，你却一点都不惊讶。

Text.en: Did you know I'd have to fight this 'Medved' for ya?
Text.zh: 你是不是早就知道我得替你对付这个“梅德维德”？

Text.en: I feel you were dishonest with me. Did you know Medved would want to fight?
Text.zh: 我觉得你对我有所隐瞒。你是不是知道梅德维德会动手？

Text.en: It was a possibility I'd accounted for, yes.
Text.zh: 我确实考虑过这种可能性。

Text.en: Was that why you sent me in first? To kill him for you?
Text.zh: 所以你才让我先进去？让我替你杀了他？

Text.en: You coulda warned me I'd be doing your dirty work for you. Killin' an ancient hero.
Text.zh: 你本可以提前说一声，让我替你干脏活，杀一个古代英雄。

Text.en: Yer friends failed to warn me o' your duplicitous ways. You damn near got me killed!
Text.zh: 你的朋友们可没跟我说你这么会耍心眼。你差点害死我！

Text.en: Well, that's not what I <i>{wanted} to happen! But I knew it might be dangerous.
Text.zh: 我可不是<i>{故意}的！但我知道可能会有危险。

Text.en: Medved is my ancestor. Over a thousand years ago, he died, and rose again, as one does on Wraeclast. <continue>I knew he'd been trapped in here for a long time, but I wasn't sure what we'd find. <continue>Guess the undead just keep kicking! Which is... a terrible fate. Best not to dwell on it. <continue>Anyway, let's have a look around, shall we? Maybe he was trapped with some treasure.
Text.zh: 梅德维德是我的祖先。一千多年前他就死了，然后又复活了——这在瓦尔克拉斯常有的事。<continue>我知道他被困在这里很久了，但不确定会遇到什么。<continue>看来亡灵就是打不死啊！这种命运……还真可怕。最好别去想它。<continue>不管怎样，四处看看吧？说不定他还留了点宝贝。

Text.en: I found this book.
Text.zh: 我找到了这本书。

Text.en: Do books count as treasure?
Text.zh: 书也算宝贝吗？

Text.en: Yes, he was. I found this book.
Text.zh: 是的，确实有。我找到这本书。

Text.en: What about this book?
Text.zh: 这本书怎么样？

Text.en: I've already found a book.
Text.zh: 我已经找到一本书了。

Text.en: I found this book, if that counts.
Text.zh: 我找到了这本书，如果这也算宝贝的话。

Text.en: I found something. A book.
Text.zh: 我找到了点东西，一本书。

Text.en: Would you fancy a book?
Text.zh: 你想要书吗？

Text.en: Well well well! This day keeps getting better! Let me have a look. <continue>This logbook... belonged to Vorana! She's another ancient Kalguuran. What was it doing here? <continue>Hmm! Interesting. I'll meet you at Makoru's ship. I'll just be a moment.
Text.zh: 哎哟，不错啊！今天真是越来越有意思了！让我看看。<continue>这本日志……是沃拉娜的！她也是古代的卡古兰人。这书怎么会在这里？<continue>嗯！有意思。我会去马克鲁的船上找你。我一会儿就到。

Text.en: In fact... is that a logbook I see?<continue>It looks like a Black Scythe logbook. Wonder what that's doing here? <continue>You grab that - we're going to need it. I'll keep looking. We'll meet at Makoru's ship.
Text.zh: 等待……我是不是看到一本日志？<continue>看起来像是黑镰的日志。不知道它怎么会在这儿。<continue>你把它拿上——我们用得着。我再继续找找。我们在马克鲁的船上碰头。

Text.en: Sure, I can tell you more about Medved. Where to start? <continue>He led the Druids of the Circle. Well... the Druids of the Broken Circle.
Text.zh: 当然，我可以跟你讲讲梅德维德的事。从哪儿说起呢？<continue>他领导着圆环祭司……嗯，应该叫裂环祭司。

Text.en: Why is it called the Broken Circle?
Text.zh: 为什么叫裂环？

Text.en: Why change the name? Sounds a little dramatic.
Text.zh: 他们为什么改名字？

Text.en: What's with the name change?
Text.zh: 为什么要改名？

Text.en: What caused them to become 'of the Broken Circle'?
Text.zh: 为什么变成裂环了？

Text.en: Because... the circle was broken? How to explain...
Text.zh: 因为……圆环碎了，该怎么说呢……

Text.en: I thought Druids were of nature, not circles.
Text.zh: 我还以为德鲁伊属于自然，而不是什么圆环。

Text.en: There are Druids where you're from?
Text.zh: 你们那儿也有德鲁伊？

Text.en: They're Druids? Like the Maji of old?
Text.zh: 他们是德鲁伊？就像上古众神一样？

Text.en: They're not Druids as you know them. Mystics might be a better term.
Text.zh: 他们不是你理解的那种德鲁伊，叫秘术师也许更贴切。

Text.en: I've heard o' their kind... though I know little o' their ways.
Text.zh: 我听说过他们这类人……但不太了解他们的路数。

Text.en: The Druids, or mystics, believed time is a circle, and that knowing the past can predict the future. <continue>Medved and his mystics crafted scrying pools, in which they saw visions of the future-past. <continue>Their purpose in joining that ancient expedition was to scry and help make decisions. <continue>When other Kalguurans began using the forbidden gems, the scrying pools stopped working. <continue>The mystics could no longer see the future-past. So Medved declared the circle broken.
Text.zh: 那些德鲁伊，或者叫秘术师，相信时间是圆环，知晓过去就能预测未来。<continue>梅德维德和他的秘术师们打造了占卜池，他们在里面能看到“未来的过去”的景象。<continue>他们加入那次古老的远征，就是为了通过占卜来辅助决策。<continue>后来其他卡古兰人开始使用禁忌宝石，占卜池就失效了。<continue>秘术师们再也看不到“未来的过去”。于是梅德维德宣告：圆环已碎。

Text.en: I see. But there is one thing I do not understand. <continue>How did the gems cause the scrying pools to stop working?
Text.zh: 我明白了。但我有一件事不明白。<continue>宝石是怎么让占卜池失效的？

Text.en: Time is a circle? How do you mean?
Text.zh: 时间是圆环？怎么讲？

Text.en: Time itself was broken? That doesn't make sense.
Text.zh: 时间本身碎掉了？这不合理啊。

Text.en: If our past determines our future, are we not destined to repeat history?
Text.zh: 如果过去决定未来，那我们不是注定要重复历史吗？

Text.en: How did these scrying pools work, then? Would each mystic see the same thing?
Text.zh: 那这些占卜池是怎么运作的？每个秘术师看到的都一样吗？

Text.en: I've not heard of time being a circle. And how could time be broken?
Text.zh: 我从没听说过时间是圆环。而且时间怎么可能被打破？

Text.en: I must admit, I don't fully understand it myself.
Text.zh: 老实说，我自己也没完全搞懂。

Text.en: What a strange practice. As I know it, time is the flowing sand. We are the wind that changes its path.
Text.zh: 这说法真够奇怪的。就我所知，时间是流沙，我们是改变它轨迹的风。

Text.en: Time is a circle, and the world is flat. Am I getting that right?
Text.zh: 时间是圆环，世界是平的。我没说错吧？

Text.en: ...Right...

What's important is that the circle was broken, and with it, the Druids' purpose. <continue>They had been pacifists, but some took up arms to help fight the relentless monsters of Wraeclast. <continue>Medved himself wielded twin axes empowered with gems.
