He'd abandoned the Druids' principles completely. <continue>One night, Medved left to challenge the leader of the monsters, and never returned. <continue>Well <i>{technically} he did return. But undead. Horrific. Now leading the monsters himself. <continue>Harrowing stuff. The Druids of the Broken Circle died with Medved on this continent.
Text.zh: 重要的是，圆环碎了，德鲁伊们的使命也就跟着崩塌了。<continue>他们本是和平主义者，但有些人拿起武器，帮助对抗瓦尔克拉斯那些没完没了的怪物。<continue>梅德维德本人更是挥舞着镶嵌宝石的双斧作战，完全抛弃了德鲁伊的信条。<continue>一天晚上，梅德维德出发去挑战怪物的首领，从此再也没回来。<continue>嗯……<i>{严格来说}他确实回来了。只是变成了亡灵，恐怖至极。而且他自己当上了怪物的首领。<continue>真让人心惊。裂环祭司也随着梅德维德一起葬身在这片大陆。

Text.en: How much do you know about the King of Kalguur?
Text.zh: 你对卡古兰的国王有什么了解？

Text.en: Ooh! Are we finally going to talk about him?
Text.zh: 哦！我们终于要聊他了吗？

Text.en: I confess I know little of the King. Can you tell me about him?
Text.zh: 说实话，我对那位国王知之甚少。你能跟我说说吗？

Text.en: No. We cannot speak freely. It is still not safe.
Text.zh: 不行。还不能随便谈论他。现在还不安全。

Text.en: I know very little about your king.
Text.zh: 我对你们的国王几乎一无所知。

Text.en: Your king is still a mystery to me.
Text.zh: 我还不是很了解你们的国王。

Text.en: Next to nothing. It seems to be a sore subject among your peers.
Text.zh: 几乎什么都不知道。感觉对你们来说，这话题挺敏感的。

Text.en: Near nothin' at all.
Text.zh: 几乎啥也不知道。

Text.en: Ach... if I knew anything of him, I've forgotten.
Text.zh: 唉……就算知道点什么，我也早忘了。

Text.en: Good. That's how it should be.
Text.zh: 很好。就该这样。

Text.en: Who?
Text.zh: 谁？

Text.en: The King of Kalguur.
Text.zh: 卡古兰的国王啊。

Text.en: That was supposed to be a joke.
Text.zh: 我刚才是在开玩笑。

Text.en: And jokes are supposed to be funny! There's nothing funny about him.
Text.zh: 玩笑就该好笑！他可一点都不好笑。

Text.en: The King of Kalguur has ways of... knowing things. It is dangerous to even <i>{speak} about him. <continue>Even now, with Kingsmarch in this state... I am certain he has spies reporting back to Kalguur. <continue>The less you know about him, the better. You'll be safer that way.
Text.zh: 卡古兰的国王有些……探知的手段。哪怕只是<i>{提起}他都很危险。<continue>即使现在君锋镇破成这样……我敢肯定这里也有他的眼线在往卡古兰报信。<continue>你知道得越少越好。这样你才更安全。

Text.en: Medved's logbook? I don't have it. What I found wasn't written by Medved anyway. <continue>It was a Remembrancer's recollection. I dropped it when the monsters surrounded me. <continue>I wish I'd held onto it. I would have made Tujen read it for you.
Text.zh: 梅德维德的日志？不在我这儿。我找到的那本也不是梅德维德写的。<continue>那是一位文书的记录。我被怪物围住时弄丢了。<continue>真希望我当时没撒手，那样就可以让图金读给你听了。

Text.en: Do you remember what the logbook said?
Text.zh: 你还记得日志里说了什么吗？

Text.en: Do you recall what was in the logbook?
Text.zh: 你记得日志里写了什么吗？

Text.en: Do you remember what was in it?
Text.zh: 你还记得里面写了什么吗？

Text.en: Do ye remember what was written in it?
Text.zh: 你记得里面写了啥不？

Text.en: For the most part.
Text.zh: 大概记得。

Text.en: What was in the logbook, then?
Text.zh: 那日志里到底写了什么？

Text.en: What did the logbook say?
Text.zh: 日志说什么了？

Text.en: Care to tell me what it said?
Text.zh: 能告诉我里面说了什么吗？

Text.en: I'd like to hear what was in the logbook.
Text.zh: 我想听听日志里写的内容。

Text.en: It was written after Medved had begun leading the monsters... and become monstrous himself. <continue>The writer was brave, and foolish. They followed Medved, trying to learn what caused this change. <continue>Had Medved chosen this betrayal? Was something controlling him, or was he a mindless undead himself?<continue>The Remembrancer was unable to find those answers. He seemed haunted by that uncertainty. <continue>He did, however, witness the undead Medved being trapped. He claimed Vorana herself was the one to trap him. <continue>Finding Vorana's logbook confirms his account. I think she meant for Medved to have it. <continue>Vorana and Medved were once lovers. They had a child together. It's all rather tragic, isn't it?
Text.zh: 那是在梅德维德开始率领怪物……自己也变成怪物之后写的。<continue>作者很勇敢，也有点傻。他们跟着梅德维德，想清楚他为什么会有这种变化。<continue>是梅德维德主动选择背叛吗？还是有什么东西在控制他？或者他自己就是个没有意识的亡灵？<continue>那位文书没能找到答案。这种不确定一直折磨着他。<continue>不过，他确实亲眼看到亡灵梅德维德被封印。他说是沃拉娜亲手将他困住的。<continue>我们找到的沃拉娜日志也印证了他的说法。我觉得沃拉娜是故意让梅德维德拿到那本日志的。<continue>沃拉娜和梅德维德曾经是恋人。他们还有一个孩子。整件事都挺悲惨的，对吧？

Text.en: It's wonderful to see Rog back out on the grand quest. Like when we were young. <continue>We weren't even dating before we came to Wraeclast, you know. I'd always fancied him. <continue>Watching Rog draw schematics, hearing his brilliant plans... I'm a lucky woman!
Text.zh: 看到罗格重新追寻伟大使命，真是太好了。就像我们年轻时一样。<continue>其实我们俩来瓦尔克拉斯之前还没开始约会呢。不过我一直挺喜欢他的。<continue>看着罗格画图纸，听他讲那些天才般的计划……我真是个幸运的女人！

Text.en: Does Dannig have a lucky woman?
Text.zh: 丹宁也有他的幸运女人吗？

Text.en: Not at the moment, that I know of. Would you like me to check?
Text.zh: 目前应该没有。需要我帮你打听一下吗？

Text.en: No! No... no... <i>{no}. Tell me more about the fat one.
Text.zh: 不！不……不……<i>{不用了}。还是继续说那个胖子吧。

Text.en: I would have thought you and Tujen had more in common.
Text.zh: 我还以为你跟图金会更合得来。

Text.en: Tujen? He's my cousin.
Text.zh: 图金？他是我表哥。

Text.en: Uhh... never mind.  <continue>Please... keep talking. You were saying something about the grand quest.
Text.zh: 呃……当我没说。<continue>请继续说吧。你刚才说到那个伟大使命来着。

Text.en: That's right.
Text.zh: 对。

Text.en: I could, uh, make you an even luckier woman!
Text.zh: 我可以，呃，让你变得更幸运哦！

Text.en: Ha! Not a chance. Rog is the only man for me.
Text.zh: 哈！想得美。罗格是我的唯一。

Text.en: I thought all four of you came here together for the quest?
Text.zh: 那你们四个是一起来这儿追寻伟大使命的？

Text.en: We did! We were all friends in Kalguur before we came here.
Text.zh: 没错！我们来这儿之前在卡古兰就是朋友了。

Text.en: I see. But only you and Rog are married?
Text.zh: 原来如此。可就你跟罗格结婚了？

Text.en: ...Yes? Tujen is my cousin. Dannig is like a brother to me. And Rog...
Text.zh: ……对啊？图金是我表哥。丹宁就像我兄弟。而罗格……

Text.en: Love is a beautiful thing. I felt the same when I would watch my Aoife dance.
Text.zh: 爱情真美好。我以前看着我的艾欧菲跳舞时，也有同感。

Text.en: There is nothing like love. It's a wonderful feeling.
Text.zh: 没有什么比得上爱情。那种感觉太美妙了。

Text.en: I am happy for you. You are both lucky.
Text.zh: 我真为你高兴。你们俩都挺幸运的。

Text.en: You have good taste.
Text.zh: 你眼光不错。

Text.en: If it works, it works.
Text.zh: 能成就是好事。

Text.en: Kingsmarch stalled our adventure, but it allowed our love to blossom. For that, I am thankful.<continue>Now we have each other, and our children. I couldn't be happier!
Text.zh: 君锋镇耽误了我们的伟大使命，但它让我们的爱情开花结果。为此我心存感激。<continue>现在我们有了彼此，还有了孩子。我已经很满足了！

Text.en: Apologies if I am too blunt, but... how did you know you were in love?
Text.zh: 恕我直言，但你……是怎么确定自己爱上他的？

Text.en: How did you capture the heart of such a man?
Text.zh: 你是怎么把这样的男人搞到手的？

Text.en: Can I ask... how did you know you were meant to be... more than friends?
Text.zh: 我想问一问……你怎么知道你们注定不只是……朋友？

Text.en: And ye got married! How did that come about?
Text.zh: 你们还结了婚！怎么走到那一步的？

Text.en: It's a wonderful story. Me and Rog...
Text.zh: 这故事特别美好。我和罗格……

Text.en: Rog's not much of a man though, is he?
Text.zh: 不过罗格也没多厉害吧？

Text.en: He's twice the man you are - in every way. He's perfect for me.
Text.zh: 他哪方面都比你强。他对我来说是完美的。

Text.en: I must ask: what do you see in him?
Text.zh: 我得问一句，你看上他什么了？

Text.en: What made you choose Rog?
Text.zh: 你为什么会选罗格？

Text.en: Forgive me if I transgress. You and Rog want opposing things. How does that work?
Text.zh: 恕我冒昧。你和罗格想要的东西背道而驰。这怎么行得通？

Text.en: We balance each other. Rog is my anchor. <continue>He is gentle and careful. A bit too careful sometimes, haha. <continue>It took him ages to catch on that I liked him, and ages more to make a move.<continue>It was worth the wait. The four of us play this delightful game of dice-based adventures that Rog concocted himself.<continue>He'd planned out a whole campaign, just him and I. He'd even carved new figurines for us!  <continue>Rog means everything to me. So keep him safe out there, will you?
Text.zh: 我们俩互相平衡。罗格就是我的锚。<continue>他温柔又谨慎，有时候甚至太过谨慎了，哈哈。<continue>他花了好长时间才看出来我喜欢他，又花了更长时间才敢行动。<continue>但等待是值得的。我们四个会玩罗格自己设计的一种骰子冒险游戏，还挺有意思的。<continue>他还专门为我们俩策划了一整套剧本，甚至亲手刻了新的棋子！<continue>罗格就是我的一切。所以出去之后把他保护好，行吗？

Text.en: All <i>{you} need to know about the comet is that we <i>{must} retrieve its Verisium before the King does. <continue>The longer we delay, the more danger we are in!
Text.zh: <i>{你}只需要知道，我们<i>{必须}在国王之前拿到那颗彗星上的维金。<continue>拖得越久，我们就越危险！

Text.en: We will find a way, I am sure.
Text.zh: 我们肯定能找到办法的。

Text.en: Watch your tone with me, Gwendolyn.
Text.zh: 格温多琳，注意你说话的语气。

Text.en: Are you alright? You seem feverish.
Text.zh: 你没事吧？你看上去像发烧了。

Text.en: Do not speak to me like I am a child.
Text.zh: 别把我当小孩子一样教育。

Text.en: I'm not your enemy, Gwen.
Text.zh: 我不是你的敌人，桂温妮。

Text.en: I'm sorry I asked!
Text.zh: 我就不该问的！

Text.en: It will be done. We have a plan.
Text.zh: 我们会搞定的。我们有计划。

Text.en: No need to be snappy, lass!
Text.zh: 说话别这么冲嘛，姑娘！

Text.en: Do you not grasp the urgency? As we speak, the King is sending his ships. <continue>Getting that Verisium first is our only chance to fight him. We must find a way.
Text.zh: 你还不明白事情有多紧急吗？就在我们说话这会儿，国王的船已经出发了。<continue>要是不能抢在他之前拿到那批维金，我们就一点反抗的机会都没有。我们必须找到办法。

Text.en: I am sorry, but I cannot help if I do not understand the threat your king poses.
Text.zh: 抱歉，可我只有知道你们的国王有多危险，才能帮上忙。

Text.en: Hard to fear your king when I know nothing about him.
Text.zh: 我对你们国王一无所知，让我怎么怕他？

Text.en: I wish to understand your terror, but I do not know your king. No one will speak openly.
Text.zh: 我想理解你们的恐惧，可我不了解你们的国王。没一个人愿意跟我解释清楚。

Text.en: You told me I shouldn't know about the King. Now you're upset that I don't know about him.
Text.zh: 你告诉我不该知道国王的事，现在又怪我什么都不知道？

Text.en: Why should I fear your king? For all you lot will tell me, I don't even know if he's real!
Text.zh: 我凭什么怕你们的国王？你们什么都不告诉我，我都不确定这人是不是真的存在。

Text.en: How am I supposed to help you if I don't know anything about your king?
Text.zh: 我要是对你的国王一无所知，如何能帮上你的忙？

Text.en: I know we must be delicate, but I need more information on your king.
Text.zh: 我知道不能冒失，但我需要多了解了解你们的国王。

Text.en: If ye want my help, I need answers. Tell me more about yer damn king.
Text.zh: 你要想让我帮忙，就把话说清楚点。多说说你们那该死的国王。

Text.en: You're right. And with all you've done for us... you deserve some honesty. <continue>The current King of Kalguur is a descendant of the King who sent our ancestors to Wraeclast.<continue>They all have the same name. I won't speak it because... he will hear it, no matter how far.
Text.zh: 你说得对。看在你帮了我们这么多的份上……我确实该坦诚一些。<continue>现在的卡古兰国王，是当年派我们祖先来瓦尔克拉斯的那个国王的后裔。<continue>他们都叫同一个名字。我不会说出来，因为……不管隔多远，他都能听到。

Text.en: He'll hear it? From his spies?
Text.zh: 他会听到？靠间谍？

Text.en: Yes, but it's rumoured he also has more... magical means.
Text.zh: 是的，但也有传闻说他还有……魔法的手段。

Text.en: How is that possible? Does he use runes? Can runecraft do that?
Text.zh: 怎么可能？他用符文吗？符文工艺能做到那种事？

Text.en: I wish I knew... but I don't. No one does.
Text.zh: 我要是知道就好了……可我不知道。没人知道。

Text.en: I thought you hated magic? That sounds <i>{extremely} magical.
Text.zh: 我还以为你们讨厌魔法。这听起来可是<i>{太}魔法了。

Text.en: For all we know, it <i>{is} magic. We have no idea how he does it.
Text.zh: 据我们所知，那可能<i>{就是}魔法。我们搞不清他是怎么做到的。

Text.en: Maybe you were taught to hate magic so you'd never learn his tricks.
Text.zh: 也许你们被教育得要痛恨魔法，是为了不让你们学会他的手段。

Text.en: That's... quite possible, actually.
Text.zh: 这……其实非常有可能。

Text.en: That sounds...  improbable. But I can see that you believe this.
Text.zh: 听起来……不太可信。但我看得出你信这个。

Text.en: I do. I know it is true.
Text.zh: 我信。我知道这是真的。

Text.en: I have not heard of such powers. Can other Kalguurans do that?
Text.zh: 我没听说过这样的力量。别的卡古兰人也能做到吗？

Text.en: No one but the King. Yet every King has had this power, somehow.
Text.zh: 只有国王可以。但不知为何，历任国王都有这个能力。

Text.en: Why would his spies care if you say his name?
Text.zh: 间谍为什么要在意你们有没有说国王的名字？

Text.en: It's not his spies that hear his name. The King himself will hear it.
Text.zh: 听到名字的不是他的间谍，是国王本人。

Text.en: That's not possible.
Text.zh: 那不可能。

Text.en: It is. I just don't know how.
Text.zh: 事实就是如此。我只是不知道他是怎么做到的。

Text.en: You lot have some strange magics, for folk who don't believe in it.
Text.zh: 你们这帮不相信魔法的人，倒是有不少古怪的魔法。

Text.en: <i>{We} do not use magic. The King is a different story.
Text.zh: 我们不用魔法。国王可不一样。

Text.en: So he uses magic, then?
Text.zh: 所以他就是在用魔法？

Text.en: I don't know <i>{what} he does. All I know is, he can always hear his name.
Text.zh: 我不知道他用了<i>{什么}。我只知道，只要说出他的名字，他就一定听得到。

Text.en: Does it only work if a Kalguuran says his name?
Text.zh: 只有卡古兰人说他的名字才有用吗？

Text.en: I've never tested that.
Text.zh: 我从来没试过。

Text.en: Well, let's try it. What's his name again? You could write it down.
Text.zh: 那我们试试。他叫什么来着？你可以写下来。

Text.en: I'm not going to risk it! Not with the state of things here.
Text.zh: 我不会冒这个险的！现在这种局势更不行。

Text.en: Is he able to speak to you with this power?
Text.zh: 他能用这种能力跟你说话吗？

Text.en: Luckily, he is not. I would like to never hear his voice again.
Text.zh: 幸好不能。我这辈子都不想再听到他的声音了。

Text.en: And does he know when you think his name? Or only when it is spoken?
Text.zh: 那你在心里想他的名字呢？还是只有说出来才会被知道？

Text.en: Only when spoken... that I know of.
Text.zh: 目前看来……只有说出来才会被知道。

Text.en: Sounds like a story ye tell yer children to keep 'em quiet.
Text.zh: 听起来像是为了骗小孩别闹腾编的那种故事。

Text.en: Ha! You're not wrong. I wish it was only a story.
Text.zh: 哈！你说得没错。我倒希望那真的只是故事。

Text.en: You actually believe it? That his ears can hear ye across the damn sea?
Text.zh: 你真信啊？信他能隔着整片大海听到你的声音？

Text.en: I don't know how, but I <i>{know} he does. He might even hear more than his name.
Text.zh: 我不知道他怎么做到的，但我<i>{确信}他做得到。甚至……可能不只是听见名字。

Text.en: The King shrouds himself in mystery. Been that way as long as I can remember. <continue>Yet each King is more tyrannical than the last. Everything that happens in Kalguur is by his design. <continue>He chartered Kingsmarch to monitor us. He also sought artefacts of power on Wraeclast, especially Vaal artefacts. <continue>This is not idle curiosity. He is planning something.<continue>In our hands, that Verisium is a shield. In his... it is a weapon.
Text.zh: 这位国王一直都很神秘。从我记事起就是这样了。<continue>可每一任国王都比上一任更残暴。卡古兰发生的所有事，都是他一手策划的。<continue>他给君锋镇签发特许经营权，就是为了监视我们。他还在瓦尔克拉斯到处搜寻蕴含力量的遗物，尤其是瓦尔遗物。<continue>这可不是什么消遣。他在谋划着什么。<continue>维金在我们手里是盾，在他手里……就是武器。

Text.en: Even I must admit, seeing the Triskelion Flame reforged is invigorating. <continue>For eons, it was a symbol wielded by the Kalguuran King. A uniting banner, under his control. <continue>The intended meaning of the Triskelion had been lost before it was even brought to Wraeclast.
Text.zh: 连我都不得不承认，看到三耀之火被重铸，真是令人振奋。<continue>千百年来，它一直象征着卡古兰王权，是被国王掌控的统一旗帜。<continue>但三耀之火原本的意义，早在被带到瓦尔克拉斯之前就被遗忘了。

Text.en: What is the Triskelion Flame's intended meaning?
Text.zh: 三耀之火原本的意义是什么？

Text.en: Oh, really? And you know its true <i>{intended} meaning?
Text.zh: 哦，真的吗？那你知道它 <i>{原本}代表什么？

Text.en: What should the Triskelion stand for?
Text.zh: 三耀之火应该象征什么？

Text.en: What was the purpose of the Triskelion Flame?
Text.zh: 三耀之火要表达什么意义？

Text.en: What sort o' symbol was the Triskelion supposed to be?
Text.zh: 三耀之火原本象征着什么？

Text.en: What was the Triskelion supposed to represent?
Text.zh: 三耀之火当初是要表达什么？

Text.en: What was the Flame's intended meaning?
Text.zh: 三耀之火原本的意义是什么？

Text.en: Do ye know the intended meaning of it?
Text.zh: 你知道它原本的意义吗？

Text.en: It was the pinnacle of runecraft. It represented the wonders Kalguurans could create together. <continue>When Aldur, the first King, died, Kalguur fractured. Runefathers forged the Triskelion Flame in his honour. <continue>There were many peaceful and prosperous years, until the... new King came along. <continue>He claimed to be a descendant of Aldur. He took the Triskelion, and had anyone who challenged his claim killed. <continue>Now, the Triskelion Flame will unite the Kalguurans once more, as a symbol of hope. As it was always meant to.
Text.zh: 它是符文工艺的巅峰，象征着卡古兰人齐心协力创造奇迹的能力。 <continue>第一任国王奥杜尔死后，卡古兰四分五裂。符文之父们为了纪念他，铸就了三耀之火。 <continue>之后有过一段和平繁荣的时期，直到……新国王出现。 <continue>他自称是奥杜尔的后裔，夺走了三耀之火，杀掉了所有质疑他继承权的人。 <continue>如今，三耀之火将再次团结起卡古兰人，作为希望的象征——这才是它最初就该有的意义。

Text.en: What's next? We're going to overthrow the bloody King, that's what's next! <continue>Caution to the wind, Cadigan be damned! Hahaha!
Text.zh: 接下来怎么办？当然是狠狠推翻那个该死的国王！<continue>管他什么谨慎不谨慎，卡迪甘去死吧！哈哈！

Text.en: Is it safe to say his name?
Text.zh: 现在可以说他的名字了？

Text.en: You said his name! Has it lost its power?
Text.zh: 你竟然说了他的名字！难道它的力量失效了？

Text.en: Down with Cadigan! Do you think he heard that?
Text.zh: 打倒卡迪甘！你觉得他听到了吗？

Text.en: Are ye allowed to say his name now?
Text.zh: 你现在可以提他的名字了吗？

Text.en: He still hears it, but who cares? Let him know who comes for him!
Text.zh: 他还是能听到，但那又怎样？让他知道是谁来找他了！

Text.en: Ha! I like this side of you, Gwen! Let's bring down Cadigan!
Text.zh: 哈！我喜欢你这股劲，桂温妮！让我们一起扳倒卡迪甘！

Text.en: It is time to bring justice to your tyrant king.
Text.zh: 是时候让那个暴君国王付出代价了。

Text.en: Yes! I've been hiding from him for too long. I feel liberated!
Text.zh: 对！我躲他已经躲太久了。现在感觉整个人都自由了！

Text.en: You spoke his name? And so freely?
Text.zh: 你说了他的名字？还这么随意？

Text.en: You said his name? Did you intend to?
Text.zh: 你说了他的名字？你是故意的吗？

Text.en: I did! I'm not afraid of him. Not anymore. Let him hear!
Text.zh: 我就是说了！我不怕他。再也不怕了。让他听见吧！

Text.en: Middengard has not known freedom since Cadigan's foul lineage soiled the throne of Kalguur. <continue>I'll say it a thousand times now! Cadigan, the Loathsome! Cadigan, the Last!
Text.zh: 自从卡迪甘那肮脏的血脉玷污了卡古兰的王座，米登加德就再也没有真正的自由。<continue>我现在要说上一千遍！可恶的卡迪甘！末路的卡迪甘！

Text.en: It seems he will not wake... at least not through any mortal means. <continue>I'd best speak with Doryani.
Text.zh: 看来他不会醒了……至少靠凡人的手段不行。<continue>我得去找多里亚尼。

Text.en: He's well and truly out. Maybe the thaumaturge knows how to wake him.
Text.zh: 他是彻底沉睡了。也许那奇术师知道怎么叫醒他。

Text.en: He does not wake. Perhaps Doryani knows how we might rouse him.
Text.zh: 他毫无反应。也许多里亚尼知道怎么唤醒他。

Text.en: He won't stir. Best seek Doryani's advice.
Text.zh: 他一点反应也没有。最好去问问多里亚尼。

Text.en: Wherever he is, he's in deep. Doryani may know more.
Text.zh: 不管他现在神游何方，都陷得太深了。多里亚尼也许知道些什么。

Text.en: The fella earned his shut eye... not much I can do about it. Maybe Doryani'll have an idea.
Text.zh: 这家伙确实该好好睡一觉……我能做的实在不多。也许多里亚尼有办法。

Text.en: He slumbers as deeply as the Dreamer himself. Doryani may know how to wake him.
Text.zh: 他的沉睡之深，堪比幻梦者。多里亚尼或许知道怎么唤醒他。

Text.en: A sleep near indistinguishable from death. Let's hope Doryani has a way to wake him.
Text.zh: 这一睡，跟死了几乎没两样。但愿多里亚尼有办法叫醒他。

Text.en: Aoife...? Can it be?
Text.zh: 艾欧菲……？真的是你吗？

Text.en: Yes. I'm here.
Text.zh: 对，我在这里。

Text.en: But how?
Text.zh: 可是怎么会呢？

Text.en: The King in the Mists... remember how we were drawn to him, believing him one of the Maji of old?
Text.zh: 迷雾之王……还记得我们当初如何被他吸引，以为他是上古众神之一吗？

Text.en: Aye... we were fools...
Text.zh: 是啊……我们当时真是蠢透了……

Text.en: Not fools... just deceived. Even I could not see the King's true face until it was too late. <continue>And when I did, he... took me from you.
Text.zh: 我们并不傻，只是被骗了。连我都没能及时看清那国王的真面目。而<continue>当我终于看清时，他……已经把你从我身边夺走了。

Text.en: I should have saved ye... I should have done more...
Text.zh: 我本该救下你的……我本该多做些什么……

Text.en: Hush... that is not your blame to carry. <continue>That grief made you wander the wilds, lost in your pain.
Text.zh: 嘘……那不是你的错。<continue>那份悲痛让你在荒野中游荡，迷失在痛苦里。

Text.en: Aye... I were lost for a long while.
Text.zh: 是啊……我迷失了好长一段时间。

Text.en: Aoife? You return to me once more!
Text.zh: 艾欧菲？你又回到我身边了！

Text.en: Yes. I need your help.
Text.zh: 是的……我需要你的帮助。

Text.en: Anything darlin'. What is it?
Text.zh: 尽管说，亲爱的。什么事？

Text.en: Listen fast. The King's presence here weakens me...
Text.zh: 快听我说……国王在这里的力量让我越来越虚弱……

Text.en: But... the King is dead. I put the bastard down.
Text.zh: 可是……那个国王已经死了。我亲手把那个混蛋干掉了。

Text.en: You killed him in your domain, love... but now he returns from his.
Text.zh: 你在你的领域里杀了他，亲爱的……但现在他从自己的领域里归来了。

Text.en: These effigies are his connection to this world. They weaken my resolve even as we speak. <continue>Please rid this place of them, for there is more I must tell you...
Text.zh: 这些雕像是他连接这个世界的纽带，就在我们说话这会儿，它们正在一点点侵蚀我的意志。<continue>求你，清理掉这个地方的这些雕像。我还有更多事……必须告诉你……

Text.en: Yes, my dear. Rest now. I'll do as ye ask.
Text.zh: 好的，亲爱的。你先休息。我照你说的做。

Text.en: Name yourself.
Text.zh: 报上名来。

Text.en: Greetings, translucent one. Who might you be?
Text.zh: 幸会， 透明人。你是哪位？

Text.en: Who are you?
Text.zh: 你是谁？

Text.en: And who might you be?
Text.zh: 你又是谁？

Text.en: Hello to you. Whoever you are.
Text.zh: 你好啊。虽然不知道你是谁。

Text.en: Who do we have here, then?
Text.zh: 这又是哪位？

Text.en: By the Dreamer... who <i>{are} you?
Text.zh: 幻梦者在上……你<i>{是}谁？

Text.en: I am Aoife. But my name is of little importance now...
Text.zh: 我是艾欧菲。但我的名字现在已不重要……

Text.en: You are dead, yes?
Text.zh: 你已经死了，对吧？

Text.en: I assume you are dead, Aoife?
Text.zh: 我猜你已经死了，艾欧菲？

Text.en: Are you of the spirit world?
Text.zh: 你是灵体吗？

Text.en: What are you? A spirit?
Text.zh: 你是什么？鬼魂？

Text.en: Aye, can see for meself you've got bigger concerns. <continue>You <i>{are} dead, are you not?
Text.zh: 是啊，看得出来你有很大的麻烦。<continue>你 <i>{确实}死了，没错吧？

Text.en: Well, no polite way of saying this, so I'll just come right out with it. <continue>Are you... you know... <i>{dead?}
Text.zh: 好吧……这话不好听，不过我还是直说了。<continue>你是不是……那个……<i>{死了？}

Text.en: Forgive me being so forthright, Aoife, but are you deceased?
Text.zh: 请恕我直言，艾欧菲，你已经过世了吗？

Text.en: Yes. I am. Killed by the King of the Nameless... The King in the Mists.
Text.zh: 是的。我死了。被无名之王……迷雾之王杀死了。

Text.en: I faced that foe back in Ogham. He is long dead.
Text.zh: 我在欧甘遇到过那死对头。他早就死透了。

Text.en: Oh! You'll be pleased to know he's dead as well. Killed him myself while I was in Ogham.
Text.zh: 哦！那你应该会高兴，他也死了。我在欧甘亲手杀了他。

Text.en: It would seem that I have already redeemed you, Aoife. I deposed him myself back in Ogham.
Text.zh: 看来我已经替你报了仇，艾欧菲。我在欧甘亲手废了他。

Text.en: Then you might be pleased to learn I avenged you. <continue>Killed the King myself back in Ogham.
Text.zh: 那你应该会高兴，我替你复仇了。<continue>我在欧甘亲手杀了那个国王。

Text.en: As it so happens, I've met the man. Killed him too – back in Ogham.
Text.zh: 巧了，我见过那人，还顺手把他杀了——就在欧甘。

Text.en: The King in the Mists... you mean that thorny fella I killed back in Ogham?
Text.zh: 迷雾之王……你说的是我在欧甘杀掉的那个浑身带刺的家伙？

Text.en: Then you may rest easy in the knowledge that I sent the King to his death back in Ogham.
Text.zh: 那你可以安息了，因为我已经在欧甘送那个君王上路了。

Text.en: You killed him in your domain... but now he returns from his.
Text.zh: 你在你的领域里杀了他……但现在他从自己的领域里归来了。

Text.en: Of course... the dead never stay dead.
Text.zh: 果然……死人是不会乖乖待着的。

Text.en: Ah... I should have known.
Text.zh: 啊……我早该想到的。

Text.en: Must everything return from the grave?
Text.zh: 难道所有东西都会从坟墓里爬回来吗？

Text.en: Well then... this time I'd best make it permanent.
Text.zh: 那好吧……这次我最好让他彻底安息。

Text.en: Ugh. Shoulda known.
Text.zh: 啧。早该料到。

Text.en: Oh, for cryin' out loud! Doesn't <i>{anyone} stay dead around here?
Text.zh: 真是受不了！这地方就<i>{没人}能死透吗？

Text.en: Ah. Of course.
Text.zh: 啊，果然如此。

Text.en: These effigies are his connection to your world. They weaken my resolve even as we speak. <continue>Please rid this place of his presence. Then, we must speak further.
Text.zh: 这些雕像是他连接这个世界的纽带……就在我们说话这会儿，它们正在一点点侵蚀我的意志。<continue>求你，清理掉这个地方有他气息的东西。然后，我们还得继续谈。

Text.en: I'll do as you ask.
Text.zh: 我会照做的。

Text.en: Oh, alright then.
Text.zh: 哦，行吧。

Text.en: Very well.
Text.zh: 很好。

Text.en: I'll do what I can.
Text.zh: 我会尽力。

Text.en: Alright then. I'll do as you say.
Text.zh: 好吧，就按你说的办。

Text.en: Not to worry, miss. I'll send 'im packin'.
Text.zh: 别担心，女士。我这就把他赶走。

Text.en: As you wish.
Text.zh: 如你所愿。

Text.en: Please, remain with me. Tell me what you must.
Text.zh: 请留在我身边。把你想说的话告诉我。

Text.en: I can do better than that... I have the strength to bring you to me now.
Text.zh: 我还能更进一步……我现在有力量把你带到我身边。

Text.en: You must enter Caer Tarth once more. Pass through the Taisce. Then I can tell you everything.
Text.zh: 你必须再次进入赛尔塔斯，穿过泰斯卡。然后我就能告诉你一切。

Text.en: No! I cannae go back... I fear what may become of me!
Text.zh: 不！我不能回去……我怕我会变成什么怪物！

Text.en: There is nothing to fear. You will not lose yourself. Not while you're with me.
Text.zh: 没什么可怕的。有我在，你不会迷失自己。

Text.en: I... I accept then. I will come to you.
Text.zh: 那……我答应你。我会去你身边。

Text.en: The task is done.
Text.zh: 事情都办完了。

Text.en: I've done your dirty work. What now?
Text.zh: 我替你干完脏活了。接下来呢？

Text.en: The King's presence has been driven from this place.
Text.zh: 国王的痕迹已经被赶出此地。

Text.en: I did as you asked. Now what?
Text.zh: 我照你的吩咐做了。现在呢？

Text.en: Won't be seein' the King or his feckless cultists 'round here anytime soon.
Text.zh: 那国王和他那帮废物信徒一时半会儿是不会再出现了。

Text.en: Dead right. Sent that bloody King a message.
Text.zh: 没错。已经好好回敬了那该死的国王。

Text.en: The King in the Mists no longer holds sway over this place.
Text.zh: 此地已不再受迷雾之王的支配。

Text.en: Thank you. Now know this: there is much more to my story. With what little strength I have... I must bring you to me.
Text.zh: 谢谢你。现在听我说，我的故事还远没有结束。凭我仅剩的一点力量……我要把你带到我身边。

Text.en: Come to me in Caer Tarth. Pass through the Taisce. There... I can tell you everything. <continue>Please... the hour grows late and I've not much time...
Text.zh: 到赛尔塔斯来找我。穿过泰斯卡。在那里……我会告诉你一切。<continue>拜托……时间紧迫，我没有多少时间了……

Text.en: Not to worry. I'll meet you there.
Text.zh: 别担心。我会去那里找你。

Text.en: You can spare me the guilt trip. I'll be there.
Text.zh: 不用给我上压力，我会去的。

Text.en: I will join you.
Text.zh: 我会去找你。

Text.en: If I must.
Text.zh: 行吧，既然非去不可。

Text.en: I know Caer Tarth by name, but nowt else. Suppose I can come see it for meself.
Text.zh: 我只听说过赛尔塔斯这个名字，其他一概不知。看来我可以亲自去瞧瞧。

Text.en: Yeah, alright. I'll see ya there.
Text.zh: 行，好吧。到时候见。

Text.en: Rest assured, Aoife. I'll hear you out.
Text.zh: 放心吧，艾欧菲。我会听你说完。

Text.en: You... you appear so alive.
Text.zh: 你……你看起来如此鲜活。

Text.en: My energy is strongest here, yes. I feel the Goddess watching over me.
Text.zh: 是啊，我的力量在这里最为强大。我能感受到女神守护着我。

Text.en: I wish to just... stay here with you. Leave the world behind. Oh, dear Aoif–
Text.zh: 我只想……和你待在这里。把整个世界抛在身后。哦，我亲爱的艾欧——

Text.en: Hush, love! Remember – we mustn't use our names in this realm, lest the Nameless steal them from us. <continue>Listen now. I need your aid. Though I'd love for nothing more than to remain here with you... it cannot be so.
Text.zh: 嘘，亲爱的！记住，我们在这个领域不能说出自己的名字。免得无名者将它们夺走。<continue>听我说，我需要你的帮助。虽然我只想和你待在这里……但是不行。

Text.en: But why?
Text.zh: 可这是为什么？

Text.en: Look around you. Our home is sickened. The dawn darkens and the land grows poisoned. <continue>Before us, the three aspects of our Goddess stand, watching over this long-healed wound upon our land. <continue>But, as the King in the Mists and the Nameless grow stronger, that barrier weakens. And the darkness below spills forth once more. <continue>Should it break free, it would bring with it eternal doom. <continue>And in truth... I am tired, love. As long as that <i>{bastard} holds my body in thrall, my soul burns and I cannot rest.
Text.zh: 看看你的周围。我们的家园病了。黎明变暗，大地染毒。<continue>在我们面前，女神的三个化身矗立着，守护着我们这片土地上这道早已愈合的伤口。<continue>但随着迷雾之王和无名者日益强大，这道屏障变弱了。下方的黑暗再次涌出。<continue>如果它挣脱出来，将带来永恒的末日。<continue>说实话……我累了，亲爱的。只要那个<i>{混蛋}还奴役着我的身体，我的灵魂就在燃烧，无法安息。

Text.en: That damn murderous swine... I'll rip his feckin' head off!
Text.zh: 该死的杀人恶徒……我要把他的脑袋拧下来！

Text.en: He must die in <i>{his} realm. Drive him and the Nameless back to the dark Nothing from whence they came! <continue>Fool him. Engage his rituals and pay tribute to him. In doing so, he will believe you honour his claim of existence. <continue>He will grant you... an audience.
Text.zh: 他必须在<i>{他的}领域里死去。把他和无名者赶回他们的黑暗虚无！<continue>愚弄他，参与他的仪式，向他献上贡品。这样做，他就会相信你尊重了他的存在。<continue>他会赐予你……一次觐见。

Text.en: Hmm... deceive him, eh? I can get behind that.
Text.zh: 嗯……骗他，是吧？这我可以。

Text.en: Aye. Return him to Nothing. But please... make him suffer first.
Text.zh: 对。把他送回虚无。不过拜托……先让他受尽折磨。

Text.en: Oh, don't you worry. He'll regret the bloody day he came to exist!
Text.zh: 哦，放一万个心，我会让他悔恨存在于世的每一天！

Text.en: It's good to see you with a bit more colour in your cheeks. And... well... the rest of you too.
Text.zh: 你的气色恢复得不错，真让人高兴。还有……嗯……你身体的其他部分也是。

Text.en: Well, well... you look almost not dead.
Text.zh: 哎呀呀……你看起来都不像死人了。

Text.en: Your ghostly visage... it is no more...
Text.zh: 你幽灵般的面容……消失了……

Text.en: You look... different. Not like a spirit at all.
Text.zh: 你看上去……不一样了。不那么像鬼魂了。

Text.en: You're lookin' well. Certainly more so than before.
Text.zh: 你气色不错，比之前好太多了。

Text.en: Well, you're lookin' less... blue.
Text.zh: 嗯，你看起来不那么……发青了。

Text.en: You appear livelier than you did before. Is it something to do with this place?
Text.zh: 你看起来比以前更有生气了。和这个地方有关吗？

Text.en: Aye. My soul is strongest here. I feel the warmth of the Goddess watching over me.
Text.zh: 是的。我的灵魂在这里最为强大。我能感受到女神在守护我。

Text.en: I see. What is it about this place that's so special, Aoif–
Text.zh: 我明白了。这个地方有什么特别的，艾欧——

Text.en: Yes, yes... it all sounds very nice, Aoif–
Text.zh: 是的，是的……听起来都挺好，艾欧——

Text.en: Fascinating. And tell me, Miss Aoif–
Text.zh: 有意思。告诉我，艾女士——

Text.en: I've never known spirits to work that way. What makes you different, Aoi–
Text.zh: 我从不知道鬼魂会有这种情况。你为什么跟别人不一样，艾欧——

Text.en: It's good to hear it, Aoi–
Text.zh: 听到你这么说真让人高兴，艾欧——

Text.en: Must be some Goddess. Well, good to see you in better shape, Aoif–
Text.zh: 一定是位了不起的女神。很高兴看到你状态好多了，艾欧——

Text.en: I see... And what is this place, Aoif–
Text.zh: 我明白了……这是什么地方，艾欧——

Text.en: Hush! Do not speak my name here! If you <i>{must} address me, you may call me the Speaker of Truths.
Text.zh: 嘘！在这里不要说我的名字！如果你<i>{一定}要称呼我……你可以叫我真理传述者。

Text.en: But why?
Text.zh: 可是为什么呢？

Text.en: What if I don't want to?
Text.zh: 如果我不想呢？

Text.en: An important title... we Maraketh have tale-women. Perhaps we are not so different. <continue>But tell me... why can I not speak your name here?
Text.zh: 很重要的头衔……我们马拉克斯人有说书人。也许我们之间没太大区别。<continue>不过请告诉我……为什么我在这里不能说出你的名字？

Text.en: Why? What's wrong with saying your name?
Text.zh: 为什么？说你的名字有什么不妥吗？

Text.en: Why's that?
Text.zh: 为什么？

Text.en: That just a personal preference, or...?
Text.zh: 这是你的个人偏好，还是……？

Text.en: Can I ask why?
Text.zh: 能问一句为什么吗？

Text.en: Names have great power here, and the Nameless are desperate to overtake this domain. Should they learn our names... well, it's not worth the risk.
Text.zh: 名字在这里拥有巨大的力量，而无名者正急于夺取这个领域。如果它们知道了我们的名字……总之，不值得冒风险。

Text.en: Well then, Speaker of Truths... it is time you explained yourself fully.
Text.zh: 好吧，真理传述者……是时候把一切都说清楚了。

Text.en: Ugh... why even call me here if you won't let me have any fun?
Text.zh: 唉……你既然不肯让我找点乐子，那叫我来干嘛？

Text.en: I see. And you wish me to aid in the fight against these Nameless?
Text.zh: 我明白了。你叫我来是要我帮你对抗这些无名者？

Text.en: If it's such a risk, then why call me here?
Text.zh: 既然风险这么大，那你还叫我来干什么？

Text.en: Fine. No names. Now, best you get to explainin'.
Text.zh: 行，不提名字。现在，你最好赶紧解释清楚。

Text.en: I'm guessin' that has somethin' to do with why you called me here.
Text.zh: 我猜这跟你叫我来这里的原因有关。

Text.en: We do not need names. That is fine. Now, tell me everything.
Text.zh: 我们不用提名字。没关系。现在，把一切都告诉我。

Text.en: I have called upon you for two reasons. One concerns us all. The other... is purely selfish. <continue>The Wildwood was once full of life. But now, it is sickened, and I can feel it slowly dying. <continue>Before us, the three aspects of the Goddess stand, watching over a long-healed wound upon this land. <continue>But, as the King in the Mists and the Nameless grow stronger, that barrier weakens. And the darkness below spills forth once more. <continue>Should it break free, it would bring with it eternal doom.
Text.zh: 我叫你来有两个原因。一个关系到我们所有人。另一个……纯粹是出于私心。<continue>荒林曾经生机勃勃。但现在它生病了，我能感觉到它在慢慢死去。<continue>在我们面前，女神的三个化身矗立着，守护着这片土地上一道早已愈合的伤口。<continue>但随着迷雾之王和无名者日益强大，这道屏障变弱了。下方的黑暗再次涌出。<continue>如果它挣脱出来，那将带来永恒的末日。

Text.en: Well, we can't have that. What can be done?
Text.zh: 嗯，那可不行。我能做些什么？

Text.en: Well we mustn't let this darkness have all the fun. <continue>What can we do about it?
Text.zh: 嗯，可不能让这黑暗横行无忌。<continue>我们能做些什么？

Text.en: This is most terrible. I will assist you. What can I do?
Text.zh: 太可怕了，我会帮助你。我能做什么？

Text.en: Evil hides in every corner of this damned world. What can we do about <i>{this} threat?
Text.zh: 该死的邪恶遍布世界的每个角落。我们该怎么应对<i>{这个}威胁？

Text.en: Well that won't do, will it? What can I do to help?
Text.zh: 那可不行，不是吗？我能帮上什么忙？

Text.en: Eternal doom... not just regular doom? <continue>What can we do about that, anyway?
Text.zh: 永恒的末日……不只是普通的末日？<continue>那我们能做些什么？

Text.en: We must contain it. Tell me what can be done.
Text.zh: 我们必须遏制它。告诉我该怎么做。

Text.en: You must find the King in the Mists in his realm. Drive him and the Nameless back to the dark Nothing from whence they came! <continue>Fool him. Engage his rituals and pay tribute to him. In doing so, he will believe you honour his claim of existence. <continue>He will grant you... an audience.
Text.zh: 你必须在迷雾之王的领域中找到他，把他和无名者赶回他们的黑暗虚无！<continue>愚弄他，参与他的仪式，向他献上贡品。这样做，他就会相信你尊重了他的存在。<continue>他会赐予你……一次觐见。

Text.en: And then?
Text.zh: 然后呢？

Text.en: There's always more to it... what else?
Text.zh: 总还有下文……还有什么？

Text.en: What is next?
Text.zh: 接下来呢？

Text.en: I see. And then?
Text.zh: 明白了。然后呢？

Text.en: Right. Deceive him. And then what?
Text.zh: 行。欺骗他。然后怎么办？

Text.en: Alright. Then what?
Text.zh: 好吧。然后呢？

Text.en: And after I deceive him?
Text.zh: 欺骗他之后呢？

Text.en: That pertains to my other request. The King in the Mists holds my body in thrall, and so, my soul burns and I cannot rest... <continue>Return my body, so I may know peace. And when you kill him, do so slowly, and painfully, if you can.
Text.zh: 这关系到我另一个请求，迷雾之王奴役着我的身体，因此，我的灵魂始终在燃烧，无法安息……<continue>把我的身体找回来，让我得到安宁。当你下手时，如果可以，让他死得慢一些，痛苦一些。

Text.en: Aye. That he has.
Text.zh: 对，他确实想见我。

Text.en: Indeed, he has.
Text.zh: 确实如此。

Text.en: Yes. The King desires an audience... with me.
Text.zh: 是的。那个国王想接见……我。

Text.en: Yes. It is so.
Text.zh: 对，就是这样。

Text.en: Yes. Finally.
Text.zh: 是的，终于来了。

Text.en: Took his sweet time, he did – but aye.
Text.zh: 他可真够磨蹭的，不过没错。

Text.en: Yep. Done the busy work.
Text.zh: 嗯，杂活干完了。

Text.en: Yes.
Text.zh: 对。

Text.en: Good. I thank you for playing his game. <continue>Now... it is time to seek him out.
Text.zh: 很好。感谢你陪他玩这场游戏。现在……是时候去找他了。

Text.en: We sadly must curse a part of the wood with his presence. The invitation will serve as a gateway to his realm.
Text.zh: 遗憾的是，我们必须用他的存在诅咒森林的一部分……这份邀约将作为通往他领域的凭证。

Text.en: Good. That shall be where you can find him. In his... Crux of Nothingness. <continue>You must go there, lest the forest be overrun by his foulness.
Text.zh: 很好……那便是你能找到他的地方。在他的……虚无之症。<continue>你必须去那里，否则森林将被他的邪秽淹没。

Text.en: Rest assured, darling... I will return with you. You will know peace at last.
Text.zh: 放心吧，亲爱的……我会带着你回来。你终将得到安息。

Text.en: He will suffer for how he has treated you.
Text.zh: 他曾那样对待你，我一定会让他付出代价。

Text.en: Worry not. He will regret laying a finger on you.
Text.zh: 别担心，他一定会后悔曾经对你动手。

Text.en: I will rid this land of his darkness.
Text.zh: 我要把这片土地从他的黑暗中解救出来。

Text.en: I will redeem you.
Text.zh: 我会救赎你。

Text.en: I'm on it. The bastard will come to heel.
Text.zh: 包在我身上。那个混蛋会乖乖就范。

Text.en: Don't worry, miss. He'll be a lost memory in no time.
Text.zh: 别担心，女士。他很快就会被遗忘在时间的长河里。

Text.en: I will end his tyranny.
Text.zh: 我会结束他的暴政。

Text.en: What is wrong?
Text.zh: 怎么了？

Text.en: Ugh. What now?
Text.zh: 唉。又怎么了？

Text.en: What concerns you?
Text.zh: 你在担心什么？

Text.en: What is it?
Text.zh: 怎么回事？

Text.en: What's the matter?
Text.zh: 发生什么了？

Text.en: What is it, miss?
Text.zh: 怎么了，女士？

Text.en: Are you okay?
Text.zh: 你还好吗？

Text.en: What is it? What is the matter?
Text.zh: 怎么回事？出什么事了？

Text.en: I am still here... but why? My body is at peace now. Why must I endure this still?
Text.zh: 我还在这里……可是为什么？我的身体已经安息了。为什么我还要继续承受这些？

Text.en: Your strength is still needed here, Speaker of Truths.
Text.zh: 这里仍然需要你的力量，真理传述者。

Text.en: The Goddess speaks... to me?
Text.zh: 女神在……对我说话？

Text.en: The King in the Mists was the beginning of our woes. But the Bodach... is the end of them.
Text.zh: 迷雾之王是我们苦难的开始。而‘伯达克’……则是它们的终结。

Text.en: What is this 'Bodach'?
Text.zh: 这个“伯达克”是什么？

Text.en: Bo– what?
Text.zh: 伯什么？

Text.en: I have never heard of such a thing. What is it?
Text.zh: 我从没听说过这种东西。那是什么？

Text.en: What is a 'bodach'?
Text.zh: “伯达克”是什么？

Text.en: Never heard of it before. What is it?
Text.zh: 以前没听过。那是什么？

Text.en: A what now?
Text.zh: 你说什么？

Text.en: What is this creature?
Text.zh: 那是什么东西？

Text.en: The Bodach... hmm... it rings a bell...
Text.zh: 伯达克……嗯……有点耳熟……

Text.en: It is the Champion of Darkness itself. Residing betwixt this realm... and the Nothing. <continue>Below this barrier, is where we, the Sisters Three, sealed that foul one.
Text.zh: 它是黑暗本身的勇士，盘踞在这个领域……与虚无之间。<continue>在这道屏障之下，我们三姐妹封印了那个邪恶之物。

Text.en: But now with our realm weakened... the Bodach means to escape. And the Darkness along with it.
Text.zh: 但现在，我们的领域被削弱了……伯达克企图逃脱。黑暗也随之降临。

Text.en: But what can I do? I have no power here nor anywhere!
Text.zh: 我能做什么？无论在这里还是别处，我都没有任何力量！

Text.en: We require a champion of our own. You must aid this one... as you have bravely done so far. <continue>To survive within the darkness below, the champion must give up their name in this realm. You must complete... the Rite of the Nameless.
Text.zh: 我们需要一个勇士。你必须帮我……就像你迄今为止那样。要在下方的黑暗中生存，勇士必须在这个领域放弃自己的名字。你必须完成……无名仪式。

Text.en: How are we to do it?
Text.zh: 我们要怎么做？

Text.en: Champion. Step forth to the effigy and place the dead King's crown upon it.
Text.zh: 勇士，走到雕像前，把死去国王的王冠放在上面。

Text.en: Seek out powerful effigies of the Nameless. Take from them... and build your own. <continue>Then with it, revoke your name.
Text.zh: 寻找无名者蕴含力量的雕像。从它们那里夺取力量……并建造你自己的雕像。<continue>然后，用它来放弃你的名字。

Text.en: We are blessed! We <i>{must} do this! It is destiny.
Text.zh: 我们受到了祝福！我们<i>{必须}这样做，这是命运的安排。

Text.en: We shall overcome this.
Text.zh: 我们一定能成功。

Text.en: I make my own destiny. But yes... this sounds fun.
Text.zh: 我的命运我自己主宰。但没错……这听起来很有趣。

Text.en: I believe you are right. It shall be done.
Text.zh: 我相信你是对的。就这么办。

Text.en: I will try my best.
Text.zh: 我会尽力的。

Text.en: Alright, alright. I'll do what I can.
Text.zh: 行行，我尽力而为。

Text.en: Well... not gonna let all these ladies down, am I?
Text.zh: 嗯……总不能辜负这些女士们，对吧？

Text.en: The Dreamer has placed me here. So yes... I think you are right.
Text.zh: 幻梦者安排我来到这里。所以是的……我觉得你说的对。

Text.en: I'll do whatever it takes to bring you peace, my love. I promise you that.
Text.zh: 为了让你得到安息，我什么都愿意做，我的爱人。我向你保证。

Text.en: Tell me... what did you see down there?
Text.zh: 告诉我……你在下面看到了什么？

Text.en: The darkest dark, darker than any night. And the face of the Bodach... I shall never forget it.
Text.zh: 极致的黑暗，比任何夜晚都更黑。还有伯达克的脸……我永远不会忘记。

Text.en: Darkness. Pure black. And the Bodach... a terrifying creature.
Text.zh: 黑暗。纯粹的黑色。还有伯达克……一个可怕的怪物。

Text.en: It was quite inspiring really. Dark. Gloomy. But the Bodach was a bit much. Not much <i>{now,} though.
Text.zh: 其实还挺震撼的。黑暗、阴沉。伯达克确实有点吓人了，不过<i>{现在}就好多了。

Text.en: A horrifying place. One I wish never to return to. But the Bodach has been slain, at least.
Text.zh: 可怕的地方。我再也不想回到那地方。但至少伯达克已经死了。

Text.en: A grim expanse of nothing. But the Bodach is dead.
Text.zh: 一片灰暗的虚无。不过，伯达克已经死了。

Text.en: I saw that foul beast up close. Damned horrible, it was.
Text.zh: 我近距离看到了那邪恶的野兽。真他妈可怕。

Text.en: It was an absolute bloody horror show, to be honest...
Text.zh: 说实话，那真他妈是一场恐怖秀……

Text.en: I saw nothing good. But it is done.
Text.zh: 没什么好看的。但一切都结束了。

Text.en: [DNT]
Text.zh: 噩梦。黑暗。不过任务成功了。

Text.en: [DNT]
Text.zh: 不得不说，就算是我……也有点扛不住。不过，它死了。

Text.en: [DNT]
Text.zh: 一片无神之地。一处污秽之所。但我们终究凯旋而归。

Text.en: [DNT]
Text.zh: 一个鬼地方。不过事情办完了。

Text.en: I am filled with pride. The man I love has saved this most precious place, <i>{and} has given me the greatest gift of all... <continue>I feel it, love... I can finally be at peace.
Text.zh: 我为你骄傲。我的爱人拯救了最为珍贵的地方，<i>{而且}给了我最好的礼物……<continue>我感觉到了，亲爱的……我终于可以安息了。

Text.en: You have done a great service to both this realm and your own. The Darkness is driven back... and the Nameless have no hope. <continue>But also you've given me the greatest of gifts... <continue>I feel it... I can finally be at peace.
Text.zh: 你为这片领域和你自己的世界，都立下了大功。黑暗已经被逼退……无名者再无指望。<continue>但你还给了我最珍贵的礼物……<continue>我感觉到了……终于可以安息了。

Text.en: Yes. It is time.
Text.zh: 是啊，是时候了。

Text.en: Will it... hurt?
Text.zh: 会……痛吗？

Text.en: Not at all, my dear.
Text.zh: 一点也不，亲爱的。

Text.en: Must you go, darling? My heart cannae take it.
Text.zh: 亲爱的，你一定要走吗？我的心受不了。

Text.en: Do not despair, my love. I'll always be with you.
Text.zh: 不要绝望，我的爱人。我会永远与你同在。

Text.en: I am... afraid.
Text.zh: 我……害怕。

Text.en: Do not be. I am with you.
Text.zh: 别怕。我陪着你呢。

Text.en: You've earned your rest, Speaker of Truths.
Text.zh: 你赢得了安息，真理传述者。

Text.en: You've already died, dear... the hard part is over.
Text.zh: 你已经死过一次了，亲爱的……最难的一关早就过了。

Text.en: One final journey...
Text.zh: 最后一程……

Text.en: Don't worry. You have nothing to fear...
Text.zh: 别担心。你没什么好怕的……

Text.en: Spirit guide you.
Text.zh: 愿万灵指引你。

Text.en: This is bringin' a tear to me eye.
Text.zh: 我的眼睛都要湿了。

Text.en: It has been a pleasure to know you.
Text.zh: 认识你是我的荣幸。

Text.en: [DNT]
Text.zh: 别难过。你现在可以安息了。

Text.en: [DNT]
Text.zh: 是时候了。

Text.en: [DNT]
Text.zh: 安息吧。

Text.en: [DNT]
Text.zh: 不要抗拒。

Text.en: I... am ready.
Text.zh: 我……准备好了。

Text.en: I love you... Aoife.
Text.zh: 我爱你……艾欧菲。

Text.en: I tell you... I see Caer Tarth in my mind's eye, but all its details are blurred and murky.
Text.zh: 听我说……我在脑海里能看见赛尔塔斯，但细节全都模糊不清。

Text.en: So... where are we, exactly?
Text.zh: 那么……我们到底在哪儿？

Text.en: Care to tell me where we even are right now?
Text.zh: 能告诉我，我们现在到底在什么地方吗？

Text.en: Caer Tarth seems... somehow removed from reality. Where are we, exactly?
Text.zh: 赛尔塔斯似乎……有点脱离现实。我们到底在哪里？

Text.en: This place... what is it? It has a different feel to anything I've ever known.
Text.zh: 这里……究竟是什么地方？感觉跟我所知的任何地方都不一样。

Text.en: So this is the storied Caer Tarth. <continue>I admit, I'm a little confused as to where in the world we actually are.
Text.zh: 所以，这就是传说中的赛尔塔斯。<continue>我承认，我有点搞不清我们到底在世界的哪个角落。

Text.en: Somethin' about this place feels... different. But I can't say how.
Text.zh: 这地方感觉……有些不一样。但我说不清是哪里不一样。

Text.en: There is something otherworldly about this 'Caer Tarth'.
Text.zh: 这个“赛尔塔斯”有种不属于尘世的气息。

Text.en: Don't despair. Those memories may return with time. Your roaming certainly took a toll on you. <continue>The Taisce of Caer Tarth was the very first gateway into the Wildwood. The place where the Goddess gathered her Azmerian followers... those who would become the Maji, and led them through. <continue>But during our great war against the Nameless, this dark wound festered here, under the guarding eyes of the Goddess. Now... it is the last bastion against the burgeoning darkness.
Text.zh: 别担心。那些记忆也许会随时间而恢复，你的游荡确实对你造成了伤害。<continue>赛尔塔斯的泰斯卡就是进入荒林的原始门户。女神在那里召集她的阿兹莫里追随者……那些将成为众神的人，并带领他们穿过此地。<continue>但在对抗无名者的伟大战争中，这道黑暗的伤口在女神的注视下慢慢溃烂。如今……它是抵御日益增长的黑暗的最后堡垒。

Text.en: As you walked through the Taisce of Caer Tarth, you left Wraeclast behind. Here you stand in the Wildwood. <continue>The Wildwood was once a safe haven from the long Winter. Now... its own safety hangs in the balance.
Text.zh: 当你穿过赛尔塔斯的泰斯卡时，瓦尔克拉斯已被远远抛在身后。你现在正站在荒林之中。<continue>荒林曾经是躲避漫长寒冬的避风港，如今……荒林自身正岌岌可危。

Text.en: The picture in my mind... it scarce resembles what I see here...
Text.zh: 我预想的画面……跟眼前看到的差得太远了……

Text.en: So... it's as simple as walking through those trees, and suddenly I'm in the Wildwood?
Text.zh: 就这么简单？穿过那些树，我就突然进入荒林了？

Text.en: So the barrier between Wraeclast and Wildwood is little more than some twisted trees?
Text.zh: 所以，瓦尔克拉斯和荒林之间的屏障不过是些扭曲的树？

Text.en: So entering the Wildwood is as simple as walking through those trees?
Text.zh: 进入荒林就这么简单？穿过那些树就行了？

Text.en: My mother used to tell stories of the Wildwood. I always imagined that entering it would be more... complicated.
Text.zh: 我母亲以前常讲荒林的故事。我一直以为，进入它会……更复杂一些。

Text.en: So those trees there... I just walk through 'em, and I'm in the Wildwood?
Text.zh: 所以那些树……我只要走过去，就进入荒林了？

Text.en: So... I just take a stroll through those trees and bang – there I am in the Wildwood?
Text.zh: 所以……我只要穿过那些树，然后砰的一下，就到荒林了？

Text.en: I see. So to pass from Wraeclast to Wildwood is as simple as crossing that threshold?
Text.zh: 我明白了。所以从瓦尔克拉斯到荒林，就这么跨过那道门槛就行了？

Text.en: Aye. The more Nameless that come to exist, the more plagued the Wildwood becomes. I can feel it... its vitality waning on the wind.
Text.zh: 嗯。存在的无名者越多，荒林受的折磨就越多。我能感觉到……它的生命力正随风消逝。

Text.en: Yes. The Taisce of Caer Tarth is the original gateway to the Wildwood, the realm of the Draíocht. <continue>This is where the Goddess invited the first Azmeri inside. Those who would become the Maji. <continue>But during our great war against the Nameless, this dark wound festered here, under the guarding eyes of the Goddess. Now... it is the last bastion against the burgeoning darkness.
Text.zh: 是的，赛尔塔斯的泰斯卡就是通往荒林——德莱契领域——的原始门户。<continue>女神正是在此处邀请第一批阿兹莫里人进入。后来，那些人成为了众神。<continue>但在对抗无名者的伟大战争中，这道黑暗的伤口在女神的注视下慢慢溃烂。如今……它是抵御日益增长的黑暗的最后堡垒。

Text.en: Is there no hope? Is it too far gone?
Text.zh: 难道没有希望，已经无可救药了？

Text.en: Is this the only way to access the Wildwood?
Text.zh: 这是进入荒林的唯一途径吗？

Text.en: Are there ways to access the Wildwood besides Caer Tarth?
Text.zh: 除了赛尔塔斯，还有其他方法进入荒林吗？

Text.en: Are there other entry points to the Wildwood?
Text.zh: 还有别的入口能进荒林吗？

Text.en: So this Taisce is the one and only way to get inside?
Text.zh: 所以泰斯卡是进到里面的唯一途径？

Text.en: Strange, to think me ancestors once stood in this very place. <continue>Is this the only gateway to the Wildwood?
Text.zh: 想到我的祖先曾站在这个地方，让我感觉很奇怪。<continue>这是进入荒林的唯一门户吗？

Text.en: There other ways to get inside? Or is this the only one?
Text.zh: 还有其他进去的方法吗？还是说只有这一个？

Text.en: You called it the <i>{original} gateway... does that mean there are others?
Text.zh: 你管它叫<i>{原始}门户……那是不是还有其他门户？

Text.en: I believe the Goddess is watching on. She will guide us. As she always has. As she always will.
Text.zh: 我相信女神在注视我们，她会指引我们，一如既往，未来亦如是。

Text.en: There are countless other gateways among the forests, if you know where to look...
Text.zh: 森林里还有无数其他的门户，只要你你懂得留心观察……

Text.en: Ahh... yes. The Goddess... uh...
Text.zh: 啊……对。那位女神……呃……

Text.en: Really? You can't even remember <i>{her?}
Text.zh: 不是吧？你连<i>{她}都记不得了？

Text.en: I'm tryin' me damn best! It's been a long day! Nay, a long twenty odd years!
Text.zh: 我已经尽我所能了！今天已经够累的了！不，是这整整二十年都太累了！

Text.en: The Sisters Three were originally one Goddess. They are aspects of a shared soul.
Text.zh: 三姐妹原本是一位女神。她们是同一个灵魂的不同面向。

Text.en: Three forms... one Goddess. How peculiar.
Text.zh: 三个形态……一位女神。真奇特。

Text.en: So she split herself in three? How greedy.
Text.zh: 所以她把自己一分为三？真是贪心。

Text.en: Why did she become three?
Text.zh: 她为什么要变成三个？

Text.en: Three Sisters... but one Goddess?
Text.zh: 三姐妹……却是一位女神？

Text.en: This is mighty confusing.
Text.zh: 这也太让人费解了。

Text.en: Uh... so it's... one lady... or three ladies?
Text.zh: 呃……所以是……一位女士……还是三位女士？

Text.en: The Dreamer had no need for such unnecessary complexity.
Text.zh: 幻梦者就不需要这种没必要的弯弯绕。

Text.en: Allow me to explain...
Text.zh: 让我来解释一下……

Text.en: In creating the Wildwood, the Goddess gave all of herself. <continue>Her Body became the forest. Nature itself. The Mhacha. <continue>Her Hope became the balance between good and evil.  The unending battlefield. The Catha. <continue>Her Will became the guide. The call to arms. The Mórrigan. The Draíocht itself, as we know it...
Text.zh: 在创造荒林时，女神奉献了自己的全部。她的身体化作了森林。自然本身，即玛查。<continue>她的希望化作了善恶之间的平衡。无尽的战场，即卡塔。<continue>她的意志化作了指引，战斗的号角，即莫丽根。也就是我们所知的德莱契本身……

Text.en: Ah, yes... the Draíocht. It brings a strong feeling to me, rather than a memory...
Text.zh: 啊，对……德莱契。它带给我的是一种强烈的感觉，而非记忆……

Text.en: The Draíocht?
Text.zh: 德莱契？

Text.en: The Draíocht?
Text.zh: 德莱契？

Text.en: The Draíocht?
Text.zh: 德莱契？

Text.en: Ah, yes. I've heard of the Draíocht.
Text.zh: 啊，对，我听说过德莱契。

Text.en: The Draíocht?
Text.zh: 德莱契？

Text.en: The Draíocht?
Text.zh: 德莱契？

Text.en: The Draíocht?
Text.zh: 德莱契？

Text.en: She knows you know her. She wouldn't forget you. Nor you her.
Text.zh: 她知道你认识她。她不会忘记你。你也不会忘记她。

Text.en: It may appear to you as wisps. A chosen few hear its voice.
Text.zh: 它可能会以鬼灵的形态出现在你面前。少数被选中者能听见它的声音。

Text.en: Ah. It sounds like you're describin' the Spirit.
Text.zh: 哼。听起来你描述的就是万灵。

Text.en: Your 'Spirit' is her Will, outside of the Wildwood. Here, she is whole. The Draíocht is but one facet.
Text.zh: 你说所的万灵就是她的意志，在荒林之外。在这里，她是完整的。德莱契只是其中一个面向。

Text.en: You think your Draíocht and the Spirit are the same?
Text.zh: 你认为你的德莱契和万灵是同一个东西？

Text.en: In many ways, they <i>{are} the same.
Text.zh: 在很多方面，它们<i>{是}相同的。

Text.en: I've followed the Spirit all my life, but I've not heard of the Draíocht. What is it, plainly as you can?
Text.zh: 我一生追随万灵，但从未听说过德莱契。请用最浅显的话告诉我，那是什么？

Text.en: The Draíocht is the Will of the Wildwood, meant to guide Maji and traveller alike. <continue>It guides them to enact the Wildwood's desires. To heal the forest, or to fight the Nameless. <continue>If the Wildwood had a body, the Draíocht would be her voice. A chosen few have even claimed to hear her speak.
Text.zh: 德莱契是荒林的意志，旨在指引众神和旅者，<continue>引导他们实现荒林的意愿：治愈森林，或对抗无名者。<continue>如果荒林有身体，德莱契就会是她的声音。少数被选中的人甚至声称听到过她说话。

Text.en: That's the Spirit alright! <i>{I'll be damned.} The Spirit guides us Azmeri so we can heal the land o' <i>{rathin.} <continue>It is an honour to see the Spirit, and even more to follow it. <continue>Though I've not heard its voice – or... <i>{her} voice – for a long while.
Text.zh: 那不就是万灵吗！<i>{真是见鬼了。}万灵指引阿兹莫里人，让我们能够治愈这片土地的<i>{腐化}。<continue>能够见到万灵已是荣幸，追随它更是莫大的荣耀。<continue>虽然我还没听见过它的声音……或者……<i>{她的}声音？

Text.en: That distinction matters not. The Will once belonged to the Goddess, but now it is its own. <continue>I cannot be certain that the Will within the Wildwood, and the Will beyond, are the same aspect of the Goddess. <continue>Her aspects ebb and flow like the passage of time. <continue>It is my understanding that both Wills are of the Goddess, given for the benefit of all Azmeri.
Text.zh: 那种区别并不重要。意志曾经属于女神，但如今它已独立。<continue>我无法确定荒林之中的意志与之外的意志是否为女神的同一个面向。<continue>她的面向如时光流逝般潮起潮落。<continue>依我所见，这两个意志都源于女神，为了所有阿兹莫里人的福祉而赐下。

Text.en: My tribe tells a story of a Goddess creating the Wildwood. And she were one of three sisters, like your Goddess. <continue>Is it possible that the Goddess is Vir-
Text.zh: 我的部族流传着一个故事，一位女神创造了荒林。而她是三姐妹之一，就像你的女神一样。<continue>有没有可能那位女神就是维——

Text.en: Yes! But please. Do not speak her name. That <i>{is} why some know it as the Viridian Wildwood. <continue>The Goddess <i>{must} be pleased that her Will still guides those who did not make the Wildwood their home.
Text.zh: 是的！但拜托，别说出她的名字。<i>{正因}如此，有些人称它为维里迪安荒林。<continue>女神<i>{一定}会感到欣慰，因为她的意志仍在指引那些没有以荒林为家的人。

Text.en: Have you never thought it strange... the Goddess created the Wildwood, yet she created it flawed. The fact that speaking a name can create such anarchy...
Text.zh: 你有没有觉得很奇怪……女神创造荒林，却留下了缺陷。光是念出一个名字就能造成这样的混乱……

Text.en: Is it safe to say the names of the Sisters Three here?
Text.zh: 在这里说出三姐妹的名字安全吗？

Text.en: So you can say the names of the Sisters Three here?
Text.zh: 所以，你可以在这里说出三姐妹的名字？

Text.en: You say it's dangerous to utter names here... but didn't you just name the Sisters Three?
Text.zh: 你说在这里说出名字很危险……但你刚才不是说出了三姐妹的名字吗？

Text.en: So names are off limits here... except for the Sisters Three?
Text.zh: 所以在这里，名字是禁忌……但三姐妹的名字除外？

Text.en: If you can't say names here, how is it you just named the Sisters Three?
Text.zh: 既然在这里不能说名字，那你刚才怎么说出了三姐妹的名字？

Text.en: Hang on a minute... accordin' to you, we can't say names here. But you just named them Sisters, didn't you?
Text.zh: 等等……照你所说，我们不能在这里说名字。可你刚才不是管她们叫三姐妹了吗？

Text.en: You've spoken of the dangers that names pose... but you just named the Sisters Three, did you not?
Text.zh: 你说过名字会带来危险……但你刚才不是喊了三姐妹的名字吗？

Text.en: I have spoken no names. Only their titles. <continue>Did you think a loving mother named her children 'the Catha', 'the Mhacha' and 'the Morrigan'?
Text.zh: 我没有说出任何名字。只说了她们的名号。 <continue>你觉得一位慈爱的母亲会给自己的孩子起名叫“卡塔”、“玛哈”和“莫丽根”吗？

Text.en: I suppose not. And why do names work like that here?
Text.zh: 我想也是。那为什么名字在这里会有这种作用？

Text.en: Find that funny, do you? You won't be laughing when I call you by your name, little miss 'Speaker of Truths'. <continue>Why does it work like that, anyway?
Text.zh: 你觉得很好笑是吧？等我说出你的名字的时候你就笑不出来了，小小的“真理传述者”女士。<continue>话说回来，这到底为什么会这样？”

Text.en: The fact that names can do such damage... it seems a fatal flaw in the Wildwood's design.
Text.zh: 名字能造成这样的破坏……这似乎是荒林设计上一个致命的缺陷。

Text.en: If this place was designed by the Goddess herself, couldn't she have designed it in such a way that you can still say a person's name?
Text.zh: 如果这个地方是女神亲手设计的，她就不能设计得让人可以随便叫名字吗？

Text.en: Why do names hold such power here?
Text.zh: 为什么名字在这里有这么大的力量？

Text.en: Well I don't know! I only just got here, didn't I? <continue>Why do names work like that here, anyhow?
Text.zh: 我怎么知道！我也才刚到这里！<continue>为什么在这里名字会有这种作用？

Text.en: A fair point. But I still fail to understand why names hold such power here.
Text.zh: 说得有理。但我还是不明白为什么名字在这里会有这么大的力量。

Text.en: Names carry a person's everything. Titles are just a description. But know that the Goddess never intended any of this...
Text.zh: 名字承载着一个人的一切。名号只是一种描述。但要知道，女神从未有意要造成这一切……

Text.en: She gave of herself entirely. Every shred of her power, so that we, her chosen children, would never be left wanting. <continue>In the end it was her selflessness that was the problem, I suppose.
Text.zh: 她完全地奉献了自己，奉献了她的每一丝力量，只为了我们，她所选的孩子们，永远不会有所求。 <continue>到头来，我想正是她的无私造成了问题。

Text.en: Mhm... it is hard to blame her for that.
Text.zh: 嗯……这倒怪不到她头上。

Text.en: He, most foul, really doesn't deserve the title of King. But he has it nonetheless. <continue>Even <i>{we} fell to his whispers. To his promises. To his commitment to bringing 'meaning'. <continue>And yet... the meaning he sought was for those who were not, rather than us that <i>{were.}
Text.zh: 他，那个极其可恶的家伙，完全不配拥有“国王”的头衔。但他却还是坐上了那个位置。<continue>就连<i>{我们}也曾被他的低语、他的承诺、他带来“意义”的决心所动摇。<continue>然而……他所追求的意义，是为了那些不存在之物，而不是我们这些<i>{存在}之物。

Text.en: The King in the Mists is no king at all. He is a usurper, ripped from the Realm of Nothingness. <continue>A loathsome fiend, brought into existence by a careless Naming.
Text.zh: 迷雾之王根本不是什么国王。他是个篡位者，是从虚无领域中被撕扯出来的东西。<continue>因一次不经意的“命名”而诞生的可憎恶魔。

Text.en: But how did he... a no one, from nowhere... come to be a king of all those who... are not?
Text.zh: 可他……一个来自虚无的无名之辈……怎么就成了所有这些……不存在之物的国王？

Text.en: He is from the Nothingness? Like the afterlife?
Text.zh: 他来自虚无？是说死后世界吗？

Text.en: The Nothingness? Is that Death?
Text.zh: 虚无？那就是死亡吗？

Text.en: Death is for those that lived. Nothingness is for those that never existed.
Text.zh: 死亡属于那些活过的事物。虚无属于那些从未存在过的事物。

Text.en: [NOAUDIO] And this 'naming' made him exist?
Text.zh: 而这个“命名”让他存在了？

Text.en: And someone forced him to exist?
Text.zh: 是有人强迫他存在的？

Text.en: He did exist in the Nothingness first... right?
Text.zh: 他确实先在虚无中存在了……对吧？

Text.en: Was he a King in the Nothingness? Or was he something else?
Text.zh: 他在虚无中是国王吗？还是别的什么？

Text.en: It is not for us to know. The Nothingness is a realm made of those things that might be, but are not.
Text.zh: 那不是我们能知道的。虚无是由那些“可能存在但实际不存在”的事物构成的。

Text.en: Well that cleared it up perfectly. What made him 'be', then?
Text.zh: 这下彻底说明白了。那是什么让他“存在”了呢？

Text.en: I see. And a 'naming' caused his existence? How?
Text.zh: 我明白了。是“命名”导致了他的存在？这怎么做到的？

Text.en: I do not follow. He was forced into existence?
Text.zh: 我没听懂。他是被迫存在的？

Text.en: Forced into existence? What does that even mean?
Text.zh: 被迫存在？这到底什么意思？

Text.en: Everything that exists, at some point, did not. They must be born, or built, or Named.
Text.zh: 一切存在之物，在某个时刻都不存在。它们必须被诞生、被建造，或被命名。

Text.en: The Spirit has taught me much, but I don't know this realm of 'Nothingness'.
Text.zh: 万灵教会了我很多，但我从没听说过这个“虚无”领域。

Text.en: The Nothingness is those things that are not known. If you know them - if you Name them - then they exist.
Text.zh: 虚无是那些未被认知的事物。如果你认知了它们——如果你为它们命名——那么它们就存在了。

Text.en: And a single naming did this? How is that possible?
Text.zh: 一次命名就做到了？怎么可能？

Text.en: The tale told is of a careless Naming. The first of its kind. A child, with a most vibrant imagination, changed the future of this sacred place. <continue>A child and his mother came to the Wildwood, seeking refuge from great dangers. <continue>The child's mind was filled with fear, known and unknown alike. He gave that fear a Name, and spoke it aloud. <continue>A howl of anger and agony echoed through the Wildwood as the King in the Mists erupted into being.
Text.zh: 传说中是一次不经意的命名，一次开先河之举。一个孩子，拥有最活跃的想象力，改变了这片圣地的未来。<continue>一个孩子和他的母亲来到荒林，为了躲避极度的危险。<continue>孩子的内心充满恐惧，有他熟悉的，也有陌生的。他给那恐惧赋予了一个名字，并大声说了出来。<continue>一声愤怒和痛苦的嚎叫回荡在整个荒林，迷雾之王骤然现世。

Text.en: In the Wildwood, we are closer to the Nothingness. This place is a bridge between realms. <continue>Those things that cease <i>{not being...} well, here... they <i>{become.} <continue>The tale is told: a Name was carelessly spoken, and a howl of anger and agony echoed through the entire Wildwood. <continue>That howl was the King in the Mists erupting into being. It tore a hole wide as the sea and thin as a needle. <continue>Since that moment, he has waged an endless campaign to bring more Nameless like himself into existence. <continue>His cultists carry his message and build his altars. Their belief empowers him.
Text.zh: 在荒林里，我们离虚无更近。这个地方是各领域之间的桥梁。<continue>那些不再<i>{不存在}的东西……嗯，在这里……它们<i>{存在}了。<continue>传说中：一个名字被不经意地说出，一声愤怒与痛苦的嚎叫回荡在整个荒林。<continue>那声嚎叫就是迷雾之王的骤然现世。它撕开了一个宽如大海、细如针尖的裂口。<continue>从那一刻起，他便发动了一场无休止的战役，要将更多无名者像他一样存在。<continue>他的邪教徒传播他的讯息，建造他的祭坛。他们的信仰为他提供力量。

Text.en: What name did this child speak? The King in the Mists bears no name I've heard.
Text.zh: 那个孩子说了什么名字？我从没听说过迷雾之王有什么名字。

Text.en: How does their belief serve him? What does he plan to do?
Text.zh: 他们的信仰如何为他所用？他打算做什么？

Text.en: Empowers him to do what? Other than be a pain in our backsides.
Text.zh: 为他提供力量去做什么？除了给我们添麻烦之外？

Text.en: What does he mean to do with all that belief?
Text.zh: 他用这些信仰来干什么？

Text.en: In what sense? What does he hope to achieve?
Text.zh: 什么意思？他到底希望达成什么？

Text.en: Empowers him to what end?
Text.zh: 为什么要给他力量？

Text.en: These Nameless are a bloody walking contradiction. <continue>What's the King get out of all this, anyhow?
Text.zh: 这些无名者真他妈是活生生的矛盾体。<continue>那国王到底能从中得到什么？

Text.en: To what end?
Text.zh: 这是为了什么呢？

Text.en: That is a question I doubt any can answer, other than the child. The name was the vessel, while the meaning behind it was the true power.
Text.zh: 恐怕除了那个孩子，这个问题没人能回答。名字是容器，而名字背后的意义才是真正的力量。

Text.en: I was foolishly part of his cult, for a time. <continue>The King's words fell on our ears with the promise of hope to end the war for the Wildwood. Twisting a dream into a nightmare... <continue>Now I know... he meant the end of the Maji.
Text.zh: 我曾愚蠢地加入过他的教团一段时间。<continue>国王的话语落入我们耳中，许下了结束荒林战争的希望，却硬生生把美梦扭曲成噩梦…… <continue>现在我明白了……他想要的是众神的终结。

Text.en: I grow tired of this scant existence, love. I've been like this too long now. I wish for peace.
Text.zh: 我厌倦了这种苟且的存在，亲爱的。我已经这样太久了。我想要安息。

Text.en: My lamenting has gone on far too long. <continue>I wish to be at peace...
Text.zh: 我的哀叹已经持续得太久了。<continue>我想要安息……

Text.en: I know. I can feel your pain. Though I desperately don't want you to go... I know you must.
Text.zh: 我知道。我能感受到你的痛苦。虽然我万分不想让你离开……但我知道你必须走。

Text.en: What does the King in the Mists want your body for?
Text.zh: 迷雾之王要你的身体做什么？

Text.en: It feels hypocritical of me to even ask, but why does the King need your corpse?
Text.zh: 我都没脸问这个问题，但国王为什么需要你的身体？

Text.en: For what purpose does the King need your body?
Text.zh: 国王要你的身体做什么？

Text.en: What is the King in the Mists doing with your body?
Text.zh: 迷雾之王在用你的尸体做什么？

Text.en: What's the King need your body for?
Text.zh: 国王要你的身体有什么用？

Text.en: I'm almost scared to ask what the King wants your body for.
Text.zh: 我都不敢问国王要你的身体干什么。

Text.en: For what reason does the King hold your body captive?
Text.zh: 国王为什么囚禁着你的身体？

Text.en: Once my body is rescued from the King's clutches, perhaps then... I can rest.
Text.zh: 一旦我的身体从国王的魔掌中被救回，也许那时……我就能安息了。

Text.en: A ritual... or a message. <continue>Either way, I am being defiled for dark ends.
Text.zh: 一个仪式……或者一则讯息。<continue>无论如何，邪恶的计划正在玷污我。

Text.en: I will avenge you, my love. You have my word.
Text.zh: 我会为你复仇，我的爱人。我向你保证。

Text.en: Disgraceful. No one deserves such a fate. <continue>But if we recovered your body, you'd finally be able to pass on?
Text.zh: 真可耻。没人该落得如此命运。<continue>但如果我们找回你的身体，你就能最终安息吗？

Text.en: But if we got your body back, then you'd be free?
Text.zh: 如果我们找回你的身体，你就能自由了吗？

Text.en: Would retrieving your body allow you to rest?
Text.zh: 找回你的身体能让你安息吗？

Text.en: And if we recovered your body... would that bring you peace?
Text.zh: 如果我们找回你的身体……那能给你带来安宁吗？

Text.en: Then we'd best retrieve your body. Surely that would bring you peace?
Text.zh: 那我们最好找回你的身体。那肯定能给你带来安宁吧？

Text.en: Let's say we get your body back. Then could you rest?
Text.zh: 假设我们拿回了你的身体，那你就能安息了吗？

Text.en: Would recovering your body bring you peace?
Text.zh: 找回你的身体能给你带来安宁吗？

Text.en: I certainly hope so... Or perhaps my soul longs for the return of one who may never come...
Text.zh: 但愿如此……又或许，我的灵魂渴望的，是某个或许永远不会回来的人……

Text.en: And so it is... I must continue on here. But at least the end is in sight.
Text.zh: 就是这样了……我必须留在这里，但至少终点已在眼前。

Text.en: My lamenting continues. But... the end is in sight.
Text.zh: 我的哀叹还在继续，但是……终点已在眼前。

Text.en: The Goddess spoke highly of you. She knows, as I do, that you are a brave soul.
Text.zh: 女神对你评价很高。她和我一样，认为你拥有勇敢的灵魂。

Text.en: I hope for your sake that's true. Do you not feel... used by the Goddess?
Text.zh: 为了你，我希望那是真的。你不觉得自己……被女神利用了吗？

Text.en: Or so your Goddess says. Do you really trust her? She's using you!
Text.zh: 也就你的女神会那么讲。你真的相信她吗？她在利用你！

Text.en: Your Goddess does not seem concerned about using you to achieve her goals. Does it not bother you?
Text.zh: 你的女神拿你当棋子使，而且一点不觉得有什么不妥。你不觉得这很有问题吗？

Text.en: Still your Goddess won't let you rest. She seems to view you more like a tool than a subject.
Text.zh: 但你的女神还是不肯让你安息。她似乎把你当工具，而不是子民。

Text.en: For your sake, I hope you're right. Seems a raw deal that you're forced to linger here.
Text.zh: 为了你着想，我希望你是对的。你被迫在此徘徊，似乎很不公平。

Text.en: Don't mean to offend you, miss, but... isn't your Goddess kinda givin' you the runaround?
Text.zh: 无意冒犯，女士，只是……你的女神是不是在耍你？

Text.en: Forgive my asking, but... do you not feel used by the Goddess?
Text.zh: 恕我冒昧，但……你不觉得自己被女神利用了吗？

Text.en: You... are too kind. But still, I grow ever tired. I will do my duty. But nothing more. I must pass over.
Text.zh: 你……太善良了。但我依然越来越疲倦。我会尽我的职责，但仅此而已。我必须离去。

Text.en: I do not think of it this way. In serving her, I am fulfilling a promise. A promise I made to the one I loved. <continue>I just hope... if we are successful... he may find his way back home.
Text.zh: 我不这么看。侍奉她，我就是在履行一个承诺。一个我对我所爱之人许下的承诺。<continue>我只希望……如果我们成功了……他能找到回家的路。

Text.en: I understand. I just wish... we could go back to better times. Spend our days in the village by the river once more.
Text.zh: 我明白。我只是希望……我们能回到更好的时光。重回河畔村庄过我们的日子。

Text.en: You remember?
Text.zh: 你还记得吗？

Text.en: How could I possibly forget? We were young. The days were long. And the nights were warm. <continue>I still remember ye on the day we first met... the sun falling across ye as ye danced. What a sight it was.
Text.zh: 我怎么可能忘记？那时我们年轻。白天很长，夜晚很暖。 <continue>我还记得初见时你的模样……你舞动时，阳光洒落在你身上。那是多么美的景象啊。

Text.en: I remember that day well too. The village by the river meant everything to the Maji. And yet... it was the first to fall.
Text.zh: 那一天我也记得很清楚。河畔村庄对众神来说意味着一切。然而……它却是第一个沦陷的。

Text.en: One day... I would see it again with my own eyes.
Text.zh: 总有一天……我会亲眼再见到它。

Text.en: It is tragic to consider how we were deceived. The King's words fell on eager ears with a promise of hope to end the war for the Wildwood. Twisting a dream into a nightmare...
Text.zh: 一想到我们如何被骗，我就感到痛心。国王的话语落入翘首以盼的耳朵里，许下了结束荒林战争的希望，却硬生生把美梦扭曲成噩梦……

Text.en: Their very existence afflicts the Wildwood with darkness and rot. <continue>The Maji and Nameless have been at war since as long as anyone can remember. <continue>Me and mine joined the King... but we knew not his true intent. We fell into his lies, thinking he promised peace. <continue>The Nameless are naught but deceivers and monsters. They do not deserve the existence they so desire!
Text.zh: 正是它们的存在，让荒林遭受黑暗与腐化的侵蚀。<continue>自有记录以来，众神就与无名者便征战不休。<continue>我和我的族人加入了国王……但我们不知道他的真实意图，落入了他的谎言，以为他许诺的是和平。<continue>无名者不过是骗子与怪物，根本不配拥有它们渴望的存在！

Text.en: We weren't to know. He knew us better than we knew ourselves. Sold us a pack of lies. <continue>His cultists still swallow his words, thinking they are to bring eternal spring to the Wildwood. <continue>Instead... they threaten to doom it forevermore.
Text.zh: 我们无从知晓。他比我们自己还了解我们，给我们灌输了成堆的谎言。他的邪教徒至今还在吞咽他的话语，以为他们会给荒林带来永恒的春天。<continue>可实际上……他们威胁要永远毁灭它。

Text.en: That's terrible. We cannot let them win.
Text.zh: 太可怕了。我们不能让他们得逞。

Text.en: This struggle reminds me of the Maraketh and the Faridun. <continue>The sands shift, but the hatred remains the same.
Text.zh: 他们的冲突与马拉克斯人和法里顿人之间的冲突如出一辙。<continue>岁月流转，但敌意依旧。

Text.en: I certainly do my fair share of slaughter. You're... sure?
Text.zh: 我确实没少杀生。你……确定？

Text.en: They took <i>{everything} from me.
Text.zh: 他们夺走了我的<i>{一切}。

Text.en: Why not leave them alone, then? Let them exist peacefully?
Text.zh: 那为什么不能放过他们？让他们安安静静地存在着？

Text.en: Could you not leave them be? Live and let live?
Text.zh: 你就不能放过他们，各自安好？

Text.en: I gather the Nameless have no interest in peace, then.
Text.zh: 我猜无名者对和平毫无兴趣。

Text.en: Our ancestors tried that. There can be no peace with such beasts.
Text.zh: 我们的祖先试过。跟这样的野兽，是不可能有和平的。

Text.en: Can't you just give them the Wildwood?
Text.zh: 你就不能把荒林让给他们吗？

Text.en: What? That's our home!
Text.zh: 什么？那是我们的家园！

Text.en: Respectfully, miss... you're dead.
Text.zh: 恕我直言，女士……你已经死了。

Text.en: His whispers are hard to ignore... and when I finally learned the truth... well, it was too late.
Text.zh: 他的低语很难忽视……当我终于得知真相时……唉，已经太晚了。

Text.en: Generations of Maji have died fighting the Nameless. They must be stopped!
Text.zh: 一代又一代的众神在与无名者的战斗中死去。必须阻止他们！

Text.en: Once the King is dead, and the Nameless are left to rot in their nonexistence, the Maji can return somehow. <continue>And then, under the light of the Goddess... bring the Wildwood back to its former glory. <continue>Maybe... even the Steward of Tales may come home.
Text.zh: 一旦国王死去，无名者便会被丢在不存在中自生自灭……众神就能以某种方式回归。<continue>然后，在女神的光芒下……让荒林重现昔日的荣光。<continue>也许……连故事守护者都能回家。

Text.en: I have faith in my heart... that you will be the one to see this through.
Text.zh: 我深信……你会是将这一切贯彻到底的人。

Text.en: I could think of no more worthy champion than you, my love. <continue>Let me bless you with a title, so your Name remains hidden.
Text.zh: 没有比你更适合的勇士了，我的爱人。<continue>让我赐予你一个名号，好让你的真名得以隐藏。

Text.en: You have proven yourself a true ally of the Maji. <continue>Allow me to bestow you a title, so that your Name remains safe.
Text.zh: 你已证明自己是众神真正的盟友。<continue>请允许我赐予你一个名号，好让你的真名得以保全。

Text.en: As you wish. What title would you give me?
Text.zh: 听你的。你要给我什么名号？

Text.en: I will call you the Champion of the Wilds. <continue>If anyone can destroy the Bodach, I know that it is you. <continue>Who'd have thought? My man... chosen to be champion by the Goddess herself! I must have good judgement.
Text.zh: 我将称你为荒野勇士。 <continue>如果说有人能摧毁伯达克，那只能是你。 <continue>谁能想到呢？我的男人……竟被女神亲自选为勇士！我的眼光真是不错。

Text.en: What is my title then?
Text.zh: 那我的名号是什么？

Text.en: You will be known as the Champion of Fortitude.
Text.zh: 你将被称为坚毅勇士。

Text.en: Go on then. Let me hear it.
Text.zh: 那就说吧。让我听听。

Text.en: You will be known as the Champion of Death.
Text.zh: 你将被称为死亡勇士。

Text.en: Go on. What is your title for me?
Text.zh: 说吧。你为我选了什么名号？

Text.en: You will be known as the Champion of the Elements.
Text.zh: 你将被称为元素勇士。

Text.en: If you must...
Text.zh: 如果你坚持的话……

Text.en: You will be known as the Champion of Nature.
Text.zh: 你将被称为自然勇士。

Text.en: Go on then. I'm ready.
Text.zh: 那就说吧，我准备好了。

Text.en: You will be known as the Champion of the Hunt.
Text.zh: 你将被称为狩猎勇士。

Text.en: I'm ready! Make it a goodun!
Text.zh: 我准备好了！给我个响亮的名号！

Text.en: You will be known as the Champion of War.
Text.zh: 你将被称为战争勇士。

Text.en: Damn, that sounds tough. I could get used to that.
Text.zh: 该死，听起来真硬气。我能接受。

Text.en: So be it.
Text.zh: 那就依你吧。

Text.en: You will be known as the Benevolent Champion.
Text.zh: 你将被称为仁善勇士。

Text.en: Destroying the Bodach may seem an insurmountable feat... but remain steadfast. <continue>The Goddess chose you for a reason. I trust her judgement above all else.
Text.zh: 摧毁伯达克或许看似不可能……但要坚定。<continue>女神选中你是有原因的。我无比信任她的判断。

Text.en: The Bodach... it is real? Weren't it just a children's tale? To keep them from misbehavin'?
Text.zh: 伯达克……是真的？不是童话故事？用来吓唬小孩别捣蛋的？

Text.en: Yes. It is real. It shares this title... but it is fearsome beyond any child's imaginings. <continue>I've waking nightmares of it since I've been trapped here. Whether they are born of the Goddess, or the darkness in the Bodach's heart, I can't be sure.
Text.zh: 没错，它是真的。它也叫这一名号……它比孩子们想象的更可怕。<continue>自从我被困在这里，就一直做有关它的噩梦。这些噩梦是源于女神，还是源于伯达克内心的黑暗，我也不确定。

Text.en: The Maji and the Nameless, both, feared that <i>{thing.} <continue>It is not Nameless... for <i>{they} can be Named. <i>{They} can be given form. That thing cannot. <continue>It never had a Name. It can never have one. It is the only thing that does exist... in the nonexistence. <continue>'The Bodach' was once just a title of a beast from a Maji children's tale...
Text.zh: 众神和无名者，都惧怕<i>{那东西。}<continue>它不是无名者……因为<i>{它们}可以被命名，<i>{它们}可以被赋予形态。而那东西不能。<continue>它从来就没有名字，也永远不可能有。它是唯一存在于……不存在之中的东西。<continue>“伯达克”曾经只是众神童话中一头野兽的名号……

Text.en: What do you see in these nightmares?
Text.zh: 你在这些噩梦里看到了什么？

Text.en: I'm still unclear on what it is. Do you know?
Text.zh: 我还是不明白它是什么。你知道吗？

Text.en: Do you even know what it is?
Text.zh: 难道你不知道它是什么？

Text.en: Do you at least know what it is?
Text.zh: 你至少应该知道它是什么吧？

Text.en: Then... what is it? Do you even know?
Text.zh: 那么……它究竟是什么？这你应该知道吧？

Text.en: If it's not Nameless, do you know what it is?
Text.zh: 如果它不是无名者，那你知道它是什么吗？

Text.en: All this nonexistence shite makes my head hurt. <continue>At least tell me you know what this Bodach thing is.
Text.zh: 这些关于“不存在”的废话让我头疼。<continue>你总该知道伯达克到底是什么东西吧？

Text.en: Have you any idea what it is?
Text.zh: 你知道它到底是什么吗？

Text.en: Very little. Flashes of its horned likeness. Then... misery. And death. <continue>It is the only thing that exists... in the realm of nonexistence. That, in itself, is horrifying. <continue>Should it escape, there is no telling what doom it may bring upon the world.
Text.zh: 知之甚少。只有它头上长角的模糊影像。然后就是……痛苦和死亡。<continue>它是唯一存在于……不存在之域的东西。这一点本身就极其恐怖。<continue>如果它逃出来，天知道会给世界带来怎样的灾难。

Text.en: I do not, but the King in the Mists knew. While I followed him, I learned fragments of the truth... <continue>The Bodach is a broken idea. A thing which cannot be. <continue>A coin with only one side. A path that leads to nowhere but itself. <continue>I know not what it intends, or who it serves. <continue>I know only that the world will slowly unravel if it ever reaches the true light of meaning.
Text.zh: 我不知道，但迷雾之王知道。我追随他时，得知了一些真相碎片……<continue>伯达克是一个破碎的理念，不可能存在之物。<continue>一枚只有一面的硬币。一条无处可去、只能通向自身的路。<continue>我不知道它的意图，也不知道它为谁服务。<continue>我只知道，一旦它触及真正的意义之光，世界就会慢慢瓦解。

Text.en: Best we see this bastard put to rest then.
Text.zh: 我们最好还是把这个混蛋彻底解决掉。

Text.en: What is this? How've you the title of 'Queen?'
Text.zh: 这怎么回事？你怎么会有“女王”的头衔？

Text.en: I believe I fought one of his concubines... he seems to think we've got some kind of connection now.
Text.zh: 我之前好像跟他的一个妃子打过一架……他好像就觉得我们之间有了某种联系。

Text.en: Be careful. He may weave an impressive tale... but do not let him deceive you. <continue>Perhaps you could be the deceiver instead... use it against him!
Text.zh: 小心些，他可能会编造一个动人的故事……别让他骗到你。<continue>也许，你反而可以成为那个欺骗者……利用这一点来对付他！

Text.en: It is good to see that you survived the recent devastation. I suspected you would. <continue>I am here on behalf of the Order of the Djinn.
Text.zh: 很高兴你能从新近的灾祸中活下来，我一点也不惊讶。<continue>我是巨灵教的一员。

Text.en: You do not know me, but I know you. I have been watching you for some time. <continue>I am an agent of the Order of the Djinn.
Text.zh: 你不认识我，但我认识你。我已经观察你一段时间了。我是巨灵教的一员。

Text.en: In all this chaos, we lost track of several extremely powerful artefacts. <continue>I am trying to track them down, so that the situation does not become... even worse.
Text.zh: 在这场混乱中，我们丢失了几件非常强大的神器。我正在设法追回它们，以免局面变得……更加糟糕。

Text.en: Was an artefact here?
Text.zh: 这里有神器吗？

Text.en: There was an artefact here? Where?
Text.zh: 这里有神器？在哪儿？

Text.en: Was one of these artefacts here?
Text.zh: 这里有其中一件神器？

Text.en: Was one of them here?
Text.zh: 其中一件在这里吗？

Text.en: One o' them was here, I take it.
Text.zh: 我猜，其中一件曾在这里。

Text.en: You... lost track of 'em? Could I have seen one?
Text.zh: 你……弄丢了？我有见过其中一件吗？

Text.en: This 'artefact'... is here?
Text.zh: 这件“神器”……在这里？

Text.en: Might I have I seen this artefact ye seek?
Text.zh: 我见过你寻找的这件神器吗？

Text.en: Yes. It is that ancient gem you found. <continue>Right now, it is likely safer with you than in our Vaults. You should keep it. Use it. <continue>I will assist you with knowledge I have learned in my travels.
Text.zh: 是的。就是你找到的那颗古老宝石。<continue>眼下，它放在你身边可能比放在我们的宝库里更安全。你该留着它，使用它。<continue>我会用我游历中所学的知识来协助你。

Text.en: This here is the next location where you might find a similar artefact.
Text.zh: 这里便是下一处可以找到类似遗物的地点。

Text.en: That is two of the four artefacts I am tracking. <continue>Allow me to continue aiding you.
Text.zh: 我追踪的四件神器，如今已找到两件。<continue>请允许我继续协助你。

Text.en: I have located the next area we should investigate.
Text.zh: 我已经确定了下一个我们应该调查的区域。

Text.en: I will meet you there when you have secured the area.
Text.zh: 等你清理完那片区域，我会在那里与你碰面。

Text.en: You could fight alongside me. Show me who you truly are.
Text.zh: 你可以跟我并肩作战。让我看看你到底是什么样的人。

Text.en: Not one for small talk, are you? Me neither.
Text.zh: 你不是个爱闲聊的人，对吗？我也是。

Text.en: We could journey there together. I miss the company of my people.
Text.zh: 我们可以一起上路。我想念同胞的陪伴。

Text.en: That's... abrupt. Not that I mind skipping the small talk.
Text.zh: 这……有点突然。不过我不介意省去闲聊。

Text.en: You're just gonna bark your orders and run off? I barely know you.
Text.zh: 你就这么丢下命令然后跑掉了？我都不认识你啊。

Text.en: That's it? Just gonna pop off then? Could fight together. Get to know each other.
Text.zh: 就这样？就这么溜了？我们可以一起战斗，互相了解了解。

Text.en: I prefer to know a bit more about those I fight alongside.
Text.zh: 我更想多了解了解与我并肩作战的人。

Text.en: Not gonna tell me who ye are, lad?
Text.zh: 你不打算告诉我你是谁吗，小子？

Text.en: My life is my oath. Jado is but a name, and behind it, there is simply... nothing there.
Text.zh: 我的生命就是我的誓言。贾多不过是个名字，名字背后，其实……空无一物。

Text.en: That is... grim.
Text.zh: 听起来真是……悲凉。

Text.en: I usually have to work harder to break someone's spirit.
Text.zh: 通常我还得再费点劲才能击垮一个人的精神。

Text.en: I understand.
Text.zh: 我理解。

Text.en: Don't walk to talk? Fine by me.
Text.zh: 不想说话？随你便。

Text.en: If you don't want to talk, you can just say so.
Text.zh: 如果你不想说话，直说就是了。

Text.en: Wow.
Text.zh: 哇噢。

Text.en: A man of duty. I relate.
Text.zh: 一个尽职的人。我能理解。

Text.en: Come now. You've not got amnesia.
Text.zh: 得了吧。你又没得失忆症。

Text.en: You are indeed a worthy ally.
Text.zh: 你确实是一位值得信赖的盟友。

Text.en: Thank you, friend.
Text.zh: 谢谢你，朋友。

Text.en: Finally, someone acknowledges my prowess unprompted.
Text.zh: 终于有人主动认可我的本事了。

Text.en: I appreciate your praise. Thank you.
Text.zh: 感谢你的称赞，谢谢。

Text.en: I'm still not sure we're 'allies,' but... thanks.
Text.zh: 我还是不太确定我们算不算“盟友”，但是……谢谢。

Text.en: Thank you. I'll take any ally I can get in times like this.
Text.zh: 谢谢你。这种时候，任何盟友我都求之不得。

Text.en: Uhh, thanks... I guess.
Text.zh: 呃，谢谢吧。

Text.en: That is much appreciated. Thank you.
Text.zh: 我很领情，谢谢你。

Text.en: As are you. Thank you kindly.
Text.zh: 你也是。多谢。

Text.en: You... are welcome. But the mission is not over yet.
Text.zh: 不客气。但任务尚未结束。

Text.en: Finding the last artefact will likely be extremely dangerous.
Text.zh: 找寻最后一件神器的旅途可能极度危险。

Text.en: I suppose we'll need some luck.
Text.zh: 我想我们还需要点运气。

Text.en: That's all? I could have told you that.
Text.zh: 就这？你不说我也知道啊。

Text.en: Great tales are told of such danger. I can handle it.
Text.zh: 这种危险，故事里可没少讲。我能应付。

Text.en: Don't people usually wish each other luck in times like this?
Text.zh: 这种时候人们通常不是会互祝好运吗？

Text.en: I can only hope the Spirit is watching over me.
Text.zh: 我只能寄希望于万灵的庇佑。

Text.en: Aren't you gonna say 'good luck'?
Text.zh: 你都不说一句“祝你好运”吗？

Text.en: May the Dreamer guide us through the challenges ahead.
Text.zh: 愿幻梦者指引我们度过前方的挑战。

Text.en: Everythin' we do is extremely dangerous.
Text.zh: 我们干的哪件事不是凶险万分。

Text.en: We are but pawns in an eternal struggle. If we die, we die, and another will replace us.
Text.zh: 我们不过是永恒纷争中的棋子。死便死了，自会有后来者接替。

Text.en: My mission is complete... for now.
Text.zh: 我的任务完成了……暂时如此。

Text.en: Perhaps now, the tide will begin to turn.
Text.zh: 或许，现在局势将开始逆转。

Text.en: I hope you are right.
Text.zh: 希望你是对的。

Text.en: You almost sound hopeful.
Text.zh: 你语气里像是带了点希望。

Text.en: Let us hope you are right.
Text.zh: 但愿你是对的。

Text.en: I certainly hope so.
Text.zh: 我当然希望如此。

Text.en: I hope so.
Text.zh: 希望如此。

Text.en: Is that a bit of hope I hear?
Text.zh: 我是不是听到了一丝希望？

Text.en: Do I detect hope in your tone?
Text.zh: 我好像从你的语气里听出了希望？

Text.en: Aye, we can hope.
Text.zh: 嗯，我们大可以抱有希望。

Text.en: Hope is a mistake. Only determination matters. And power.
Text.zh: 希望是个错误，唯有决心和力量才是实在的。

Text.en: There will be other artefacts we need, but the danger has passed for now. <continue>I will see you again soon. <i>{You...} will see me when I deem it necessary.
Text.zh: 我们还需要寻找其他神器，但眼下的危机已经过去。<continue>我很快就会再与你见面。等我认为必要时，<i>{你……}自会见到我。

Text.en: Goodbye... for now.
Text.zh: 再会了……暂且别过。

Text.en: We have begun the long task of recovering the artefacts that were stolen twenty years ago. <continue>The Forbidden Vaults are where we contain those dangerous objects.
Text.zh: 我们开启了漫长的任务，追回二十年前被盗的那些神器。<continue>隐秘宝库就是我们存放那些危险物品的地方。

Text.en: And where is that?
Text.zh: 那地方在哪儿？

Text.en: Sounds like a place I should visit. Where is it?
Text.zh: 听起来像是我该去的地方。在哪儿？

Text.en: Is it in the same place as the previous Vaults? The tale-women say it is near an oasis...
Text.zh: 和以前的宝库在同一个地方吗？说书人说它在某个绿洲附近……

Text.en: Where is it? It can't be near here.
Text.zh: 在哪儿？不可能在这附近吧。

Text.en: I reckon I can help. Where is it?
Text.zh: 我觉得我能帮上忙。在哪儿？

Text.en: And where's that then?
Text.zh: 在哪儿呢？

Text.en: Where is this 'Forbidden Vault'?
Text.zh: 这个“禁忌宝库”在哪儿？

Text.en: Where would you hide somethin' like that?
Text.zh: 你会把那种东西藏在哪儿？

Text.en: I'm not permitted... wait a minute. Did you really think I would just tell you?
Text.zh: 我不能说……等等，你不会真以为我会告诉你吧？

Text.en: I do my best to earn the trust placed in me.
Text.zh: 我会尽力不辜负别人对我的信任。

Text.en: Can't blame a girl for trying.
Text.zh: 姑娘家多问一句又不犯法。

Text.en: Yes? I probably know the location anyway...
Text.zh: 是吗？反正我大概已经知道在哪儿了……

Text.en: You never know.
Text.zh: 说不准呢。

Text.en: Fair enough. Guess I got used to people in need bein' quite open with their troubles.
Text.zh: 有道理。大概是我见惯了那些有难处的人，会把麻烦一股脑儿全倒出来。

Text.en: Mate, it's not you, it's me. Everywhere I go, it's 'nice to meet you. Here's my life story.' Then, they hit me with a job.
Text.zh: 朋友，不是你的问题，是我的问题。我去哪儿都是“很高兴认识你，这是我的生平故事”，然后就甩给我一堆活。

Text.en: Most people I've met outside the Monastery do seem to divulge a great deal about their wants and needs...
Text.zh: 我在修道院外遇到的大多数人，似乎确实会透露很多他们的欲求……

Text.en: Ease up, lad. I've no plans to rob you.
Text.zh: 放松点，小子。我没打算打劫你。

Text.en: I know only one thing about the events of the past: we were betrayed.
Text.zh: 关于过去的事，我只知道一点——我们被背叛了。

Text.en: Betrayed? By who?
Text.zh: 背叛？被谁？

Text.en: Betrayal? Now you've got my interest. Tell me more.
Text.zh: 背叛？这下你勾起我的兴趣了。再多说点。

Text.en: I believe I heard this, but only in hushed whispers. What do you know?
Text.zh: 我听说过这件事，但只是私下八卦。你知道些什么？

Text.en: Trust is a dangerous game. What happened?
Text.zh: 信任是场危险的游戏。究竟发生了什么？

Text.en: I'd be keen to hear that story.
Text.zh: 我很想听听这个故事。

Text.en: Oof.
Text.zh: 唉。

Text.en: Betrayal is unforgivable. Who was it?
Text.zh: 背叛不可饶恕。究竟是谁？

Text.en: Betrayed? By who?
Text.zh: 背叛？被谁？

Text.en: Supposedly, we were betrayed by one of our own members. <continue>The Order will never make the same mistakes again.
Text.zh: 据说，叛徒是我们内部的成员。<continue>教团绝不会再犯同样的错误。

Text.en: There are many objects in this world one would consider powerful. <continue>Above that, there are relics that undermine the very foundations of our existence.
